EDCS-00380004.translation: To Larcia Priscilla and Arria Priscilla. Gaius Nasennius Musaeus made (this).
EDCS-00380004.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Larcia Priscilla and Arria Priscilla by Gaius Nasennius Musaeus.

EDCS-00380006.summary: This inscription records that Gaius Bryttius Vitalis was the maker of an unspecified object or monument.

EDCS-00380008.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Sabucius Perpetuus.

EDCS-00380010.translation: Habinnericus the son of Gallicanus made (this).
EDCS-00380010.summary: This inscription records that Habinnericus, son of Gallicanus, was the maker of an unspecified object or monument.

EDCS-00380012.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---] of Quintus Hortensius Nereus, lictor, the mother [---].
EDCS-00380012.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment potentially for Quintus Hortensius Nereus, a lictor, and mentions his mother.

EDCS-00380032.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Vitalis Sosiorum. Quintus Sosius Victorinus, her husband, and his children Victorina and Victorinus, made (this) for their most dear mother, who deserved well.
EDCS-00380032.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a husband and children for their mother, Vitalis Sosiorum.

EDCS-00380033.translation: A place consecrated to Regina, wife of Isidorus, with sacred rites restored. Fuficia Onomaste made (this). Gaius Iulius Eumolpus.
EDCS-00380033.summary: This inscription marks a place consecrated to Regina, wife of Isidorus, with rites restored, and names Fuficia Onomaste and Gaius Iulius Eumolpus as dedicators.

EDCS-00380034.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Avilius Victor. Aemilia Primigenia (made this) for her excellent husband.
EDCS-00380034.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aemilia Primigenia for her husband, Marcus Avilius Victor.

EDCS-00380035.translation: Lucius Subocrinus Eros. Lucius, son of Lucius, freedman of Mio. In the field, 20 feet.
EDCS-00380035.summary: This inscription likely marks a property boundary, indicating Lucius Subocrinus Eros and Lucius, freedman of Mio, owned or controlled a plot of land measuring 20 feet.

EDCS-00380036.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Eutychia Fortunata, daughter. Her mother Serena made (this) for her well-deserving daughter. She lived 10 years, 7 months. Her mother lived 45 years.
EDCS-00380036.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Eutychia Fortunata, detailing her age at death and the age of her mother, Serena, who erected the monument.

EDCS-00380038.translation: To the departed spirits. Hilius erected this for his son Hermetis, a slave. He lived 1 year and 7 months.
EDCS-00380038.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Hilius for his son, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380043.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the mother Refria Heorte Albana.

EDCS-00380044.translation: To Gaius Refrius Orestes, father, of Albana.
EDCS-00380044.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the father Gaius Refrius Orestes.

EDCS-00380048.translation: To Livia Prisca, daughter, aged 3? years. To Gaius Fonteius Severus, son of Gaius of the Palatina tribe, son-in-law, born [to live] 20 years.
EDCS-00380048.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of Livia Prisca and her son-in-law Gaius Fonteius Severus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380053.translation: Of Gaius Julius Euenus Charito, freedman. In front 24 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-00380053.summary: This inscription likely marks the dimensions of a tomb plot belonging to Gaius Julius Euenus Charito, a freedman, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380054.translation: Of Marcus Veturius Niger, freedman of Marcus. In front 12 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-00380054.summary: This inscription likely marks the dimensions of a tomb plot belonging to Marcus Veturius Niger, a freedman, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380055.translation: Of Marcus Veturius Niger, freedman of Marcus. In front 12 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-00380055.summary: This inscription likely marks the dimensions of a tomb plot belonging to Marcus Veturius Niger, a freedman, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380056.translation: Lucius Postumius, son of Quintus, of the Lemonia tribe. In front 36 feet, in the field 25 feet, and a kitchen in front 16 feet, in the field 5 feet.
EDCS-00380056.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a tomb plot, including a separate kitchen area, belonging to Lucius Postumius, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380062.translation: To Decimus Lucretius Valens, son of Decimus, of the Menenia tribe.
EDCS-00380062.summary: This is an inscription honouring Decimus Lucretius Valens, likely a dedication on a public building or monument.

EDCS-00380066.translation: Gaius Vividius Verus lived 20 years and 50 days.
EDCS-00380066.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Vividius Verus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-00380068.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Trebonia Quinta and Quintus Stonichius Maximus made this monument from the ground up with their own money for themselves and for their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-00380068.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Trebonia Quinta and Quintus Stonichius Maximus for themselves and their freedmen and freedwomen.

EDCS-00380070.summary: This is a dedication or a funerary epitaph for Lucius Titius Auctus.

EDCS-00380071.translation: Lucius Tullius Festinus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-00380071.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a tombstone or a label.

EDCS-00380073.summary: This epitaph records the name and age at death of Publicia Primilla.

EDCS-00380077.translation: To Gaius Nerōnius Rufus, son of Gaius, from the Falernian tribe; to [---], son of Gaius, from the Falernian tribe; to Abuttia [---]; to Publius Nerōnius, son of Gaius, from the Falernian tribe; to Cassia Prima, daughter of Quintus, from the Falernian tribe; and for himself and his family.
EDCS-00380077.summary: This is a funerary inscription for multiple family members of the Nerōnius family, including Gaius Nerōnius Rufus and others.

EDCS-00380078.translation: Lucius Appuleius Proculus was asked whether he had the 300 poles which had been deposited with Nonius Primigenius. Lucius Cominius Primus questioned Lucius Appuleius Proculus whether he had the 300 poles from him, by the decision of the arbitrator Tiberius Crassus Firmus in the dispute which was about the boundaries of the estate of Lucius Cominius Primus, Numidian estate, and the estate of Lucius Appuleius Proculus, which they had deposited with Marcus Nonius Primigenius. There Lucius Appuleius Proculus responded: I received my 300 poles, which had been cut by you, which had been deposited with Marcus Nonius Primigenius, by the decision of the arbitrator Tiberius Crassus Firmus. Done at Herculaneum, the seventh day before the Kalends of February, under the emperor Galba Otho Caesar. Witnesses: Marcus Caecilius Potitus, Tiberius Crassus Firmus, Lucius Mammius Marianus, Numerius Blaesus Saturninus, [---] Mari Celsinus, Marcus Livius Epagathus, Caius Lūsius Secundianus, Lucius Venidius Ennychus, Marcus Nonius Primigenius, Marcus Nonius Cyparus.
EDCS-00380078.summary: This inscription records a legal proceeding and arbitration concerning the boundary dispute between two estates and the return of 300 poles deposited as evidence, dated to the reign of Emperor Galba Otho Caesar.

EDCS-00380079.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufilla, dedicated by a sibling.

EDCS-00380082.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Julius Proculus, centurion of the 14th Legion Gemina, by the foreigners. Gaius Julius Valerianus, for his best brother.
EDCS-00380082.summary: This funerary monument is dedicated to Gaius Julius Proculus, a centurion, by 'foreigners' and his brother Gaius Julius Valerianus.

EDCS-00380084.summary: This inscription records a dedication to 'Populus', likely the Roman people or a local populace.

EDCS-00400118.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Lucius Calpurnius Rufus, scribe of the praetorian fleet at Misenum. He died here in Ephesus while on duty and is buried in a marble sarcophagus. Marcus Sittius Africanus, a fellow townsman and heir, made this memorial near the camp. Fleet [---]
EDCS-00400118.summary: This funerary monument in Ephesus commemorates Lucius Calpurnius Rufus, a scribe of the Misenum fleet, who died in service, and was erected by his heir Marcus Sittius Africanus.

EDCS-00600142.translation: Syntyche, freedwoman of Egrilia Apella, made this for herself and for her fellow freedman Aulus Egrilius Narcissus.
EDCS-00600142.summary: This is a funerary dedication on a tombstone made by a freedwoman for herself and a fellow freedman.

EDCS-00600410.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pinarius Proclus made this for his dearest son, Eutychetes.
EDCS-00600410.summary: This is a funerary dedication on a tombstone made by Pinarius Proclus for his son Eutychetes.

EDCS-00600856.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Theopropus made this for his excellent brother, Felix.
EDCS-00600856.summary: This is a funerary dedication on a tombstone made by Theopropus for his brother Felix.

EDCS-02900019.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aulus Aenius Philargyrus made this for his deserving wife, Aenia Secunda.
EDCS-02900019.summary: This is a funerary dedication on a tombstone made by Aulus Aenius Philargyrus for his wife Aenia Secunda.

EDCS-02900020.translation: To the spirits [of the underworld]. Coelia [---]. Marcus Dete[---] made this for himself and [---]
EDCS-02900020.summary: This is a funerary dedication on a tombstone with incomplete text, likely made for the deceased and the dedicator.

EDCS-02900022.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Germanus, deserving and sweetest son, who lived 19 years, 6 months. [---]
EDCS-02900022.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of death for a young man named Germanus.

EDCS-02900023.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely a dedication to a husband, with incomplete text.

EDCS-02900031.translation: Of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Pertinax Augustus, our lord.
EDCS-02900031.summary: This is an inscription fragment referring to the reign of Emperor Septimius Pertinax, likely from an official monument.

EDCS-02900032.translation: Of Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, our lord.
EDCS-02900032.summary: This is an inscription fragment referring to the reign of Emperor Septimius Severus, likely from an official monument.

EDCS-02900033.translation: Of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-02900033.summary: This is an inscription fragment, likely a titulature of Emperor Caracalla, from an official monument.

EDCS-02900034.translation: Of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus
EDCS-02900034.summary: This is a titulary inscription likely part of a larger dedication or building project honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius, dating to his reign (161-180 CE).

EDCS-02900035.translation: Of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus
EDCS-02900035.summary: This is a titulary inscription, a partial dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, dating to his reign (161-180 CE).

EDCS-02900036.translation: Of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus
EDCS-02900036.summary: This inscription, possibly indicating a multiple of ten (or a repetition of 'three' and 'two'), is a titulary fragment honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius, dating to his reign (161-180 CE).

EDCS-02900038.translation: Of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus
EDCS-02900038.summary: This inscription, likely indicating a multiple of eight (or 'three' and 'ten'), is a titulary fragment honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius, dating to his reign (161-180 CE).

EDCS-02900040.translation: Of Emperor Marcus Severus Alexander Augustus
EDCS-02900040.summary: This is a titulary inscription, likely part of a dedication honoring Emperor Severus Alexander, dating to his reign (222-235 CE).

EDCS-02900043.translation: Marcus Antonius, son of Marcus, and Gaius Coranus, son of Gaius of Tusculum, as duovirs and aediles, repaired the road and bridge and retaining walls at their own expense.
EDCS-02900043.summary: This inscription records the public works of repairing a road and bridge, funded by two local magistrates, Marcus Antonius and Gaius Coranus, likely in the Italian town of Tusculum during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900044.translation: Quintus Subatius Onesimus fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Tifatinus.
EDCS-02900044.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus Subatius Onesimus dedicating something to Jupiter Tifatinus, likely from the region around Mount Tifata in Campania, during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900045.translation: For the health of Publius Campanius Dexianus, Aprilis, his slave, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Tifatinus.
EDCS-02900045.summary: This is a complex votive inscription, likely duplicated, where the slave Aprilis fulfilled a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Tifatinus for the health of his master Publius Campanius Dexianus, from the region of Mount Tifata.

EDCS-02900046.translation: Gaius Lartius Eutyches, by order, gave this gift to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Tifatinus.
EDCS-02900046.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Lartius Eutyches dedicating a gift to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Tifatinus, likely from the region of Mount Tifata, during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900048.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius [F]adius (?) [restored part of the text] ... [restored part of the text]
EDCS-02900048.summary: This is a funerary inscription that begins with a dedication to the 'Dis Manibus' (departed spirits) and likely names the deceased and the dedicator, though much of it is lost.

EDCS-02900049.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Lucius Pacideius Antullus, freedman of Lucius, who lived 50 years. Pacideia Procula, freedwoman, to a deserving father.
EDCS-02900049.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Pacideius Antullus, set up by his freedwoman.

EDCS-02900050.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Utia Justinia, dearest wife, Gaius Adius Saturninus made [this] with [her] with whom he lived for 3 years, 2 months, and Col[...]. FVPON[...]
EDCS-02900050.summary: A funerary epitaph for Utia Justinia, set up by her husband, Gaius Adius Saturninus.

EDCS-03000001.translation: The place granted to himself in this monument or tomb by Laberius Acyndinus, which is on the right side upon entering and below the staircase of Laurentia, Euthychia for himself.
EDCS-03000001.summary: A funerary inscription specifying the location of a burial plot granted by Laberius Acyndinus to Euthychia.

EDCS-03000002.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Antonius Furus, a colonist, who lived 29 years, 9 months, 27 days. His brothers made [this] for their dearest brother.
EDCS-03000002.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Furus, a colonist, set up by his brothers.

EDCS-03000003.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Quintius Successus restored for himself and his kin, freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants, at his own expense, a columbarium which had been destroyed, to his mother Articuleia and to Quintius Successus, deserving parents.
EDCS-03000003.summary: A funerary inscription recording the restoration of a columbarium by Titus Quintius Successus for his parents and their descendants.

EDCS-03000004.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Cincia Felissima, daughter of Lucius, who lived 3 years, 8 months, [...]
EDCS-03000004.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cincia Felissima, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-03000006.translation: [Sacred] to the departed spirits. [...] Zosimeni and Hermetus, grandsons, and Augustia[nus(?)], to their incomparably deserving mother Augu[stina(?)] and Hermetus, [sons] made [this].
EDCS-03000006.summary: A funerary epitaph for Zosimeni, Hermetus, and Augustina, set up by their sons and grandsons.

EDCS-03000010.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Titus Tettius Lenis, son of Gaius, Viuir Augustalis, for [himself] deserving.
EDCS-03000010.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Tettius Lenis, a Viuir Augustalis.

EDCS-03000012.summary: A fragmentary inscription that likely names Servilia Irene as a recipient of funerary honors or as a co-dedicant.

EDCS-03000013.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Sergius Hellanicus, who lived 7 months, 5 days. Hellanicus of the colonists and Sergia Hygia, parents, made [this].
EDCS-03000013.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sergius Hellanicus, set up by his parents, Hellanicus of the colonists and Sergia Hygia.

EDCS-03000016.translation: To the shades of Vitalio, adopted son of Lucius Annius Callistus and his wife Longenia Optata.
EDCS-03000016.summary: This is a funerary dedication for an adopted son, Vitalio, by his adoptive parents.

EDCS-03000017.translation: Titus Statilius Antiochus, freedman of Titus Statilius Taurus, carpenter.
EDCS-03000017.summary: This inscription identifies an individual, Titus Statilius Antiochus, by his name, patron, and profession as a carpenter.

EDCS-03000018.translation: Gaius Julius Pothus for himself, his son Gaius Julius Celer, and his wife Valeria Paula. Sextus Avilius Eutychus and Valeria Drosis for themselves, their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. To Ocia Procula, daughter of Spurius, from her own peculium, for herself and her mother Ocia Trophima, and their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. In front 25 feet, in the field 25 feet.
EDCS-03000018.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription recording multiple individuals and family lines, including property dimensions, possibly for a tomb or burial plot.

EDCS-03000019.translation: To Aulus Egilius Heliades, Augustalis, also quinquennial. Egilia Storge to her most dutiful father. To the shades of Aulus Egilius Magnus, son of Aulus, of the Palatina tribe, by decree of the decurions, first praetor for performing the sacred rites of Volcanus. He lived 8 years, 5 months, 7 days, 5 hours. Aulus Egilius Heliades and Egilia [---] to their most dutiful son.
EDCS-03000019.summary: This is a bilingual funerary inscription with two parts: one honoring a father (Augustalis and quinquennial official) and another for a son who died young, mentioning a decree by the decurions and his role in religious rites.

EDCS-03000045.translation: To Rubria Salvilla, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-03000045.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to Rubria Salvilla.

EDCS-03000057.translation: To Publius Ligarius Maximus Silvanus, son of Publius, of the Teretina tribe, a patron honored with the public equestrian status, priest of the sacred rites of the Savadians. The council of the Aquinati [---] for his merits.
EDCS-03000057.summary: This is a dedication by the council of Aquinum to Publius Ligarius Maximus Silvanus, a patron and priest, in recognition of his merits.

EDCS-03000059.translation: To Lucius Sergius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe, according to his will.
EDCS-03000059.summary: This inscription records a bequest from a will for Lucius Sergius Rufus.

EDCS-03000064.translation: To the shades of Helpidius, her most holy wife, a woman who deserved well. Lucius, her husband, made this. She lived 26 years, 6 months.
EDCS-03000064.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband, Lucius, to his wife Helpidius, noting her age at death.

EDCS-03000065.translation: To the shades of Iulius Eleutherus, a well-deserving husband. Iulia Vitalis made this. He lived with her 15 years.
EDCS-03000065.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a wife, Iulia Vitalis, to her husband Iulius Eleutherus, mentioning the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-03000085.translation: Our lords, the Emperors Diocletianus Invictus Augustus and Maximianus Invictus Augustus, and the most noble Caesars Constantius and Maximianus. 88.
EDCS-03000085.summary: This inscription records the names and titles of four Roman emperors and caesars, likely related to a building project or official act, possibly with a numerical designation of 88.

EDCS-03000087.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pious, Happy, Invincible Augustus, son of the divine Constantius, Pious.
EDCS-03000087.summary: An inscription honouring the emperor Constantine, likely a dedication on a public monument.

EDCS-03000090.translation: Diogenes, a slave of Pomponius, made this.
EDCS-03000090.summary: A funerary inscription stating that the slave Diogenes, belonging to Pomponius, created the monument.

EDCS-03000093.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Ireneus, freedman of the emperor, and Aelia Filadelfia made this for their son, Publius Aelius Telesphorus, who deserved well and lived for one year.
EDCS-03000093.summary: A funerary monument set up by parents for their young son, indicating the parents' status as imperial freedmen.

EDCS-03000095.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Terentia Ianuaria and Aurelius Epafroditus made this for their most beloved son, who lived 18 years, 6 months, and 15 days, their incomparable son.
EDCS-03000095.summary: A funerary epitaph by parents for their deceased son, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-03000096.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Manius Mummeius Euathlus, friend and heir of Marcus Claudius Trypho, set this up for Marcus Claudius Trypho, Augustalis duplicarius, a dealer in silver vessels, and for his wife, Maria Quarta.
EDCS-03000096.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a friend and heir to a prominent businessman and his wife.

EDCS-03000097.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Cornelius Apoplaustus, a eunuch, lies here.
EDCS-03000097.summary: A funerary inscription stating that Gaius Cornelius Apoplaustus, identified as a eunuch, is buried here.

EDCS-03000098.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Aelia Agathemeris, most holy, most dutiful, most upright, most modest, who lived 24 years, 8 months, and 26 days. Publius Aelius Aristo for his well-deserving, most beloved wife. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03000098.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young woman, set up by her husband, with typical expressions of grief and a wish for peace.

EDCS-03000099.translation: Tiberius Claudius Clemens made this for himself and his family; Claudia Albana for Claudia Antonia, freedwoman of the divine Claudius, daughter of Claudius. She lived for as many years as fate granted her.
EDCS-03000099.summary: A funerary inscription recording a man who built a tomb for himself and his family, including a freedwoman related to Emperor Claudius.

EDCS-03000100.translation: Faenia Fausta, freedwoman of a woman.
EDCS-03000100.summary: A very brief inscription identifying Faenia Fausta as a freedwoman, likely indicating ownership by a female patron.

EDCS-03000101.translation: Flavia Epagathe purchased the bedroom, land, and underground chamber for eternity for herself and for Publius Aelius Libanus, freedman of the emperor, her husband, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-03000101.summary: A funerary inscription recording the purchase of burial space and a tomb by a woman for herself, her husband (an imperial freedman), and their dependents.

EDCS-03000102.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Maria Prima placed this for her mother Mariae Helpidi, a slave girl, who lived 17 years, 8 months, and 4 days, for her own slave girl.
EDCS-03000102.summary: A tombstone set up by Maria Prima for her mother and a slave girl, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000105.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gnaeus Domitius Chrysanthus, freedman of Augusta, a procurator, was placed here inside the monument in a sarcophagus for Domitia and himself. He lived 60-something years, 5 months, and [some] days.
EDCS-03000105.summary: A funerary inscription for Gnaeus Domitius Chrysanthus, a freedman and procurator, and Domitia, possibly his wife or patron, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000106.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcia Tryphaena for her well-deserving slave, Lucius Marcius Peculiaris.
EDCS-03000106.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcia Tryphaena for her slave Lucius Marcius Peculiaris, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000192.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Oratius Severus, a soldier from the praetorian fleet of Misenum, made this monument for his well-deserving slave girl.
EDCS-03000192.summary: A tombstone erected by Quintus Oratius Severus, a soldier in the Misenum fleet, for a slave girl, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000194.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Crontha and Marina, for their most sweet son Petronius, who lived 5 years [and some months].
EDCS-03000194.summary: A funerary inscription for a child named Petronius, son of Crontha and Marina, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000195.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Salvius Plocamus, who lived 70 years. Gaius Salvius Bacchus and Threptus made this for their excellent father.
EDCS-03000195.summary: A tombstone dedicated by Gaius Salvius Bacchus and Threptus to their father, Gaius Salvius Plocamus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000196.summary: An incomplete funerary dedication possibly to a member of the Cornelius family, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000197.summary: An incomplete inscription, likely funerary, with incomplete names and origins, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000198.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the divine [Julius Caesar], by decree of the Senate, 1146 (square) feet, 240 (square) feet.
EDCS-03000198.summary: An inscription related to Emperor Augustus, possibly recording land allocation or construction measurements, dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-03300001.translation: For her daughter, Megiste. For her wife, Verginia Megiste, who lived 20-something years. Megiste, the mother, [dedicated this]. Megiste lived 1 year, 9 months, and 3 days.
EDCS-03300001.summary: A tombstone dedicated by Megiste to her wife Verginia Megiste and their daughter Megiste, indicating a same-sex couple and child, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300002.translation: Quintus Florius, second praetor, having been appointed [pr=ae]tor. Titus T[...], second aedile, under Emperor Severus Alexander and his colleagues as consuls. Marcus Tut[ius?], first praetor, having been appointed [pr=ae]tor. Caius Gen[...], first aedile.
EDCS-03300002.summary: This inscription lists Roman magistrates, likely praetors and aediles, serving during the consulship of Emperor Severus Alexander, possibly related to public works or administrative functions.

EDCS-03300003.translation: Belenius Verus made [this] for Marcus Gavius Maximus, the eminent praetorian prefect.
EDCS-03300003.summary: This is a dedication of a work, possibly a monument or building, made by Belenius Verus for Marcus Gavius Maximus, a high-ranking imperial official, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03300005.translation: May those below perish, I beg you, Icimas. May Ru[fa?] and Papria Unaesi[me] and Lu[p]us die. May Trimigenia and [Res]tuti, Res{i}pecti, Iulia Fort(u)nata, and Epia Scant[illa?], Iulia Pia perish. Iucunda, Mevia, Mucan[a], Iucunda, Maevia, Procla, Iulia, [T]ripe(a), Prote, Minucia M[...], Cuarta and Fadia, Nereis, Po[...], Postidia, Aur(elia) Musario, [...]
EDCS-03300005.summary: This is a series of curses or maledictions against individuals, likely inscribed on a tomb or as a magical text, invoking underworld deities and listing numerous names of women, possibly to ensure their demise or to protect the deceased.

EDCS-03300006.translation: To Mars Ficanus. Agathon, the slave of Caesar, the steward of the rural estates, with his dependents, willingly gave [this] as a vow.
EDCS-03300006.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Mars Ficanus by Agathon, a slave and overseer of rural estates, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300010.translation: Terentia Prim[...} and Aurelia Terentia Q[...], fulfilled their vow to Juno.
EDCS-03300010.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the goddess Juno by two women, Terentia and Aurelia, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300011.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. Publius Cornelius Helianus, son of Publius Cornelius (of the Maecia tribe), lived for 7 years, 2 months, and 2 days. Publius Cornelius Callicrates and Flavia Helias, his parents.
EDCS-03300011.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Publius Cornelius Helianus, detailing his age and identifying his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300014.translation: Anniena lives at her own expense; this monument.
EDCS-03300014.summary: This inscription indicates that Anniena is funding the construction or maintenance of this monument herself, likely a tomb or memorial, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300015.translation: Of Caius Egnatius Philargurus, son of Lucius [?].
EDCS-03300015.summary: This is a fragmented inscription likely identifying the owner or dedicatee of a property or monument, possibly a tomb, belonging to Caius Egnatius Philargurus.

EDCS-03300024.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. For Quintus Calpurnius Genialis, priest of the sacred rites, duovir of the colony of Formiae, quaestor of the supplies, also prefect of the treasury, and presiding magistrate. He lived for 50 years. Calpurnia Irene, to her well-deserving wife.
EDCS-03300024.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Calpurnius Genialis, detailing his various public offices in the colony of Formiae and his age, and dedicated by his wife Calpurnia Irene, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300027.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. For Publius Aelius Hadrianus [...].
EDCS-03300027.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the departed, mentioning Publius Aelius Hadrianus and possibly another individual.

EDCS-03300028.translation: To the departed spirits, for Sextus [---] Linu[---] [---]o.
EDCS-03300028.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring a person named Sextus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300036.translation: [---] Domitius / [---]carpus / [---]on, a member of the VIvirate, gave 2,000 sesterces for the games, to [---]o Fortunatus, one of the IIvirs, and [---] Norbanus.
EDCS-03300036.summary: This is a dedication for games by a member of the VIvirate, recording a donation of 2,000 sesterces to two officials, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300041.translation: To Herennia Rufa, daughter of Gaius. Herennia [---] to her mother.
EDCS-03300041.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Herennia Rufa, set up by her mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300045.translation: To Gaius Julius Maro, son of Gaius, of the Claudian tribe, a veteran of our Augusti, appointed from the scribes of the praetorian fleet at Misenum into the order of decurions, who served as a duumvir and perpetual curator of the guild of the propolae. For the honor of a statue previously decreed to him by them, he provided in his will for 20,000 sesterces to be given. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-03300045.summary: This is a funerary monument honoring Gaius Julius Maro, a veteran and local official, who funded his own statue, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300046.translation: To Marcus Granius Kano, son of Marcus, former praetor and consul, honored by the urban plebs.
EDCS-03300046.summary: This is a public honorific inscription for Marcus Granius Kano, a prominent figure who served as praetor and proconsul, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-03300137.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a funerary epitaph or ownership mark, for Mindius Anicetus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300138.summary: This is a personal inscription, possibly a funerary marker or a record of achievement, indicating Lucius Terentius Lucilianus completed something at the age of 25, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700009.translation: To the departed spirits. For Flavia Nigrina, daughter of Titus. Quintus Canusius Praenestinus, her son.
EDCS-03700009.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Nigrina, set up by her son Quintus Canusius Praenestinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700011.translation: Albinus brought [this] for 15 miles to the people of Casinum.
EDCS-03700011.summary: This inscription records a construction or transport effort by Albinus, likely related to infrastructure or supply for the town of Casinum, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700013.translation: To Lucius Arrunttius Scribonianus, son of Lucius, of the Aniensis tribe(?), publicly, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-03700013.summary: This is a public honorific inscription by decree of the decurions for Lucius Arrunttius Scribonianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700014.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Pontia, mother of Pyrallis.
EDCS-03700014.summary: A funerary dedication to Pontia, mother of Pyrallis.

EDCS-03700015.translation: Publius Satrius, son of Publius, of the Oufentina tribe, soldier of cohort [3]. For Publius Satrius, son of Publius, of the Oufentina tribe, his father; for Octavia [3], his mother; [for] Satrius, son of Publius, of the Oufentina tribe, Basso, his brother; [for] Satrius, son of Publius, of the Oufentina tribe, his brother; [for] Satria, daughter of Publius [3], his sister. By will, 10,000 HS, [under the supervision] of Marcus Valerius Niger, son of Marcus, of the Oufentina tribe, and Lucius Helvius [3] of the Oufentina tribe, from Lucius Helvius Erophilus, son of Quintus Faler[3]onis.
EDCS-03700015.summary: A funerary dedication by a soldier, Publius Satrius, for his father, mother, brothers, and sister, funded by his will, with specific consuls and supervisors mentioned, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-03700017.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Antonius Felici[o]ni, Dossennia Victorina, his incomparable wife, well-deserving, made [this]. He lived 70 years, 2 months.
EDCS-03700017.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife for her husband, Marcus Antonius Felicionius, specifying his lifespan.

EDCS-03700020.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pinaria Valentina, daughter of Marcus, for herself and for her freedmen and freedwomen and their freedmen and their freedwomen and their descendants, made [this].
EDCS-03700020.summary: A funerary dedication by Pinaria Valentina for herself, her freedmen, and their descendants.

EDCS-03700026.translation: Tablets of Lucius Cominius Primus. Received expenses: for Pompeia Anthis, 1,000 HS, with Gaius Vibius Eyrytus as guardian and surety. [Received] 1,000 HS from the chest. He ordered the aforementioned 1,000 HS to be theirs, on his responsibility and risk, with Lucius Cominius Primus questioning. Gaius Vibius Eyrytus, for Pompeia Anthis, in addition to other tablets, 10,000 HS. Done at Herculaneum, the 11th of May, with Gaius Fonteius Capito and Gaius Vipstanus Apronianus as consuls. [Witnesses:] Quintus Junius Theophilus, [Marcus] Noni Cypaeri(?), Gaius Messienus Nymphicus, Gaius Vibius Eyryti, Sextus Atellius Felicis, Marcus [3] Hym[...]
EDCS-03700026.summary: A record of a financial transaction involving tablets, sureties, and loans, dated to 11 May under consuls Gaius Fonteius Capito and Gaius Vipstanus Apronianus, likely in the early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-03700029.summary: An incomplete inscription fragment, likely naming a person.

EDCS-03700030.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Asclepius. Lucius Avidius Eleuther, immunes.
EDCS-03700030.summary: A dedication to the god Apollo (Asclepius) by Lucius Avidius Eleuther, who held a privileged status (immunes).

EDCS-03700031.translation: In the year of Lucius Julius Urso Servianus II and Lucius Licinius Sura II as consuls, on the 3rd of January, at Misenum in the temple of Augustus which belongs to the Augustales, at the request of Tullius Eutyches, perpetual curator, concerning the confirmation of his stated intention of 30,000 HS, the increase of which sum is to be divided annually among the Augustales corporation, and this is to be permanently secured. It was resolved that Tullius Eutyches, with his most generous will, has enriched our common affairs by offering to our corporation 30,000 HS, the income of which sum is to be divided among the members of the corporation annually on the day before the Ides of June, the birthday of the municipality, and it is to be our solemn duty to preserve this gift of his forever. It was therefore resolved that the Augustales should annually on the day before the Ides of June, from the increase of 30,000 HS, make a division among the Augustales corporation, and that the curators of each year should observe this, and that no curator or anyone else should do anything differently or report anything differently or establish or decree anything contrary to this decree. Whoever, as curator or Augustalis, does or allows anything against these provisions shall be condemned to pay 50,000 HS to the common property of the Augustales, or if it is the fault of the Augustales of Misenum that the aforementioned things are not done, then 30,000 HS shall go to the public treasury of the municipality of Misenum. And in order that this may be observed, a bronze tablet inscribed with this decree should be placed in our temple, so that future generations may also eternally know and enjoy this gift of his.
EDCS-03700031.summary: A decree from Misenum, dated to 3 January under consuls Lucius Julius Urso Servianus II and Lucius Licinius Sura II, detailing a perpetual endowment of 30,000 HS by Tullius Eutyches for the Augustales corporation, with specific provisions for its annual distribution and penalties for violations.

EDCS-03700032.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Apollo. Marcus Calpurnius Narcissus, curator.
EDCS-03700032.summary: A dedication to Apollo, sacred to Augustus, by Marcus Calpurnius Narcissus, who served as curator.

EDCS-03700033.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Liber Pater. Lucius Laecanius Primitivus, curator. Under the curation of Lucius Licinius Primitivus. Dedicated the 27th of December, with the Emperors Antoninus III and Verus II as consuls.
EDCS-03700033.summary: A dedication to Liber Pater, sacred to Augustus, by Lucius Laecanius Primitivus, under the curatorship of Lucius Licinius Primitivus, dedicated on 27 December during the consulship of Antoninus III and Verus II (likely 145 CE).

EDCS-03700034.translation: To Emperor Caesar Augustus Nerva, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the fatherland. Gnaeus Volusius Atimetus, an Augustalis, in the name of the Augustales.
EDCS-03700034.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Nerva by Gnaeus Volusius Atimetus, acting on behalf of the Augustales.

EDCS-03700035.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-03700035.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus, conqueror of Germans, conqueror of Dacians, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 16th time, imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 6th time, father of the fatherland. Lucius Kaninius Hermes, Lucius Kaninius Philippus, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, son, having been elected to the order of decurions; Lucius Kaninius Hermes the younger. For the Augustales, with their own money. Under the consulship of Lucius Publilius Celsus II and Gaius Clodius Crispinus, on the 10th day before the Ides of November, at Misenum, in the temple of Augustus, which belongs to the Augustales, under the supervision of Lucius Tullius Eutyches, perpetual curator, when all the Augustales had assembled and Lucius Kaninius Hermes the elder, with his own most generous will, had satisfied public piety by setting up a statue of the Best and Greatest Prince, Emperor Nerva Traianus Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, public parent, with his own name and that of his son Kaninius Philippus and of Kaninius Hermes the younger inscribed, and had added to his benefits by giving in the name of Kaninius Hermes the younger, on account of the honor of his immunity, 6,000 sesterces to our treasury, and for the dedication of the statue, a banquet and 12 sesterces to each person, and since it is our solemn custom to respond to the merits of good citizens, it was agreed, with the consent of all the Augustales, that Kaninius Hermes the elder and Kaninius Hermes the younger be reckoned among the immune among the immune, and that the decree be inscribed on the base of the statue and also that a shield be publicly set up for these Kaninii in our temple.
EDCS-03700035.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to Emperor Trajan by Lucius Kaninius Hermes and his family, along with a substantial donation and a banquet for the Augustales, detailing the honors bestowed upon them for their piety and generosity.

EDCS-03700037.translation: To Emperor Caesar Augustus Nerva, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the fatherland. Publius Herennius Callistus, an Augustalis, in the name of the Augustales, with his own money. Under the consulship of Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus for the 3rd time and Lucius Verginius Rufus for the 3rd time, on the 19th day before the Kalends of October. On the occasion of whose dedication he gave a banquet to the Augustales and 12 sesterces to each person.
EDCS-03700037.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Nerva by Publius Herennius Callistus, an Augustalis, who provided a banquet and a donation to the Augustales.

EDCS-03700038.translation: Sacred to the divine Augustus. Venus. Sextus Sextilius Demetrius, curator.
EDCS-03700038.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Augustus and Venus, made by Sextus Sextilius Demetrius, who held the position of curator.

EDCS-03700040.translation: Cassia Victoria, daughter of Gaius, priestess of the Augustales, with columns and epistyles, in her own name and that of her husband Lucius Laecanius Primitivus, on account of their exceptional goodwill towards her, on the occasion of whose dedication she gave a banquet and 12 nummi to each person.
EDCS-03700040.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Cassia Victoria, a priestess of the Augustales, honoring herself and her husband, and including a banquet for the Augustales.

EDCS-03700041.translation: Quintus Baebius Natalis, an Augustalis, an immune, paved the dining room of Constantia with his own money and dedicated it.
EDCS-03700041.summary: This inscription records the paving and dedication of a dining room by Quintus Baebius Natalis, an Augustalis and immune.

EDCS-03700042.translation: To Gaius Julius Phoebus, perpetual curator, by the Augustales, from money collected. Under the consulship of Aulus Cornelius Palma and Quintus Sosius Senecio, on the Kalends of January. On the occasion of whose dedication he gave a banquet to the Augustales and 12 nummi to each person.
EDCS-03700042.summary: This inscription records a collective contribution by the Augustales to honor their perpetual curator, Gaius Julius Phoebus, including a banquet and a monetary gift.

EDCS-03700046.translation: To Lucius Saevius Panthes, son of Lucius, of the Pupinia tribe, my father.
EDCS-03700046.summary: This is a dedication to a father, Lucius Saevius Panthes, likely by his son.

EDCS-03700047.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valeria Januaria, his well-deserving wife. Marcus Marius Adiutor made this.
EDCS-03700047.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Januaria, dedicated by her husband Marcus Marius Adiutor.

EDCS-03700050.translation: from the will of ... daughter of Quintus, his wife
EDCS-03700050.summary: This fragment from a will mentions a wife, daughter of Quintus, likely indicating a bequest.

EDCS-04800007.text_edition: \n Publius Fulcinius Vergilius Marcellus praefectus fabrum tribunus militum legionis VII geminae felicis praefectus equitum alae Parthorum subcurator aedium sacrarum et operum locorumque publicorum subpraefectus classis praetoriae Misenensis curio populi Romani sacris faciundis Herculi Victori\n
EDCS-04800007.translation: Publius Fulcinius Vergilius Marcellus, prefect of the troops of the VII Gemina Felix legion, military tribune, cavalry prefect of the Alae Parthorum, sub-curator of sacred buildings, public works, and lands, sub-prefect of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, curio of the Roman people performing sacrifices, to Hercules Victor.
EDCS-04800007.summary: This is a dedication to Hercules Victor by Publius Fulcinius Vergilius Marcellus, who lists a remarkable series of military and administrative offices, suggesting a high-ranking individual.

EDCS-04800028.translation: Faustus, son of Marcus, military tribune, gave [this] as a gift.
EDCS-04800028.summary: This is a dedication by Faustus, son of Marcus, who held the rank of military tribune, indicating a dedication of a gift.

EDCS-04800034.text_edition: \n Lucius Laenius Anteros dissignator magister Herculis et Augusti Laenia Prima Lucius Laenius Elegans Lucius Laenius Suavis Lucius Laenius Amianthus Lucius Laenius Artema Lucius Laenius Secundus Loco CXXXVII EAT\n
EDCS-04800034.translation: Lucius Laenius Anteros, dispatcher, master of Hercules and Augustus; Laenia Prima; Lucius Laenius Elegans; Lucius Laenius Suavis; Lucius Laenius Amianthus; Lucius Laenius Artema; Lucius Laenius Secundus. Location 137 EAT.
EDCS-04800034.summary: This inscription lists several individuals associated with the cult of Hercules and Augustus, with the term 'dissignator' suggesting a role in organizing ceremonies or games.

EDCS-04800067.translation: Lived 3 years, 9 months, 12 days. To [his?] very dear son(?).
EDCS-04800067.summary: This is a funerary epitaph that records the lifespan of a child and is likely dedicated by a parent to their beloved son.

EDCS-04800073.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Heliodorus lived 25 years. Statilia Zopyra made [this] for her well-deserving brother.
EDCS-04800073.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Statilia Zopyra for her brother Heliodorus, who lived for 25 years.

EDCS-04800076.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Januarius, father, and Primitiva, nurse, made [this] for Liberta, their foster-child, who lived 1 year, 8 months, 47 days, [and was] well-deserving.
EDCS-04800076.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Liberta, a foster-child, erected by her father Januarius and her nurse Primitiva, detailing her short lifespan.

EDCS-04800084.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [This is for] Lucius Maecius Donatus. His freedmen [made this] for their well-deserving patron.
EDCS-04800084.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by freedmen for their deceased patron, Lucius Maecius Donatus.

EDCS-04800089.translation: Aulus Mevius Valens, son of Publius, lived 7 years.
EDCS-04800089.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the name and age of a child, Aulus Mevius Valens.

EDCS-04800110.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tiburtinus lived approximately 13 years. Sextus Annius Corinthus, father, made [this] for himself and his family.
EDCS-04800110.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Sextus Annius Corinthus for his son Tiburtinus, who died around age 13, and also for himself and his household.

EDCS-04800116.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [This is for] Gaius Volcacius Artemidorus, [their] most devoted parent.
EDCS-04800116.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Gaius Volcacius Artemidorus, described as a most devoted parent.

EDCS-04800199.text_edition: \n Albulae Isidi Deanam sic Costorius Italus donum dedit\n
EDCS-04800199.translation: Costorius Italus gave this gift to the goddess Isis of Albulae.
EDCS-04800199.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Isis by Costorius Italus.

EDCS-04900003.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Dasumius Euhelpides made this for his most worthy wife Urtilia Benedicta, with whom he lived without fault for 11 years.
EDCS-04900003.summary: A tombstone for Urtilia Benedicta, erected by her husband Quintus Dasumius Euhelpides, recording their 11 years of marriage.

EDCS-04900004.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Pacatus and Lucius Sertorius Pollio, his heirs, made this for their well-deserving friend Lucius Licinius Probus, a veteran.
EDCS-04900004.summary: A tombstone for the veteran Lucius Licinius Probus, erected by his heirs Publius Aelius Pacatus and Lucius Sertorius Pollio.

EDCS-04900007.translation: To the departed spirits. Fontaea Glyca made this for her well-deserving husband Gaius Fontaeus Hedistus.
EDCS-04900007.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Fontaeus Hedistus, erected by his wife Fontaea Glyca.

EDCS-04900008.translation: To the departed spirits. For her well-deserving husband Fabio Mithra.
EDCS-04900008.summary: A tombstone for Fabio Mithra, erected by his wife.

EDCS-04900010.translation: To the departed spirits. Fuficulenia Asclepias made this for her well-deserving ward Fuficulenia Felicissima, who lived 25 years. // To the departed spirits. Publius Durdenius Achaicus made this for his most sweet son Publius Durdenius Euodianus, who lived 5 years and 8 months.
EDCS-04900010.summary: Two tombstone inscriptions; one for Fuficulenia Felicissima, erected by her patrona, and one for Publius Durdenius Euodianus, erected by his father.

EDCS-04900012.translation: To the departed spirits. For his most sweet mother Caecilia Tertia. Caecilia Irene, his daughter, made this for her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-04900012.summary: A tombstone for Caecilia Tertia, erected by her daughter Caecilia Irene.

EDCS-04900015.translation: To the departed spirits. For his ward Liberalis. Marcus Titius Fructus.
EDCS-04900015.summary: A tombstone for Liberalis, an adopted son, erected by Marcus Titius Fructus.

EDCS-04900018.translation: Marcus Gallius Fabullus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, from Tuccitanus in Baetica, aged 19, was granted burial and a burial place at public expense by decree of the decurions of Casinum.
EDCS-04900018.summary: A funerary monument recording the public grant of burial and plot for Marcus Gallius Fabullus, a 19-year-old from Baetica.

EDCS-04900023.translation: Successus, a client, asks for Lucius Ceius Secundus, who was a IIvir (duumvir). Papilio.
EDCS-04900023.summary: An inscription indicating a client's request for a former duumvir, Lucius Ceius Secundus.

EDCS-04900024.translation: The people request Gnaeus Helvius Sabinus, a worthy aedile, with their own people.
EDCS-04900024.summary: A public request for Gnaeus Helvius Sabinus to be appointed aedile.

EDCS-04900025.summary: A tombstone naming Optata, a freedwoman.

EDCS-04900026.translation: Eumachia, daughter of Lucius, a statuette.
EDCS-04900026.summary: An inscription identifying Eumachia, daughter of Lucius, and referencing a statuette, likely a dedication or ownership marker.

EDCS-04900032.summary: An ownership inscription for Marcus Octavius Felicionis.

EDCS-05000001.summary: A dedication to Auctas for Arto, possibly referring to a type of pigment or substance.

EDCS-05000002.translation: Optata, daughter of Cacus, a young bull.
EDCS-05000002.summary: An inscription naming Optata, daughter of Cacus, and mentioning a young bull, possibly a dedication or part of a funerary context.

EDCS-05000003.translation: Of Caesia, 6 pounds 3 unciae 15 scripula, 16 1/2 scripula.
EDCS-05000003.summary: An inscription detailing the weight of a substance belonging to Caesia, likely related to trade or accounting.

EDCS-05000005.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cassander, slave of Egrilius Plarianus, made this for Gemella, his well-deserving fellow servant.
EDCS-05000005.summary: A tombstone set up by Cassander, a slave, for his fellow servant Gemella.

EDCS-05000016.translation: Tigris, or Generosa, who lived 19 years and 18 days, sleeps here in peace.
EDCS-05000016.summary: A tombstone for a woman named Tigris (or Generosa), detailing her lifespan.

EDCS-05000020.translation: Aurelia Polucratia, who lived 35 years, 7 months, 10 days, and 3 hours, and Cipia Aeliana, who lived 11 years, 5 months, and 3 days. Carminius Plotinianus made this for his well-deserving wife, who rests here.
EDCS-05000020.summary: A tombstone set up by Carminius Plotinianus for his wife Aurelia Polucratia and daughter Cipia Aeliana, including their lifespans.

EDCS-05000024.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Agape set up for her son Mandraslos, dearest, who lived 9 years, 3 months, 20 days, 8 hours. He sleeps here.
EDCS-05000024.summary: A funerary dedication set up by a mother for her young son, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-05000025.translation: For Aurelius Lisistratus, in peace with his own people, Hepagatus.
EDCS-05000025.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Lisistratus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000030.translation: Celerinus is placed here in peace.
EDCS-05000030.summary: A simple funerary inscription stating that Celerinus has been buried in peace, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000035.translation: Silo sleeps here in peace, who lived approximately 20 years. Maximilla, his wife, set this up for herself and her deserving husband.
EDCS-05000035.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife for herself and her deceased husband, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000042.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Donata made this for her most devoted father, Aristonius (?).
EDCS-05000042.summary: A funerary dedication by a daughter for her father, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000043.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Aelius Polydeuces made this for his most cherished, deserving wife, Vitellia Gavinia.
EDCS-05000043.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his deceased wife, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000044.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Rutilia Chrysopolis made this for her deserving husband, Alypos, son of Ionicus, who lived approximately 35 years.
EDCS-05000044.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife for her deceased husband, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000045.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Aelius Longinus made this for himself, and for his most devoted son Iulius Longinus, who lived 18 years, and for his deserving wife Iulia Sittia, and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-05000045.summary: A complex funerary dedication for a family including a son, wife, and freedpersons, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000046.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Silanus Protus made this for his most devoted and deserving Aphrodite, who lived 17 years and 5 months.
EDCS-05000046.summary: A funerary dedication by a spouse for his deceased wife, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000047.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For [---] Mario, [---] Ascanio, [---]
EDCS-05000047.summary: A fragmented funerary dedication to multiple individuals, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000055.translation: Tiberius Claudius Diophantus, son of Claudia Tertia, freedwoman.
EDCS-05000055.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Diophantus, identified by his patron Claudia Tertia, likely a freedwoman.

EDCS-05000062.translation: To Minerva from the household of Considia. Naevineia, daughter of Lucius, from her own funds.
EDCS-05000062.summary: This is a dedication to Minerva by Naevineia, daughter of Lucius, who funded it from her personal resources.

EDCS-05000064.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Ulpius Primus, perhaps, made this for his incomparably deserving wife, Septimia Veneria.
EDCS-05000064.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Ulpius Primus for his wife, Septimia Veneria, likely in the imperial period.

EDCS-05000076.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Sempronia Eutychia made this for her deserving freedwoman, Sempronia Successa, who lived 18 years and 6 days.
EDCS-05000076.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sempronia Successa, a freedwoman, erected by her patron Sempronia Eutychia, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000079.translation: Sacred to Jupiter, from the grove. Aurelius Cosmus(?), [---] vowed [---].
EDCS-05000079.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, possibly by Aurelius Cosmus, found in a sacred grove.

EDCS-05000092.translation: Of Publius Marius L[---]. To Quinaria V. Of Tiberius Julius Primus. Of Tiberius Julius PR[---].
EDCS-05000092.summary: This inscription lists names and possibly ownership or familial relationships, likely related to property or a tomb, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000118.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Seius Severus, his well-deserving husband, Paconia Helpis.
EDCS-05000118.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Paconia Helpis to her husband, Seius Severus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000119.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Her dearest mother made this for Nanonia Grata.
EDCS-05000119.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nanonia Grata, erected by her mother, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000121.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Marcus Nonius Reparatus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000122.translation: Sabidius Secundinus set this up at the god's command, to Jupiter Dolichenus.
EDCS-05000122.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Sabidius Secundinus, who claims to have acted on the god's instruction, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000170.translation: Titus Rausius Agapomenus, freedman of Titus, 25 feet in front. Nomius, freedman, erected this.
EDCS-05000170.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Nomius, a freedman, for Titus Rausius Agapomenus, also a freedman, likely marking a tomb or burial plot with a stated frontage.

EDCS-05000175.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Vettia Demetrias, my dearest wife. She lived 45 years. I cannot forget her memory. Staphilus, servant of Caesar, made this for one well-deserving.
EDCS-05000175.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated by Staphilus, a servant of Caesar, to his "dearest wife" Vettia Demetrias, lamenting her death after 45 years of life.

EDCS-05000180.translation: The Emperor Caesar, son of the deified [Caesar], by decree of the Senate, 2024 [years since founding of Rome], 240 feet.
EDCS-05000180.summary: This inscription, likely related to a public works project, records a measurement of 240 feet authorized by Emperor Augustus by senatorial decree, dated to 24 (CMXXIV) years after Rome's founding (equivalent to 48/49 CE).

EDCS-05000185.translation: Marcia. The Emperor Caesar, son of the deified [Caesar], by decree of the Senate, 2019 [years since founding of Rome], 240 feet.
EDCS-05000185.summary: This inscription, similar to EDCS-05000180, likely marks a public work measurement of 240 feet authorized by Emperor Augustus by senatorial decree, dated to 19 (CMXCI) years after Rome's founding (equivalent to 43/44 CE).

EDCS-05200003.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Minuciae Quinti filiae Maximillae filiae et Quinto Minucio Hermaisco filio et Aucto viro piissimo et Quinto Minucio Hermeti liberto bene merenti posuit pecunia sua Aburiae Sexti Aburi Primigeni libertae Ideni piissimae posuit libertis libertabus posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-05200003.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Minucia Maximilla, daughter of Quintus Minucius, and for Quintus Minucius Hermaiscus, her son, and for Auctus, my most devoted husband, and for Quintus Minucius Hermetes, his deserving freedman, placed [this] with her own money. For Aburia Primigenia, freedwoman of Sextus Aburius Idenus, most devoted, and for her freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-05200003.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription from Aburia Primigenia, dedicating burial plots or monuments to her husband Auctus, his freedman Hermetes, her son Hermaiscus, and Minucia Maximilla, with provisions for their descendants.

EDCS-05200004.text_edition: \n Caius Norbanus Sorex secundarum parasitus Dianae Nemorensi dono dedit\n
EDCS-05200004.translation: Gaius Norbanus Sorex, a second performer, gave this gift to Diana of Nemi.
EDCS-05200004.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Norbanus Sorex, identified as a 'second performer' (likely an actor or entertainer), to Diana Nemorensis.

EDCS-05200011.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate X imperator V consul V pater patriae restituendum curavit XLIII\n
EDCS-05200011.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 10th time, Imperator for the 5th time, Consul for the 5th time, Father of the Fatherland, had [this] restored, 43.
EDCS-05200011.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Trajan, listing his titles and powers (including 10th tribunician power, 5th consulship, 5th acclamation as Imperator), and states that he oversaw a restoration project, possibly a building or infrastructure, indicated by the number 43.

EDCS-05200013.text_edition: \n In feci Decimus Aemilius Decimus Aemilius Ae[milius ------\n
EDCS-05200013.translation: I made this. Decimus Aemilius [---] Decimus Aemilius Ae[milius ------
EDCS-05200013.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that seems to record the completion of a work by Decimus Aemilius, with the full text and subject matter lost.

EDCS-05200016.text_edition: \n Quod ? verba fecit de Verrio Eubulide de ea re ita censuerunt hodie cum semper imitatus pietatem praeteritam ad Capitolini Iovis cultum se sibi aemulum fecerit cumque tabellis suis vel aliena liberalitate contenderit placere huic ordini commemorari gratissimam populi voluntatem darique Verrio Eubulidi biselli honorem cui commendationem iudiciorum nostrum parem facet sic censuerunt adfuerunt\n
EDCS-05200016.translation: What Verrius Eubulides said concerning the matter. They voted thus today: Since he has always imitated past piety and made himself a rival in the worship of Jupiter Capitolinus, and has striven with his tablets or the liberality of others to please this order, let the most grateful will of the people be declared, and let the honor of Verrius Eubulides be granted a double seat, and let our judgment make his commendation equal. Thus they voted. Present:
EDCS-05200016.summary: This inscription records a senatorial or council decision to honor Verrius Eubulides for his piety and efforts to please the ruling body, granting him a special seating honor.

EDCS-05200018.text_edition: \n Dexiae Rufae ex testamento arbitratu Cai Canini Fortunati\n
EDCS-05200018.translation: To Dexia Rufa, according to her will, by the arbitration of Gaius Caninius Fortunatus.
EDCS-05200018.summary: This inscription records a bequest or legacy to Dexia Rufa, administered according to her will by Gaius Caninius Fortunatus.

EDCS-05200023.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marciae Antoniae Marcus Antonius Doles miles classis praetoriae Misenensis coniugi karissimae bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-05200023.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antonius Doles, a soldier of the Praetorian fleet of Misenum, made this for his dearest and well-deserving wife Marcia Antonia.
EDCS-05200023.summary: A soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum dedicated this tombstone to his wife.

EDCS-05200024.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Naevi Bithi manipulari III Iunone natione Besso militavit annis XXVI vixit nnis XLVI heres bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-05200024.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Naevius Bithus, a manipularius (rank-and-file soldier) of the Third Legion, of the Bessian nation, who served for 26 years and lived for 46 years. His heir made this for the well-deserving man.
EDCS-05200024.summary: An heir dedicated this tombstone to Naevius Bithus, a soldier who served for 26 years and lived for 46 years.

EDCS-05200026.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Annio Callisto Marcus Annius Callistianus patri merenti\n
EDCS-05200026.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Annius Callistus. Marcus Annius Callistianus made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-05200026.summary: Marcus Annius Callistianus dedicated this tombstone to his father, Marcus Annius Callistus.

EDCS-05200027.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Castricio Caulio Zosimo patri optimo Andragathianus filius\n
EDCS-05200027.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Quintus Castricius Caius Zosimus, his most excellent father. Andragathianus, his son, made this.
EDCS-05200027.summary: Andragathianus dedicated this tombstone to his excellent father, Quintus Castricius Caius Zosimus.

EDCS-05200033.text_edition: \n Marco Nonio Marci filio Balbo praetori proconsuli Marcus Nonius Balbi libertus Volusianus\n
EDCS-05200033.translation: For Marcus Nonius Balbus, son of Marcus, praetor, proconsul. Marcus Nonius Balbus, his freedman, Volusianus, made this.
EDCS-05200033.summary: Volusianus, freedman of Marcus Nonius Balbus (a former praetor and proconsul), dedicated this monument to him.

EDCS-05200034.summary: This is a simple memorial inscription for Lucius Crassus Tertius.

EDCS-05200038.text_edition: \n Caecilia Luci liberta Agathia viva sibei sic fecit et Lucio Caecilio Luci liberto Dioscuridi viro suo\n
EDCS-05200038.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Agathia, freedwoman of Lucius, made this for herself while alive, and for her husband, Lucius Caecilius Dioscurides, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-05200038.summary: Caecilia Agathia, a freedwoman, dedicated this tombstone for herself and her husband, Lucius Caecilius Dioscurides, while alive.

EDCS-05200041.text_edition: \n Lucium Lusium Saturninum praetorem virum bonum oro vos faciatis\n
EDCS-05200041.translation: I beg you to make Lucius Lusius Saturninus, a good man and praetor, [honored].
EDCS-05200041.summary: This is a plea, possibly from a tombstone, to honor Lucius Lusius Saturninus, a praetor.

EDCS-05200042.text_edition: \n Novia Cai liberta Amoena sibi et suis et Lucio Iacellio Virillioni\n
EDCS-05200042.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Novia Amoena, freedwoman of Caius, for herself and her family, and for Lucius Iacellius Virillio.
EDCS-05200042.summary: Novia Amoena, a freedwoman, dedicated this memorial for herself, her family, and Lucius Iacellius Virillio.

EDCS-05200043.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Cornelia Quieta.

EDCS-05200044.translation: Caius Novius Lupercus, freedman of a woman.
EDCS-05200044.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Novius Lupercus, a freedman.

EDCS-05200045.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Turranius Primus vixit annos XXXX\n
EDCS-05200045.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Cnaeus Turranius Primus.

EDCS-05200046.text_edition: \n Caio Veranio Quinti filio Rufo IIviro Verania Quinti liberta Clara optimo patrono sibi et suis\n
EDCS-05200046.translation: To Caius Veranius Rufus, son of Quintus, twice-a-year magistrate, Verania Clara, freedwoman of Quintus, for an excellent patron, for herself and her family.
EDCS-05200046.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Verania Clara to her patron Caius Veranius Rufus, an official.

EDCS-05200047.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Verania Clara, a freedwoman.

EDCS-05200048.text_edition: \n Caius Veranius Quinti filius Rufus aedilis\n
EDCS-05200048.translation: Caius Veranius Rufus, son of Quintus, aedile.
EDCS-05200048.summary: This inscription records Caius Veranius Rufus, son of Quintus, who held the office of aedile.

EDCS-05200050.translation: Marcus Blaesius Malchio, freedman of a woman.
EDCS-05200050.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Blaesius Malchio, a freedman.

EDCS-05200052.translation: Marcus Lollius Lucrio, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-05200052.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Lollius Lucrio, a freedman.

EDCS-05200055.text_edition: \n Lucius Nonius Celer vixit annum et menses quattuor\n
EDCS-05200055.translation: Lucius Nonius Celer lived one year and four months.
EDCS-05200055.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the brief lifespan of Lucius Nonius Celer.

EDCS-05200056.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Statius Moschus.

EDCS-05200057.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Hygia.

EDCS-05200058.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Mercurialis, a child who died at age 11.

EDCS-05200059.summary: The name of Calventia Primilla, likely a dedication or a funerary marker.

EDCS-05200060.translation: Calventia Cloe to her father Numisius Auctus.
EDCS-05200060.summary: A dedication from Calventia Cloe to her father, Numisius Auctus.

EDCS-05200061.text_edition: \n Lucius Calventius Amplius vixit annos VI\n
EDCS-05200061.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Calventius Amplius, a child who died at age 6.

EDCS-05200062.text_edition: \n Lucius Calventius Cytherus vixit annis XX\n
EDCS-05200062.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Calventius Cytherus, who died at age 20.

EDCS-05200063.summary: A dedication to Lucius Calventius Stapylus.

EDCS-05200065.summary: The name of Gaius Auficius Clemens, likely a dedication or a funerary marker.

EDCS-05200066.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Valentinus, a child who died at age 5.

EDCS-05200067.text_edition: \n Publio Cluvio Attico magistro pagi Suburbani\n
EDCS-05200067.translation: To Publius Cluvius Atticus, magistrate of the Suburani pagus.
EDCS-05200067.summary: A dedication to Publius Cluvius Atticus, who served as a local magistrate (magister pagi).

EDCS-05200068.summary: The name of Calidia Propolis, likely a dedication or a funerary marker.

EDCS-05200069.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calidia Aucta.

EDCS-05200070.text_edition: \n Marcus Decidius Marci filius Macer vixit annos VIII\n
EDCS-05200070.translation: Marcus Decidius Macer, son of Marcus, lived 8 years.
EDCS-05200070.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the child Marcus Decidius Macer, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-05200071.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Callidia Soteris.

EDCS-05200072.text_edition: \n Tito Vibio Titi liberto Philodespoto et Popidiae |(mulieris) libertae Augeni et Tito Vibio Primo liberto\n
EDCS-05200072.translation: To Titus Vibius Philodespotos, freedman of Titus, and to Popidia Augeni, her freedwoman, and to Titus Vibius Primus, freedman.
EDCS-05200072.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Titus Vibius Philodespotos and Popidia Augeni, and Titus Vibius Primus, likely freedmen, possibly on a funerary monument.

EDCS-05200101.text_edition: \n Caius Ocratius Cai filius Hanno libertus curarunt\n
EDCS-05200101.translation: Gaius Ocratius Hanno, freedman, son of Gaius, took care of this.
EDCS-05200101.summary: This inscription indicates that Gaius Ocratius Hanno, a freedman, financed or commissioned some public work or monument.

EDCS-05200103.text_edition: \n Graniae Luci filiae Rufae Marco Granio Marci filio Gallo Marco Granio Marci filio Teretina Caprae Marco Granio Marci filio Teretina Capellae\n
EDCS-05200103.translation: To Grania Rufa, daughter of Lucius; to Marcus Granius Gallus, son of Marcus; to Marcus Granius, son of Marcus; to Capra, daughter of Marcus Granius; to Capella, daughter of Marcus Granius.
EDCS-05200103.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating multiple members of the Grania family, likely part of a larger monument.

EDCS-05200105.text_edition: \n Quintus Fufius Quinti filius Teretina Rufus aedilis quaestor IIvir sibi et Quinto Fufio Marci filio patri Gaviae Marci filiae Rufae matri Fufiae Quinti filiae Pollae sorori\n
EDCS-05200105.translation: Quintus Fufius Rufus, son of Quintus, aedile, quaestor, duovir, for himself and for his father Quintus Fufius Rufus, son of Marcus; for his mother Gavia, daughter of Marcus; and for his sister Fufia Polla, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-05200105.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Fufius Rufus, who held several magistracies, and his family members, including his parents and sister.

EDCS-05200106.summary: This inscription identifies Sontius Cimber as a military tribune.

EDCS-05200107.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Eutycheti Myrtilus Dama et L O et philo \n
EDCS-05200107.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Eutychetus, Myrtilus, Dama, L. O., and Philo.
EDCS-05200107.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the deceased Eutychetus, Myrtilus, Dama, L. O., and Philo.

EDCS-05200108.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the name and parentage of Servia.

EDCS-05200653.text_edition: \n Fuerit post me et post Gaudentiam Nicenem veto alium sic quisquis hunc titulum legerit mihi et illei sic feci Tito Aelio Euangelo homini patienti merum profundat\n
EDCS-05200653.translation: May it be that after me and after Gaudentia Nicene, I forbid anyone else who reads this inscription to do so; whoever reads it, may they pour pure wine for me and for her, whom I made this way, Titus Aelius Euangelus, a patient man.
EDCS-05200653.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Titus Aelius Euangelus, making a personal curse against those who would desecrate his monument.

EDCS-05700001.translation: Since it is pleasing that your shores have provided a joyful contest, Castor and honored Pollux, I, Catius Sabinus, greatly increased by the fasces of the city and overjoyed to have held games for Neptune the father, have dedicated the very image of the contest itself to your seats as a reward for such a great gift.
EDCS-05700001.summary: This is a dedication by Catius Sabinus, likely a magistrate or official, of a statue or image related to a contest, dedicated to the divine twins Castor and Pollux, patrons of sailors and games.

EDCS-05700006.translation: An altar to the Holy Household Fortune, for the health and return of Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus and Decimus Clodius Septimius Albinus Caesar, Lucius Valerius Frontinus, a centurion of the II cohort of vigiles, placed it at his own expense with his own people. Flavinus Julianus, a centurion of the IIII cohort of vigiles, also dedicated it.
EDCS-05700006.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to Fortuna Domestica Sancta for the safety and return of emperors Severus and Albinus, set up by two centurions of the vigiles.

EDCS-05700008.translation: To the Genius of the Colony of Ostia, Marcus Cornelius Epagathus, curator of the Augustales, gave 10 pounds of silver as a gift on the occasion of its dedication; he also gave 1 sestertius per person. On the Ides of December, when Stloga and Severus were consuls.
EDCS-05700008.summary: This inscription records a gift of silver and individual donations by Marcus Cornelius Epagathus, a curator of the Augustales, to the Genius of Ostia, dated by consuls.

EDCS-05700010.translation: To the Genius of the Guild of Skinners of Ostia, to whom it is permitted to meet by decree of the Senate. Marcus Aurelius Lamprocles, freedman of the emperor and patron, gave and dedicated [a gift] at his own expense.
EDCS-05700010.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius of the Ostian Skinners' Guild by its patron, Marcus Aurelius Lamprocles, an imperial freedman.

EDCS-05700013.translation: To Hercules. Cassius Ligus, tribune of the IIII cohort of vigiles, gave [this] as a gift, with Valerius Frontinus (centurion of the II cohort of vigiles) and Vario Fuficiano [---] and Leone Aemilio Catullino [---], centurion, overseeing the work.
EDCS-05700013.summary: This inscription records a gift to Hercules by Cassius Ligus, a tribune of the vigiles, with two centurions named as overseers.

EDCS-05700016.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-05700016.translation: For the health of our Emperor Caesar Augustus Pertinax, to Holy Hercules Numini, with a marble base. Gaius Sentius Portesis gave [this] as a gift at his own expense to the tax collectors and the earth tax collectors.
EDCS-05700016.summary: This is a votive offering to Hercules Numini by Gaius Sentius Portesis for the health of Emperor Septimius Severus Pertinax, possibly related to tax collection.

EDCS-05700018.translation: For the health of the Emperor Caesar [---] Pius Felix Augustus. Camurenius Verus, priest of the goddess Isis of the Capitolium(?), and the other initiates of Isis restored [this] from his own resources.
EDCS-05700018.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a monument or temple to the goddess Isis by Camurenius Verus, a priest of the Isiac cult, for the health of the emperor.

EDCS-05700019.translation: Having undertaken a vow, Calventia Severina and Aurelia Severa, granddaughter of Megarus, enlarged [this].
EDCS-05700019.summary: This inscription records the enlargement of a structure or monument by two women, Calventia Severina and Aurelia Severa, undertaken possibly in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-05700021.translation: To Isis Bubastis. Caltilia Diodora, a Bubastis priestess, gave by will a silver Venus weighing 1.5 pounds, a gold crown weighing 3 ounces and 3 scripula, and an analempsiac crown weighing 5 ounces and 8 scripula.
EDCS-05700021.summary: This is a posthumous dedication of precious objects to Isis Bubastis by Caltilia Diodora, a priestess of the cult, as recorded in her will.

EDCS-05700022.translation: To Iuppiter Dolichenus, for the safety of our Emperor Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus Pius Felix Augustus, Lucius Rubrius Maximus, prefect of the cavalry of the Spanish ala, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05700022.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Lucius Rubrius Maximus, a cavalry prefect, to the god Iuppiter Dolichenus for the emperor's safety.

EDCS-05700030.translation: For the safety of the Augusti, Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Pius Felix, Junia Marciana made this sacred offering to Liber Pater Commodianus, fulfilling a vow.
EDCS-05700030.summary: This inscription records a sacred offering made by Junia Marciana to Liber Pater Commodianus for the safety of the emperors, likely in the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05700032.translation: For the safety of the Augustus. Aulus Ostiensis Asclepiades, custodian of the Capitol, gave as a gift a statue of Mars for the body of freedmen and slaves of the public service.
EDCS-05700032.summary: This inscription records the gift of a statue of Mars by Aulus Ostiensis Asclepiades, a temple custodian, for the benefit of public freedmen and slaves.

EDCS-05700033.translation: Titus Annius Lucullus, duovir, also quinquennalis, honored, gave and dedicated a statue of Mars from the dendrophori of Ostia as a gift on the Ides of May, when Torquatus and Herodes were consuls.
EDCS-05700033.summary: This inscription records the gift and dedication of a statue of Mars by Titus Annius Lucullus, a prominent civic official, during the consulship of Torquatus and Herodes (likely 196 CE).
