Heuresis, freedwoman of Gnaeus Domitius Fortunatus, made this for herself and for her daughter Domitia Procula, who lived 10 years, 2 months, 10 days, and for her son Gnaeus Cornelius Fabianus, who lived 18 years, 9 months, and for her husband Gnaeus Cornelius Apollonius.
EDCS-05700925.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a freedwoman for herself, her deceased children, and her husband.

EDCS-05700926.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Domitia Quadratilla. Julia Threpte, her daughter, and Gaius Domitius Successus and Gaius Cassius Liberius, for their brother and sister, set this up.
EDCS-05700926.summary: A funerary epitaph for Domitia Quadratilla, set up by her daughter and two other individuals, possibly her siblings or their spouses.

EDCS-05700928.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Donatia Florentina.
EDCS-05700928.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Donatia Florentina.

EDCS-05700932.translation: To the departed spirits. To Egnatius Severianus, who lived 35 years. Aurelia Paula for her well-deserving husband. Ostraci.
EDCS-05700932.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated to Egnatius Severianus by his wife Aurelia Paula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05700935.translation: To the departed spirits. Of Aulus Eg Rilius Agapetus, who lived 8 years, 4 months, 18 days. Aulus Eg Rilius Chrysodorus and Aelia Anthusa, parents, made this.
EDCS-05700935.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young boy named Aulus Eg Rilius Agapetus, made by his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05700936.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aulus Eg Rilius Alipus. Aulus Eg Rilius Alexander made this for his well-deserving freedman.
EDCS-05700936.summary: This tombstone was erected by Aulus Eg Rilius Alexander for his freedman Aulus Eg Rilius Alipus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05700937.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aulus Eg Rilius Callinicus, who lived 4 years, 1 month, 15 days. Aulus Eg Rilius Onesimus and Aegrilia Aphrodite made this for their most sweet pupil.
EDCS-05700937.summary: This tombstone commemorates Aulus Eg Rilius Callinicus, a young boy, and was made by his teachers Aulus Eg Rilius Onesimus and Aegrilia Aphrodite, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05700938.translation: To the departed spirits. For Eg Rilius Epigonus.
EDCS-05700938.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated to Eg Rilius Epigonus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05700939.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aulus Eg Rilius Epitynchanus, who lived 12 years, 7 months, 25 days. Aulus Eg Rilius Helios and Aulus Eg Rilius Philadespotus and Egrilia Philete made this for their most beloved pupil and son.
EDCS-05700939.summary: This tombstone records the death of Aulus Eg Rilius Epitynchanus, who died at the age of 12, and was made by his fathers and mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05700941.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulianus made this for his son Aulus Eg Rilius Eutyches.
EDCS-05700941.summary: This is a tombstone inscription by Iulianus for his son Aulus Eg Rilius Eutyches, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05700942.translation: For Aulus Eg Rilius Faustus, dearest pupil(?). Aulus Eg Rilius Hilario gave [this] as a gift. He lived 25 years, 6 months.
EDCS-05700942.summary: This tombstone likely records the death of Aulus Eg Rilius Faustus, possibly a student, with a dedication from Aulus Eg Rilius Hilario, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05700943.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gelasinus, dearest, most pious, most holy son, of incomparable affection towards parents, who lived 18 years, 10 months, 19 days. Aulus Eg Rilius Gelasinus, most unhappy father.
EDCS-05700943.summary: This tombstone commemorates Gelasinus, who died at 18, and was dedicated by his father, Aulus Eg Rilius Gelasinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05700946.translation: To the departed spirits. Of Aulus Eg Rilius Hermes, who lived 14 years, 26 days. Aulus Eg Rilius Timotheus and Egrilia Daphne, parents, made this.
EDCS-05700946.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aulus Eg Rilius Hermes, who died at 14, made by his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05700947.translation: To the divine shades. Flavia Successa made this for her well-deserving husband, Aulus Egrilius Ianuarius.
EDCS-05700947.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05700948.translation: To the divine shades. Egrilius Lucrion made this for his well-deserving wife, Iulia Vera.
EDCS-05700948.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his wife, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05700949.translation: To the divine shades. For Aulus Egrilius Marcianus, the sweetest infant, who lived for two years, ten months, and eight days.
EDCS-05700949.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an infant, Aulus Egrilius Marcianus, specifying his short lifespan, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05700950.translation: To the divine shades. Aulus Egrilius Privatio made this for his most devoted son, Aulus Egrilius Masculinus, who lived for nine years, two months, and five days, and for his freedman Lesbius.
EDCS-05700950.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father for his son and a freedman, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05700951.translation: To the divine shades. Aulus Egrilius Niceforus made this for his sweetest son, Egrilius Marcianus, who lived for six months and thirteen days.
EDCS-05700951.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father for his infant son, Egrilius Marcianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05700952.translation: To the divine shades. For Aulus Egrilius Ostiensis, who lived for four years, four months, and twenty-six days, for ten hours. Aulus Egrilius Carpus made this for his deserving freedman.
EDCS-05700952.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by an owner for his freedman, Aulus Egrilius Ostiensis, including a precise lifespan and duration of life in hours, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05700953.translation: To the divine shades. Aulus Egrilius Phlogius made this for himself and for his well-deserving wife, Syntyche.
EDCS-05700953.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a couple, set up by one spouse for both, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05700954.translation: To the divine shades. For Sophos, son of Aulus Aegrilius, who lived for twenty years, two months, and fourteen days.
EDCS-05700954.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sophos, son of Aulus Aegrilius, indicating his lifespan, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05700955.translation: To the divine shades. Egrilia Helpis and her son Egrilius Lucifer made this for Egrilius Thallianus, who lived for seventeen years, two months, and eight days, their well-deserving relative.
EDCS-05700955.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother and son for another son/brother, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05700956.translation: To eternal Security. Aulus Egrilius Hedonicus, of the Palatina tribe, made this for his excellent father, Aulus Egrilius Thallus, and for his freedwoman Egrilia Philete.
EDCS-05700956.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to a father and a freedwoman, potentially related to a mystery cult, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05700957.translation: To the departed spirits. To Egrilius Verianus, who lived 5 years, 8 months, and 21 days. Egrilia Haprodite made this for her dearest son.
EDCS-05700957.summary: A funerary dedication by Egrilia Haprodite for her young son, Egrilius Verianus.

EDCS-05700958.translation: To the departed spirits. To Aulus Egrilius Urbicus, who lived 28 years. Aegrilia Afrodite, his wife, made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-05700958.summary: A funerary dedication by Aegrilia Afrodite for her husband, Aulus Egrilius Urbicus.

EDCS-05700961.translation: To the departed spirits. For Egrilia, daughter of Aulus, [---]. Aulus Egrilius Hilarus and Helipis, parents, for their daughter [---] who lived one year [---] days.
EDCS-05700961.summary: A funerary dedication by parents for their infant daughter, Egrilia, who died at around one year old.

EDCS-05700964.translation: To the departed spirits. For Egrilia Felicitas, who lived 28 years. Carpophorus, her freedman.
EDCS-05700964.summary: A funerary dedication for Egrilia Felicitas, set up by her freedman Carpophorus.

EDCS-05700965.translation: To the departed spirits. For Egrilia Felicula.
EDCS-05700965.summary: A funerary dedication for Egrilia Felicula.

EDCS-05700966.translation: To the departed spirits. For Egrilia Festa. Aulus Egrilius Helius, heir.
EDCS-05700966.summary: A funerary dedication for Egrilia Festa, set up by her heir, Aulus Egrilius Helius.

EDCS-05700967.translation: To the departed spirits. For Egrilia Helpidi. Aulus Egrilius Agathopus made this for his well-deserving fellow freedwoman.
EDCS-05700967.summary: A funerary dedication by Aulus Egrilius Agathopus for his fellow freedwoman, Egrilia Helpidi.

EDCS-05700968.translation: To the departed spirits. Phoebe Egrilia made this for her well-deserving husband Zosimus.
EDCS-05700968.summary: A funerary dedication by Phoebe Egrilia for her husband, Zosimus.

EDCS-05700969.translation: To the departed spirits. Egrilia Procla made this for her well-deserving father, Aulus Egrilius Leonatus.
EDCS-05700969.summary: A funerary dedication by Egrilia Procla for her father, Aulus Egrilius Leonatus.

EDCS-05700970.translation: To the departed spirits. For Egrilia Severa, who lived 10 years, 8 months, and 26 days. Egrilia Nymphidia made this for her dearest daughter.
EDCS-05700970.summary: A funerary dedication by Egrilia Nymphidia for her young daughter, Egrilia Severa.

EDCS-05700971.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Egrilia Storges, daughter of Aulus, a univira, who lived 48 years, 5 months, 26 days, survived by three children and as many grandchildren. Gaius Erucianus Achilleus [erected this] for his most devoted wife, with whom he lived from their earliest youth for 34 years and 2 days, without infamy, without complaint.
EDCS-05700971.summary: This is a tombstone for Egrilia Storges, erected by her husband Gaius Erucianus Achilleus, providing her age at death and noting her surviving family.

EDCS-05700973.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aegrilia Thetis, from Aulus Livius Secundus, to his most sweet mother.
EDCS-05700973.summary: This is a tombstone for Aegrilia Thetis, dedicated by her son Aulus Livius Secundus.

EDCS-05700975.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Erotianeti, also called Ionia, his most sweet daughter, who lived [approximately] 30 years and 2 days.
EDCS-05700975.summary: This is a tombstone for Erotianeti, also known as Ionia, a fragment indicating her age at death and relationship to the dedicator.

EDCS-05700977.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Erucian(a) O[---]ocla, who lived 4 years, [?] months, 4 days, 10 hours.
EDCS-05700977.summary: This is a tombstone for Erucian(a) O[---]ocla, providing her age at death in years, months, days, and hours.

EDCS-05700978.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Eruciana Gemellina, to Erucianus Silvestrus, her incomparable husband, who lived with me 22 years without any complaint, to a well-deserving husband.
EDCS-05700978.summary: This is a tombstone for Eruciana Gemellina's husband, Erucianus Silvestrus, erected by her and noting his lifespan and their marital harmony.

EDCS-05700980.translation: [---]natia, freedwoman of Marcus, [---]euche, who lived 4 years, 4 months.
EDCS-05700980.summary: This is a tombstone for a freedwoman named [---]natia, providing her age at death and her patron.

EDCS-05700981.translation: [---]us Eunus [erected this] for his wife [---]giae, freedwoman of Aulus, and for his descendants, [in an area] 20 feet long.
EDCS-05700981.summary: This is a funerary inscription mentioning a husband, his wife who was a freedwoman, and their descendants, with a note on the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-05700983.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Archontis made [this] for Euodus, a well-deserving [man], who lived 76 years.
EDCS-05700983.summary: This is a tombstone for Euodus, erected by Archontis, noting his age at death and his merit.

EDCS-05700984.translation: [This was] made by [---] Julius(?) Euphran(or?) for his deserving wife and for Flavia Helpidi, his mother, [with a plot] 5 feet wide in front and 7 feet long in the back.
EDCS-05700984.summary: This is a tombstone for a deserving wife and her mother, Flavia Helpidi, erected by the husband/son, with dimensions for the burial plot.

EDCS-05700989.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Eutyches, his most sweet son, who lived 1 year, 3 months, 8 days. Carpitana Myrsine, mother, made [this].
EDCS-05700989.summary: This is a tombstone for Eutyches, erected by his mother Carpitana Myrsine, and provides his age at death.

EDCS-05700991.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [...]lius Primitibus made [this] for Eutyches, well deserving.
EDCS-05700991.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Eutyches, likely made by a relative or friend, and is dated by its style to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05700993.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gratius Centaurus, his patron, made [this] for Eutychia, his most deserving wife, who lived 45 years, 5 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-05700993.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a patron to his deceased wife, Eutychia, providing her age at death and dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05701000.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fabia Creste made [this] for her well-deserving husband, Sextus Fabio Rogatus.
EDCS-05701000.summary: A funerary dedication by Fabia Creste for her husband, Sextus Fabio Rogatus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05701005.translation: To the spirits of the departed. The place was given by Papinius Paulinus for his dearest son, Sexti Fadi Apollinaris, who lived 11 years, 8 months, and 28 days.
EDCS-05701005.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sexti Fadi Apollinaris, provided by Papinius Paulinus, detailing his age at death and dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05701006.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Fadius Priamus [made this] for his dearest wife, Fadia Nice.
EDCS-05701006.summary: A funerary epitaph by Sextus Fadius Priamus for his wife, Fadia Nice, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05701024.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [...] Victoria [made this] for [...]o Felix, most dutiful.
EDCS-05701024.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely for a dutiful person named Felix, with Victoria as the dedicant, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05701030.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Firmius Fortunatus received the columbaria (dovecotes) II and ollae (jars) IIII for the purpose of donation from Gaius Kasius Primigenius, freedman of Gaius, for his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-05701030.summary: This inscription records a funerary donation of burial spaces (columbaria and ollae) by Lucius Firmius Fortunatus from Gaius Kasius Primigenius, intended for his freedmen and their descendants, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05701038.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Antipater et Aurelia Laladice fecerunt sibi et suis et libertis libertabusque suis posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-05701038.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Antipater and Aurelia Laladice made [this] for themselves, their families, and their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-05701038.summary: A family tomb inscription by Titus Flavius Antipater and Aurelia Laladice for themselves and their descendants, including freedmen, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05701040.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Metrobius, freedman of the emperor, made [this] for his most dutiful son, Titus Flavius Felix, who lived 25 years and 17 days. For himself and his family, and their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. The place was given by Primigenius, a fellow freedman.
EDCS-05701040.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Titus Flavius Metrobius for his son Titus Flavius Felix, providing the son's age at death and also serving as a family tomb, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05701041.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Flavius Flavianus [made this] for his dearest son, Sextus Flavius Bellicus, the first of all to be called praetextatus, patron of the 'fontani' and the 'Herclianorum', who lived 12 years and [...] months.
EDCS-05701041.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Sextus Flavius Bellicus, who held the title 'patronus' of two groups, likely administrative or social organizations, and died at age 12, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05701044.translation: To Titus Flavius Primus, Aelia Eu[---] made (this) for her well-deserving son, who lived 19 years, 11 months, 29 days, 7.5 hours.
EDCS-05701044.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Primus, detailing his age at death, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701047.translation: To the departed spirits. Sextia Flora for her most excellent husband, Sextus Flavius Secundus.
EDCS-05701047.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Sextia Flora to her husband, Sextus Flavius Secundus, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701048.translation: To the departed spirits. For Flavius Victor [---]. Ulpius Rufus [---] for his most sweet son, who lived 2 years, 2 months, 13 days.
EDCS-05701048.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavius Victor, set up by Ulpius Rufus for their son, detailing the child's age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701051.translation: [---] freedman of Augustus. [---]rianus made (this) for himself and for [---]ste his wife, with whom he lived without any complaint, and for [---] most dutiful and Flavia [---] and for their descendants, [---] 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-05701051.summary: A funerary epitaph for an freedman of Augustus and his family, recording burial plot dimensions and possibly indicating a specific burial area, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701055.translation: To the departed spirits. For Flavia Didyme, who lived 50 years, mother of the freedmen of Flavius Capitolinus and Serenus.
EDCS-05701055.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Didyme, who lived 50 years, set up by the freedmen of Flavius Capitolinus and Serenus, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701056.translation: To the departed spirits. For Flavia Eutychia. Gaius Veturius Athenio for his most loving mother, 2.5 feet wide and 9 feet long. // To the departed spirits. For Veturia Pr[---]. Gaius Veturius Athenio for his freedwoman, well-deserving, 2.5 feet wide and 9 feet long. // from this tomb sacrilegiously...
EDCS-05701056.summary: A complex funerary inscription with two separate dedications by Gaius Veturius Athenio, one to his mother Flavia Eutychia and another to his freedwoman Veturia Pr[---], also mentioning sacrilege, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701059.translation: To the departed spirits. For Flavia M[---]. For Titus Flavius Aux[---], her husband, well-deserving, he made (this).
EDCS-05701059.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Flavia M[---] to her husband Titus Flavius Aux[---], likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701065.translation: To the departed spirits. For Flavia Romana, her sweetest daughter, who lived 5 years, 2 months, 22 days.
EDCS-05701065.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Romana, set up by her parent(s), detailing her age at death, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701071.translation: To the departed spirits. For Fonteia [---], who lived [---] 12 days [---].
EDCS-05701071.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Fonteia, mentioning a short lifespan of 12 days, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701072.translation: To the departed spirits. For Fonteia Severa.
EDCS-05701072.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Fonteia Severa, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701074.translation: To Titus Aelius Fortunatus, my husband, and Bennia Agape, for herself, and for Virginus, dearest and incomparable, who lived with me for 5 years and 4 months.
EDCS-05701074.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Bennia Agape to her husband Titus Aelius Fortunatus, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05701075.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. [To...] Secinus Fortunatus, who lived...
EDCS-05701075.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription to Secinus Fortunatus, indicating a burial from the Roman period.

EDCS-05701076.translation: Phoebus, lived 26 years, 2 months. Fortunata made this for her faithful servant.
EDCS-05701076.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Fortunata for her slave Phoebus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701077.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Publius Fraeganius Augustalis made this for his dearest son, who lived 9 years, 5 months, 24 days. Publius Fraeganius Augustalis (his father) made this.
EDCS-05701077.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy, erected by his father Publius Fraeganius Augustalis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701078.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Freganius Augustalis [who] lived 46 years, [X] months, [X] days. Victoria, to her well-deserving wife, with whom she lived [X] years, 6 months, 1 day.
EDCS-05701078.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph, likely for a couple, one of whom is named Freganius Augustalis, with dates of life and marriage mentioned, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701081.translation: Caius Fufeicius Auctor, freedman of Caius, lived 6 years.
EDCS-05701081.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Caius Fufeicius Auctor, son of a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701087.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aemilia Tertia [for her] son Lucius Furius Aelianus.
EDCS-05701087.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aemilia Tertia to her son Lucius Furius Aelianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701091.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caius Gabinius Aequanius made this for himself, and for his freedwoman wife Gabinia Parthenope, well-deserving, and for his descendants and all posterity.
EDCS-05701091.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Caius Gabinius Aequanius for himself, his wife Gabinia Parthenope, and their descendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701104.translation: To Marcus Graecinius Blando. Graecinia Callirhoe, mother, made this for her most dutiful son, who lived 36 years, 11 months, 16 days; and for Titus Mallius Terpnus, her most excellent husband, well-deserving.
EDCS-05701104.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by Graecinia Callirhoe for her son Marcus Graecinius Blando and her husband Titus Mallius Terpnus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701105.translation: Sextus Granius Silo, freedman of Sextus. Quintia Athenais, freedwoman of a woman. Lucius Ovius Anteros, freedman of Lucius. Ovia Musa, freedwoman of Publius. Publius Optatus, freedman of a woman. In front, 10 feet; in the field, 20 feet.
EDCS-05701105.summary: This inscription lists the names of several freedmen and freedwomen, likely as property owners or occupants, with dimensions of land, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701106.translation: To the departed spirits. Grania Auge made this for her fellow freedman and sweetest wife, well deserving.
EDCS-05701106.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by Grania Auge for her husband and fellow freedman, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05701108.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Hedius Natalis, son of Marcus, made this for his most dutiful mother, Hedia Acte, who lived 85 years, well deserving.
EDCS-05701108.summary: This funerary inscription records a son dedicating a monument to his dutiful mother, Hedia Acte, who lived to 85 years old.

EDCS-05701109.translation: To the departed spirits. [...] Heliodorus [...]ena Theodote [...] for himself and his family, and freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, and for Flavius Demetrius, a friend [...]
EDCS-05701109.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by Heliodorus and Theodote for themselves, their family, freedmen, and a friend, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-05701112.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Helvius Felicissimus. Marcus Helvius Agricola made this for his sweetest pupil, who lived 5 years, 3 months, and 6 days.
EDCS-05701112.summary: This funerary epitaph records Marcus Helvius Agricola dedicating a monument to his young pupil, Felicissimus, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-05701117.translation: To the departed spirits. Herennuleia Primilla, freedwoman of Aulus, received this columbarium and 2 urns in perpetuity as a gift from Kakia Euhodia, freedwoman of Gaius. A second burial niche, to the right as one enters, for Aulus Herennuleius Speratus, freedman of Aulus, brother of his most dutiful sister.
EDCS-05701117.summary: This funerary inscription details the transfer of ownership of a columbarium and urns, and designates a burial space for a brother and sister.

EDCS-05701121.translation: To the departed spirits. Hilaraes Vet[---] his freedwoman [---] who lived 3 years, 11 months, 6 days [---] Aulus Cornelius Ama[---]
EDCS-05701121.summary: This incomplete funerary epitaph records a freedwoman named Hilaraes Vet[---] and her lifespan, with a partial dedication by Aulus Cornelius Ama[---].

EDCS-05701122.translation: To the departed spirits. Egrilia Tyche made this for her fellow slave Hilarus.
EDCS-05701122.summary: This funerary epitaph records Egrilia Tyche dedicating a monument to her fellow slave, Hilarus.

EDCS-05701125.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Ortesius Caricus. Lucius Ortesius Doryphorus made this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-05701125.summary: This funerary epitaph records Lucius Ortesius Doryphorus dedicating a monument to his father, Lucius Ortesius Caricus.

EDCS-05701126.translation: To the departed spirits. For Hortensia Paula, incomparable wife. Lucius Hortesius Caricus, her husband, made this. She lived 34 years, 8 months, and 27 days.
EDCS-05701126.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Hortesius Caricus dedicating a monument to his incomparable wife, Hortensia Paula, noting her lifespan.

EDCS-05701130.translation: To the departed spirits. For Hostilia Callytyche, her mother. Elpidephorus.
EDCS-05701130.summary: This funerary epitaph records Elpidephorus dedicating a monument to his mother, Hostilia Callytyche.

EDCS-05701133.translation: Humania Iucunda, freedwoman of Publius, made this for Publius Humanus Diomedes, freedman of a woman, her patron. Also Humania Angelis, freedwoman of Publius; Terpolia Nice, freedwoman of a woman; Marcus Terpolius Nicephorus, freedman of a woman; Marcus Terpolius Philargyrus, freedman of a woman. Marcus Terpolius Barnaeus, freedman of Marcus and Humania; Marcus Petronius Sotericus; Petronia Nicenis, freedwoman of Marcus and a woman; Petronia Leda, freedwoman of Marcus and a woman; Marcus Petronius Hermes, freedman of Marcus and a woman; for themselves and their families. In front 20 feet, in depth 25 feet.
EDCS-05701133.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Humania Iucunda for her patron, Publius Humanus Diomedes, and lists other freedpersons and family members, likely for a tomb enclosure or columbarium, with dimensions.

EDCS-05701135.translation: To the departed spirits. Of Hypphore [---] sweet [---] [---]OT[---]
EDCS-05701135.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to departed spirits and mentioning a name, possibly 'Hypphore', and an adjective 'sweet'.

EDCS-05701138.translation: To the departed spirits. For Ianuarius, my brother, pious in his own ways. He lived 28 years. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-05701138.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Ianuarius, who lived 28 years, with a wish for his rest.

EDCS-05701145.translation: To the departed spirits. For Caius Iulius Cognatus, the most devoted patron. Iulia Chorinthias made this altar.
EDCS-05701145.summary: This is an inscription recording that Iulia Chorinthias dedicated an altar to her most devoted patron, Caius Iulius Cognatus.

EDCS-05701146.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Iulius Agathopus. He lived 5 years, 9 months, 11 days. Iulia Calligenia, mother, made this for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-05701146.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Gaius Iulius Agathopus and made by his mother, Iulia Calligenia.

EDCS-05701149.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Tiberius Iulius Asclepiades, a devoted husband, and for Rebennius, a son. Iulia Tatia made this for them, who deserved well.
EDCS-05701149.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Iulia Tatia dedicated a monument to her deserving husband, Tiberius Iulius Asclepiades, and her son, Rebennius.

EDCS-05701150.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Iulius Berianus. Who lived 8 years, 5 months, 17 days.
EDCS-05701150.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Gaius Iulius Berianus, who lived 8 years, 5 months, and 17 days.

EDCS-05701151.translation: Gaius Iulius Botrus, freedman of Gaius Iulius Philemon of Hortensius. Iulia Sima, freedwoman of Gaius. Antiochis. Antiochis. Gaius Iulius Isidorus, freedman of a woman of Gaius, of Marcus. In front 20 feet, in depth 20 feet.
EDCS-05701151.summary: This inscription records the names of several freedpersons and patrons, likely related to a family burial plot, with dimensions for the property.

EDCS-05701155.translation: For Gaius Iulius Cimbri and Agelenis.
EDCS-05701155.summary: This inscription records the names of Gaius Iulius Cimbri and Agelenis, possibly a dedication or a memorial.

EDCS-05701157.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Iulius Dryantius. Iulia Secunda, Tata, Naevia Sperata made this for their well-deserving wife/spouse. She lived 28 years.
EDCS-05701157.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Iulia Secunda and Naevia Sperata dedicated a monument to their deserving spouse, Gaius Iulius Dryantius, who lived 28 years.

EDCS-05701158.translation: Gaius Julius Epaphroditus lived 40 years. Iulia Historia lived 30 years. Gaius Julius Cimber, freedman of Gaius Julius, lived 60 years. Gaius Julius Alcides, freedman of Gaius Julius, lived 10 years. Iulia Zele.
EDCS-05701158.summary: This inscription lists the ages at death for several individuals, likely family members, associated with the name Gaius Julius.

EDCS-05701160.translation: To the divine shades. To Iulius Euangelus who lived 31 years, 7 months, and 10 days. His mother made this for her dearest son.
EDCS-05701160.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulius Euangelus and was made by his mother.

EDCS-05701165.translation: To the divine shades. Iulia Gemella made this for her well-deserving husband, Iulius Grumentinus, who lived 40 years and 2 months.
EDCS-05701165.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Grumentinus, dedicated by his wife Iulia Gemella.

EDCS-05701166.translation: To the divine shades. For Quintus Iulius Hermaiscus, an incomparable pupil, who lived 3 years and 10 months. Quintus Iulius Elpidephorus and Quintus Iulius Telesphorus made this for him, a deserving recipient.
EDCS-05701166.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young boy, Quintus Iulius Hermaiscus, and was made by two men, likely his guardians or family.

EDCS-05701167.translation: To the divine shades. Quintus Iulius Hermes, father, and Iulia Diodoris, mother, for their well-deserving son Quintus Iulius Cyrionius, who lived 17 years, 7 months, and 17 days. For themselves and their descendants, and for all their posterity without malice, whoever shall dismantle or violate this title, monument, or tomb, shall be condemned to pay 200,000 sesterces to the public treasury, and the heir shall not acquire this monument or tomb from the seller.
EDCS-05701167.summary: This funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Cyrionius includes a severe penalty clause for any desecration or sale of the tomb, intended to protect the family's burial plot.

EDCS-05701170.translation: To the divine shades. Gaius Julius Maximus made this monument for himself and for his wife Flavia Alexandria and for their children, freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. In the front 20 feet, in the field 25 feet.
EDCS-05701170.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription set up by Gaius Julius Maximus for himself, his family, and his slaves, specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-05701171.translation: To the divine shades. Tiberius Iulius Norbanus made this for himself and for his well-deserving wife Maria Victoria, and for his freedwoman Mindia Saturnina, and for his freedwoman Iulia Helpidis, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and his descendants. In the front 26 feet, in the field 25 feet.
EDCS-05701171.summary: This funerary inscription records that Tiberius Iulius Norbanus established a burial plot for himself, his wife, his freedwomen, and their descendants, indicating the plot's dimensions.

EDCS-05701175.translation: To the divine shades. For Quintus Iulius [---]. He lived [---]. [---] who lived 2 months and 20 days. Pompeia Tyches, mother, and Quintus Iulius Eutyches, father, made this for their dearest son.
EDCS-05701175.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a son of Quintus Iulius and Pompeia Tyches, providing his age at death.

EDCS-05701176.translation: To the divine shades. Gaius Iulius Pr[---] made this for Eutych[---], his wife.
EDCS-05701176.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription indicating that Gaius Iulius Pr[---] dedicated a monument to his wife.

EDCS-05701177.translation: To the divine shades. For Gaius Iulius Proculus. Gaius Iulius Proculus, his son, made this for his most dear father. For himself and for all his descendants. He lived 47 years.
EDCS-05701177.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Iulius Proculus, who lived 47 years, and was dedicated by his son.

EDCS-05701178.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Julius Quietus made this for his most sweet wife, Julia Pompeia. He made it while alive. She lived 19 years, 8 months, 5 days.
EDCS-05701178.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a husband for his deceased wife, with her age at death provided.

EDCS-05701180.translation: Gaius Julius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Voturia tribe, made this for himself and for his father, Gaius Julius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Voturia tribe; for his mother, Julia Optata, daughter of Spurius; for his brother, Gaius Julius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Voturia tribe; for his brother, Gaius Julius Rufinus, son of Gaius, of the Voturia tribe; for his sister, Julia Restituta, freedwoman of Gaius; for his freedman Potho; and for all his freedmen and freedwomen. In front 79 feet, in the land 20 feet.
EDCS-05701180.summary: This is a funerary monument purchased by Gaius Julius Rufus for himself and his extended family, specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-05701181.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Soteris made this for her husband, Iulius Secundus, who lived 45 years, for his deserving merit.
EDCS-05701181.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius Secundus at age 45 and was set up by his wife, Iulia Soteris.

EDCS-05701182.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Simplicius and Ca[e]lia Glauce made this for their son, Gaius Julius Simplicius, who lived [X] years.
EDCS-05701182.summary: This funerary epitaph, though incomplete, records a monument made by parents for their deceased son, Gaius Julius Simplicius.

EDCS-05701183.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Simplicius made this for his deserving wife, Caelia Glaucenis. Glauce, fare well. To you who greet me.
EDCS-05701183.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Gaius Julius Simplicius for his wife Caelia Glaucenis, containing a farewell message.

EDCS-05701184.translation: Gaius Julius Telesphorus, from Ravenna, made this for himself and for his wife, Calpurnia Gemellina, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. In front 20 feet, in the land 25 feet.
EDCS-05701184.summary: This is a funerary monument purchased by Gaius Julius Telesphorus of Ravenna for himself, his wife, and their descendants, with plot dimensions specified.

EDCS-05701191.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Nonia Crescentina made this for her deserving mother, Iulia Alexandria.
EDCS-05701191.summary: This funerary epitaph records a monument erected by a daughter, Nonia Crescentina, for her mother, Iulia Alexandria.

EDCS-05701192.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [I]ulia Aphrodite [donated] this place for her daughter, Iulia Hermione, who lived 2 years, 3 months, 9 days. Decimus Publicius Ph[3]mus.
EDCS-05701192.summary: This funerary epitaph records a burial plot donated by Iulia Aphrodite for her young daughter, Iulia Hermione, with the donor of the space also mentioned.

EDCS-05701195.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For his most sweet daughter, Iulia Hilara, who lived 4 years, 7 months, 20 days.
EDCS-05701195.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulia Hilara at age 4 and was set up by her mother, Iulia Cornificia.

EDCS-05701196.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Julius Eyodus and Julius Abascantus and Iulia Fortunata made this for their deserving mother, Iulia Euodia, who lived 75 years, 5 months, 9 days.
EDCS-05701196.summary: This funerary epitaph records a monument made by three children for their deceased mother, Iulia Euodia, noting her age at death.

EDCS-05701198.translation: To the departed spirits. To Julia Felicissima, who lived 20 years and 9 days. Balerius Synphor to his deserving wife.
EDCS-05701198.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julia Felicissima, set up by her husband Balerius Synphor.

EDCS-05701199.translation: To Filetena Iulia, who lived 15 years and 10 months. Gaius Iulius Felicianus made this for his most sweet daughter.
EDCS-05701199.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Filetena Iulia, dedicated by her father Gaius Iulius Felicianus.

EDCS-05701201.translation: To the departed spirits. To Iulia Fortunata, daughter of Gaius. She lived 20 years. Her sister made this for her deserving sister.
EDCS-05701201.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Fortunata, erected by her sister.

EDCS-05701202.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Historia, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-05701203.translation: To the departed spirits. Malius Mamfamo made this for his deserving wife, Iulia Gatane.
EDCS-05701203.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Gatane, set up by her husband Malius Mamfamo.

EDCS-05701204.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Iulius Septiminus made this for his most sweet daughter, Iulia Maximilla.
EDCS-05701204.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Maximilla, dedicated by her father Quintus Iulius Septiminus.

EDCS-05701215.translation: To the departed spirits. To Iulia Victoria. Iulius Caliste and Nicetes made this for their deserving daughter.
EDCS-05701215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Victoria, erected by her parents Iulius Caliste and Nicetes.

EDCS-05701221.translation: To Marcus Iunius Telesphorus. His wife made this for him, deserving.
EDCS-05701221.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iunius Telesphorus, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-05701224.translation: To the departed spirits. Iunianus and Agrippina made this for their most sweet daughter, Iunia Prenestina.
EDCS-05701224.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iunia Prenestina, dedicated by her parents Iunianus and Agrippina.

EDCS-05701229.translation: Quintus Laberius Achilles, freedman of Quintus, made this for Laberia Nigella, freedwoman of Quintus (and his wife), and Laberia Eleutheris, freedwoman of Quintus (and his wife), and Laberia Auta, freedwoman of Quintus (and his wife), and for the freedmen and freedwomen and their freedmen and freedwomen of both of them, in front for 30 feet.
EDCS-05701229.summary: This is a funerary epitaph likely for a freedman establishing a burial plot for himself, his freed family members, and their descendants, possibly dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05701230.translation: To the departed spirits. Laberia Hilaritas made this for her well-deserving mother, Laberia Marcellina.
EDCS-05701230.summary: A funerary dedication by Laberia Hilaritas for her mother, Laberia Marcellina.

EDCS-05701233.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Laelius Lamyros made this for his most devoted son, Lucius Laelius Lamyris, who lived for 19 years.
EDCS-05701233.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Laelius Lamyros for his son, Lucius Laelius Lamyris, who died at age 19.

EDCS-05701236.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Lalius Polytimus made this for his son Polytimus, most devoted, who lived for 2 years, 8 months, and 3 days.
EDCS-05701236.summary: A funerary dedication for Polytimus, son of Lucius Lalius Polytimus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-05701241.translation: To the departed spirits. The patron made this for his freedman, Caius Lasuccius Primitivus, who deserved well.
EDCS-05701241.summary: A funerary dedication by a patron for his well-deserving freedman, Caius Lasuccius Primitivus.

EDCS-05701243.translation: To the departed spirits. The mother made this for her daughter Liberalini, who deserved well, who lived for 3 years, 6 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-05701243.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother for her daughter Liberalini, recording her age at death.

EDCS-05701246.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Licinius Phaeder and Marcus Ulpius Dionysius made this for themselves, and for their wives Renniae Faustinae and Ulpiae Eutychiae, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants; this monument shall not pass to an outside heir.
EDCS-05701246.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Licinius Phaeder and Marcus Ulpius Dionysius for themselves, their wives, and descendants, with a clause about inheritance.

EDCS-05701248.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Aelius Probus made this for his dearest wife, Licinia Marcia, daughter of Marcus, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-05701248.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Aelius Probus for his wife Licinia Marcia and their freedmen and descendants.

EDCS-05701249.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Papius Crescens made this for his dearest wife, Licinia Marcia, who lived for 22 years, 5 months, and 8 days.
EDCS-05701249.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Papius Crescens for his wife Licinia Marcia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-05701251.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelius Romulus made this for Licinia Trophime.
EDCS-05701251.summary: A funerary dedication by Cornelius Romulus for Licinia Trophime.

EDCS-05701254.translation: To the departed spirits. [Belonging to] Aulus Livius Earini.
EDCS-05701254.summary: A funerary dedication indicating ownership by Aulus Livius Earini.

EDCS-05701255.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aulus Livius Epictetus.
EDCS-05701255.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Aulus Livius Epictetus, likely a freedman.

EDCS-05701259.translation: To the departed spirits. For Livia Trophime, companion of Aulus Livi Modestus, well-deserving, and for Livia Trophimeni.
EDCS-05701259.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Aulus Livi Modestus to his companion Livia Trophime and another Livia Trophimeni, likely a relative.

EDCS-05701261.translation: Aulus Livius Phileros, freedman of Aulus; Aulus Livius Pantagathus, freedman of Aulus; Aulus Livius Musaeus, freedman of Aulus; Livia Prima, freedwoman of Aulus. In the front 25 feet, in the field 25 feet.
EDCS-05701261.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land and the names of four individuals, likely freedmen and a freedwoman of the Livius family, who may have purchased or are buried here.

EDCS-05701263.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aulus Livius Sabinus, son of Aulus.
EDCS-05701263.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Aulus Livius Sabinus, son of Aulus.

EDCS-05701275.translation: To the departed spirits. For Livia Hagia, daughter of Aulus.
EDCS-05701275.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Livia Hagia, daughter of Aulus.

EDCS-05701276.translation: To the departed spirits. For Livia Helpidi, wife of Aulus Livius Restitutus, who lived in marriage for 58 years; to Livia Iusta, daughter of Aulus; to Livia Hagia, daughter of Aulus; to Livius Sabinus, son of Aulus.
EDCS-05701276.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription dedicated to Livia Helpidi, wife of Aulus Livius Restitutus, and also mentions her daughter, her mother, and a brother, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05701279.translation: To the departed spirits. For Livia Rufina, who lived 7 years, 3 months, 28 days. Livius Aeromenus and Callinoe [dedicated this] to their most sweet daughter.
EDCS-05701279.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Livia Rufina, a young girl, made by her parents Livius Aeromenus and Callinoe.

EDCS-05701282.translation: To Quintus Lollius Liberalis, who lived 20 years, 5 months, 6 days. His mother made [this] for her most beloved son.
EDCS-05701282.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a mother for her son, Quintus Lollius Liberalis.

EDCS-05701284.translation: Longina Diliges made [this] for herself and for Lucius Longinius Fortunatus, a well-deserving patron, and for Cassia Nice, mother and daughter, and for Cassis Augustalis, father and son, and for Florus, son, and for freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. This monument, established for sacred purposes, has a front of 10 feet and a field of 10 feet.
EDCS-05701284.summary: This is a funerary monument established by Longina Diliges for herself, her patron, and several family members, including freedmen and their descendants, with specified dimensions.

EDCS-05701285.translation: To the departed spirits. Longinia Procla made [this] for herself and her own freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. Those who left me while I was alive shall have neither access nor entrance into this monument.
EDCS-05701285.summary: This funerary inscription by Longinia Procla establishes the monument for herself and her freed dependents, with a clause excluding those who predeceased her.

EDCS-05701298.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Parents made this for their most sweet sons, Marcus Lutatius Euporianus, who lived 9 years and 8 months, and Marcus Lutatius Marcianus, who lived 4 years and 30 days.
EDCS-05701298.summary: A funerary epitaph for two young boys, erected by their parents, dating likely to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05701299.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Marcus Faustinus made this for himself and for his wife Annia Maria, his relative, and for his freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-05701299.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Marcus Faustinus for himself, his wife, and his freed dependents, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05701300.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tyce and Olympas [made this] for their well-deserving spouse.
EDCS-05701300.summary: A funerary epitaph possibly for a married couple, honoring a deceased spouse, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05701302.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Bonosa made this for Maiorica, her mother, who lived 18 years, 3 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-05701302.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maiorica, erected by her mother Bonosa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701312.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Mamius Carpus made this for his well-deserving wife, Mamia Festa.
EDCS-05701312.summary: A funerary epitaph for Mamia Festa, erected by her husband Lucius Mamius Carpus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701313.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Caricus [made this] for his most chaste wife, Manilia Quinta, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-05701313.summary: A funerary epitaph for Manilia Quinta, erected by her husband Titus Flavius Caricus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701314.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ofillia Alexandria [made this] for her parent Ofillia Faustina, and for her wife [i.e., Ofillia Alexandria's wife] Titus Flavius Caricus, who possessed incomparable chastity, and for herself.
EDCS-05701314.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Ofillia Alexandria for her parent and her wife, and for herself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701316.translation: [The tomb] of Titus Manlius Eutychus, entering on the right side of the dining room, part of the pigeon-house, [chamber] I.
EDCS-05701316.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely marking a burial space within a larger funerary complex, possibly associated with a columbarium, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701318.translation: [About] Manlius, a freedman of a woman, Fa[---]; [about] Fabia T[---]; [about] Valeria Sympherusa Ru[---]; [by] the arbitration of Publius Manlius, freedman of Publius Plo[ ---]; [for] this monument two iugera of land fall [in the part] which leads to the public (?) villa, from the testament.
EDCS-05701318.summary: A complex funerary inscription detailing the division of property and the establishment of a monument based on a will, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701319.translation: Aulus Manlius Gratus, freedman of Aulus, [and] Manlia Helpis, freedwoman of Aulus. // Aulus Manlius Eros, freedman of Aulus and Naevia. 'Manlia Blanda, freedwoman of Aulus'. Aulus Manlius Felix Optatus, freedman of Aulus. Aulus Manlius Eutychus, freedman of Aulus [and] of a woman. Aulus Manlius Epaphra, freedman of Aulus. Manlia, freedwoman of Aulus [and] of a woman. Aulus Manlius Lucrio, freedman of Aulus. Primilla, freedwoman. // Aulus Manlius Putolanus, freedman [and] Aulus Manlius Crescens. In front 42 feet; in the field '20 feet'.
EDCS-05701319.summary: An inscription documenting a family and their freedmen, likely related to property ownership and burial rights, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701325.translation: To Irene, freedwoman of Publius, and to Publius Manlius Philodamus, freedman of Publius, her patron, and to Crispus Antiochus, freedman of Publius, his fellow freedman, by the authority of Philonis, freedman of Publius Manlius and a woman. 10 feet in front, 25 feet in the field. This monument will not pass to the heir.
EDCS-05701325.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Irene, establishing her relationship to her patron and fellow freedman, with dimensions and a clause about inheritance.

EDCS-05701328.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Manlia Primigenia made this for her well-deserving husband Aulus Egrilius Festus, and for herself.
EDCS-05701328.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Manlia Primigenia for her husband and herself, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05701329.translation: Manlia Tyche made this for herself, for her husband Titus Marcellus Hilarus, and for their descendants. 5 feet in front, 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-05701329.summary: A funerary epitaph with dimensions, set up by Manlia Tyche for herself, her husband, and their descendants.

EDCS-05701333.translation: Lucius Marcius Euodus made this for his dutiful son Lucius Marcius Fidelis, who lived 10 years. 8 feet in front; in the field... Glycinna, [companion] of Gaius Oppius...
EDCS-05701333.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Marcius Fidelis, recording his age and the dimensions of his plot, set up by his father.

EDCS-05701334.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Montania Capitolina made this for her most dutiful mother, Marcia Affia.
EDCS-05701334.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Montania Capitolina for her mother Marcia Affia.

EDCS-05701336.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcia Epicharis made this for herself, for her daughter Marcia Crescentina, and for her husband Marcus Valerius Exoriens, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. 12 feet in front, 22 feet in the field.
EDCS-05701336.summary: A funerary epitaph with dimensions, set up by Marcia Epicharis for herself, her daughter, and her husband.

EDCS-05701337.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcia Crescentina made this for her most dutiful mother Marcia Epicharis.
EDCS-05701337.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Marcia Crescentina for her mother Marcia Epicharis.

EDCS-05701340.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tuccia Soteris [made this] for her well-deserving freedman Lucius Marius Alexander, who lived 35 years, 5 months, and 25 days.
EDCS-05701340.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Marius Alexander, set up by his freedwoman owner Tuccia Soteris.

EDCS-05701342.translation: Marcus Marius Fortis, Marcus Marius Primigenius, and Maria Fortunata, fellow freedmen, [made this] for themselves and their descendants. 25 feet in front, 25 feet in the field.
EDCS-05701342.summary: A funerary epitaph with dimensions, set up by three fellow freedmen for themselves and their descendants.

EDCS-05701344.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [His] mother made [this] for Publius Marius Maximus.
EDCS-05701344.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by the mother for her son, Publius Marius Maximus.

EDCS-05701346.translation: Marcus Marius Pius, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe; Cornelia Maxima, daughter of Quintus; Quintus Manlius Crispus, son of Quintus, of the Pollia tribe; his son lived 16 years. Caius Marius Macer, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe; Marcus Marius Potitus, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe; Titus Marius Marinus, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe. Manlia Crispina, daughter of Quintus and his freedwoman Faustilla; Maria Marina, daughter of Marcus. Quintus Iucundus, freedman of Quintus and Maria, made this for his patrons, himself, his family, and his descendants. In front 27 feet, in depth 25 feet.
EDCS-05701346.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Quintus Iucundus for his patrons and his own family, detailing the names and relationships of the deceased.

EDCS-05701347.translation: Decimus Pylades, freedman of Decimus; Decimus Eutychus, freedman of Decimus; Caius Primigenius, freedman of Caius; Volusia Epimelia, freedwoman of Caius. In front [
EDCS-05701347.summary: This is a funerary inscription naming several freedmen and freedwomen, likely part of a family burial plot.

EDCS-05701351.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Maximino.
EDCS-05701351.summary: A simple funerary dedication to Maximino.

EDCS-05701359.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Messenia Issi Maemucs(?), daughter of Servan(d=S)dus pa(te=P)r, who lived 5 years, 10 months, and 24 days.
EDCS-05701359.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Messenia Issi Maemucs, daughter of Servandus, at a young age.

EDCS-05701360.translation: [for] Messia, daughter of Gaius [...]ssime Gaius Messius [...] lived [X] years, II days [
EDCS-05701360.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription detailing the lifespan of Messia and mentioning a Gaius Messius.

EDCS-05701364.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Mettia Paulina, daughter, who lived 2 years, 5 months, and 6 days.
EDCS-05701364.summary: A funerary inscription for Mettia Paulina, recording her young age at death.

EDCS-05701370.translation: Minidia Homilia, freedwoman of Caius, made this for herself and for Minidia Merope, her daughter, and for Quintus Iegius Merops, and for Caius Iulius Hilario, her husband, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. In front 20 feet, in depth 20 feet. [inscription details missing] for herself [inscription details missing]
EDCS-05701370.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Minidia Homilia for herself and her family, including her husband and daughter, specifying plot dimensions.

EDCS-05701372.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Quintus Minucius Primigenius and Minucia Successa, his wife. Quintus Minucius Karus made this for his most devoted parents.
EDCS-05701372.summary: A funerary monument erected by Quintus Minucius Karus for his parents, Quintus Minucius Primigenius and Minucia Successa.

EDCS-05701380.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Marcus Iustus, freedman of Marcus. His father, Modius Felix, freedman of a woman, and his mother, Modia Epicharis, freedwoman of a woman, made this. He lived 20 years and 12 days.
EDCS-05701380.summary: A funerary inscription set up by the parents for their son, Marcus Iustus, detailing his age at death.

EDCS-05701387.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Mollicius Fedimus, who lived 29 years, 3 months, and 20 days. Caedicia Ianuaria [for her] most devoted son.
EDCS-05701387.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Caedicia Ianuaria to her son, Mollicius Fedimus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-05701388.translation: To the departed spirits. For Mollicia Felicianeti, dearest daughter, who lived 9 years, 5 months, and 16 days. Mollicius Eutychianus, father. To the departed spirits.
EDCS-05701388.summary: A funerary inscription for a young girl named Mollicia Felicianeti, set up by her father.

EDCS-05701395.translation: Munatia Primitiva is buried here. Lucius Munatius Hermes and Protus, fellow freedmen, made (this) for their deserving freedwoman.
EDCS-05701395.summary: A funerary epitaph for Munatia Primitiva, erected by her fellow freedmen.

EDCS-05701397.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Mundicius Mystes made (this) for his most sweet son, Gaius Mundicius Felix, who lived 4 years, 7 months. The plot was donated by Lucius Agrius Primus, part of the bedroom on the exterior entrance.
EDCS-05701397.summary: A funerary inscription for a young boy, Gaius Mundicius Felix, made by his father, specifying a donated burial plot.

EDCS-05701398.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Kanius Felix made (this) for Marcus Musamius Felix, his deserving friend.
EDCS-05701398.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Musamius Felix, set up by his friend Quintus Kanius Felix.

EDCS-05701400.translation: Lucius Naebius Grhysogonus made (this) for himself and for Clodia Victoria, his most worthy wife, and for Marcus Aurelius Vitalio, freedman of the Augusti, and for Marcus Aurelius Peculiari, freedman of the Augusti, his sons, and likewise for his dearest grandsons, and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. 24.5 linear feet, in the field 25.5 feet.
EDCS-05701400.summary: A self-funded funerary monument for Lucius Naebius Grhysogonus, his wife, sons, grandsons, and freedmen, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-05701401.translation: Caius Naevius Alexander, son of freedman Caius Naevius; Caius Naevius Neon, son of freedman Caius Naevius; Naevia Hilara, freedwoman of the two Caii; Asclepiades, son of freedman Caius Naevius, while alive, made (this) for himself and his family. In front 20 feet, in the field 25 feet.
EDCS-05701401.summary: A funerary monument erected by Caius Naevius Asclepiades for himself and his family, detailing the plot dimensions.

EDCS-05701403.translation: To the departed spirits. For Publius Naevius Elpidephorus, dearest son, who lived two years and 37 days.
EDCS-05701403.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Naevius Elpidephorus, a young son, specifying his age at death.

EDCS-05701404.translation: To the departed spirits. For Caius Naevius Fortunatus, son of freedman Caius.
EDCS-05701404.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Caius Naevius Fortunatus, son of a freedman.

EDCS-05701405.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Naevius Martialis made (this) for Plaria Felicitas, his dearest wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-05701405.summary: A funerary epitaph for Plaria Felicitas, erected by her husband Lucius Naevius Martialis.

EDCS-05701408.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Nammius Agathopus. Septimia Marcella made (this) for her husband.
EDCS-05701408.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Nammius Agathopus, set up by his wife Septimia Marcella.

EDCS-05701414.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Domitia Bithynia, daughter of Nausonices, made this for her mother.
EDCS-05701414.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitia Bithynia, dedicated to her mother.

EDCS-05701416.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. [---]morpho of Nicaea, from [---]nicus, and his daughters [---]ote, and to his daughter Agrippina, and to his daughters.
EDCS-05701416.summary: This is a partially preserved funerary inscription likely dedicated to multiple individuals, possibly a family.

EDCS-05701419.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Nonio Eutycheti Publius Nonius Alphius fratri rarissimo\n
EDCS-05701419.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Publius Nonius Eutychetes, Publius Nonius Alphius made this for his most beloved brother.
EDCS-05701419.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Nonius Eutychetes, dedicated by his brother.

EDCS-05701420.translation: Nonius For[---] made and Eleuther completed this for his deserving brother Ter[---]; he lived 12 years, 2 months, 5 days.
EDCS-05701420.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual who lived 12 years, dedicated by his brother and completed by Eleuther.

EDCS-05701422.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Nonius Silvanus made this for his wife Nonia.
EDCS-05701422.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nonia, dedicated by her husband Nonius Silvanus.

EDCS-05701424.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aulus Nonius Vitalis made this for his son Aulus Nonius Rufinus.
EDCS-05701424.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Nonius Rufinus, dedicated by his father Aulus Nonius Vitalis.

EDCS-05701428.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Publius Acilius made this for Novellia Victoria.
EDCS-05701428.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Novellia Victoria, dedicated by Publius Acilius.

EDCS-05701436.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Q(uintus) Octavius [---] made this for Q(uintus) Octavius Eutychetes.
EDCS-05701436.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Q. Octavius Eutychetes, dedicated by Q. Octavius.

EDCS-05701437.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Octavia Portesia. Quintus Octavius Hermes and Octavia Victrix, her parents, made this for their most beloved daughter; she lived 5 years, 6 months, 4 days.
EDCS-05701437.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Octavia Portesia, who died at the age of 5, dedicated by her parents.

EDCS-05701440.translation: Olympicus, slave of our Caesar, made this at his own expense for his relative Licinia Quieta; he lived 35 years, 6 months, 21 days.
EDCS-05701440.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia Quieta, dedicated by her relative and slave of Caesar, Olympicus.

EDCS-05701444.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Convarisia Victorina made this for Lucius Ostiensis Hilarus Samannarion, who lived for 6 years and 8 months, his well-deserving pupil.
EDCS-05701444.summary: This is a tombstone for a young boy, Lucius Ostiensis Hilarus Samannarion, set up by his adoptive mother, Convarisia Victorina.

EDCS-05701447.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Ostiensis Suntrofus rebuilt this for himself, his wife Cornelia Epictesis, and their freedmen, freedwomen, and descendants.
EDCS-05701447.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Lucius Ostiensis Suntrofus, who rebuilt a monument for his family, including his wife and their freed slaves and their descendants.

EDCS-05701448.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Combarisius Eutyches for his well-deserving wife Ostiensia Victorina, who lived 41 years, 7 months, and 17 days.
EDCS-05701448.summary: This is a tombstone for Ostiensia Victorina, set up by her husband Lucius Combarisius Eutyches, providing her age at death.

EDCS-05701451.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Decimus Otacilius Valerianus made this for his most dutiful and well-deserving brothers, Decimus Otacilius Fabianus and Decimus Otacilius Fortunatus.
EDCS-05701451.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Decimus Otacilius Valerianus for his two brothers, Decimus Otacilius Fabianus and Decimus Otacilius Fortunatus.

EDCS-05701452.translation: Decimus Otacilius Felix made this for himself and for Otacilia Hilara, fellow freedwoman, for Decimus Otacilius Hilarus, freedman, for Decimus Otacilius Eudoxus, freedman, in the council, having been manumitted, for Luria Musa, his wife, and for my other freedmen and freedwomen and all their descendants, except those whom I shall exclude in my will. In front 30 feet, in depth 25 feet.
EDCS-05701452.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Decimus Otacilius Felix, detailing his burial arrangements for himself, his wife, fellow freedpersons, and other freed slaves, and specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-05701460.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Palaus Hermes and Aemilia Attalis for their dearest pupil, Publius Palao Primitivus.
EDCS-05701460.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Palao Primitivus, set up by Publius Palaus Hermes and Aemilia Attalis, presumably his teachers or guardians.

EDCS-05701461.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Telesphorus for his well-deserving wife Panthia, who lived 17 years, 11 months, and 16 days.
EDCS-05701461.summary: This is a tombstone for Panthia, set up by her husband Telesphorus, providing her age at death.

EDCS-05701468.translation: For freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants from the household of my name. Quintus Patrius Victor, son of Quintus, purchased this, to whom the right of this monument was granted by the sons and heirs of the maker. In front 25.5 feet, in depth 30 feet.
EDCS-05701468.summary: This inscription records the purchase of a burial plot by Quintus Patrius Victor for his freed slaves and their descendants, with dimensions specified.

EDCS-05701474.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For his patron, Aulus Petronius Crescens for himself and his descendants, for his wife Britidia Affi, the patron who lived 40 years, the freedman of Aulus Petronius Victor, son of Aulus.
EDCS-05701474.summary: This is a tombstone by Aulus Petronius Crescens for his patron, his wife, and himself, indicating familial relationships and the patron's lifespan.

EDCS-05701479.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For his most excellent fellow slave, Homonoa. Phileros, freedman of Palaes Hermes, made this.
EDCS-05701479.summary: This is a tombstone by Phileros, a freedman, for his fellow slave Homonoa, identifying their relationship and former owner.

EDCS-05701481.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Of Philippi.

EDCS-05701483.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Protis his wife and Philocyrius his freedman erected this monument to Phoebus, her husband, most excellent and dutiful, who died not in the fullness of his age, but I, impious, am buried after you. And he was placed [here] on the ninth day before the Kalends of June.
EDCS-05701483.summary: A funerary inscription for Phoebus, set up by his wife and freedman, with a date.

EDCS-05701485.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcia Helias made [this] for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Phoebus Tribellius.
EDCS-05701485.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Phoebus Tribellius, dedicated by his wife Marcia Helias.

EDCS-05701490.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Plinius Hermes made [this] for his well-deserving wife, Plinia Euresis.
EDCS-05701490.summary: A funerary inscription for Plinia Euresis, dedicated by her husband Plinius Hermes.

EDCS-05701492.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fecit Plotius Imperatus for his pupil.
EDCS-05701492.summary: A funerary inscription for an unnamed pupil, dedicated by Plotius Imperatus.

EDCS-05701494.translation: Plotia Optata, freedwoman of Caius, for herself and for her husband Caius Cornelius Mahetus. In front 20 feet, in the field 15 feet.
EDCS-05701494.summary: A funerary epitaph for Plotia Optata and her husband Caius Cornelius Mahetus, including plot dimensions.

EDCS-05701501.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Pomponius Nigrinus, who lived 7 years, 11 months, and 18 days. His parents Pomponius Theseus and Lutatia Euhodia made [this] for their good son. [With good wishes].
EDCS-05701501.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Pomponius Nigrinus, detailing his age at death and dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-05701505.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sempronius Postumus made [this] for his well-deserving wife, Pomponia Castula, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-05701505.summary: A funerary inscription for Pomponia Castula, detailing her age at death and dedicated by her husband Sempronius Postumus.

EDCS-05701506.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For their sweetest daughter, Pomponia Rufina. Rufinus and Pomponia Tyche, her parents, made [this] for the well-deserving one.
EDCS-05701506.summary: A funerary inscription for Pomponia Rufina, dedicated by her parents Rufinus and Pomponia Tyche.

EDCS-05701519.translation: For his wife [---]oli and for his freedwoman Popillia Isiadis and for his freedman Lucius Popillius Primitivus and for Popillia Pelagia, his freedwoman, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, and for those written above, of both families, and for Lucius Terentius Abascantius, his friend.
EDCS-05701519.summary: A complex funerary inscription likely for a tomb owner, dedicating plots to family members, freedpersons, and a friend, with illegible portions and names.

EDCS-05701529.translation: To the departed spirits. [---] made this for his dearest [---] and for his own [---] and for Hypatianus's nurse.
EDCS-05701529.summary: This funerary inscription, with significant lacunae, was erected by an unknown dedicator for several individuals, including Primigenius, Callicora, and a nurse named Hypatianus.

EDCS-05701531.translation: To the departed spirits. Procilia, mother, made this for her most beloved daughter, who lived 18 years.
EDCS-05701531.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Procilia erected a monument for her daughter who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-05701537.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Turranius Augustalis, with his sister Aemilia, made this for their most devoted mother, Publilia Callo.
EDCS-05701537.summary: This funerary inscription indicates that Publius Turranius Augustalis and his sister Aemilia erected a monument for their mother, Publilia Callo.

EDCS-05701541.translation: To the departed spirits. Quartus, slave of our Emperor [Caesar], made this for his own [---] to [his wife/relative] [---].
EDCS-05701541.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records that Quartus, a slave of the emperor, erected a monument for a female relative.

EDCS-05701542.translation: To the departed spirits. Quietus Appaenius and Tannia Felicitas made this for their deserving son; the plot was donated by Titus Iulius Primianus.
EDCS-05701542.summary: This funerary inscription records that Quietus Appaenius and Tannia Felicitas erected a monument for their son, with the burial plot donated by Titus Iulius Primianus.

EDCS-05701546.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Quintilius Zoticus, freedman and son-in-law, and Quintilia Euphemia, daughter, made this for the well-deserving Quintus Quintilius Andronicus, who lived 56 years.
EDCS-05701546.summary: This funerary inscription honors Quintus Quintilius Andronicus, who lived 56 years, and was erected by his freedman and son-in-law, Quintus Quintilius Zoticus, and his daughter, Quintilia Euphemia.

EDCS-05701551.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Quintilius Her[---], Quintilia Pieris [---] for their dearest [freedmen], and their freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-05701551.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Marcus Quintilius Her[---] and possibly his wife Quintilia Pieris, and includes their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.

EDCS-05701561.translation: To the departed spirits. Ragonia Irenes made this for her patroness, Ragonia Samiramis, who deserved well.
EDCS-05701561.summary: This funerary inscription records that Ragonia Irenes erected a monument for her patroness, Ragonia Samiramis.

EDCS-05701562.translation: To the departed spirits. Ramennia Chlides lived 14 years, 7 months, and 10 days. Iulius Fortunatus and [---] [made this].
EDCS-05701562.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription records the age of Ramennia Chlides at her death and was likely erected by Iulius Fortunatus and another person.

EDCS-05701568.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Romanus Felicissimus, who lived 21 years and 11 months. Flavia Claudiane made this for her well-deserving brother. To the departed spirits. For Titus Egnatius Buzion. Valeria Lucretia, his wife [made this].
EDCS-05701568.summary: This inscription combines two funerary notices: one for Lucius Romanus Felicissimus, erected by his sister Flavia Claudiane, and another for Titus Egnatius Buzion, erected by his wife Valeria Lucretia.

EDCS-05701570.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fortunatus, father, made this for his most sweet daughter Romula, who lived for 13 years.
EDCS-05701570.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a father for his daughter, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701572.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Rubrius Thallus for his incomparable wife Rubria Stratonicena.
EDCS-05701572.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his wife, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701579.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Onesimus and Primigenia for their most sweet daughter Rustica, who lived for 1 year, 10 months, and [unknown number of] days.
EDCS-05701579.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by parents for their young daughter, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701581.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Claudia Sabbas, who lived for [unknown number of]XXV years, 11 months, and 18 days. Claudius Filocyrius for his well-deserving wife. This place is full, below the lettered inscription.
EDCS-05701581.summary: A funerary epitaph for a woman, likely set up by her husband, with details about her age and the burial space, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701584.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Sacconia Secundilla. Caius Servius Gratus and Severia Madoce, her heirs.
EDCS-05701584.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sacconia Secundilla, dedicated by her heirs, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701586.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Salinator Demetrius, who lived for 3 years, 2 months, 11 days, and 7 hours. Salinatoria Antiochis for her most sweet son.
EDCS-05701586.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her young son, with detailed age at death, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701588.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Salinator Sabinus, son of Marcus, is buried here. He lived for 50 years, [unknown number of] months, and 15 days. [This monument is] for himself and his family.
EDCS-05701588.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Salinator Sabinus, indicating he is buried here and the tomb is for his family, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701591.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Salinatorian Iustiana. Marcus Salinator, father, made this for his most sweet daughter, who lived for 4 years, 7 months, and 24 days.
EDCS-05701591.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a father for his young daughter, with detailed age at death, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701600.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Scantio Macedo.
EDCS-05701600.summary: A funerary dedication to Scantio Macedo, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701601.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Scantia Deutara.
EDCS-05701601.summary: A funerary dedication to Scantia Deutara, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05701607.translation: Gaius Seius Fronto, freedman of Gaius, and Aviania Arbuscula, freedwoman of Avianus, lie here.
EDCS-05701607.summary: This is a tombstone marking the burial place of two freedmen, Gaius Seius Fronto and Aviania Arbuscula.

EDCS-05701608.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Tinucius Faustinus made this for his well-deserving relative Seius Stephanus.
EDCS-05701608.summary: This funerary inscription records Titus Tinucius Faustinus dedicating a monument to his relative Seius Stephanus.

EDCS-05701612.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Sentius Epagatus made this for his dearest wife Sentia Adiecta, and Gaius Sentius [---].
EDCS-05701612.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Sentius Epagatus to his wife Sentia Adiecta, with space for another individual.

EDCS-05701614.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Diapyrus, freedman of Gaius Septicius Clarus, made this for his dearest son, Caius Septicius Cissus, who lived 3 years, 10 months, and 3 days.
EDCS-05701614.summary: This funerary inscription records Diapyrus, a freedman, setting up a monument for his son, Caius Septicius Cissus, and includes the son's age at death.

EDCS-05701617.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Septimius Pontianus, freedman of the Augusti, purchased this for himself and for his most sacred wife Petronia Lucilla in his lifetime. Pontiane, you lie in this tomb [---] by your labors, whose pious reputation among the gods is always remembered [---] for your simplicity [---].
EDCS-05701617.summary: This funerary inscription is for Lucius Septimius Pontianus and his wife Petronia Lucilla, with a poetic epitaph praising the deceased.

EDCS-05701622.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Cn(aeus) Sergius Hermes (!).
EDCS-05701622.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription for Cn(aeus) Sergius Hermes.

EDCS-05701624.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Serius Glaucus, who lived 15 years.
EDCS-05701624.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Serius Glaucus, aged 15.

EDCS-05701626.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sertoria Rufina made this for her most sweet and well-deserving patroness Sertoria Prisca.
EDCS-05701626.summary: This funerary inscription records Sertoria Rufina setting up a monument for her patroness Sertoria Prisca.

EDCS-05701630.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Servilius Hermes made this for his daughter Servilia Felicitas.
EDCS-05701630.summary: This funerary inscription records Servilius Hermes dedicating a monument to his daughter Servilia Felicitas.

EDCS-05701636.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Sextilius Communis and his freedman heir Publius Sextilius Pothinus made this for Sextilia Iuliana, freedwoman of Publius.
