EDCS-05802002.translation: Marcus Marcius [...] and Marcia[...] and their freedwoman Marcia Nomas restored the temple of Bona Dea, which had fallen into ruin from age, from the ground up.
EDCS-05802002.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to Bona Dea by Marcus Marcius, Marcia, and their freedwoman Marcia Nomas.

EDCS-05802004.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Antoninus Pius, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 16th time, consul for the 3rd time, the best and most indulgent princeps. The alimentary children, boys and girls, of Ficolenses.
EDCS-05802004.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus and records the contribution of the alimentary children of Ficolenses, likely dating to 166 CE.

EDCS-05802008.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Lucius Arbustius Valentinus of Cremona, son of Lucius, of the Aniensis tribe, primipilaris of the IV Flavia Felix legion, evocatus of Augustus, from the 4th Praetorian cohort, centurion of the 2nd Vigilum cohort, centurion of the 11th Urban cohort, centurion of the 7th Praetorian cohort, centurion of the VII Claudia(?) legion, centurion of the VII Gemina Pia Felix legion. Lucius Arbustius Valens and Gaius Arbustius Vitalis, his sons [...].
EDCS-05802008.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Arbustius Valentinus, a distinguished soldier, erected by his sons, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-05802009.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Proculus, veteran of our Emperors.
EDCS-05802009.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius Proculus, a veteran of the Roman Emperors.

EDCS-05802010.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Clementiana made this for her most dear and deserving husband, Aurelius Valerianus, a soldier of the II Parthica legion, of the second cohort, with whom she lived for 3 years, 5 months, and 16 days, and who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-05802010.summary: A funerary epitaph for a soldier of the II Parthica legion, set up by his wife.

EDCS-05802012.translation: Burial place. 450 feet in front and 1295 feet in depth. From Eschines, freedman of Augustus, from the scribal office.
EDCS-05802012.summary: An inscription marking the dimensions and ownership of a burial plot, belonging to a freedman in Augustus' service.

EDCS-05802013.text_edition: \n Marci Consii Cerinthi patris accensi velati divo Claudio in consulatu in monte Albano ferias Latinas condenti ad sacra eidem censum populi in campo agenti et lustrum condenti ad sacra apparenti dein Imperatori Neroni Claudio divi Claudi filio Caesari Augusto Germanico in consulatu in monte Albano ferias Latinas condenti ad sacra item apparenti consulibus in monte Albano ferias Latinas condentibus apparenti ex Marco Licinio Crasso Frugi Lucio Calpurnio Pisone dedicatum Imperatore Nerone IIII Caio Cornelio Lentulo consulibus\n
EDCS-05802013.translation: Dedicated by Marcus Consius Cerinthus, father, accompanied by a veiled priest, to the deified Claudius during his consulship on the Alban Mount when performing the Latin Festival rites, and to the same when performing the census of the people in the Campus Martius and the lustration, and appearing for sacred rites; then to the Emperor Nero Claudius, son of the deified Claudius, Caesar Augustus Germanicus, during his consulship on the Alban Mount when performing the Latin Festival rites and appearing for sacred rites; also to the consuls during the Latin Festival on the Alban Mount, appearing for sacred rites, in the consulship of Nero for the fourth time and Gaius Cornelius Lentulus.
EDCS-05802013.summary: A complex dedication commemorating the religious duties performed by Marcus Consius Cerinthus and imperial officials during the Latin Festival and other sacred rites under Claudius and Nero.

EDCS-05802021.summary: A name inscription, possibly a dedication or ownership mark for Lucius Funisulanus Vettonianus.

EDCS-05802022.translation: Tiberius Claudius Phoenix made this for Quintus Servilius Pudens.
EDCS-05802022.summary: A funerary inscription indicating that Tiberius Claudius Phoenix erected the monument for Quintus Servilius Pudens.

EDCS-05802025.translation: Marcus Aurelius... Men... Lip... lived for [3] years, 9 days... veteran of our Augustus, and they and their descendants made [this].
EDCS-05802025.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph for a veteran of Augustus, mentioning his lifespan and that it was set up by him and his descendants.

EDCS-05802026.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Eutyches and Zosime made this for their most sweet son Aurelianus, who lived for 2 years and 2 months.
EDCS-05802026.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young child, set up by his parents.

EDCS-05802027.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Codonia Felicitas made this for her incomparable brother Lucius Codonius Fructus, well-deserving, who lived for 23 years.
EDCS-05802027.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Codonius Fructus, set up by his sister.

EDCS-05802028.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Cornelius Heraclitus.
EDCS-05802028.summary: A funerary dedication to Gaius Cornelius Heraclitus.

EDCS-05802034.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Naevius Creticus and Marcus Lubius Charito placed this for their excellent friend Marcus Lartidius Myrtilus.
EDCS-05802034.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Lartidius Myrtilus, set up by his friends.

EDCS-05802035.translation: Maecilia Prima, freedwoman of Maecilia, deceased. Maecilia Secunda, freedwoman of Publius, deceased. Publius Maecilius Apollonius, freedman of the late Maecilia, and her freedman. Maecilia Hilara, freedwoman of Maecilia. Marcus Maecilius Salvius, freedman of Maecilia. In the front 12 feet, in the field 15 feet.
EDCS-05802035.summary: This inscription is a boundary marker for a funerary plot, listing deceased members of the Maecilius family and their freedpersons.

EDCS-05802036.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Gaius Numisius Clusinus. His mother, Numisia Helpis, for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-05802036.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by a mother to her son, Gaius Numisius Clusinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802038.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Gaius Poppaeus Gemellus, who lived 90 years, 3 months, and 2 days. Poppaeus Primigenius made this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-05802038.summary: This tombstone records the death of Gaius Poppaeus Gemellus, who lived to 90 years old, and was erected by his son, Poppaeus Primigenius.

EDCS-05802039.summary: This is a tombstone fragment dedicated to a person named Purpuri, likely a child, expressing affection.

EDCS-05802040.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Rhodine. Dorus and Syntyche took care to have this made for their parent.
EDCS-05802040.summary: This tombstone indicates that Dorus and Syntyche commissioned a monument for their parent, Rhodine.

EDCS-05802041.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Servata. Syntrophus, fellow slave, for his fellow slave.
EDCS-05802041.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a fellow slave, Syntrophus, to his fellow slave, Servata.

EDCS-05802043.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Lucius Titius Achilleus, a deserving husband. Mantenia Helpis, for the sake of remembrance.
EDCS-05802043.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by Mantenia Helpis to her well-deserving husband, Lucius Titius Achilleus.

EDCS-05802045.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Lucius Vallius Tranquillianus, who lived 1 year, 5 months, and 20 days. Vallius Telesphorus and Vallia Aphrodisia, parents.
EDCS-05802045.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lucius Vallius Tranquillianus at a young age and was erected by his parents, Vallius Telesphorus and Vallia Aphrodisia.

EDCS-05802047.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Quintus Vehilius Trophimus.
EDCS-05802047.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Quintus Vehilius Trophimus, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-05802048.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Veienus Cicero.
EDCS-05802048.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Veienus Cicero, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-05802049.translation: To the departed spirits. Veturia sacre[d].
EDCS-05802049.summary: A funerary dedication to Veturia, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802050.translation: Marcus Ulpius Eutropus and Flavia Victorina made this for Marcus Ulpius Eutropus, their dearest son, and for themselves, their freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-05802050.summary: A funerary monument set up by Marcus Ulpius Eutropus and Flavia Victorina for their son, themselves, and their descendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802059.translation: For Sextus Acerraena Lupus, their dearest son, who lived for 7 years and 8 months. Urbanus and Iustina, his most loving parents, [dedicated this].
EDCS-05802059.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Acerraena Lupus, set up by his parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802060.translation: For Sextus Acerrana Ursa, our holy son, who lived 6 years, 8 months, and 7 days. To our dearest son, our holy Ursa, in peace. Urbanus and Iustina, parents, [dedicated this].
EDCS-05802060.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Acerrana Ursa, set up by his parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802062.translation: To the divine power of the Augusta's household. Blastus Eutactianus and Secundus, freedmen of the consul twice, Julius Quadratus, for the honor of the VIvirate, and Italia, his freedwoman, for the magistracy of the Bona Dea, dedicated this on the 14th day before the Kalends of October, during the consulship of Marcus Clodius Lunensis and Publius Licinius Crassus, on which day they also offered a feast. The senate of Fidenates restored this, which was consumed by fire.
EDCS-05802062.summary: A dedication by freedmen of a former consul and his freedwoman for their public offices in Fidenas, rededicated after a fire, dated to the consulship of Lunensis and Crassus.

EDCS-05802063.translation: To the Great and Invincible Emperor Gallienus Pius Felix Augustus. The senate of Fidenates, devoted to his divine majesty, [dedicated this], with Gaius Petronius Podalirius as dictator and Titus Aelius October as curating, and Titus Terentius October.
EDCS-05802063.summary: A dedication by the senate of Fidenas to Emperor Gallienus, likely during his reign.

EDCS-05802066.translation: To the departed spirits. To Lucius Gratius Pr[...], Roman equestrian, who lived 10 years [...], to the departed spirits [...]. Caesonia [...] to her incomparable wife, well-deserving, alive [...].
EDCS-05802066.summary: A fragmented funerary dedication to Lucius Gratius Pr[...], a Roman equestrian, and possibly to his wife Caesonia.

EDCS-05802067.translation: To the departed spirits. To Artemtidorus, freedman of Augustus, appointed to the memoria, who lived 17 years and 17 days. Valeria Philocene [dedicated this] to her dearest son.
EDCS-05802067.summary: A funerary epitaph for Artemtidorus, a freedman of Augustus, set up by his mother Valeria Philocene, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802070.translation: To the departed spirits. To Caelia Secundilla, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-05802070.summary: A funerary dedication to Caelia Secundilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802072.translation: To the departed spirits. Hermais, daughter of Hermes, lived 2 years, 2 months, and 14 days. Her father made this.
EDCS-05802072.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hermais, set up by her father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802093.translation: To Tiberius Caesar Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 32nd time.
EDCS-05802093.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Caesar Augustus's tribunician power, likely part of a larger public monument from his reign.

EDCS-05802103.translation: Gaius Cartilius Poblicola, son of Gaius, duovir, censor for the second time; Gaius Fabius, duovir for the second time; Aulus Terentius Aris, freedman of Aulus, Quintius, son of Lucius; Dama, freedman of Tiberius; Marcus Fabius Apollonius, freedman of Publius, had this made.
EDCS-05802103.summary: This inscription lists several local magistrates who commissioned the construction of a public work, likely a building or infrastructure, possibly in the early Imperial period.

EDCS-05802110.translation: Fausta, freedwoman of Gaius and his wife, made this for herself, for Gaius Baberius Cerialis, freedman of Gaius and his wife, Aug., and for Baberia Symphersa, freedwoman of Gaius and his wife, and for Baberius Lucrio, freedman of Gaius and his wife. In the front, 40 feet, in the field, 40 feet.
EDCS-05802110.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedwoman and her family, indicating the dimensions of a plot of land, likely for a tomb, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05802111.translation: To Marcus Junius Faustus, son of Marcus, of the Palatina tribe, who was a decurion admitted to the order, a flamen of the deified Titus, duovir, a grain merchant, quaestor of the treasury, a flamen in Rome, and patron of the emperors, curator of the merchant fleets, owner of all African ships, and also of the Sardinians. The place was granted publicly by decree of the decurions. Dedicated on the 12th day before the Kalends of October, when Severus and Pompeianus were consuls for the second time, with Publius Aufidius, Marcus Clodius Fortunatianus Pudens, and Lucius Tadius Felix serving as officials.
EDCS-05802111.summary: This is a public dedication honoring Marcus Junius Faustus, a prominent local official and businessman involved in maritime trade, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05802113.translation: To Gaius Veturrius Amandus, son of Gaius, of the Testius family, Roman knight, patron and defender of five guilds of boatmen of Ostia. All the shipowners of the five guilds, for his outstanding diligence in defending and protecting them, and for his exceptional service, to a most worthy and abstemious man, for his merits, of the five most distinguished guilds of bargemen. The place was granted publicly by decree of the decurions. Dedicated [during the consulship of] Praesens and Extricatus.
EDCS-05802113.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Veturrius Amandus, a Roman knight and patron of several guilds of boatmen in Ostia, for his service in defending their interests, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05802126.translation: To the divine shades. To [3]lius, a young man of integrity, who lived 23 years, 9 months, and 5 days. To Geminae Petroniae, a girl, his fortunate wife, who lived 19 years, 7 months, and 13 days.
EDCS-05802126.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young man and his wife, providing their names and ages at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802130.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. To Marcus Reptineus Probi[anus?], who lived [3] years and 10 months.
EDCS-05802130.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Reptineus Probi[anus?], giving his name and lifespan, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802164.translation: To Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, imperator for the 6th time, father of the fatherland, consul for the 3rd time, designated consul for the 4th time, from the Senate and People of Aricia.
EDCS-05802164.summary: This inscription records honors bestowed upon Emperor Vespasian by the city of Aricia, likely related to his public works or visits, dating to his reign (69-79 CE).

EDCS-05802168.translation: To Titus Arricinus [3], Marcus Manneius Domesticus Ma[3], Lucius Oppuneius Augurinus, and Titus Voltedius Indictor, quaestors of Aricia; [3] Marcellus and Gaius Lucius Pius, you are one of the sacred ones to whom Diana's gifts are given, which the people granted to the people.
EDCS-05802168.summary: This inscription appears to honor officials of Aricia, possibly including quaestors and individuals associated with the cult of Diana, and mentions the transfer of gifts from the people, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802180.translation: Pamphilus, slave of our Emperor, made this.
EDCS-05802180.summary: This is a simple inscription by a slave named Pamphilus, indicating he made something for the emperor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802185.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the names of a wife and daughter, possibly in a funerary context.

EDCS-05802194.translation: To Aurelius Victorinus, who lived 15 years, 11 months. Aurelius Priscianus [---] to his [---] son, Gaius Aurelius [---] Gresa, mother of Aurelius Priscinus [---] years.
EDCS-05802194.summary: A funerary inscription for Aurelius Victorinus, set up by his father Aurelius Priscianus and mother Gresa, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802196.translation: To Aurelia Mari[---], mother, who lived [---] years.
EDCS-05802196.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription honoring Aurelia Mari[---], the mother of the deceased.

EDCS-05802197.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Claudia Agidia, sister of Phylacis, freedwoman of Augustus. She lived 19 years.
EDCS-05802197.summary: A funerary inscription for Claudia Agidia, set up by her sister Phylacis, a freedwoman of the emperor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802200.translation: A virgin girl who lived 10 years, 2 months, 25 days, [---] hours, lies here.
EDCS-05802200.summary: A funerary inscription for a young girl, recording her age at death and mentioning her placement in a tomb, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802201.translation: [---] Junius, freedman of his wife, Eupranor; Maria, freedwoman of Marcus, Alexandria; Maria, freedwoman of her husband, Nicella; Junius, son of Gaius [---] Dionysius [---] in front 18 feet, in the field 18 feet.
EDCS-05802201.summary: This inscription likely marks a burial plot with dimensions, mentioning freedmen and women, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802215.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Flavia Tyche, freedwoman of Augustus.
EDCS-05802215.summary: A funerary dedication to Flavia Tyche, a freedwoman of the emperor, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802220.text_edition: \n Manio Acilio Mani filio Galeria Glabrioni Cnaeo Cornelio Severo consuli pontifici IIIviro aere argento auro flando feriundo VIviro turmae equitum Romanorum tribuno militum legionis XV Apollinaris salio collino legato provinciae Cretae Cyrenarum legato provinciae Africae quaestori Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii praetori legato Asiae senatus populusque Tiburs patrono municipi quinquennali designato\n
EDCS-05802220.translation: To Manius Acilius Manius son Galeria Glabrio Gnaeus Cornelius Severus, consul, pontifex, member of the board of III for casting bronze, silver, and gold, member of the board of VI for the cavalry turma, military tribune of the XV Apollinaris legion, Salian of the Collina tribe, legate of the province of Crete and Cyrene, legate of the province of Africa, quaestor of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Pius, praetor, legate of Asia; the senate and people of Tibur, patron of the municipality, designated quinquennalis.
EDCS-05802220.summary: A lengthy honorific inscription detailing the extensive career of Manius Acilius Manius son Galeria Glabrio Gnaeus Cornelius Severus, likely from the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-05802225.translation: Set up by Publius Manilius son of Publius, Galeria Vopiscos Vicinillianus, Lucius Elufrius Severus Iulius Quadratus Bassus, consul, pontifex, flamen, praetor, quaestor of the deified Trajan Parthicus, military tribune of the IIII Scythica legion, member of the board of III for casting bronze, silver, and gold, Salian of the Collina tribe, curator of the temple of Hercules Victor. Numerius Prosius Platanus with his wife Manilia Eutychia and his children Vibia Vicinilla, Manilis Vopiscana, and Atticus.
EDCS-05802225.summary: A funerary monument set up by Numerius Prosius Platanus and his family for themselves and their children, honoring the deceased and detailing the significant public and religious career of Lucius Elufrius Severus Iulius Quadratus Bassus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802240.translation: Under the supervision of Musonius Iulius Antullus, patron of the municipality. Dedicated on the 7th day before the Ides of June, in the consulship of Anullinus II and Fronto. [---] [[---]] [[---]] Under the edileship of Augustus. Lucius Aurelius Apolaustus, freedman of the Augusti, Memphius, master [---] To Lucius Aurelius Apolaustus, freedman of the Augusti, Memphius, pantomime, crowned three times in his time by the Augusti, priest of Apollo Herculanus, Augustalis. The senate and people of Tibur also honored him with the ornaments of the decurionate. [---] Orestes. [---] TYNA T. [---] XII N. [---] To the Troad women. [---]
EDCS-05802240.summary: This complex inscription records a dedication overseen by Musonius Iulius Antullus and includes a lengthy tribute to Lucius Aurelius Apolaustus, a pantomime and priest, alongside some Greek text, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05802245.translation: His father at the dedication of the amphitheater gave 200,000 sesterces and work for 200 men. For Marcus Tullius Rufus, son of Marcus Tullius of the Camilia tribe, son of Marcus Tullius Blaesus. His mother Tullia Berenice and his sister Tullia Blaesilla dedicated this monument. The place was granted by decree of the Senate.
EDCS-05802245.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating Marcus Tullius Rufus, with details about his father's contribution to the amphitheater's dedication.

EDCS-05802264.text_edition: \n Iulia Imperator Caesar Divi filius Augustus ex senatus consulto CCXXCI pedes CCXL\n
EDCS-05802264.translation: From Emperor Augustus, son of the deified one, by decree of the Senate, 221 paces (in length) and 240 feet (in width).
EDCS-05802264.summary: This inscription, likely related to a public works project or boundary marker, states measurements authorized by the Senate under Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-06100064.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae matri castrorum Caius Cipius Corinthianus quinquennalis perpetuus ex argenti pondo II dono dedit Claudia Secundina ob dedicationem dedit epulum et \n
EDCS-06100064.translation: Gaius Cipius Corinthianus, quinquennialis perpetuus, gave silver weighing 2 pounds as a gift to Julia Augusta, mother of the camp. Claudia Secundina gave a banquet on the occasion of the dedication.
EDCS-06100064.summary: This inscription records a dedication of silver and a banquet by Gaius Cipius Corinthianus and Claudia Secundina to Julia Augusta, mother of the camp.

EDCS-06100065.text_edition: \n Lucio Aelio Commodo Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae filio Lucius Naevius Saecularis et Publius Sulpicius Hera Augustalis quinquennalicius corporis traiectus Luculli sua pecunia posuerunt\n
EDCS-06100065.translation: Lucius Naevius Saecularis and Publius Sulpicius Hera Augustalis, quinquennalicii of the Traiectus Luculli corps, erected this at their own expense for Lucius Aelius Commodus, son of Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-06100065.summary: This is a dedication of a monument erected by two officials for Lucius Aelius Commodus, son of Emperor Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-06100066.text_edition: \n Lucius Rennius Aspri libertus Platanus sevir Augustalis fecit sibi in parte dexteriore et Egriliae Auli libertae Quartae coniugi suae et Renniae Proculae vernae et Lucio Rennio Luci liberto Philodoxo et libertis libertabus suis posterisque eorum in fronte pedes XXXX in agro pedes XXXISsemis hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-06100066.translation: Lucius Rennius Aspri, freedman Platanus, who was a sevir Augustalis, made this monument for himself on the right side, and for his wife Egrilia Auli, freedwoman Quartae, and for Rennius Proculus, a freedman, and for Lucius Rennius Luci, freedman Philodoxus, and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. 40 feet in front and 31.5 feet in the field. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-06100066.summary: This is a funerary monument established by Lucius Rennius Aspri Platanus for himself, his wife, and his freedmen and their descendants, with specific dimensions and a clause about inheritance.

EDCS-06100068.translation: In the consulship of Vetere and Aelianus.
EDCS-06100068.summary: This is a dating formula indicating the year based on the consuls Vetere and Aelianus.

EDCS-06100070.text_edition: \n Hadriano nostro III Quinto Dasumio Rustico consulibus\n
EDCS-06100070.translation: In the third consulship of Hadrianus and the consulship of Quintus Dasumius Rusticus.
EDCS-06100070.summary: This is a dating formula indicating the year based on the consuls Hadrianus (in his third consulship) and Quintus Dasumius Rusticus.

EDCS-06100072.text_edition: \n Claro II consule ex ratione Claudi Zeloti\n
EDCS-06100072.translation: In the second consulship of Clarus, from the account of Claudius Zelotes.
EDCS-06100072.summary: This is a dating formula indicating the year based on the second consulship of Clarus, also noting it's from the account of Claudius Zelotes.

EDCS-06100073.text_edition: \n Ex ratione Lucili Hermae et Claudi Soterici Glabrioni sic et Homullo consulibus\n
EDCS-06100073.translation: From the account of Lucilius Hermas and Claudius Sotericus, and in the consulship of Glabrio and Homullus.
EDCS-06100073.summary: This inscription, related to an account from Lucilius Hermas and Claudius Sotericus, is dated by the consulship of Glabrio and Homullus.

EDCS-06100074.text_edition: \n Rustico II et Aquilino consulibus ex ratione Sexti et Hermae\n
EDCS-06100074.translation: In the second consulship of Rusticus and Aquilinus, from the account of Sextus and Hermas.
EDCS-06100074.summary: This is a dating formula indicating the year based on the second consulship of Rusticus and Aquilinus, and from the account of Sextus and Hermas.

EDCS-06100076.translation: In the consulship of Vetere and Valente.
EDCS-06100076.summary: This inscription records a date based on the consulship of Vetere and Valente.

EDCS-06100078.text_edition: \n Claro II consule ex ratione Claudi Zeloti\n
EDCS-06100078.translation: In the consulship of Claro II, from the accounts of Claudius Zelotus.
EDCS-06100078.summary: This inscription records a date based on the consulship of Claro II and mentions accounts of Claudius Zelotus.

EDCS-06100079.text_edition: \n Gallicano et Vetere consulibus ex ratione Claudi Zeloti\n
EDCS-06100079.translation: In the consulship of Gallicanus and Vetere, from the accounts of Claudius Zelotus.
EDCS-06100079.summary: This inscription records a date based on the consulship of Gallicanus and Vetere and mentions accounts of Claudius Zelotus.

EDCS-06100080.summary: This inscription records a date based on the consulship of Augurinus.

EDCS-06100081.translation: In the consulship of Collega and Priscinus.
EDCS-06100081.summary: This inscription records a date based on the consulship of Collega and Priscinus.

EDCS-06100082.translation: In the consulship of Palma II and Tullus.
EDCS-06100082.summary: This inscription records a date based on the consulship of Palma II and Tullus.

EDCS-06100083.text_edition: \n Imperatore Hadriano nostro II consule ex ratione Restituti Caesaris nostri servi\n
EDCS-06100083.translation: In the consulship of our Emperor Hadrian II, from the accounts of Restitutus, our emperor's freedman.
EDCS-06100083.summary: This inscription records a date based on the second consulship of Emperor Hadrian and mentions accounts of Restitutus.

EDCS-06100084.text_edition: \n Gallicano et Vetere consulibus ex ratione Aureli M\n
EDCS-06100084.translation: In the consulship of Gallicanus and Vetere, from the accounts of Aurelius M.
EDCS-06100084.summary: This inscription records a date based on the consulship of Gallicanus and Vetere and mentions accounts of Aurelius M.

EDCS-06100085.text_edition: \n Praesente et Rufino consulibus ex ratione Aureli Georgi\n
EDCS-06100085.translation: In the consulship of Praesens and Rufinus, from the accounts of Aurelius Georgius.
EDCS-06100085.summary: This inscription records a date based on the consulship of Praesens and Rufinus and mentions accounts of Aurelius Georgius.

EDCS-06100086.text_edition: \n Marco Aurelio Vero III et Lucio Vaelio sic Caesare II consulibus ex ratione Aureli Lis\n
EDCS-06100086.translation: In the third consulship of Marcus Aurelius Verus and the second consulship of Caesar Lucius Velius Paulus, from the accounts of Aurelius Lis.
EDCS-06100086.summary: This inscription records a date based on the consulships of Marcus Aurelius Verus III and Lucius Velius Paulus II and mentions accounts of Aurelius Lis.

EDCS-06100087.text_edition: \n Laeliano Pastore consulibus ex ratione Sexti et \n
EDCS-06100087.translation: During the consulship of Laelianus Pastor, according to the accounts of Sextus and...
EDCS-06100087.summary: This inscription records financial accounts from the consulship of Laelianus Pastor.

EDCS-06100088.text_edition: \n Hermolai Macrino et Celso consulibus ex ratione Sextti et Hermae\n
EDCS-06100088.translation: During the consulship of Hermolaius, Macrinus, and Celsus, according to the accounts of Sextus and Herma...
EDCS-06100088.summary: This inscription details financial accounts from the consulship of Hermolaius, Macrinus, and Celsus.

EDCS-06100089.summary: This inscription records an event or decree during the consulship of Augurinus.

EDCS-06100090.text_edition: \n Macrino et Celso consulibus ex ratione Aureli Suavis\n
EDCS-06100090.translation: During the consulship of Macrinus and Celsus, according to the accounts of Aurelius Suavis...
EDCS-06100090.summary: This inscription pertains to financial accounts managed by Aurelius Suavis during the consulship of Macrinus and Celsus.

EDCS-06100097.text_edition: \n Publio Sulpicio Luci filio Oufentina Peregrino equiti speculatori\n
EDCS-06100097.translation: To Publius Sulpicius Peregrinus, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe, a cavalryman and bodyguard...
EDCS-06100097.summary: This inscription honors Publius Sulpicius Peregrinus, a cavalryman and bodyguard, likely a dedication or memorial.

EDCS-06100098.text_edition: \n Claudiae Frequenti feminae simplicissimae vixit annos LX Honoratus publicus sodalium Augustalium nutrici suae bene merenti\n
EDCS-06100098.translation: To Claudia Frequens, a most simple woman, who lived 60 years. Honoratus, a public official of the Augustales association, to his well-deserving nurse...
EDCS-06100098.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Honoratus to his nurse, Claudia Frequens, who lived 60 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-06100099.summary: This inscription records a dedication or monument initiated by Gaius Eppa for himself and another person.

EDCS-06100101.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Caio Valerio Maxentio Felici Invicto et aeterno Augusto XCVII\n
EDCS-06100101.translation: To our Lord Gaius Valerius Maxentius, the Happy, Unconquered, and Eternal Augustus, 97...
EDCS-06100101.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Maxentius, likely from the early 4th century CE, possibly related to a numerical count or measure.

EDCS-06100102.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clodiae Cami Caius Asinius Primigenius coniugi pientissimae fecit\n
EDCS-06100102.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Asinius Primigenius made this for his most dutiful wife, Clodia Camia...
EDCS-06100102.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Asinius Primigenius to his wife, Clodia Camia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100104.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cantinianus vixit annis numero XIII diebus XV Cirrus pater fecit\n
EDCS-06100104.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Cantinianus lived 13 years and 15 days. Cirrus, his father, made this...
EDCS-06100104.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Cantinianus, dedicated by his father Cirrus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100105.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Vibio Pederoti vixit annos XX menses VIII dies \n
EDCS-06100105.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius Vibius Pederotus lived for 20 years, 8 months, and [number] days.
EDCS-06100105.summary: A funerary dedication to Caius Vibius Pederotus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-06100106.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix [et] [Publius Septimius Geta] [nobilissimus Caesar] viam quae ducit a Minturnis ad Aquas Vescinas sua pequnia sic straverunt\n
EDCS-06100106.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix [and] [Publius Septimius Geta] [most noble Caesar] paved the road that leads from Minturnae to Aquae Vescinas with their own money.
EDCS-06100106.summary: An imperial dedication by Septimius Severus and Caracalla (and possibly Geta) recording the paving of a road from Minturnae to Aquae Vescinas, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06100107.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix [et] [Publius Septimius Geta] [nobilissimus Caesar] viam quae ducit a Minturnis ad Aquas Vescinas sua pecunia straverunt\n
EDCS-06100107.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix [and] [Publius Septimius Geta] [most noble Caesar] paved the road that leads from Minturnae to Aquae Vescinas with their own money.
EDCS-06100107.summary: An imperial dedication by Septimius Severus and Caracalla (and possibly Geta) recording the paving of a road from Minturnae to Aquae Vescinas, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06100109.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Domitia Elate.
EDCS-06100109.summary: A funerary dedication to Domitia Elate.

EDCS-06100113.summary: A dedication to the deified Emperor Hadrian, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-06100115.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate X imperatori II consuli IIII patri patriae colonia Iulia Augusta Venafrum\n
EDCS-06100115.translation: To Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan the Parthian, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontiff, holding tribunician power for the 10th time, acclaimed emperor twice, consul four times, father of the fatherland, from the colony Julia Augusta Venafrum.
EDCS-06100115.summary: An honorific inscription from Venafrum to Emperor Antoninus Pius, detailing his titles and parentage, likely from the period of his 10th tribunician power (146-147 CE).

EDCS-06100118.summary: The name Lucius Critonius Salvillus, possibly a dedication or a tomb marker.

EDCS-06100119.text_edition: \n Marco Equitio Marci liberto Primo Marco Equitio Marci liberto Hilaro Marco Equitio MMarcorum liberto Dardano Cassiae Marci libertae Rufae\n
EDCS-06100119.translation: To Marcus Equitius Primus, freedman of Marcus; to Marcus Equitius Hilarus, freedman of Marcus; to [Dardanus?], freedman of Marcus, [son?] of Cassia Rufa, freedwoman of Marcus.
EDCS-06100119.summary: A dedication or funerary record mentioning several freedmen and their patrons or family members.

EDCS-06100121.text_edition: \n Ossa hic sita sunt Fausti Plini Marci Decuma sic mater fecit\n
EDCS-06100121.translation: The bones are buried here. Faustus [left them?] for Plinius. Marcus Decumus's mother did this.
EDCS-06100121.summary: A funerary inscription indicating the burial place of Faustus and mentioning his mother and potentially a relative named Marcus Decumus.

EDCS-06100130.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Loreiae Marci Vitali Lucius Iulius Gemellus pater coniugi karissimae\n
EDCS-06100130.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Loreia, wife of Marcus Vitalis. Lucius Iulius Gemellus, her husband, [erected this] for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-06100130.summary: A funerary dedication from Lucius Iulius Gemellus to his wife Loreia, daughter of Marcus Vitalis.

EDCS-06100131.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of a husband, Aulus Marius.

EDCS-06100132.text_edition: \n Luci Plauti Terti ossa hic sita sunt\n
EDCS-06100132.translation: The bones of Lucius Plautius Tertius lie here.
EDCS-06100132.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Lucius Plautius Tertius.

EDCS-06100134.summary: This dedication records a dedication to Sextia, a freedwoman of Quintus.

EDCS-06100136.text_edition: \n Caius Naevius Cai libertus Fronto vixit annos LXIII\n
EDCS-06100136.translation: Gaius Naevius Fronto, freedman of Gaius, lived 63 years.
EDCS-06100136.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Gaius Naevius Fronto, a freedman.

EDCS-06100138.translation: Gaius Messenius Eunonus in the curia.
EDCS-06100138.summary: This inscription records Gaius Messenius Eunonus' presence or activity in the curia (senate house or meeting hall).

EDCS-06100139.translation: We ask Mamia Anicetus in the Augustan curia.
EDCS-06100139.summary: This inscription appears to be a request or summons related to Mamia Anicetus within the Augustan curia.

EDCS-06100140.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording the name and filiation of Lucius Arrius.

EDCS-06100141.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording the name and filiation of Arrius.

EDCS-06100143.translation: To cucumas // Asses 4 per sextarius, asses 3 per sextarius, asses 3 per sextarius, asses 2 half per sextarius.
EDCS-06100143.summary: This inscription appears to be a price list or record of sale for some commodity, possibly wine, at different rates per sextarius.

EDCS-06100144.translation: Nola, the scribe, from Capua in April.
EDCS-06100144.summary: This inscription records the name of a scribe, Nola, and his origin from Capua, possibly dating to April.

EDCS-06100158.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gellius Agathopus Besiae Speni coniugi karissimae fecit\n
EDCS-06100158.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gellius Agathopus made this for his dearest wife, Besia Speni.
EDCS-06100158.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to a wife by her husband, Gellius Agathopus.

EDCS-06100193.text_edition: \n Caio Mario |(mulieris) liberto Atyi sexviro Augustali Caio Mario |(mulieris) liberto Maturo Mariae Cai libertae Pieridi\n
EDCS-06100193.translation: To Gaius Marius Atyus, freedman of a woman, Augustal sexvir, and to Gaius Marius Maturus, freedman of a woman, and to Pieris, freedwoman of Maria.
EDCS-06100193.summary: This is an epitaph commemorating multiple individuals, possibly family members, including a freedman and an Augustal sexvir.

EDCS-06500003.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Lucretius Alexander made this for his dearest brother, Gaius Julius Alexander, a soldier of the 11th Urban Cohort, who lived 25 years. [He was] well-deserving. On the 22nd.
EDCS-06500003.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Julius Alexander, a soldier in the 11th Urban Cohort, and was erected by his brother.

EDCS-06500004.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. His heirs made this for Gaius Julius Germanus, a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, a trireme sailor on the Tigris, who lived 40 years. [He was] well-deserving.
EDCS-06500004.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Julius Germanus, a sailor in the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, and was erected by his heirs.

EDCS-06500006.summary: This is a dedication to Numerius Plaetorius Successus, who held the rank of Augustalis.

EDCS-06500007.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus Tiberio Claudio Diogenis filio Quirina Diogeni vixit nnis LXXXIIII mensibus X diebus VI Claudia Vera liberta patrono bene merenti medico clinico sibi posterisque eius\n
EDCS-06500007.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Claudia Vera, his freedwoman, made this for her patron, Diogenes, son of Tiberius Claudius Diogenes, a clinician physician, who lived 84 years, 10 months, and 6 days, well-deserving, for himself and his descendants.
EDCS-06500007.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Diogenes, a physician, and was erected by his freedwoman, Claudia Vera.

EDCS-06500011.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Salluvia Felicissima made this for her dearest freedman, Acheronnius Sabinus, who lived 3 years, 11 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-06500011.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Acheronnius Sabinus, a freedman, and was erected by Salluvia Felicissima.

EDCS-06500012.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. The parents made this for their sweetest daughter, Aelia Minervina, who lived 2 years, 4 months, and 2 days.
EDCS-06500012.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aelia Minervina, a young daughter, and was erected by her parents.

EDCS-06500014.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Her mother erected this monument for Caecina Saturnina, who lived 18 years and 13 days; her friend made the tomb for her. For the one who lived 1 year, 4 months, he made [this] well-deserving.
EDCS-06500014.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caecina Saturnina and another individual, with the monument erected by her mother and the tomb made by a friend.

EDCS-06500016.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caesia Iunia made this for her patroness, Caesaea Eucarpia, well-deserving.
EDCS-06500016.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caesaea Eucarpia and was erected by her patroness, Caesia Iunia.

EDCS-06500017.translation: Calpurnia made this for her freedwoman Myrsine Prima, who lived 12 years.
EDCS-06500017.summary: A funerary dedication by Calpurnia for her freedwoman Myrsine Prima, who died at age 12.

EDCS-06500019.summary: A funerary epitaph for Chreste, who died at age 20.

EDCS-06500020.translation: To the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Narcissus lived 30 years. His mother, Claudia Prima, set this up for her most devoted son.
EDCS-06500020.summary: A funerary dedication by Claudia Prima for her son Tiberius Claudius Narcissus, who died at age 30.

EDCS-06500021.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudia Eutychia set up this monument for her mother Claudia Zosima, who deserved well. She lived [---] years.
EDCS-06500021.summary: A funerary dedication by Claudia Eutychia for her mother Claudia Zosima, who is described as deserving well.

EDCS-06500022.translation: To the departed spirits. Clodia Ursula lived 25 years.
EDCS-06500022.summary: A funerary epitaph for Clodia Ursula, who died at age 25.

EDCS-06500026.translation: To the departed spirits. Eclectes lived 10 years. Her mother made this for her daughter.
EDCS-06500026.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother for her daughter Eclectes, who died at age 10.

EDCS-06500027.summary: A damaged inscription possibly related to a funerary monument for Epillia.

EDCS-06500029.translation: To the departed spirits. Philetus made this for his wife Firmilla, who deserved well. She lived 32 years.
EDCS-06500029.summary: A funerary dedication by Philetus for his wife Firmilla, who died at age 32 and is described as deserving well.

EDCS-06500030.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia [---] made this for her slave Pyrrhicus, who deserved well.
EDCS-06500030.summary: A funerary dedication by Flavia for her well-deserving slave Pyrrhicus.

EDCS-06500032.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Irius Erasinus, who died at age 9.

EDCS-06500035.translation: To Maxima, daughter of Simon, who lived 16 years, 2 months, and 7 days. Simon made [this] for his most loving daughter.
EDCS-06500035.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young woman named Maxima, erected by her father Simon, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06500039.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Numerius Sittius Primigenius lived 24 years, 3 months, and 12 days. Thetis Miserina lived 22 years, 6 months, and 7 days. The patroness Sittia Aula made [this] for her freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-06500039.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the ages of two individuals, Numerius Sittius Primigenius and Thetis Miserina, with an indication that Sittia Aula dedicated it for her freed dependents.

EDCS-06500040.translation: To Ovia Autodice, who lived 21 years.
EDCS-06500040.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Ovia Autodice, recording her name and age at death, likely Roman Imperial.

EDCS-06500041.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Plautia Cronie made [this] for her well-deserving husband, Lucius Plautius Hyginus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-06500041.summary: A funerary epitaph indicating that Plautia Cronie dedicated a monument to her husband, Lucius Plautius Hyginus, a freedman.

EDCS-06500042.translation: Gnaeus Pompeius Philadelphus made [this] for himself, and for Pompeia Zosima, and for his son Iucundus, and for his own [family].
EDCS-06500042.summary: A funerary epitaph indicating that Gnaeus Pompeius Philadelphus dedicated a tomb for himself, his wife Pompeia Zosima, their son Iucundus, and other family members.

EDCS-06500044.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Baleria Berecunda made [this] for her well-deserving husband, Ulpius Eutychetus, who lived 27 years, 6 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-06500044.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpius Eutychetus, detailing his age at death and noting that his wife Baleria Berecunda erected the monument.

EDCS-06500056.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Calatoriae Constantinae vixit annos XVIIII menses II dies XXIII Marcus Calatorius Successus filiae pientissimae\n
EDCS-06500056.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Calatorius Successus made [this] for his most dutiful daughter, Calatoria Constantina, who lived 17 years, 2 months, and 23 days.
EDCS-06500056.summary: A funerary epitaph for Calatoria Constantina, who died young, erected by her father Marcus Calatorius Successus.

EDCS-06500058.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Lucretius Nilas made [this] for his well-deserving wife, Flavia Agathemeris.
EDCS-06500058.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Quintus Lucretius Nilas to his wife, Flavia Agathemeris.

EDCS-06500060.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Quintus Messius Alexander.
EDCS-06500060.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Quintus Messius Alexander.

EDCS-06500061.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. His wife Memmia Zosime and his freedman Claudius Graphicus made [this] for their well-deserving Ti(berius) Claudius Ampliatus, who lived 80 years.
EDCS-06500061.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Ampliatus, erected by his wife Memmia Zosime and his freedman Claudius Graphicus.

EDCS-06500068.translation: To the divine spirit of Atenius, son of Avenaria, who lived 3 years, 4 months, and 25 days. Aulus Atinius [name unclear] and TESYCO father [name unclear]. Aulus Atinius [name unclear], son of Mineria, and Enoxia, mother [name unclear], made this for ASI and father [name unclear].
EDCS-06500068.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atenius, with his parents Aulus Atinius and Enoxia mentioned as dedicants, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-06500070.translation: To the divine spirits of Thallia Sedulina, who lived 23 years, 9 months, and 18 days. Photinus, a home-born slave of our Emperor, placed this for his most rare and most chaste wife, and [[for himself]] <<for his daughter>>.
EDCS-06500070.summary: A funerary inscription for Thallia Sedulina, dedicated by her husband Photinus, a slave of the emperor, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-06500071.translation: To the divine spirits of Lucius Coponius Celer, son of [name unclear]. Lucius Coponius [name unclear] lived 41 years.
EDCS-06500071.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Coponius Celer, with his father Lucius Coponius also mentioned, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-06500073.text_edition: \n Martialis vixsit sic nnm unum mensses sic sexs sic et dies XII\n
EDCS-06500073.translation: Martialis lived for one year, six months, and 12 days.
EDCS-06500073.summary: A very brief funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a child named Martialis, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-06500074.text_edition: \n Calendioni coniugi karissimo et bene merenti Pelagia fecit\n
EDCS-06500074.translation: Pelagia made this for her most beloved and deserving husband, Calendio.
EDCS-06500074.summary: A funerary or commemorative inscription dedicated by Pelagia to her husband Calendio, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-06500075.translation: To the divine spirits of Marcia Iusta, daughter of Lucius. Iustus the father [dedicated this].
EDCS-06500075.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcia Iusta, dedicated by her father Iustus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-06500077.translation: To Emperor our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, son of the divine Constantius. Year 16.
EDCS-06500077.summary: An honorific inscription dedicating something to Emperor Constantine I, likely commemorating a public work or event in the 16th year of his reign (around 322/323 CE).

EDCS-07000149.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Salinator Fortunatus Salinatore sic Ianuara sic fecit alumnae suae bene merenti\n
EDCS-07000149.translation: To the divine spirits. Marcus Salinator Fortunatus made this for his well-deserving foster-daughter Salinator Ianuara.
EDCS-07000149.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Salinator Fortunatus to his foster-daughter Ianuara, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07000154.text_edition: \n Calpetanius Trophimus sevir Augustalis idem quinquennalis fecit sivi sic et suis libertis libertabusque posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-07000154.translation: Calpetanius Trophimus, a *sevir Augustalis* and also *quinquennalis*, made this for himself and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-07000154.summary: A funerary inscription by Calpetanius Trophimus, a local magistrate (*sevir Augustalis* and *quinquennalis*), for himself and his dependents, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07000157.text_edition: \n Caius Cartilius Cai et mulieris lib(ertus) Onesimus Marcus Antistius Marci libertus Epagathus et Cartilia Cai filia Iusta et Cartilia Cai et Marci liberta Venusta fecerunt sibi et suis et Publio Nonio Publi filio Quirina Iusto decurioni libertis libertabus posterisque eorum hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur in fronte pedes XXX in agro pedes XXVIISsemis []\n
EDCS-07000157.translation: Gaius Cartilius Onesimus, freedman of Gaius and his wife; Marcus Antistius Epagathus, freedman of Marcus; and Cartilia Iusta, daughter of Gaius and Cartilia; and Cartilia Venusta, freedwoman of Gaius and Marcus, made this for themselves and their relatives, and for Publius Nonius Iustus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, decurion, and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. This monument shall not pass to an heir. Frontage: 30 feet; in the field: 27.5 feet.
EDCS-07000157.summary: A funerary monument inscription by multiple individuals, including freedmen and family members, establishing perpetual burial rights and specifying dimensions, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07000161.text_edition: \n Caius Curtius Terti libertus Felix Augustalis fecit sibi et Curtiae Ianuariae libertae suae et suis libertis libertabusque posterisque eorum omnibus in fronte pedes XXXVII in agro pedes XXV\n
EDCS-07000161.translation: Caius Curtius Felix, freedman of Terti, an Augustalis, made this for himself and for Curtia Ianuaria, his freedwoman, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants, with a front of 37 feet and a depth of 25 feet.
EDCS-07000161.summary: A private funerary monument, likely a tomb or columbarium, erected by a freedman and his freedwoman, specifying dimensions for their burial plot.

EDCS-07000162.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Egnatia Publi filia Aescennia Procula flaminica divae Faustinae hic sita est Marcus Modius Marci filius Successianus honoribus functus coniugi pudicissimae fecit\n
EDCS-07000162.translation: To the Divine Shades. Egnatia Procula, daughter of Publius, of the Aescennia family, priestess of the deified Faustina, is buried here. Marcus Modius Successianus, son of Marcus, who held public office, made this for his most virtuous wife.
EDCS-07000162.summary: A tombstone set up by a husband for his deceased wife, who held a religious office, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07000165.text_edition: \n liberta N fecit sibi et lio Auli et Cai liberto Euchro seviro Augustali coniugi et colliberto suo bene merenti et Bassillae filiae et Secundae libertae et Stephano Hermetis liberto mei liberti et Eutychiae libertae et Speratae libertae et Nomadi libertae libertis libertabus posterisque eorum in fronte pedes X in agro pedes XXVSsemis\n
EDCS-07000165.translation: The freedwoman N made this for herself and for [---], freedman of Aulus, and for Euchro, freedman of Caius, her husband and fellow freedman, who deserved well, and for her daughter Bassilla, and for Secunda, her freedwoman, and for Stephano, freedman of my freedman, and for Eutyche, her freedwoman, and for Sperata, her freedwoman, and for Nomus, her freedwoman, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants, with a front of 10 feet and a depth of 20.5 feet.
EDCS-07000165.summary: A private funerary monument likely indicating a burial plot purchased by a freedwoman for herself and a complex network of relatives and freedpersons.

EDCS-07000167.text_edition: \n Iuniae Titi Pompei Dii filiae At Marco Manlio Stephano Augustali Titiae Pyrallidi Marcus Manlius Diligens Augustalis quinquennalis fecit sibi et Epaphrodito liberto Augustali quinquennali procuratori suo Titiae Verae uxori libertis libertabus suis eis quos testamento aut codicillis manumisero posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-07000167.translation: For Iunia At [daughter] of Titus Pompei Dii, for Marcus Manlius Stephanos, Augustalis, and Titia Pyrallis. Marcus Manlius Diligens, Augustalis, quinquennial, made this for himself and for Epaphroditus, his Augustalis freedman and quinquennial procurator, for his wife Titia Vera, for his freedmen and freedwomen whom I shall manumit by will or codicils, and for their descendants.
EDCS-07000167.summary: A funerary monument erected by a local official (Augustalis, quinquennial) for himself, his wife, his freedman, and others, specifying future manumissions and descendants.

EDCS-07000170.text_edition: \n Publio Celerio Publi filio Palatina Amando decreto decurionum decurio adlectus hunc decuriones funere publico efferendum censuerunt eique honores omnes decreverunt et turis pondo XX pater honore usus inpensam remisit vixit annos XIIX menses XI dies XIIX Publius Celerius Publi libertus Chryseros et Scantia Lanthanusa parentes fecerunt sibi et suis libertis libertabus posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-07000170.translation: The decurions decreed that Publius Celerius Amandus, son of Publius, of the Palatina tribe, a decurion elected by decree of the decurions, should be carried with public funeral rites, and they decreed all honors for him, and 20 pounds of incense. His father, having used this honor, remitted the expense. He lived 12 years, 11 months, 18 days. Publius Celerius Chryseros, freedman of Publius, and Scantia Lanthusa, his parents, made this for themselves and their kin, freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-07000170.summary: A funerary monument for a young boy honored with public funeral rites by the town council, paid for by his parents who also included themselves and their descendants.

EDCS-07000171.text_edition: \n Quintus Plarius Herclianus sevir Augustalis item quinquennalis fecit sibi et Nasenniae Charitaeni sic coniugi suae et libertis libertabusque posterisquem sic eorum in fronte pedes cum ariam sic adherente XXV in agro pedes LII et Noviae Chrysidi coniugi\n
EDCS-07000171.translation: Quintus Plarius Herclianus, sevir Augustalis and also quinquennial, made this for himself and for Nasennia Charitaena, his wife, and their descendants, with a front of 25 feet, including the adjoining area, and a depth of 52 feet, and for Novia Chrysida, his wife.
EDCS-07000171.summary: A private funerary monument specifying dimensions for a burial plot, erected by a sevir Augustalis for himself, his wives, and descendants.

EDCS-07000175.text_edition: \n Caius Aemilius Cai filius Palatina Aus decurio coloniae Ostiensis IIvir sacerdos Geni coloniae flamen divi Antonini G sodalis Herculanus flamen Romae et Augusti \n
EDCS-07000175.translation: Caius Aemilius, son of Caius, of the Palatina tribe, a decurion of the colony of Ostia, formerly IIvir, priest of the Genius of the colony, priest of the deified Antoninus, and a sodalis Herculanus, priest of Rome and of Augustus.
EDCS-07000175.summary: An honorific inscription for a prominent local magistrate and priest in the colony of Ostia, detailing his various civic and religious offices.

EDCS-07000176.text_edition: \n Publius Attius Publi et |(mulieris) libertus Felicio sevir Augustalis idem quinquennalis fecit sibi et Siliae libertae Helene uxori et Attiae Publi filiae Vitali et Publio Attio Publi filio Palatina Siliano praetori sacris Volkani faciundis et Caio Attio Palatina Attiano et Attiae Saturninae sorori libertis libertabus posterisque eorum omnium in fronte pedes XXXISsemis in agro pedes XX Publio Attio Publi et |(mulieris) liberto Tyrano et Publio Attio Publi filio Palatina Felici\n
EDCS-07000176.translation: Publius Attius Felicio, freedman of Publius and [his wife], sevir Augustalis and also quinquennial, made this for himself and for Helena, his freedwoman and wife, and for his daughter Attia Vitalis, daughter of Publius, and for Publius Attius Silianus, son of Publius, of the Palatina tribe, praetor, responsible for the rites of Vulcan, and for Caius Attius Attianus and his sister Attia Saturnina, for their freedmen, freedwomen, and all their descendants, with a front of 30.5 feet and a depth of 20 feet, and for Publius Attius Tyrannus, freedman of Publius and [his wife], and for Publius Attius Felix, son of Publius, of the Palatina tribe.
EDCS-07000176.summary: A funerary monument with significant dimensions, erected by a sevir Augustalis and his wife for themselves, children, siblings, and extensive family including freedpersons, detailing multiple beneficiaries.

EDCS-07000177.text_edition: \n Caio Calpetano Trofimo seviro Augustali idem sic quinquennali\n
EDCS-07000177.translation: For Caius Calpetanus Trofimus, sevir Augustalis and also quinquennial.
EDCS-07000177.summary: A very brief inscription likely marking a tomb or honorific dedication for a sevir Augustalis.

EDCS-07000178.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Egriliae Clusinae item libertis libertabusque qui quaeve iam sunt qui quaeve futuri Aulus Egrilius Hilarus coactor argentarius sevir Augustalis idem quinquennalis quinquennalis collegii fabrum tignuariorum Ostiensium lustri XXVI et Egrilia Iustina patroni fecerunt et posterisque eorum earumve in fronte pedes XIII in agro pedes XXV\n
EDCS-07000178.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aulus Egrilius Hilarus, a tax collector and silversmith, sevir Augustalis, also quinquennial of the college of carpenters of Ostia for 26 lustra, and Egrilia Iustina, patrons, made this for Clusina, [their] freedwoman, and for [their] freedmen and freedwomen, who now are or hereafter shall be, and for their descendants, with a front of 13 feet and a depth of 25 feet.
EDCS-07000178.summary: A funerary monument erected by patrons, a tax collector and a sevir Augustalis, for their freedwoman and future freedpersons, indicating the length of service of one of the patrons in a trade guild.

EDCS-07000188.text_edition: \n Marco Umbilio Marci filio Arnensi Maximino praetextato clarissimo puero patrono coloniae sacerdoti Geni coloniae Publius Calpurnius Princeps equo publico omnibus honoribus functus educator dedicata Kalendis Martiis Severo et Victorino consulibus\n
EDCS-07000188.translation: To Marcus Umbilio Maximinus, son of Marcus of the Arnensian tribe, a renowned boy, patron of the colony, priest of the Genius of the colony, who has held all offices, a Roman knight. Publius Calpurnius Princeps, who has performed all offices, dedicated this [monument] on the Kalends of March, when Severus and Victorinus were consuls.
EDCS-07000188.summary: This is a dedication of a monument by Publius Calpurnius Princeps to Marcus Umbilio Maximinus, a young patron and priest of the colony, likely from the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07000191.text_edition: \n Virtute in hostes pietate erga suos aequiperanti triumphalem patrem domino nostro Flavio Iulio Constanti victori maximo semper Augusto Aurelius Avianius Symmachus vir clarissimus praefectus annonae urbis Romae\n
EDCS-07000191.translation: To our lord Flavius Julius Constantius, victor, greatest, ever Augustus, for his virtue against enemies and his piety towards his own, equal to the triumphal father, Aurelius Avianius Symmachus, a most distinguished man, prefect of the grain supply of the city of Rome.
EDCS-07000191.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated by Aurelius Avianius Symmachus, a prefect of Rome, to the emperor Constantius, likely from the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-07000195.text_edition: \n Lucius Quinctius Luci libertus Nicephorus machinator Lucius Quinctius Luci libertus Achiba Quinctia Luci liberta Salvia Lucius Quinctius Luci libertus Alexa hoc monumentum Quinctia Luci liberta Salvia de sua pecunia fecit Lucius Quinctius |(mulieris) libertus Hyla \n
EDCS-07000195.translation: Lucius Quinctius Nicephorus, freedman of Lucius, engineer; Lucius Quinctius Achiba, freedman of Lucius; Quinctia Salvia, freedwoman of Lucius. Quinctia Salvia, freedwoman of Lucius, made this monument with her own money. Lucius Hyla, freedman of [a woman].
EDCS-07000195.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription recording the makers and benefactors, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07000198.text_edition: \n Decimus Folius Decimi libertus Mela Decimus Critonius Decimi et |(mulieris) libertus Dionysius Critonia Decimi et |(mulieris) liberta Chila Critonia |(mulieris) liberta Auge in fronte pedes XXV in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-07000198.translation: Decimus Folius Mela, freedman of Decimus; Decimus Dionysius, freedman of Decimus and [a woman]; Critonia, freedwoman of Decimus and [a woman]; Critonia Auge, freedwoman of [a woman]. 25 feet in front, 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-07000198.summary: This inscription records property boundaries for a tomb, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07000202.text_edition: \n Aedem Dianae pro salute et reditu Luci Veri nostri Graphicus libertus et Asclepiacus filius ex voto restituerunt\n
EDCS-07000202.translation: Graphicus, freedman of Lucius Verus, and his son Asclepiacus, restored this temple to Diana in fulfillment of a vow for the safety and return of our Lucius Verus.
EDCS-07000202.summary: This is a dedication for the restoration of a temple to Diana by a freedman and his son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07000203.text_edition: \n Lucius Ucus Luci libertus Strato Marcus Largius Marci libertus Nicephor Apollonius Fundani Cai servus Antiochus Aureli Marci servus Mena publicus servus Apollonius Samiari Auli servus Artemo Sabidieni sic Falis<c=L>ei sic Spuri servus Philippus Caecili Luci servus Agenor Plauti Cai servus Lucrio Saufei Auli servus Salvius Galli Ovi servus Chrestio Eppiorum servus \n
EDCS-07000203.translation: Lucius Strato, freedman of Lucius Ucus; Marcus Nicephor, freedman of Marcus Largius; Apollonius, slave of Caius Fundanius; Antiochus Mena, slave of Marcus Aurelius; Apollonius, public slave; Artemo, slave of Samiarus Aulus; Philippus, slave of Spurius Faliscius; Agenor, slave of Caius Plautius; Salvius, slave of Aulus Saufeius; Chrestio, slave of Ovius Gallus.
EDCS-07000203.summary: This inscription lists numerous slaves, possibly recording ownership or a communal work group, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07000204.text_edition: \n i Cai liberti Aselli Helvia Clarice mater\n
EDCS-07000204.translation: Helvia Clarice, mother of Caius Asellus, freedman.
EDCS-07000204.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Helvia Clarice, mother of a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07000206.text_edition: \n Titus Allius Meoptolomus sic sibi et Annidiae Eucheni in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-07000206.translation: Titus Allius Meoptolomus [dedicated this] for himself and Annidia Eucheni. 12 feet in front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-07000206.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording dimensions for a tomb, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07000207.text_edition: \n ------ Pet]illi Hermae Staiae Luci libertae P Luci Petilli Luci liberti Hilari in agro pedes \n
EDCS-07000207.translation: [To] Hermas, freedman of Petillius; Statius, freedman of Lucius Petillius Hilarius. In the field [XX feet].
EDCS-07000207.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment recording names and dimensions, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07000208.text_edition: \n Vivit Cai Numisi |(mulieris) liberti Chresti in fronte pedes XIII in agro pedes XII\n
EDCS-07000208.translation: Caius Numisius Chrestus, freedman of [a woman], lives. 13 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-07000208.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording dimensions for a tomb, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07000209.text_edition: \n Luci Pomponi Te Mattiae Feli Luci Matti Familiaris\n
EDCS-07000209.translation: Lucius Pomponius Felix, son of Titus, loyal companion of Lucius Matti.
EDCS-07000209.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication for Lucius Pomponius Felix, identified as a loyal companion.

EDCS-07000211.text_edition: \n Severus Marci Sei Varani servus vixit annos III- Serenus Marci Sei Varani dispensator et Anthusa filio fec(erunt)\n
EDCS-07000211.translation: Severus, slave of Marcus Seius Varanus, lived for 3 years. Serenus, steward of Marcus Seius Varanus, and Anthusa made (this) for their son.
EDCS-07000211.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Serenus and Anthusa for their son Severus, who was also the slave of Marcus Seius Varanus.

EDCS-07000213.text_edition: \n Quinto liberto Tigris viro carissimo fecit\n
EDCS-07000213.translation: Tigris made (this) for Quintus, her dearest freedman.
EDCS-07000213.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Tigris to her freedman Quintus.

EDCS-07000214.summary: This inscription likely records the ownership or burial of Publius Petronius Heron.

EDCS-07000216.text_edition: \n Luci Orbi Luci liberti Dioscuri leibertis leibertabusque sueis Marci Capri Marci liberti Philodami leibertis leibertabusque sueis Aniae |(mulieris) libertae Suriscae \n
EDCS-07000216.translation: Lucius Orbius, freedman of Lucius, for his freedmen and their freedmen; Marcus Caprinus, freedman of Marcus, for his freedmen and their freedmen; for Ania, freedwoman of [?], Surisca.
EDCS-07000216.summary: This is a complex funerary dedication establishing burial rights for multiple freedmen and their descendants.

EDCS-07000221.text_edition: \n Quintus Aeclanius Proximus et Numerius Hernelius Felix IIIIviri quinquennales aequitatem faciendam curaverunt ex pecunia publica\n
EDCS-07000221.translation: The four quinquennial magistrates, Quintus Aeclanius Proximus and Numerius Hernelius Felix, undertook the work of maintaining equality using public funds.
EDCS-07000221.summary: This inscription records a public works project for maintaining equality, commissioned by two quinquennial magistrates.

EDCS-07000225.text_edition: \n simo sibi et Tito Veturio Cnaei filio Lucio Catieno Lucifero\n
EDCS-07000225.translation: Simus for himself and for Titus Veturius, son of Cnaeus, and for Lucius Catienus Lucifer.
EDCS-07000225.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Simus for himself and for Titus Veturius and Lucius Catienus Lucifer.

EDCS-07000226.text_edition: \n Caius Sextius Suavis sibi et Pomponiae Titi libertae Optatae et suis\n
EDCS-07000226.translation: Gaius Sextius Suavis for himself and for Pomponia Optata, freedwoman of Titus, and for his own (family).
EDCS-07000226.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Sextius Suavis for himself, his wife Pomponia Optata, and his family.

EDCS-07000227.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Plotio Rustico qui vixit\n
EDCS-07000227.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Publius Plotus Rusticus, who lived...
EDCS-07000227.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Publius Plotus Rusticus.

EDCS-07000228.text_edition: \n Efi Cai servi Tiberio Claudio Publio Quinctilio consulibus Faustus Suetti Cai servus Princeps Plaesci Luci servus magistri Mercuriales dederunt Libeso sculpsit \n
EDCS-07000228.translation: During the consulship of Caius Efi, slave of Tiberius Claudius Publius Quinctilius, Faustus, slave of Caius Suettius, chief steward of Lucius Plaescius, gave (this) under the supervision of the Mercuriales. Libes sculpted.
EDCS-07000228.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by slaves under specific magistracies during a particular consulship, with the sculptor identified.

EDCS-07000229.translation: Nicephorus, freedman of Decimus Mammius Marcus.
EDCS-07000229.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedman named Nicephorus.

EDCS-07000230.text_edition: \n Vivi fecerunt Caius Pomponius Fortunatus et Caediciae He coniugi posterisque in fronte pedes XXIIII in agro pedes XII\n
EDCS-07000230.translation: Caius Pomponius Fortunatus and Caedicia [made this] for her husband and their descendants; in front 24 feet, in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-07000230.summary: This funerary inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land purchased by Caius Pomponius Fortunatus for his wife and descendants.

EDCS-07000231.text_edition: \n Vivit Luci Minati Luci filii Lucrionis in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XII\n
EDCS-07000231.translation: Lucius Minatius Lucrio, son of Lucius, is alive; in front 12 feet, in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-07000231.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot of 12x12 feet, stating that Lucius Minatius Lucrio is still alive.

EDCS-07000233.text_edition: \n Quinti Folei Quinti filii Cornelia in fronte pedes XIII in agro pedes XV\n
EDCS-07000233.translation: Of Quintus Foleius, son of Quintus, of the Cornelia tribe; in front 13 feet, in the field 15 feet.
EDCS-07000233.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a burial plot of 13x15 feet belonging to Quintus Foleius.

EDCS-07000234.text_edition: \n Cai Vinuci Cai Luci liberti Crysis Cai Vinuci Albani vivunt in fronte pedes XIIII in agro pedes XVI\n
EDCS-07000234.translation: Caius Vinucius Crysis, freedman of Caius Lucius, and Caius Vinucius Albanus, are alive; in front 14 feet, in the field 16 feet.
EDCS-07000234.summary: This funerary inscription records that Caius Vinucius Crysis and Caius Vinucius Albanus are alive and specifies the dimensions of their plot.

EDCS-07000236.text_edition: \n Lucius Volumnius Secundus et Curtiliae Liberae contubernali et Trophimo filio posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-07000236.translation: Lucius Volumnius Secundus and [made this] for Curtilia Libera, his companion, and Trophimus, their son, and their descendants.
EDCS-07000236.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a burial plot by Lucius Volumnius Secundus for his companion Curtilia Libera, their son Trophimus, and their descendants.

EDCS-07000237.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Atiliae Marci filiae Restitutae Marco Atilio Restituto patri aedili IIviro quaestori et Pompeiae Coene matri Liberalis et Profuturus patronis bene merentibus fecerunt libertis libertabusque posterisque suis\n
EDCS-07000237.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Atilia Restituta, daughter of Marcus, who was father, aedile, duovir, and quaestor, and for Pompeia Mater Liberalis, and for Profuturus, patrons who deserved well, [they made this] for their freedmen, freedwomen, and descendants.
EDCS-07000237.summary: This elaborate funerary inscription is dedicated to Atilia Restituta and her family by their freedmen and descendants, honoring the deceased's father, who held several civic offices.

EDCS-07000238.text_edition: \n Caio Nipio Mani filio Oufentina ex testamento\n
EDCS-07000238.translation: To Gaius Nipius, son of Manius, of the Oufentina tribe, by his will.
EDCS-07000238.summary: This inscription records a bequest or dedication made in memory of Gaius Nipius, son of Manius, as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-07000240.text_edition: \n Publi Muci Lepidi et Paestiniae Caletycheni in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XXI\n
EDCS-07000240.translation: For Publius Mucius Lepidus and Paestinia Caletyche; in front 12 feet, in the field 21 feet.
EDCS-07000240.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot of 12x21 feet dedicated to Publius Mucius Lepidus and Paestinia Caletyche.

EDCS-07000241.text_edition: \n Cornelio Cai filio Oufentina centhurioni sic legionis XIII\n
EDCS-07000241.translation: To Cornelius, son of Caius, of the Oufentina tribe, centurion of the 13th legion.
EDCS-07000241.summary: This inscription honors Cornelius, a centurion in the 13th legion, identifying his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-07000242.text_edition: \n Vivit Luci Crysi Luci liberti Menae et Crysiae Luci libertae Ma\n
EDCS-07000242.translation: Lucius Mena, freedman of Lucius Crysius, and his wife, freedwoman of Lucius Crysius, [lived].
EDCS-07000242.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the names of freedpersons, likely a married couple, who lived in the past.

EDCS-07000243.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment of a funerary or dedication text, possibly listing names.

EDCS-07000244.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a funerary monument, recording the name and filiation of an individual.

EDCS-07000245.text_edition: \n Cai Mammi Cai filii Chaereae in fronte pedes XIII in agro pedes XIIII\n
EDCS-07000245.translation: Of Caius Mammius, son of Caius, called Chaereas: 13 feet in front, 14 feet in the field.
EDCS-07000245.summary: This is a boundary inscription, likely for a funerary plot, specifying dimensions and identifying the owner.

EDCS-07000246.text_edition: \n Maenander Pediae |(mulieris) libertae Nothidi uxori bene meritae\n
EDCS-07000246.translation: To Maenander, for his wife Nothis, his freedwoman, who deserved well.
EDCS-07000246.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, from a freedman to his wife who was also his freedwoman.

EDCS-07000247.text_edition: \n Sexti Satri Publi filii Oufentina in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-07000247.translation: Of Sextus Satrius, son of Publius, of the Oufentina tribe: 12 feet in front, 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-07000247.summary: This is a boundary inscription, likely for a funerary plot, specifying dimensions and identifying the owner and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-07000248.text_edition: \n Mindiae uxori optimae Lucius Plotius Hermes sevir Aquini sibi et Curt[iae ------\n
EDCS-07000248.translation: To his excellent wife Mindia, Lucius Plotius Hermes, *sevir* of Aquinum, for himself and Curt[ia ------
EDCS-07000248.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a *sevir* to his wife, with an incomplete dedication to another individual, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-07000249.translation: Purpurinia Privigna, daughter of Lucius, [wife] of Curvius.
EDCS-07000249.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment of a funerary monument, recording the name and filiation of a woman, and possibly her husband.

EDCS-07000250.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sulpiciae Victorinae filiae dulcissimae vixit annos XV Canedius sic Victorinus pater fecit\n
EDCS-07000250.translation: To the divine shades: Sulpicia Victorina, dearest daughter, lived 15 years. Canedius, her father, also known as Victorinus, made this.
EDCS-07000250.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Sulpicia Victorina, erected by her father, dating likely to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-07000251.text_edition: \n Marco Runtio Marci filio Oufentina Paestinio Prisco IIviro II quinquennali aedili Iulia Cai filia Longina marito optimo\n
EDCS-07000251.translation: To Marcus Runtius, son of Marcus, of the Oufentina tribe, Paestinius Priscus, *duovir*, *quinquennalis*, *aedilis*; his wife Iulia, daughter of Caius Longina, [erected this] for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-07000251.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a wife to her husband, a prominent local magistrate in Paestum, likely dating to the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-07000252.text_edition: \n Caius Trebellius Cai libertus Antiochus sibi et @(obitae) Trebelliae Cai libertae Helenae et Diomedi liberto in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XV\n
EDCS-07000252.translation: Caius Trebellius Antiochus, freedman of Caius, for himself and for his deceased wife Trebellia Helena, freedwoman of Caius, and for Diomedes the freedman; in front 12 feet, in the field 15 feet.
EDCS-07000252.summary: This is a funerary boundary inscription set up by Caius Trebellius Antiochus for himself, his wife, and a freedman, specifying land dimensions.

EDCS-07000253.text_edition: \n Alfia Marci liberta Salvia et Marcus Alfius Marci libertus Priamus Marco Alfio Marci liberto Lucrioni de suo in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XVI\n
EDCS-07000253.translation: Alfia Salvia, freedwoman of Marcus, and Marcus Alfius Priamus, freedman of Marcus, for their Marcus Alfius Lucrioni, freedman of Marcus, from their own resources; in front 12 feet, in the field 16 feet.
EDCS-07000253.summary: This is a funerary boundary inscription set up by Alfia Salvia and Marcus Alfius Priamus for their relative Marcus Alfius Lucrioni, specifying land dimensions.

EDCS-07000254.text_edition: \n Publi Valgi Philarguri sibi et suis in fronte pedes XIIX in agro pedes X Valgiae Secundae libertae Liberali libertae et Cai Atti Advenae |(mulieris) liberti\n
EDCS-07000254.translation: Publius Valgius Philargyrus for himself and his family; in front 18 feet, in the field 10 feet, for Valgia Liberalis, freedwoman of Secunda, and for the freedman of the woman Cai Attius Advena.
EDCS-07000254.summary: This is a funerary boundary inscription set up by Publius Valgius Philargyrus for himself and his family, specifying land dimensions and naming beneficiaries.

EDCS-07000257.summary: This is a tombstone inscription identifying Lucia, daughter of Marcus, as a priestess.

EDCS-07000260.text_edition: \n Plotia Auli et |(mulieris) liberta Helena sibi et Aulo Plotio Lucrioni viro bene merenti et Caio Hostio Primogenio et Aulo Plotio Felici liberto et Plotiae Veneriae libertae Aulo Plotio Crescenti liberto et posterisque eorum\n
