EDCS-11500826.translation: Tiberius Claudius Philippus, dia[conus] and gerusiarch, built the wall.
EDCS-11500826.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Claudius Philippus, identified by his Greek title 'gerusiarch' (elder) and possibly a Christian 'deacon', building a wall, suggesting a date in the later Roman empire.

EDCS-11500828.inscription_type: carmina, tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-11500828.translation: To Honor, the patron of the dining club of Elvenius, Caius Marius Fortinatus. Rufina, the haruspex, may the gods preserve you with your family.
EDCS-11500828.summary: This is an inscription dedicated to a patron named Caius Marius Fortinatus by members of his dining club, with a mention of a priestess (haruspex) named Rufina, indicating a date in the imperial period.

EDCS-11500829.translation: From the workshop of Sextilius Clemens.
EDCS-11500829.summary: This inscription identifies the origin of a product or artwork as being from the workshop of Sextilius Clemens, with the date being indeterminate.

EDCS-11500830.translation: Of Tiberius Caesar. Emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus Germanicus.
EDCS-11500830.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, likely appearing on a public building or monument, dating to his reign (14-37 AD).

EDCS-11500831.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus, likely appearing on a public building or monument, dating to his reign (41-54 AD).

EDCS-11500833.translation: Of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, our lord.
EDCS-11500833.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Septimius Severus, likely part of a larger inscription on a public monument.

EDCS-11500846.summary: This inscription is a dedication or ownership marker for Gaius Cassius Camillus.

EDCS-11500847.translation: Of Claudia Acte, freedwoman of the Emperor.
EDCS-11500847.summary: This inscription records the name of Claudia Acte, a freedwoman of an emperor, possibly for a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-11500848.summary: This inscription likely records a measurement of 10 feet belonging to Gaius Julius Proculus, possibly for property or a construction project.

EDCS-11500849.summary: This inscription is a dedication or ownership marker for Metilia Marcia and Rufina.

EDCS-11500853.translation: To Admetis, my incomparable wife, who lived about 25 years. To no other woman did she leave a place to precede her. Most chaste, most faithful, most dutiful, whose many marvelous good deeds I wished to describe in detail, but neither time nor place was sufficient. Hierolces to Caecilia Materna, most illustrious woman, her manager. If Admetis alone will truly have such a monument, farewell, most holy, and rest well.
EDCS-11500853.summary: This is a poignant funerary epitaph from Hierolces to his wife Caecilia Materna (also referred to as Admetis), praising her virtues and lamenting the impossibility of fully recounting her good deeds, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500854.translation: To the Shades below. Doryphorus, a fellow slave, placed this for Epaphroditus, the manager of Gnaeus Hai Proculus, who deserved well.
EDCS-11500854.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a fellow slave to Epaphroditus, the manager of Gnaeus Hai Proculus, indicating a master-slave relationship and affection.

EDCS-11500855.translation: To the Shades below. Hygia, daughter, made this for her most sweet and deserving father, Brasidas, the manager.
EDCS-11500855.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a daughter, Hygia, to her father, Brasidas, who served as a manager, highlighting familial love and respect.

EDCS-11500856.translation: To the Shades below. For the deserving manager Haia Zosime, from Mallo.
EDCS-11500856.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Haia Zosime, identified as a manager, with Mallo as the dedicator, suggesting a relationship of respect or obligation.

EDCS-11500857.translation: To the Shades below. For Onesimus, manager of the ironworks. Onesiphorus, son, to his most dutiful father. Place granted by the magistrates of Minturnae (?).
EDCS-11500857.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Onesiphorus to his father Onesimus, a manager of an ironworks, with the grant of the burial plot by local magistrates, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500858.translation: To the departed spirits. Bromius the money changer.
EDCS-11500858.summary: A funerary dedication to the departed spirits by Bromius, a money changer.

EDCS-11500859.translation: Baiani. Gaius Julius Eros the money changer. Anchoria Polla, daughter of Marcus. Lucius Iulius Eros. Anchoria Polla.
EDCS-11500859.summary: A funerary inscription likely for members of the Baiani family, including money changers and family members.

EDCS-11500860.translation: To the departed spirits. Gnostius, freedman of Augustus, [son?] ofioris, a garland maker, lived for 37 years. Hermione, his wife, [for her?] most deserving husband.
EDCS-11500860.summary: A funerary inscription for Gnostius, a garland maker and freedman of Augustus, who died at 37, with a dedication from his wife Hermione.

EDCS-11500861.translation: To the departed spirits. Pompeia Eutychia made [this] for her deserving husband Afranius Felix, who lived for 74 years and who she cherished for 23 years.
EDCS-11500861.summary: A funerary monument erected by Pompeia Eutychia for her well-deserving husband Afranius Felix, detailing his age at death and the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-11500862.summary: An inscription recording a dedication by Quintus Marcius Felix to Ionius, a steward.

EDCS-11500863.translation: The Fates sought their day, which has returned [him]. Philadelphus, steward of Sexstus Cerinthus, who lived for 35 years, rests here.
EDCS-11500863.summary: A funerary epitaph for Philadelphus, steward of Sexstus Cerinthus, who died at 35.

EDCS-11500864.translation: To the departed spirits. Victoria Vitalis for her deserving husband Stephen, steward.
EDCS-11500864.summary: A funerary dedication by Victoria Vitalis to her deserving husband Stephen, who was a steward.

EDCS-11500865.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Atilius Fortunatus, an intestinal cloth maker, who lived for 31 years, made [this]. Julius Felicisimus [made this] for his deserving adopted son.
EDCS-11500865.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Atilius Fortunatus, an intestinal cloth maker, with a dedication from his adopted son Julius Felicisimus.

EDCS-11500866.translation: Gaius Caesonius Demetrius, a timber carpenter, for himself and for Nymphenis, his fellow freedwoman, and for Gaius Caesonius Metrophanes, freedman, and for Secunda, freedwoman, and for Primogenius, freedman, freedman of Caesonia Ampliata, Metrophanes, [for his wife?], lived for 14 years and 3 months.
EDCS-11500866.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Caesonius Demetrius, a timber carpenter, and several freedmen and freedwomen, including his fellow freedwoman Nymphenis and others connected to his household and Caesonia Ampliata.

EDCS-11500869.translation: To the departed spirits. Prisca for her deserving husband Paeregrinus, the myrmillo (gladiator).
EDCS-11500869.summary: A funerary dedication by Prisca to her deserving husband Paeregrinus, who was a myrmillo gladiator.

EDCS-11500870.translation: To the departed spirits. Fabia Secundina made this for her husband Aequoreus, a retiarius, who lived 25 years, a well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11500870.summary: A tombstone for Aequoreus, a retiarius (gladiator who used a net), erected by his wife Fabia Secundina.

EDCS-11500872.translation: To the departed spirits. Synecdemus made this for Pannyches, a most rare physician and friend.
EDCS-11500872.summary: A tombstone for the physician Pannyches, dedicated by his friend Synecdemus.

EDCS-11500874.translation: To the departed spirits. Acibas, freedwoman, made this for Publius Caulius Coeranus, a businessman in charge of iron mines and a wine trade, a deserving patron.
EDCS-11500874.summary: A tombstone for Publius Caulius Coeranus, a businessman in mining and wine trade, dedicated by his freedwoman Acibas.

EDCS-11500875.translation: To the departed spirits. Voconius Polybius [made this] for Fortunatus, a notary.
EDCS-11500875.summary: A tombstone for Fortunatus, a notary, dedicated by Voconius Polybius.

EDCS-11500876.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Zosimus made this for his most devoted wife Coelia Hagne, a midwife.
EDCS-11500876.summary: A tombstone for Coelia Hagne, a midwife, erected by her husband Marcus Ulpius Zosimus.

EDCS-11500878.translation: Chrematine, Numisiae, an ornament maker, died on the 6th day before the Ides of October, during the consulship of Paullus Fabius Maximus and Quintus Aelius. The bones were collected on the 10th day before the Ides of October, during the same consulship. Diaphyrus, a fellow slave.
EDCS-11500878.summary: An epitaph and ossuary inscription for Chrematine, an ornament maker, with details of her death and burial, and mentioning a fellow slave Diaphyrus.

EDCS-11500879.summary: An ownership mark or epitaph for Sextus Numisius Carus.

EDCS-11500880.summary: An ownership mark or epitaph for Lucius Numisius Eroticus.

EDCS-11500881.translation: Belonging to Lucius Numisius Splendidus, son of Lucius, on the 3rd day before the Kalends of October, during the consulship of Gnaeus Lentulus and Marcus Licinius. An olla (urn).
EDCS-11500881.summary: An epitaph for Lucius Numisius Splendidus, with a date of death and mentioning a burial urn, dating to the consulship of Gnaeus Lentulus and Marcus Licinius.

EDCS-11500882.translation: Belonging to Lucius Numisius Turianus, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe.
EDCS-11500882.summary: An ownership mark or epitaph for Lucius Numisius Turianus, son of Lucius, belonging to the Oufentina tribe.

EDCS-11500883.translation: The bones of Numisia, daughter of Marcus, V. (perhaps with additional titles).
EDCS-11500883.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph for a woman named Numisia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500884.translation: Clymene, dresser of children, lived 13 years. Lucrio, slave of Piso (?), lives.
EDCS-11500884.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clymene, who worked as a dresser for children, and mentions Lucrio, possibly her owner or partner.

EDCS-11500885.translation: Valeria Callityche, freedwoman of Aulus and of the woman (his wife), dresser.
EDCS-11500885.summary: This inscription identifies Valeria Callityche as a freedwoman and a 'dresser', likely a profession related to clothing or adornment.

EDCS-11500886.translation: Lucius Calpurnius Phaedon, tutor of the Calpurnii, for Calpurnia Iache, his wife.
EDCS-11500886.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Lucius Calpurnius Phaedon honoring his wife, Calpurnia Iache, and identifies himself as a tutor.

EDCS-11500887.translation: To the departed spirits. Symphorus, the tutor, made this for Pinna, who deserved well; he lived 15 years.
EDCS-11500887.summary: This is a tombstone for Symphorus, a tutor, set up by Pinna, who is described as deserving well, and it records his age at death.

EDCS-11500888.translation: For Tuccia Cleopatra, daughter of Spurius. To the departed spirits. For Gnaeus Cossutius Atimetus, the paenularius. Cris[---] Spuri [---]
EDCS-11500888.summary: This inscription appears to be a complex funerary monument mentioning at least two individuals, Tuccia Cleopatra and Gnaeus Cossutius Atimetus, with a fragmentary section at the end.

EDCS-11500889.summary: This is a brief inscription identifying Gaius Ummidius Actius Anicetus as a pantomimus (a type of actor).

EDCS-11500890.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Aurelius Primo Victori Hyperichio, dearest pupil. Aurelius Eutychianus.
EDCS-11500890.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Aurelius Eutychianus to his 'dearest pupil', Marcus Aurelius Primo Victori Hyperichio.

EDCS-11500893.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Calpurnius Festus, the painter, who lived 21 years.
EDCS-11500893.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lucius Calpurnius Festus, a painter, and states his age at death.

EDCS-11500894.translation: For Lollia Victorina, dearest wife. Lollianus Porresmus, procurator, bought (this) for her, who deserved well, with whom he lived 20 years without any reproach, and mutually, that is, loved.
EDCS-11500894.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Lollianus Porresmus, a procurator, to his wife Lollia Victorina, and commemorates their 20 years of life together without reproach.

EDCS-11500895.translation: To Apollo. From Caius Julius Socrates, scribe. And Apollonius, son of Caius Julius Socrates, scribe. His wife Iulia Plate(?).
EDCS-11500895.summary: This is a dedication to Apollo by Caius Julius Socrates, a scribe, and possibly his wife Iulia Plate, with their freedman Apollonius also mentioned.

EDCS-11500896.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Nana, his most beloved mother, who deserved well, Rustius Numerius, a scribe of the fleet, made this.
EDCS-11500896.summary: A funerary dedication by Rustius Numerius, a scribe of the fleet, to his beloved mother Nana, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11500897.translation: To Paccio Lucianus, scribe, who lived 43 years and 26 days, an incomparable man. His sons and wife made this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-11500897.summary: A funerary monument for Paccio Lucianus, a scribe, set up by his sons and wife, indicating he was an 'incomparable man'.

EDCS-11500898.translation: To Aelia Felina, most chaste, who lived 21 years, 3 months, and 11 days. Saturius Gratus, scribe, made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-11500898.summary: A funerary inscription for Aelia Felina, wife of the scribe Saturius Gratus, who died at a young age.

EDCS-11500900.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Antonia Victoria, his incomparable wife. Lucius Valerius Iulianus, scribe and temple attendant, made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-11500900.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Valerius Iulianus, a scribe and temple attendant, to his highly praised wife Antonia Victoria.

EDCS-11500901.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Perpernius Zmaragdus, his deserving master, the builder.
EDCS-11500901.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Perpernius Zmaragdus, a master builder, set up by someone who was his subordinate or student.

EDCS-11500904.translation: Sacred. Lucius Faenius Alexander, freedman of Lucius, the incense merchant of Puteoli, a most excellent man, lived well.
EDCS-11500904.summary: An inscription recording the virtuous life of Lucius Faenius Alexander, an incense merchant from Puteoli, likely set up by himself or a friend.

EDCS-11500906.translation: To Onomastus, the tonsor. He lived 20 years, died on the 8th day before the Kalends of March [?]. In the consulship of Marcus Junius Silanus and Lucius Norbanus Balbus. Out of affection from a fellow slave.
EDCS-11500906.summary: This inscription commemorates the short life and death of Onomastus, a tonsor (barber), likely set up by a fellow slave and dating to 45 BCE.

EDCS-11500907.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Licinia Primigenia, perfumer. Licinius Amomus made this for his deserving mother, who lived 71 years.
EDCS-11500907.summary: A funerary inscription by Licinius Amomus for his mother Licinia Primigenia, a perfumer, who died at age 71.

EDCS-11500909.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Anteius Priscus, of Italian origin, who lived 35 years, 5 months, and 18 days. Iulia Felicitas made this for her incomparable husband, who deserved well.
EDCS-11500909.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulia Felicitas for her husband Marcus Anteius Priscus, of Italian origin, who died at age 35.

EDCS-11500910.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antonius Serenus, of Italian nationality, lived 36 years. Petronia Felissima made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11500910.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Serenus, set up by his wife Petronia Felissima.

EDCS-11500911.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Rufina, of Cilician nationality, lived 50 years. Lucius Iulius Amonianus made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11500911.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Rufina, set up by her husband Lucius Iulius Amonianus.

EDCS-11500912.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Asclepiodote, daughter of Cymnodorus of Nicomedia, lived 30 years. Her brother made this for his well-deserving sister.
EDCS-11500912.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Asclepiodote, set up by her brother.

EDCS-11500914.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Iulia, of Raetian nationality, lived 40 years. Her husband Claudius Victor and her children Tiberius, Victorina, and Iulia made this for her well-deserving.
EDCS-11500914.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Iulia, set up by her husband and children.

EDCS-11500915.translation: Dionysius of Amastris is buried here.
EDCS-11500915.summary: This is a simple burial inscription for Dionysius of Amastris.

EDCS-11500916.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Furius Ispartacus made this for his incomparable brother Lucius F. Valerianus Garfanius, of African nationality.
EDCS-11500916.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius F. Valerianus Garfanius, set up by his brother Furius Ispartacus.

EDCS-11500917.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Faenia Felicitas, of Syrian nationality, lived 40 years. Saturninus Antiochus made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11500917.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Faenia Felicitas, set up by her husband Saturninus Antiochus.

EDCS-11500918.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Glycero, the foster child, of Galatian nationality, who lived 16 years. [Lacunae]. He asks passers-by to stop.
EDCS-11500918.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Glycero, a foster child, with a plea to passers-by, likely from a later period given the fragmentary nature and the inclusion of a plea.

EDCS-11500919.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Grata Stabiana, sweetest daughter, who lived 8 years, 4 months, and 4 days. Caninius Gratus, father, and his wife Grata, made this for their well-deserving.
EDCS-11500919.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Grata Stabiana, set up by her parents.

EDCS-11500920.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Iulius Carallitanus, of Italian nationality, who lived 15 years and 6 months. Cassia Fortunata made this for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-11500920.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Iulius Carallitanus, set up by his mother Cassia Fortunata.

EDCS-11500921.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Eustatia made this for her well-deserving Iulius Hermogenes, a Syrian by birth, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-11500921.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulius Hermogenes, who lived 35 years and was of Syrian origin, set up by Eustatia.

EDCS-11500923.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Modius Valentinus made this for his most sweet, well-deserving mother, Iulia Auta, a native slave of Nucherina.
EDCS-11500923.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Auta, described as a native slave of Nucherina, set up by her son, Gaius Modius Valentinus.

EDCS-11500924.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius Marchianus, her husband, made this for his most loving, well-deserving wife, Iulia Petronia, an Italian by birth, who lived 27 years, 2 months, and 24 days.
EDCS-11500924.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Petronia, who lived 27 years, 2 months, and 24 days and was Italian by birth, set up by her husband, Marcus Aurelius Marchianus.

EDCS-11500925.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Maia Tertulla, who lived 28 years, an African by birth. Gellius Quadratus made this for his well-deserving wife, chaste and good.
EDCS-11500925.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maia Tertulla, who lived 28 years and was African by birth, set up by her husband, Gellius Quadratus.

EDCS-11500927.translation: To Pannycis Ascle, daughter of a Syrian from Caesarea, who lived 12 years.
EDCS-11500927.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pannycis Ascle, daughter of a Syrian from Caesarea, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-11500928.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Petronius Valens made this for his well-deserving freedwoman, Petronia Victoria, a Phrygian by birth, who lived 15 years.
EDCS-11500928.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Petronia Victoria, a Phrygian by birth who lived 15 years, set up by her patron Lucius Petronius Valens.

EDCS-11500929.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Brinnia Marciane made this for her most sweet and pious son, Gaius Sallustius Marcianus Italicus, 9 years, 2 months [old].
EDCS-11500929.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Sallustius Marcianus Italicus, who died at the age of 9 years and 2 months, set up by his mother, Brinnia Marciane.

EDCS-11500930.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, mentioning a freedman of Cn(aeus).

EDCS-11500931.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Felicissima made this for her well-deserving daughter, Aurelia Maria, who lived 11 years, 5 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-11500931.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Maria, who lived 11 years, 5 months, and 5 days, set up by her mother, Aurelia Felicissima.

EDCS-11500932.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpia, his wife, [made this] for well-deserving Abdaeus, a slave of our Caesar.
EDCS-11500932.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Abdaeus, a slave of the emperor, set up by his wife, Ulpia.

EDCS-11500933.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Abuccius Rufus, son of Lucius, made this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-11500933.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Abuccius Rufus for his father.

EDCS-11500934.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Acilius Prosdectus(?) made this for his well-deserving wife, Maria.
EDCS-11500934.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Acilius Prosdectus(?) for his wife, Maria.

EDCS-11500935.translation: To the spirits of the departed. The underworld is honored [by the burial] of Marcus Acilius Proculus, my most excellent son.
EDCS-11500935.summary: This funerary inscription laments the death of Marcus Acilius Proculus, described as an excellent son.

EDCS-11500936.translation: To Acilia Macrina, daughter of Lucius, my most holy wife, who lived 24 years, 3 months, 11 days, 2 hours. Her most unhappy husband [erected this].
EDCS-11500936.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Acilia Macrina, recording her age at death and erected by her grieving husband.

EDCS-11500937.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Acindynus lived 20 years, 3 months.
EDCS-11500937.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Acindynus, stating his name and age at death.

EDCS-11500939.translation: Cassius Theon made this for his son Acutius Basileus, who lived 22 years, 6 months, 13 days, a well-deserving recipient.
EDCS-11500939.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Cassius Theon for his son Acutius Basileus, detailing his age at death.

EDCS-11500940.translation: Adauctula Pia lived 8 years, 9 months. [Erected by] C().
EDCS-11500940.summary: This inscription records the death of Adauctula Pia, who lived 8 years and 9 months, with a partial dedication.

EDCS-11500941.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aprulla made this for her well-deserving wife, Aegialus, wife of Quintus Lucretius Secundus.
EDCS-11500941.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Aprulla for her well-deserving wife, Aegialus.

EDCS-11500942.translation: Aelius Agrippa made this for Beriola, who lived 17 years, a well-deserving [recipient].
EDCS-11500942.summary: This funerary inscription records that Aelius Agrippa erected a monument for Beriola, who died at age 17.

EDCS-11500943.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aelius Dionysius, husband, well-deserving.
EDCS-11500943.summary: This funerary dedication is for Aelius Dionysius, identified as a well-deserving husband.

EDCS-11500944.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Publius Aelius Erasus, who lived 60 years. Iulia Helpis, to her deserving husband.
EDCS-11500944.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulia Helpis to her husband, Publius Aelius Erasus.

EDCS-11500945.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Marcus Aelius Eutyches.
EDCS-11500945.summary: A funerary dedication to Marcus Aelius Eutyches.

EDCS-11500946.translation: To Aelius Felicior, dearest infant. Felix and Felicitas [dedicate this] to Fabacius the younger, who lived 4 years and 13 days, [from his] unhappy parents.
EDCS-11500946.summary: A funerary dedication by parents Felix and Felicitas to their infant son, Aelius Felicior and Fabacius.

EDCS-11500947.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Lucius Aelius Hetaerus and Aelia Capitalina, patrons. Aelia Chrysothoe, his freedwoman, made this for them as a deserving gesture.
EDCS-11500947.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedwoman, Aelia Chrysothoe, to her former patrons, Lucius Aelius Hetaerus and Aelia Capitalina.

EDCS-11500948.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Marcus Aelius Iulianus, who lived 6 years, 8 months, and 12 days.
EDCS-11500948.summary: A funerary dedication for a child, Marcus Aelius Iulianus, who died at a young age.

EDCS-11500949.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Lucius Aelius Pius. Lucius Aelius Serenus, to his best brother.
EDCS-11500949.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Aelius Serenus to his brother, Lucius Aelius Pius.

EDCS-11500951.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Publius Aelius Puteolanus, who lived 5 years, 4 months, and 17 days. Neroni Germanus and Pontice made this for their dearest son.
EDCS-11500951.summary: A funerary dedication by Neroni Germanus and Pontice to their son, Publius Aelius Puteolanus.

EDCS-11500952.translation: To Aelius Rufinus, dearest, who lived 8 years and 2 months.
EDCS-11500952.summary: A funerary dedication for a child, Aelius Rufinus, who died at a young age.

EDCS-11500954.translation: To Aelia Chridis, a deserving wife. Lucius Vitellius Paulus made this.
EDCS-11500954.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Vitellius Paulus to his wife, Aelia Chridis.

EDCS-11500955.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Helena made this for her exceedingly rare husband, Titus Aelius Borysthenetus, who lived 26 years, as a deserving gesture.
EDCS-11500955.summary: A funerary dedication by Aelia Helena to her husband, Titus Aelius Borysthenetus.

EDCS-11500956.translation: To the departed spirits. To Aelia Eutychia, who lived 45 years, a well-deserving wife. Claudius Amaranthus made this.
EDCS-11500956.summary: A funerary dedication by Claudius Amaranthus to his wife Aelia Eutychia.

EDCS-11500957.translation: Aelia Decorata, together with her daughter-in-law Sextilia Laurina, built the upper chambers in memory of her deserving husband Isio, for visiting the resting places of the dead, for herself, her family, and their descendants; furthermore, the shop with its upper rooms can in no way be separated from this sacred and religious place for the protection of the deceased, and if anyone attempts to violate this, they will each pay 50,000 sesterces to the Republic of Puteoli, as is also stated on the inscription outside, in the name of the Eucherii grandchildren; the survivors will take care to provide the income from the small plot of land and the building for the repairs of the sacred chambers and the building situated there.
EDCS-11500957.summary: A complex funerary and endowment inscription from Puteoli by Aelia Decorata, detailing the construction of funerary chambers and a shop, with provisions for upkeep and penalties for violation.

EDCS-11500959.translation: To the departed spirits. Aelias Conserva made this for Aelius, a well-deserving mother.
EDCS-11500959.summary: A funerary dedication by Aelias Conserva for Aelius, likely her son, referring to herself as a 'well-deserving mother'.

EDCS-11500961.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Caesius Larix made this for Aelia Synforosa, his wife, while alive.
EDCS-11500961.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Caesius Larix to his wife Aelia Synforosa, made during his lifetime.

EDCS-11500962.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Cusinius made this for his well-deserving wife Aelia Ven[...].
EDCS-11500962.summary: A fragmented funerary dedication by Lucius Cusinius to his wife Aelia Ven[...].

EDCS-11500963.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Aemilius, freedman of Titus, lived for 4 years, 10 months, and 8 days. Autronia Rufilla made this for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-11500963.summary: A funerary dedication by Autronia Rufilla for her young son Lucius Aemilius, who died at the age of four.

EDCS-11500964.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Aemilius Abascantus made this for himself and for his well-deserving wife Aemilia Nice.
EDCS-11500964.summary: A funerary dedication by Publius Aemilius Abascantus for himself and his wife Aemilia Nice.

EDCS-11500965.translation: To Lucius Aemilius Primus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-11500965.summary: A funerary dedication or memorial inscription for Lucius Aemilius Primus.

EDCS-11500966.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aemilia Clementilla.
EDCS-11500966.summary: A funerary dedication to Aemilia Clementilla.

EDCS-11500967.summary: A simple inscription bearing the name Aemilia Demetria, possibly a funerary dedication or ownership mark.

EDCS-11500968.translation: Aemilia Lade made [this] for Publius Firmus Lentinus and Publius Firmus Coenus and their family.
EDCS-11500968.summary: This is a private dedication by Aemilia Lade to two individuals, Publius Firmus Lentinus and Publius Firmus Coenus, and their family.

EDCS-11500969.translation: To the departed spirits. Arescius, the husband, [made this] for his most sweet wife, Aemilia Pesta.
EDCS-11500969.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband to his wife, Aemilia Pesta, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500970.translation: Her heir, Curtius Domitius, [made this] for Aemilia Rufina, daughter of Quintus, as one who deserved well.
EDCS-11500970.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aemilia Rufina, daughter of Quintus, set up by her heir, Curtius Domitius.

EDCS-11500972.translation: To the departed spirits. Aerullia, who lived 70 years, [this was made] by her freedmen and heirs, Aerullus Verna, Firmilla, and Felicitas, as one who deserved well.
EDCS-11500972.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aerullia, set up by her freedmen and heirs, Aerullus Verna, Firmilla, and Felicitas.

EDCS-11500973.translation: To the departed spirits. Egnatius Romulus [made this] for his most sweet and incomparable wife, Euodia, who lived 18 years, 9 months, and 3 days, as one who deserved well.
EDCS-11500973.summary: A funerary epitaph for Euodia, who lived 18 years, 9 months, and 3 days, erected by her husband Egnatius Romulus.

EDCS-11500974.translation: To the departed spirits. Agnus and Successa made [this] for their son Agnia, who lived 6 years and 2 months, as one who deserved well.
EDCS-11500974.summary: A funerary epitaph for Agnia, who lived 6 years and 2 months, set up by his parents Agnus and Successa.

EDCS-11500975.translation: Agria Anthis made [this] for her foster-daughter Agria Lucilla.
EDCS-11500975.summary: A private dedication by Agria Anthis for her foster-daughter, Agria Lucilla.

EDCS-11500976.translation: To the departed spirits. [This was made] by his brother for Lucius Agrippinus, who lived 31 years, 8 months, and 12 days, as one who deserved well.
EDCS-11500976.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Agrippinus, who lived 31 years, 8 months, and 12 days, set up by his brother.

EDCS-11500977.translation: Aiula, his wife, [made this] for her amiable and most upright husband, who lived about 30 years, as one who deserved well.
EDCS-11500977.summary: A funerary epitaph for a man who lived approximately 30 years, set up by his wife Aiula.

EDCS-11500979.translation: Primigenia, daughter of Alexander, who lived 4 years and 9 months.
EDCS-11500979.summary: A funerary epitaph for Primigenia, daughter of Alexander, who lived 4 years and 9 months.

EDCS-11500981.translation: To Alexandria, my sweetest infant, who lived 3 years, 11 months, 20 days. Pelagius, father.
EDCS-11500981.summary: A funerary epigram for a child, set up by her father, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-11500982.translation: To the Departed Spirits. A plot of sepulchre and pathway for Publius Alfenius Anterus, except for the area in front of 11 feet and 34 feet in the field, and with a penalty of 20,000 sesterces excepted, and for Publius Alfenius Rusticus, and Alfena Lita, freedwoman of Publius, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-11500982.summary: A funerary dedication concerning the purchase and use of a burial plot, specifying dimensions and penalties, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500983.translation: Monument number 120. Dedicated on the Kalends of September, in the consulship of Publius Lentulus and Titus Quintius Valerianus. Given by Lucius Allius Epaphra. [Re-dedication on the Kalends of September, in the consulship of Publius Lentulus Scipio and Titus Quintius Crispinus.]
EDCS-11500983.summary: A funerary monument with two dedication dates, suggesting a re-dedication or adjustment, with consuls providing a date range in the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11500984.translation: Of Marcus Allius Princeps, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-11500984.summary: A brief funerary inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, Marcus Allius Princeps.

EDCS-11500985.translation: To the Departed Spirits. To Alphia Maxima(?) Belitia Saturninus, my wife, and to [my] sons, freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-11500985.summary: A funerary dedication to a wife and her descendants, set up by a spouse, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500986.translation: To the Departed Spirits. To Amatio Lucrio, who lived 11 years, 8 months, 18 days. Amatia Strenua set this up for her dearest son, who deserved well.
EDCS-11500986.summary: A funerary epigram for a child, set up by his mother, Amatia Strenua, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500987.translation: To the Departed Spirits. To Amatia Ofilia Marciana, daughter of Caius, granddaughter of Caius. From [her] parents.
EDCS-11500987.summary: A funerary dedication from parents to their daughter, Amatia Ofilia Marciana, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500988.translation: To the Departed Spirits. To Ambibia Parthenope, for her most devoted and most deserving mother. To the Departed Spirits. Ambibia Fructosa and [---] [---]. [To the Departed Spirits.] [[A[...]]
EDCS-11500988.summary: A complex funerary monument with at least two layers of inscriptions, honoring a mother and possibly indicating further burials, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500990.translation: To the Departed Spirits. For Marcus Amulius Epinicus. Lucius Acilius Sosus, father, and Amulia Maximilla, mother, [set this up]. He lived 31 years, 5 months, 21 days, for their dutiful son.
EDCS-11500990.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young man, Marcus Amulius Epinicus, set up by his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500991.translation: To the Departed Spirits. For Marcus Amulius Lupus.
EDCS-11500991.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Amulius Lupus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500992.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Lucius Munatius Montanus made this for his well-deserving wife, Amullia Eutychia.
EDCS-11500992.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his wife, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11500993.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Anicio Hermeti et Pompeiae Cytheridi\n
EDCS-11500993.translation: To the spirits of the departed for Anicius Hermetus and Pompeia Cytheris.
EDCS-11500993.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to a couple, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11500995.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Anicetus, a boy of nine years, lies here.
EDCS-11500995.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of a young boy named Anicetus.

EDCS-11500996.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph dedicated to a person named Anicetus.

EDCS-11500997.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Her husband Rusticus made this for Annaea Nice[n]i, his wife, her preserver and well-deserving.
EDCS-11500997.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his wife, praising her virtues.

EDCS-11500998.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Annea Pompeiana Afra, a foreigner worthy of her husband, lived piously for 36 years, 4 months, and 3 days, pious to her family.
EDCS-11500998.summary: A funerary epitaph for Annea Pompeiana Afra, a foreign woman who lived 36 years, 4 months, and 3 days.

EDCS-11501000.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Victoria made this for her well-deserving husband, Annio Celebrino, who died young at the age of 22 years, 9 months, and 2 days.
EDCS-11501000.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband who died at a young age.

EDCS-11501001.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Annius Fortunatus, who lived 19 years. His parents, Lucius Marius Zenon and Annia Chrysis, [made this for] their most dutiful son.
EDCS-11501001.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by parents for their son who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-11501002.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Annius Luciferus. His heir made this for [him], the well-deserving.
EDCS-11501002.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by an heir for Gaius Annius Luciferus.

EDCS-11501003.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Annia Maxima made this for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Annius Primitivus.
EDCS-11501003.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband.

EDCS-11501004.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Annia Stafyle made this for her husband Annio Primo, who lived 48 years and 7 months.
EDCS-11501004.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annio Primo, set up by his wife Annia Stafyle, with details of his age at death.

EDCS-11501007.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Annius Corint(h)us for his most excellent wife Annia Eutychia.
EDCS-11501007.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annia Eutychia, set up by her husband Lucius Annius Corint(h)us.

EDCS-11501008.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Junius Hermes made this for his incomparable wife Annia Longina, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-11501008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annia Longina, set up by her husband Titus Junius Hermes.

EDCS-11501010.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudius made this for his chaste Annia Pri[---], who lived 6 months and [---] years, his [---] husband, as deserving.
EDCS-11501010.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annia Pri[---], set up by her husband Claudius, with partial information about her age.

EDCS-11501011.translation: Julius Antoninus made this for his sister Julia Victorina, who lived 13 years, and for her mother, his dearest, most innocent Annia Victorina, as deserving.
EDCS-11501011.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annia Victorina and her daughter Julia Victorina, set up by Julius Antoninus, the brother of the mother and uncle of the daughter.

EDCS-11501012.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Anatolius made this for his deserving son Gaius Antistius Respectus, who lived 4 years, 9 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-11501012.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Antistius Respectus, set up by his father Anatolius, with details of his age at death.

EDCS-11501013.translation: Antipatra, sweet one, you are here and not here.
EDCS-11501013.summary: This is a short, poignant epitaph for Antipatra, likely a funerary marker.

EDCS-11501015.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ponticus the brother, with his colleagues, for Antonio, as deserving, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-11501015.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonio, set up by his brother Ponticus and his colleagues, with his age at death and likely belonging to a collegium.

EDCS-11501016.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antonius Epigonus made this for his deserving wife Turrania Sabina, who lived 40 years and 3 months, and likewise for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-11501016.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Turrania Sabina and her descendants, set up by her husband Marcus Antonius Epigonus, with details of her age at death.

EDCS-11501017.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pontia Eutychia made this for her deserving husband Antonio Bellicus.
EDCS-11501017.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonio Bellicus, set up by his wife Pontia Eutychia.

EDCS-11501018.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Antonius Hyginus lived for 45 years. Marcus Antonius [erected this].
EDCS-11501018.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Hyginus, who lived 45 years, likely erected by a relative with the same name.

EDCS-11501019.translation: To the departed spirits. [To ---]ae, who lived 10 [---] years. Antonius Ierax made this for his incomparable and deserving wife.
EDCS-11501019.summary: A funerary epitaph for a woman, presumably the wife of Antonius Ierax, who lived an incomplete number of years.

EDCS-11501020.translation: [To ---]. Antonius Isidorus [made this] for his [brother(?)], who deserved well.
EDCS-11501020.summary: A funerary epitaph, partially reconstructed, likely for a brother of Antonius Isidorus.

EDCS-11501021.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Antonius Numida lived 9 years, 9 months, 3 days. Antonius Gallus made this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-11501021.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Antonius Numida, who died at the age of 9 years, 9 months, and 3 days, erected by his father.

EDCS-11501022.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Antonius Primus, who lived 12 years, 4 months, and 21 days, [for a] deserving parent.
EDCS-11501022.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antonius Primus, who lived 12 years, 4 months, and 21 days, erected by a deserving parent.

EDCS-11501023.translation: To the departed spirits. Tiberius Antonius Urbanus, who lived 1 year, 7 months, and 12 days. Urbicus and Faustina made this for their sweetest, deserving son.
EDCS-11501023.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Antonius Urbanus, who lived 1 year, 7 months, and 12 days, erected by his parents.

EDCS-11501024.translation: To the departed spirits. Antonia Athenais lived 35 years. Dinnius Potens, her husband, [made this] for her deserving wife.
EDCS-11501024.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antonia Athenais, who lived 35 years, erected by her husband.

EDCS-11501025.translation: To the departed spirits. Antoniae Censorinae lived 58 years, 3 months. Lucius Livius Aprilis(?) made this for his deserving mother.
EDCS-11501025.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antonia Censorina, who lived 58 years and 3 months, erected by her son.

EDCS-11501026.translation: To Antonia, daughter of Marcus, Laid.
EDCS-11501026.summary: A funerary dedication to Antonia Lais, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-11501027.translation: To the departed spirits. To Antonia Laodice. Lucius Iunius [---] Quarta, daughter, [made this] for her deserving [wife?].
EDCS-11501027.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antonia Laodice, likely erected by her daughter, Lucius Iunius Quarta.

EDCS-11501029.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Antonia Saturnina, who lived 34 years and 29 days. Marcellus Diomedes made [this] for his mother, who deserved well.
EDCS-11501029.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Antonia Saturnina and its dedication by her son, Marcellus Diomedes.

EDCS-11501030.translation: To good memory. Antoninus Apolinaris lived [---].
EDCS-11501030.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Antoninus Apolinaris, with the text cut off.

EDCS-11501031.summary: A fragmentary inscription, likely a name or epitaph, with the beginning missing.

EDCS-11501033.translation: Lucius Appuleius Barcus, freedman of Lucius; Appuleia Menophanta, freedwoman of Lucius; Lucius Appuleius Princeps, freedman of Lucius; Lucius Appuleius [---], freedman of Lucius; and Appuleia [---], freedwoman of Lucius; [and their] freedmen.
EDCS-11501033.summary: A funerary monument inscription listing freedmen and freedwomen of the Appuleian family, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-11501034.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Decimus Apuleius Iconicus made [this] for his wife Claudia Phileta, most excellent and most loving towards him, deserving well.
EDCS-11501034.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Decimus Apuleius Iconicus to his wife Claudia Phileta.

EDCS-11501035.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Afrodiso Liburnas made [this].
EDCS-11501035.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicating a monument to Afrodiso Liburnas.

EDCS-11501036.translation: [---] for his deserving freedman Felix, who lived 60 years. Afrodisia made [this].
EDCS-11501036.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of a freedman named Felix, dedicated by Afrodisia, likely his wife or patron.

EDCS-11501037.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aprodisia the pious lived 35 years. Nilas arranged for [this] to be made for her, deserving well.
EDCS-11501037.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aprodisia, detailing her age and that Nilas arranged for her monument.

EDCS-11501039.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aquila, Caesar's slave, lived 3 years. Rufus made [this] for his son.
EDCS-11501039.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young slave named Aquila, dedicated by Rufus, likely his father.

EDCS-11501040.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or funerary notice.

EDCS-11501042.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Arius Romanus lived 1 year 6 months. Arrius Frontinus made this for his most devoted son.
EDCS-11501042.summary: This is a tombstone for a child, Arius Romanus, set up by his father Arrius Frontinus.

EDCS-11501044.summary: This is a tombstone for Arria Aphrodisia, identified as a freedwoman.

EDCS-11501045.translation: [For Ar]ria Hostilia, daughter of Marcus, Valentina [---] / [---] Arrio Carpo [---]
EDCS-11501045.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a dedication or tombstone for Arria Hostilia Valentina and Arrio Carpo.

EDCS-11501046.translation: [---] Prima, freedwoman of Arria, and for her patroness Arria Elpis, freedwoman of Gaius.
EDCS-11501046.summary: This tombstone is for Prima, a freedwoman, and her patroness Arria Elpis.

EDCS-11501047.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Arruntius Capricornus lived 75 years. Gaius Arrius Philadelfus for his colleague, and Lucceia Vitalis for her mother, and Misena for her wife, made this for one deserving well.
EDCS-11501047.summary: This tombstone records the death of Arruntius Capricornus at age 75 and was set up by his colleague, his mother-in-law, and his wife.

EDCS-11501048.translation: [---] years old, [---] Arruntia [---] Pheste [---] for [---] most deserving well [---]
EDCS-11501048.summary: This is a fragmented tombstone recording the age and name of Arruntia.

EDCS-11501049.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Arulenus Zosimus made this for his wife Clodia Charidi.
EDCS-11501049.summary: This tombstone was set up by Lucius Arulenus Zosimus for his wife Clodia Charidi.

EDCS-11501050.translation: Asclepias of Serapis, Ammia Heliodorus, Sidonis.
EDCS-11501050.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or a list of names, possibly related to a cult or funerary context.

EDCS-11501051.translation: Asclepiades Reguli for himself and for his fellow slave Glucia, and for Euche, and Calybene, and Philodamus.
EDCS-11501051.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Asclepiades Reguli for himself and several other individuals, possibly fellow slaves or household members.

EDCS-11501052.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucio Asellio Mamilianus, son of Lucius, lived 17 years, 5 months, 5 days. His parents, Lucius Asellius Hermes, freedman of Lucius, and Mamilia Lyris, for their most devoted son. And Gaius Valerius Verus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-11501052.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lucio Asellio Mamilianus at age 17 and was set up by his parents and also includes Gaius Valerius Verus.

EDCS-11501053.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Rubrius Florus made this for his well-deserving father, Gnaeus Asinius Felix, who lived 61 years.
EDCS-11501053.summary: This is a funerary dedication on a tombstone for Gnaeus Asinius Felix, made by his son Lucius Rubrius Florus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501054.translation: To Marcus Asinius Hymnus. Renata, his wife.
EDCS-11501054.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Asinius Hymnus, set up by his wife Renata, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501057.translation: To Ateia Prima, freedwoman of Gaius Ateius. To Ateia Latina, freedwoman of a woman. This monument will not follow its heir.
EDCS-11501057.summary: A funerary dedication on a tombstone for two women, Ateia Prima and Ateia Latina, likely from the Imperial period, indicating the burial plot is not meant to be inherited.

EDCS-11501058.translation: To the departed spirits. Decimus Aterius Hermes for himself and his family, and for his wife Onesima, and for their descendants, and for his grandson Aterius Priscus. He lived 5 years, 5 months, 10 days.
EDCS-11501058.summary: A funerary dedication on a tombstone by Decimus Aterius Hermes for himself, his family including his wife Onesima and grandson Aterius Priscus, and also records the lifespan of a child, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501059.translation: To the departed spirits. To Athenais, daughter of Athenion. She lived 8 years, 4 months, 8 days.
EDCS-11501059.summary: A funerary dedication on a tombstone for a young girl named Athenais, daughter of Athenion, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501061.translation: To the departed spirits. To Atilia Iadis. She lived 67 years, 8 months, 11 days. Atilia Fructuosa, her granddaughter, for her well-deserving relative.
EDCS-11501061.summary: A funerary dedication on a tombstone by Atilia Fructuosa for her grandmother Atilia Iadis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501062.translation: To the departed spirits. To Atilia Tyche. [?]utiola. [?]biae.
EDCS-11501062.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication on a tombstone for Atilia Tyche, with some damaged or illegible names, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501063.translation: To the departed spirits. Attius Epictetus and Processa Secundina made this for their husband/wife while they were alive.
EDCS-11501063.summary: A funerary dedication on a tombstone by Attius Epictetus and Processa Secundina, likely a married couple, who set up the monument for one of them during their lifetime, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501064.summary: A fragmentary inscription beginning with the name Quintus Attius, possibly a dedication or ownership mark, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501065.summary: An inscription recording the name Quintus Attius Resus, possibly a name stone or ownership mark, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501066.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Attia Afrodisia set this up for her well-deserving patroness Attia Nebridis, as a mark of honor.
EDCS-11501066.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Attia Afrodisia for her patroness Attia Nebridis.

EDCS-11501068.translation: Titus Aufidius Alexander lived 85 years. Aufidia Procula, daughter of Titus, lived 26 years. Aufidius Cornelianus and Lucia Aufidiana made this.
EDCS-11501068.summary: This funerary monument records the ages of Titus Aufidius Alexander and Aufidia Procula, and was erected by Aufidius Cornelianus and Lucia Aufidiana.

EDCS-11501069.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caius Aufidius Ampliatus for his well-deserving patron Caius Aufidius Eutychus.
EDCS-11501069.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Caius Aufidius Ampliatus for his patron Caius Aufidius Eutychus.

EDCS-11501070.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Aufidius Proculus and Neria Quartilla for their daughter Neria Successa, who lived 7 years and 2 months.
EDCS-11501070.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Neria Successa, daughter of Titus Aufidius Proculus and Neria Quartilla, at the age of 7 years and 2 months.

EDCS-11501071.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Titus Aufidius Saturninus, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-11501073.translation: Aufidia Euphrosyne, freedwoman of a woman, for herself and for her son Titus Aufidius Templitanus, son of Titus, of the Falernian tribe, who lived 19 years, and for her other family members.
EDCS-11501073.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aufidia Euphrosyne for herself, her son Titus Aufidius Templitanus who died at 19, and other family members.

EDCS-11501074.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Aufidia Gamic(a), who lived 60 years, 3 months, and 5 days, her loving daughter made this for her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-11501074.summary: This funerary dedication was made by a loving daughter for her mother, Aufidia Gamic(a), who lived to be 60 years, 3 months, and 5 days old.

EDCS-11501075.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Augurio, unparalleled, sweet, and destructive, who lived 2 years and 8 months, his lovers made this, though he did not deserve it.
EDCS-11501075.summary: This funerary dedication was made by 'lovers' for Augurio, who died at the age of 2 years and 8 months.

EDCS-11501080.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Aulia Hya lived for 45 years.

EDCS-11501081.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Tiberius Aurelius Artemisius, father, who lived 65 years and 15 days, Cattia Apricula made this for him, as he deserved well.
EDCS-11501081.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Cattia Apricula for her father Tiberius Aurelius Artemisius, who lived 65 years and 15 days.

EDCS-11501082.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His brothers had this made for Aurelius Chariton, who lived 22 years.
EDCS-11501082.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by brothers for their deceased sibling, Aurelius Chariton, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-11501083.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publilia Vitalis for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Aurelius Chrysanthus, freedman of the emperor, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-11501083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, Marcus Aurelius Chrysanthus, a freedman of the emperor, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-11501085.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Trofime made this for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Aurelius Festus, an incomparable man, who lived 29 years and 15 days.
EDCS-11501085.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, Marcus Aurelius Festus, who died at the age of 29 years and 15 days.

EDCS-11501086.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Misenus made this for his well-deserving brother, Aurelius Flavius, an incomparable brother, who lived 18 years, 9 months, and 26 days.
EDCS-11501086.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a brother for his deceased sibling, Aurelius Flavius, who died at the age of 18 years, 9 months, and 26 days.

EDCS-11501087.translation: Aurelius Fortunatus made this for his well-deserving wife, Felicula, who lived 35 years, 11 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-11501087.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his wife, Felicula, who died at the age of 35 years, 11 months, and 9 days.

EDCS-11501088.translation: Aurelius Hospitianos and Julius Serenus placed this for their well-deserving Julia Dioscoruti, who lived 18 years and 6 days. (Greek text follows with same content).
EDCS-11501088.summary: This funerary epitaph, bilingual in Latin and Greek, was set up by Aurelius Hospitianos and Julius Serenus for their deceased companion, Julia Dioscoruti, who died at the age of 18 years and 6 days.

EDCS-11501089.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Casta, mother, made this for her well-deserving son, Aurelius Misenus, who lived 16 years, 2 months, and 25 days.
EDCS-11501089.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her son, Aurelius Misenus, who died at the age of 16 years, 2 months, and 25 days.

EDCS-11501090.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Natalis made this for his well-deserving wife, Aurelia Bassa Marianetis, also called Simplicia, his sweetest, incomparable, honest, and good wife, who lived 22 years, 2 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-11501090.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his wife, Aurelia Bassa Marianetis, also known as Simplicia, who died at the age of 22 years, 2 months, and 21 days.

EDCS-11501091.translation: Marcus Aurelius Nicolaus made this for his most beloved, well-deserving wife, Aurelia Hygias.
EDCS-11501091.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by Marcus Aurelius Nicolaus for his wife, Aurelia Hygias.

EDCS-11501092.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aquilia Calliste made this for her well-deserving husband, Aurelius Quirinus Merodotus, who lived fifty years.
EDCS-11501092.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, Aurelius Quirinus Merodotus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-11501093.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelius Romanio, who lived 15 years, 4 months. Aurelia Aerais made [this] for her well-deserving son. Acilius Primus, father.
EDCS-11501093.summary: This is a tombstone set up by a father for his deceased son, specifying his age at death.

EDCS-11501094.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelio Ursulo, who lived 40 years, 2 months, 12 days. Aurelia Libertus, daughter.
EDCS-11501094.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating Aurelio Ursulo, likely his daughter Aurelia Libertus placed it, and his age at death is given.

EDCS-11501095.translation: To the departed spirits. Gessius Ianuarius made [this] for his well-deserving wife, Aurelia Hellenis.
EDCS-11501095.summary: This is a tombstone set up by a husband for his deceased wife, calling her well-deserving.

EDCS-11501096.translation: To Aurelia Valeriana, who lived 24 years, 7 months, 13 days. Elvia Chresta [made this] for her most beloved daughter.
EDCS-11501096.summary: This tombstone honors Aurelia Valeriana, recording her age at death and erected by her mother, Elvia Chresta.

EDCS-11501097.translation: To the departed spirits. Emilia Afrodita. Aurelia Puteolana made [this] for her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-11501097.summary: This tombstone was dedicated to the departed spirits by Aurelia Puteolana for her well-deserving mother, Emilia Afrodita.

EDCS-11501098.translation: To the departed spirits. For Autronia Rhodina, daughter of Marcus, who lived 15 years. Her mother, Rupilia Eutychia.
EDCS-11501098.summary: This tombstone is for Autronia Rhodina, who died at 15 years of age, and was erected by her mother.

EDCS-11501099.translation: To the departed spirits. For Babullia Hermerotis. Publius Marcius Fortis, father.
EDCS-11501099.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Babullia Hermerotis, with Publius Marcius Fortis identified as her father.

EDCS-11501100.summary: This inscription fragment seems to record the name and filiation of an individual named Badius Stabilio.

EDCS-11501102.translation: To the departed spirits. For Publius Baebius Sollemnianus, who lived 20 years. His heir Maevia Sabina made [this] for him, well-deserving.
EDCS-11501102.summary: This tombstone was erected by the heir, Maevia Sabina, for Publius Baebius Sollemnianus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-11501103.translation: To the departed spirits. For Baebia Venusta, wife, who lived 28 years, 9 months. Gnaeus Pompeius Modestus, husband, made [this] for her, well-deserving.
EDCS-11501103.summary: This tombstone records the death of Baebia Venusta at age 28 years and 9 months, and was erected by her husband.

EDCS-11501104.summary: This is a tombstone for Barbatia Felicula, recording her age at death.

EDCS-11501105.translation: Titus Barbius Ascla, freedman of Titus; Baria Prota, freedwoman of Titus; Lucius Fabius Homuncio, freedman of Lucius, alive; Fabia Thais, freedwoman of Lucius, mother.
EDCS-11501105.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, likely family members, and their relationship, possibly on a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-11501108.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Bellienus Genialis, who lived for 33 years, 7 months, and 14 days. Fania Sicundina made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11501108.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Lucius Bellienus Genialis by his wife Fania Sicundina, and records his age at death.

EDCS-11501109.translation: To the departed spirits. Benustus lived for 30 years, 3 months, and 7 days. Ampelis made this for her well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11501109.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Benustus by his wife Ampelis, and records his age at death.

EDCS-11501111.translation: To the departed spirits. Bibia Bico[---] [---]M[---]
EDCS-11501111.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone, likely dedicated to Bibia Bico, with the name of the dedicator or a further description missing.

EDCS-11501112.translation: To the departed spirits. Martius Zenon made this for his most deserving wife Bisellia Felicisima, who lived for 28 years and 6 hours of the night.
EDCS-11501112.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Bisellia Felicisima by her husband Martius Zenon, and records her age at death.

EDCS-11501113.translation: To the departed spirits. Blaesius Sustus made this for his well-deserving mother Blaesia Fortunata, who lived for more or less 50 years.
EDCS-11501113.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Blaesia Fortunata by her son Blaesius Sustus, and records her approximate age at death.

EDCS-11501115.translation: To the departed spirits. For Bovius Procula, daughter of Lucius, the most miserable mother.
EDCS-11501115.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to Bovius Procula, identified as a 'most miserable mother'.

EDCS-11501119.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aelius Maximo, husband, made this for his well-deserving wife Brinnia Epigonia, also called Flavia, the excellent, the incomparable, who lived with me in marriage for 14 years and 6 days, while he himself is alive, for himself and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-11501119.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by Marcus Aelius Maximo to his wife Brinnia Epigonia, recording their marriage duration and also serving as a dedication for himself and his freedmen.

EDCS-11501121.translation: Lucius Bruttius Firmus made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-11501121.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Bruttius Firmus for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-11501123.translation: To Timesius Buccio for himself and for Timesius Glyco, patron, and for Bellia, freedwoman of Sextus, named Isia, and for Bellia, freedwoman of a woman, named Callista, and for the freedman Themidos, and his wife, and for the freedman Diatheton.
EDCS-11501123.summary: This funerary inscription records a self-dedication and dedications to patrons and freedpersons, likely for a burial plot or collegium, with the names suggesting a mid-to-late Imperial date.

EDCS-11501124.translation: Quintus Caecilius Diadumenus lived for 9 years, 3 months, and 14 days.
EDCS-11501124.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Quintus Caecilius Diadumenus, a child, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501125.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Caecilius Eutyches, Caecilia Pothine, his freedwoman, and Marcus Caecilius Epaphroditus, fellow freedman, [dedicated this] to an excellent man.
EDCS-11501125.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by freedmen and a freedwoman to their patron, Marcus Caecilius Eutyches, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501127.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Caecilio Primioni et adiutrici coniugi vivis faciendum curavit Caecilia Triphona fratri pro piaetati sic consacratu sic\n
EDCS-11501127.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caecilia Triphona had this made during their lifetime for her brother, Marcus Caecilius Primio, and for his helper wife, out of dutiful affection, thus consecrated, thus.
EDCS-11501127.summary: This funerary inscription records a wife, Caecilia Triphona, commissioning a monument for her brother and his wife, indicating a late antique or Imperial date.

EDCS-11501128.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sabina made [this] for her well-deserving nurse, Caecilia Eupraesia.
EDCS-11501128.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Sabina to her nurse, Caecilia Eupraesia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501129.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Heraclia, daughter of Marcus Caecilius, lived for 18 years, 8 months, and 28 days.
EDCS-11501129.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Heraclia, daughter of Marcus Caecilius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501131.translation: Caecilia Nice lived for 56 years, 3 months, and 10 days. Marcus Ocabius made [this] for his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-11501131.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Caecilia Nice and was made by her son, Marcus Ocabius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501132.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Valerius Theophilus [made this] for his excellent wife, Caecilia Pyramis.
EDCS-11501132.summary: This funerary inscription records a husband, Marcus Valerius Theophilus, dedicating a monument to his wife, Caecilia Pyramis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501133.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Fabius Taurus made [this] for his most rare wife, Caecilia Tryphena, who lived for 65 years.
EDCS-11501133.summary: This funerary inscription records a husband, Marcus Fabius Taurus, dedicating a monument to his wife, Caecilia Tryphena, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501134.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Tiberius Caepius Ponticus. Caepia Onesime, his wife, [made this] for her husband, who deserved well, and for herself, and for their descendants.
EDCS-11501134.summary: This funerary inscription records a wife, Caepia Onesime, dedicating a monument to her husband, Tiberius Caepius Ponticus, and for herself and their descendants, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11501135.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aulus Caesellius Helico made this for Aulus Caesellius Atticus, his student.
EDCS-11501135.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a patron named Helico to his student Atticus, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501136.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Lucius Caeselius Satus, who [...]
EDCS-11501136.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication to Lucius Caeselius Satus, with the dedicant and further details missing.

EDCS-11501137.translation: Marcus Caesius Augustales, who lived for 8 months; his mother Iulia Galleia [made this] for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-11501137.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a very young child, Marcus Caesius Augustales, made by his mother, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501138.translation: [Gaius] Caesius, son of Gaius, freedman of Gaius P[...], his father; Gaius Caesius, son of Gaius, [made this] C[...]
EDCS-11501138.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription, possibly indicating a father and son relationship with partially restored names and filiations.

EDCS-11501139.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caesius Regillus [made this] for his most excellent father, Gaius Caesius, freedman of Gaius, Rufinus.
EDCS-11501139.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a son, Caesius Regillus, to his father, Gaius Caesius Rufinus, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501140.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Numisius Ingenuus made this for his most excellent wife, Caesias, daughter of Gaius, Isityche, who deserved well.
EDCS-11501140.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband, Gaius Numisius Ingenuus, to his wife, Caesias Isityche, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501141.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sacconius Eufemus [made this] for his brother, Publius Caesonio Agathursus, who lived for 23 years.
EDCS-11501141.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a brother, Sacconius Eufemus, to his deceased brother, Publius Caesonio Agathursus, who died at age 23.

EDCS-11501142.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Polus made this for his well-deserving fellow slave, Caesonia Luccie, who lived for 28 years.
EDCS-11501142.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a fellow slave, Polus, to Caesonia Luccie, who died at age 28, indicating a relationship of co-enslavement.

EDCS-11501144.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Genucius Pancalus made this for his well-deserving stepdaughter, Calabrica, who lived for 17 years, 8 months, and 7 days.
EDCS-11501144.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a stepfather, Lucius Genucius Pancalus, to his stepdaughter, Calabrica, who died at age 17, 8 months, and 7 days.

EDCS-11501145.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Phoebe, wife, [made this] for Numerius Calavius Eusebes.
EDCS-11501145.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a wife, Claudia Phoebe, to her husband, Numerius Calavius Eusebes.

EDCS-11501146.translation: To the departed spirits. [His] fellow slave Cale made [this] for [her] well-deserving self.
EDCS-11501146.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a fellow slave to Cale, likely a woman, in the Roman era.

EDCS-11501147.translation: To the departed spirits. Calinius Felix lived for 32 years.
EDCS-11501147.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Calinius Felix, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-11501148.translation: To the departed spirits. Lived for 24 years, 9 months, and 20 days. Cassia Nicomedia and Callinicus [made this] for their most devoted and rarest son.
EDCS-11501148.summary: A funerary epitaph from the Roman period, set up by parents for their young son.

EDCS-11501149.translation: To Callisto, son of Callistus. Sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-11501149.summary: A funerary dedication to Callisto, son of Callistus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11501150.translation: To the departed spirits of Calpurnius Apollinaris.
EDCS-11501150.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph from the Roman era, dedicated to Calpurnius Apollinaris.

EDCS-11501154.translation: To the departed spirits. Calpurnius Phi[---] for himself and for Calpurnia [---] his most excellent wife.
EDCS-11501154.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph from the Roman period, intended for a man, his wife, and possibly others.

EDCS-11501155.translation: To the departed spirits of Lucius Calpurnius Speratus.
EDCS-11501155.summary: A funerary epitaph from the Roman era, dedicated to Lucius Calpurnius Speratus.

EDCS-11501156.translation: Of Gaius Calpurnius Voltidianus. Larcia Paulina.
EDCS-11501156.summary: A dedication from the Roman period, possibly a tombstone, mentioning Gaius Calpurnius Voltidianus and Larcia Paulina.

EDCS-11501158.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia Thyme [made this] for Calpurnia Braemusa, [her] most devoted mother.
EDCS-11501158.summary: A funerary epitaph from the Roman period, set up by a daughter for her devoted mother.

EDCS-11501159.translation: To the departed spirits of Calpurnia Capitoline, [and] for [her] freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-11501159.summary: A funerary inscription from the Roman era, dedicated to Calpurnia Capitoline and her freed persons and their descendants.

EDCS-11501160.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Calpurnius December made this for his excellent wife, Calpurnia Lacaena.
EDCS-11501160.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife, Calpurnia Lacaena, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501161.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Calpurnia Procula lived for 60 years. Gaius Iulius Maximus and Gaius Iulius Dionysius, her son and heir, made this for one deserving of kindness.
EDCS-11501161.summary: A funerary dedication for Calpurnia Procula, placed by her son and heir, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501163.translation: For Gaius Calvius Amomus, freedman of Gaius Iunius Iuniae Tertullae; for Atticus, freedman of Iunia Thais, freedwoman of Gaius Iunius Tertullus; and for Theodotos, freedman of Atticus, and for their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-11501163.summary: A dedication to freedmen and freedwomen of several families, likely an epitaph for Atticus and Theodotos and their dependents, possibly from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-11501164.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Calvio Ofellioni qui vixit annis VIII Calvia Callityche fratri dulcissimo fecit\n
EDCS-11501164.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Calvia Callityche made this for her dearest brother, Marcus Calvius Ofellio, who lived for 8 years.
EDCS-11501164.summary: A funerary dedication by a sister for her younger brother, Marcus Calvius Ofellio, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501166.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Calybe. Her children, Fuscina and Faustinus, made this for their deserving mother.
EDCS-11501166.summary: A funerary dedication by children for their mother, Calybe, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501167.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Lucius Campylius Liberalis made this for himself while alive, and for Campylia Domitia, and for their freedmen, and he forbade anyone from outside to be buried here.
EDCS-11501167.summary: A self-funded funerary monument for Lucius Campylius Liberalis and Campylia Domitia, with a restriction on further burials, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501172.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Flavia Augustiana made this for Carisia Gaurana.
EDCS-11501172.summary: A funerary dedication by Flavia Augustiana for Carisia Gaurana, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501173.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Carpus lived 16 years. Claudius Herennianus made this for his excellent pupil.
EDCS-11501173.summary: A funerary dedication by Claudius Herennianus for his young pupil, Carpus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501174.summary: A dedication, likely an epitaph, for Marcus Carvilius Thallus, possibly from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-11501175.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Cascellia Agrippina.
EDCS-11501175.summary: A funerary dedication for Cascellia Agrippina, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501176.translation: Casonia, freedwoman of Quintus, and Servia.
EDCS-11501176.summary: This inscription records the names of two women, Casonia and Servia, likely from a funerary context.

EDCS-11501178.translation: To the departed spirits. His most unfortunate mother made this for Lucius Cassius Maximus, well-deserving.
EDCS-11501178.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a mother to her son, Lucius Cassius Maximus.

EDCS-11501179.translation: To the departed spirits. Apphi made this for her relative Cassius Primigenius and for her daughter Olympias, well-deserving.
EDCS-11501179.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Apphi to her relative Cassius Primigenius and her daughter Olympias.

EDCS-11501181.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. His sons and wife made this for Gnaeus Cassius Zosimus, his father and patron, well-deserving.
EDCS-11501181.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by sons and a wife to Gnaeus Cassius Zosimus, who was both father and patron.

EDCS-11501182.translation: To the departed spirits. Cassiae Lucillae, daughter of Cassia Procula, and Cassius Proculus, grandson.
EDCS-11501182.summary: This funerary inscription lists the names of Cassiae Lucillae and Cassius Proculus, indicating family relationships.

EDCS-11501183.translation: To the departed spirits. Octavia Charitus made this for her granddaughter Cassia Rufina, who lived for 4 years and 25 days, well-deserving.
EDCS-11501183.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cassia Rufina, who died at a young age, dedicated by her grandmother Octavia Charitus.

EDCS-11501184.translation: To the departed spirits. Castricia Demetria made this for her husband Quintus Castricius Aprilis, an incomparable man, well-deserving.
EDCS-11501184.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her husband, Quintus Castricius Aprilis.

EDCS-11501185.translation: To the departed spirits. Pontia Lupula made this for her most devoted son Sextus Castricius Felix, who lived 29 years, 6 months, and 12 days.
EDCS-11501185.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Sextus Castricius Felix at the age of 29, dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-11501189.translation: To Caius Claudius Tri[---], freedman of Caius. Claudia Philumina, freedwoman of Caius, [dedicated] this statue to her patron, well-deserving, truthful, and innocent. Hail, farewell.
EDCS-11501189.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to a patron, Caius Claudius Tri[---], by his freedwoman Claudia Philumina.

EDCS-11501190.translation: To the departed spirits. Clodius Fructuosus and Ceionia Helias made this for their incomparable son Lucius Ceionius Fructuosus, who lived 6 years, 9 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-11501190.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Lucius Ceionius Fructuosus at a young age, dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-11501191.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Ceionius Olenius made this for his foster son Alfio, who deserved well, who lived 27 years, 4 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-11501191.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Ceionius Olenius, commemorating his foster son and stating his age at death.

EDCS-11501193.translation: To the departed spirits. Celeris [lived?] [---]. Claudia [---] gave [this] to her slave.
EDCS-11501193.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph potentially recording the death of a slave named Celeris, dedicated by Claudia.

EDCS-11501194.translation: To the departed spirits, of good memory. Mettia Candida made this for her husband Cepio Polycleto, who lived 26 years, 5 months, and 23 days, with whom she lived 15 years.
EDCS-11501194.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cepio Polycleto, set up by his wife Mettia Candida, stating his age at death and the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-11501195.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Lucius Charisius Cyrus and Pollia Tyche.
EDCS-11501195.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits for Lucius Charisius Cyrus and Pollia Tyche.

EDCS-11501196.translation: Charite, of the same [family?], lived 21 years.
EDCS-11501196.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Charite, stating her age at death.

EDCS-11501197.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a boy named Cladus, stating his age at death.
