EDCS-11501199.translation: To the departed spirits. [---]tus, [son?] of [---]ilius, [son?] of [---]ipus, the good [---], lived [---] years, 3 months.
EDCS-11501199.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph that likely records the death of a man named [---]tus, mentioning his parentage and age at death.

EDCS-11501201.translation: To the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Baccus [set this up?] for Tiberius Claudius Basitanus, [his son?], untimely [lost?].
EDCS-11501201.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely set up by Tiberius Claudius Baccyllus for Tiberius Claudius Basitanus, lamenting his premature death.

EDCS-11501202.translation: To the departed spirits. Rutilia Margaris made this for her well-deserving husband Tiberius Claudius Biton, who lived 85 years.
EDCS-11501202.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Biton, set up by his wife Rutilia Margaris, stating his age at death.

EDCS-11501203.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudia Saturnina [made this?] for her most devoted brother Tiberius Claudius Chrysanthus(?), who lived 17 years, 30 days.
EDCS-11501203.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Chrysanthus, set up by his sister Claudia Saturnina, stating his age at death.

EDCS-11501204.translation: To the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Damas for himself and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants and their heirs.
EDCS-11501204.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Tiberius Claudius Damas for himself and his descendants and freedmen, likely on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-11501206.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Diadumenus, a friend. Iulia Barrilla [dedicates this].
EDCS-11501206.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Iulia Barrilla to her friend Tiberius Claudius Diadumenus, likely on a monument or tomb.

EDCS-11501207.translation: Tiberius Claudius Eutyches lived for 4 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-11501207.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Tiberius Claudius Eutyches, recording his name, lifespan, and burial, dating to his lifetime.

EDCS-11501208.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Minucius Minucianus and his freedman Processus to their excellent friend Tiberius Claudius Eutyches.
EDCS-11501208.summary: This funerary inscription by Marcus Minucius Minucianus and his freedman Processus honors their friend Tiberius Claudius Eutyches, likely on his tomb.

EDCS-11501209.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Fructus lived for 13 years and 4 months.
EDCS-11501209.summary: This funerary inscription records the age at death of Tiberius Claudius Fructus, likely on his tomb.

EDCS-11501210.translation: Iulia Auge [dedicates this] to her husband Tiberius Claudius Fructus, freedman of Alpinius, who deserved well.
EDCS-11501210.summary: This is a dedication by Iulia Auge to her husband Tiberius Claudius Fructus, who was also a freedman, likely on his tomb.

EDCS-11501211.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudius Fuficianus [dedicates this] to his most worthy patron Titus Claudius Fuficianus.
EDCS-11501211.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Claudius Fuficianus to his patron Titus Claudius Fuficianus, likely on the patron's tomb.

EDCS-11501212.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudius Gemellus lived 19 years. Valerius Bitalis, his heir, [dedicates this] to him who deserved well.
EDCS-11501212.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Claudius Gemellus and was made by his heir Valerius Bitalis, likely on his tomb.

EDCS-11501213.translation: To the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Mercurialis lived 26 years. Tiberius Claudius Mercurius made [this] for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-11501213.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Tiberius Claudius Mercurialis and was made by his father, Tiberius Claudius Mercurius.

EDCS-11501215.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Tiberius Claudius Calligenis, son of Quirin(a) Scymnus.
EDCS-11501215.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication to Tiberius Claudius Calligenis by his parent Quirin(a) Scymnus, likely on his tomb.

EDCS-11501216.translation: To the shades of the departed. Lucius Claudius [Seleucus?] wishes this place not to be alienated for himself and for Claudia Iusta and Antonia Lacena and their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-11501216.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a private individual, likely from the Roman Imperial period, stating the owner's wish that the burial plot not be sold or transferred.

EDCS-11501217.translation: To the shades of the departed. Tiberius Claudius Sextus made this for his son Tiberius Claudius Severus, who lived for 7 years, 11 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-11501217.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child, Tiberius Claudius Severus, who died at a young age, set up by his father who also established a burial for another son. It is likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501218.translation: To the shades of the departed. Tiberius Claudius Stephanus and Mindia Drosis made this for their exceptionally rare daughter, Claudia Heraclia, who lived 17 years, 5 months, and 23 days. (She was) deserving of it.
EDCS-11501218.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Heraclia, set up by her parents, Tiberius Claudius Stephanus and Mindia Drosis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501219.translation: Tiberius Claudius Successus lived 28 years. Fortunata his wife [made this] for her deserving husband.
EDCS-11501219.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Successus, erected by his wife Fortunata, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501220.translation: Sacred to the shades of the departed. Severa his wife [made this] for Claudius Threptolemus.
EDCS-11501220.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Threptolemus, set up by his wife Severa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501221.translation: To the shades of the departed. Flavia Cyteria made this for her deserving husband, Tiberius Claudius Trophimus, who lived 26 years, 9 months, and 29 days.
EDCS-11501221.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Trophimus, erected by his wife Flavia Cyteria, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501222.translation: Claudius [---] son, sweetest, who lived two years.
EDCS-11501222.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for a very young child named Claudius, set up by his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501224.translation: To the shades of the departed. Tiberius Claudius Adiutor made this for his deserving grandmother, Claudia Alypias, daughter of Spurius, who lived 54 years and 7 days.
EDCS-11501224.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Alypias, set up by her grandson Tiberius Claudius Adiutor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501225.translation: To the shades of the departed. Titus Flavius Claudianus made this for his deserving mother, Claudia Antiochis, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-11501225.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Antiochis, set up by her son Titus Flavius Claudianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501226.translation: Sacred to the shades of the departed. Lucius Munatius Hermes made this for his deserving wife, Claudia Asteris.
EDCS-11501226.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Asteris, erected by her husband Lucius Munatius Hermes, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501227.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Caenis [dedicated this] for herself [and others].
EDCS-11501227.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia Caenis, likely a self-dedication.

EDCS-11501228.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Claudia Parthenis, her dearest friend.
EDCS-11501228.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by an unknown person to their "dearest friend," Claudia Parthenis.

EDCS-11501229.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valerius Restutus made this for his deserving wife, Claudial Felicula, who lived 24 years.
EDCS-11501229.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Claudia Felicula at age 24 and was made by her husband, Valerius Restutus.

EDCS-11501230.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Claudia Fortunata and her children Fortuna and Laetus, who deserved well. Abascantus, fellow freedman, made this. Whoever disturbs the Manes will incur their wrath.
EDCS-11501230.summary: This funerary inscription by Abascantus is for Claudia Fortunata and her children, Fortuna and Laetus, and includes a curse against those who disturb the grave.

EDCS-11501231.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Claudia Graptene, her most dutiful daughter, who lived 8 years, 4 months, and 20 days. Her parents [dedicated this].
EDCS-11501231.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Claudia Graptene, who died at a young age, and was dedicated by her parents.

EDCS-11501232.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Ianua lived 59 years. Flavius Longus [dedicated this] to his most sacred wife.
EDCS-11501232.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Claudia Ianua at age 59 and was dedicated by her husband, Flavius Longus.

EDCS-11501233.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Claudia Maria.

EDCS-11501234.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Claudia Nice, for her deserving husband, Aulus Cossinius Felix, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-11501234.summary: This funerary inscription records Claudia Nice dedicating a tomb to her deserving husband, Aulus Cossinius Felix.

EDCS-11501235.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Claudia Primilla, who lived 18 years, 1 month, and 10 days. Claudia Primigenia and Primus, her parents, [dedicated this] to their most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-11501235.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Claudia Primilla at age 18 and was dedicated by her parents, Claudia Primigenia and Primus.

EDCS-11501236.translation: To Claudia Zosima, for her dearest mother, Claudia Onesime.
EDCS-11501236.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Claudia Onesime to her dearest mother, Claudia Zosima.

EDCS-11501237.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Clodius Alexander, who lived for [about] eight months and 20 days. Stratonice erected this for her most delightful granddaughter.
EDCS-11501237.summary: A funerary dedication for Clodius Alexander, erected by his granddaughter Stratonice, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501238.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Clodius December and Sulpicia Fortunata erected this for Phoebus, their cherished slave, who lived for 25 years.
EDCS-11501238.summary: A funerary epitaph for the slave Phoebus, commissioned by his owners Lucius Clodius December and Sulpicia Fortunata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501239.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Clodius Primitivus, who lived for 37 years. Clodius Poplares saw to its construction for his son.
EDCS-11501239.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Clodius Primitivus, erected by his father Clodius Poplares, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501240.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Clodia Apates. Marcus Iulius Mitulus [erected this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11501240.summary: A funerary dedication for Clodia Apates, set up by her husband Marcus Iulius Mitulus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501241.translation: Clodia Natalis erected this for herself and for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11501241.summary: A funerary dedication for Clodia Natalis and her husband, self-commissioned, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501242.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Clodia Piroclea, who lived for 40 years. Lucia Hamonilla, her granddaughter and heir, erected this for her well-deserving [grandmother].
EDCS-11501242.summary: A funerary epitaph for Clodia Piroclea, erected by her granddaughter and heir Lucia Hamonilla, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501243.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Clodia Soteria, deserving of good will. Clodia Cleopatra dedicated this to her mother.
EDCS-11501243.summary: A funerary dedication for Clodia Soteria, set up by her daughter Clodia Cleopatra, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501244.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Claudia Paulina, for herself and for her children, and for her freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. Placed here. If anyone should sell [this tomb]...
EDCS-11501244.summary: A funerary dedication for Claudia Paulina, intended for herself, her descendants, and her freedmen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501246.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cluvia Corinthia Dionysia, [daughter] of Laconidus, her most beloved mother.
EDCS-11501246.summary: A funerary dedication for Cluvia Corinthia Dionysia, erected by her daughter, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501248.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cocceius Idaeus, who lived for 19 years. His mother, Dellia Successa, [erected this for her] well-deserving son.
EDCS-11501248.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cocceius Idaeus, erected by his mother Dellia Successa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501249.translation: To the divine shades. To Marcus Cocceius Zosimus, his well-deserving wife Junia Philumene lived 27 years and 3 months.
EDCS-11501249.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Cocceius Zosimus, set up by his wife.

EDCS-11501250.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Gaius Valerius Philocles for Cocceia Glaphyra, his freedwoman.
EDCS-11501250.summary: A funerary dedication to Cocceia Glaphyra by Gaius Valerius Philocles.

EDCS-11501251.translation: To Cocceia Thallusae, who lived 16 years. Lucius I[---] Cocceius Coeranus [dedicated this to his] wife Blandae. You who read this inscription, why do you labor to know my name, to whom I was married, or to whose freedwoman I belonged? My years, what I have suffered, you would surely pity if you knew. Therefore, I beg you, listen, so that there may be no grief. Everything perishes with life and becomes nothing. Since we commonly believe this and are persuaded, that a guest should leave indications of his life inscribed on a tombstone, I ask that it not be burdensome to know in a few words the uncertain fates I endured and how fierce a desire there is for anyone yet to be born to proceed further in time. When the thirteenth year brought me forth, I took my husband's name and rested in his embrace. But I did not touch my desires; fate brought me. For in a short time I had these happy omens, and they were arranged with the resources of Blandae, before cruel, creeping death resolved all things. The third sun rising, I saw with my husband, and yet not entirely, and scarcely the fifth of the Kalends thereafter. From this, you will know that I lived my sixteenth year. The fates gave this as the end of my life. [---] this resting place, my husband [---] to me, to whom happy [---]
EDCS-11501251.summary: A long and elaborate funerary poem for Cocceia Thallusae, who died at age 16, written by her husband Lucius I[---] Cocceius Coeranus, lamenting her early death and reflecting on the transience of life.

EDCS-11501252.translation: To the divine shades. To Marcus Coelius Callistinus, who lived 24 years, 3 months, and 23 days. Callistus, his father, to his deserving son.
EDCS-11501252.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Coelius Callistinus, set up by his father.

EDCS-11501253.translation: To Gaius Coelius Coelianus. Marcus Coelius Tertullus, [his] foster-father.
EDCS-11501253.summary: A dedication to Gaius Coelius Coelianus by his foster-father, Marcus Coelius Tertullus.

EDCS-11501255.translation: To the divine shades. Coelia Ianuaria made this for herself, and for her husband Marcus Coelius Vitalis, and for their son Marcus Coelius Iulianus, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. In front 15.5 feet, in depth 14 feet.
EDCS-11501255.summary: A funerary dedication for a family and their descendants, specifying plot dimensions, made by Coelia Ianuaria.

EDCS-11501256.translation: To the divine shades. To Coelia Symferusa. Cognitus, her son, [dedicated this to his] most rare and affectionate woman.
EDCS-11501256.summary: A funerary epitaph for Coelia Symferusa, set up by her son Cognitus.

EDCS-11501257.translation: To the divine shades. To Coelia Tertulla, 13 years and 7 months. Her father [dedicated this].
EDCS-11501257.summary: A funerary epitaph for Coelia Tertulla, who died at age 13 years and 7 months, set up by her father.

EDCS-11501259.translation: To the divine shades. To Lucius Cominius Hercules, his well-deserving.
EDCS-11501259.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Cominius Hercules.

EDCS-11501261.translation: To the divine shades below. To our sweetest son Concordianus, who lived 1 year and 17 days. His parents made this for their best son.
EDCS-11501261.summary: A funerary epitaph for Concordianus, who died in infancy, set up by his parents.

EDCS-11501262.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Consius Chaeres.
EDCS-11501262.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Consius Chaeres.

EDCS-11501263.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For a well-deserving patron, made by Cornelia Max[--], freedwoman and wife, daughter of Marcus Cornelius Decimus.
EDCS-11501263.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a freedwoman and wife for her well-deserving patron.

EDCS-11501264.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cornelius Aquilinus, who lived 36 years.
EDCS-11501264.summary: A funerary dedication for Cornelius Aquilinus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11501265.translation: For Lucius Cornelius Ascla, a dutiful mother, to Valeria Isidora.
EDCS-11501265.summary: This inscription records a dedication from Lucius Cornelius Ascla to his dutiful mother, Valeria Isidora.

EDCS-11501267.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Cornelius Euarestus.
EDCS-11501267.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Cornelius Euarestus.

EDCS-11501269.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Cornelius Heraes, who lived 10 years and 8 months. Lucius Cornelius Ianuarius, his father, [erected this] for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-11501269.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a father for his son, Lucius Cornelius Heraes, who died at a young age.

EDCS-11501270.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Publius Cornelius Philetus.
EDCS-11501270.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Publius Cornelius Philetus.

EDCS-11501271.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Servius Cornelius Repentinus, who lived 40 years. Sabinia Euthycia, his incomparable wife, made [this] for him, a well-deserving husband. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-11501271.summary: A funerary monument erected by a wife for her well-deserving husband, Servius Cornelius Repentinus, including a common epitaph wishing rest.

EDCS-11501272.translation: [Erected by] Lucius Cornelius Rusticus, son of Lucius, of the Sabina tribe, [as] father for his son, which is the most miserable [thing].
EDCS-11501272.summary: This inscription records a father's grief for his deceased son.

EDCS-11501274.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cornelia Epig[--], made by her tutor Iulius Tullinus, who deserved well.
EDCS-11501274.summary: This funerary dedication was made by a tutor for his ward, Cornelia Epig[--].

EDCS-11501275.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Cornelia Euhodia, my dearest wife, Lucius Calpurnius Nicander made this.
EDCS-11501275.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Euhodia, set up by her husband Lucius Calpurnius Nicander, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501276.summary: A fragment of a funerary epitaph mentioning Cornelia Felicula, likely set up by her husband.

EDCS-11501279.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Cornelia Magna. Her sister Cornelia Caerusa made this.
EDCS-11501279.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Magna, erected by her sister Cornelia Caerusa.

EDCS-11501280.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Cornelia Marilla, who lived about 40 years. Cornelius Iustinus made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11501280.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Marilla, erected by her husband Cornelius Iustinus, indicating she lived to approximately 40 years of age.

EDCS-11501281.translation: To Cornelia Secunda, who lived 33 years. Adrastus made this for his well-deserving wife and for himself.
EDCS-11501281.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Secunda, erected by her husband Adrastus, who also dedicated a space for himself.

EDCS-11501285.translation: Place of two jars. Of Aulus Cossinius Moschus.
EDCS-11501285.summary: A label or ownership inscription for a place containing two jars, belonging to Aulus Cossinius Moschus.

EDCS-11501286.translation: To Aulus Cossinius Primigenius. Lamyra, his mother, from her own property. He lived 25 years and 3 months.
EDCS-11501286.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aulus Cossinius Primigenius, erected by his mother Lamyra, stating he lived to 25 years and 3 months.

EDCS-11501287.translation: ...cio Crescen[ti(?)], his freedmen... Urbanae... Charidi... eniae, freedwoman...
EDCS-11501287.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to record a dedication by freedmen to their former master, Crescens, and possibly other individuals.

EDCS-11501288.translation: Crescens, deputy of Nardus. Lived 4 years.
EDCS-11501288.summary: A funerary epitaph for Crescens, identified as a deputy or assistant of Nardus, who died at the age of 4.

EDCS-11501289.summary: A fragment of an inscription that records the lifespan of Crescentis, who lived 35 years.

EDCS-11501291.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Crispino Ery[something] made this for his deserving [relative/friend].
EDCS-11501291.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication to the spirits of the departed for Marcus Crispino Ery[something].

EDCS-11501292.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aviania Deutera made this for Quintus Critonius Ianuarius, her husband, who lived 45 years, and with whom she lived 37 years, her deserving husband who was full of devotion, whom she gladly made.
EDCS-11501292.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Aviania Deutera to her husband Quintus Critonius Ianuarius, providing their ages at death and marriage.

EDCS-11501293.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying Marcus Culcius Especatus.

EDCS-11501294.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Culcius Ephoebicus made this for his deserving wife, Culcia Euphrosyne.
EDCS-11501294.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Culcius Ephoebicus dedicating a monument to his wife, Culcia Euphrosyne.

EDCS-11501295.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Cupiennius Primitibus gave this place for his deserving brother and relative, Gaius Cupiennius Chrestus, his son, Minucia Cyprigenia, his mother, and Gaius Cupiennius Chrestus, his father.
EDCS-11501295.summary: This funerary inscription shows Gaius Cupiennius Primitibus dedicating a burial plot for his deserving family members.

EDCS-11501297.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Valerius Idaeus made this for Cynegis, his beloved ward, who lived 2 years and 20 days.
EDCS-11501297.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Valerius Idaeus dedicating a monument to his ward, Cynegis, and states her age at death.

EDCS-11501298.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Mar[something] made this for his deserving wife, Aelia Cyril[something], with whom he lived 11 years, 5 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-11501298.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Marcus Mar[something] to his wife, Aelia Cyril[something], and specifies their duration of marriage.

EDCS-11501299.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Danae, daughter of Tyrannidius, her deserving [relative] who lived 15 years, 6 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-11501299.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication to Danae, daughter of Tyrannidius, and states her age at death.

EDCS-11501300.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Deccia Victoria, his freedwoman, made this for her deserving [relative], Deccia Fortunata.
EDCS-11501300.summary: This funerary inscription records Deccia Victoria dedicating a monument to her deserving Deccia Fortunata.

EDCS-11501301.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Felicitas made this for her most beloved ward, Decimina, who lived 16 years and 2 days, her deserving [relative].
EDCS-11501301.summary: This funerary inscription records Aelia Felicitas dedicating a monument to her ward, Decimina, and states her age at death.

EDCS-11501302.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Claudia Paeza's slave Demetrius placed this himself.
EDCS-11501302.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Claudia Paeza for her slave Demetrius.

EDCS-11501303.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Bevila Saturnina made this for her well-deserving husband, Aulus Diavius Successus.
EDCS-11501303.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Bevila Saturnina for her husband Aulus Diavius Successus.

EDCS-11501305.translation: Publius Domitius Adestus and Festa, his parents, gave this from their own resources for Cerialis, their son, aged 20.
EDCS-11501305.summary: This is a funerary dedication by parents for their son Cerialis, who died at age 20.

EDCS-11501307.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Licinia Tatis for her most excellent husband, Marcus Domitius Fidelis.
EDCS-11501307.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Licinia Tatis for her husband Marcus Domitius Fidelis.

EDCS-11501308.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Domitius Heraclianus made this for his well-deserving son, Domitius Heraclianus, who lived for 1 year and 2 months.
EDCS-11501308.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a father for his son, Domitius Heraclianus, who died in infancy.

EDCS-11501309.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Domitius Successus.
EDCS-11501309.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication for Domitius Successus.

EDCS-11501310.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Parents made this for their well-deserving Domitius Victor, who lived 25 years and 7 months.
EDCS-11501310.summary: This is a funerary dedication by parents for their son Domitius Victor, who died at age 25.

EDCS-11501311.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Sittius Theomnestus made this for his well-deserving grandmother, Domitia Parthenope.
EDCS-11501311.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Sittius Theomnestus for his grandmother Domitia Parthenope.

EDCS-11501312.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Domitius Annianus made this for his incomparable wife, Domitia Zenodora, who lived 40 years, 7 months, and 6 days.
EDCS-11501312.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Domitius Annianus for his wife Domitia Zenodora, including her age at death.

EDCS-11501313.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Liburnas made this for his most dutiful daughter, Donata, the sweetest, who lived 11 years, 10 months, and 17 days.
EDCS-11501313.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a father, Liburnas, for his daughter Donata, including her age at death.

EDCS-11501314.translation: To the departed spirits. Donata is buried here. She lived for 17 years. Salvius made this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-11501314.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife, dated by the age of the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501315.summary: A simple funerary epitaph recording the name and age of the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501316.translation: To the departed spirits. Doto lived for 27 years, a little one. With her son surviving, Aridelus made this for his wife, a woman of rare example, well-deserving.
EDCS-11501316.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife, praising her virtues and noting her young age and surviving son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501317.translation: To the departed spirits. Ducenia Homonia lived for 30 years. Primus made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11501317.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife, noting her age and good qualities, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501318.translation: To the departed spirits. For Ducenia Prima, the excellent woman. Aulus Ducenius Carpus made this for himself, his brother Aulus Ducenius Trophimus, Ducenia Arescusa, Ducenia Laidi, and Ducenius Chrysippus.
EDCS-11501318.summary: A funerary dedication by Aulus Ducenius Carpus for his family members, including himself and his brother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501319.translation: For Gaius Egnatius Cinnamus, son of Gaius. Egnatius Amomus and Nardus for their excellent parent. And Egnatia [---] and [---] her companion(?).
EDCS-11501319.summary: A funerary dedication for Gaius Egnatius Cinnamus by his children, also mentioning other family members, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501320.translation: Manius Egnatius Lucullus, freedman of Manius, was manumitted on the day before the Nones of January, when Emperor Caesar Augustus was consul for the 12th time and Lucius Cornelius Sulla was consul. He died on the same day, dear to his friends, aged 31.
EDCS-11501320.summary: A funerary inscription recording the manumission and death of a freedman on the same day during the consulship of Augustus and Sulla, dated to 1 BC.

EDCS-11501322.translation: To the departed spirits. For Ennia Putelola, his foster-daughter, well-deserving. She lived 20 years, 11 months, 8 days. Ennius Epagathus, her patron, made this.
EDCS-11501322.summary: A funerary dedication by a patron for his foster-daughter, detailing her age at death and likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501324.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Epidius Philodespotos. Marcus Epidius Telesphorus made this for his well-deserving fellow freedman, and for himself. // To the departed spirits. Marcus Epidius [Atimetus] made this for his well-deserving fellow freedmen, Philodespotos and Telesphorus.
EDCS-11501324.summary: A funerary monument erected by Marcus Epidius Telesphorus for himself and his fellow freedmen, Philodespotos and Atimetus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501325.summary: A fragmentary inscription with incomplete names and titles, likely funerary, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501328.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Erucius Maximus and [his son?] Verus made this for Claudia Dicarichiae, his deserving wife, who lived 68 years and 3 months.
EDCS-11501328.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband and son for a deceased wife and mother.

EDCS-11501329.translation: Escionia Ompale lived 20 years, 3 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-11501329.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Escionia Ompale.

EDCS-11501330.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [?] Euhelpistus [lived for] 5 months and 12 days. His father Aristo [and?] his mother [set this up?].
EDCS-11501330.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely for a child, with parents named as dedicants.

EDCS-11501331.translation: Eyprepes lived 25 years. Ana made this.
EDCS-11501331.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Eyprepes, made by Ana.

EDCS-11501333.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [To?] Euty/[ch]us, a most [beloved?] youth. Prob[a?] [set this up?].
EDCS-11501333.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for a young man, possibly set up by Prob[a].

EDCS-11501335.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Eutyches lived 15 years. His father Ponticus [made this].
EDCS-11501335.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Eutyches, set up by his father Ponticus.

EDCS-11501338.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription with visible names and tribal affiliations, likely funerary.

EDCS-11501339.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aulus Fabius Comicus.
EDCS-11501339.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Fabius Comicus.

EDCS-11501341.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Fabius Modestus made this for himself and for all his family, by his own efforts and labors.
EDCS-11501341.summary: This is a funerary epitaph of self-dedication by Lucius Fabius Modestus for himself and his family.

EDCS-11501345.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Fabia Rhodines. Fabia Dasumilla [made this for her] most excellent freedwoman.
EDCS-11501345.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Fabia Dasumilla for her freedwoman Fabia Rhodines.

EDCS-11501346.translation: To the spirits of the dead. For Fabricia Attica, whom Gaius Salvidienus Speratus, her husband, who lived with her for 37 years, made for his well-deserving wife, and for himself.
EDCS-11501346.summary: A tombstone set up by a husband for his deceased wife and himself, mentioning their 37 years of marriage.

EDCS-11501347.translation: Quintus Fadius Symmachus, son of Quintus, for himself, and for Cameria Philinna, freedwoman of Titus, his wife, and for their family.
EDCS-11501347.summary: A funerary epitaph establishing rights for the dedicator, his wife, and their descendants.

EDCS-11501348.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Marcus Valerius Niger made this for his well-deserving wife, Fadiae Zmaragdini.
EDCS-11501348.summary: A tombstone erected by a husband for his deserving wife.

EDCS-11501349.translation: To the spirits of the dead. For Faenia Secunda and her husband, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-11501349.summary: A funerary inscription dedicating a tomb to a couple and their freedmen and their descendants.

EDCS-11501350.translation: To the spirits of the dead. For Faltonia Helpis, his freedwoman and wife, whom Marcus Faltonius Dolicus, his deserving wife who lived 24 years, made for himself.
EDCS-11501350.summary: A tombstone set up by Marcus Faltonius Dolicus for his freedwoman and wife, who died at age 24.

EDCS-11501351.translation: Fausta Longini for herself and for her son Peloris, who lived 5 years.
EDCS-11501351.summary: An epitaph for a mother dedicating a tomb to herself and her young son.

EDCS-11501352.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Faustion lived 13 years, 11 months, and 25 days.
EDCS-11501352.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of a child named Faustion.

EDCS-11501354.translation: Felix for his most beloved pupil, [Aelius?] Kenna, a good man.
EDCS-11501354.summary: A dedication to a beloved pupil, likely a tombstone fragment.

EDCS-11501356.translation: To the spirits of the dead. [---] Felicula, [---] with whom [---] lived 6 years.
EDCS-11501356.summary: A damaged tombstone fragment likely commemorating a woman who died after 6 years of marriage.

EDCS-11501357.translation: To the spirits of the dead. For [---] Festa, his incomparable wife, [---] Martialis, his well-deserving wife, made this.
EDCS-11501357.summary: A fragment of a tombstone dedicated by Martialis to his esteemed wife.

EDCS-11501358.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Festinius Montanus made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11501358.summary: A funerary dedication by Festinius Montanus to his wife.

EDCS-11501359.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aulus Firmius Crescentis, son of Aulus, lived for 1 year, 8 months, 11 days, 5 hours.
EDCS-11501359.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of a child named Aulus Firmius Crescentis.

EDCS-11501360.translation: To the spirits of the departed. They made this for Aulus Firmus Venutus, their deserving father, who lived 92 years.
EDCS-11501360.summary: A funerary dedication by children to their father, Aulus Firmus Venutus, who lived to 92.

EDCS-11501361.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Irena, mother, made this for her well-deserving son, Firminus, who lived 11 years and 3 months.
EDCS-11501361.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother, Irena, to her son, Firminus.

EDCS-11501362.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cornelius Ianuarius for his well-deserving wife, Fisias Daphne, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-11501362.summary: A funerary dedication by Cornelius Ianuarius to his wife, Fisias Daphne.

EDCS-11501363.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Anius Priscus, freedman, for his excellent patroness, Fisia Sozusa, well-deserving.
EDCS-11501363.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman, Lucius Anius Priscus, to his patroness, Fisia Sozusa.

EDCS-11501365.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavius Anthus Maximianus, most sweet in marriage, lived 16 years, 3 months, 17 days.
EDCS-11501365.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Flavius Anthus Maximianus.

EDCS-11501366.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Atticus (?) for his friend Ulpia Hyginiana (?), and Ulpia Syn[---], patroness, well-deserving.
EDCS-11501366.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication potentially mentioning Titus Flavius Atticus, Ulpia Hyginiana, and Ulpia Syn[---].

EDCS-11501367.translation: To Titus Flavius Coracinus, freedman of Augustus.
EDCS-11501367.summary: An inscription honoring Titus Flavius Coracinus, a freedman of Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-11501368.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Albanus, son of Titus Flavius Diogenes, for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-11501368.summary: A funerary dedication by a son, Albanus, to his father, Titus Flavius Diogenes.

EDCS-11501369.translation: For the memory of Flavius Felicianus, who lived 9 years, 4 months, 27 days.
EDCS-11501369.summary: This inscription is a memorial for a child named Flavius Felicianus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11501370.translation: lived 17 years, 8 months, 15 days. Flavius Felicissimus and Acutia, parents (?).
EDCS-11501370.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of an individual, with Flavius Felicissimus and Acutia identified as the likely parents.

EDCS-11501372.translation: To Titus Flavius Hieraticus, freedman of the Emperor, Titus Flavius Iaspis, his freedman, his brother, who deserved well, from his will. To the Divine Manes.
EDCS-11501372.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by Titus Flavius Iaspis to his brother and fellow freedman of the emperor, Hieraticus.

EDCS-11501373.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Flavius Yachinto, his brother, who deserved well, who lived 19 years, 11 months, 18 days. Flavia Felicitas, his most unhappy sister.
EDCS-11501373.summary: This tomb inscription is dedicated by Flavia Felicitas to her brother Flavius Yachinto, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11501374.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Titus Flavius Iulianus, who lived more or less 18 years. Claudia Paulina, his unloving mother, made this.
EDCS-11501374.summary: This tomb inscription is dedicated by Claudia Paulina to her son Titus Flavius Iulianus, recording his approximate age at death.

EDCS-11501375.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Flavius Macedo. Iulia Optata, to her most rare husband, who deserved well, made this.
EDCS-11501375.summary: This tomb inscription is dedicated by Iulia Optata to her husband Flavius Macedo, praising his character.

EDCS-11501376.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Titus Flavius Onesimus, who lived two years. (He) gave willingly.
EDCS-11501376.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated to Titus Flavius Onesimus, a very young child, possibly by a parent or other relative.

EDCS-11501377.translation: Titus Flavius Paris made this monument to be possessed by Marcia Aura, his deserving wife, and by Aeclanius Genialis and Tyche, his fellow soldiers.
EDCS-11501377.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument by Titus Flavius Paris for his wife Marcia Aura and his fellow soldiers Aeclanius Genialis and Tyche.

EDCS-11501378.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavia Feli/cula, patrona, made this for her deserving freedman, Flavius Paulinus, who lived 55 years.
EDCS-11501378.summary: This tomb inscription is dedicated by Flavia Feli/cula to her deserving freedman Flavius Paulinus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11501380.translation: To Titus Flavius Rufus, son of Titus Flavius, who lived 14 years, 5 months, 12 days. Titus Flavius Rufinus and Cornelia Veneria, his most unhappy parents, made this.
EDCS-11501380.summary: This tomb inscription is dedicated by Titus Flavius Rufinus and Cornelia Veneria to their son Titus Flavius Rufus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11501381.translation: To the shades of the departed. Ulpia Octavia made this for her well-deserving husband, Titus Flavius Smaragdus.
EDCS-11501381.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her deserving husband, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11501382.translation: To the shades of the departed. For Flavius [---]usta[---]ni[---].
EDCS-11501382.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for an individual named Flavius, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11501383.translation: To the shades of the departed. Euresius and his sons made this for their incomparable wife and mother, Flavia Antonia, a holy woman, who lived 47 years, 8 months, and 18 days. To her deserving.
EDCS-11501383.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband and sons for their deserving wife and mother, Flavia Antonia, providing her age at death, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11501384.translation: To the shades of the departed. Gaius Aufidius Eutychus for his well-deserving wife, Flavia Cerialis.
EDCS-11501384.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deserving wife, Flavia Cerialis, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11501385.translation: To the shades of the departed. Flavia Didyme made this for her deserving daughter, Flavia Didyme, who lived 22 years and 30 days.
EDCS-11501385.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her deserving daughter, Flavia Didyme, providing her age at death, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11501386.translation: To the shades of the departed. Flavius Saturninus made this for his well-deserving wife, Flavia Eutychia.
EDCS-11501386.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deserving wife, Flavia Eutychia, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11501387.translation: To the shades of the departed. Bucolus for his well-deserving wife, Flavia Eutychis.
EDCS-11501387.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deserving wife, Flavia Eutychis, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11501388.translation: To the shades of the departed. Titus [Flavius?] Eutycus made this for himself and his deserving wife, Flavia Felicita.
EDCS-11501388.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband, Titus [Flavius?] Eutycus, for himself and his deserving wife, Flavia Felicita, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11501390.translation: To the shades of the departed. Cocceius Gelos made this for his well-deserving wife, Flavia Isis.
EDCS-11501390.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deserving wife, Flavia Isis, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11501391.translation: To the shades of the departed. Valerius Valens made this for his deserving wife, Flavia Maximilla, who lived 19 years.
EDCS-11501391.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deserving wife, Flavia Maximilla, providing her age at death, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11501392.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Titus Flavius Antigonus, his freedman, to Flavia Paulina, his most excellent patroness and deserving wife.
EDCS-11501392.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman to his patroness and wife, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-11501393.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Flavia Restuta and Flavius Respectus, his dearest children. The girl lived 4 years, 10 months, 21 days; the boy lived 1 year, 6 months, 16 days. Flavius Respectus, their father, made the tomb for his well-deserving children.
EDCS-11501393.summary: A funerary monument set up by a father for his two young children, detailing their ages at death, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11501394.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For [name illegible], Flavia Victoria and Flavius Agathus for themselves and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, and for Quintus Titius, for his father Titus Fortunatus, who deserved well, from a donation, except for one couch, up to the road, on the right side within the monument.
EDCS-11501394.summary: A funerary dedication and land grant inscription, specifying burial rights for the dedicator, their family, freedmen, and descendants, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-11501396.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Follia Tertulla.
EDCS-11501396.summary: A simple funerary dedication to Follia Tertulla, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-11501397.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Fortunata, his most dutiful mother. Primus, son of Gorgias, made this.
EDCS-11501397.summary: A funerary dedication by Primus to his devoted mother, Fortunata, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-11501398.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Fortunata lived 40 years. Palmillia [name illegible] made this.
EDCS-11501398.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Fortunata and indicating it was made by Palmillia, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-11501399.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Fortunatus lived 10 years. Delphina, his mother, made this for her dearest son.
EDCS-11501399.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother, Delphina, for her son Fortunatus, who died at the age of ten, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-11501400.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aulus Fraucius Carpus sibi et Frauciae Cyrillae coniugi libertis libertabusque posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-11501400.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aulus Fraucius Carpus for himself and for his wife Fraucia Cyrilla, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-11501400.summary: A funerary dedication by Aulus Fraucius Carpus for himself, his wife, and their freedmen and descendants, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-11501401.translation: Aulus Fuficius Secundus to the Spirits of the Underworld, for Fuficia Successa, his own slave woman [rest illegible].
EDCS-11501401.summary: A funerary dedication by Aulus Fuficius Secundus to his slave woman Fuficia Successa, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-11501402.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Fuficia Felicissima, who lived 21 years, 10 months, 21 days. Plautia Oenone made this for her most dutiful and deserving daughter.
EDCS-11501402.summary: A funerary inscription by Plautia Oenone for her daughter Fuficia Felicissima, detailing her age at death, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-11501403.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Furrius Firmus, his dearest son, who lived 1 year, 2 months, and 27 days. Lucius Furrius Firmus and Pompeia Victoria, his parents, made this.
EDCS-11501403.summary: A funerary monument set up by parents for their deceased son, Lucius Furrius Firmus.

EDCS-11501404.translation: To the departed spirits. Furnia Prima to Aemilius Zanurius, her devoted freedman and pupil, who deserved well, who lived 19 years and 3 months.
EDCS-11501404.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Aemilius Zanurius, a freedman and pupil, set up by Furnia Prima.

EDCS-11501406.translation: To the departed spirits. Gabiniae Lascivae. Gabinia Venusta, her most excellent patroness.
EDCS-11501406.summary: A funerary dedication to Gabinia Lasciva by her patroness Gabinia Venusta.

EDCS-11501408.translation: To Gallianus... who lived 31 years... 5 days... felicitous... to the most beloved... deserving, made this.
EDCS-11501408.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription honoring a deceased individual named Gallianus, likely set up by a loved one.

EDCS-11501409.translation: Corinthia(?) who lived 10 years... Gamus made this monument for his preserved wife and for himself.
EDCS-11501409.summary: A funerary monument possibly set up by Gamus for his wife Corinthia(?) and himself.

EDCS-11501410.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Vettius Rufus made this for his wife Gavia Primitiva and his daughter Vettia Rufina, who deserved well.
EDCS-11501410.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Titus Vettius Rufus to his wife and daughter.

EDCS-11501411.translation: To the departed spirits. Gavidi Eutychi, who lived 60 years. Gavidia Mansueta, his patroness, for her deserving freedman.
EDCS-11501411.summary: A funerary inscription set up by a patroness, Gavidia Mansueta, for her freedman Gavidi Eutyches.

EDCS-11501412.translation: To the departed spirits. Gelliae Iadi, who lived 26 years. Gellius Crescens made this for his deserving freedwoman.
EDCS-11501412.summary: A funerary monument erected by Gellius Crescens for his freedwoman, Gellias Ias.

EDCS-11501413.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gemellina, who lived 23 years and 28 days.
EDCS-11501413.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Gemellina, who died at the age of 23 years and 28 days.

EDCS-11501415.translation: To the departed spirits. For Geminia Sirica, a simple woman, who lived approximately 20 years. Minicia Parthenope made this for her most beloved freedwoman, who deserved well.
EDCS-11501415.summary: A funerary monument set up by Minicia Parthenope for her freedwoman Geminia Sirica, who died around age 20.

EDCS-11501417.translation: To the departed spirits. Genialis, a well-deserving freedman, made this for his well-deserving [colleague?].
EDCS-11501417.summary: A funerary dedication for Genialis, made by Antonia Buttis, likely a freedwoman, possibly to a freedman.

EDCS-11501418.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Gerellanus Silvanus, who lived 35 years, 6 months, and 15 days. Gerellana Victoria made this for her most beloved son, well-deserving.
EDCS-11501418.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Gerellanus Silvanus, set up by his mother Gerellana Victoria, with his lifespan recorded.

EDCS-11501419.translation: To the departed spirits. For Glyptes, my most excellent and faithful wife, her husband made this. Here lies the sweet name of Glyptes, who was all things: once destined for good things as inspected by Venus, and approved by everyone's judgment, and a friend of modesty, and not without joy her witty conversation spoke; if you spoke about an important matter, she carried the palm; if about beauty, no one denied it. This one day, perfidious, unhappy, and dreadful, took away from us so many great gifts.
EDCS-11501419.summary: A funerary epitaph for Glyptes, praising her virtues and lamenting her death, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11501420.translation: For Quintus Granius Augustalis, who lived 4 years. Sacred.
EDCS-11501420.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Granius Augustalis, who died at a young age.

EDCS-11501422.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Grania Pistis.

EDCS-11501423.translation: The place of Graniaes Primi/geniaes. Whoever removes this monument will incur the wrath of the spirits who are buried here.
EDCS-11501423.summary: A grave marker for Graniaes Primi/geniaes, warning against disturbing the burial site.

EDCS-11501424.translation: To the departed spirits. For Grania Prisca, who lived 50 years, 3 months, and 10 days. Cerdo Agaclyti made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11501424.summary: A funerary dedication for Grania Prisca, made by her husband Cerdo Agaclyti, with her lifespan detailed.

EDCS-11501426.translation: To the departed spirits. Graphice lived 1 year, 10 months, 27 days. Anthus, her father, [made this].
EDCS-11501426.summary: A funerary dedication for Graphice, set up by her father Anthus, recording her short life.

EDCS-11501427.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gratilius [---]
EDCS-11501427.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication for Gratilius.

EDCS-11501428.text_edition: \n Gratiliae Fel Gratiliae Maximae[3] libertis libertabusque et Caio Gratilio Cai liberto Iso et Caio Cassio Cai liberto Gauro et Caio Gratilio Cai liberto Syneroti et Mario Syneroti\n
EDCS-11501428.translation: For Gratilia Fel[---] Gratilia Maxima[---], for their freedmen and freedwomen, and for Gaius Gratilius, son of Gaius, freedman Iso, and Gaius Cassius, son of Gaius, freedman Gauro, and Gaius Gratilius, son of Gaius, freedman Synerotus, and Marius, son of Synerotus.
EDCS-11501428.summary: A dedication listing beneficiaries including freedmen and freedwomen, likely related to a family trust or funerary college.

EDCS-11501429.translation: To Haia Rufina, her most unfortunate parents.
EDCS-11501429.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents for their daughter, Haia Rufina.

EDCS-11501430.translation: To Halea Agrippina, a slave girl, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-11501430.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a slave girl named Halea Agrippina.

EDCS-11501431.translation: To Hammonia Auge, for herself and her family.
EDCS-11501431.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hammonia Auge, intended for herself and her relatives.

EDCS-11501432.translation: To the gods of the underworld. It was not enough for parents to live in misery with one sorrow, because they had already lost their only daughter; but now, alas, they have buried another, and both are dead. But what sorrow is this, Harmonia Rufina, that you have left us? We parents, I the father Harmonius Ianvarius, with Bestra, her mother, who groans, lives and grieves, and we do not doubt that we are bereft of life, you who lived with us for 13 years, 6 months, and 26 days. We too wish to hasten to you. Cloedia Trophime does not doubt that she is bereft of life.
EDCS-11501432.summary: This is a complex funerary epitaph from Harmonia Rufina's parents, lamenting the loss of two daughters and expressing their own grief and desire for death.

EDCS-11501433.translation: To Heliodorus, a slave. Lollia Severa, his mistress, made this for him, a deserving slave.
EDCS-11501433.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mistress for her deserving slave, Heliodorus.

EDCS-11501434.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Helix, stating her age at death.

EDCS-11501435.translation: Gnaeus Helvius [---], [---] years old. Gnaeus Helvius Er[---]. Helvia Lychn[is ---].
EDCS-11501435.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming several members of the Helvius family.

EDCS-11501438.translation: To the gods of the underworld. For Caius Helvius Marcianus, who lived 4 years, 6 months, 27 days; and for Euphorianus, who lived 3 years, 1 month, 22 days. Caius Helvius Marcianus, father, for his dearest [son(s), made this].
EDCS-11501438.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father, Caius Helvius Marcianus, for his two young sons, Caius Helvius Marcianus and Euphorianus.

EDCS-11501440.translation: [---]nis L[---]NIV[---]. [---]M died wretchedly at seven months of age, Heracleon, with applied months, and then ten more days. You who read this, I warn you, live: death hastens.
EDCS-11501440.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Heracleon, who died at seven months of age, with a moralizing message to the living.

EDCS-11501442.translation: To the gods of the underworld. For Er[r]ennius Claudianus, who lived 36 years and 2 months and 10 days. Flavia Agape made this for her incomparable husband.
EDCS-11501442.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife, Flavia Agape, for her husband, Errennnius Claudianus.

EDCS-11501443.translation: To the departed spirits of Lucius Herennius Florus, son of Lucius, who lived 1 year, 11 months, 16 days. Lucius Herennius Florus and Claudia Prisca, his parents.
EDCS-11501443.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child, Lucius Herennius Florus, with his lifespan and parental dedication.

EDCS-11501444.translation: Marcus Herennius Mnemon, by the benefit of Genialis.
EDCS-11501444.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Herennius Mnemon, possibly indicating patronage or a benefaction.

EDCS-11501445.translation: To the departed spirits of Herennia Lucilla, who lived 36 years and 5 months. Pompeius Sotericianus, to his most virtuous wife.
EDCS-11501445.summary: A funerary epitaph for Herennia Lucilla, with her lifespan and dedication by her husband.

EDCS-11501446.translation: To the departed spirits of Herennia Martus, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-11501446.summary: A funerary epitaph for Herennia Martus, with her lifespan and dedication.

EDCS-11501447.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Herennia Secunda, incomplete.

EDCS-11501449.translation: To the departed spirits of Hermes, who lived 4 years, 9 months, 28 days. His loving mistress made this for him, a well-deserving person.
EDCS-11501449.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Hermes, detailing his lifespan and dedicated by his mistress.

EDCS-11501450.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Hermes, possibly indicating his age at death and a dedicant.

EDCS-11501452.translation: To the departed spirits of Hermes Lacaenae, fellow slave, who deserved well. Made this.
EDCS-11501452.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hermes, a fellow slave, dedicated by an unknown person.

EDCS-11501453.translation: To the departed spirits of Hermiones. Irenaeus and Carpias, parents, made this for their dearest son, who lived 7 years, 2 months, 22 days. They donated this place to Quintus Cissonius Vitalis and Cerdo, and also to Flavia Irene, daughter, who lived 13 years, 4 months, 13 days.
EDCS-11501453.summary: A double funerary epitaph for two children, Hermiones and Flavia Irene, by their parents and specifying land donation.

EDCS-11501454.translation: To the departed spirits of Herria Felicitas, an innocent and most innocent infant.
EDCS-11501454.summary: A funerary epitaph for an infant named Herria Felicitas, emphasizing her innocence.

EDCS-11501457.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Hilaritas lived for 8 years and 3 months. Aulus Plautius Calliparnthus made this for his foster-daughter.
EDCS-11501457.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a girl named Hilaritas, set up by her foster-father.

EDCS-11501458.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Hilarus, dearest son, who lived for 9 years, 4 months, and 4 days. Hilarus the father and Pimenia the mother made this for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-11501458.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Hilarus, set up by his parents, indicating his age at death.

EDCS-11501459.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Honorata Marcellina, who lived for 7 years and 35 days. Marcellinus made this for his most cherished daughter.
EDCS-11501459.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Honorata Marcellina and was erected by her father.

EDCS-11501460.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Horatia Eutychia. Marcus Horatius Trophimus and Horatia Tryphosa made this for their well-deserving patroness.
EDCS-11501460.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Horatia Eutychia, a patroness, and was erected by her freedmen.

EDCS-11501461.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Titus Hordeonius Ianuarius, patron.
EDCS-11501461.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Hordeonius Ianuarius, identified as a patron.

EDCS-11501462.translation: Lucius Hortensius Marinus lived for 18 years, 2 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-11501462.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Lucius Hortensius Marinus.

EDCS-11501463.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Publius Ortensius Severus, who lived for 18 years. Cassia Charis, mother, for her most devoted son.
EDCS-11501463.summary: This funerary epitaph for Publius Ortensius Severus was erected by his mother.

EDCS-11501464.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Caius Hosidius Leona. Arescusa, freedwoman, for her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-11501464.summary: This funerary inscription records a freedwoman, Arescusa, setting up a monument for her patron.

EDCS-11501465.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For their well-deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-11501465.summary: This fragmentary funerary epitaph appears to record a monument set up for a fellow slave.

EDCS-11501466.translation: Hospes. Marcus [---] lived for 5 years. Anthus.
EDCS-11501466.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the death of a child named Hospes, possibly related to Anthus.

EDCS-11501467.translation: To the shades of the departed. His brother and freedmen made this for Quintus Hostius Castianus, a man beyond compare, well deserving.
EDCS-11501467.summary: A funerary dedication by a brother and freedmen for Quintus Hostius Castianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501468.summary: A very brief epitaph for a woman identified as Nebrinis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501469.translation: To the shades of the departed. Ianuaria, Caesar's freedwoman, lived for 6 years and 8 months.
EDCS-11501469.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ianuaria, a freedwoman of Caesar, who died in childhood, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501470.translation: Sacred to the shades of the departed. Ianuarius made this for himself while alive, and for his wife Ianuaria, and for all his freedmen and freedwomen, they will be here in their own burial place forever until the last day and the final time.
EDCS-11501470.summary: A funerary dedication by Ianuarius for himself, his wife, and his freedmen, establishing a burial plot, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501471.translation: To the shades of the departed. Turrania Fortunata made this for her well-deserving husband Ianuarius.
EDCS-11501471.summary: A funerary dedication by Turrania Fortunata for her husband Ianuarius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501473.translation: Instania Aletheia, freedwoman of the woman, [made this] for herself while alive.
EDCS-11501473.summary: An epitaph for Instania Aletheia, a freedwoman, who set it up for herself during her lifetime, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501474.translation: To the shades of the departed. [---] Iucundus [---]cianus [---]aenae [---]imae [---]ini, to his sister.
EDCS-11501474.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication for Iucundus and his sister, with illegible names, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501475.summary: A short greeting inscribed on an unknown object, likely by a person named Julius I Philippiensis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501476.translation: To the shades of the departed. Tiberius Julius Achaicus for himself and for Julius Corinthus and for Julia Fortunata and for his own people.
EDCS-11501476.summary: A funerary dedication by Tiberius Julius Achaicus for himself and other family members, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501477.translation: To the shades of the departed. Caius Julius Eutyches, the father, made this for his most unfortunate young son, Caius Julius Agrippa, who lived for 17 years, 8 months, and 6 days.
EDCS-11501477.summary: A funerary dedication by a father, Caius Julius Eutyches, for his son Caius Julius Agrippa, who died at a young age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501479.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulius Antisphorus [dedicated this] to Maximianus.
EDCS-11501479.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Iulius Antisphorus to Maximianus.

EDCS-11501480.translation: Titus Iulius Ba[--] to Germanic[us --] freedman [dedicated this] to his mother Epapr(a)e Ahi[--].
EDCS-11501480.summary: This is a fragmented funerary dedication by a freedman of Titus Iulius, possibly to his mother.

EDCS-11501481.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Of Caius Iulius Bucolus, priest.
EDCS-11501481.summary: This inscription identifies Caius Iulius Bucolus as a priest and is a funerary dedication.

EDCS-11501482.translation: Caius Iulius Castor, freedman of Caius Iulius, lived for 24 years.
EDCS-11501482.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Castor, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11501483.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iulius Kasto, who lived 3 years, 11 months, and 25 days. Aur(elia) Marciane and Lic(inius) Fortun(a)t(u)s [dedicated this] to their well-deserving son.
EDCS-11501483.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aurelia Marciane and Licinius Fortunatus to their son Iulius Kasto, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11501484.translation: Caius Iulius Crocus, son of Caius Iulius, lived for 1 year and 4 months.
EDCS-11501484.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Crocus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11501485.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iulio Diosoro, who lived one year and 10 months. His parents [dedicated this] out of dutiful affection.
EDCS-11501485.summary: This is a funerary dedication by parents to their son Iulio Diosoro, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11501486.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iulio Euphrosyno, who lived 38 years, 2 months, and 3 days. Iulia Auge made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11501486.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Iulia Auge to her husband Iulio Euphrosyno, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11501487.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Caius Iulius Eutyches, who lived 60 years. Elpis, his freedwoman, made this for her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-11501487.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Elpis, freedwoman of Caius Iulius Eutyches, to her patron, recording his age at death.

EDCS-11501488.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Faenius [dedicated this] for himself, and for Claudia Tryfosa, and for his [family].
EDCS-11501488.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Caius Iulius Faenius for himself, his wife Claudia Tryfosa, and other family members.

EDCS-11501489.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Stephanis made this for her patron, Tiberius Iulius Felicisimus, with whom she lived for 25 years, and who lived for 70 years and 9 months, a well-deserving patron.
EDCS-11501489.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Iulia Stephanis for her patron Tiberius Iulius Felicisimus.

EDCS-11501490.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Iulius Felix lived 11 months and 3 days.
EDCS-11501490.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Caius Iulius Felix.

EDCS-11501491.translation: To the departed spirits. Tiberius Iulius Fortunatus made this for himself and for his wife Clodia Felicissima, and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-11501491.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Tiberius Iulius Fortunatus for himself, his wife, and their dependents.

EDCS-11501495.translation: Caius Iulius Hypoptus and Caius Iulius Narcissus, freedman of Nigri, made this from their own funds for themselves and their families.
EDCS-11501495.summary: This is a funerary inscription jointly set up by Caius Iulius Hypoptus and Caius Iulius Narcissus for their families.

EDCS-11501496.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Iulius Marinus made this for his wife Iulia Aelias.
EDCS-11501496.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Gaius Iulius Marinus for his wife Iulia Aelias.

EDCS-11501497.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcia Chrysopolis made this for her most devoted brother, Iulius Maximus.
EDCS-11501497.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcia Chrysopolis for her brother Iulius Maximus.

EDCS-11501498.translation: Caius Iulius Narcissus, freedman of Nigri.
EDCS-11501498.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying Caius Iulius Narcissus as a freedman of Nigri.

EDCS-11501499.translation: To the departed spirits. Apuleia Cratia and her son Iulius Fortunius made this for their well-deserving Caius Iulius Natalis, freedman of Polemius, who lived 35 years and 7 months.
EDCS-11501499.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Apuleia Cratia and her son for her husband and their father, Caius Iulius Natalis.

EDCS-11501500.translation: To the departed spirits. Vituiria Fortunula made this for her well-deserving foster-son, Iulius Onesimus, who lived 13 years and 16 days.
EDCS-11501500.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Vituiria Fortunula for her foster-son, Iulius Onesimus.

EDCS-11501502.translation: Caius Iulius Oriens, freedman of Nigri, lived 45 years, for his most worthy partner, Iade, who was not permitted to receive his last breath in her own hands.
EDCS-11501502.summary: This funerary inscription honors Caius Iulius Oriens and laments that his partner Iade could not be with him at his death.

EDCS-11501503.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tiberius Julius Parthenopaeus.
EDCS-11501503.summary: A funerary dedication to Tiberius Julius Parthenopaeus.

EDCS-11501504.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Julius Philostorgus.
EDCS-11501504.summary: A funerary dedication to Gaius Julius Philostorgus.

EDCS-11501505.translation: Gaius Julius Primigenius and Julia Irenetis made [this] for themselves for their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-11501505.summary: A funerary monument established by Gaius Julius Primigenius and Julia Irenetis for their freed slaves.

EDCS-11501506.summary: A dedication to Gaius Julius Priscus.

EDCS-11501508.translation: Licinia Felicitas made [this] for her son Secundinus, who lived 16 years, well deserving.
EDCS-11501508.summary: A funerary monument for Julius Secundinus, erected by his mother Licinia Felicitas, indicating he died at 16 years old.

EDCS-11501509.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Julius Secundus, most sweet patron, who lived ten months and three days and one day. Iulia Kapriola [made this].
EDCS-11501509.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulia Kapriola to her patron Gaius Julius Secundus, who died after living for 10 months and 3 days.

EDCS-11501510.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication to Gaius Julius Syntrophus.

EDCS-11501511.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Julius Theodorus for Julia Diodora, his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-11501511.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Julius Theodorus to his mother Julia Diodora.

EDCS-11501512.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Julius Onesimus [made this] for his well-deserving son Publius Julius Tryfo, who lived one year, 4 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-11501512.summary: A funerary monument for Publius Julius Tryfo, erected by his father Publius Julius Onesimus, indicating he died at the age of 1 year, 4 months, and 19 days.

EDCS-11501513.translation: To the spirits of the departed. A monument for the well-deserving Julia Maetina, who lived 70 years, 3 months, and 20 days. Julius Victor made [this].
EDCS-11501513.summary: A funerary monument for Julia Maetina, erected by Julius Victor, indicating she lived to 70 years, 3 months, and 20 days.

EDCS-11501514.translation: To the departed spirits. For Tiberius Julius Zoticus, a freedman and a deserving patron, they made [this].
EDCS-11501514.summary: A funerary dedication for Tiberius Julius Zoticus, who is described as a freedman and a deserving patron.

EDCS-11501515.translation: To the departed spirits. For Julia, an infant who lived for 6 years, 11 months. Julius made [this] for her deserving self.
EDCS-11501515.summary: A funerary dedication for an infant named Julia, made by Julius.

EDCS-11501518.translation: To the departed spirits. For Julia Agathe, her dearest mother. Moschio Hermogenes made [this] for her deserving self.
EDCS-11501518.summary: A funerary dedication for Julia Agathe, the mother of Moschio Hermogenes.

EDCS-11501519.translation: For Julia Antiochis, her dearest daughter, who lived 3 years, 8 months, and 18 days. Gaius Julius Alcides made [this] for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-11501519.summary: A funerary dedication for Julia Antiochis, the daughter of Gaius Julius Alcides.

EDCS-11501520.translation: To the departed spirits. For Julia Aphrodisia, [his] mother. Lucius Antonius Aristo the Younger made [this] for her deserving self.
EDCS-11501520.summary: A funerary dedication for Julia Aphrodisia, the mother of Lucius Antonius Aristo the Younger.

EDCS-11501521.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Julia Arescusa, [his] mother.
EDCS-11501521.summary: A funerary dedication to Julia Arescusa, identified as a mother.

EDCS-11501522.translation: To Julia Ma, also called Aresa, who lived approximately 38 years. Lucius Samius Agilis made [this] for his most beloved wife, who even in death is incomparable [---].
EDCS-11501522.summary: A funerary dedication for Julia Ma (also Aresa), the wife of Lucius Samius Agilis, who lived approximately 38 years.

EDCS-11501523.translation: For Julia Auge, freedwoman of Nigra, [as] her mother.
EDCS-11501523.summary: A dedication to Julia Auge, freedwoman of Nigra, as her mother.

EDCS-11501525.translation: To the departed spirits. For Julia Chreste. Junius Phoebion for his deserving wife [---].
EDCS-11501525.summary: A funerary dedication by Junius Phoebion for his wife, Julia Chreste.

EDCS-11501526.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Julia Coetonis. Iulio Epaphroditus made [this] for his deserving father.
EDCS-11501526.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulio Epaphroditus for his father, Iulia Coetonis.

EDCS-11501527.translation: To the departed spirits: Iulia Crispin... Iulia...
EDCS-11501527.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Crispin by Iulia, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501528.translation: To the departed spirits: To Iulia Crispin(a), his most obedient wife, Capitolinus to his most devoted mother.
EDCS-11501528.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Capitolinus to his wife Iulia Crispin(a) and his mother, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501529.translation: To the departed spirits: Hermias made this for his deserving wife Iulia Domitia, who lived 30 years and 2 months.
EDCS-11501529.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Hermias to his wife Iulia Domitia, who lived 30 years and 2 months, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501530.translation: To the departed spirits: To Iulia Epigone, a most rare woman. Tiberius Iulius Eupor to his deserving wife, with whom he lived 30 years.
EDCS-11501530.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Tiberius Iulius Eupor to his wife Iulia Epigone, who lived 30 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501531.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Eutychidi nnorum XVII Iulius Iulianus patronus et Ulpius Arpocration coniugi bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-11501531.translation: To the departed spirits: Iulius Iulianus, her patron, and Ulpius Arpocration made this for their deserving wife Iulia Eutychis, aged 17 years.
EDCS-11501531.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by the patron Iulius Iulianus and Ulpius Arpocration to their wife Iulia Eutychis, aged 17, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501532.translation: ... lived 90 years. Iulia Ianuaria Castricia Felicitas(?); the heirs made this for the deserving person.
EDCS-11501532.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of Iulia Ianuaria Castricia Felicitas(?) and the dedication by her heirs, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501533.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age of Iulia Flora, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501534.translation: Iulia Fortunata lived 44 years and 3 months. Herennius Beryllus made this for his most sweet, deserving wife.
EDCS-11501534.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, age, and dedication by Herennius Beryllus to his wife Iulia Fortunata, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501535.translation: To the departed spirits: To Iulia Gemella, his most holy and incomparable wife, who lived 32 years and 5 months, bore 18 children. Philadelphus, the husband, to his deserving wife. May your soul speak for eternity.
EDCS-11501535.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Philadelphus to his wife Iulia Gemella, detailing her age, number of children, and including a Greek epitaph, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501536.translation: To the departed spirits: To Iulia Iuliana, who lived 20 years, 11 months, 7 days. She possesses unjustly this place, where a body quickly received its iniquitous end; who deserved such a life that the elderly would not want her; the gods quickly returned what was hers to the gods. She was inimical to no one. Caius Iulius Victorinus, and Aelia Theodote, and Caius Vicirrius Soter, the husband, made this for the deserving person.
EDCS-11501536.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Iuliana at a young age and laments her fate, with a dedication from her husband and others, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501537.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iulia Longina, who lived 20 years. Iulia Masae and Iulius Marinus, parents, made this. And Iulius Valens made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11501537.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Longina, erected by her parents and husband, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501538.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Lucilla lived 10 years, 8 months, and 20 days. Caius Calventius Valens made this for his incomparable, well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-11501538.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Lucilla, erected by her father, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501539.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iulia Marulla, sweetest daughter to her parents, who lived 14 years, 8 months, and 4 days, a virgin who deceived (death). Farewell.
EDCS-11501539.summary: A tombstone for the young virgin Iulia Marulla, erected by her parents, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501541.text_edition: \n Iulia Cai liberta Nice Iuliae Pelagiae\n
EDCS-11501541.translation: Iulia Nice, freedwoman of Iulia, to Iulia Pelagia.
EDCS-11501541.summary: An inscription likely recording a dedication or inheritance from Iulia Nice to Iulia Pelagia, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501542.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iuliae Paci. Publius Aelius Herme[--] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11501542.summary: A tombstone fragment for Iuliae Paci, with part of the dedicant's name missing, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501543.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iulia Primigenia, who lived 1 year and 6 months and 9 days. Hermes and Tyche for their dearest daughter.
EDCS-11501543.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Primigenia, erected by her parents Hermes and Tyche, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501544.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iulia Procula, who lived 25 years, 5 months, and 10 days. Granius Marcianus made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-11501544.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Procula, erected by her husband Granius Marcianus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501545.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iuliae Pullaena, who lived 46 years, 11 months, and 15 days. Iulia Frontina, daughter, made this for her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-11501545.summary: A tombstone for Iuliae Pullaena, erected by her daughter Iulia Frontina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.
