EDCS-11501546.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iulia Secunda, who lived 2 years and 15 days. Lollia Ionice, mother, for her most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-11501546.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Secunda, erected by her mother Lollia Ionice, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501547.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iulia Secunda, daughter of Caius. Secundus, father, and Philartn, mother, made this.
EDCS-11501547.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Secunda, erected by her parents Secundus and Philartn, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501548.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Julia Sebera, for her well-deserving husband, Iulius Irezectes.
EDCS-11501548.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband to his well-deserving wife, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11501549.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Sidone, for her well-deserving husband Iulius Quirinus, made this.
EDCS-11501549.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife to her well-deserving husband, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11501550.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Julia Stephani, mother, well-deserving, Tiberius Iulius Felicissimus, her son, made this.
EDCS-11501550.summary: A funerary dedication by a son to his well-deserving mother, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11501551.translation: For Julia Tyche, wife, and Iulius Afrodisius, son, well-deserving, Gaius Iulius Dionysius purchased the plot from Publius Apuleius Hermen(es?) and enclosed it with a wall, the inner half measuring 30 feet long and 6.5 feet wide, for himself, his heirs, freedmen and freedwomen. Passage and access will be provided. This monument will remain with my family.
EDCS-11501551.summary: A funerary and property deed recording the purchase of a burial plot by Gaius Iulius Dionysius for his wife and son, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11501552.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Julia Victorina, his incomparable sister, Iulius Antoninus, for her well-deserving, made this.
EDCS-11501552.summary: A funerary dedication by a brother to his well-deserving sister, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11501555.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For M. Iunius Eglectus. Marcus Iunius Pudens made this for his well-deserving patron.
EDCS-11501555.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman to his well-deserving patron, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11501556.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iunius Fortunatus, Prima, for her well-deserving wife, made this.
EDCS-11501556.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife to her well-deserving husband, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11501557.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Iunius Rufus, who lived 3 years.
EDCS-11501557.summary: A funerary dedication for a young child who died at age three, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11501558.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For M. Iunius Tauriscus, patron, well-deserving. Marcus Iunius Pudens.
EDCS-11501558.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman to his well-deserving patron, Marcus Iunius Tauriscus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11501560.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iunia Artemidora. Myrismus, freedman of the emperor, arranged for her, his well-deserving ward.
EDCS-11501560.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman of the emperor for his ward, Iunia Artemidora, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-11501561.translation: To the divine shades. Iunia Bal[---] lived for [---] years, 9 months, 5 days. Her husband made this for his [---] wife.
EDCS-11501561.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iunia Bal[---], likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, recording her lifespan and erected by her husband.

EDCS-11501564.translation: To the divine shades. Iunia Salla, daughter of Decimus, lived 18 years. Decimus Iunius Donax for himself and Poblicia Lexa and his [family].
EDCS-11501564.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iunia Salla and her family, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, with Decimus Iunius Donax as the dedicator.

EDCS-11501565.translation: To the divine shades. Iuniae Severae lived 30 years, 7 months, 20 days. Iunia Venusta [made this] for her most dutiful sister.
EDCS-11501565.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iuniae Severae, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, erected by her sister Iunia Venusta.

EDCS-11501566.translation: To the divine shades. Publius Rufinius Serenus made this for his wife, Iuniae Theodotae.
EDCS-11501566.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iuniae Theodotae, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, erected by her husband Publius Rufinius Serenus.

EDCS-11501569.translation: Iustus Caesaris made [this] for himself and for his dearest wife Lucilia Prisca, who lived 20 years, 7 months, 15 days, and for his descendants; in front 5 feet, in the ground 10 feet; a common monument with Cosmo.
EDCS-11501569.summary: A funerary epitaph and plot marker for Iustus Caesaris, his wife Lucilia Prisca, and descendants, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11501570.summary: A fragmentary epitaph, likely funerary, with the names Polla and Iustus visible, provenance and date uncertain.

EDCS-11501571.translation: To the divine shades. For Lucius Laberius Marcianus, who lived 26 years, 3 months, 11 days. Sextus Patulcius Hermes made this for his incomparable, well-deserving brother.
EDCS-11501571.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Laberius Marcianus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, erected by his brother Sextus Patulcius Hermes.

EDCS-11501572.translation: To the divine shades. For Lacaena, who lived 21 years.
EDCS-11501572.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Lacaena, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, recording her age at death.

EDCS-11501573.translation: To the divine shades. For Laecania Artemisia and Laecanius Eutychetes. Laecanius Venerianus made [this] for his most sweet, well-deserving parents.
EDCS-11501573.summary: A funerary epitaph for Laecania Artemisia and Laecanius Eutychetes, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, erected by their son Laecanius Venerianus.

EDCS-11501574.translation: To the divine shades. For Laecania Onesima. Gaius Laecanius Zethus [made this] for his well-deserving freedwoman.
EDCS-11501574.summary: A funerary epitaph for Laecania Onesima, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, erected by her former master Gaius Laecanius Zethus.

EDCS-11501575.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Marcus Laelius Garmalla.
EDCS-11501575.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Laelius Garmalla, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-11501576.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Marcus Laelius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Falerna tribe.
EDCS-11501576.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Laelius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Falerna tribe, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-11501577.translation: Burial place of Marcus Laelius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Falerna tribe.
EDCS-11501577.summary: This inscription marks the burial place of Marcus Laelius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Falerna tribe, from the Roman period.

EDCS-11501579.translation: Laelia Priscilla, daughter of Marcus, lived for 23 years.
EDCS-11501579.summary: This epitaph records the life and age of Laelia Priscilla, daughter of Marcus, from the Roman period.

EDCS-11501580.translation: To Lucius Laetorius [---] Iantho, for a deserving father. [---
EDCS-11501580.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Laetorius Iantho, likely a funerary monument from the Roman period, though partially lost.

EDCS-11501581.translation: To the departed spirits. For Laetoria Maxima, the foster-child, who lived 18 years, 3 months, 7 days. Laetorius Adf[---]s and Egna tius Malchio, father (?), made this for the deserving one.
EDCS-11501581.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Laetoria Maxima, made by Laetorius Adf[---]s and Egna tius Malchio, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-11501582.translation: Here lie only ashes and bones. [---] the life and holy soul. [---] what is perceived by the departed spirits. [---] to her was taken away, and to the soul also may the earth be light. [---] Lais Genicaes, freedwoman, lived 45 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-11501582.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lais Genicaes, a freedwoman, that includes a poetic lament and states her age and burial location, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-11501583.translation: Gaius Lappius December lived for 30 years. Hermione made this for her deserving husband. December, son, lived for 17 years.
EDCS-11501583.summary: This inscription records the life of Gaius Lappius December and his son, and is a dedication from his wife Hermione, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-11501585.translation: Larcia Stacte, freedwoman, lived for 26 years. Carisia Chreste, freedwoman, mother.
EDCS-11501585.summary: This epitaph records the life of Larcia Stacte and identifies Carisia Chreste as her mother, from the Roman period.

EDCS-11501586.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Lepidius Iucundus, who lived 3 years, 2 months. Gaius Lepidius Felix made this for his most dutiful son, and for himself, his freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants, except for Phlegusa the freedwoman, for whom no entry into this monument shall be given.
EDCS-11501586.summary: This is a family funerary monument for Gaius Lepidius Iucundus and his descendants, including a specific exclusion of a freedwoman named Phlegusa, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-11501587.translation: Lucius Licinius Atime[t]us for himself and his wife Grania Menusa, and his children Granius Zoilus and Licinia Hora[e]a, and their freedmen, freedwomen, and descendants.
EDCS-11501587.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Licinius Atime[t]us for himself and his family, including relatives and freedpersons.

EDCS-11501588.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Licinius Hermeros lived for 31 years. His mother made this for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-11501588.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother for her son, Gaius Licinius Hermeros, who died at the age of 31.

EDCS-11501589.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Licinia Ammia, freedwoman, well-deserving. Gaius Licinius Modestus made this. She lived for 20 years.
EDCS-11501589.summary: A funerary dedication made by Gaius Licinius Modestus for his freedwoman, Licinia Ammia, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-11501590.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Licinia Nice, his mother, made this for her incomparable son, Licinius Puteolanus, who lived for 3 years, 5 months, and 17 days.
EDCS-11501590.summary: A funerary dedication by Licinia Nice for her son, Licinius Puteolanus, who died at the age of 3 years, 5 months, and 17 days.

EDCS-11501591.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Licinia Fortunata.
EDCS-11501591.summary: A funerary dedication to Licinia Fortunata.

EDCS-11501592.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Licinia Lucilla, his excellent wife.
EDCS-11501592.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife, Licinia Lucilla.

EDCS-11501593.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Herennius Salvius for his wife, Licinia Venusta.
EDCS-11501593.summary: A funerary dedication by Herennius Salvius for his wife, Licinia Venusta.

EDCS-11501594.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Limbricius Crescens, father; for Limbricia Fortunata, wife; for Marcus Limbricius Crescens, son.
EDCS-11501594.summary: A funerary dedication for multiple family members: Marcus Limbricius Crescens (father), Limbricia Fortunata (wife), and Marcus Limbricius Crescens (son).

EDCS-11501595.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vitruvius Aprilis made this for his well-deserving wife, Limbricia Theodora, who lived for 28 years, 11 months, an incomparable woman.
EDCS-11501595.summary: A funerary dedication by Vitruvius Aprilis for his wife, Limbricia Theodora, who died at the age of 28 years and 11 months.

EDCS-11501596.translation: The remains of Marcus Livius Rectus, son of Marcus Livius. He lived for 10 years.
EDCS-11501596.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Marcus Livius Rectus, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-11501597.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Thallusa Lolli(a), wife of Quintus Lollius Agilius, made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-11501597.summary: A funerary dedication from an unnamed wife to her deceased husband, Quintus Lollius Agilius.

EDCS-11501598.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Lollius Epaphroditianus.
EDCS-11501598.summary: A funerary dedication to Lucius Lollius Epaphroditianus, likely a freedman or slave given the common naming convention.

EDCS-11501602.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Lollius Severus and Otacilia Apollonia made this for their dearest and deserving son, Lucius Lollius Severus Eusebius, who lived 5 years, 4 months, and 4 days.
EDCS-11501602.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by parents for their young son, Lucius Lollius Severus Eusebius, who died in childhood.

EDCS-11501603.summary: A fragmented inscription that likely records a dedication or funerary epitaph involving individuals named Longinus, Pieris, and Nemerita.

EDCS-11501604.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valerius Longus for his deserving son, Gaius Longinius Seleucus, who lived 24 years and 7 days.
EDCS-11501604.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a father for his deceased son, Gaius Longinius Seleucus.

EDCS-11501605.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gnaeus Lucceius Maron and Prima Bruittia, while alive, made this for themselves, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-11501605.summary: A funerary monument made by a couple, Gnaeus Lucceius Maron and Bruittia Prima, for themselves and their descendants, including freedpersons.

EDCS-11501606.translation: Lucceia Herophila, freedwoman of Balbus, nurse. Publius Carpinarius, freedman of Publius.
EDCS-11501606.summary: An epitaph honoring Lucceia Herophila, a nurse and freedwoman, with Publius Carpinarius, a freedman, also mentioned.

EDCS-11501607.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Ver(n)a made this for his deserving daughter, Luccia Ver(n)a, who lived 3 years and 43 days.
EDCS-11501607.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a father for his young daughter, Luccia Ver(n)a.

EDCS-11501608.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Secundila made this for her incomparable and deserving husband, Lucius Victor(i)us, who lived 45 years, 2 months, 11 days, and 5 hours.
EDCS-11501608.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her deceased husband, Lucius Victor(i)us.

EDCS-11501609.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Petron(ius) Fortunatus made this for his sweetest wife, Lucia Celerina, who was also called Victoria, aged 23 years.
EDCS-11501609.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deceased wife, Lucia Celerina (Victoria).

EDCS-11501610.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucia Papiria lived 52 years. Iulius Ingenus and Papirius Ingenus and Papiria Vitalis made this for their incomparable wife and most beloved mother.
EDCS-11501610.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of the deceased Lucia Papiria and was erected by her husband and son.

EDCS-11501611.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucilia Honorata, dearest daughter, who lived 4 years and 6 months.
EDCS-11501611.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of the deceased Lucilia Honorata.

EDCS-11501612.translation: Lucilia Primigenia, of Marcus Lucilius Erotis, lived 16 years.
EDCS-11501612.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of the deceased Lucilia Primigenia.

EDCS-11501613.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vitalis Baendigonis made this for his wife Lucilla, who lived 45 years, 5 months, and 9 days, as a well-deserving recipient.
EDCS-11501613.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of the deceased Lucilla and was erected by her husband Vitalis Baendigonis.

EDCS-11501614.translation: Marcus Lucretius Serenus for himself and his family and their descendants and memory, and for Nices, his freedwoman.
EDCS-11501614.summary: This dedication records Marcus Lucretius Serenus setting up a monument for himself, his family, their descendants, and his freedwoman Nices.

EDCS-11501615.translation: Tychius made this for his most chaste wife Lucretia Terentia and his most beloved son Marcus Valerius Eytychianus, who lived 3 years, 6 months, and 17 days, as a well-deserving recipient.
EDCS-11501615.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of the deceased Marcus Valerius Eytychianus and was erected by his father Tychius for him and his wife Lucretia Terentia.

EDCS-11501616.translation: --- February the 13th. ---crinus. --- Epaphra. ---ictus, freedman of Aulus. ---rius.
EDCS-11501616.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a funerary epitaph mentioning a date and several names, possibly from the late Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-11501619.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Lutatius Rufus made this for his son Lucius Lutatius Celer and for his deserving wife Valeria Isias.
EDCS-11501619.summary: This funerary epitaph records Lucius Lutatius Rufus dedicating a monument to his son Lucius Lutatius Celer and his wife Valeria Isias.

EDCS-11501620.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lysia Zosimes lived 29 years, 7 months, and 12 days. Lysius Crescens and Cutia Ingenua made this for their most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-11501620.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of the deceased Lysia Zosimes and was erected by her parents Lysius Crescens and Cutia Ingenua.

EDCS-11501621.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Victor, who lived 15 years and 11 months. M. Victor, father, made this for his deserving son.
EDCS-11501621.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of the deceased Victor and was erected by his father, M. Victor.

EDCS-11501626.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Maicius Felix.
EDCS-11501626.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Maicius Felix.

EDCS-11501628.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Satura made this for her well-deserving daughter, Maecia Sperata, who lived for 2 years, 10 months, and 4 days.
EDCS-11501628.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aurelia Satura for her daughter Maecia Sperata, including her age at death.

EDCS-11501629.translation: Of Lucius Magius Martialis, son of Lucius. The dimensions of the monument: 20 feet in the field, 10 feet in the front.
EDCS-11501629.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a monument belonging to Lucius Magius Martialis.

EDCS-11501630.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Magnus for his daughter Fortunata, who lived for two years and nine days.
EDCS-11501630.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Magnus for his daughter Fortunata, including her age at death.

EDCS-11501631.translation: Magnia Ironia for herself, and for her freedman Numerius Magnus [---], and for her freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-11501631.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Magnia Ironia for herself, her freedman Numerius Magnus, and other freedpersons.

EDCS-11501632.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a funerary dedication, recording a name and a length of life.

EDCS-11501633.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aulus Mercurius for his deserving wife, Manlia Nemenes.
EDCS-11501633.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aulus Mercurius for his wife Manlia Nemenes.

EDCS-11501634.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Marcus Gemellinus made this for his adopted son Gaius Marcus Gemellinus, who lived for 22 years and 4 months, his deserving one.
EDCS-11501634.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Marcus Gemellinus for his deserving adopted son, also named Gaius Marcus Gemellinus, including his age at death.

EDCS-11501635.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcius Faustus for his son Agathopodes, son of Marcus, who lived for 12 years, 5 months, and 20 days, his deserving one.
EDCS-11501635.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcius Faustus for his son Agathopodes, including his age at death.

EDCS-11501636.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Icanius, a household slave, made this for his deserving Marcius Faustus [---].
EDCS-11501636.summary: This is a funerary dedication by the household slave Icanius for his deserving freedman Marcius Faustus.

EDCS-11501637.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Titus Marcius Maximianus set this up for his very excellent parents, Titus Marcius Felix and Marcia Psecas.
EDCS-11501637.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Titus Marcius Maximianus for his parents, Titus Marcius Felix and Marcia Psecas.

EDCS-11501639.translation: Quintus Marcius Protus, freedman of (a woman).
EDCS-11501639.summary: This inscription records Quintus Marcius Protus, a freedman, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-11501640.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Drusius Valens and Quadratilla set this up for their most sweet daughter, Marcia Drusilla.
EDCS-11501640.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by parents Drusius Valens and Quadratilla to their daughter, Marcia Drusilla.

EDCS-11501641.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Titus Flavius Onesimus set this up for his deserving wife, Marcia Festa, daughter of Gaius, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-11501641.summary: This funerary inscription is for Marcia Festa, daughter of Gaius, who lived 25 years, set up by her husband Titus Flavius Onesimus.

EDCS-11501642.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Ancharius Proculus and Proclianus set this up for their most holy mother, Marcia Marciana.
EDCS-11501642.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Ancharius Proculus and Proclianus for their mother, Marcia Marciana.

EDCS-11501643.translation: Marcia Procula, daughter of Gaius, lived 21 years.
EDCS-11501643.summary: This inscription records the life span of Marcia Procula, daughter of Gaius, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-11501644.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Lucius Laecanius Hermes set this up for his deserving wife, Marcia Puteolana.
EDCS-11501644.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Lucius Laecanius Hermes for his wife, Marcia Puteolana.

EDCS-11501647.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Margaris, freedwoman of Crispus, [freedwoman] of Crispus Diomedes.
EDCS-11501647.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Margaris, a freedwoman of Crispus and Crispus Diomedes.

EDCS-11501648.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Mario Crato.
EDCS-11501648.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mario Crato.

EDCS-11501649.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Maria Euthymia set this up for her dearest father, Lucius Marius Eutychetus, who lived 70 years, 5 months, and 8 days, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-11501649.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Maria Euthymia for her father, Lucius Marius Eutychetus, who lived 70 years, 5 months, and 8 days.

EDCS-11501651.translation: To the departed spirits. To Marcus Marius Proculus, who lived 3 years, 4 months, 9 days. Marcus Marius Fronto and Cosconia Hygia, his most unfortunate parents, made this for their most devoted son, and for themselves, their freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants. If he had not been snatched away by fate, his mother would have preceded him in this tomb; and you, as you pass by, say, "Proculus, may the earth lie lightly on you."
EDCS-11501651.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Marius Proculus, set up by his parents, also including a dedication for themselves and their freedmen and descendants, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501652.translation: To the departed spirits. To Lucius Marius Syntrophus. Lucius Marius Eutychianus made this for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-11501652.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Marius Syntrophus, dedicated by his son, Lucius Marius Eutychianus, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501653.translation: Maria [---] Gatinia lived [---]X years, 5 months [---]. Quintus Castricius [---]accus, her husband, lived [---]LXX years, [---] months, [---] days.
EDCS-11501653.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the ages of a woman named Maria Gatinia and her husband Quintus Castricius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501654.translation: Maria, daughter of Julius, lived 70 years.
EDCS-11501654.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maria, daughter of Julius, recording her age, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501655.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maria Ligustina, recording her age, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501658.translation: To the departed spirits. To Maria Romana. Lucius Marius Euphrates had this made for his most sacred and deserving wife.
EDCS-11501658.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maria Romana, dedicated by her husband Lucius Marius Euphrates, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501659.translation: To the departed spirits. To Maria Rufina. Marius Sabinianus made this for his deserving patroness.
EDCS-11501659.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maria Rufina, dedicated by her patron Marius Sabinianus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501660.translation: To the departed spirits. To Matitia Aganis, his excellent and deserving wife. Lived 58 years. Caius Cartorius Spendon, her husband, made this.
EDCS-11501660.summary: A funerary epitaph for Matitia Aganis, dedicated by her husband Caius Cartorius Spendon, recording her age, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501661.translation: To the departed spirits. For Matteia Polla, daughter of Caius. Lived 52 years. Fortunata, freedwoman.
EDCS-11501661.summary: A funerary epitaph for Matteia Polla, daughter of Caius, recording her age, and mentioning a freedwoman named Fortunata, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501662.translation: To the departed spirits. To Matutina Lacaena, a most sacred woman. You who read, hail and farewell.
EDCS-11501662.summary: A funerary epitaph for Matutina Lacaena, a sacred woman, with a common closing invocation, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501663.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Musaeus made this for Maxima, his sweetest and incomparable wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-11501663.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Musaeus to his wife Maxima.

EDCS-11501665.translation: Celadus, freedman of Aulus Memmius and his wife, who failed to provide anything.
EDCS-11501665.summary: An epitaph for Celadus, a freedman, possibly noting his neglect in providing for someone.

EDCS-11501667.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Memia Creste made this for her son Memmius Rufus, who lived 3 years, 4 months, and 10 days, who deserved well.
EDCS-11501667.summary: A funerary epitaph for the young Memmius Rufus, dedicated by his mother Memia Creste.

EDCS-11501668.translation: Lucius Mimmius Terentianus, who lived 18 years and 30 days, and lived for 6 months and 21 days after his mother. Sentinius, his father, for his dearest son.
EDCS-11501668.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Sentinius to his son Lucius Mimmius Terentianus, also mentioning the son's mother.

EDCS-11501669.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sozomenes made this for his son Mercurius, who deserved well, who lived 10 years.
EDCS-11501669.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Sozomenes to his son Mercurius.

EDCS-11501670.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for a girl named Meroe.

EDCS-11501671.translation: [...]dito who lived 6 years, 6 months. Gaius Messius Epaphroditus, [---]ania daughter of Atius.
EDCS-11501671.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the age of a child and naming several individuals, possibly family members.

EDCS-11501673.translation: Gaius Messius Tertius, freedman of Gaius Messius, for Milia; Gaius Messius Rufus; Gaius Messius Clemens, son of Rufus; Gaius Messius Faustus, freedman of Gaius Messius; jointly from their own resources.
EDCS-11501673.summary: A funerary inscription listing multiple individuals, likely family members, and Gaius Messius Tertius, who jointly funded the monument.

EDCS-11501675.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. The mother made this for her most dutiful freedwoman daughter.
EDCS-11501675.summary: A funerary epitaph from a mother to her freedwoman daughter, Metilia Supera.

EDCS-11501676.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Metti[---] Pudent[---]
EDCS-11501676.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph mentioning the names Metti[---] and Pudent[---].

EDCS-11501678.translation: Minatia Eupolis, freedwoman of Titus, lived for 6 years.
EDCS-11501678.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of a young girl named Minatia Eupolis.

EDCS-11501679.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Mindius Elpidephorus made this for his deserving brother, Claudio Theseus, who lived for 50 years and 4 months.
EDCS-11501679.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Mindius Elpidephorus and dedicating the monument to his brother, Claudio Theseus.

EDCS-11501680.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Baebia Nais made this for her incomparable and deserving husband, Gaius Mindius Mercurius.
EDCS-11501680.summary: A funerary epitaph by Baebia Nais for her husband, Gaius Mindius Mercurius.

EDCS-11501681.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Mindius Cerdophorus made this for his excellent freedwoman, Mindia Chloe, and for himself, and for Mindius Hegemon, and for Mindia Calliope.
EDCS-11501681.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Titus Mindius Cerdophorus for his freedwoman Mindia Chloe, and also for himself and two other individuals, indicating a shared family burial plot.

EDCS-11501683.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Minucius Iulianus and Eassidia Praetextata, her mother, [made this] for their sweetest daughter, Minucia Praetextata, who lived for 2 years and 3 months.
EDCS-11501683.summary: A funerary epitaph for Minucia Praetextata, who died at a young age, set up by her parents.

EDCS-11501685.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Modestus, adopted son, who lived for 24 years.
EDCS-11501685.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Modestus, an adopted son.

EDCS-11501686.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. [He/She] made this for Gaius Modestius Agathyrsus, his/her son.
EDCS-11501686.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Modestius Agathyrsus, set up by a parent.

EDCS-11501687.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Modia Marcellina. For Erennia Tatina.
EDCS-11501687.summary: A funerary epitaph potentially dedicating a tomb to Modia Marcellina, with Erennia Tatina also mentioned, possibly as the dedicator or another occupant.

EDCS-11501688.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Modia Marcellina [made this] for her sister, Modia, who lived for 54 years.
EDCS-11501688.summary: A funerary epitaph by Modia Marcellina for her sister, Modia.

EDCS-11501690.translation: Publius Multillius Tertius, freedman of a woman.
EDCS-11501690.summary: An ownership or identification inscription for Publius Multillius Tertius, a freedman.

EDCS-11501691.translation: From the will of Titus Munatius Chrysaonis, for himself and his family, by the decision of his wife Munatia.
EDCS-11501691.summary: This inscription records a testament concerning property for the testator Titus Munatius Chrysaonis and his family, executed by his wife Munatia.

EDCS-11501692.translation: Marcus Munatius Dardanus, freedman of Marcus, and Marcus Hostius Eros, freedman of the two Marcuses, have laid here Nimisis, who brought her chaste body to the ashes, whom her father and mother eagerly sought to preserve, the thirteenth hour begrudged her life. Farewell sweet mother, your daughter says farewell. Hail to you too.
EDCS-11501692.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Nimisis, commemorating her by her freedmen Marcus Munatius Dardanus and Marcus Hostius Eros, with a lament for her early death.

EDCS-11501693.translation: Lucius Munatius Dionysius. Lucius Munatius Rufus.
EDCS-11501693.summary: This inscription simply lists the names Lucius Munatius Dionysius and Lucius Munatius Rufus, possibly indicating a shared tomb or a familial relationship.

EDCS-11501694.translation: To the departed spirits. Manius Paccius Hermes made this for Munatia Extricata, his wife of the rarest chastity, most deserving, and for himself and his family, their freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-11501694.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Manius Paccius Hermes for his highly deserving wife Munatia Extricata, also reserving space for himself and future descendants and freedmen.

EDCS-11501696.translation: To the departed spirits. Murronia Ianuaria lived 40 years. Antistia Delicata [erected this].
EDCS-11501696.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Murronia Ianuaria, who lived 40 years, and was commemorated by Antistia Delicata.

EDCS-11501697.translation: To the departed spirits. Musa made this for Ascanio, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11501697.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Musa for her well-deserving husband Ascanio.

EDCS-11501698.translation: To the departed spirits. For Musidia Elpis, who lived 40 years. Her daughter Aelia Felicitissima and her relative Aurelius Paulus made this for her deserving of kindness.
EDCS-11501698.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Musidia Elpis, who lived 40 years, and was commemorated by her daughter Aelia Felicitissima and her relative Aurelius Paulus.

EDCS-11501699.translation: To the departed spirits. Myronides and Ippodamia, most unhappy parents, for their sweetest son Holocalus, who lived 4 years, 5 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-11501699.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Holocalus, a young son who died at the age of 4, set up by his grieving parents Myronides and Ippodamia.

EDCS-11501700.translation: To the departed spirits. For Naevia Arsinoe, daughter of Lucius, who lived 20 years, 9 months, and 7 days, and for Gaius Sextilius Felix, son of Gaius, who lived 1 year, 7 months, and 11 days. Gaius Sextilius Victor made this for his most rare wife and his sweetest son.
EDCS-11501700.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Naevia Arsinoe and her infant son Gaius Sextilius Felix, and was dedicated by the husband and father Gaius Sextilius Victor.

EDCS-11501701.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aufellius Hierus for his well-deserving wife Naevia Felix.
EDCS-11501701.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Lucius Aufellius Hierus for his well-deserving wife Naevia Felix.

EDCS-11501705.translation: To Marcus Nasius, son of Marcus, freedman of Marcus. To Helvia Thallusa, freedwoman of Gnaeus. For Marcus Nasius Fronto(?), freedman of Marcus and of the woman. For Marcus Nasius Fortunatus, freedman of Marcus and of the woman. This building, surrounded by a wall, 50 feet in front, 50 feet in the field, for the protection of the monument.
EDCS-11501705.summary: This inscription records the construction of a building by members of the Nassi family, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-11501707.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Neapolitanus Liberalis, who lived 40 years and 8 months. His mother, Tannonia Veneria, made this for her dearest and well-deserving son.
EDCS-11501707.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Neapolitanus Liberalis, set up by his mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501708.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Nemonius Theodotus. His wife, Aelia Karula.
EDCS-11501708.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Nemonius Theodotus, dedicated by his wife Aelia Karula.

EDCS-11501709.translation: To the departed spirits. For Nemonia Marciana, daughter of Marcus, who lived 20 years and 39 days. Her husband made this for his most pious and deserving wife.
EDCS-11501709.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nemonia Marciana, set up by her husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11501710.translation: To the departed spirits. Neon Nepos, son, made this for his daughter Nepotilla, deserving. She lived 2 years, 4 months, 11 days.
EDCS-11501710.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nepotilla, made by her father Neon Nepos, indicating her young age at death.

EDCS-11501711.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-11501711.translation: Nepotilla and her son Nepotillianus are buried in this place.
EDCS-11501711.summary: This inscription records the burial of Nepotilla and her son Nepotillianus in this location, likely a tomb or cemetery.

EDCS-11501712.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Netatinus Eutychus set this up for himself while alive.
EDCS-11501712.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Gaius Netatinus Eutychus for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-11501713.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Nice, who lived one year, 10 months, 21 days. Her father and mother made this for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-11501713.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Nice, dedicated by her parents.

EDCS-11501714.translation: Nicephor, freedman of Marce[---]. Gaius Munius Philargurus, freedman of Gaius.
EDCS-11501714.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary dedication, possibly mentioning Nicephor and Gaius Munius Philargurus, with some damaged or erased text.

EDCS-11501717.translation: Ninnia Epigenia, freedwoman. Lucius Maecius Hilarus.
EDCS-11501717.summary: This inscription lists two individuals, Ninnia Epigenia and Lucius Maecius Hilarus, likely in a funerary context, indicating their names and status.

EDCS-11501718.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ninnia Magna lived 29 years, 2 months, and 14 days. Marcus Ninnius Euhelpistus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-11501718.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Marcus Ninnius Euhelpistus for his wife Ninnia Magna, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-11501719.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lollius Sollemnis made this for his deserving wife, Ninnia Sancta, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-11501719.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Lollius Sollemnis for his wife Ninnia Sancta, noting her lifespan.

EDCS-11501721.translation: Marcus Norceianus Auctus, freedman of Marcus, lived 24 years. His brother and sister, Herbula, lived 17 years.
EDCS-11501721.summary: An epitaph recording the lives of two siblings, Marcus Norceianus Auctus and Herbula, likely erected by the surviving brother.

EDCS-11501723.translation: Novia Flavia Thallusa. Her husband made this for his deserving wife, who lived 38 years.
EDCS-11501723.summary: A funerary epitaph for Novia Flavia Thallusa, set up by her husband and recording her lifespan.

EDCS-11501725.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Numeria Felicitas lived 50 years. Licinius Strato made this for his wife.
EDCS-11501725.summary: A funerary epitaph for Numeria Felicitas, set up by her husband Licinius Strato, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-11501726.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Numisia Bassa made this for her most sweet husband, Caius Numisius Martialis, who lived 33 years, 10 months, and 27 days.
EDCS-11501726.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Numisius Martialis, set up by his wife Numisia Bassa, detailing his lifespan.

EDCS-11501727.translation: To the spirits of the departed for Numisia Maxima, deserving wife, [and for] her descendants. Caius Mestrius Cam[---] lived [---] years.
EDCS-11501727.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph dedicated to Numisia Maxima and her descendants, with partial information about Caius Mestrius Cam[---].

EDCS-11501728.translation: [To the spirits of the departed]. Numisia Pietas made this for her most dutiful mother, Numisia Saturnina, who lived [---] years.
EDCS-11501728.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Numisia Pietas for her mother Numisia Saturnina, recording her lifespan in part.

EDCS-11501729.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Felicla made this for her most sweet sister, Numisia Victoria, who lived 27 years, 8 months, and 16 days.
EDCS-11501729.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Felicla for her sister Numisia Victoria, detailing her lifespan.

EDCS-11501730.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Occius Epigonus, freedman, made this for Quintus Occius Narcissus.
EDCS-11501730.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by the freedman Quintus Occius Epigonus for his patron Quintus Occius Narcissus.

EDCS-11501732.translation: Gaius Octavius Agathopus the younger, weary from east to west, Regilla has placed me here; I rest, daughter of Gnaeus Sentius and Quintus Pomponius, during their consulship.
EDCS-11501732.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Octavius Agathopus the younger, placed during the consulship of Gnaeus Sentius and Quintus Pomponius.

EDCS-11501733.summary: This is a funerary epitaph naming Marcus Octavius Eusebius Gemellus.

EDCS-11501734.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudia Veneria made this for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Octavius Felix, who lived 47 years and 4 months.
EDCS-11501734.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Claudia Veneria to her husband, Marcus Octavius Felix, who lived 47 years and 4 months.

EDCS-11501736.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Octavius Secundus placed this.
EDCS-11501736.summary: This is a funerary epitaph indicating that Quintus Octavius Secundus placed it, likely for himself or a family member.

EDCS-11501737.translation: To the spirits. Quintus Octavius Secundus.
EDCS-11501737.summary: This is a funerary epitaph naming Quintus Octavius Secundus.

EDCS-11501738.translation: To the departed spirits. For Octavia Crispilla, daughter of Lucius, who lived 27 years, 7 months, and 12 days. Tiberius Iulius Iucundinus made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11501738.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Octavia Crispilla, daughter of Lucius, who lived 27 years, 7 months, and 12 days, erected by Tiberius Iulius Iucundinus.

EDCS-11501740.translation: To the departed spirits. For Octavia Quinta, most dutiful mother. Her daughter, Lutatia Quintilla, made this.
EDCS-11501740.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Lutatia Quintilla to her most dutiful mother, Octavia Quinta.

EDCS-11501741.translation: To the departed spirits. For Octabia Secunda and Marcus Octabius Secundus, who lived 2 years, 10 months, and 14 days. Their wretched mother made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-11501741.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Octabia Secunda and Marcus Octabius Secundus, erected by their mother.

EDCS-11501742.translation: For Octavia Venusta, dearest daughter, who lived 2 years, 7 months. Her most unhappy parents made this.
EDCS-11501742.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Octavia Venusta, dearest daughter, erected by her unhappy parents.

EDCS-11501743.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Ofillius Staphylus, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-11501744.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ofilia Ampliata lived 22 years, 4 months; she is the girl whom you destined to remain in the country for a delicate body.
EDCS-11501744.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ofilia Ampliata, recording her age and a poignant statement about her death.

EDCS-11501746.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Nestor made this for his dearest and most deserving wife, Ofilia Procilla, who lived 21 years and 8 months.
EDCS-11501746.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating Ofilia Procilla, erected by her husband Aurelius Nestor and stating her age.

EDCS-11501747.translation: Luchnis lived 37 years; for himself and his father, and for Oinadi his mother, and for his companion; Successus, son of Spurius, [lived] 9 years; and for his family.
EDCS-11501747.summary: A funerary inscription for Luchnis and his family members, including his father, mother, and companion, with ages partially preserved.

EDCS-11501748.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Olius Euhodus made this for his dearest son, Caius Olius Euhodianus, who lived two years, seven months, and twenty days.
EDCS-11501748.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Olius Euhodianus, recording his age and erected by his father, Caius Olius Euhodus.

EDCS-11501749.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Parents made this for their dearest son, Titus Oppius Victorinus, who lived eleven months and twenty days.
EDCS-11501749.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Oppius Victorinus, recording his young age and dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-11501750.translation: Oppia Bassilla, daughter of Titus, the most unhappy mother, made this for her best and most pious son, Bassus, and for herself, and for Amandus, freedman of Augustus, her husband, and for the freedmen and freedwomen, both of my son Bassus and my own, and for Amandus and their born or to be born children, in a purchased place measuring three jugera of land and surrounded by all buildings with a wall.
EDCS-11501750.summary: A funerary inscription purchased for a family plot, commemorating Oppia Bassilla's son Bassus, her husband Amandus, and other family members.

EDCS-11501751.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Oppia Sponsa, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-11501751.summary: A funerary epitaph for Oppia Sponsa, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-11501752.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Practicus made this for his deserving brother, Lorenio Telesphorus, a most rare man.
EDCS-11501752.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lorenio Telesphorus, described as a 'most rare man', erected by his brother Practicus.

EDCS-11501753.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Orfia Vitalis, stating her name and age.

EDCS-11501754.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Ostorius Italus made this for his deserving freedman, Caius Ostorius Anthimus.
EDCS-11501754.summary: A funerary epitaph for the freedman Caius Ostorius Anthimus, erected by his patron Caius Ostorius Italus.

EDCS-11501755.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Lollius Severus made this for his most rare and incomparable wife, Otacilia Apollonia Marcella, who lived 40 years and 3 months.
EDCS-11501755.summary: A funerary epitaph for Otacilia Apollonia Marcella, set up by her husband Lucius Lollius Severus.

EDCS-11501756.translation: To Gnaeus Ovellius Bassus. Trebia Crysaris, freedwoman of the man Trebius, and Gnaeus Ovellius Bassus. He lived 23 years.
EDCS-11501756.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gnaeus Ovellius Bassus, possibly commemorating two individuals with the same name, set up by Trebia Crysaris.

EDCS-11501757.translation: To the departed spirits. Papiria Eunia arranged for this to be made for her patroness, Papiria Ionica, who deserved well.
EDCS-11501757.summary: A funerary epitaph for Papiria Ionica, dedicated by her freedwoman Papiria Eunia.

EDCS-11501758.translation: To Caius Pacius Epaphroditus, freedman of Caius Pacius.
EDCS-11501758.summary: A dedication to Caius Pacius Epaphroditus, a freedman.

EDCS-11501759.translation: To the departed spirits. Pacia Spes made this for Lucius Pacius Justus, her patron and husband, who deserved well.
EDCS-11501759.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Pacius Justus, set up by his wife and former freedwoman, Pacia Spes.

EDCS-11501760.translation: Pacia Basa lived 45 years. Her daughter made this for her mother.
EDCS-11501760.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pacia Basa, set up by her daughter.

EDCS-11501761.translation: Paccia Euposia, freedwoman of Lucius. Sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-11501761.summary: A funerary inscription for Paccia Euposia, a freedwoman, dedicated by Pia.

EDCS-11501764.translation: To the departed spirits. Magius Felicisimus made this for his deserving wife, Paconia Vitalini, who lived 37 years and 6 months.
EDCS-11501764.summary: A funerary epitaph for Paconia Vitalini, set up by her husband Magius Felicisimus.

EDCS-11501765.translation: To the departed spirits. Papadi, who lived for one year and 65 days. His mother made this.
EDCS-11501765.summary: A funerary epitaph for the infant Papadi, set up by his mother.

EDCS-11501766.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulius Sabinianus made this for his deserving wife, Papiria Concordies, who lived 43 years.
EDCS-11501766.summary: A funerary epitaph for Papiria Concordies, set up by her husband Iulius Sabinianus.

EDCS-11501767.translation: To the departed spirits. Melippus (?) made this for his well-deserving wife Parthenis.
EDCS-11501767.summary: A funerary dedication from a husband to his wife, dating possibly to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501768.translation: Lucius Patulcius Felix for himself and for his patron Lucius Patulcius Cerdon and for his patron Lucius Patulcius Hermias and for his freedwoman mother Dionysia and for his sister Prima, freedwoman of Sextus, and for his wife Methe, freedwoman of Sextus, and for his daughter Lucius? Prepusia and for Lucius Fausto, freedman of Lucius Patulcius and for Sophron, freedman of a woman, and for Sophe, freedwoman of a woman, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and all their kin.
EDCS-11501768.summary: A complex funerary dedication for an extended family and their freedmen, indicating a well-established household, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501769.summary: A simple tombstone recording the name and age at death of Lucius Patulcius Primigenius, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501770.translation: To the departed spirits of Patulcia Synichi.
EDCS-11501770.summary: A funerary dedication to Patulcia Synichi, dating possibly to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501771.translation: Gaius Pavillius Heracleo, freedman of Gaius, and Marcia Laudica, freedwoman of a woman.
EDCS-11501771.summary: An epitaph likely recording the names of two individuals, possibly a couple, from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501773.translation: Gaius Pavillius Nicia, freedman of Gaius; Pavillia Hilara, freedwoman of Gaius; Pavillia Erotis, freedwoman of Gaius; Secundio, aged 9.
EDCS-11501773.summary: A funerary inscription listing several individuals, including a child who died at age 9, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501774.translation: Pavillia, daughter of Manius; Tiberius Pavillius, son of Manius; Gaius Pavillius, son of Tiberius; Gaius Pavillius, son of Gaius, grandson of Tiberius; Tiberius Pavillius, son of Gaius; Pavillia, daughter of Gaius; Instania, daughter of Marcus; Gaius Pavillius, son of Gaius, grandson of Gaius; Pavillia Cornuta, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-11501774.summary: A family record listing multiple generations of the Pavillius family, potentially an epitaph or a genealogy marker, dating possibly to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501775.summary: An epitaph for Pavillia Synethea, a freedwoman, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501776.summary: A simple epitaph for Aulus Paxeus Atticus, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501777.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pereliae Tyche Publius Anteius Quadreius sic matri amatae merenti fecit\n
EDCS-11501777.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Anteius Quadreius made this for his beloved and deserving mother Perelia Tyche.
EDCS-11501777.summary: A funerary dedication from a son to his mother, dating possibly to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-11501778.translation: To the departed spirits. Sacred to Aulus Perpernius Demetrius.
EDCS-11501778.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits for Aulus Perpernius Demetrius.

EDCS-11501780.translation: Lucius Pescennius Cilo, son of Marcus, of the voting tribe Quirina, is alive. Aurelia Olympias, freedwoman of Gaius.
EDCS-11501780.summary: This inscription records the lives of Lucius Pescennius Cilo and Aurelia Olympias, possibly as a joint epitaph or testament.

EDCS-11501782.translation: Peticia Hedone has been placed here.
EDCS-11501782.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Peticia Hedone has been buried here.

EDCS-11501783.translation: Marcus Petronius Martial, son of Marcus, of the voting tribe Velina, is here.
EDCS-11501783.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Marcus Petronius Martial has been buried here.

EDCS-11501785.translation: To the departed spirits. To Petronia Regina, most virtuous wife, from Gaius Vibius Aventinus.
EDCS-11501785.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Gaius Vibius Aventinus to his wife Petronia Regina.

EDCS-11501787.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription records the age of a person named Philadelphus.

EDCS-11501789.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Philematio Pequliaris, with likely errors in spelling.

EDCS-11501791.translation: Phoebus to Hedone, his deserving wife, lived 19 years.
EDCS-11501791.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Hedone, wife of Phoebus, who lived 19 years.

EDCS-11501792.translation: Gnaeus Pitius Primigenius for himself and for Babbia Agathemeris, his deserving wife, and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, as the owner of one-and-a-quarter parts of this monument.
EDCS-11501792.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gnaeus Pitius Primigenius for himself, his wife, their freedpeople, and descendants, indicating ownership of a portion of the monument.

EDCS-11501793.translation: To the departed spirits. Of Aulus Plautius Onesimianus, lived 8 years and 3 days... parents
EDCS-11501793.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Aulus Plautius Onesimianus, recording his age and likely intended to be completed by his parents.

EDCS-11501794.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Domitius Gamio erected this for his brother, Lucius Plutius Gelasinus.
EDCS-11501794.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Lucius Domitius Gamio to his brother, Lucius Plutius Gelasinus.

EDCS-11501795.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Felicula erected this, deserving, for Lucius Plutius Quintus, who lived 45 years and 11 days.
EDCS-11501795.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Felicula for Lucius Plutius Quintus, who lived 45 years and 11 days.

EDCS-11501796.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Plautia Procula, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-11501796.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Plautia Procula, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-11501797.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Demetrius erected this, deserving, for his wife Pollia Victoria, who lived 32 years.
EDCS-11501797.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Demetrius for his wife Pollia Victoria, who lived 32 years.

EDCS-11501798.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pollia Privata lived 5 years.
EDCS-11501798.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pollia Privata, who lived 5 years.

EDCS-11501802.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life span of Gnaeus Pompeius Ampliatus, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-11501803.translation: Gnaeus Pompeius Euphrosynus and his wife Iunia Gemella acquired this from their own property for themselves.
EDCS-11501803.summary: This inscription records Gnaeus Pompeius Euphrosynus and his wife Iunia Gemella acquiring property for themselves.

EDCS-11501804.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Pompeius Demetrius placed this for his stepson, Sextus Pompeius Mercurius.
EDCS-11501804.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Sextus Pompeius Demetrius for his stepson, Sextus Pompeius Mercurius.

EDCS-11501805.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gnaeus Pompeius Maximus erected this for Gnaeus Pompeius Veranianus, Pompeia Maxima, Iulius Diadumenus, and Duronia Callityche.
EDCS-11501805.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Gnaeus Pompeius Maximus for Gnaeus Pompeius Veranianus, Pompeia Maxima, Iulius Diadumenus, and Duronia Callityche.

EDCS-11501807.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pompeia, the mother, erected this for her most dutiful daughter, Pompeia Acteni, who lived 23 years, 9 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-11501807.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by a mother for her dutiful daughter, Pompeia Acteni, who lived 23 years, 9 months, and 20 days.

EDCS-11501810.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Pompeia Hiera, for whom Quintus Pompeius Eutyches (?) made this [monument] as a deserving wife.
EDCS-11501810.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband to his wife.

EDCS-11501811.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pompeia Tyche (freedwoman) and the slave girl Ianuaria set up this monument to Pompeius their patron, who deserved well.
EDCS-11501811.summary: This funerary epitaph records freedwomen and a slave setting up a monument for their patron.

EDCS-11501812.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---] Caius Pomponius [---] Caius Pomponius [---] Caius Pomponius [---] Lucius Iulius Faustus [---]
EDCS-11501812.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely for multiple individuals named Caius Pomponius and possibly Lucius Iulius Faustus.

EDCS-11501813.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Pomponius Abascantus, who lived 27 years. Pomponius Axius to his dearest freedman.
EDCS-11501813.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from a master to his freedman, including the freedman's age at death.

EDCS-11501814.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Pomponis Crescens, his grandsons Rheno and Danuvius, and to their father Euphrates, a most simple man. Pomponius Rhenus, his father, made [this monument] for me, who took care of me when I did not deserve it.
EDCS-11501814.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father for his son and grandsons, with a note of gratitude.

EDCS-11501815.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Pomponius Rhenus, a most excellent man. His heirs [erected this].
EDCS-11501815.summary: This funerary epitaph records the erection of a monument by the heirs to an excellent man named Gaius Pomponius Rhenus.

EDCS-11501818.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gnaeus Pomponius Primus, son of Spurius, made [this] for himself while alive.
EDCS-11501818.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording a man who built his own tomb while still living.

EDCS-11501821.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gnaeus Pontius Zosimus. Pontia Felicula to her deserving husband.
EDCS-11501821.summary: This funerary epitaph records a wife dedicating a monument to her deserving husband.

EDCS-11501822.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Pontia Capitoina, daughter of Lucius. Marcus Amullius Exoratus, son of Marcus, to his most dutiful wife, who deserved well, who lived 19 years, 11 months, and 18 days.
EDCS-11501822.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pontia Capitoline, recording her age at death and erected by her husband.

EDCS-11501823.summary: This is a short inscription, possibly a dedication or ownership mark, related to Pontia Euhemeris.

EDCS-11501824.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Pontia Frontis, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-11501824.summary: A funerary dedication for Pontia Frontis, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-11501826.translation: Lucius Popillius Balbus, son of Marcus, [restored name/patronymic].
EDCS-11501826.summary: A fragmentary inscription identifying Lucius Popillius Balbus, son of Marcus.

EDCS-11501827.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Pothusa.

EDCS-11501828.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Potiolana Tyche. Marcus Ulpius Euangelus made this.
EDCS-11501828.summary: A funerary dedication for Potiolana Tyche, made by Marcus Ulpius Euangelus.

EDCS-11501829.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Potus [restored name/patronymic], son of Caesar [restored patronymic/name].
EDCS-11501829.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication for Potus.

EDCS-11501830.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Prastina Felix. Gaius Prastina Paccatus and Caelerina, his patrons, made this.
EDCS-11501830.summary: A funerary dedication for Gaius Prastina Felix, set up by his patrons.

EDCS-11501831.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For [restored name] Prastinia, [restored name] Trofima, his wife who well deserved. Onesimus [restored name] made this, who lived for 36 years, 3 months, 23 days.
EDCS-11501831.summary: A funerary dedication for Trofima, wife of Onesimus, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-11501832.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Prepusia. Antigonus made this for his well-deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-11501832.summary: A funerary dedication for Prepusia, made by her fellow slave Antigonus.

EDCS-11501833.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Prima lived for 30 years, 7 months, 6 days.
EDCS-11501833.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Prima.

EDCS-11501834.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Primi lived for 11 years [restored]. Phlegon and Felici [restored name] made this for him, who well deserved.
EDCS-11501834.summary: A funerary dedication for Primi, made by Phlegon and Felici.

EDCS-11501838.translation: To the departed spirits. Profuturus, a slave, lived for 50 years. Pomponia Procullina, his mistress, made this for him as one who deserved well.
EDCS-11501838.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a slave named Profuturus, erected by his mistress Pomponia Procullina.

EDCS-11501840.translation: To the departed spirits. For Publicia Januaria, who lived 17 years, 5 months. Publicius Januarius and Valeria Felicula, parents, [made this] for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-11501840.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publicia Januaria, erected by her parents Publicius Januarius and Valeria Felicula.

EDCS-11501841.translation: To the departed spirits. For Pupia Celsa, who lived 27 years, 4 months, 25 days. Her most unhappy mother made this.
EDCS-11501841.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pupia Celsa, erected by her grieving mother.

EDCS-11501842.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Pullius Primus.

EDCS-11501843.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph, likely just the name of the deceased, Pullia Arethusa.

EDCS-11501844.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Pullia Epaphrodite.

EDCS-11501845.translation: To the departed spirits. For Puteolani, who lived 3 years, 4 months, 22 days. Felix and Vitalis made this for their dearest son.
EDCS-11501845.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Puteolani, erected by his parents Felix and Vitalis.

EDCS-11501846.translation: To the departed spirits. For Quartilla, his wife. Vitalio made this. She lived 40 years.
EDCS-11501846.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quartilla, erected by her husband Vitalio.

EDCS-11501847.translation: Quintia Saturnina for herself and for her husband Marcus Octavius Euhodus and for their freedmen and their descendants.
EDCS-11501847.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Quintia Saturnina for herself, her husband, and their dependents, possibly indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-11501848.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Quintilius Puteolano, his son, who lived 25 years, 8 months, 17 days. Manius Quintilius Primus, his most unhappy father, made this for one who deserved well. Farewell, son.
EDCS-11501848.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Quintilius Puteolano, erected by his grieving father, Manius Quintilius Primus.

EDCS-11501849.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintilia Chaerusa.

EDCS-11501851.translation: To the departed spirits. Plotio Laius...
EDCS-11501851.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated to Plotio Laius.

EDCS-11501853.translation: Vitor. Gaius Ravius Menander and Vibbia Callituche.
EDCS-11501853.summary: This inscription names two individuals, possibly a couple or owner and slave.

EDCS-11501855.translation: To the departed spirits. To Regina, a wife well-deserving, who lived 50 years, 6 months, 17 days, and 9 hours. Primetivus made this.
EDCS-11501855.summary: This tombstone records the death of Regina and was erected by her husband Primetivus.

EDCS-11501856.translation: To Restutus, who lived 40 years and 4 months. Flavia Romula made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-11501856.summary: This tombstone records the death of Restutus and was erected by his wife Flavia Romula.

EDCS-11501857.translation: To the departed spirits. To Rhode, most beloved daughter. Lacon, father, made this.
EDCS-11501857.summary: This tombstone records the death of Rhode and was erected by her father Lacon.

EDCS-11501858.translation: To the departed spirits. To Lucius Roscio Prepon. His aunt, Roscia Praepis, made this for her well-deserving nephew, who lived 20 years and 5 days.
EDCS-11501858.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lucius Roscio Prepon and was erected by his aunt.

EDCS-11501860.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Rutilius Faustus, who lived 21 years and 26 days. His parents, to their most loving son.
EDCS-11501860.summary: This tombstone records the death of Quintus Rutilius Faustus and was erected by his parents.

EDCS-11501861.translation: To Quintus Rutilius Filoxenus, who lived 14 years, 5 months, and 17 days. Rutilia Isias, his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-11501861.summary: This tombstone records the death of Quintus Rutilius Filoxenus and was erected by his mother.

EDCS-11501862.translation: To the departed spirits. To Marcus Rutilius Proculus, who lived 60 years, 2 months, and 13 days. The two Rutilii Proculi made this for their excellent father.
EDCS-11501862.summary: This tombstone records the death of Marcus Rutilius Proculus and was erected by his sons.

EDCS-11501863.translation: To the shades of the departed. Gaius Rutilius Rufus lived 14 years, 3 months, 12 days. Gaius Rutilius Rufus, his father, made this for his son.
EDCS-11501863.summary: A funerary dedication by a father for his young son, dated by the mention of the deceased's name.

EDCS-11501864.translation: To the shades of the departed. For Sabidia Secunda, aged 21 years, 3 months, 16 days. Publius Sabidius Trophimus and Sabidia Primitiva made this monument or tomb for their most dutiful daughter; it will not pass to an external heir.
EDCS-11501864.summary: A funerary dedication by parents for their deceased daughter, including a clause about inheritance, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11600006.translation: To the shades of the departed. For Saturnina, born on a Saturday, who died on a Saturday. She lived 3 years, 5 months, 20 days.
EDCS-11600006.summary: A funerary dedication for a child named Saturnina, possibly from the 2nd or 3rd century AD, noting her birth and death on a Saturday.

EDCS-11600008.translation: Lucius Saufeius Arabis, for himself and for those whose names are inscribed. Aulus Plautius, [---]
EDCS-11600008.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a dedication or funerary dedication by Lucius Saufeius Arabs for himself and others, with the remains of another name possibly mentioned.

EDCS-11600009.translation: To the Infernal Gods. For Quintus Scaentius Sabinianus, most sacred youth, who lived 30 years, 3 months, 11 days. Scaentius Geminus, his brother, for his worthy fellow member, made this.
EDCS-11600009.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Scaentius Sabinianus, set up by his fellow member and brother, Scaentius Geminus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11600011.translation: To the Gods Below. Scalia Magna lived 22 years, 4 months. Scalia Tyche, for her well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-11600011.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Scalia Magna, erected by her mother Scalia Tyche, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11600012.translation: To the Infernal Gods. Scentia Domitia, freedwoman of Scentius Secundus, lived 10 years. Scentia Domitia, for her well-deserving one, made this.
EDCS-11600012.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Scentia Domitia, erected by her patron or relative also named Scentia Domitia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11600013.translation: The bones of Gaius Scribonius Erotes.
EDCS-11600013.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription recording the remains of Gaius Scribonius Erotes.

EDCS-11700692.translation: To Gaius Valerius Hermetus. In recognition of his decurional honors, the most splendid order honored him, the order of the Augustales, their patron, for his merits. A place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11700692.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial place to Gaius Valerius Hermetus by the order of the Augustales for his merits and his decurional honors.

EDCS-11700697.translation: To the Infernal Gods. For Victorina. Quintus Cluvius Iocimus made this for his well-deserving wife, who lived 48 years and lived with her husband for 22 years.
EDCS-11700697.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Victorina, erected by her husband Quintus Cluvius Iocimus, detailing her age and the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-11700708.translation: To the Infernal Gods. For Marcus Poblicius Primitivus, freedman of Marcus Poblicius. Gaius Vennius Firmus, for his beloved pupil and tent-mate, who lived 24 years, 1 month, 23 days.
EDCS-11700708.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Poblicius Primitivus, set up by Gaius Vennius Firmus, indicating a close relationship likely formed in a military or collegial context.

EDCS-11700714.translation: To the Infernal Gods. For Fabia Sperata. Albinus Primitius made this for his well-deserving wife, with whom he lived 25 years.
EDCS-11700714.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia Sperata, erected by her husband Albinus Primitius, who also notes the length of their marriage.

EDCS-11700716.translation: While alive. Gaius Lucius Amoenus, freedman of Gaius Lucius, for himself and for Gaius Lucius Philonicus, freedman of Gaius Lucius, his patron, and for Luicia Chrestine, freedwoman of Gaius Lucius. In front 12 feet, in the field 14 feet.
EDCS-11700716.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Gaius Lucius Amoenus for himself and two freedpersons (one his patron), specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-11700718.translation: While still alive, Titus Pomponius Hilarus, freedman of Titus, a *seviri*, for himself and his living wife Pomponia, freedwoman of Titus, and their living slave Logismus, with a frontage of 12 feet and a depth of 12 feet (?).
EDCS-11700718.summary: This is a funerary monument for Titus Pomponius Hilarus and his family, set up by himself while alive, dating to the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11700719.translation: While still alive, Titus Pomponius Hilarus, freedman of Titus, a *seviri*, for himself and his living wife Pomponia, freedwoman of Titus, and their living slave Logismus, with a frontage of 12 feet and a depth of 12 feet (?).
EDCS-11700719.summary: This is a funerary monument for Titus Pomponius Hilarus and his family, set up by himself while alive, dating to the late 1st or 2nd century CE, and is a very similar but partially restored version of EDCS-11700718.

EDCS-11700720.translation: Quintus Sextius Protomacus, freedman of Quintus, and Sextia Philotis, freedwoman of Quintus, for themselves and their family, with a frontage of 12 feet.
EDCS-11700720.summary: This is a funerary monument for Quintus Sextius Protomacus and Sextia Philotis and their family, set up by themselves while alive, dating to the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11700731.translation: To the departed spirits. Atilius Liberalis, son, and his husband Aurelius Celadio, made this for their well-deserving parents Atilius Felicissimus and Cominia Primilla.
EDCS-11700731.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication made by a son and his husband to his parents, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11700737.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Statilius Germanus made this tomb for his most beloved and well-deserving wife, Luria Philete, with whom he lived for about 30 years. This tomb was given as a gift, with the permission of his co-heirs.
EDCS-11700737.summary: This funerary inscription records a tomb made by Titus Statilius Germanus for his wife Luria Philete, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11900007.translation: For the safety of the Emperors Augustus Severus and Antoninus, and the most noble Caesar Geta, and Julia Augusta, mother of the Emperors and of the camps, and for the Genius of the salt workers of the whole city of the Roman salt works. Restitutianus Cornelianus from the treasury of the 16th region, and the cashier of the Roman salt works, together with his daughter Ingenua, gave this gift. Dedicated by Sallustius Saturninus and Orfitus, procuators of our Emperors.
EDCS-11900007.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the imperial family and the Genius of the salt workers by treasury officials and a cashier, dating to the reign of Septimius Severus and Caracalla (circa 198-211 CE).

EDCS-11900010.text_edition: \n Herculi Invicto sacrum Decimus Caecilius Aprilis iussu eius statuam cum basi inpensa sua posuit\n
EDCS-11900010.translation: Sacred to the Invincible Hercules. Decimus Caecilius Aprilis, by his command, placed this statue with its base at his own expense.
EDCS-11900010.summary: This inscription records a statue dedicated to Hercules by Decimus Caecilius Aprilis, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11900012.translation: Publius Cornelius Victorinus, son of Publius, follower of Isis and Anubis, and a clerk and scribe of the city of Ostia, dedicated this statue of Mars with its little chariot to the Queen Isis Restitutrice for his health.
EDCS-11900012.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue of Mars and a chariot to Isis by Publius Cornelius Victorinus, a scribe in Ostia, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11900015.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Varius Secund VIvir Augustalis idem ex viso Lares argen numero X cum hypobasi argentea titulo donum \n
EDCS-11900015.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Quintus Varius Secundus, *seviri Augustalis*, and also by vision, gave as a gift ten silver Lares with a silver base and inscription.
EDCS-11900015.summary: This inscription records a dedication of silver Lares to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Varius Secundus, an *seviri Augustalis*, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11900031.translation: Marcus Lollianus Callinicus, the father, gave this altar as a gift to the god.
EDCS-11900031.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to an unnamed god by Marcus Lollianus Callinicus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11900035.text_edition: \n Deum vetusta religione in velo formatum et umore obnubilatum marmoreum cum throno omnibusque ornamentis a solo omni impendio suo fecit Sextus Pompeius Maximus pater qui sacerdos Solis est et praesepia marmoravit pedes LXVIII idem sua pecunia\n
EDCS-11900035.translation: Sextus Pompeius Maximus, father and priest of Sol, made this marble statue with its throne and all its adornments from the ground up at his own expense, as well as the marble mangers 68 feet long, which had been formed from an ancient religious image on a veil and obscured by dampness; he did the same with his own money.
EDCS-11900035.summary: An inscription recording the donation of a marble statue, throne, adornments, and manger by Sextus Pompeius Maximus, a priest of Sol, likely for a public religious site.

EDCS-11900040.text_edition: \n Numini domus Augusti Victor et Hedistus vernae dispensatores cum Traiano Augusti liberto A X OM\n
EDCS-11900040.translation: Victor and Hedistus, freedmen household slaves, stewards, with Traianus, freedman of Augustus. For the divine power of the imperial household. In the year 10.
EDCS-11900040.summary: An inscription by two imperial freedman stewards, Victor and Hedistus, dedicating something to the divine power of the imperial household, likely during the reign of Trajan.

EDCS-11900042.text_edition: \n Numfabus sic Titus Aminnericus donum fecit\n
EDCS-11900042.translation: Numbfabus made this offering, as did Titus Aminnericus.
EDCS-11900042.summary: A brief inscription recording offerings made by Numbfabus and Titus Aminnericus, likely at a shrine.

EDCS-11900045.text_edition: \n Saluti Caesaris Augusti Glabrio patronus coloniae decreto decurionum faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-11900045.translation: Glabrio, patron of the colony, had this made by decree of the decurions for the health of Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-11900045.summary: An inscription stating that Glabrio, patron of the colony, commissioned a monument by decree of the town council for the health of Emperor Caesar Augustus.

EDCS-11900047.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Nervae Traiani Caesaris Augusti Germanici Dacici Hilarus sociorum vectigalis ferrariarum servus donum dedit aquae Traianae feliciter\n
EDCS-11900047.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. For the health of the Emperor Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus. Hilarus, a slave of the tax collectors for the iron mines, gave this offering. May the waters of Trajan be fortunate!
EDCS-11900047.summary: An inscription dedicated to Silvanus for the health of Emperor Trajan by Hilarus, a slave working for the iron mine tax collectors, also mentioning the aqueduct.

EDCS-11900060.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesari Augusto Quintus Orfius P ex voto\n
EDCS-11900060.translation: To Tiberius Caesar Augustus. Quintus Orfius, from a vow.
EDCS-11900060.summary: A dedication to Emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus by Quintus Orfius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-11900075.translation: To Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of Emperor Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 21st time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of his country, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, with tribunician power, consul for the 2nd time.
EDCS-11900075.summary: A dedicatory inscription to Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of Hadrian and heir, detailing his titles and lineage up to Nerva, likely erected during his lifetime.

EDCS-11900076.translation: To Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, with tribunician power, designated consul for the 2nd time.
EDCS-11900076.summary: A dedicatory inscription to Emperor Antoninus Pius, detailing his imperial titles and ancestry back to Nerva, likely erected during his reign.

EDCS-11900085.text_edition: \n Divo Pio Pertinaci Augusto collegium fabrum tignuariorum Ostiensium curam agentibus Caio Plotio Ca Salinatore Ianuario Lucio Faianio Olympo magistris quinquennalibus lustri XXVIII\n
EDCS-11900085.translation: To the deified Pius Pertinax Augustus. The guild of carpenters of Ostia, with Gaius Plotius Ca. Salinatore Ianario and Lucius Faianius Olympos as quinquennial magistrates for the 28th lustrum, took care of this.
EDCS-11900085.summary: An inscription commemorating the deified Emperor Pertinax, dedicated by the guild of Ostian carpenters during their quinquennial magistracy.

EDCS-11900086.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, consul, the Best and Most Pious.
