EDCS-12401857.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely for Aquillius Rufus, detailing his magistracies and dedicating the tomb to his family members and concubine.

EDCS-12401858.translation: To Claudia Fadilla, daughter of Tiberius, a woman of the highest distinction, priestess of the divine Augustae, for her excellent love of country. By the Augustales, at public expense.
EDCS-12401858.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Augustales to Claudia Fadilla, a priestess of the deified empresses, honoring her patriotism.

EDCS-12401860.translation: Caedia Festa, his most unhappy mother, made this arch and altar to Lucius Fadius Caedianus, son of Lucius, Roman knight, a son of most rare piety. He lived 25 years, 5 months, and 4 days.
EDCS-12401860.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by a mother for her son, Lucius Fadius Caedianus, noting his virtues and lifespan.

EDCS-12401861.translation: To Lucius Fadius Piero, twice-governor, most munificent citizen, who, on account of the honor of the decurionate, in the same year he was appointed, gave 30 pairs of gladiators and a hunt of African wild animals, and after a few months, during his term as twice-governor, having received 13,000 sesterces from the state, he gave full hunts and 21 pairs of gladiators; likewise the following year, he presented stage plays at his own expense. The Augustales. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12401861.summary: This inscription records the extensive public benefactions of Lucius Fadius Piero, a local magistrate, in the form of gladiatorial games, hunts, and theatrical performances, likely in the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-12401864.translation: To Marcus Granius Cordus, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, brother of Cordus, tribune of the soldiers, prefect of the cavalry, prefect of the artisans, twice-governor, three times quinquennial, aedile, quaestor, curator of the aqueduct at Allifae, by decree of the decurions; and to Oppidia Rufa, daughter of Caius, mother.
EDCS-12401864.summary: This is a funerary monument honoring Marcus Granius Cordus and his mother, Oppidia Rufa, listing Marcus's extensive career in local magistracies and military/civil appointments, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-12401865.translation: To Sextus Minius Silvanus, son of Sextus, of the Teretina tribe, aedile, twice-governor, twice quinquennial, patron of the colony of Allifae, quaestor of the alimentary fund, quaestor of the public treasury, defender of the state, overseer of the grain supply, also curator of the citizenship of the Atinatium, also curator of the Ligures Corneliani, comrade of the Veneris conventicle, patron for his merits; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12401865.summary: This inscription honors Sextus Minius Silvanus, detailing his significant public career and patronage roles in various communities, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401867.translation: To Gaius Naevoleius Rufus, son of Marcus, of the Teretina tribe, twice-governor, aedile, quaestor; and to Naevoleia Procula, daughter of Gaius, wife of Labeo.
EDCS-12401867.summary: This is a dedication honoring Gaius Naevoleius Rufus for his magistracies and his wife, Naevoleia Procula, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-12401868.translation: To Quintus Ovius Scaevola, son of Caius, of the Teretina tribe, quaestor, aedile, twice-governor, by his will.
EDCS-12401868.summary: This inscription records the posthumous honors bestowed upon Quintus Ovius Scaevola by his will, detailing his local magistracies, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-12401869.translation: To Sontia, priestess of Venus, by her will.
EDCS-12401869.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Sontia, a priestess of Venus, made by her will, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12401870.text_edition: \n Statio Luci filio Rutilo tribuno militum praefecto equitum IIviro iterum quinquennali aedili ? Augustales honoris causa fecerunt\n
EDCS-12401870.translation: The Augustales made this in honor of Statia, son of Lucius Rutilus, tribune of the soldiers, twice-governor, quinquennial, aedile, with honor.
EDCS-12401870.summary: This inscription records an honorary monument erected by the Augustales for Statia Rutilus, acknowledging his various civic and military offices, likely dating to the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-12401873.translation: Lucius Acilius Philomusus, freedman of Lucius, Augustalis at Allifae, master of the sacred rites of Juno, for himself and his family.
EDCS-12401873.summary: This is a dedication by Lucius Acilius Philomusus, an Augustalis and master of Juno's rites at Allifae, for himself and his family, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12401874.translation: Epaphroditus, slave of Aedia, lived 3 years. Marcus Aedius Epaphra, freedman of Aedia Servilia, Augustalis at Allifae, quaestor of the Augustales, master of the sacred rites of Juno, for himself and Aedia Megiste, his wife, and for his brothers Martialis and Anterus.
EDCS-12401874.summary: This funerary inscription records Epaphroditus, a slave, and Marcus Aedius Epaphra, an Augustalis, listing his offices and dedicating the monument to his family, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401875.translation: Lucius Aedius Optatus, freedman of Lucius, Augustalis, quaestor of the Augustales at Allifae, for himself and Methe, freedwoman, and his family, made this while alive.
EDCS-12401875.summary: This is a dedication by Lucius Aedius Optatus, an Augustalis and quaestor of the Augustales at Allifae, made during his lifetime for himself, his freedwoman, and his family, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12401876.translation: Marcus Aedius Pylades, freedman of Servilia, was honored by decree of the decurions as magistrate of Juno's sacred rites, quaestor of the Augustales at Allifae. He ordered by his will that 20,000 sesterces be spent, under the supervision of Aedia Irene, his mother, to establish this monument for himself and for Marcus Aedius Rhodones, his freedman, father, Augustalis at Allifae.
EDCS-12401876.summary: This is a funerary and building inscription by Marcus Aedius Pylades, who held several magistracies and ordered the construction of a monument for himself and his freedman father with 20,000 sesterces.

EDCS-12401877.translation: To Lucius Flavius Posidonius, freedman of Lucius, Augustalis, and Flavia Eutychia.
EDCS-12401877.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Flavius Posidonius, an Augustalis, and Flavia Eutychia.

EDCS-12401878.translation: Marcus Pontius Eleuther, freedman of Marcus, Augustalis at Allifae and quaestor of the Augustales, made this for himself and his family. Flavia Erotine, freedwoman of Lucius, and Marcus Pontius Princeps, freedman of Marcus, his brother. Pontia Severa, daughter of Marcus, lived 17 years.
EDCS-12401878.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Marcus Pontius Eleuther, an Augustalis and quaestor at Allifae, for himself and his family, including his brother and his sister Pontia Severa who died at 17.

EDCS-12401879.translation: Caius Purellius Papia, Augustalis and quaestor of the Augustales at Allifae, for himself, and for Caius Atilius Natalis, Augustalis and quaestor of the Augustales at Allifae, and for his concubine Atilia Iarina, and for his friend Caius Atilius Quartio, this monument shall not follow the heir.
EDCS-12401879.summary: This is an funerary monument inscription by Caius Purellius Papia, an Augustalis and quaestor at Allifae, for himself and several other individuals, stipulating that the monument is not to be inherited.

EDCS-12401881.translation: To Marcus Viccius Demetrius, freedman of Marcus, a physician, by his will.
EDCS-12401881.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Marcus Viccius Demetrius, a physician and freedman, established by his will.

EDCS-12401882.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Agilis, son of Marcus Aedius, lived 16 years, 5 months, and 8 days. Agilis, his father, made this for his deserving son.
EDCS-12401882.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Agilis for his son, Marcus Aedius Agili, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-12401884.translation: To Caius Onesimus, freedman of Caius, and to Ofilia Iphigenia, freedwoman of Lucius. Montanus, a freedman, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-12401884.summary: This is a funerary monument erected at his own expense by Montanus, a freedman, for Caius Onesimus and Ofilia Iphigenia.

EDCS-12401885.translation: For Caius Apscillaniusหลังจาก Heron, freedman of Caius, and Petronia Italia, freedwoman of Marcus, and Caius Apscillanius Rufus, freedman of Caius, and Caius Apscillanius Suavis, freedman of Caius, and Apscillana Secunda, freedwoman of Caius. 'The living shall not follow the heir for themselves and for those for whom they made the inscription.'
EDCS-12401885.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription for multiple individuals from the Apscillani family, stipulating that the monument shall not be inherited.

EDCS-12401886.translation: Caius Arrius, son of Numerius, from the Teretina tribe, by his will.
EDCS-12401886.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Caius Arrius according to his will.

EDCS-12401887.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to Caius Strato, a freedman.

EDCS-12401888.translation: Gaius Asellius Rufus and his wife, Munnia, daughter of Quintus, and her husband, Lucius Marius Bassus, and his wife, Valeria Donata, made this for themselves and their family.
EDCS-12401888.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by several individuals for themselves and their families.

EDCS-12401889.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that begins with the name Gaius Attius.

EDCS-12401890.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that begins with the name Marcus Attius.

EDCS-12401892.translation: To the divine shades. Gaius Avilius Anteros made this for his well-deserving mother, Avilia Nice[ni].
EDCS-12401892.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a son to his mother.

EDCS-12401893.translation: This monument or tomb belongs to Avillia Suavilla and Marcus Vinnius Cupidus, for themselves and C[---] and Vinnia Polytucha; the heir shall not follow.
EDCS-12401893.summary: This is a funerary monument belonging to Avillia Suavilla and Marcus Vinnius Cupidus, with a clause about heirs.

EDCS-12401894.translation: For Lucius Badis, son of Marcus, [and] Percennia, daughter of Gaius, 25 feet in the field, 25 feet in the front.
EDCS-12401894.summary: This inscription seems to record land dimensions, possibly for a funerary plot.

EDCS-12401895.translation: For Sextus Cassius Stelletina, son of Publius, father; for Fulvia, daughter of Gaius, mother; for Lucius Cassius Stelletina Longinus, brother, son of Sextus; for Caecilia Charini, freedwoman of Quintus, wife; Sextus Cassius Stelletina Macer, son of Sextus, made [this] by testament.
EDCS-12401895.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating parents, a brother, and a wife, erected by a son according to his will.

EDCS-12401896.translation: Lucius Modius Gaurus to his well-deserving wife, Cornelia Sotheris, with whom he lived for 30 years.
EDCS-12401896.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a wife, indicating the length of her marriage.

EDCS-12401897.translation: Publius Curiatius Tertius, freedman of the woman, rests here honestly, weary from labor. For his brother, Publius Curiatius Balbus, freedman of the woman. For their mother, Clovatia, freedwoman of the woman. Teia Attice, freedwoman of the woman, made this for herself and her family.
EDCS-12401897.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a freedwoman for herself, her family, and two other freedmen, one of whom is her brother.

EDCS-12401898.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Adaugendus made this for his well-deserving wife, Domitia Ampliata, with whom he lived for 18 years.
EDCS-12401898.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a wife, indicating the length of her marriage.

EDCS-12401899.translation: Menolaus, freedman of Lucius Egnatius Amphion, freedman of Lucius, made this.
EDCS-12401899.summary: This is a funerary epigram for Menolaus, a freedman, made by himself and indicating his patron.

EDCS-12401900.translation: To the departed spirits. For Egnatia Parata, who lived 88 years, 3 months, 12 days. Claudia Prisca, daughter of Titus, for her most devoted mother.
EDCS-12401900.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Egnatia Parata, specifying her age at death and set up by her daughter.

EDCS-12401901.translation: Fabia Leuca, freedwoman of Marcus, for herself and for Lucius Aurunculeius Faustus, Fabia Venusta, freedwoman of a woman, and Fabia Prima, freedwoman of a woman, by will.
EDCS-12401901.summary: This funerary inscription records a self-dedication and dedications to others, to be enacted by will.

EDCS-12401902.translation: For Gaius Fadius Auctus... Claudia Fadilla, daughter of Titus, most noble lady... for her parent...
EDCS-12401902.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Fadius Auctus, set up by Claudia Fadilla.

EDCS-12401903.translation: To the departed spirits. For Caius Fadius Falernus, who lived two years, 5 months, 20 days. Fadia Stephania, his most devoted mother.
EDCS-12401903.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Caius Fadius Falernus, specifying his very young age at death and set up by his mother.

EDCS-12401904.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Fadius Successus. Fadia Felicitas made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-12401904.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Fadius Successus, set up by his wife.

EDCS-12401905.translation: To the departed spirits. For Fabia Calliope, freedwoman of Caius, his excellent wife. Caius Fadius Hermeros made this.
EDCS-12401905.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Fabia Calliope, set up by her husband.

EDCS-12401906.translation: Of Marcus Fudius, freedman of a woman, Chrysinus.
EDCS-12401906.summary: This inscription likely records the name of a freedman of Marcus Fudius.

EDCS-12401907.translation: To Publius Fuficius Pollio, son of Publius, my son, and to Gnaeus Cluvius, son of Caius, my husband. Ahernia Rufa, daughter of Caius, made this monument, which the heir will not inherit.
EDCS-12401907.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates a son and husband, set up by Ahernia Rufa, with a clause regarding inheritance.

EDCS-12401908.translation: ...nio by the arbitration of Manllia, his wife, Manllia, daughter of Lucius... Marcus Grottonius Marcius, son of Spurius.
EDCS-12401908.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely related to a legal settlement or testament, mentioning Marcus Grottonius Marcius and his wife Manllia.

EDCS-12401909.translation: Hilara lived 25 years; Prima, her sister, lived 35 years.
EDCS-12401909.summary: This is a funerary epigram recording the ages at death of two sisters.

EDCS-12401910.translation: Iucundus, freedman of Piladis, son of Quartas, lived 20 years. He lies here.
EDCS-12401910.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iucundus, a freedman, recording his age and burial place.

EDCS-12401912.translation: To Titus Julius Dexter, his most sweet son, a most dutiful youth, who lived [3] years and 5 months. Iulia Tatiana, his mother, made this monument.
EDCS-12401912.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a mother for her deceased son, recording his age at death.

EDCS-12401913.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Lucilia Marcellina, daughter of Marcus. Heria Marcellina, [her] most excellent daughter.
EDCS-12401913.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucilia Marcellina by her mother.

EDCS-12401914.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Marcia Henioche by Statilius.

EDCS-12401915.translation: Marcus Minatius Hilarus, freedman of a woman, for himself and Horme, a freedwoman, and his [relatives]. In the field, 10 feet [deep], 12 feet in front.
EDCS-12401915.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Minatius Hilarus, his freedwoman Horme, and others, specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-12401916.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, with partially legible names.

EDCS-12401917.translation: [---], freedman of Nicephor. [---] by will. [---], freedwoman of Caius, Aristia.
EDCS-12401917.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary dedication, mentioning freedpersons and a will.

EDCS-12401918.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Marcus Octavius Proculus, who lived 35 years. Avillia Procula, [his] most dutiful mother [dedicated this].
EDCS-12401918.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mother for her son, recording his age at death.

EDCS-12401919.translation: To Quintus Oppidius Faustus, freedman of a woman. To Quintus Oppidius Probus, freedman of a woman, who lived 20 years. Oppidia Specula, freedwoman of a woman, lived 21 years. Oppidia Phylacium, freedwoman of a woman, made [this] for herself and her relatives.
EDCS-12401919.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Oppidia Specula and Oppidia Phylacium for themselves and their relatives, recording the ages at death of Faustus, Probus, and Specula.

EDCS-12401920.translation: Orbia Gnome, freedwoman of Lucius, and her children erected this for Lucius Orbius Antiochus, freedman of Lucius, their patron, and for Marcus Statilius Chilo, son of Marcus, and for Hilara, freedwoman of Orbia (a woman), and for Lucius Orbius Scymnus, freedman of Orbia (a woman); for themselves and their family.
EDCS-12401920.summary: This funerary inscription records a family's erection of a monument for themselves and their patrons.

EDCS-12401921.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the name of Lucius Pacilius Labeo, possibly on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-12401922.translation: To Plotia, daughter of Publius, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12401922.summary: This inscription records a public dedication to Plotia, daughter of Publius, authorized by the town council.

EDCS-12401923.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Her son made this for his most beloved mother, Ianuaria Pompeia, well-deserving.
EDCS-12401923.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication from a son to his well-deserving mother, Ianuaria Pompeia.

EDCS-12401924.translation: [...] Salluvius, freedman, by order of Salluvia Postuma, his patroness.
EDCS-12401924.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a commission by a patroness, Salluvia Postuma, to her freedman.

EDCS-12401926.translation: To Quintus Saufeius, son of Publius, of the Stellatina tribe.
EDCS-12401926.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Quintus Saufeius, son of Publius.

EDCS-12401927.translation: To Statius, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, Capit[o], and to Nicini, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-12401927.summary: This inscription records names and tribal affiliations, likely for a funerary monument, mentioning Statius Capito and Clodia Nicini.

EDCS-12401928.translation: The bones of Suetia Hilara, freedwoman of Gnaeus, are buried here.
EDCS-12401928.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription indicating the burial place of Suetia Hilara.

EDCS-12401929.translation: Lucius Sulpicius Pollio, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe; Lucius Sulpicius, his father; and Epistolionis, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-12401929.summary: This inscription records the names of three individuals, likely family members, including Lucius Sulpicius Pollio and his father.

EDCS-12401930.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that appears to record a dedication by Verecundus to his spouse.

EDCS-12401931.translation: To the departed spirits. To Titus Valvennius Faustinus, who lived 35 years. Valvennia Felicula, mother, and Forviria Tertulla, wife.
EDCS-12401931.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Valvennius Faustinus, erected by his mother and wife.

EDCS-12401932.translation: To Gaius Vesonius Balbus, son of Gaius Vesonius Balbus, of the Teretina tribe, to Mulleia Galla, daughter of Gaius, wife of Gaius Vesonius Balbus.
EDCS-12401932.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication from Gaius Vesonius Balbus to his son and wife.

EDCS-12401933.translation: Lucius Vettius, son of Gaius, [---] Bumbria, daughter of [---], Ruf[---]
EDCS-12401933.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph for Lucius Vettius and possibly Bumbria.

EDCS-12401934.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Vistuleia Lupula, daughter of Numerius. Marcus Servilius Aprilis.
EDCS-12401934.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vistuleia Lupula, dedicated by Marcus Servilius Aprilis.

EDCS-12401935.translation: To Vitalis, freedman of Aedia, who lived 24 years.
EDCS-12401935.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vitalis, a freedman, recording his age at death.

EDCS-12401940.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Cladus.
EDCS-12401940.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph dedicated to someone named Cladus.

EDCS-12401945.translation: [---] Herius, son of Numerius?, [---] Minatia [---] Herius, son of Gaius? [---]
EDCS-12401945.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record at least two individuals, possibly Herius and Minatia.

EDCS-12401947.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription that seems to record familial relationships, including a son and a wife.

EDCS-12600274.translation: To the departed spirits. Of Marcus Licinius.
EDCS-12600274.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Licinius.

EDCS-12700114.text_edition: \n Lucio Publilio Marci filio Probato clarissimo viro consuli curatori rei publicae municipi et patrono curatori Beneventanorum legato provinciae Africae per Numidiam cur(atori) Lunensium et Volaterranorum praetori kandidato lecto inter tribunicios IIIIviro viarum curandarum pontifici ordo populusque Nolanus\n
EDCS-12700114.translation: The council and people of Nola to Lucius Publilius Probatus, son of Marcus, a most distinguished man, consul, curator of the republic and patron of the town, curator of the province of Africa through Numidia, curator of Lunensium and Volaterranorum, praetor candidate, chosen among the tribunes, a commissioner for roads, a pontiff.
EDCS-12700114.summary: This is a public dedication by the city of Nola honoring Lucius Publilius Probatus for his many distinguished civic and administrative roles.

EDCS-12700126.text_edition: \n Marcus Vinicius Publi filius Marci nepos Publi pronepos consul II VIIvir epulonum sodalis Augustalis thriumphalibus sic ornamentis quinqies viam ab angiporto aedis Iunonis Lucinae usque ad aedem Matutae et clivom ab ianu ad gisiarios portae Stellatinae et viam patulam ad portam laevam et ab foro ad portam domesticam sua pecunia stravit\n
EDCS-12700126.translation: Marcus Vinicius, son of Publius, grandson of Marcus, great-grandson of Publius, twice consul, member of the board of seven for banquets, associate of Augustus, paved with his own money the road from the lane near the temple of Juno Lucina to the temple of Matuta, and the slope from the gate to the fish market of the Porta Stellatina, and the wide road to the Porta Laeva, and from the forum to the Porta Domestica.
EDCS-12700126.summary: This inscription records public works funded by Marcus Vinicius, a prominent Roman official and member of an Augustan college.

EDCS-12700128.text_edition: \n Lucio Flavio Bitho veterano exscribae classis praetoriae Misenensis natione Bessus sic vixit nnos LXII Flavia Potamilla et Flavius Bithus patri fecerunt\n
EDCS-12700128.translation: To Lucius Flavius Bithus, veteran scribe of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, a Bessian by birth, who lived 62 years. Flavia Potamilla and Flavius Bithus made this for their father.
EDCS-12700128.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Flavius Bithus, a veteran scribe of the Misenum fleet, set up by his wife and son.

EDCS-12700129.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Herennio Valenti manipulario III Tauro natione Alexandrinus vixit nnos XXXVI militavit nnos XVI s Valens heres? bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-12700129.translation: To the departed spirits. Valens, the heir (?), made this for Herennius Valens, a soldier of the 3rd Cohort Taurinum, an Alexandrian by birth, who lived 36 years and served 16 years.
EDCS-12700129.summary: This is a tombstone for Herennius Valens, a soldier who served for 16 years, erected by his heir.

EDCS-12700130.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Iuli Decorati manipularii classis praetoriae Misenensis natione Retus militavit annos XIII vixit annos XXX Caius Annius Sabinianus Marcus Trebius Sossianus heredes bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-12700130.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Annius Sabinianus and Marcus Trebius Sossianus, heirs, made this for Caius Julius Decoratus, a soldier of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, a Retus by birth, who served 13 years and lived 30 years, to one who deserved well.
EDCS-12700130.summary: This tombstone commemorates Caius Julius Decoratus, a soldier of the praetorian fleet, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-12700139.text_edition: \n ------ Ma]cri matri eius servos sic publicus ut appareat decretum est et ius sedendi ut habeat in meminerunt item locum lecticae in amphitheatro VC decerni Caio Cupiennio Satrio Marciano ad statuas Tiberi Caesaris Augusti et Iuliae Augustae dedicandas immolationes faciant hostiis maioribus ex pecunia publica praetextas habeant et servos sic publicus is appareat circinsibus sic pulvinaribus et ceteris diebus festis statua eius ut ponatur ludis omnibus in theatro et pompis et circinsibus sic cum corona laurea interesse liceat liberis posterisque eius virile secus decerni Augustalibus ius sedendi ut habeant loco contra munerarium proxume iunco ae Macri matri eius servos sic publicus ut appareat prout omnibus magistris decretum est et ius sedendi ut habeat\n
EDCS-12700139.translation: [To Ma]cria, his mother, that slaves be public, as it appears, it has been decreed, and that she should have the right to sit, and that a place for a litter be awarded to her in the amphitheater, that Caio Cupiennio Satrio Marciano, for dedicating statues to Tiberius Caesar Augustus and Julia Augusta, should make sacrifices with larger victims from public funds, that they should wear bordered togas and that public slaves appear around the circus seats, and on other festival days that his statue be placed in all games in the theater, and processions, and circus games, and that he be allowed to attend with a laurel wreath, and that his children and descendants, of the male sex, be granted the right to sit among the Augustales, and the right to sit in a place opposite the nearest munerarius to the reed. To Macri, his mother, that slaves be public, as it appears, as it has been decreed for all magistrates, and that she should have the right to sit.
EDCS-12700139.summary: This inscription details honors decreed for Caio Cupiennio Satrio Marciano and his mother Macria, including public slaves, seating rights, and statue dedications, funded by public money.

EDCS-12700149.text_edition: \n Caesari Augusto sacrum Caius Sallustius Epagathic ob honorem Augustalitatis locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-12700149.translation: Sacred to Caesar Augustus. By decree of the decurions, the place was given to Caius Sallustius Epagathic in honor of his holding the office of Augustalis.
EDCS-12700149.summary: This inscription records the granting of a place to Caius Sallustius Epagathic, likely in a public venue, for his honor in holding the office of Augustalis.

EDCS-12700150.text_edition: \n Caius Pomponius Cai libertus Tigranus viam plostralem fecit de sua pecunia HS nummum |(mille) Dquingentis sene sic aiutore sic vicanis\n
EDCS-12700150.translation: Caius Pomponius Tigranus, freedman of Caius, built the cart road at his own expense for 1500 sesterces, with the help of the villagers.
EDCS-12700150.summary: This record states that Caius Pomponius Tigranus funded the construction of a road for carts at his own expense.

EDCS-12700157.text_edition: \n Ulpius Maximinus protector CE militavit annnis XXV menses S VI VI <p=R>ili posterioris Aurelia Martana coniunx sic et Iunius Maximus filius eiusdem bene merenti fecerunt \n
EDCS-12700157.translation: Ulpius Maximinus, a protector, served 25 years and 6 months. Aurelia Martana, his wife, and Iunius Maximus, his son, made this for one who deserved well.
EDCS-12700157.summary: This is a tombstone for Ulpius Maximinus, a soldier identified as a 'protector', erected by his wife and son.

EDCS-12700175.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus Lucio Antonio Ionico sodali iuvenum collegii Martis Salutaris et quinquennali collegii lotorum Nemorensium quinquennali Cornelia Thallusa coniugi suo bene merenti fecit et sibi cum quo vixit annos XXX\n
EDCS-12700175.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelia Thallusa made this for her well-deserving husband, Lucius Antonius Ionikus, associate of the youth college of Mars Ultor and quinquennial of the college of the Nemorensian lot-holders, and for herself, with whom he lived 30 years.
EDCS-12700175.summary: This tombstone commemorates Lucius Antonius Ionikus, a member of several colleges, and was erected by his wife Cornelia Thallusa.

EDCS-12700190.text_edition: \n Quintus Baebius Quinti filius Caius Birrius Cai filius duoviri redegerunt Isi Serapi sacrum\n
EDCS-12700190.translation: The duoviri, Quintus Baebius, son of Quintus, and Caius Birrius, son of Caius, established this sacred precinct for Isis and Serapis.
EDCS-12700190.summary: This inscription records the establishment of a sacred precinct for Isis and Serapis by two duoviri.

EDCS-12800141.text_edition: \n or domo Ursinus domo principis prioris Mercator domo Terebento sic Felicissimus domo Terebento sic Aur Casinas domo Casino Secun Valentinus domo Siscia Glauc Marcellinus domo Siscia centuria L s domo Sabaria candidatus Tur domo Vulceis donis donatus Arr domo Vulceis candidatus Aur Chers Erm genes Au\n
EDCS-12800141.translation: From the household of Ursinus, from the household of the former prince Mercator, from the household of Terebentus, so Felicissimus, from the household of Terebentus, so Casinas from Aur, from the household of Cassius, so Secundus, from the household of Siscia, Glaucus, from the household of Siscia, Marcellinus, from the household of Siscia, centuria L S, from the household of Sabaria, candidatus of the Tur [family], from the household of Vulceis, having been honored with gifts, Arr, from the household of Vulceis, candidatus of Aur, Chers, Erm, Genes, Au.
EDCS-12800141.summary: This inscription lists names of individuals, possibly soldiers or members of different households, who may have received honors or gifts, originating from various locations and possibly associated with military units.

EDCS-12800221.text_edition: \n Caio Paccio Cnaei filio Poblilia Caio Paccio Cai filio Pedoni ex t[estamento ------\n
EDCS-12800221.translation: To Gaius Paccius, son of Gnaeus, of the Poblilia tribe; to Gaius Paccius Pedon, son of Gaius, by testament [---].
EDCS-12800221.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates a monument to Gaius Paccius and his son Gaius Paccius Pedon, with the latter's dedication made according to a will.

EDCS-12800222.text_edition: \n Ex testamento Aburenae Quartae sacra reddita\n
EDCS-12800222.translation: From the testament of Aburena Quarta, sacred rites were restored.
EDCS-12800222.summary: This inscription records that sacred rites were restored according to the will of Aburena Quarta.

EDCS-12800223.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription commemorating Lucius Albius, who held the position of VIvir Augustalis.

EDCS-12800295.text_edition: \n Lustratio VI Kalendas Iulias dedicata Kalendis Ianuaris Tiberio Claudio Caisare Augusto V Servio Cornelio Orfito consulibus\n
EDCS-12800295.translation: Purification on the 6th day before the Kalends of July, dedicated on the Kalends of January, when Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus and Servius Cornelius Orfitus were consuls.
EDCS-12800295.summary: This inscription records the date of a purification ceremony and the date of dedication, mentioning the consuls Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus and Servius Cornelius Orfitus, likely dating it to 45 CE.

EDCS-12800306.text_edition: \n Hospes paullisper morare si non est molestum et quid evites cognosce amicum hunc quem speraveram mi esse ab eo mihi accusato res subiecti et iudicia instaurata deis gratias ago et meae innocentiae omni molestia liberatus sum qui nostrum mentitur eum nec di penates nec inferi recipiant\n
EDCS-12800306.translation: Stranger, linger a little, if it is not troublesome, and learn what you avoid: this friend whom I had hoped would be mine, accused me, but the matters and trials have been reinstated. I give thanks to the gods and am freed from all trouble by my innocence. May neither the household gods nor the gods below receive whoever lies among us.
EDCS-12800306.summary: This is a personal inscription found on a tomb, expressing relief from false accusations and praising the speaker's innocence, with a curse against liars.

EDCS-12800355.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Volumniae Tryphaenae hic condita est fecerunt liberti patronae bene merenti\n
EDCS-12800355.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Volumnia Tryphaena is buried here. Her freedmen erected this for their well-deserving patroness.
EDCS-12800355.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the memory of Volumnia Tryphaena by her freedmen.

EDCS-12800359.text_edition: \n Lucius Upellius Mami sic filius Menenia\n
EDCS-12800359.translation: To Lucius Upellius, son of Mamius, of the Menenia tribe.
EDCS-12800359.summary: This is a brief dedication to Lucius Upellius, son of Mamius, noting his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-12800363.text_edition: \n Lucio Caesonio Ovinio Manlio Rufiniano Basso clarissimo viro consuli II pontifici maiori pontifici dei Solis salio Palatino praefecto urbis comiti Auggustorum iudici sacrarum cognitionum vice Caesaris sine appellationem sic cognoscendi inter fiscum et privatis sic item inter privatos Roma et in provincia Africa electo a divo Probo ad praesidendum iudicio magno proconsuli provinciae Africae tertium curatori coloniae Carthaginiensium legato provinciae Africae Carthaginiensis curatori albei sic Tiberis et cluacarum sic sacrae urbis curatori rei publicae Veneventanorum sic praetori candidato quaestori candidato seviro turmae deducendae triumviro kapitali patrono praestantissimo Caesonius Achilleus libertus posuit\n
EDCS-12800363.translation: To Lucius Caesennius Ovinus Manlius Rufinianus Bassus, most illustrious man, consul for the second time, chief priest, priest of Sol Invictus, Salian Palatine, prefect of the city, count of the Augustan majesties, judge of sacred litigations in place of Caesar, to judge without appeal between the fisc and private individuals, and likewise between private individuals, elected by the deified Probus to preside over the great court, proconsul of the province of Africa for the third time, curator of the colony of Carthage, legate of the province of Africa, curator of the Tiber and its sewers, curator of the sacred city, curator of the republic of Benevento, praetor designate, quaestor designate, Sevirus of the troop to be led forth, triumvir capitalis, most excellent patron. His freedman Caesennius Achilleus erected this.
EDCS-12800363.summary: This is a lengthy honorary inscription dedicated to Lucius Caesennius Ovinus Manlius Rufinianus Bassus, listing his numerous high-ranking imperial and civic offices, and was erected by his freedman Caesennius Achilleus.

EDCS-13100052.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Publius Aelius Zmaragdus.
EDCS-13100052.summary: This is a short funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Aelius Zmaragdus.

EDCS-13100053.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aeliae Clementinae quae vixit a menses VIIII dies fecit Clemens Augusti libertus pater\n
EDCS-13100053.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelia Clementina, who lived 9 months and [---] days, was made by Clemens, freedman of Augustus, her father.
EDCS-13100053.summary: A funerary dedication for Aelia Clementina, made by her father, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100054.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Prisca made this for her dearest sister, Aelia Aburnia, her well-deserving brother.
EDCS-13100054.summary: A funerary dedication for Aelia Aburnia, made by her sister Aelia Prisca, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100055.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Aemilius Donatus and Aemilia Maria made this for their most dutiful father, Lucius Aemilius Donatus.
EDCS-13100055.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Aemilius Donatus, made by his son and daughter-in-law, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100056.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Albia Urbica made this for her dearest stepson, Marcus Octavius Aerius, who lived 10 years, 7 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-13100056.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Octavius Aerius, made by his stepmother Albia Urbica, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100057.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Sextilius Pannychianus made this for his most beloved wives, Alfia Procilla, daughter of Marcus, and Claudia Hygia, and for himself and the descendants of his family.
EDCS-13100057.summary: A funerary dedication for two wives, made by Publius Sextilius Pannychianus for them, himself, and his descendants, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100058.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Allius Successus made this for his well-deserving wife Cosconia Thallus.
EDCS-13100058.summary: A funerary dedication for Cosconia Thallus, made by her husband Allius Successus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100059.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His household slaves arranged for this monument to be placed for Caius Annaeus Atticus Pictus, deceased, from the province of Aquitania, aged 37.
EDCS-13100059.summary: A funerary dedication for Caius Annaeus Atticus Pictus, arranged by his household slaves, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100060.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Rustius Sympheros made this for his well-deserving wife, Annia Phegias.
EDCS-13100060.summary: A funerary dedication for Annia Phegias, made by her husband Publius Rustius Sympheros, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100061.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Antonius Agathias aediculam puram ex sepulchro Marci Coccei Daphni cuius heres est facta divisione inter se et coheredes suos adiecto de suo pariete medio et ostio libero facto fecit sibi et libertis libertabusque posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-13100061.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antonius Agathias made this structure, taken from the tomb of Marcus Cocceius Daphnus, whose heir he became through division among himself and his co-heirs, with the addition of his own wall and a free entrance, for himself and his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-13100061.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Antonius Agathias and his dependents, involving the acquisition and modification of an existing tomb, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100062.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antonius Pius made this for Aemilia Maiorica and Cominius Silvanus, with permission for entry into the right part, for his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-13100062.summary: A funerary dedication for Aemilia Maiorica and Cominius Silvanus, and for the freedmen and descendants of Marcus Antonius Pius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100063.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Antonius Plocamus, Marcus Antonius Neophytianus, Marcus Antonius Crispinus, Iulia Epictesis, Ostoria Fortunata, and their patron Marcus Antonius Neophytus, the best. Antonia Pithusa made this for herself, her freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-13100063.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Antonia Pithusa for a group of people, likely family members and a patron, to the departed spirits.

EDCS-13100064.inscription_type: leges, tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-13100064.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antonius Vitalis and Marcus Antonius Verus, son of Marcus, made this for themselves and their family, their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. If anyone after the death of Marcus Antonius Vitalis wishes to sell or donate or alienate in any other way this monument or anything within its enclosure, or if anyone brings the body or bones of a non-Roman into this monument under the name inscribed above, then for each body, a penalty of 3,000 sesterces shall be paid to the worshippers of the Lares of Portus Augustus; this monument shall not pass to an external heir.
EDCS-13100064.summary: This is a funerary inscription that sets strict conditions for the sale or use of the monument and its enclosure, including a penalty and restriction on external heirs, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100065.translation: To the spirits of the departed for Antonia Achaica.
EDCS-13100065.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication for Antonia Achaica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100066.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Nice made this for herself, her husband Aulus Plotus Primus, her son Plotus Secundinus, and their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-13100066.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Antonia Nice for herself, her husband, son, and their descendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100067.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [Antonia] made this for herself, her husband, and her son Secundinus, and their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-13100067.summary: This is a funerary dedication with some missing names, made by Antonia for herself, her husband, son, and their descendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100068.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antonius Vitalis Capito and Ianuarius, his most dutiful sons, made this for their most excellent mother, Antonia Tyche.
EDCS-13100068.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by two sons, Marcus Antonius Vitalis Capito and Ianuarius, for their mother Antonia Tyche, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13100069.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Appius Saturninus, son of Quintus, made this for his well-deserving wife Annia Donata, for their children, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. This monument shall not pass to an heir, nor shall those to whom they leave it have the right to sell or donate it. Frontage 10 feet, depth 12 feet.
EDCS-13100069.summary: This is a funerary dedication that specifies the dimensions of the plot and restricts future alienation of the property, set up by Quintus Appius Saturninus for his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13300623.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Numerius Ogulnius Hermes made this for his dearly beloved wife Ogulnia Proba and for himself.
EDCS-13300623.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Numerius Ogulnius Hermes for his wife and himself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13302923.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antonia Iuliana for her husband Antonius Logismus.
EDCS-13302923.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Antonia Iuliana for her husband Antonius Logismus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13400136.text_edition: \n Marco Iulio Marci filio Aemilia Maximo primo pilo legionis VI victricis praefecto castrorum legionis XXII Primigeniae piae fidelis donis donato a divo Traiano ob bellum Armeniacum et Parthicum auguri nni soli patrono coloniae ordo decurionum sua pecunia posuit\n
EDCS-13400136.translation: Marcus Julius Maximus, son of Marcus, former centurion of the VI Victrix legion, prefect of the camp of the XXII Primigenia Pia Fidelis legion, honored with gifts by the deified Trajan for the war in Armenia and Parthia, and sole augur, patron of the colony; the town council erected this at its own expense.
EDCS-13400136.summary: This is a public monument erected by the town council to honor Marcus Julius Maximus, a distinguished military officer and priest, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13400137.text_edition: \n Aulo Scantio Auli filio Aelio Larciano procuratori provinciae Mauretaniae Tingitanae procuratori provinciae Delmatiae procuratori XX hereditatium Romae tribuno cohortis IIII praetoriae X urbanae IV vigilum primo pilo bis patrono coloniae ordo Augustalium locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-13400137.translation: To Aulus Scantius Auli filius Aelius Larsianus, procurator of the province of Mauretania Tingitana, procurator of the province of Dalmatia, procurator of the inheritance tax (XX hereditatium) in Rome, tribune of the IV Praetorian cohort, X of the Urban cohort, IV of the Vigiles, twice chief centurion, patron of the colony; a place was granted to him by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13400137.summary: This inscription records a grant of public space to Aulus Scantius Auli filius Aelius Larsianus, a high-ranking imperial official, by the town council.

EDCS-13400189.text_edition: \n Ager publicus colonorum coloniae Ostiensis legatus testamento Decimi Noni Grati in fronte pedes DCXV in agro pedes \n
EDCS-13400189.translation: Public land of the colonists of the colony of Ostia, according to the will of Decimus Nonius Gratus, 615 feet in front and in the territory [of length unspecified].
EDCS-13400189.summary: This inscription defines the boundaries of public land belonging to the colony of Ostia, as stipulated in the will of Decimus Nonius Gratus.

EDCS-13500001.translation: To the divine shades. Lucius Cornelius Ianvarius to his wife, Pomponia Fortunata.
EDCS-13500001.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Lucius Cornelius Ianvarius to his wife, Pomponia Fortunata.

EDCS-13500002.translation: To the divine shades. Gaius Pomponius [---] made for himself and his [---] daughter, daughter of Gaius, and Pomponia [---]. She lived 10 years [---] he placed [---].
EDCS-13500002.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication from Gaius Pomponius for himself, his daughter, and possibly Pomponia, specifying a lifespan of 10 years for one of the deceased.

EDCS-13500003.translation: To the divine shades. Pontia Dativa is placed here, this chest, or two sarcophagi, Caius Terentius Lucipher donated to her husband, Lucius Tonneius Caelestinus.
EDCS-13500003.summary: This inscription records the donation of a tomb (chest or two sarcophagi) by Caius Terentius Lucipher for Pontia Dativa and her husband Lucius Tonneius Caelestinus.

EDCS-13500004.translation: To the divine shades. Fabia Tertullina made this for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Popilius Phoebus, who lived 63 years, 6 months, and 3 days.
EDCS-13500004.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Fabia Tertullina to her husband, Marcus Popilius Phoebus, specifying his age at death.

EDCS-13500005.translation: To the divine shades. Gaius Praecilius made this.
EDCS-13500005.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication by Gaius Praecilius, likely for himself or a family member.

EDCS-13500007.translation: To the divine shades. Marcus Pullaenius Salvius made this for himself and Pullaenia Zmyrna, his patroness who deserved well of him, and also for his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. My co-freedmen will not have access into this monument, unless the one whom my patroness has manumitted after the monument is completed. The place was granted by Pomponia Rufina, who has the rights of three children.
EDCS-13500007.summary: This funerary inscription by Marcus Pullaenius Salvius for himself, his patroness, and his freedpersons includes specific restrictions on who can be buried in the monument, granted by Pomponia Rufina.

EDCS-13500008.text_edition: \n Restutus Piscinensis et Prima Restuta Primae Florentiae filiae carissimae fecerunt qui sic ab Orfeu maritu sic in Tiberi decepta est December cognatus posuit quae vixit nnos XVIS\n
EDCS-13500008.translation: Restutus Piscinensis and Prima Restuta made this for their most beloved daughter, Prima Florentia, who was deceived by Orfeus her husband and by the Tiber. December, a relative, placed this. She lived 16 years.
EDCS-13500008.summary: This inscription records the death of Prima Florentia, who lived 16 years, and was apparently deceived and drowned in the Tiber, with a relative placing the memorial.

EDCS-13500010.translation: To the divine shades. Malchion made this for Rutilia Psycheni.
EDCS-13500010.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Malchion to Rutilia Psycheni.

EDCS-13500011.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Sabinus Taurius, his most dutiful son. He made this.
EDCS-13500011.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a parent to their devoted son, Sabinus Taurius.

EDCS-13500012.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Sabina, who lived 6 years, 9 months, and 20 days. Aelia Salviane, her home-born slave, made this for her deserving slave.
EDCS-13500012.summary: A funerary monument erected by Aelia Salviane for her home-born slave, Sabina, who died at a young age.

EDCS-13500014.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Sallustia Romana. Lucius Sallustius Ianuarius made this for his most dutiful sister, who lived 21 years.
EDCS-13500014.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sallustia Romana, erected by her brother Lucius Sallustius Ianuarius.

EDCS-13500015.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Satyra made this. Iason, her father, who lived 8 months and 8 days and 3 hours.
EDCS-13500015.summary: A funerary monument for Satyra, erected by her father Iason, recording her short lifespan.

EDCS-13500016.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Scantia Salvina and Marcus Sulpicius Fortunatus made this for their most dutiful daughter, Scantiae Sabina, and for themselves, their children, freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants; this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-13500016.summary: A funerary monument for Scantiae Sabina, erected by her parents, with provisions for family and descendants, and a clause preventing it from passing to an outside heir.

EDCS-13500017.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Scribonius Priscianus and Sulpicia Strattonice made this for Donata, their sweetest home-born slave, who lived 8 years, 11 months, and 21 days, for her deserving.
EDCS-13500017.summary: A funerary inscription by Scribonius Priscianus and Sulpicia Strattonice for their young, home-born slave Donata.

EDCS-13500018.translation: May malice be absent from this monument. To the spirits of the departed. Scribonia Attice made this for herself and for Marcus Ulpius Amerimnus, her husband, and for Scribonia Callityche, her mother, and for Dioclus and her own family, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, except Panaratus and Prosdocia; this monument will not pass to an outside heir.
EDCS-13500018.summary: A complex funerary monument established by Scribonia Attice for herself and her family, including specific exclusions, with a clause about inheritance.

EDCS-13500019.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Sempronius Fortunatus, who lived for eight months. Lucius Sempronius Quintianus, a centurion of the frumentarii, made this for his sweetest son.
EDCS-13500019.summary: A funerary inscription by Lucius Sempronius Quintianus, a centurion, for his infant son, Lucius Sempronius Fortunatus.

EDCS-13500020.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gnaeus Sentius Saturninus, who lived 17 years and 3 months. Fabricia Urbana made this for her sweetest son.
EDCS-13500020.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gnaeus Sentius Saturninus, who died at the age of 17, erected by his mother Fabricia Urbana.

EDCS-13500021.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Septimia Tyche. Publius Aemilius Tyrannus made this for his dearest wife, and Lecanius Zosimus for his most dutiful sister. And for all their own.
EDCS-13500021.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Publius Aemilius Tyrannus to his wife Septimia Tyche and by Lecanius Zosimus to his sister.

EDCS-13500022.translation: To the departed spirits. Septueia Vera erected this monument for her mother Vibullia Regilla and her father Quintus Septueius Abascantus, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants; this monument will not pass to an heir, nor to an external heir.
EDCS-13500022.summary: A funerary monument erected by Septueia Vera for her parents, with a clause preventing it from passing to an external heir.

EDCS-13500023.translation: To the departed spirits. For Decimus Sergius Felix and Petronia Auxesis, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-13500023.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to freedmen and their descendants.

EDCS-13500025.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Silius Pylades erected this for himself and his family, and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-13500025.summary: A funerary inscription self-dedicated by Caius Silius Pylades for himself, his family, and his freedmen.

EDCS-13500026.translation: To the departed spirits. For Silia Frontida, who lived 8 years, 10 months, and 7 days. Gaius Silius Mercurius and Silia Fortunata erected this for their most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-13500026.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Silia Frontida, who died at a young age, erected by her parents.

EDCS-13500028.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Betilienus Synegdemus erected this for his well-deserving friend Lucius Sittius Crescens.
EDCS-13500028.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Betilienus Synegdemus to his friend Lucius Sittius Crescens.

EDCS-13500029.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Domitius Ismaragdus erected this for his well-deserving student Caius Pomponius Genialis, and for Pomponia Antiochis, and for Marius, a servant of our emperor.
EDCS-13500029.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Domitius Ismaragdus to his student, a freedwoman, and an imperial slave.

EDCS-13500030.translation: To the departed spirits. Hedychrus erected this for his most dutiful daughter Spaei Zotica, who lived 2 years.
EDCS-13500030.summary: A funerary inscription erected by Hedychrus for his young daughter Spaei Zotica.

EDCS-13500032.translation: To the departed spirits. Stedia Thyrannis, placed with her mother and father.
EDCS-13500032.summary: A funerary inscription indicating that Stedia Thyrannis is buried with her parents.

EDCS-13500033.translation: To the departed spirits. For Istenia Saturnina, who lived 4 years, 11 months, and 16 days. The plot was donated by Flavius Parides Amacius.
EDCS-13500033.summary: A funerary inscription for Istenia Saturnina, whose burial plot was donated by Flavius Parides Amacius.

EDCS-13500034.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Suallius Eupor erected this for his most cherished fellow freedwoman Aphrodisia, who deserved well.
EDCS-13500034.summary: A funerary inscription erected by Lucius Suallius Eupor for his fellow freedwoman Aphrodisia.

EDCS-13500035.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Suallia Rhembadi, innocent, lived 2 years, 5 months, 23 days, 8 hours. Her parent Lucius Suallius Lupio [dedicated this].
EDCS-13500035.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Suallia Rhembadi, recording her age and dedication by her parent, Lucius Suallius Lupio.

EDCS-13500036.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Tanonia Marina.
EDCS-13500036.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph dedicated to Tanonia Marina.

EDCS-13500038.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For his well-deserving wife Acte, and for his son Gaius Terentius Felix, and for his natural brother Gaius Terentius Rufus, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. Gaius Terentius Eutych[---] [dedicated this].
EDCS-13500038.summary: This funerary epitaph, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE, records the dedication by Gaius Terentius Eutych[---] to his wife Acte, son Felix, and brother Rufus, along with their descendants.

EDCS-13500039.translation: To the spirits of the departed. The co-freedmen Gaius Terentius Lucifer, along with his co-heirs, dedicated [this] to Gaius Terentius Felix and his wife Ulpia Chrysopolis.
EDCS-13500039.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by co-freedmen, including Gaius Terentius Lucifer, to Gaius Terentius Felix and his wife Ulpia Chrysopolis.

EDCS-13500040.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Terentius Lucifer and Terentiae Kallotyceni and their sons made [this] for themselves; whoever places a body or bones in this sarcophagus shall pay 30,000 sesterces to the treasury of Saturn.
EDCS-13500040.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Terentius Lucifer, Terentiae Kallotyceni, and their sons, which also includes a penal clause of 30,000 sesterces for unauthorized burials.

EDCS-13500041.translation: [Dedicated] to [---]. Gaius Terentius Narcissus made [this] for his well-deserving wife, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-13500041.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication by Gaius Terentius Narcissus to his wife, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-13500043.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sergius Fortunatianus and Mindius Felix made [this] for the well-deserving Titinia Rufina, who lived 19 years, 2 months, 17 days.
EDCS-13500043.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication by Sergius Fortunatianus and Mindius Felix to Titinia Rufina, detailing her age at death.

EDCS-13500044.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Torquatus Novellus Senior, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 76 years, 2 months, 12 days.
EDCS-13500044.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Torquatus Novellus Senior, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, recording his age at death.

EDCS-13500045.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Torquatus Novellus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 23 years, 3 months, 16 days.
EDCS-13500045.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Torquatus Novellus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, recording his age at death.

EDCS-13500046.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Trophimus, slave of our Caesar, and Claudia Tyche for themselves and for their most dutiful daughter Claudia Saturnina, who lived 15 years, 6 months, 13 days, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. The place was purchased from Valeria Trophime for four parts of this monument.
EDCS-13500046.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Trophimus (a freedman of the emperor), Claudia Tyche, their daughter Saturnina, and descendants, with a note about the purchase of burial space from Valeria Trophime.

EDCS-13500047.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia Hygia placed this, donated by Attia Helena, for Marcus Ulpius Cte tus, her most excellent husband, and for herself.
EDCS-13500047.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Ulpius Ctetus, placed by his wife Flavia Hygia.

EDCS-13500048.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Mercurius made this for himself, with an entrance on the left, together with a sarcophagus, a small shrine, and six columbaria; and Munatia Tyche made for herself an entrance on the right, with a sarcophagus and fourteen jars; likewise Hermes, our Emperor's household slave and messenger, made for himself an entrance on the left, a sarcophagus with fourteen jars.
EDCS-13500048.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription detailing provisions for multiple individuals, including a household slave, within a burial complex.

EDCS-13500050.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Paulus made this for himself and his brothers.
EDCS-13500050.summary: A funerary dedication made by Marcus Ulpius Paulus for himself and his brothers.

EDCS-13500051.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Onesimus and Marcus Vipsanius Felix made this for themselves, their relatives, freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-13500051.summary: This funerary inscription records a self-dedicated tomb for Titus Flavius Onesimus, his family, and freedmen, with a correction indicating Marcus Vipsanius Felix as a co-dedicant.

EDCS-13500052.translation: To the departed spirits. The heirs made this for Marcus Ulpius Symphorus and his daughter Ulpia Paulina. The plot was granted.
EDCS-13500052.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Ulpius Symphorus and his daughter Ulpia Paulina, erected by their heirs.

EDCS-13500053.translation: To the departed spirits. Olympus, a slave of Matidia, daughter of Augusta, made this for his Urbica, with whom he lived for 1 year, 8 months, 22 days, and 3 hours; she died at the age of 14 years, 11 months.
EDCS-13500053.summary: A funerary dedication for Urbica, commemorating her relationship with the slave Olympus and detailing her lifespan.

EDCS-13500054.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Valerius Afrodisius made this for himself and for his wife Tettia Helpis, and for his household slave Tettia Helpis, and for his stepson Domitius Demetrius, and for his descendants after my death. Whoever shall place a foreign body or bring bones into this monument shall incur a penalty of 10,000 sesterces payable to the public treasury. This monument shall not be inherited by a foreign heir.
EDCS-13500054.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius Valerius Afrodisius for himself and his family, including strict regulations against unauthorized burials and a penalty for violations.

EDCS-13500055.translation: To the departed spirits. Sergia Ianuaria made this for her most dutiful son Marcus Valerius Fortunatus. For himself and his own. He lived 3 years, 8 months, 25 days. In front 10 feet, in depth 10 feet.
EDCS-13500055.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Fortunatus, erected by his mother, detailing his short lifespan and the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-13500056.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Valerius Fortunatus.
EDCS-13500056.summary: A fragmented funerary dedication for Marcus Valerius Fortunatus.

EDCS-13500057.summary: A partial funerary inscription mentioning a woman named Valeria and her lifespan.

EDCS-13500058.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valeria Tyche was placed and buried here.
EDCS-13500058.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Tyche, dating from the Roman period.

EDCS-13500059.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valeria Tyche made this for her well-deserving husband, and for herself, her family, her freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-13500059.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a monument by Valeria Tyche for her husband, herself, and their descendants.

EDCS-13500060.translation: Publius Varius Ampelus and Varia Ennuchis made this for themselves, for their patroness Varia Servanda, daughter of Publius, and for their freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants. Thus, no sarcophagus is to be brought into this monument; this monument will not follow an external heir. In front: 10.5 feet; in the plot: 33 feet.
EDCS-13500060.summary: This funerary inscription from the Roman period details the construction of a monument by Publius Varius Ampelus and Varia Ennuchis for themselves, their patroness, and their descendants, including specific burial restrictions and dimensions.

EDCS-13500061.translation: Publius Varius Ampelus and Varia Ennuchis made this for themselves, for their patroness Varia Servanda, daughter of Publius, and for their freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants. Thus, no sarcophagus is to be brought into this monument; this monument will not follow an external heir. In front: 10.5 feet; in the plot: 33 feet.
EDCS-13500061.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the Roman period, records the construction of a monument by Publius Varius Ampelus and Varia Ennuchis for themselves, their patroness, and their descendants, specifying burial regulations and dimensions.

EDCS-13500062.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ampelus and Ennychis, freedmen, placed this for their well-deserving patroness Varia Servanda, daughter of Publius, at their own expense.
EDCS-13500062.summary: This funerary epitaph from the Roman period records that freedmen named Ampelus and Ennychis erected a monument for their patroness, Varia Servanda.

EDCS-13500064.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Verria Zosime made this for herself and for her well-deserving husband, Lucius Verrius Eucharistos, for her freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. This monument will not follow an external heir. In front: 10 feet; in the plot: 10 feet.
EDCS-13500064.summary: This Roman funerary inscription records the dedication of a monument by Verria Zosime for herself, her husband, and their dependents, specifying burial dimensions and restrictions.

EDCS-13500065.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Verria Zosime and Verrius Euhelpistus made this for themselves, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-13500065.summary: This funerary inscription from the Roman period records a couple, Verria Zosime and Verrius Euhelpistus, dedicating a monument for themselves and their dependents.

EDCS-13500066.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Verria Zosime made this for herself and for her husband, Verrius Euhelpistus.
EDCS-13500066.summary: This funerary epitaph for Verria Zosime and her husband Verrius Euhelpistus, dating from the Roman period, records a dedication to their spirits.

EDCS-13500067.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Vetronius Victor made this for his well-deserving wife, Naphame, freedwoman of Publius, for himself, his family, and their descendants.
EDCS-13500067.summary: This Roman funerary inscription records Publius Vetronius Victor dedicating a monument for his wife, Naphame, himself, and their descendants.

EDCS-13500068.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Vibennius Donatus, a veteran of Augustus, made this for himself, for his wife Caninia Pia, for his sons Vibennius Maximus and Quartula, for his stepdaughter Cannia Pia, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and descendants. The place, or area, and crypt granted to him by Lucius Munatius Marinus, with its access and boundary.
EDCS-13500068.summary: This Roman funerary inscription by Marcus Vibennius Donatus, an Augustan veteran, details the dedication of a plot and crypt for his family and dependents, specifying the grantor and rights.

EDCS-13500069.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Zosimes, who lived 8 years, 7 months, and 16 days. Aelia Herois and Maternus, freedman of Augustus, made this for their beloved foster-daughter.
EDCS-13500069.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of a young girl named Zosimes, dedicated by her foster parents, Aelia Herois and Maternus.

EDCS-13500074.translation: Iulia Eunia made this for her most deserving husband. In peace.
EDCS-13500074.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a wife, Iulia Eunia, to her deceased husband.

EDCS-13500075.translation: She lived 11 years [---]. [---]io Anni[---]. [---] lived 8 years. [---] and Annio [---] lived [---].
EDCS-13500075.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a funerary monument recording the ages of individuals, likely children, who lived for specific durations.

EDCS-13500099.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ostensiae Filumeneti bene merenti fecit Ostensius Simplicius coniugi PI pientissimae fecit\n
EDCS-13500099.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ostensius Simplicius made this for his well-deserving wife, Ostensia Filumena, a most devoted wife.
EDCS-13500099.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Ostensius Simplicius to his deceased wife, Ostensia Filumena.

EDCS-13500120.translation: [---]O[---]. Theodo[---], a Roman knight [---], lived [---] years.
EDCS-13500120.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that appears to record the life and status of an individual identified as Theodo, a Roman knight.

EDCS-13500149.translation: For Publius Iuventius, from the workshops of Domitia, daughter of Publius, Lucilla. Dolliary work of Quintus Oppius Justus.
EDCS-13500149.summary: This inscription records a commission for 'dolliary' (related to pottery or earthenware) work from the workshops of Domitia Lucilla, for Publius Iuventius, by Quintus Oppius Justus.

EDCS-13500259.text_edition: \n Marciae Ulpiae Marci filiae Sossiae Calligonae stolatae matronae sodalicium iuvenum Herculanorum patronae\n
EDCS-13500259.translation: For Marcia Ulpia Sossia Calligona, daughter of Marcus, a matron of rank, patroness of the association of young men of Herculaneum.
EDCS-13500259.summary: This is an honorary inscription dedicating a monument to Marcia Ulpia Sossia Calligona, recognizing her status as a matron and patroness of a youth association.

EDCS-13600054.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Novius Felix and Pompeia Niceni [?], for them and their descendants who deserved well, in front 20 feet, in the field [---], this monument [---]
EDCS-13600054.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Novius Felix and Pompeia Niceni, intended for them and their descendants, specifying dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-13600069.summary: This fragmented inscription seems to record the ages of at least two individuals, possibly named Iucundus and Iucunda, who died at a young age.

EDCS-13600092.translation: For his wife Rufina. In peace. Agricolae, having fallen asleep(?).
EDCS-13600092.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Agricola to his wife Rufina, possibly with a reference to their peace in death.

EDCS-13600120.translation: Under the supervision of Claudius Secundinus, of the freedwoman Annia Allonis, freed by the emperor, from the workshop of Demetrius.
EDCS-13600120.summary: This is an inscription related to a workshop, likely mentioning the owners or managers of the workshop of Demetrius, possibly dating to the Antonine period.

EDCS-13600181.text_edition: \n Euhelpistus Aricinorum Iovi Optimo Maximo lecticarius voto trichlia sic ex permissione fecit suo inpendio\n
EDCS-13600181.translation: Euhelpistus of the Aricinians, a litter-bearer, made this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow, at his own expense, with permission.
EDCS-13600181.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Euhelpistus, a litter-bearer from Aricia, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-13600367.text_edition: \n Divae Faustinae Antonini Strenion libertus a cubiculo\n
EDCS-13600367.translation: Freedman of the divine Faustina, freedman from the bedroom.
EDCS-13600367.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated to a freedman of the divine Faustina, who served in the imperial bedchamber.

EDCS-13600422.text_edition: \n Caius Stonicius Trophimianus pa<v>imentum sua pequnia sic marmorabit sic\n
EDCS-13600422.translation: Gaius Stonicius Trophimianus will pave this with marble at his own expense.
EDCS-13600422.summary: This inscription records a promise by Gaius Stonicius Trophimianus to pave an area with marble at his own expense.

EDCS-13600426.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, mentioning the names Abdia Volp and Scitici.

EDCS-13600430.text_edition: \n Marcus Marius Numeri filius Sophus Marcus Rufrius Numeri filius Cimber IIIIviri quinquennales aquam ddendam lacus faciendos ex decreto decurionum curaverunt\n
EDCS-13600430.translation: Marcus Marius Sophus, son of Numerius, and Marcus Rufrius Cimber, son of Numerius, the four magistrates with quinquennial powers, took care of the assignment of water and the construction of basins, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-13600430.summary: This inscription records the completion of public works, specifically water supply and basin construction, by two quinquennial magistrates in Rome.

EDCS-13700297.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Eptecentus miles legionis II Parthicae |(cohortis) VIIII principis posterioris qui vixit nnos XL militavit nnos XIIII Aurelius Bitus miles legionis II Parthicae |(cohortis) I adstatus sic prior sic frater et heres bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-13700297.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Eptecentus, a soldier of Legio II Parthica, 9th cohort, posterior primary centurion, who lived 40 years, served 14 years. Aurelius Bitus, soldier of Legio II Parthica, 1st cohort, attached to the prior centurion, his brother and heir, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-13700297.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Eptecentus, a soldier in Legio II Parthica, set up by his brother and heir.

EDCS-13700299.text_edition: \n nae IIII magistro Saliorum Baebia Cai filia Marcella coniugi bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-13700299.translation: For his wife, well-deserving, the master of the Salii, Baebia Marcella, daughter of Caius, made this.
EDCS-13700299.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Baebia Marcella, daughter of Caius and master of the Salii, for her deserving wife.

EDCS-13700381.text_edition: \n Genio corporis splendidissimi inportantium sic et negotiantium vinariorum Caius Septimius Quietus praeco vinorum dono dedit\n
EDCS-13700381.translation: Gaius Septimius Quietus, town crier for the wine merchants, dedicated this as a gift to the genius of the most distinguished body of importers and wine merchants.
EDCS-13700381.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Gaius Septimius Quietus, a town crier for wine merchants, to the genius of the wine trade.

EDCS-13700382.text_edition: \n Neptuno Castori Polluci Lucius Catius Celer praefectus urbi\n
EDCS-13700382.translation: To Neptune, Castor, and Pollux. Lucius Catius Celer, prefect of the city.
EDCS-13700382.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Neptune, Castor, and Pollux by Lucius Catius Celer, the prefect of the city, likely during his term of office.

EDCS-13700383.text_edition: \n Aulo Egrilio Auli filio Auli nepoti Auli pronepoti Voturia Rufo decurionum decreto decurioni adlecto aedili quaestori IIviro flamini Romae et Augusti OVIBV V\n
EDCS-13700383.translation: To Aulus Egrilius Rufus, son of Aulus, grandson of Aulus, great-grandson of Aulus, of the Voturia tribe, enrolled as a decurion by decree of the decurions, formerly aedile, quaestor, duovir, and flamen of Rome and Augustus.
EDCS-13700383.summary: This is a dedication honoring Aulus Egrilius Rufus, detailing his career in local government and priesthood, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-13700384.text_edition: \n Marcus Acilius Marci filius Priscus decreto decurionum decurio adlectus quaestor aerarii suffragio decurionum IIvir aedilis IIvir quinquennalis praefectus IIviris praefectus collegii fabrum Ostiensium continuo triennio praefectus cohortis Bracaraaugustanorum tribunus cohortis IIX voluntariorum tribunus militum legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis flamen Romae et Augusti pontifex Volcani testamento fieri iussit per Aulum Egrilium Primigenium\n
EDCS-13700384.translation: Marcus Acilius Priscus, son of Marcus, a decurion enrolled by decree of the decurions, quaestor of the treasury, by vote of the decurions duovir, aedile, quinquennial duovir, prefect of the Ostian Guild of Builders for three continuous years, tribune of the cohort of Bracara Augustanorum, tribune of the 8th cohort of volunteers, military tribune of the 11th Claudian legion, pias fidelis, and flamen of Rome and Augustus, and pontifex of Vulcan, ordered this to be made in his will through Aulus Egrilius Primigenius.
EDCS-13700384.summary: This epitaph records the extensive public career and priesthood of Marcus Acilius Priscus, who commissioned his own tomb, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13700387.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli II Marcus Acilius Priscus Egrilius Plarianus cum Quinto Egrilio Plariano filio\n
EDCS-13700387.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, Marcus Acilius Priscus Egrilius Plarianus, together with his son Quintus Egrilius Plarianus.
EDCS-13700387.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Hadrian, likely made by Marcus Acilius Priscus Egrilius Plarianus and his son, placing it within the reign of Hadrian (117-138 AD).

EDCS-13700390.text_edition: \n Publio Martio Quirina Philippo curatori viae Praenestinae aedilicio curuli viatori quaestori ab aerario tribuno fabrum navalium Portensium plebes corporis fabrum navalium Ostiensium quibus ex senatus consulto coire licet patrono optimo sua pecunia posuit\n
EDCS-13700390.translation: To Publius Martius Philippus, of the Quirina tribe, curator of the Praenestine road, aedilician, quaestor from the treasury, tribune of the Port of Ostia naval builders, to the most excellent patron of the Ostian naval builders' college, which is permitted to assemble by senatorial decree, erected at his own expense.
EDCS-13700390.summary: This is a dedication by the Ostian naval builders to their patron, Publius Martius Philippus, recognizing his service in public works and administrative roles, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13700393.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titi Flavi Augusti liberti Stephani praeposito sic camellorum\n
EDCS-13700393.translation: To the Divine Shades, of Titus Flavius Stephanes, freedman of Augusts, overseer of camel stables.
EDCS-13700393.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Titus Flavius Stephanes, a freedman who managed camel stables, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13700398.text_edition: \n Voltidia Cai liberta Moschis sibi et Vergiliae Quinti filiae Priscae filiae suae hanc decuriones in honorem Quinti Vergili Mariani viri eius funere publico efferri censuerunt Quinto Vergilio QQuintorum |(mulieris) liberto Apollonio Quinto Vergilio Quinti |(mulieris) liberto Stephano Vergilia Moschis libertis libertabus posterisque eorum omnibus in fronte pedes XXX in agro pedes XXXISemis\n
EDCS-13700398.translation: Voltidia Moschis, freedwoman of Caius, concerning herself and her daughter Vergilia Prisca, daughter of Quintus, ordered this to be carried in public funeral procession. The decurions decreed that it should be in honor of Quintus Vergilius Marianus, her husband, and of Quintus Vergilius Apollonius, freedman of Quintus Vergilius, and of Quintus Vergilius Stephanus, freedman of Quintus Vergilius, and of Vergilia Moschis, freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. In front 30 feet, in the field 30 feet.
EDCS-13700398.summary: This funerary inscription details the arrangements for Voltidia Moschis and her family, including a public funeral for her husband Quintus Vergilius Marianus, and specifies plot dimensions, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13700399.text_edition: \n Lucio Mario Luci filio viro clarissimo Quirina Maximo Aureliano fetiali bis consuli proconsuli Asiae proconsuli Africae praefecto urbi Marius Lysimachus\n
EDCS-13700399.translation: To Lucius Marius Maximus Aurelianus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, a most distinguished man, fetial twice, former proconsul of Asia, former proconsul of Africa, prefect of the city, Marius Lysimachus [erected this].
