EDCS-13700399.summary: This dedication honors Lucius Marius Maximus Aurelianus, listing his high-ranking priestly and administrative offices, including proconsulships and prefect of the city, likely dating to the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-13700409.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare Augusto Vespasiano II Tito Caesare Augusti filio Vespasiano consulibus Iulias Felix Ulpiae Plotinae servus scripsi me percipere ab Lucio Cominio Primo in solutum denarios I actum Herculani Felicis Ulpiae Plotinae Auli Furi Saturnini Marci Ulpi Moschionis Marci Noni Facundi Felicis Ulpiae Plotinae\n
EDCS-13700409.translation: During the consulship of Emperor Caesar Augustus Vespasian for the 2nd time and Titus Caesar Vespasian, son of Augustus, for the 1st time, I, Iulias Felix, slave of Ulpia Plotina, acknowledge receiving from Lucius Cominius Primus the sum of 1 denarius in full payment. This was transacted by Herculanus Felix, Ulpia Plotina, Aulus Furius Saturninus, Marcus Ulpius Moschion, Marcus Nonius Facundus, Felix Ulpia Plotina.
EDCS-13700409.summary: This is a receipt for a payment of 1 denarius made by Lucius Cominius Primus to Iulias Felix, slave of Ulpia Plotina, during the consulship of Vespasian and Titus, likely dating to 71 AD.

EDCS-13700410.text_edition: \n quem Luci Comini Primioris Publi Corneli Poppaei Corneli Eleupori emisse mancipioque accepisse Lucius Cominius Primus HS MMMMLquattuor milia quinquaginta Publio Cornelio Poppaeo Erasto libripende Luci-- N Phr hunc hominem sanum furtis noxisque solutum esse praestari et si quis eum hominem partemve quam eius evicerit quominus Lucium Cominium Primum heredemve eius habere uti frui possidere recte liceat simplam pecuniam populi Romani dari haec ita uti adsolet recte praestari stipulatus est Lucius Cominius Primus spopondit Publius Cornelius Poppaeus actum in Pompeiano in figlinis Arrianis Poppaeae Augustae VIII Idus Maias Caio Memmio Regulo Lucio Verginio Rufo consulibus Luci Comini Primi Quinti Grani Abascanti Quinti Iuni Peregrini Marci Cerrini Aucti Publi Corneli Abascanti Cai Vibi Fabati Corneli \n
EDCS-13700410.translation: Lucius Cominius Primus has bought and received in mancipium from Lucius Cominius Primioris, Publius Cornelius Poppaeus, Cornelius Eleuporus [or their representatives] for 4050 sesterces. With Publius Cornelius Poppaeus Erastus acting as libripens, Lucius Cominius Primus stipulated that this man be warranted sound and free from crimes and faults, and if anyone should evict him or any part of him from Lucius Cominius Primus or his heir having the right to possess and enjoy him, he will pay double the sum to the Roman people. These things are warranted as usual. Lucius Cominius Primus pledged; Publius Cornelius Poppaeus responded. Transacted in Pompeii, in the brickyards of Arria Augusta, on the 8th day before the Ides of May, with Gaius Memmius Regulus and Lucius Verginius Rufus as consuls. [Witnesses:] Lucius Cominius Primus, Quintus Granius Abascantus, Quintus Junius Peregrinus, Marcus Cerrinius Auctus, Publius Cornelius Abascantus, Gaius Vibius Fabatus, Cornelius [---].
EDCS-13700410.summary: This is a legal document detailing the sale of a slave by Lucius Cominius Primor and others to Lucius Cominius Primus, including warranty clauses and witnesses, likely dating to the consulship of Regulus and Rufus (consuls around 49 AD or 151 AD, context suggests the earlier date).

EDCS-13800162.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely a dedication or funerary text belonging to Marcus Aurelius Cleander, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13800167.translation: To Memmia Nerulla, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-13800167.summary: This is a dedication to Memmia Nerulla, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-13800221.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Sextus Avienius Zosimus, a freedman of the Augustales, twice quinquennial, also twice quinquennial of the guild of boatmen of Ostia. Avienia Zosime [erected this] to her most beloved father.
EDCS-13800221.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Avienius Zosimus, a prominent member of the guild of boatmen of Ostia, erected by his daughter.

EDCS-13800225.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To [---]si, son of Marcus Bassus, decurialis, armamentarius, architect, and plebeian assistant in the procuratorate of the alabarchy of Pelusium.
EDCS-13800225.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bassus, who held multiple administrative and architectural roles, likely in the Roman province of Egypt.

EDCS-13800239.translation: Poppaea Fausta, freedwoman of Quintus, [daughter] of Blastus.
EDCS-13800239.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription for Poppaea Fausta, a freedwoman.

EDCS-13800242.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the name and lifespan of Primogenes.

EDCS-13800346.summary: This is an inscription indicating that Philetus Celeris bought a burial plot for himself while alive.

EDCS-13900429.text_edition: \n Quintus Safinius rex sacrorum magister? Octavia Marci filia Rec vivi sibi fecerunt et suis\n
EDCS-13900429.translation: Quintus Safinius, rex sacrorum, and his colleague? Octavia, daughter of Marcus, made this for themselves and their family while alive.
EDCS-13900429.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Safinius, a priest, and Octavia, his wife, indicating they prepared their tomb while alive.

EDCS-13900431.text_edition: \n Meam heredem uti ius habere possidereque recte liceat tum quanti homo emptus est tantam pecuniam stipulatus est Hamillus Vibidiae Proculae servus spopondit Claudia Musa tute recte mea sponte licet DCCCC HS eius nomine numerata simul et habere se dixit Claudia Musa ab Hamillo Vibidiae Proculae servo actum Herculaneo\n
EDCS-13900431.translation: Hamillus, slave of Vibidia Procula, guarantees that my heir may legally possess her. He stipulated payment for the value of the person. Hamillus, slave of Vibidia Procula, promised. Claudia Musa herself said that it is legally permitted, and named a price of 900 sesterces for her. Claudia Musa said she acted of her own free will before Hamillus, slave of Vibidia Procula, at Herculaneum.
EDCS-13900431.summary: This inscription records a legal transaction, likely the sale of a slave named Claudia Musa, detailing the terms of sale and the parties involved, including the location at Herculaneum.

EDCS-13900536.text_edition: \n Quintum Postumium Proculum aedilem dignum rei publicae Masculus rogat\n
EDCS-13900536.translation: Masculus asks Quintus Postumius Proculus, a worthy aedile for the state.
EDCS-13900536.summary: This is a political endorsement or request for support for Quintus Postumius Proculus, who is running for the office of aedile.

EDCS-13900537.summary: This is a statement of political preference, with Masculus supporting Ceius for the office of aedile.

EDCS-13900541.text_edition: \n Quintum Postumium Proculum Marcum Cerrinium Vatiam aediles\n
EDCS-13900541.translation: Quintus Postumius Proculus and Marcus Cerrinius Vatia as aediles.
EDCS-13900541.summary: This inscription records Quintus Postumius Proculus and Marcus Cerrinius Vatia holding the office of aedile.

EDCS-13900544.text_edition: \n Idibus Iulis inaures positas ad Faustillam pro |(denariis) II usurae deduxit aeris assem ex summa XXX\n
EDCS-13900544.translation: On the Ides of July, having put down the earrings for Faustilla, he reduced the principal amount of 30 denarii by 2 denarii in interest.
EDCS-13900544.summary: This inscription records a financial transaction involving interest payments on earrings, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900546.text_edition: \n Lucium Ceium Secundum IIvirum orat faciatis Papilio\n
EDCS-13900546.translation: Make Lucius Ceius Secundus the duovir, Papilio.
EDCS-13900546.summary: This inscription is a plea to appoint Lucius Ceius Secundus as duovir, likely a political or administrative request.

EDCS-13900547.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli possessionis, tituli sacri
EDCS-13900547.text_edition: \n Rustico Abco antemissum exemplar transvexit XVCC in nave Cai Umbrici Amprioci vecta Iovis et Iunonis parasemi Victoria sic Publi Pompili Saturi magister Marcus Lartidius Vitalis domo Clupeis Vectigal estis soldo gratis MCC solutio facta pridie Idus Octobres\n
EDCS-13900547.translation: Rusticus Abcus transported the preceding sample, carried on the ship of Caius Umbricius Ampriocius, under the auspices of Jupiter and Juno, with the toll of Victoria; thus Publius Pompilius Satura, magistratus, Marcus Lartidius Vitalis from Clupeis. The tax is paid, 1200 soldos, payment made the day before the Ides of October.
EDCS-13900547.summary: This inscription details a logistical and financial transaction concerning the transport of goods, including customs and payment details, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14201041.translation: To the divine shades. To Stercoria, dearest daughter, most beloved by all, who lived 18 years, 2 months, and 26 days. For her well-deserving, Geminus and Sozusa, her parents, erected this.
EDCS-14201041.summary: This is a tombstone inscription commemorating Stercoria, a young woman, set up by her parents.

EDCS-14800782.translation: To Turrania Atalante and Turrania Ter[t]ia.
EDCS-14800782.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the names of two individuals, possibly dedicants or recipients.

EDCS-14801745.translation: To the divine shades. Primus erected this for his well-deserving freedwoman, Gnesi, and his well-deserving slave, Turpillia Eutychia, his companion. She lived 35 years.
EDCS-14801745.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Primus for his freedwoman and slave, Turpillia Eutychia.

EDCS-14802482.translation: An olla for Venusta, his freedwoman.
EDCS-14802482.summary: This inscription records an olla, likely a funerary urn or a gift, dedicated to Venusta, a freedwoman.

EDCS-14805790.translation: To the divine shades. Titus Malius Afrodisius and Malia Crysa erected this for their well-deserving patroness. Publius Appuleius Primus.
EDCS-14805790.summary: This is a tombstone inscription erected by Titus Malius Afrodisius and Malia Crysa for their patroness, with a mention of Publius Appuleius Primus.

EDCS-15000040.text_edition: \n Caius A Phoebus cultoribus deae Magnae donum dedit\n
EDCS-15000040.translation: Gaius A. Phoebus dedicated this gift to the goddess Magna for her worshippers.
EDCS-15000040.summary: This inscription records a dedication of a gift by Gaius A. Phoebus to the goddess Magna for her devotees.

EDCS-15000108.translation: To Jupiter Dolichenus, Plinius Nigrinus, quinquennalis.
EDCS-15000108.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Jupiter Dolichenus by Plinius Nigrinus, who held the office of quinquennalis.

EDCS-15100142.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Seius Verecundus, who lived 60 years, 2 months, 5 days, 9 hours. [His wife?] made [this] for an incomparable father.
EDCS-15100142.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Seius Verecundus, recording his lifespan and stating that a family member erected the monument.

EDCS-15100143.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Seia Affiadis, his deserving wife. Marcus Caecilius Urbicus [made this].
EDCS-15100143.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Marcus Caecilius Urbicus for his wife, Seia Affiadis.

EDCS-15100144.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Sempronius Adauctus. Sempronia Caelestina [made this] for her well-deserving brother.
EDCS-15100144.summary: This funerary inscription records the deceased Sempronius Adauctus and identifies Sempronia Caelestina as the dedicant, who was his sister.

EDCS-15100146.translation: Sempronia Psecas for herself and for Sextilia Hyperbole, her freedwomen and their descendants, and for Tiberius Claudius Alcimus: this monument or tomb will not follow its heir.
EDCS-15100146.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sempronia Psecas, her freedwomen, their descendants, and Tiberius Claudius Alcimus, establishing a trust for the property.

EDCS-15100148.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Sentia Rufina, daughter of Gaius, who lived 25 years. Marcus Flavius Agrippinus [made this] for his chaste wife.
EDCS-15100148.summary: This funerary dedication is for Sentia Rufina, who died at age 25, and was erected by her husband, Marcus Flavius Agrippinus.

EDCS-15100149.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Septimius Felix, a bladder-maker (?), who lived 70 years, 2 months, 12 days. His wife Lartidia, his son Felix, his daughter [---], and his son Lartius made [this] for him as deserving.
EDCS-15100149.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Lucius Septimius Felix, a bladder-maker, and records his age and the family members who erected the monument.

EDCS-15100150.translation: For Septimia Julia, his deserving wife, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-15100150.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Septimia Julia, who died at the age of 20, presumably erected by her husband.

EDCS-15100151.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Septimia Lucilla, dearest daughter, who lived 19 years.
EDCS-15100151.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Septimia Lucilla, the daughter of the deceased, who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-15100152.translation: For Septimia Ursula, who lived 24 years, 1 month, 18 days. Aurelius Marcus, her husband, made [this] for his most precious wife.
EDCS-15100152.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Aurelius Marcus for his wife, Septimia Ursula, recording her age at death.

EDCS-15100153.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Julius Silvanus made this for his deserving wife Sestilia Iusta.
EDCS-15100153.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Julius Silvanus to his wife Sestilia Iusta.

EDCS-15100154.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Primi[genia?] dedicated this for Severus, her deserving and pious son.
EDCS-15100154.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aelia Primi[genia?] for her son Severus.

EDCS-15100155.translation: Lucius Sextilius Menothemis made this for Lucius Sextilius Taurus, for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-15100155.summary: This epitaph records the foundation of a burial plot by Lucius Sextilius Menothemis for his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.

EDCS-15100156.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titius Sicillius Saturninus for Sicillia Procula, his dearest and most beloved wife, good, pious, faithful to me and to our children. May the earth lie lightly on you. She lived with me for 33 years, 6 months.
EDCS-15100156.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Titius Sicillius Saturninus to his wife Sicillia Procula, noting her fidelity and their life together.

EDCS-15100157.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Siculus.
EDCS-15100157.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph dedicated to a person named Siculus.

EDCS-15100158.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Asinia Secunda for her deserving husband T. Silius Severus. He lived 35 years.
EDCS-15100158.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of T. Silius Severus, who lived 35 years, and was dedicated by his wife Asinia Secunda.

EDCS-15100159.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Ulpius Soterichus, freedman of Augustus, for his wife Silia Fabulla, the best of women and one who deserved well of him. She lived 50 years, 2 months, 10 days.
EDCS-15100159.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Marcus Ulpius Soterichus for his wife Silia Fabulla, specifying her age at death and his appreciation for her.

EDCS-15100160.translation: Marcus Siminius Crescens lived 25 years. He is buried here. His mother Felicula made this from her own funds.
EDCS-15100160.summary: This epitaph records the death of Marcus Siminius Crescens at age 25 and was funded by his mother Felicula.

EDCS-15100161.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Simpitius.
EDCS-15100161.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph dedicated to a person named Simpitius.

EDCS-15100162.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sigerus. He lived 5 years, 9 months, 24 days.
EDCS-15100162.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a child named Sigerus, specifying his age at death.

EDCS-15100163.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Siternia Dynamis lived for 68 years. Her son had this made for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-15100163.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Siternia Dynamis and commissioned by her son.

EDCS-15100164.translation: Sittius Primus made this for his deserving wife, Sittia Epicteis, who lived for 58 years, 11 days, and 10 hours.
EDCS-15100164.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sittia Epicteis, detailing her lifespan and commissioned by her husband, Sittius Primus.

EDCS-15100165.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [This was made] for Sittia Euthychia, freedwoman, and for Mamanius Sitti Ianuarius, her patron, who deserved well.
EDCS-15100165.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sittia Euthychia and her patron Mamanius Sitti Ianuarius, likely commissioned by Mamanius.

EDCS-15100166.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Sosius Felix, my dearest foster-son, who lived for two years, seven months, and fifteen days. Sosius Dafnus [had this made].
EDCS-15100166.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sosius Felix, a young child, commissioned by Sosius Dafnus.

EDCS-15100167.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. His wife made this for Quintus Sossius Phoebion, who lived for 43 years, [her] incomparable husband.
EDCS-15100167.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Sossius Phoebion, detailing his lifespan and commissioned by his wife.

EDCS-15100168.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [This was made] for Sossia Polygenia, [and] for Gaius Julius Paulus, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-15100168.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sossia Polygenia and her husband Gaius Julius Paulus.

EDCS-15100170.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [This was made] for Soterichus, who lived approximately 30 years, [my] only brother.
EDCS-15100170.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Soterichus, recording his approximate age and relationship to the dedicator.

EDCS-15100171.translation: Stallius Gaius protects these seats, belonging to Hauranus, from Epicurus, the joyous-living chorus.
EDCS-15100171.summary: An inscription related to property ownership or protection, possibly related to a philosophical school or group.

EDCS-15100172.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Statilius [son of Tiberius]... lived for...
EDCS-15100172.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Gaius Statilius, recording part of his lineage and incomplete lifespan.

EDCS-15100173.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Statilius Polycarpus made this for Aristobulus, his master, who deserved well.
EDCS-15100173.summary: A funerary epitaph commissioned by Titus Statilius Polycarpus for his patron, Aristobulus.

EDCS-15100174.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Stenia Secunda lived 44 years, 10 months, and 10 days. Her very devoted husband made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-15100174.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Stenia Secunda, set up by her husband.

EDCS-15100177.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Straton, son of Straton, who lived 3 years, 5 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-15100177.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Straton, a child, recording his age at death.

EDCS-15100178.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Plutius Fortunatus made this for his deserving wife Successa.
EDCS-15100178.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Successa, dedicated by her husband Plutius Fortunatus.

EDCS-15100179.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the age of a deceased person named Felici[---].

EDCS-15100182.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Suettia Victoria, daughter of Lucius, lived 22 years and 3 months. Her son Marcianus and mother Tryphenia, the heirs, [set this up].
EDCS-15100182.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Suettia Victoria, set up by her son and mother.

EDCS-15100183.translation: Sulla donated [this] to her freedman Dionysus.
EDCS-15100183.summary: This inscription records a donation made by Sulla to her freedman Dionysus.

EDCS-15100184.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Sullius Albanus lived 11 years, 4 months, 4 days, and 4 hours. Marcus Aeclanius Chrysogonus made this for his most devoted son.
EDCS-15100184.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Sullius Albanus, set up by his father Marcus Aeclanius Chrysogonus.

EDCS-15100185.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sulpicia Trophime made this for her deserving husband Gaius Sulpicius Rusticus, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-15100185.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Sulpicius Rusticus, set up by his wife Sulpicia Trophime.

EDCS-15100186.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Sulpicius Teri lived 17 years, 11 months, 20 days, and 7 hours. Gaius Sulpicius Saturninus, his father, [made this] for his most devoted son.
EDCS-15100186.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Sulpicius Teri, set up by his father Gaius Sulpicius Saturninus.

EDCS-15100187.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph indicating the burial place of Sulpicia Amanda.

EDCS-15100188.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Sulpicia Chloe, for whom Gaius Clodius Epaphroditus made this monument as her husband.
EDCS-15100188.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his wife, Sulpicia Chloe.

EDCS-15100189.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Sulpicia, daughter of Gaius. She lived 18 years and 11 months.
EDCS-15100189.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Sulpicia, daughter of Gaius, at her death.

EDCS-15100190.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Sulpicia Naidis. The heirs, Valeria Naïs and Lucius Valerius Carpentarius, set this up.
EDCS-15100190.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sulpicia Naidis, set up by her heirs, Valeria Naïs and Lucius Valerius Carpentarius.

EDCS-15100192.translation: Fragmentary inscription mentioning individuals named Helpid[---] and possibly a freedman.
EDCS-15100192.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription that contains names of individuals but is too incomplete to provide further details.

EDCS-15100193.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Tadius Restitutus and Mucia Crescentina, for their most dutiful daughter, Mucia Crescentina. She lived 25 years.
EDCS-15100193.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents for their daughter, Mucia Crescentina, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-15100194.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Gaius Tantilius Secundinus. Gaius Tantilius Secundinus, his father, for his incomparable son, who lived 17 years, 9 months, 18 days, and 7 hours.
EDCS-15100194.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father for his son, Gaius Tantilius Secundinus, detailing his age at death.

EDCS-15100197.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Aulus Terentius Augurinus, who lived 17 years and 8 months. Augurinus, for his most rare brother.
EDCS-15100197.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a brother for his deceased sibling, Aulus Terentius Augurinus.

EDCS-15100198.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Gaius Terentius Potho, son of Gaius, who lived 13 years. For Gaius Terentius Pothinus, son of Gaius, who lived 11 years. For Gaius Terentius Pothinianus, son of Gaius, who lived 7 years and 15 days. For Gaius Terentius Potho, son of Gaius, who lived 10 months and 12 days. Gaius Terentius Pothinus, the father, most unfortunate, for his dearest sons, and for himself, and for Marcia his wife, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-15100198.summary: This is a large funerary monument set up by an unfortunate father for multiple deceased sons, along with himself, his wife, and descendants.

EDCS-15100199.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Terentia Ampelis lived 38 years, 7 months, and 23 days.
EDCS-15100199.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of Terentia Ampelis at her death.

EDCS-15100200.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Terentia Augurina lived 6 years, 11 months, and 20 days. Terentius Caecilianus made this for his most sweet daughter, well deserving.
EDCS-15100200.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father, Terentius Caecilianus, for his daughter, Terentia Augurina.

EDCS-15100201.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Terentia Augurina lived for 6 years, 11 months, and 20 days. Terentius Caecilianus, her father, made this for his most sweet daughter, well-deserving.
EDCS-15100201.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Terentia Augurina, set up by her father.

EDCS-15100202.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Bennius Celer and Terentia Procilla, parents, made this for their most sweet daughter, Terentia Celerina, well-deserving, who lived for 18 years, 10 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-15100202.summary: A funerary epitaph for Terentia Celerina, set up by her parents.

EDCS-15100203.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Antonius Lucianus made this for his most sweet son, Terentianus, well-deserving, who lived for 6 years and 3 months.
EDCS-15100203.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Terentianus, set up by his father.

EDCS-15100204.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. His sister made this for her well-deserving brother, Tertius, who lived for approximately 31 years. For Orco Peregrino.
EDCS-15100204.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tertius, set up by his sister, possibly indicating he died abroad.

EDCS-15100205.translation: Thallus made this for Flora, his most sweet daughter.
EDCS-15100205.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flora, set up by her father, Thallus.

EDCS-15100207.translation: Pedu(caea?), freedwoman of Titus, Hediste, lived for 35 years. Thoria Auge made this from her own property.
EDCS-15100207.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hediste, freedwoman of Titus, set up by Thoria Auge.

EDCS-15100208.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Threptus, freedman of Propinquus, the good one, is buried here for himself and his family.
EDCS-15100208.summary: A funerary epitaph for Threptus, a freedman, intended for him and his descendants.

EDCS-15100209.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Pacatianus and Titus Pacata made this for their well-deserving brother, Tito Pacato, who lived for 30 years and 4 months.
EDCS-15100209.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tito Pacato, set up by his brothers.

EDCS-15100210.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Volusius Hermes made this for his well-deserving wife, Titia Coetonis, and for himself.
EDCS-15100210.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titia Coetonis, set up by her husband, Quintus Volusius Hermes.

EDCS-15100211.translation: To the sacred spirits of the underworld. Titia Datiba set up this monument for her son, Gaius Victor, and for her most sweet husband, (Gaius?), who lived for 35 years, 5 months, 10 days, and 11 hours; the wife lived as long as she could.
EDCS-15100211.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Titia Datiba for her husband and son, with detailed lifespans.

EDCS-15100212.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titius Ingenuus and Iulia Optima made this for their dearest children Titius Paestanius and Titia Lucida, who lived for 3 years and 18 days and 4 years and 24 days, respectively, and for their well-deserving children.
EDCS-15100212.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by parents for their two young children.

EDCS-15100213.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Valeria Gemella made this for her well-deserving daughter Titia Successa, who lived for 15 years.
EDCS-15100213.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a mother for her deceased daughter.

EDCS-15100214.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pelacisanus Daximia, mother, and Ammonius Sabinus, stepfather, made this for their well-deserving son Titinius Claeus Felix, who lived for 16 years.
EDCS-15100214.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a mother and stepfather for their son.

EDCS-15100216.translation: Aulus Titinius Fructus is buried here.
EDCS-15100216.summary: This is a simple tombstone indicating the burial of Aulus Titinius Fructus.

EDCS-15100217.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aulia Hya made this for her well-deserving husband Aulus Titinius Nigellio, who lived for 75 years.
EDCS-15100217.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a wife for her husband.

EDCS-15100218.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. The mother Pompeiia Felicitas made this for her well-deserving daughter Tittia Festa, who lived for 5 years, 3 months, and [number] days.
EDCS-15100218.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a mother for her young daughter.

EDCS-15100219.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Memmius Serapio made this for his incomparable wife [name missing], who lived for 70 years.
EDCS-15100219.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a husband for his wife.

EDCS-15100220.translation: Gaulanus made this with his own money for Gaius Trebius Alexander, freedman of Gaius; Gaius Trebius Gaulanu, son of Gaius; Gaius Trebius Rufus, son of Gaius; and Levia Secunda, daughter of Gaius; for himself and his father, brother, and mother.
EDCS-15100220.summary: This is a funerary monument built by a son for his father, brother, mother, and himself, likely indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-15100221.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Evaristus, father, [made this] for his most dutiful daughter Trebonia Marsilla, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-15100221.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a father for his daughter.

EDCS-15100222.translation: Lucius Triarius Hermogenes(?), freedman of Lucius. Clodia Musa, freedwoman of a woman. Chreste, mother. Dionysius Hermogenes(?).
EDCS-15100222.summary: This inscription lists individuals, likely family members, associated with a tomb, with the primary deceased being Lucius Triarius Hermogenes(?).

EDCS-15100224.translation: For the memory of Lucius Trisenius Echion, his wife Cornelia made this.
EDCS-15100224.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cornelia to her husband, Lucius Trisenius Echion.

EDCS-15100227.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Reginus made this for Truttedius Zmaracdo, well deserving father.
EDCS-15100227.summary: A funerary inscription by Reginus for his father, Truttedius Zmaracdo, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100228.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Gaius Tu[---] Celsinus.
EDCS-15100228.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Tu[---] Celsinus.

EDCS-15100230.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gnaeus Saenius Phoebianus arranged to have this made for his incomparable wife, Tullia Vitelliana, who lived 35 years and 2 months.
EDCS-15100230.summary: A funerary inscription by Gnaeus Saenius Phoebianus for his wife, Tullia Vitelliana, who lived 35 years and 2 months.

EDCS-15100232.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Turronius Adiuctor lived 36 years and 40 days. His wife Betutia Procula and his parents Tamusius Adiuctor and Turronia Epictesis [made this].
EDCS-15100232.summary: A funerary inscription set up by the parents and wife for Lucius Turronius Adiuctor, who lived 36 years and 40 days.

EDCS-15100233.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. [---]distus made this for his well-deserving wife, Tutilia Felicula, with whom he lived [---] years.
EDCS-15100233.summary: A funerary inscription by a husband for his wife, Tutilia Felicula, mentioning their period of cohabitation.

EDCS-15100236.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Valerius V[---], who lived [---]. Aurelia [---] for her husband(?).
EDCS-15100236.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription possibly dedicated by Aurelia to her husband, Valerius V[---].

EDCS-15100238.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Valerius Agesilaus for himself and for his most excellent wife Valeria Theonoe, deserving of all good, and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, and for those whom I have manumitted in my will. If anyone wishes to sell or alienate this place enclosed by material or this tomb, he shall give to the Republic of Puteoli 10,000 sesterces and to Ulpia Marcia the plot in the hypogeum.
EDCS-15100238.summary: A funerary inscription by Marcus Valerius Agesilaus for himself, his wife, freedmen, descendants, and others, with a penalty clause for alienation of the tomb and a bequest to the city of Puteoli and Ulpia Marcia.

EDCS-15100239.translation: Marcus Valerius Arator lived 6 sweet years [---]
EDCS-15100239.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Arator, who died at the age of 6.

EDCS-15100241.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Valerius Cogitatus Clodianus Pius lived 17 years, 7 months, 10 days. He lies here.
EDCS-15100241.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Valerius Cogitatus Clodianus Pius, who lived 17 years, 7 months, and 10 days.

EDCS-15100242.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valerius Diodorus, son of Quintus. He lived 30 years. Gaius Julius Fronto made this for one well-deserving.
EDCS-15100242.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Diodorus, set up by Gaius Julius Fronto, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15100243.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Valerius Dionysianus Julianus, who lived 19 years, 9 months, and 17 days. Valerius Julianus and Valeria Dionysias made this for their dearest son, against the vow.
EDCS-15100243.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Dionysianus Julianus, erected by his parents, Valerius Julianus and Valeria Dionysias.

EDCS-15100244.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Valerius Eumorphianus, in the 20th year, 1st month, and 5th day of his life. Valerius and Sempronia, miserable parents, made this for their most holy and reverent son.
EDCS-15100244.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Eumorphianus, erected by his parents, Valerius and Sempronia.

EDCS-15100245.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Quintus Valerius Eutyches, father. His son made this for one well-deserving.
EDCS-15100245.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Valerius Eutyches, set up by his son.

EDCS-15100246.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Valerius, son of Marcus Valerius Felissimus. He lived 3 years, 6 months, and 4 days. [---] Olympicus, his dearest son.
EDCS-15100246.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius, a child, erected by his father, Marcus Valerius Felissimus, and possibly mentioning another son, Olympicus.

EDCS-15100247.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Valerius Fortunatus. He lived 32 years, a worthy youth. Licinia Maxima made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-15100247.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Fortunatus, erected by his wife, Licinia Maxima.

EDCS-15100248.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Valerius Hermetes. Larcia Helpis made this for her excellent husband.
EDCS-15100248.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Hermetes, erected by his wife, Larcia Helpis.

EDCS-15100250.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Valerius Laotus, also called Africanus, my dearest and most beloved son above all others, most dutiful. He lived 6 years, 7 days, and 3 hours. Aurelia Lais, his mother, made this for her dearest son, who well deserved it.
EDCS-15100250.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Laotus Africanus, a young child, erected by his mother, Aurelia Lais.

EDCS-15100252.translation: The remains of Marcus Valerius Primio, son of Caius.
EDCS-15100252.summary: A simple ossuary inscription for Marcus Valerius Primio, son of Caius.

EDCS-15100253.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valerius Rufus, dearest son, who lived 9 years, 10 months, and 15 days. His parents made this.
EDCS-15100253.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Rufus, erected by his parents.

EDCS-15100254.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Valerius Rufus lived 9 years, 4 months, and 27 days. His father, Gaius Valerius Rufus.
EDCS-15100254.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Valerius Rufus, set up by his father of the same name, who died at the age of 9 years, 4 months, and 27 days.

EDCS-15100255.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Valerius Severinus lived 30 years and 36 days. Pontia Priscilla for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-15100255.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Valerius Severinus, set up by his mother Pontia Priscilla, who died at the age of 30 years and 36 days.

EDCS-15100256.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Verinianus lived 4 years and 7 days. Valeria Iustina, his mother, made this for her most dutiful and deserving son.
EDCS-15100256.summary: This is a tombstone for Valerius Verinianus, set up by his mother Valeria Iustina, who died at the age of 4 years and 7 days.

EDCS-15100257.translation: Valeria Cimbiliata made this for her well-deserving husband, Lucius Valerius Xinnas.
EDCS-15100257.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Valerius Xinnas, set up by his wife Valeria Cimbiliata.

EDCS-15100258.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria, daughter of Spurius, who lived 9 years and 10 months. Gaius Valerius Priscus and Iulia Secundina, her parents, for their most sweet daughter.
EDCS-15100258.summary: This is a tombstone for Valeria, daughter of Spurius, set up by her parents Gaius Valerius Priscus and Iulia Secundina, who died at the age of 9 years and 10 months.

EDCS-15100259.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Afrodisia, who lived 27 years, an incomparable, chaste wife. Aurelius Theseus for his virgin wife, on account of her chastity.
EDCS-15100259.summary: This is a tombstone for Valeria Afrodisia, set up by her husband Aurelius Theseus, who died at the age of 27 years and was praised for her chastity and uniqueness.

EDCS-15100260.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Callipolis. Marcus Titius Rufus made this for himself, his family, and his deserving wife.
EDCS-15100260.summary: This is a tombstone for Valeria Callipolis, set up by Marcus Titius Rufus for himself, his family, and his wife.

EDCS-15100262.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For his most sweet daughter, Valeria Constantia, who lived 18 years and 10 days. Lucius Plaetorius Primitivus.
EDCS-15100262.summary: This is a tombstone for Valeria Constantia, set up by her father Lucius Plaetorius Primitivus, who died at the age of 18 years and 10 days.

EDCS-15100263.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Eupraxia, freedwoman of Lucius. Plotii Apolaustus and Nestor, sons, for their deserving mother.
EDCS-15100263.summary: This is a tombstone for Valeria Eupraxia, freedwoman of Lucius, set up by her sons Plotii Apolaustus and Nestor.

EDCS-15100264.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Ianuaria. Quintus Hordeonius Gelasinus, her father.
EDCS-15100264.summary: This is a tombstone for Valeria Ianuaria, set up by her father Quintus Hordeonius Gelasinus.

EDCS-15100265.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Iusta, to her incomparable husband Valerius Heraclifes, with whom she lived.
EDCS-15100265.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a husband by his wife, Valeria Iusta.

EDCS-15100267.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Philia.
EDCS-15100267.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to Valeria Phila.

EDCS-15100268.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Pia, most sweet daughter, who lived 5 years, 10 months, and 25 days. Valerius Hermogenes and Aviania Glafyra, her parents, made this.
EDCS-15100268.summary: This is a funerary dedication by parents for their young daughter, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-15100269.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Rufina. She lived 15 years. Antonius Paulus, to his deserving wife.
EDCS-15100269.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a wife, Rufina, by her husband Antonius Paulus.

EDCS-15100270.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Servata.
EDCS-15100270.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to Valeria Servata.

EDCS-15100271.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valeria Spes lived 30 years, 5 months. Sextilius Rufus, her husband, and Sextilius Sentianus, her son, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-15100271.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband and son for Valeria Spes.

EDCS-15100273.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Successa.
EDCS-15100273.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to Valeria Successa.

EDCS-15100274.translation: Nicolavus, freedman of Publius. Valeria Truphera, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-15100274.summary: This inscription records the names and freedman status of two individuals, Nicolavus and Valeria Truphera.

EDCS-15100275.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For //. Valerius Silvanus made this for his most dutiful mother, the well-deserving.
EDCS-15100275.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a son, Valerius Silvanus, for his mother.

EDCS-15100276.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Victoria. She lived 3 years, 7 months, and 15 days. Gaius Valerius Serenus, her father, made this for the well-deserving. Good Fortune.
EDCS-15100276.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a father for his young daughter, Victoria, who died at the age of 3.

EDCS-15100277.translation: To the divine shades. Lucius Istavarius Ireneus and Istavaria Victorina [made this] for their most sweet mother Valeria Victoria, who lived 48 years, 6 months, and 27 days, and for their most sweet grandmother Istavaria Victorina, [her] benefactress.
EDCS-15100277.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by children and grandchildren to Valeria Victoria, who lived to be 48 years old.

EDCS-15100280.translation: To Varia Iusta, daughter of Spurius(?). Lucius Marius Iunianus [made this] for his wife of rarest virtue, who lived 25 years, 11 months, and 18 days.
EDCS-15100280.summary: A funerary monument for Varia Iusta, dedicated by her husband Lucius Marius Iunianus, who records her lifespan of 25 years.

EDCS-15100281.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Probus [made this] for his deserving wife, Varia Tertulla.
EDCS-15100281.summary: A funerary dedication to Varia Tertulla, made by her husband Probus.

EDCS-15100282.translation: To the divine shades. [---]veidius [---]lianus lived 3[X?] years and 10 days.
EDCS-15100282.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the lifespan of an individual whose name is mostly lost.

EDCS-15100283.translation: To the divine shades. For Venerianus, dearest son, who lived 5 years and 9 months. [His] father [made this] for [him] as a benefactor.
EDCS-15100283.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a father to his son Venerianus, who died at the age of 5 years and 9 months.

EDCS-15100284.translation: For the deserving wife Venusta, who lived 16 years, 10 months, and 14 days.
EDCS-15100284.summary: A funerary dedication for Venusta, who died at the age of 16 years, 10 months, and 14 days.

EDCS-15100286.translation: For Veratia Euanthidis, daughter of Lucius, [his] incomparable wife. [This was made by] Cornelius Marcellinus, [her] husband.
EDCS-15100286.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Cornelius Marcellinus to his wife Veratia Euanthidis.

EDCS-15100290.translation: To the divine shades. For Verria Agathemeris.
EDCS-15100290.summary: A funerary dedication to Verria Agathemeris.

EDCS-15100291.translation: To the divine shades. For Verria Agatetyche.
EDCS-15100291.summary: A funerary dedication to Verria Agatetyche.

EDCS-15100292.translation: To the divine shades. For Verria Eutychini, who lived 35 years and 6 days. Eunus [made this] for [his] wife.
EDCS-15100292.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Eunus to his wife Verria Eutychini, who lived 35 years and 6 days.

EDCS-15100293.translation: To Verria Grapte, most dutiful mother, who lived 75 years.
EDCS-15100293.summary: This is a tombstone for Verria Grapte, set up by her children, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100294.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aulus Vesonius Paramythus. Vesonia Prima, his wife, and Eutyches, freedman, and Zoticus, freedman, [set this up] for their well-deserving patron.
EDCS-15100294.summary: A funerary dedication for Aulus Vesonius Paramythus, set up by his wife and freedmen, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100298.translation: To the departed spirits. For C. Vetthenius Timotheus, who lived 28 years, 4 months, 20 days. Vetthenius Aeros made [this] for his well-deserving son.
EDCS-15100298.summary: A funerary monument for Vetthenius Timotheus, set up by his father, dating to the Imperial period given the funerary formula.

EDCS-15100299.translation: [To the] departed spirits. For [---]rcia Vettia, [who lived] 27 years. [---]us Rufus [set this up] for his most beloved wife, well-deserving.
EDCS-15100299.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication for Vettia, set up by her husband, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100300.translation: To Vettia Dorides, his incomparable wife. Gaius Valerius Hermes [made this].
EDCS-15100300.summary: A dedication to Vettia Dorides, set up by her husband Gaius Valerius Hermes, likely a tombstone from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100301.translation: To the departed spirits. For the Veturii: Citiades, Anicetus, and Alia Citiadis, and Severa. Gaius Veturius Anicetus, the father, and likewise for himself, and for Veturia Charis, his freedwoman and wife, and for Veturia Felicitas, his freedwoman, and for their descendants.
EDCS-15100301.summary: A complex funerary dedication for multiple members of the Veturii family, including freedmen and descendants, set up by the paterfamilias Gaius Veturius Anicetus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100303.translation: [To the] departed spirits. For Vibellius Nestor, his most sweet son, who lived 8 years and 6 months. [He/She] made [this] for [him], well-deserving.
EDCS-15100303.summary: A funerary monument for Vibellius Nestor, a young child, set up by his parent, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100305.translation: To the departed spirits. For Vibius Celsus and Vibia Severa, well-deserving parents. Their children made [this].
EDCS-15100305.summary: A funerary dedication for parents, Vibius Celsus and Vibia Severa, set up by their children, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100307.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Vibbius Philadespotes, who lived 70 years and 7 months.
EDCS-15100307.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Vibbius Philadespotes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100309.translation: To the departed spirits. For Vibia Faustina, who lived 14 years, 3 months, 18 days. Aelia Tyche [set this up] for her most sweet daughter.
EDCS-15100309.summary: A tombstone for Vibia Faustina, set up by her mother Aelia Tyche, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100311.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vibias Onesima made this for herself.
EDCS-15100311.summary: A funerary inscription recording Vibias Onesima dedicating a monument to herself, likely in the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-15100313.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Publius Vicrius Apollinaris, Publius Vicrius Tachinus, his most incomparable fellow freedman, made this.
EDCS-15100313.summary: A funerary inscription by Publius Vicrius Tachinus for his fellow freedman Publius Vicrius Apollinaris, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100314.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His brother made this for Victor.
EDCS-15100314.summary: A funerary inscription by a brother for Victor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100315.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Victoria, who lived 5 years, 2 months, and 20 days. Servea Marcellina, her nurse, made this for the deserving.
EDCS-15100315.summary: A funerary inscription by a nurse for Victoria, recording her age at death and dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100316.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Victoria, his wife...
EDCS-15100316.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to a woman named Victoria, presumably by her husband.

EDCS-15100318.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Vinicia Rufina, daughter of Aulus, his most rare wife. Lucius Julius Tertullinus, her husband, [made this].
EDCS-15100318.summary: A funerary inscription by Lucius Julius Tertullinus for his highly esteemed wife, Vinicia Rufina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100319.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Vibias Cornelia Pharesia Adaugenda, his chaste, modest, and incomparable wife and mistress. Virius Fortunatus made this for the deserving with affection.
EDCS-15100319.summary: A funerary inscription by Virius Fortunatus for his wife Vibias Cornelia Pharesia Adaugenda, emphasizing her virtues and his affection, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100321.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vitelliae Felicitati vixit nnos VIII menses V Vitellia Helpis filiae bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-15100321.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vitellia Helpis made this for her daughter Vitellia Felicitas, who lived 8 years and 5 months, the deserving.
EDCS-15100321.summary: A funerary inscription by Vitellia Helpis for her daughter Vitellia Felicitas, recording her age, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15200081.text_edition: \n Marcarianus vir clarissimus tauroboliatus Matri Deum dono dat\n
EDCS-15200081.translation: Marcarianus, a most distinguished man, having undergone the taurobolium, dedicates this to the Mother of the Gods.
EDCS-15200081.summary: An inscription recording Marcarianus' dedication to the Mother of the Gods after a taurobolium ritual, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15200082.text_edition: \n Aulus Livius Proculus Publius Lucilius Gamala filius IIvir praefectus Caesaris locum quod aedes Bellonae fieret impensa lictorum et servorum publicorum qui in corpore sunt adsignaverunt decreto decurionum curaverunt Marco Naevio Fructo et []\n
EDCS-15200082.translation: Aulus Livius Proculus and Publius Lucilius Gamala, as duovirs and prefect of Caesar, have assigned the place where the temple of Bellona was to be built, at the expense of the lictors and public slaves who are in the corps, and they supervised it by decree of the decurions, with Marcus Naevius Fructus and [---].
EDCS-15200082.summary: A public inscription concerning the assignment of land for the construction of a temple to Bellona, decreed by local officials and funded by lictors and public slaves, possibly from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-15200086.text_edition: \n Imperatori Marco Aurelio Antonino principales corporis hastiferum sic Ostiensum dedikata sic III Idus Decembres Plautiano II et Getae sic consulibus curavit Caius Anneius Zeno\n
EDCS-15200086.translation: To the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, the main standard-bearers of the Ostian people dedicated [this]; it was completed on December 11, under the consulship of Plautianus II and Geta, seen to by Gaius Anneius Zenas.
EDCS-15200086.summary: A dedication by the standard-bearers of Ostia to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, dated to December 11, during the consulship of Plautianus II and Geta.

EDCS-15200087.text_edition: \n Genio decurionum Ostiensium Lucio Aelio Aurelio Commodo Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae filio Lucius Laelius Spuri filius Herennianus scriba cerarius sua pecunia posuit\n
EDCS-15200087.translation: To the Genius of the Ostian Decurions, Lucius Laelius Herennianus, son of Lucius Spurius, scribe and wax-merchant, erected this at his own expense in honour of Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus, son of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-15200087.summary: A dedication by Lucius Laelius Herennianus, a scribe and wax-merchant, to the Genius of the Ostian Decurions and in honour of Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus.

EDCS-15200088.text_edition: \n Caius Rubrius Fortunatus Caius Rubrius Iustus et Iscantia Prima patroni hastoforum sic Ostiensium aedem vetustatis collapsam sua pecunia fecerunt dedicata III Nonas Augustas Geta et Antonino consulibus\n
EDCS-15200088.translation: Gaius Rubrius Fortunatus, Gaius Rubrius Iustus, and Iscantia Prima, patrons of the Ostian standard-bearers, rebuilt this temple, collapsed from age, at their own expense; it was dedicated on August 3, under the consulship of Geta and Antoninus.
EDCS-15200088.summary: A dedication by patrons of the Ostian standard-bearers to rebuild a temple that had collapsed due to age, dated to August 3, during the consulship of Geta and Antoninus.

EDCS-15200093.text_edition: \n Hospes resiste nisi molestust perspice monumentum quod sibi Publius Publi Granius sibi et sueisque vivos fecit Euhodus turarius salve vale\n
EDCS-15200093.translation: Stranger, stop, if it doesn't bother you, and look at this monument which Publius Granius, son of Publius, made for himself and his family while he was alive. Euhodus, a seller of cups, farewell, be well.
EDCS-15200093.summary: An epitaph for Publius Granius, son of Publius, and his family, created by himself while alive, also mentioning Euhodus, a seller of cups.

EDCS-15200177.text_edition: \n AG Crispinae ex argenti pondo III et clipeum m aereum et signum vir clarissimus dendrophorus Ostiensis decreto decurionum ? dedicavit et dedit VL sportulas X\n
EDCS-15200177.translation: Crispina's precinct, from 3 pounds of silver, and a bronze shield and a statue, the most distinguished dendrophore of Ostia, dedicated and presented by decree of the decurions, with 10 sportulas [of gifts].
EDCS-15200177.summary: A dedication by a distinguished dendrophore of Ostia, on behalf of Crispina, including a silver offering, a bronze shield, and a statue, by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-15300214.text_edition: \n Marcus Cluvius Iucundus et Lalus Salliae magistri Fortunae Confluenti donum dederunt\n
EDCS-15300214.translation: Marcus Cluvius Iucundus and Lalus, magistrates, gave this gift to Fortuna Confluens.
EDCS-15300214.summary: A dedication by magistrates Marcus Cluvius Iucundus and Lalus to Fortuna Confluens.

EDCS-15300215.text_edition: \n Quod Marcus Ofillius Celer IIvir iterum verba fecit pertinere at sic municipi dignitatem meritis Marci Noni Balbi respondere de ea re ita censuerunt cum Marcus Nonius Balbus quo hac vixerit parentis animum cum plurima liberalitate singulis universisque praistiterit placere decurionibus statuam equestrem ei poni quam celeberrimo loco ex pecunia publica inscribique Marco Nonio Marci filio Menenia Balbo praetori proconsuli patrono universus ordo populi Herculanienssis sic ob merita eius item eo loco quo cineres eius conlecti sunt aram marmoream fieri et constitui inscribique publice Marco Nonio Marci filio Balbo exque eo loco parentalibus pompam duci ludisque gymnicis qui soliti erant fieri diem adici unum in honorem eius et cum in theatro ludi fient sellam eius poni censuerunt\n
EDCS-15300215.translation: Concerning the matter which Marcus Ofillius Celer, twice duumvir, brought up, regarding the dignity of the municipium and responding to the merits of Marcus Nonius Balbus, they resolved thus: Since Marcus Nonius Balbus, during his life, showed a parental spirit with the greatest generosity to individuals and the community, it is decreed by the decurions that an equestrian statue be erected to him in the most crowded location, and inscribed to Marcus Nonius Balbus, son of Marcus, of the Menenian tribe, praetor and proconsul and patron; and that by the entire order of the people of Hercules, for his merits, a marble altar be made and set up in the same location where his ashes were collected, and inscribed to Marcus Nonius Balbus, son of Marcus; and that from that place, a procession be led for his funeral rites, and one day be added to the games which were accustomed to be held in his honour; and when games are held in the theatre, that his seat be placed.
EDCS-15300215.summary: A decree by the town council and people of Herculaneum to erect an equestrian statue and marble altar, add a day to games, and reserve a seat in his honour for Marcus Nonius Balbus, son of Marcus, for his many merits and generosity.

EDCS-15300216.text_edition: \n Vitelliae Calliste fecit se viva ne quis ossua cineres exter inferat in fronte pedes X in agro pedes X id aedificium si quis transferre voluerit inferet HS CCCC in arcam\n
EDCS-15300216.translation: Vitellia Calliste made this while alive, so that no one may bring bones or ashes inside; in front 10 feet, in the field 10 feet. If anyone wishes to transfer this building, they shall pay 400 sesterces into the strongbox.
EDCS-15300216.summary: A funerary inscription by Vitellia Calliste, specifying the dimensions of her monument and setting conditions and a price for its transfer.

EDCS-15300241.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate consul V pater patriae fecit exacta cura Marci Rutili Lupi praefecti annonae\n
EDCS-15300241.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul V, father of the fatherland, made this, under the supervision of Marcus Rutilius Lupus, prefect of the grain supply.
EDCS-15300241.summary: A construction or dedication by Emperor Trajan, overseen by the prefect of the grain supply, Marcus Rutilius Lupus.

EDCS-15300345.text_edition: \n Divo Augusto sacrum Lucius Sonteius Luci filius Florus Marcus Obultronius Marci filius Cultellus IIviri\n
EDCS-15300345.translation: Sacred to the deified Augustus. Lucius Sonteius Florus, son of Lucius, and Marcus Obultronius Cultellus, son of Marcus, served as duumvirs.
EDCS-15300345.summary: A sacred dedication to the deified Augustus, made by the duumvirs Lucius Sonteius Florus and Marcus Obultronius Cultellus.

EDCS-15400012.text_edition: \n Caius Cartilius Cai filius duovir tertium Poplicola\n
EDCS-15400012.translation: Gaius Cartilius, son of Gaius, served as duovir for the third time, Poplicola.
EDCS-15400012.summary: This is a public inscription recording the magistracy of Gaius Cartilius Poplicola, who served as duovir three times.

EDCS-15400544.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Vitorios Stephanus lived 33 years. Vitoria Ianuaria, his mother, for her most devoted son, and Aurelia Stephans for her father and sons, daughters, and their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-15400544.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Vitorios Stephanus, erected by his mother and grandmother, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15400546.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Ulpius [---], Aufidia [---], his wife [---], CI[---], B[---].
EDCS-15400546.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph mentioning Marcus Ulpius and Aufidia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15400547.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Ulpius Alexi, who lived 60 years and 3 months. Laelia Atrista, his wife, made this for him, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-15400547.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Ulpius Alexi, erected by his wife Laelia Atrista, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15400548.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Telesina, his wife, made this for Ulpius Bonioni, her incomparable husband, a well-deserving man. He lived 35 years.
EDCS-15400548.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpius Bonioni, erected by his wife Telesina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15400550.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpia Helpis made this for her husband, Marcus Ulpius Dexter, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-15400550.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Ulpius Dexter, erected by his wife Ulpia Helpis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15400551.translation: Ulpius Faustinus lived 17 years and 7 months. Ulpia Gemella made this.
EDCS-15400551.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpius Faustinus, erected by Ulpia Gemella, likely his mother or wife, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15400552.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Ulpius Liberalis, who lived 7 years and 59 days. Marcus Ulpius Epitynchanus and Ulpia Victoria, his slaves, for him, a well-deserving person.
EDCS-15400552.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Ulpius Liberalis, erected by his slaves Marcus Ulpius Epitynchanus and Ulpia Victoria, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15400553.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpia made this for her patron, Marcus Ulpius Nicephorus, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-15400553.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Ulpius Nicephorus, erected by his freedwoman Ulpia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15400554.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Ulpius Primitivus. Larcia Tertulla, his grandchild.
EDCS-15400554.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpius Primitivus, erected by his granddaughter Larcia Tertulla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15400555.translation: To the departed spirits. To Marcus Ulpius Telephus, his well-deserving wife Claudia Felicissima made this.
EDCS-15400555.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife to her husband.

EDCS-15400556.translation: To the departed spirits. To Marcus Ulpius Tertullus, son of Marcus Ulpius and grandson of Publius, who lived 18 years, 1 month, and 15 days. His most cherished son Ulpius Puteolanus made this.
EDCS-15400556.summary: A funerary dedication by a father to his son, specifying the deceased's age at death.

EDCS-15400557.translation: To the departed spirits. To Vippius Valerius, who lived 32 years, 2 months, and 5 days. His wife made this for him as a well-deserving husband.
EDCS-15400557.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife to her husband, specifying the deceased's age at death.

EDCS-15400558.translation: To the departed spirits. To Marcus Ulpius Zosimus. Marcus Ulpius Mamertinus made this.
EDCS-15400558.summary: A funerary dedication by a father (or relative with the same gentile name) to his son or relative.

EDCS-15400559.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Ulpia Beronice.
EDCS-15400559.summary: A funerary dedication to Ulpia Beronice, with the dedicant unspecified.

EDCS-15400560.translation: To the departed spirits. Organius Proculus made this for Ulpia Dorid, in memory.
EDCS-15400560.summary: A funerary dedication by Organius Proculus to Ulpia Dorid, likely a relative or beloved.

EDCS-15400561.translation: To the departed spirits. To Ulpia Euprepia, who lived 25 years, 3 months, and 5 days. Her father made this for her as a well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-15400561.summary: A funerary dedication by a father to his daughter, specifying her age at death.

EDCS-15400562.translation: Ulpia Ianuaria made this for her husband Laberius Tatianus, who lived 37 years and 5 months.
EDCS-15400562.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife to her husband, specifying his age at death.

EDCS-15400563.translation: To the departed spirits. To Ulpia Phoediana. Her husband.
EDCS-15400563.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband to his wife.

EDCS-15400564.translation: To the departed spirits. To Ulpia Sabina, who lived 11 years and 5 days.
EDCS-15400564.summary: A funerary dedication to Ulpia Sabina, specifying her age at death.

EDCS-15400565.translation: To the departed spirits. For Ulpia Tryphosa.
EDCS-15400565.summary: This is a funerary dedication (dis manibus) for Ulpia Tryphosa.

EDCS-15400567.translation: To the departed spirits. For Ulpia Vitalis, whom Ulpius Hermes made for his wife. She lived for 13 years and 7 months. For his daughter Seleuci.
EDCS-15400567.summary: This funerary dedication records the death of Ulpia Vitalis, daughter of Seleucus, who lived 13 years and 7 months, and was set up by her husband Ulpius Hermes.

EDCS-15400568.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aulus Umbricius Lupus, who lived 20 years and 10 days. Sallustia Restuta and Lupus set this up for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-15400568.summary: This funerary dedication records the life of Aulus Umbricius Lupus, who lived 20 years and 10 days, and was set up by his parents.

EDCS-15400569.translation: To the departed spirits. For Umbricia Afilia Iusta, who lived 15 years, 7 months, and 10 days. Aulus Umbricius Magnus and Clodia Felicitas, parents for their incomparable daughter, [did not set this up as] parents ought to set [it up] for their children, death intervened; the parents set it up for their daughter.
EDCS-15400569.summary: This funerary dedication for Umbricia Afilia Iusta, who lived 15 years, 7 months, and 10 days, was set up by her parents who express their grief at her early death.

EDCS-15400570.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Voconius Verus lived 55 years. Staphile his wife and Hygia his sister set this up for him as well-deserving.
EDCS-15400570.summary: This funerary dedication records that Quintus Voconius Verus lived 55 years and was set up by his wife and sister.

EDCS-15400571.translation: To the departed spirits. Volumnia Genna lived 19 years. Novicius, father for his most dutiful daughter, set this up.
EDCS-15400571.summary: This funerary dedication for Volumnia Genna, who lived 19 years, was set up by her father.

EDCS-15400572.translation: Volunilla the freedwoman is buried here.
EDCS-15400572.summary: This is a simple burial inscription for Volunilla, a freedwoman.

EDCS-15400574.translation: To the departed spirits. For Volussia Pollitta.
EDCS-15400574.summary: This is a funerary dedication (dis manibus) for Volussia Pollitta.

EDCS-15400575.translation: Let this tomb be frequented by those who are my children who are around me whom I wish to leave behind or to manumit, and afterwards to be open to them and to those who shall be born from them. Volusia Pia Annia [A?3]na, daughter of Quintus, I have so ordered, so let it be done. Sacred to the departed spirits.
EDCS-15400575.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription from Volusia Pia Annia concerning the use and manumission of slaves within her tomb, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15400576.translation: [She lived] ten months and seven days, she who endured sixteen full [years]. Her mother made a space [for] her most wretched [son]. Urbica Curtia(?) with her devoted husband [for themselves?].
EDCS-15400576.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a child who lived ten months and seven days after reaching sixteen years of age, and mentions her mother, a son, and a husband.

EDCS-15400577.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ursena Christa lived 27 years. Aelius Apollonides made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-15400577.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Aelius Apollonides for Ursena Christa, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-15400578.translation: [...] for his daughter San[3]ia, who lived [...] years. Zethus [...] parents [...]
EDCS-15400578.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph that likely records the death of a daughter named San[3]ia and mentions her parents.

EDCS-15400579.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Zeyxidamus and Gailla, parents, for their most dutiful daughter Epicharis, who lived 7 years, 3 months, 25 days.
EDCS-15400579.summary: A funerary epitaph for Epicharis, who died at the age of 7 years, 3 months, and 25 days, set up by her parents Zeyxidamus and Gailla.

EDCS-15400581.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia T[3]IA[ set this up for her well-deserving daughter Zosimena.
EDCS-15400581.summary: A funerary epitaph for Zosimena, likely set up by her mother Claudia T[3]IA[.

EDCS-15400582.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Zosime who lived 25 years, 5 months, 5 days.
EDCS-15400582.summary: A funerary epitaph for Zosime, who died at the age of 25 years, 5 months, and 5 days.

EDCS-15400583.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Arcanius made this for Zosimus, reverent and most dutiful.
EDCS-15400583.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Arcanius for Zosimus.

EDCS-15400584.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For their dearest son Zosimus, who lived 8 years, 2 months, 31 days. Dicaeus Sextior and Apisia Fortunata, parents.
EDCS-15400584.summary: A funerary epitaph for Zosimus, who died at the age of 8 years, 2 months, and 31 days, set up by his parents Dicaeus Sextior and Apisia Fortunata.

EDCS-15400650.translation: [...] for his [...] peregrine [...] Corn[elius] [...] Enco [...] for his spouse [...] to the spirits of the departed [...] Sex[tus] Cor[nelius] Enco [...]
EDCS-15400650.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph likely for a spouse, possibly named Enco, set up by Sextus Cornelius Enco.

EDCS-15400654.translation: [...] for himself and for Ota[...], freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-15400654.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph possibly recording a dedication for oneself and freedpersons.

EDCS-15400674.summary: A fragmentary inscription praising someone as worthy and incomparable.

EDCS-15400700.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary epitaph for a freedwoman named Livia Sabina, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-15400732.translation: to the man of most illustrious memory, [---]nilla
EDCS-15400732.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription honouring a deceased man of high status, possibly an epitaph, dating from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-15400736.translation: Here lies Formica. She died in peace, having lived more or less twenty years, and passed over pure to the gods; may her soul be among the righteous.
EDCS-15400736.summary: This is a Christian epitaph for a woman named Formica, who died around the age of twenty, likely dating from the 4th or 5th century CE.

EDCS-15400740.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-15400740.translation: Gaius Nonius Flavianus, having been sought after for his speeches for many years, lived for one year and eleven months. In his honour, this basilica was acquired and roofed by his parents. He rested in peace on the 18th day before the Kalends of January.
EDCS-15400740.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Gaius Nonius Flavianus, for whom his parents dedicated a basilica, dating to the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-15400742.translation: To Victoria, well deserving, in peace.
EDCS-15400742.summary: This is a short epitaph dedicated to a person named Victoria, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-15400765.translation: Valerius Valens, perfectissimus, prefect of the fleet of Misenum, Pious Avenger, Gordian, fulfilled his vow to the Great God and Good Fate. Valens, having received the rank of commander, erected this altar as prefect of the fleet of Misenum, fulfilling my vow.
EDCS-15400765.summary: This bilingual inscription (Latin and Greek) records a naval prefect named Valerius Valens fulfilling a vow to Deus Magnus and Fatus Bonus (or their Greek equivalents), likely dating to the reign of Gordian III.

EDCS-15500034.text_edition: \n Marci Noni Marci liberti Damae paries perpetuus privatus Iuliae paries privatus perpetuus\n
EDCS-15500034.translation: Marcus Noni, freedman of Marcus Dama, private wall, forever; Julia, private wall, forever.
EDCS-15500034.summary: This inscription likely marks boundary walls belonging to Marcus Noni and Julia, indicating private property ownership, possibly from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-15500036.text_edition: \n Corneliae Gratiae Marci Corneli Frontonis filiae\n
EDCS-15500036.translation: To Cornelia Gratia, daughter of Marcus Cornelius Fronto.
EDCS-15500036.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to Cornelia Gratia, daughter of Marcus Cornelius Fronto, likely an epitaph or a dedication of a monument, from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-15500134.text_edition: \n Quintus Caecilius Quinti libertus Alexander et Lucius Maclonius Luci libertus Mahes in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XVI\n
EDCS-15500134.translation: Quintus Caecilius Alexander, freedman of Quintus, and Lucius Maclonius Mahes, freedman of Lucius, twelve feet in front, sixteen feet in the field.
EDCS-15500134.summary: This inscription appears to define property boundaries related to Quintus Caecilius Alexander and Lucius Maclonius Mahes, indicating land measurements, possibly from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-15600515.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Caulius Aciba sibi fecit\n
EDCS-15600515.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Caulius Aciba made this for himself.
EDCS-15600515.summary: This is a funerary inscription (dis manibus) recording that Publius Caulius Aciba dedicated this monument for himself, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-15601032.translation: The collegium of armorers provided, constructed, and dedicated this wooden bridge, which for a long time had collapsed and was ruined by age, making passage impossible, to Flavius Marianus, most excellent, prefect of the fleet and curator of the Misenum republic, in honor of his merits. The most honorable order dedicated it to their worthy patron on the Ides of April in the consulship of Quintillus and Priscus.
EDCS-15601032.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a wooden bridge, restored by the collegium of armorers, to the prefect Flavius Marianus in Misenum, likely in the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-15601033.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aurelia Verecundinia Vera Agrippina, daughter of Marcus Verecundinius Verus, most dutiful, prefect of the rear division of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum. Gaius Valerius [---] Picentinus erected this for his wife, as a testament of his love.
EDCS-15601033.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Aurelia Verecundinia Vera Agrippina, daughter of a fleet official, erected by her husband Gaius Valerius Picentinus, likely in Misenum.

EDCS-15601036.translation: To the departed spirits. For Titus Flavius Antoninus, primus pilus of the I Adiutrix Legion, formerly chief boatswain of the fleet, who lived 70 years. His sons to their deserving father.
EDCS-15601036.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Titus Flavius Antoninus, a high-ranking soldier and naval officer, likely from the late 2nd or 3rd century CE, erected by his sons.

EDCS-15601037.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelius Candidus, boatswain and chief helmsman of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, and Aurelia Demetria, his wife, and their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants, erected this for themselves while still living.
EDCS-15601037.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Candidus, a naval officer at Misenum, his wife, and their descendants, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-15601039.translation: For Quintus Augusius Varus, who lived 14 years and 25 days. For Quintus Augusius Iulianus, who lived 9 years, 11 months, and 27 days. For Iulia Hilara, his only wife, an incomparable spouse, who lived 24 years, 11 months, and 9 days. For Quintus Augusius Varus, boatswain of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum.
EDCS-15601039.summary: This tomb inscription records the deaths of two young boys and a wife, dedicated by the father, Quintus Augusius Varus, a naval officer at Misenum.

EDCS-15601040.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Memorius, dearest son. Saturninus Isidorus, boatswain, and Vibia Serena, parents.
EDCS-15601040.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Memorius, a young boy, erected by his parents, Saturninus Isidorus, a naval officer, and Vibia Serena.

EDCS-15601041.translation: To the departed spirits. For Caius Arrius Aponianus, trierarch of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum.
EDCS-15601041.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caius Arrius Aponianus, a trierarch (naval officer) of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum.

EDCS-15601042.translation: To the departed spirits. For Felicionis, a most reverent youth, who lived 21 years, 4 months, and 28 days. Gaius Valerius Macrinus, trierarch of the Augusti, erected this for his deserving freedman.
EDCS-15601042.summary: This tomb inscription honors Felicionis, a young freedman, erected by his master Gaius Valerius Macrinus, a trierarch of the Augustan fleet.

EDCS-15601043.translation: To the departed spirits. For Fortunatus, a most reverent boy, who lived 15 years, 7 months, and 12 days. Caius Valerius Macrinus, trierarch, erected this for his deserving pupil.
EDCS-15601043.summary: This tomb inscription honors Fortunatus, a young boy, erected by his master Caius Valerius Macrinus, a trierarch, likely in Misenum.

EDCS-15601044.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aelia Chriseis, daughter of Aelius, a most chaste woman. Marcus Cocceius Stephanus, trierarch of the Augusti, erected this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-15601044.summary: This tomb inscription honors Aelia Chriseis, erected by her husband Marcus Cocceius Stephanus, a trierarch of the Augustan fleet, likely in Misenum.

EDCS-15601047.translation: To the departed spirits. Artemidorus, Julius, and Heraclidas, his heirs, had this made from 6,000 sesterces from his will for Gaius Julius Heraclidas, a trierarch of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum.
EDCS-15601047.summary: This is a tombstone erected by the heirs of Gaius Julius Heraclidas, a trierarch in the Misenum fleet, using funds from his will.

EDCS-15601048.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Terentianus, a tribune, was appointed to carry out the will of Gaius Julius Postumus, a soldier in the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, who ordered this monument or tomb to be made from 2,000 sesterces; the monument does not pass to the heir.
EDCS-15601048.summary: This epitaph records a soldier of the Misenum fleet, Gaius Julius Postumus, whose tomb was funded by his will and overseen by a tribune.

EDCS-15601050.translation: To the departed spirits. For his wife Stonicia Ansia Heliana Onomasta, without whose presence life is empty. Gaius Antonius Atinianus, a tribune.
EDCS-15601050.summary: An epitaph dedicated by Gaius Antonius Atinianus, possibly a tribune, to his wife Stonicia Ansia Heliana Onomasta.

EDCS-15601051.translation: For Valeria Severa, daughter of a trierarch, who lived 1 year and 5 months.
EDCS-15601051.summary: An epitaph for Valeria Severa, the daughter of a trierarch, recording her young age at death.

EDCS-15601053.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gnaeus Arrius Myron Formianus, a veteran centurion of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, who served on the four-banked ship Vesta, lived 50 years, 8 months, and 12 days. His wife Valeria Crispina made this for him, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-15601053.summary: This tombstone commemorates Gnaeus Arrius Myron Formianus, a veteran centurion of the Misenum fleet, erected by his wife.

EDCS-15601054.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aurelia Iulia, who lived 6 years and 5 months. Aurelius Macedo, a centurion, made this for his dearest, well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-15601054.summary: A tombstone for Aurelia Iulia, who died at a young age, erected by her father Aurelius Macedo, a centurion.

EDCS-15601055.translation: For [C(aius?)] Coelius Dorg[ius?], centurion of the Praetorian fleet, who lived 60 years. [S(extus?)]cius Severinus, freedman of [C(aius?) C]otris, [erected this...]
