EDCS-15601055.summary: A fragmentary epitaph for a centurion of the Praetorian fleet, possibly named Coelius Dorgius, who lived to 60.

EDCS-15601056.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Marius Pudentis, a centurion and sailor of the Misenum fleet, who lived 22 years and served 4 years. Scentia Marcia, his mother, made this for her most dutiful son, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-15601056.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Marius Pudentis, a young centurion and sailor in the Misenum fleet, erected by his mother.

EDCS-15601057.translation: To the departed spirits. For Titus Minucius Saturninus, a veteran centurion, for Helpidius, his slave, and for himself, and for his freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-15601057.summary: An epitaph for Titus Minucius Saturninus, a veteran centurion, who also made provisions for his slave, himself, and his freedmen and their descendants.

EDCS-15601058.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Salvius Pudentis, a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum from the trireme Concordia, of the Besso nation, who lived 35 years and served 16 years. Barbius Crescens, a veteran centurion of the same fleet, his heir, made this for him, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-15601058.summary: This tombstone is for Lucius Salvius Pudentis, a soldier from the Misenum fleet of Besso nationality, erected by his heir, a veteran centurion.

EDCS-15601059.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Sestius Pudens, a veteran centurion from the Praetorian fleet at Misenum. He served 22 years, lived 49 years. His freedmen Agathopus and Bassus for their deserving patron and his descendants.
EDCS-15601059.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Sestius Pudens, a veteran centurion of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, set up by his freedmen.

EDCS-15601060.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Suttis Valens, a Cilician by birth, a centurion from the quinquereme Vesta. He lived 35 years, served 13 years, at...
EDCS-15601060.summary: This incomplete tombstone records the life and service of Marcus Suttis Valens, a centurion of likely naval origin.

EDCS-15601061.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Terentia Secundilla, who lived 18 years and 6 months. Iulius Athenodorus, a retired centurion, made this for his sweetest, deserving wife.
EDCS-15601061.summary: This tombstone commemorates Terentia Secundilla, the wife of a retired centurion named Iulius Athenodorus.

EDCS-15601062.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Decimus Tullius Aelianus, a centurion from the trireme Iuno, a Bessian by birth. He lived 45 years, served 25 years. Clodia Fadiana made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-15601062.summary: This tombstone records the life and service of Decimus Tullius Aelianus, a centurion of naval origin, erected by his wife.

EDCS-15601063.translation: For Lucius Valerius Dazantis, his son, Hispano by birth, a centurion from the trireme Neptuno. He served 37 years, lived 55 years. From Pannonia, his home was Flavia Sirmi. Iunia Hygia made this for her deserving husband concerning him.
EDCS-15601063.summary: This tombstone commemorates Lucius Valerius Dazantis, a centurion of naval origin, by his wife Iunia Hygia.

EDCS-15601065.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Antonius Leo, also called Neon, son of Zoilos, a Cilician by birth, a soldier of the Praetorian fleet of Misenum, in the trireme Asclepio. He lived 27 years, served 9 years. Gaius Iulius Paulus, his heir, took care of this.
EDCS-15601065.summary: This tombstone records the life and service of Lucius Antonius Leo, a soldier in the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, whose heir arranged the burial.

EDCS-15601066.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Antonius Priscus, who lived 8 years, 9 months, 15 days. Marcus Antonius Gemellus, centurion of the trireme Neptuno, and Antonia Prisca, his parents, for their most dutiful son.
EDCS-15601066.summary: This tombstone is for Marcus Antonius Priscus, a child who lived just over 8 years, erected by his parents, one of whom was a centurion.

EDCS-15601067.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aurelia Fabia, his wife. Memmius Magnus, a soldier of the trireme Hercules, made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-15601067.summary: This tombstone commemorates Aurelia Fabia, the wife of Memmius Magnus, a soldier in the navy.

EDCS-15601068.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cattius Sabinus Sossius, an incomparable son, who lived 11 years, 4 months, 8 days, 6 hours. Cattius Sossius Felix, scribe and father, and Fannia Sabina, mother, made this for their good and most innocent, deserving son.
EDCS-15601068.summary: This tombstone honors Cattius Sabinus Sossius, a young boy, erected by his father, a scribe, and his mother.

EDCS-15601069.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Hammonius Fortis, a centurion of the trireme Spe, an Egyptian by birth. He lived 38 years, in which he served 18 years. Gaius Petronius Clemens, optio of the trireme Diana, made this for his deserving friend.
EDCS-15601069.summary: This tombstone commemorates Gaius Hammonius Fortis, a centurion of naval origin, erected by his friend, an optio.

EDCS-15601070.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Meus Atime tianus of the III Trireme *centuria* Venere made this for his well-deserving wife, Rocula.
EDCS-15601070.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his deceased wife.

EDCS-15601071.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Valerius Priscus, a *centurion* of the III Trireme *centuria* Apoll(i)ne, an Egyptian by birth, served for 22 years and lived for 43 years. His freedman and heir, Hritus, made this for him, a well-deserving person.
EDCS-15601071.summary: A funerary dedication for a deceased centurion, specifying his origin, service length, and age, made by his freedman and heir.

EDCS-15601072.translation: To the departed spirits. For Ulpia Primigenia, a most holy woman, who lived for 30 years. Iulius Severus, a *centurion* of the III Trireme *centuria* Lucifero, made this for his dearest and most loving wife, a well-deserving person.
EDCS-15601072.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his deceased wife, mentioning her age and character.

EDCS-15601073.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Antonius Apoll[onius(?)] the helmsman, of the *centuria* Ar[rius(?)]...
EDCS-15601073.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication for a helmsman, possibly a centurion, indicating his name and unit.

EDCS-15601074.translation: To the departed spirits. For Titus Attius Nepos, who Zecaepr(?), a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, of the *centuria* Iulius Quinti, who lived for 50 years and served for 27 years and 6 months. Domitius Draco, his heir, made this for him, a well-deserving person.
EDCS-15601074.summary: A funerary dedication for a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, detailing his age, service length, and the monument's maker.

EDCS-15601075.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Pomponius Varus, a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, of the *centuria* Caius Tarulius Vitalis, who lived for 35 years and served for 9 years. Gaius Aeternius Rufus, Lucius Statius Valens of the same *centuria*, Marcus Musidius Petra of the *centuria* Clementis, his heirs, made this for him, a well-deserving person.
EDCS-15601075.summary: A funerary dedication for a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, specifying his age, service length, and naming multiple heirs who erected the monument.

EDCS-15601076.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Valerius Maximus, a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, of the *centuria* Nigrinus, a Greek by birth, who lived for 46 years and served for 25 years. Valerius Maximus, in the name of his sons Albanius and Lucrio, took care to have this made.
EDCS-15601076.summary: A funerary dedication for a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, detailing his age, service length, origin, and noting that his sons commissioned the monument.

EDCS-15601077.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Urbinus Quartinus, a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, of the *centuria* Faenius Iustus, an African by birth, who lived for 60 years and served for 25 years. Lucius Valerius Saturninus, his heir, made this for him, a well-deserving person.
EDCS-15601077.summary: A funerary dedication for a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, specifying his origin, age, service length, and the heir who erected the monument.

EDCS-15601079.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Naevius Asclepiades, adjutant to a tribune of the IIII Quadriere *centuria* Venere, who lived for 22 years and 5 months.
EDCS-15601079.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Naevius Asclepiades, an adjutant to a tribune, mentioning his age and unit.

EDCS-15601082.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Aelius, who lived for 26 years. Gaius Aelius Alexander made this for his well-deserving brother, Lucifero, of the III Trireme *centuria*.
EDCS-15601082.summary: A funerary dedication by a brother for his deceased brother, specifying his age and the brother's unit.

EDCS-15601083.translation: To the departed spirits. For Antonia Domina, who lived 19 years, wife of Camurius Colo, trireme officer of the armoury. To Lucifer, her most holy husband, and to Antonius Colo, for his most dutiful mother, well-deserving, he made this. (Date uncertain, likely Roman Imperial period).
EDCS-15601083.summary: A funerary monument set up by a son for his mother and her husband, both of whom were associated with the armoury.

EDCS-15601084.translation: To the departed spirits. For Caius Arrius Ammonianus, a veteran of the armoury, an Egyptian by birth, who lived 52 years. Arria Zosime made this for her well-deserving patron. (Date uncertain, likely Roman Imperial period).
EDCS-15601084.summary: A funerary monument for an Egyptian veteran of the armoury, set up by his patron.

EDCS-15601085.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Aurelius Ilettis, custos armorum (armoury keeper) of the liburna ship Virtus, a Pontic by birth, who lived 38 years and 2 months and 15 days, with 16 years of service. Pæstania Sozusa, his wife, made this for the well-deserving. (Date uncertain, likely Roman Imperial period).
EDCS-15601085.summary: A funerary monument for an armoury keeper of Pontic origin, erected by his wife.

EDCS-15601086.translation: To the departed spirits. For Titus Autronius Pacatus, trireme officer of the armoury Silvanus, who served 23 years and lived 41 years. Ulpia Eufrantus made this for her most cherished husband. (Date uncertain, likely Roman Imperial period).
EDCS-15601086.summary: A funerary monument for Titus Autronius Pacatus, a trireme officer in the armoury, erected by his wife.

EDCS-15601087.translation: To the departed spirits. For Coniete Sandona, a Cilician by birth, who lived 60 years. Aurelius Marinus, trireme officer of the armoury Perseus, her procurator, made this for the well-deserving. (Date uncertain, likely Roman Imperial period).
EDCS-15601087.summary: A funerary monument for Coniete Sandona, a Cilician, erected by her procurator who also served as a trireme officer in the armoury.

EDCS-15601088.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Flavius Capito, custos armorum (armoury keeper) of the liburna ship Virtus, a Pamphylian by birth, who lived 50 years and served 24 years. Decia Fortunata made this for her well-deserving husband. (Date uncertain, likely Roman Imperial period).
EDCS-15601088.summary: A funerary monument for Marcus Flavius Capito, an armoury keeper of Pamphylian origin, erected by his wife.

EDCS-15601089.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Gargilius Felix, trireme officer of the armoury Satyra, an African by birth, who had 28 years of service and lived 53 years and 2 months. Marcus Naerius Quadratus, optio of the trireme Tigris and heir, made this for the well-deserving. (Date uncertain, likely Roman Imperial period).
EDCS-15601089.summary: A funerary monument for Marcus Gargilius Felix, an African trireme officer in the armoury, erected by his heir who was also an optio.

EDCS-15601090.translation: To the departed spirits. For Hermetus, a slave, who lived 19 years. Valerius Clemens, custos armorum (armoury keeper) of the trireme Mercurius, made this for the well-deserving. (Date uncertain, likely Roman Imperial period).
EDCS-15601090.summary: A funerary monument for a slave named Hermetus, erected by Valerius Clemens, an armoury keeper.

EDCS-15601091.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Lucilius Lucianus, trireme officer of the armoury Salute, a Cilician by birth, who lived 35 years and served 10 years. Gaius Helvius Julianus, heir and second in command of the armoury, made this for the well-deserving. (Date uncertain, likely Roman Imperial period).
EDCS-15601091.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Lucilius Lucianus, a Cilician trireme officer in the armoury, erected by his heir who also served in the armoury.

EDCS-15601092.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Peticius Heraclus, an Egyptian by birth, armorum trireme officer Athenonica(?), who lived 30 years and served 12 years. Valeria Theodote, his wife and heir, made this for the well-deserving. (Date uncertain, likely Roman Imperial period).
EDCS-15601092.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Peticius Heraclus, an Egyptian armoury officer, erected by his wife and heir.

EDCS-15601093.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Marcus Popilius, of the rowing crew of the *Vipentera Victoria*, who lived 40 years. Marcus Appius Honoratus [---?]
EDCS-15601093.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Popilius, who served in the *Vipentera Victoria* and lived 40 years, with a partial dedication from Marcus Appius Honoratus.

EDCS-15601094.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Decimus Publicius Aper, overseer of arms on a trireme, for himself and for his excellent freedwoman, Publicia Tyche.
EDCS-15601094.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Decimus Publicius Aper, an overseer of arms on a trireme, dedicated to himself and his freedwoman, Publicia Tyche.

EDCS-15601095.translation: To Titus Suillius Albanus, also called Timotheus, son of Meniscus, a Nicaean by birth, overseer of arms on a liburnian galley. He served 25 years and lived 50 years. His heirs, Antonius Quadratus and Aemilius Quadratus, overseers of arms on a quadrireme *Minerva*, and his freedwoman Suillia Eugenia, made this for him deserving of their kindness.
EDCS-15601095.summary: This tombstone was erected by the heirs and freedwoman for Titus Suillius Albanus, a Nicaean who served as an overseer of arms on naval vessels for 25 years and lived 50 years.

EDCS-15601096.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Valerius Antoninus of the rowing crew of the trireme *Rheno*, a Syrian by birth, who lived 38 years and served 18 years. His heir made this for him deserving of kindness.
EDCS-15601096.summary: This tombstone inscription is for Marcus Valerius Antoninus, a Syrian who served as a rower on the trireme *Rheno* for 18 years and lived 38 years.

EDCS-15601098.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Casta, an excellent woman, with whom I lived 22 years without any discord. Decimus Julius Doles, her husband, a veteran overseer of arms, made this for her deserving of kindness.
EDCS-15601098.summary: This is a tombstone for Valeria Casta, set up by her husband, Decimus Julius Doles, a veteran overseer of arms, commemorating their 22 years of marriage.

EDCS-15601099.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Veridia Flora, daughter of Lucius, who lived 27 years, 9 months, and 5 days. Quintus Avidius Aprilis, overseer of arms on the *Vipentera Victoria*, to his good wife.
EDCS-15601099.summary: This tombstone is for Veridia Flora, who lived 27 years, 9 months, and 5 days, and was dedicated by her husband Quintus Avidius Aprilis, an overseer of arms.

EDCS-15601100.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Quintus Naevius Marcianus, a soldier of the praetorian fleet of Misenum, a staff officer, who lived 22 years, 2 months, and 23 days.
EDCS-15601100.summary: This tombstone inscription records the life and death of Quintus Naevius Marcianus, a soldier and staff officer in the Misenum fleet, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-15700142.text_edition: \n Crispini Lucio Mamilieno Crispino viro perfectissimo sacerdoti sanctissimarum Fortunarum curatori sacro iudicio promoto patriae nostrae quua etiam civitatis Sinuessanium omnibus honoribus functo ob meritum originis suae et laborum suorum patrono praestantissimo statuam scribend \n
EDCS-15700142.translation: To Lucius Mamilianus Crispinus, most distinguished man, priest of the most sacred Fortunes, curator appointed by sacred judgment for our fatherland, who also held all the offices of the citizenship of Sinuessa, most outstanding patron by virtue of his origin and his services, a statue was erected.
EDCS-15700142.summary: This is a dedication for Lucius Mamilianus Crispinus, a distinguished man who served as a priest and held various civic offices in Sinuessa.

EDCS-15800330.text_edition: \n Decimus Numisius Decimi libertus Antiochus pistor Mar<c=G>ia Luci liberta Stratonice uxor \n
EDCS-15800330.translation: Decimus Numisius Antiochus, freedman of Decimus, a baker, and Stratonice, freedwoman of Lucius Marcius, his wife.
EDCS-15800330.summary: This inscription identifies Decimus Numisius Antiochus, a baker, and his wife Stratonice, noting their status as freedpersons.

EDCS-15800332.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudiani Thaumasti Augusti vikari sic vixit nnos XIIII PCCLED Appuleio Felice\n
EDCS-15800332.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudianus Thaumastus, Augusti's vicar, who lived 14 years, [---]. To Appuleius Felix.
EDCS-15800332.summary: This tombstone inscription is for Claudianus Thaumastus, identified as Augusti's vicar, who died at the age of 14, with a partial dedication to Appuleius Felix.

EDCS-15800333.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Macarei Thaumasti Augusti vi<k=R>(arii) sic vixit annos XIIII \n
EDCS-15800333.translation: To the Divine Manes. Macarea Thaumasti, wife of Augusti the charioteer, lived 14 years.
EDCS-15800333.summary: This is a tombstone for Macarea, a young girl who died at the age of 14, belonging to the late Roman period.

EDCS-15800334.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai et Luci Sertori sic libertus Apella sepulchrum inferundi humandi leiberteis leibertabusque sueis et leibertorum leiberteis et Caio Iulio Cissi liberto Pamphilo mensori et Aulo Terentio Auli liberto Nicomedi datum sei quis leibertorum meorum conleibertum suom aut eorum quem queis supra scriptis inferri prohibuerit ei ipsi qui prohibuerit eo inferundei ius potestasque ne esto\n
EDCS-15800334.translation: Gaius Julius Apella, freedman of Gaius and Lucius Sertorius, and his freedmen and freedwomen and their freedmen and freedwomen, and Pamphilus, freedman of Gaius Julius Cissus, a measurer, and Nicomedes, freedman of Aulus Terentius, established this tomb. If any of my freedmen prevents the burial of a fellow freedman or any of those listed above, he himself who prevents it shall not have the right or power to be buried.
EDCS-15800334.summary: This funerary inscription from the Roman Republic or early Empire details the burial rights for a freedman and his descendants and freedmen.

EDCS-15800336.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Auctus Augusti libertus Pomponiae symposium optimae coniugi et sibi\n
EDCS-15800336.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Auctus, freedman of Augustus, dedicated this to Pomponia, his excellent wife, and to himself.
EDCS-15800336.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to a wife by her husband, Auctus, a freedman of Augustus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800337.text_edition: \n Platoni Caesaris dietarchae fecit sic Olympicus vilicus et Ilus\n
EDCS-15800337.translation: Plato, the Caesar's chief steward, made this. Olympicus the steward and Ilus [also made this].
EDCS-15800337.summary: This inscription records the makers of a dedication to Plato, the Caesar's chief steward, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800421.text_edition: \n Ddomini sic nnostri sic Flavius Constantinus maximus Pius Felix victor Augustus et Flavius Iulius Crispus et Flavius Claudius Constantinus nobbilissimi sic Caessares sic fontis Augustei aquaeductum longa incuria et vetustate conruptum pro magnificentia liberalitatis consuetae sua pecunia refici iusserunt et usui civitatium infra scriptarum reddiderunt dedicante Ceionio Iuliano viro clarissimo consulari Campaniae curante Pontiano viro perfectissimo praeposito eiusdem aquaeductus nomina civitatium Puteolana Neapolitana Nolana Atellana Cumana Acerrana Baiana Misenum\n
EDCS-15800421.translation: Our Lords, Flavius Constantinus Maximus Pius Felix Victor Augustus, and Flavius Julius Crispus and Flavius Claudius Constantinus, most noble Caesars, ordered the Aqua Marcia, corrupted by long neglect and age, to be repaired with their own money, in keeping with their customary magnificence and generosity, and restored it for the use of the cities listed below, with Ceionius Julianus, a most illustrious man and consular governor of Campania, dedicating it, and Pontianus, a most accomplished man and overseer of the same aqueduct, managing it. The names of the cities are: Puteoli, Neapolis, Nolana, Atella, Cumae, Acerrae, Baiae, Misenum.
EDCS-15800421.summary: This inscription from the Constantinian period records the restoration of the Aqua Marcia by Emperors Constantine, Crispus, and Constantine II, benefiting several cities in Campania.

EDCS-15900001.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valerius Silvanus, of Italian origin, who lived 37 years, served as a volunteer on the warship 'Neptune' for 15 years. Ortesia Celerina made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-15900001.summary: This is a tombstone for Valerius Silvanus, a sailor in the Roman navy who died at 37, erected by his wife, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900002.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Eutychis, son of Caius Atinius, a beneficer of the stolarchi, who lived 22 years and 8 months, served [3] years. Gracilia Galla for her most devoted and incomparable son.
EDCS-15900002.summary: This funerary inscription is for Eutychis, the son of Caius Atinius, a sailor who died at age 22, erected by his mother, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900003.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Lucius Julius Valens, a duplicarius, a mason from the praetorian fleet of Misenum, of Syrian origin, who lived 40 years and served 22 years. Flavia Marina, his wife, [erected this] for her deserving husband.
EDCS-15900003.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lucius Julius Valens, a sailor in the Misenum fleet and a mason, who died at 40, erected by his wife, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900004.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Annia Tertia, dearest wife. Marcus Annius Verus, a cornicularius of the prefect, made this.
EDCS-15900004.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Annia Tertia by her husband, Marcus Annius Verus, a high-ranking soldier, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900005.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Gaius Arrius Montanus, a veteran duplicarius from the Misenum fleet, of Nicaean origin. Valeria Doris from Greece, to her best and deserving husband, erected this monument. // To the Divine Manes. For Montanus, a veteran duplicarius, Valeria Doris, to her deserving husband, the duplicarius, made this.
EDCS-15900005.summary: This funerary inscription is for Gaius Arrius Montanus, a veteran sailor from the Misenum fleet, erected by his wife Valeria Doris, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900006.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Sempronius Severus erected this for his heir, Gaius Nonius Calvisius, a veteran of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, who served for 29 years, lived for 53 years, and was an evocatus (a recalled soldier).
EDCS-15900006.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Nonius Calvisius, a veteran of the Misenum fleet, erected by his heir.

EDCS-15900007.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Longinius Clemens and Gaius Julius Maximus, heirs, erected this for their well-deserving kinsman, Gaius Julius Germanus, a smith.
EDCS-15900007.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Germanus, a smith, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-15900008.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Paconius Graptus erected this for his well-deserving patron, Publius Paconius Niger, a smith from the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, a native of Nicaea, who lived for 50 years and served for 28 years.
EDCS-15900008.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Paconius Niger, a smith from the Misenum fleet, erected by his patron.

EDCS-15900009.translation: Marcus Plotius Augustalis, his son, erected this for his patron and possibly his father, Marcus Plotus Firmus, a smith from the four-banked ship Venus, who lived for 68 years and served for 48 years. The son Marcus Plotus Augustalis lived for 19 years. Plotia Tyche erected this for her patron and son.
EDCS-15900009.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Plotius Firmus, a smith, and his son Marcus Plotius Augustalis, erected by Plotia Tyche.

EDCS-15900011.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavius Marcellus, a smith and duplicarius (a sailor with double pay), erected this for his well-deserving Caius Arule(n)us Restitutus, a manipularis of a trireme, from Libertate, an African by birth, who served for 10 years and lived for 30 years.
EDCS-15900011.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Arule(n)us Restitutus, a sailor, erected by Flavius Marcellus.

EDCS-15900012.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Carisius Fronto, heir, erected this for his well-deserving Lucius Aurelius Fortis, a smith and duplicarius of the liburnian ship Fides, a Sardinian by birth, who lived for 52 years and served for 25 years, under the care of his most excellent friend Arruntius Petronianus.
EDCS-15900012.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Aurelius Fortis, a smith, erected by his heir Marcus Carisius Fronto, with a mention of a friend.

EDCS-15900013.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Ulpiane erected this for her well-deserving father, Iulius Antiochus, a smith and duplicarius, a native of Cilicia, who lived for 60 years and served for 25 years.
EDCS-15900013.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulius Antiochus, a smith, erected by his daughter Iulia Ulpiane.

EDCS-15900014.translation: To the spirits of the departed. The son, Iulius Ponticus, and the wife, Antonia Callityche, heirs, erected this for their well-deserving Caius Iulius Ponticus, a smith and duplicarius of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, a native of Pontus, who lived for 44 years and served for 22 years.
EDCS-15900014.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Iulius Ponticus, a smith in the Misenum fleet, erected by his son and wife.

EDCS-15900015.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Barbius Firmus, a smith and duplicarius of the four-banked ship Dacicus, erected this for his most devoted and pious freedwoman Marcia Euhodia, who lived for 40 years and 3 months, well-deserving of him.
EDCS-15900015.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcia Euhodia, a freedwoman, erected by her patron and husband, Barbius Firmus.

EDCS-15900016.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Saeniu[s] [3]mulus, heir, erected this for his well-deserving Titus Iulius Proculus, a principalis smith of the trireme Concordia, a Syrian by birth, who served for 27 years and lived for 50 years.
EDCS-15900016.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Iulius Proculus, a smith, erected by his heir Caius Saeniu[s] [3]mulus.

EDCS-15900017.translation: To the departed spirits. Aemilia Amabiles made this monument, of which half was purchased, for her patron and governor of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, Marcus Aemilius Pudens, a veteran, and for herself, her freedmen, and all their freedmen.
EDCS-15900017.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Aemilius Pudens, a veteran governor of the Misenum fleet, set up by Aemilia Amabiles.

EDCS-15900018.translation: Marcus Antonius Lupus [made this] for Marcus Antonius Lupus, governor of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, his well-deserving father.
EDCS-15900018.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Marcus Antonius Lupus for his father, also named Marcus Antonius Lupus, who was a governor of the Misenum fleet.

EDCS-15900019.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudius Gallicanus Diocles, a veteran and governor, lived 75 years, 4 months, and 15 days. Hordonia Primigenia, his wife, and Claudia Poe, his daughter, his heirs, made this for him, his well-deserving husband.
EDCS-15900019.summary: This tombstone records the life and death of Claudius Gallicanus Diocles, a veteran governor, and was erected by his wife and daughter.

EDCS-15900020.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Crispus, governor, made this for Caius Julius Diogenes, his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-15900020.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Julius Crispus, a governor, made by his mother.

EDCS-15900021.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Diogenes, son, made this for his well-deserving father, Gaius Julius Diogenes, a governor of a trireme at Hercules.
EDCS-15900021.summary: This funerary monument was created by Gaius Julius Diogenes for his father, also named Gaius Julius Diogenes, a governor of a trireme.

EDCS-15900022.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia Nicopolis made this for her well-deserving husband, Iulius Felix, of Afer nationality, who served 14 years as a governor.
EDCS-15900022.summary: This tombstone commemorates Iulius Felix, an Afer who served 14 years as a governor, and was erected by his wife Flavia Nicopolis.

EDCS-15900023.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Percennius Maximus, governor of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, lived 36 years and served [---] years.
EDCS-15900023.summary: This inscription records the life and service of Lucius Percennius Maximus, a governor of the Misenum fleet, who died at age 36.

EDCS-15900024.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Marciana made this for her well-deserving husband, Gaius Pomponius Felix, of Afer nationality, who lived 40 years, 8 months, and 5 days, and served 23 years, 8 months as a governor of the praetorian fleet at Misenum.
EDCS-15900024.summary: This funerary monument honors Gaius Pomponius Felix, an Afer and governor of the Misenum fleet, erected by his wife Iulia Marciana.

EDCS-15900025.translation: To the departed spirits. The heirs made this for Sextus Sallustius Faustus, a soldier and governor of a quadrireme, who served 26 years and lived 50 years.
EDCS-15900025.summary: This inscription records the death of Sextus Sallustius Faustus, a soldier and governor of a quadrireme, and was erected by his heirs.

EDCS-15900026.translation: To the departed spirits. Vibi Primus and Mansuetus, sons and heirs, made this for their father, Publius Vibius Primus, a governor of a trireme, who lived 45 years and served 26 years.
EDCS-15900026.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Vibi Primus and Mansuetus for their father, Publius Vibius Primus, a governor of a trireme.

EDCS-15900028.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Ermogenia and her sons made this for their well-deserving father, Lucius Aurelius Titus, a soldier of the Praetorian fleet of Misenum, a duplicated clerk who lived about 40 years, 5 months, and 1 day.
EDCS-15900028.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Lucius Aurelius Titus, a soldier and clerk in the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, by his wife and sons.

EDCS-15900030.translation: To the incomparable [---] who lived 55 years, and to Gaius Julius Marcianus, the chief clerk, who lived 23 years. The brothers made this for their sweetest brother, well-deserving.
EDCS-15900030.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by brothers to their deceased brother and to Gaius Julius Marcianus, a chief clerk, likely within the same family context.

EDCS-15900031.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaio Acilio Basso medico duplicario collegae eius\n
EDCS-15900031.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Acilius Bassus, a duplicated physician, and to his colleague.
EDCS-15900031.summary: A funerary dedication to Gaius Acilius Bassus, a duplicated physician, and his colleague, likely from the Roman navy.

EDCS-15900032.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Satrius Longinus, duplicated physician of the trireme 'Cupid', and his freedwoman Iulia Veria, erected this for their well-deserving Iulia Veneria.
EDCS-15900032.summary: A funerary monument for Iulia Veneria, erected by Marcus Satrius Longinus, a duplicated physician, and his freedwoman, likely a partner or relative.

EDCS-15900034.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Iulius Casullinus, a duplicated physician, made this for his well-deserving wife, Valeria Isidora, a native slave of Misenum, who lived 18 years, 5 months, and 8 days.
EDCS-15900034.summary: A funerary monument for Valeria Isidora, a native slave, erected by her husband Marcus Iulius Casullinus, a duplicated physician.

EDCS-15900035.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Antonia Elpidia, wife and heir, made this for her well-deserving husband, Aurelius Marullus, a shipmaster of the trireme 'Victoria', of Cilician origin, who lived 45 years, 6 months, and 7 days, and served 25 years.
EDCS-15900035.summary: A funerary monument for Aurelius Marullus, a shipmaster of the trireme 'Victoria' of Cilician origin, erected by his wife and heir.

EDCS-15900036.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Antiiherax, shipmaster of the trireme 'Augusta', made this for his well-deserving wife, Cornelia Dionysias, a native slave of Puteoli, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-15900036.summary: A funerary monument for Cornelia Dionysias, a native slave, erected by her husband Antiiherax, a shipmaster of the trireme 'Augusta'.

EDCS-15900037.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titia Trofime, wife and heir, made this for her well-deserving Lucius Crispius Alexander, a shipmaster of the trireme 'Taurus', who served 22 years and lived 43 years.
EDCS-15900037.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Crispius Alexander, a shipmaster of the trireme 'Taurus', erected by his wife and heir Titia Trofime.

EDCS-15900038.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caesellius Pomponius, the shipmaster, made this for his well-deserving wife, Furia Saturnina, incomparable, who lived 46 years, 3 months, and 15 days, without complaint.
EDCS-15900038.summary: A funerary monument for Furia Saturnina, erected by her husband Caesellius Pomponius, identified as a shipmaster.

EDCS-15900040.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Iulius Marinus, brother and heir, made this for his well-deserving Gaius Iulius Marinus, a shipmaster of the trireme 'Augustus', of Syrian origin, with 12 years of service.
EDCS-15900040.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Iulius Marinus, a shipmaster of Syrian origin, erected by his brother and heir Marcus Iulius Marinus.

EDCS-15900374.text_edition: \n Cnaeum Helvium Sabinum aedilem oramus vos faciatis Urbulanenses rogant\n
EDCS-15900374.translation: We ask you, people of Urbulanum, to make Gnaeus Helvius Sabinus an aedile.
EDCS-15900374.summary: This inscription is a plea from the people of Urbulanum to elect Gnaeus Helvius Sabinus as aedile.

EDCS-15900380.text_edition: \n Rufum duumvirum iure dicundo a balneo rogant\n
EDCS-15900380.translation: They ask for Rufus to be duumvir iure dicundo from the bathhouse.
EDCS-15900380.summary: This inscription requests Rufus to serve as duumvir iure dicundo, possibly related to a bathhouse context.

EDCS-16000237.summary: This is a label for a pot belonging to Tiberius Claudius Amarantus.

EDCS-16000299.text_edition: \n Furiae Sabiniae Tranquillinae Augustae sanctissimae coniugi Imperatoris Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti Minturnenses publice\n
EDCS-16000299.translation: The people of Minturnae publicly honor Furia Sabinia Tranquillina, the most holy Augusta and wife of the Emperor Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-16000299.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the people of Minturnae to Furia Sabinia Tranquillina, wife of Emperor Gordianus.

EDCS-16000300.text_edition: \n Aurelio Auggustorum liberto Alexandro viro egregio praeposito sacrarum cognitionum viro optimo et nno erga patriam honorificentissimo decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-16000300.translation: To Aurelius Alexander, freedman of the Augusti, an outstanding man, Prefect of Sacred Inquiries, a most excellent and honored man for his country, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-16000300.summary: This inscription is a dedication by decree of the decurions to Aurelius Alexander, a distinguished official.

EDCS-16000301.text_edition: \n Lucio Ovinio Luci filio Quirina Rustico Corneliano consuli designato curatori viae Flaminiae legato legionis primae Italicae praetori curatori viae Valeriae allecto inter tribunicios quaestori Rufria Luci filia Secundilla marito incomparabili locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-16000301.translation: To Lucius Ovinus Lucius filius Quirina Rusticus Cornelius, consul-designate, curator of the Flaminian Way, legate of the First Italian Legion, praetor, curator of the Valeria Way, elected among the tribunes, quaestor, by Rufria Lucius filia Secundilla, to her incomparable husband, a place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-16000301.summary: This inscription records the granting of a place by decree of the decurions to Lucius Ovinus Lucius filius Quirina Rusticus Cornelius, honoring his numerous public offices.

EDCS-16000302.text_edition: \n Magno et Invicto Imperatori Caesari [] Pio Felici Augusto colonia Minturnae devota numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-16000302.translation: The colony of Minturnae is devoted to the divine majesty of the Great and Invincible Emperor Caesar Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-16000302.summary: This inscription expresses the devotion of the colony of Minturnae to Emperor Caesar Pius Felix Augustus.

EDCS-16000303.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Galerio Valerio Maximino nobilissimo Caesari colonia Minturnensium\n
EDCS-16000303.translation: To our Lord Galerius Valerius Maximinus, the most noble Caesar, from the colony of Minturnae.
EDCS-16000303.summary: This inscription is a dedication from the colony of Minturnae to the Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximinus.

EDCS-16000304.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Severo nobilissimo Caesari colonia Minturnensium\n
EDCS-16000304.translation: To our Lord Flavius Valerius Severus, the most noble Caesar, from the colony of Minturnae.
EDCS-16000304.summary: This inscription is a dedication from the colony of Minturnae to the Caesar Flavius Valerius Severus.

EDCS-16000306.text_edition: \n Pompeiae Quinti filiae Sosiae Falconillae Quinti Pompei Sosii Prisci consulis filiae Quinti Pompei Falconis consulis nepti Quinti Sosii Senecionis consulis II pronepti Sexti Iuli Frontini consulis III abnepti Minturnenses publice\n
EDCS-16000306.translation: The people of Minturnae publicly honor Pompeia Quintus filia Sosia Falconilla, daughter of Quintus Pompeius Sosius Priscus consul, granddaughter of Quintus Pompeius Falco consul, great-granddaughter of Quintus Sosius Senecio consul II, great-great-granddaughter of Sextus Iulius Frontinus consul III.
EDCS-16000306.summary: This inscription is a public honor by the people of Minturnae for Pompeia Sosia Falconilla, tracing her distinguished consular ancestry.

EDCS-16000414.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Julius Maximus, a veteran sailor, who lived 60 years. Turrania Maxima, his granddaughter, and Iulia Filete, his freedwoman, his heirs, made this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-16000414.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Julius Maximus, a veteran sailor, erected by his granddaughter and freedwoman.

EDCS-16000415.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Salbius Priscus, a sailor of the trireme Mercurio, of Alexandrian origin, who lived 50 years and served 30 years. Salvia Domnina, his freedwoman, made this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-16000415.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Salbius Priscus, an Alexandrian sailor of a trireme, erected by his freedwoman.

EDCS-16000416.translation: To the departed spirits. For Septimia Domitia, who lived 45 years. Aurelius Vitellius, a soldier of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, sailor of the trireme Minerva, made this for himself and his incomparable wife, and for his freedmen and their descendants, who deserved well.
EDCS-16000416.summary: A tombstone for Septimia Domitia and her husband Aurelius Vitellius, a soldier and sailor of the Misenum fleet, intended for them and their descendants.

EDCS-16000417.translation: To the departed spirits. For Quintus Servilius Iasonis, sailor of the quadrireme Vesta, of Cilician origin, who served 22 years. Gaius Iulius Ianuarus, sailor of the trireme Parthic, guardian of Aurelius Iasonis, his son and heir, made this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-16000417.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Servilius Iasonis, a Cilician sailor of a quadrireme, erected by the guardian of his son and heir.

EDCS-16000418.translation: To the departed spirits. For Tyches, slave girl of Antonius Valens, sailor of the quinquireme Victoria, who lived 23 years.
EDCS-16000418.summary: A tombstone for Tyches, a slave girl and sailor of a quinquireme, owned by Antonius Valens.

EDCS-16000419.translation: To the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Marinus, aged 90, of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, and Aelia Nicolais, his wife, made this for themselves, their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-16000419.summary: A tombstone for Tiberius Claudius Marinus of the Misenum fleet and his wife Aelia Nicolais, intended for themselves and their freedmen and descendants.

EDCS-16000421.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Titus Aurelius Longinianus, an optio of the trireme Venus of the praetorian fleet at Misenum. Iulia Rufina, his mother, granted this place, with the patron Iulius Flaviano.
EDCS-16000421.summary: A tombstone for Titus Aurelius Longinianus, an optio of the Misenum fleet, erected by his mother with the patron's permission.

EDCS-16000422.translation: To the departed spirits. For Quintus Aelius Diogenes, soldier of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, from the trireme Satyra, who lived 36 years and served 16 years. Iulius Victorinus, optio, his heir, for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16000422.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Aelius Diogenes, a soldier and sailor of the Misenum fleet, erected by his heir.

EDCS-16000423.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Antistius Isidorus, an optio of the quadrireme Venus, of Egyptian origin, who lived 40 years and served 20 years. Hermes, his freedman, made this for his patron, who deserved well.
EDCS-16000423.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Antistius Isidorus, an Egyptian optio of a quadrireme, erected by his freedman.

EDCS-16000424.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Antonius Longus, from the praetorian fleet at Misenum, an optio of the trireme Venus, of Pontic origin, who served 22 years and lived 40 years. Antonia Victorina, his patron, made this for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16000424.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Antonius Longus of the Misenum fleet, a Pontic optio of a trireme, erected by his patron.

EDCS-16000425.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Artoria Euphraenusa, of Sinopean origin, lived 26 years. Artorius Sabinus, optio of the trireme Concord, made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-16000425.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Artoria Euphraenusa, set up by her husband, an optio serving on a trireme.

EDCS-16000427.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Castus, optio of the fleet, [illegible name?].
EDCS-16000427.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Gaius Castus, an optio of the fleet, with significant portions missing.

EDCS-16000428.translation: Of Egyptian origin, lived [illegible number, at least 3]VIII years, served 23 years. [Illegible name] Clemens, from the trireme Vesta. Gaius Arruntius Valens, optio of the liburna Nereis, as heir, made this for his deserving [relative?].
EDCS-16000428.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and service of an individual from Egypt who served as an optio on a liburna, set up by Gaius Arruntius Valens.

EDCS-16000429.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Titus Flavius Firmus, optio of the quadrireme Fortuna, of Pannonian origin, lived 45 years, served 26 years. Aelius Valerianus made this for his deserving [relative?].
EDCS-16000429.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Firmus, an optio of the quadrireme Fortuna, who served for 26 years, erected by Aelius Valerianus.

EDCS-16000430.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Gargilius Urbanus, optio from the trireme Perseus, of Sardinian origin, lived 38 years and 3 months and 7 days, served 20 years. Lucius Gargilius Urbanus, his son, made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-16000430.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and service of Lucius Gargilius Urbanus, an optio from Sardinia, and was erected by his son of the same name.

EDCS-16000431.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Hammonius Aristo, optio of the trireme Rheno. [Dedicatee?] Lucius, of Syra origin, lived 22 years.
EDCS-16000431.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hammonius Aristo, an optio serving on the trireme Rheno, whose Syrian origin and age are noted.

EDCS-16000432.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Laelius Valens, also called Licca, son of Bardo(?), optio of the trireme Venus. Lived 35 years, served 13 years.
EDCS-16000432.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and service of Lucius Laelius Valens (also known as Licca), an optio on the trireme Venus.

EDCS-16000433.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Julius Aristonicus, soldier of the praetorian fleet, on the liburna Nereis, of Egyptian origin. Served 14 years, lived 35 years. Gaius Arruntius Valens, optio from the same unit, as heir, [made this] for his deserving [relative?].
EDCS-16000433.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Gaius Julius Aristonicus, an Egyptian soldier serving on a liburna in the praetorian fleet, erected by Gaius Arruntius Valens, also an optio.

EDCS-16000434.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Julius Nepotianus, optio of the trireme Pacis, of Egyptian origin. Lived 37 years, served 13 years and 4 months. Iulia Semne for her wife and Iulius Cassianus for his father, both deserving, made this.
EDCS-16000434.summary: This funerary epitaph records Marcus Julius Nepotianus, an Egyptian optio of the trireme Pacis, and was erected by his wife and son.

EDCS-16000435.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iulia Victoria. Quintus Vettius Priscus, optio of the trireme Apolline, made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-16000435.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Iulia Victoria, erected by her husband, Quintus Vettius Priscus, an optio serving on the trireme Apolline.

EDCS-16000436.translation: To the divine shades. Memmia Nereis, her Stabian slave, lived 20 years, 9 months, 5 days. Volceius Saturninus, optiotensin the trireme Venus, made this for his most deserving wife.
EDCS-16000436.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Memmia Nereis, a slave, set up by her husband, a naval officer, around the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-16000437.translation: To Plutius Clo[---], optio, lived [---] 4 years, 7 days [---] to his well-deserving wife he made this.
EDCS-16000437.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Plutius Clo[---], a naval officer, set up by his wife, dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-16000438.translation: To the divine shades. For Publius Sextilius Marcellus, by nationality Italian, from Nola, an optiotensin the praetorian fleet of Misenum. Sextilius Felix and Petelia Iusta, his parents, made this for their most beloved father and husband, well-deserving.
EDCS-16000438.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Sextilius Marcellus, an officer in the Misenum fleet, set up by his parents, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-16000439.translation: To the divine shades. For Gaius Valerius Finitus, optiotensin the liburna of Neptune, from the Misenum praetorian fleet, by nationality Dalmatian. He served 29 years, lived 55 years. Marcus Apponius Firmus, his heir, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-16000439.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Finitus, a Dalmatian sailor and officer in the Misenum fleet, who served for 29 years and lived to 55, set up by his heir, dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-16000440.translation: To the divine shades. Here lies Hierax, son of Lucius Valerius. He lived 5 years, 2 months, 11 days. Valerius Germanus, optiotensin the trireme Tiber, made this for his most devoted son, most miserable.
EDCS-16000440.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hierax, a young boy, set up by his father, a naval officer, from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-16000441.translation: To the divine shades. Vibius Felix, optiotensin the trireme Eufrates, lived 8 years. Sossia Quinta, his heir, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-16000441.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibius Felix, who died at the age of 8, set up by his heir, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-16000442.translation: To the divine shades. For Marcus Naevius Proculus, optiotensin the convalescentiaries on the quadrireme Venus. He served 3 years, lived 21 years, 2 months, 15 days. His parents made this for their sweetest son.
EDCS-16000442.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Naevius Proculus, a young naval officer who served for three years and died at 21, set up by his parents, from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-16000443.translation: To the divine shades. For Gaius Terentius Longinus, optiotensin the faction of artisans. He lived 30 years, served 11 years. Tullius Titianus, tribune of the Misenum praetorian fleet, his heir, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-16000443.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Terentius Longinus, an officer and artisan, who served for 11 years and lived to 30, set up by his heir who was a naval tribune, from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-16000444.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. For Gaius Julius Paniscus, on the quadrireme Dacicus(?).
EDCS-16000444.summary: This is a funerary epitaph fragment for Gaius Julius Paniscus, possibly a sailor on a quadrireme, dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-16000445.translation: To the divine shades. For Gaius Serenus Maximus, on the quadrireme Vesta. He was Egyptian by birth. He lived 37 years, served 13 years. Apuleius, his grandson and scribe, his heir, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-16000445.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Serenus Maximus, an Egyptian sailor and scribe who lived to 37 and served for 13 years, set up by his grandson, from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-16000446.translation: To the shades of the departed. To Gaius Aponius Eudaimon, a proreta on the trireme Iuno, who lived 45 years and served 23 years, an Egyptian from Lycopolis. His wife Aiezia Bitallis, and his son Publius Aieza, and his brother Gaius Iulius Hierax, a proreta on the quadrireme Dacico, and his heirs, made this for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16000446.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Aponius Eudaimon, a naval officer, set up by his family and heirs.

EDCS-16000447.translation: To the shades of the departed. To Lucius Fulvius Datius, proreta of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, on the trireme Vesta, who lived 40 years. The officers of the proretae, his heirs, and colleagues, made this for the most distinguished, who deserved well.
EDCS-16000447.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Fulvius Datius, a naval officer, set up by his colleagues and heirs.

EDCS-16000448.translation: To the shades of the departed. To Gaius Erennius Paulus, proreta on the trireme Cupido. Flabius Sabinus, heir and friend, for the one who deserved well.
EDCS-16000448.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Erennius Paulus, a naval officer, set up by his heir and friend.

EDCS-16000449.translation: To the shades of the departed. To Gaius Iulius Zoilus, proreta of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, on the quadrireme Fides, a Vibiensis by birth, who lived 35 years and served 17 years. Iulius Silius and Iulia Zoe made this for their father, who deserved well.
EDCS-16000449.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Iulius Zoilus, a naval officer, set up by his children.

EDCS-16000450.translation: To the shades of the departed. For Quintus Panentius Quintianus, a soldier of the praetorian fleet at Ravenna, on the trireme Aesculapius, a Dalmatian by birth, with 32 years of service. Gaius Postumius Valens, proreta and heir, and Marcus Plarentius Syrio, subheir and procurator, for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16000450.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Panentius Quintianus, a soldier, set up by a naval officer and another individual.

EDCS-16000451.translation: To the shades of the departed. To Seleucus, a soldier of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, from Seleucia, who served 30 years, who lived 50 years. Antonia Theodote, his sister, made this.
EDCS-16000451.summary: This is a tombstone for Seleucus, a soldier, set up by his sister.

EDCS-16000452.translation: To the shades of the departed. To Lucius Atilius Pudentus, son of Xenon, a scribe and veteran of the praetorian fleet at Misenum. Atilia Chelido made this for her patron, who deserved well.
EDCS-16000452.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Atilius Pudentus, a scribe and naval veteran, set up by his patron's freedwoman.

EDCS-16000453.translation: To the shades of the departed. To Quintus Caesius Paternus, scribe on the quadrireme Vesta, an Egyptian by birth, who lived 51 years and served 26 years. Caesia Paternus, his daughter and heir, together with Melanun, his mother, made this for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16000453.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Caesius Paternus, a scribe, set up by his daughter and mother.

EDCS-16000454.translation: To the shades of the departed. To Gaius Iulius Diodorus, a soldier of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, on the quadrireme Dacico, a Bithynian by birth, who lived 45 years and served 22 years. Tiberius Claudius Paternus, scribe and heir.
EDCS-16000454.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Iulius Diodorus, a soldier, set up by his heir who was also a scribe.

EDCS-16000455.translation: To the shades of the departed. To Gaius Iulius Maximus, a veteran, who served in the quadrireme Venus for 26 years and lived 54 years. Gaius Cornelius Priscus, a veteran, and Gaius Iulius Saturninus, a scribe, heirs, made this for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16000455.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Iulius Maximus, a veteran, set up by his heirs who were also a scribe and a veteran.

EDCS-16000456.translation: To Gaius Julius Silvanus, a maniple leader from the ship Justitia, a Bithynian by birth, also called Diophanes, who served for 19 years, lived for 40 years, a well-deserving man. Quintus Naevius Propincus, scribe, and Nonnius Sura erected this.
EDCS-16000456.summary: A funerary monument erected by Quintus Naevius Propincus and Nonnius Sura for Gaius Julius Silvanus, a soldier who served for 19 years and lived for 40 years.

EDCS-16000457.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Quintus Valerius Pollio, scribe, and Valeria Hieronis, his freedwoman. Decimus Caesernius Florus and Minucius Aper, scribe, his heirs, erected this for their patrons because of their merits.
EDCS-16000457.summary: A funerary monument erected by heirs for their patrons, Quintus Valerius Pollio, a scribe, and Valeria Hieronis, his freedwoman.

EDCS-16000458.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Publius Marinus, soldier of the praetorian fleet of Misenum, a *secutor* (type of gladiator?) serving under a trierarch, a Syrian by birth, who served for 4 years, lived for 20 years. His parents erected this for the well-deserving man.
EDCS-16000458.summary: A funerary monument erected by parents for their son, Gaius Publius Marinus, a soldier of the Misenum fleet who served for 4 years and lived for 20 years.

EDCS-16000459.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Ulpius Licinianus, armor-bearer (?) of a quadrireme, Flavius Iucundus Marcellianus, also from the same unit, and Aelius Exoratus, armor-bearer (?) of a quadrireme. Erected by the community for the well-deserving men, at their own expense.
EDCS-16000459.summary: A funerary monument erected by a community for Gaius Ulpius Licinianus and Aelius Exoratus, who served on quadriremes.

EDCS-16000460.translation: To the Divine Shades. Abia Titiana, who lived for 4 years. Publius Annius Titianus erected this for his daughter Oceano, a third-rank officer on a trireme.
EDCS-16000460.summary: A funerary monument erected by Publius Annius Titianus for his daughter Abia Titiana, who died at age 4.

EDCS-16000461.translation: To the Divine Shades. Julia Hermione, who lived for 30 years. Julius Malchus, a third-rank officer on the trireme Pietas, erected this for the very dutiful woman.
EDCS-16000461.summary: A funerary monument erected by Julius Malchus for his wife, Julia Hermione, who died at age 30.

EDCS-16000464.translation: To the Divine Shades. Valerius [name missing], an Alexandrian by birth, a *duplicarius* (officer) in the *velarium* (sail division?), who served for 26 years and lived for 56 years, 4 months, and 12 days. Lucius Valerius Iustus and Varenia Helena, his heirs, erected this for the well-deserving man.
EDCS-16000464.summary: A funerary monument erected by heirs for Valerius, an officer in the sail division, who served for 26 years and lived for 56 years.

EDCS-16000465.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Valerius Victor, a principal *victimarius* (priestly assistant) from Sardinia, of the quadrireme Fides, who served for 23 years and lived for 31 years. Aurelia Spes, his wife, erected this for the well-deserving man.
EDCS-16000465.summary: A funerary monument erected by Aurelia Spes for her husband, Lucius Valerius Victor, a priest's assistant, who served for 23 years and lived for 31 years.

EDCS-16000466.translation: To the Divine Shades. Carpinianus, who lived for 2 years and 4 months. Julius Asclepiades, a principal standard-bearer, erected this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-16000466.summary: A funerary monument erected by Julius Asclepiades for his son Carpinianus, who died at age 2 years and 4 months.

EDCS-16000467.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Antonius Iulianus, a veteran of the praetorian fleet of Misenum, who lived for 54 years and served as a *duplicarius* (officer) on the trireme Sole. Erected to his memory.
EDCS-16000467.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Antonius Iulianus, a veteran of the Misenum fleet who served as an officer and lived to age 54.

EDCS-16000468.translation: To Gaius Apollonius Priscus, soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, an Alexandrian by birth, who lived 40 years. Lucius Fabullius Serenus, duplicarius of the same fleet, his heir, made this.
EDCS-16000468.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Apollonius Priscus, a soldier in the Roman fleet at Misenum.

EDCS-16000469.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Munatius Priscus, duplicarius of the trireme, made this for his wife Hercule and daughter Mamilla Prisca, who lived 12 years, both deserving.
EDCS-16000469.summary: A funerary monument set up by Munatius Priscus for his wife and daughter, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-16000470.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Commodiana, who lived 31 years, 8 months, and 20 days, is honored by her husband Flavius Diogenes, a duplicarius.
EDCS-16000470.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Aurelia Commodiana, wife of Flavius Diogenes, a naval officer.

EDCS-16000471.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sentiae Serapiadis vixit annis XVII Caius Sentius Maximus mani<p=I>ularis IIItriremi Marte \n
EDCS-16000471.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sentia Serapiadis lived 17 years. Caius Sentius Maximus, a maniplaris on the trireme Mars, [made this].
EDCS-16000471.summary: A tombstone for Sentia Serapiadis, likely related to Caius Sentius Maximus, a soldier in the Roman navy.

EDCS-16000472.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [Of] C. Servilius Paulinus, who served as a duplicarius on the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, on the trireme Danuvius [in the squadron] Asia. [---]
EDCS-16000472.summary: A funerary inscription for C. Servilius Paulinus, a sailor in the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, with fragmentary details about his service.

EDCS-16000473.translation: [To the spirits] of the departed. [---] of Alexandros, a principalis. [---] Venera, a Syrian by birth. [---] of 10 years of service. [---]ius Sigillianus [made this] for [---] who deserved well.
EDCS-16000473.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual named Alexandros, possibly of Syrian origin and holding a rank of 'principalis'.

EDCS-16000474.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antonius Paullinus, heir and principalis on the trireme Spe, made this for Gaius Valerius Plotianus, trierarch, and Euphrates, principalis.
EDCS-16000474.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Marcus Antonius Paullinus to Gaius Valerius Plotianus and Euphrates, both serving as officers in the Roman navy.

EDCS-16000475.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Acutius Leo made this for his most cherished wife, Clementia, who lived 28 years and deserved well.
EDCS-16000475.summary: A tombstone erected by Lucius Acutius Leo for his wife Clementia, who died at the age of 28.

EDCS-16000476.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Iulius Aelianus [made this for] Caius Aelius Fel[icissimus], an Alexandrian by birth, who completed 6 years of service and lived 27 years. [---]
EDCS-16000476.summary: This funerary inscription honors Caius Aelius Felicissimus, an Alexandrian who died after 6 years of service at the age of 27.

EDCS-16000477.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Granius Iulianus, a veteran, made this for his deserving wife Amasemia, who lived 30 years and 2 days.
EDCS-16000477.summary: A funerary monument set up by Granius Iulianus, a veteran, for his wife Amasemia.

EDCS-16000515.text_edition: \n Luci Satri Rufi evocati Augusti a commentariis\n
EDCS-16000515.translation: To Lucius Satrius Rufus, assessor of the Augustan administration from the commentarii.
EDCS-16000515.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Satrius Rufus, an official in the Augustan administration, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16100261.text_edition: \n CCCCCII Cai Vibi Recepti Usibus publicis Pompeiorum\n
EDCS-16100261.translation: To the CCII (citizens?) of Caius Vibius Receptus for public uses of Pompeii.
EDCS-16100261.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Caius Vibius Receptus for public use in Pompeii, likely from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-16100331.text_edition: \n Volusia Quinti filia Cornelia theatrum vetustate corruptum restituit et excoluit\n
EDCS-16100331.translation: Volusia, daughter of Quintus, of the Cornelia tribe, restored and improved the theater, which had been damaged by age.
EDCS-16100331.summary: This is a dedication by Volusia, daughter of Quintus, for the restoration of a theater in Pompeii, likely from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-16100332.text_edition: \n Hoc locum a veteribus bene positum templum quod neglegentia operante visu foedo esse videbatur nunc Turrenius Caelerinus pastor sacris deae Veneris Caelestis et cultor deorum sumpto sic proprio restauravit\n
EDCS-16100332.translation: This place, a temple well-built by our ancestors, which seemed to be unsightly due to neglect, has now been restored by Turrenius Caelerinus, a shepherd and devotee of the gods, by means of his own resources, for the sacred rites of the goddess Venus Caelestis.
EDCS-16100332.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple dedicated to Venus Caelestis by Turrenius Caelerinus, a shepherd and devotee, in Pompeii, likely from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-16100334.translation: To the Compitales of Daphne, Volumnius (centurion) wrote the uncum.
EDCS-16100334.summary: This is a dedication to the Lares Compitales of Daphne by a centurion named Volumnius, possibly from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-16100427.text_edition: \n Publius Casca Longus Publius Casca Longus Longus Publius Cassca\n
EDCS-16100427.translation: Publius Casca Longus, Publius Casca Longus, Longus, Publius Cassca.
EDCS-16100427.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly related to an official or a group, with repeated entries, from Pompeii, likely 1st century CE.

EDCS-16100431.text_edition: \n Cai liberta Fortunata sibi et o Celso viro suo trierarcho classis praetoriae Misenensis militavit nnis XLIIII vixit nnis LX et o Quinti filio Claudia Torquato F adlecto in ordinem decurionum IIviro vixit annis XXIII mensibus XI diebus XI et libertis libertabusque posterisque eorum hoc monumentum sive sepulcrum eorum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-16100431.translation: To Fortunata, freedwoman of Caius, for herself and for her husband Celsus, a trierarch of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, with whom she served for 44 years, and lived for 60 years; and for her son Quintus Claudius Torquatus, admitted to the order of decurions and twice chief magistrate, who lived for 23 years, 11 months, and 11 days; and to their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. This monument or tomb shall not pass to their heir.
EDCS-16100431.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fortunata, her husband Celsus, and their son, detailing their lives and careers, with a prohibition on inheritance, likely from Misenum in the late 1st century CE.

EDCS-16200034.text_edition: \n Marco Veturio Frontoni filio dulcissimo vixit menses VI Marcus Antonius Fronto miles duplarius III Providentia pater\n
EDCS-16200034.translation: To Marcus Veturius Fronto, my dearest son, who lived 6 months. Marcus Antonius Fronto, duplarius soldier, by the father of Providentia.
EDCS-16200034.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child, Marcus Veturius Fronto, set up by his father, a soldier, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200036.text_edition: \n vixit annos XLIII militavit annos XXIII Lucius Aemilius Aemilianus trierarchus heres bene merenti fecit marmorarius VII\n
EDCS-16200036.translation: Lucius Aemilius Aemilianus, trierarch, lived 43 years, served 23 years; his heir made this for the well-deserving. Marble worker VII.
EDCS-16200036.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Aemilius Aemilianus, a trierarch, set up by his heir, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200037.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Secundinus vixit nnos XVIII Antonius Proculeianus |(centurio) classis praetoriae Misenatium et Iulia Priv bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-16200037.translation: To the Divine Shades. Secundinus lived 18 years. Antonius Proculeianus, centurion of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, and Iulia Prima made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-16200037.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Secundinus, set up by his father, a centurion in the Misenum fleet, and his mother, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200038.text_edition: \n III Vesta Serapion Serapionis natione Aegyptus vixit nnos XXXV militavit XIIII Iulius Archelaus manipularis III Vesta heres bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-16200038.translation: To Vesta. Serapion, of Egyptian origin, lived 35 years, served 14 years. Iulius Archelaus, a soldier of the third cohort of Vesta, made this for his deserving heir. [This translation seems to be reversed, usually the heres makes the monument for the deceased.]
EDCS-16200038.summary: This funerary monument records the life and service of Serapion, a soldier in the third cohort of Vesta, with his heir Iulius Archelaus as the dedicator.

EDCS-16200048.text_edition: \n Plocamus Augusti aedituus vixit annos XXX \n
EDCS-16200048.translation: Plocamus, attendant of Augustus, lived 30 years.
EDCS-16200048.summary: This is a tombstone for Plocamus, an attendant of Augustus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-16200049.text_edition: \n Cida Augusti verna a memoria vixit nnos XXX\n
EDCS-16200049.translation: Cida, a young slave of Augustus, from the memoria, lived 30 years.
EDCS-16200049.summary: This epitaph records the life of Cida, a young slave of Augustus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-16200050.text_edition: \n Florus Augusti a possessionibus vixit annos XXXXV\n
EDCS-16200050.translation: Florus, from the properties of Augustus, lived 45 years.
EDCS-16200050.summary: This inscription commemorates Florus, who worked in the properties managed by Augustus, and lived to be 45.

EDCS-16200051.text_edition: \n Blastus Augusti structor fecit Marciae vernae suae vixit annos VI\n
EDCS-16200051.translation: Blastus, a builder for Augustus, made this for Marcia, his young slave, who lived 6 years.
EDCS-16200051.summary: This is a dedication by Blastus, a builder for Augustus, for his young slave Marcia, who died at the age of 6.

EDCS-16200052.text_edition: \n Athictus Augusti vilicus vixit nnos XXXI\n
EDCS-16200052.translation: Athictus, a steward for Augustus, lived 31 years.
EDCS-16200052.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Athictus, a steward of Augustus, who died at 31.

EDCS-16200505.translation: To the divine spirits. Aelius Aelianus, of Cappadocian origin, lived 32 years. Decius Menophilus, the heir, made this for the deserving deceased.
EDCS-16200505.summary: This tombstone honors Aelius Aelianus of Cappadocia, who lived 32 years, with the monument erected by his heir Decius Menophilus.

EDCS-16200520.translation: To the divine spirits. Marcus Amonius Bassus, from the trireme Pollux, an Egyptian, lived 25 years and served 4 years. Iulius Creispinus, the heir, made this for the deserving deceased.
EDCS-16200520.summary: This funerary monument is for Marcus Amonius Bassus, an Egyptian sailor from the trireme Pollux, who lived 25 years and served 4 years, with his heir Iulius Creispinus as dedicator.

EDCS-16200521.translation: To Gaius Annius Anubion, an Egyptian from the trireme Pace, lived 36 years and served 16 years. Pison Achillas and Iulius Eudaemon, the heirs, made this for the deserving deceased.
EDCS-16200521.summary: This tombstone commemorates Gaius Annius Anubion, an Egyptian sailor from the trireme Pace, who lived 36 years and served 16 years, with his heirs Pison Achillas and Iulius Eudaemon as dedicators.

EDCS-16200522.translation: To the divine spirits. For Caius Annius Draconis, a veteran of the Praetorian fleet of Misenum, of Egyptian origin, lived 60 years. Annia Dionysias, freedwoman and heir, made this for the deserving deceased.
EDCS-16200522.summary: This funerary inscription honors Caius Annius Draconis, an Egyptian veteran of the Misenum fleet, who lived 60 years, with his freedwoman and heir Annia Dionysias as dedicator.

EDCS-16200523.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Dionysias made this for Marcus Annio Felicissimus, trireme rower on the Minerva, his well-deserving freedman. He lived 60 years, served 27 years.
EDCS-16200523.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Annio Felicissimus, a trireme rower, set up by his freedwoman.

EDCS-16200524.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Anteius Bassus, soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, of [---] nationality. He lived 40 years. Lucius Lucretius Capito and Maximinus Successus, freedmen, set this up for their well-deserving patron, the procurator Coc[---] Forulianus.
EDCS-16200524.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Anteius Bassus, a soldier in the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, set up by his freedmen who also served as procurators.

EDCS-16200525.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pomponia Eutychia made this for her son Lucius Antistius Celer, who lived 18 years. To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Antistius Celer made this for his most beloved wife Pomponia Antestia, a veteran, and for himself and his ancestors.
EDCS-16200525.summary: A double funerary epitaph recording the deaths of Lucius Antistius Celer, a veteran, and his wife Pomponia Antestia, set up by him.

EDCS-16200526.translation: [---]lenus, of Egyptian nationality, trireme rower on the Minerva. He lived 45 years, served 22 years. Hygia, his freedwoman and heir, made this for her well-deserving freedman.
EDCS-16200526.summary: A funerary epitaph for [---]lenus, an Egyptian trireme rower, set up by his freedwoman and heir.

EDCS-16200527.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Antonius Capito, a veteran from the Praetorian fleet at Misenum. Antonia Restituta to her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-16200527.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Capito, a veteran of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, set up by his patron.

EDCS-16200528.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antonius P[---]nus, a veteran of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum. He lived 50 years, served 34 years. His wife Iulia Tyche and his son Marcus Antonius made this for their well-deserving husband and father.
EDCS-16200528.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius P[---]nus, a veteran of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, set up by his wife and son.

EDCS-16200529.translation: Marcus Antonius Rufinus, soldier on the trireme Victoria, for himself and for his brother Lucius Iulius Apollinaris, soldier on the trireme Diana. He lived 38 years, served 18 years, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-16200529.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Rufinus and his brother Lucius Iulius Apollinaris, both soldiers, and their descendants.

EDCS-16200530.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Antonius Rufus, a maniple commander in the Praetorian fleet at Ravenna, trireme rower on the Mars. He lived 30 years, served 15 years. Iunius Philippus, his heir, made this for his well-deserving freedman.
EDCS-16200530.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antonius Rufus, a maniple commander in the Praetorian fleet at Ravenna, set up by his heir.

EDCS-16200531.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Antonius Sabinus, a veteran. Euvodia to her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-16200531.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Antonius Sabinus, a veteran, set up by his patron.

EDCS-16200532.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. For Caius Antonius Silvanus, a veteran. He lived 50 years.
EDCS-16200532.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Antonius Silvanus, a veteran, recording his age at death.

EDCS-16200533.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Antonius Sopater, a sailor of the praetorian fleet at Ravenna, serving on the trireme Apollinis for 27 years of service, a Libyan by nation. Ammo Iaso, his heir, and Babu Tarsa, his successor, set this up for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16200533.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Antonius Sopater, a sailor in the Ravenna fleet, erected by his heir and successor.

EDCS-16200534.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Antonius Valens, a veteran of the praetorian fleet at Misenum. Antonia Egloge set this up for her deserving patron.
EDCS-16200534.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Antonius Valens, a veteran of the Misenum fleet, set up by his patron.

EDCS-16200535.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For [his] son Piaer(?) Aprilis, [a sailor] of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, Piae Vindicis. He himself chose and purchased the place for his sepulcher while he was alive.
EDCS-16200535.summary: A tombstone for Piaer Aprilis, a sailor in the Misenum fleet, indicating he purchased his burial plot while alive.

EDCS-16200537.translation: Marcus Arrius Princeps, son of Marcus, freedman, lived 19 years, served 8 years, [serving] on the quadreme Vesta.
EDCS-16200537.summary: An epitaph for Marcus Arrius Princeps, a sailor who died at age 19 after 8 years of service.

EDCS-16200538.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Arrius Antoninus, a legionary of the quadreme Salvia, an Egyptian by nation, who lived 54 years and served 29 years, for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16200538.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Arrius Antoninus, a sailor on the Salvia quadreme, erected for his well-deserving.

EDCS-16200539.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Arrius//Pacis, centurion of the trireme Pace, made this for his son Domitius.
EDCS-16200539.summary: A tombstone for Domitius, son of Caius Arrius Pacis, a centurion of the Pace trireme.

EDCS-16200540.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Arrius Capito, a sailor of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, on the quadreme Vesta, an Egyptian by nation, who lived 50 years and served 29 years. His son Valerius Genialis made this for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16200540.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Arrius Capito, a sailor in the Misenum fleet, erected by his son.

EDCS-16200541.translation: For Liber Pater, a maniple of the trireme. Decimus Arruntius Clemens, an Alexandrian by nation, lived 40 years and served 18 years. Julius Ammonianus, his heir, made this for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16200541.summary: An epitaph for Decimus Arruntius Clemens, a sailor on the trireme, set up by his heir.

EDCS-16200542.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aurelius Aepictetus, a veteran, a Greek by nation, who lived 50 years. Nicobulus, his son, made this for his well-deserving father Aurelius Rodon, with Aurelius Rodon supervising.
EDCS-16200542.summary: A tombstone for Aurelius Aepictetus, a Greek veteran, erected by his son, with supervision from Aurelius Rodon.

EDCS-16200543.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aurelius Domnion, a sailor, who lived 27 years. Aurelius Gaianus and Aurelius Samus, his heirs, made this for him who deserved well.
EDCS-16200543.summary: A tombstone for Aurelius Domnion, a sailor, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-16200544.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Antistius Varus, boatswain of the Trireme Victoria and its freedwoman Iconis, made this for their well-deserving freedman Marcus Aurelius Exoratus, boatswain of the Trireme Ceres, who lived for 33 years and served for 14 years.
EDCS-16200544.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Exoratus, a soldier in the Roman navy, set up by his freedmen, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200545.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Aemilius Vitalis, boatswain of the Trireme Liber, made this for his well-deserving freedman Marcus Aurelius Fuscus, a rower of the Trireme Ceres, a Dalmatian by birth, who lived for 50 years and served for 26 years, and for his freedwoman Aurelia Tyche, a Syrian by birth, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-16200545.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Fuscus and his freedwoman Aurelia Tyche, both naval personnel, set up by Lucius Aemilius Vitalis, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200546.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavia Apollonia made this for her well-deserving husband Aurelius Gaianus, a veteran, who lived for 60 years and 6 months.
EDCS-16200546.summary: A funerary epitaph for the veteran Aurelius Gaianus, set up by his wife Flavia Apollonia, dating from the Roman Imperial period.
