EDCS-17900603.translation: The bones of Caius Sertorius Labeo, son of Caius, of the Classici, lie here.
EDCS-17900603.summary: This is a simple epitaph marking the burial place of Caius Sertorius Labeo, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-17900604.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Servilius Vicanus, veteran of the 12th Fulminata legion. Servilia Felicissima, his freedwoman, and Quintus Servilius Sabinianus Vicanus Rufinus, to their excellent patron.
EDCS-17900604.summary: This dedication is from a freedwoman and another individual to their patron, Quintus Servilius Vicanus, a veteran of the 12th Legion, probably from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-17900605.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Silvanus Silvestris, a soldier of the 1st Primigenia Severiana legion, who served 4 years and lived 25 years, born in Colonia Agrippinensis. Deccius Matutinus, his heir, made this in memory of his deserving relation.
EDCS-17900605.summary: This funerary inscription honors a soldier of the 1st Praetorian Legion who died young, erected by his heir and likely fellow soldier, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-17900606.translation: [---]lius Valens, son of Caius, of the Pupinia tribe, a soldier of the 7th Praetorian cohort. He lived 25 years and served 6 years.
EDCS-17900606.summary: This tombstone records a soldier of the 7th Praetorian cohort, detailing his age and length of service, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-17900607.translation: [---] to Quintus, his grandson, [---] great-grandson. [---] Velleius Pius, urban [---] to his son Iuvenalis [---] prefect of the 5th Macedonian legion [---] to Seia Urbica, his son [---] the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-17900607.summary: This inscription fragment likely records familial relationships and honors a prefect of the 5th Legion, with a grant of land confirmed by the local council, possibly dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-17900608.translation: To Lucius Viccius, son of Gaius, of the Arnensis tribe, military tribune.
EDCS-17900608.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Viccius, a military tribune.

EDCS-18000291.translation: Caius Alfius Juda, archon and archisynagogus, who lived 70 years, 7 months, and 10 days. Alfia Soteris, with whom he lived for 48 years, made this for her incomparable and deserving husband.
EDCS-18000291.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Alfius Juda, a synagogue official, erected by his wife.

EDCS-18000293.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Annius Ianuarius, inspector of public works and the theatre from its foundations. For his merits, the town council decreed him the honor of Augustalis free of charge. He lived 71 years and made this for himself while alive and for his descendants.
EDCS-18000293.summary: This epitaph records the posthumous honors granted by the town council to Quintus Annius Ianuarius, a public works inspector, who also erected his own tomb.

EDCS-18000294.text_edition: \n Lucius Popilius Auli filius Balbus IIvir\n
EDCS-18000294.translation: Lucius Popilius Balbus, son of Aulus, twice duovir.
EDCS-18000294.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely related to Lucius Popilius Balbus, who held the office of duovir.

EDCS-18000295.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Rufinus, son of Tiberius, of the Palatina tribe, an equestrian, chief engineer, twice duovir, quaestor, and quinquennalis. The young Augustales, whose patron he was, grateful for the honor, placed this at their own expense. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-18000295.summary: This is a dedication to Tiberius Claudius Rufinus, a prominent local official, erected by the Augustales whom he patronized.

EDCS-18000296.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Marcus Cornelius Iustus Acutianus, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, of the Publilia tribe, four-time duovir with quinquennial powers, quaestor of the treasury, quaestor of provisions, curator of public works, curator of the Falerian road, augur, prefect of Tiberius Statilius Severus, quaestor, and patron of the guild of wool-workers. Marcus Cornelius Acutianus, Chrysippus, and Iusta made this for their excellent father.
EDCS-18000296.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by sons for their father, Marcus Cornelius Iustus Acutianus, who held numerous important civic and religious offices, including patron of a guild.

EDCS-18000299.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-18000299.translation: To Gaius Lartius Gabinius Fortuitus, son of Publius, of the Palatina tribe, dictator of Lanuvium, duovir of Capua, for his generosity in paving the road of Diana from the Volturnensian gate to the village at his own expense, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-18000299.summary: This is a public dedication honoring Gaius Lartius Gabinius Fortuitus for paving a road at his own expense.

EDCS-18000304.translation: To Gaius Pomponius Capito, son of Gaius, of the Teretina tribe, decurion of Calenum; to Philero[t]us, freedman of Gaius, Augustalis; and to Nicene, freedwoman of Gaius.
EDCS-18000304.summary: This is a funerary monument for members of the Pomponius family, including a decurion and freedmen.

EDCS-18000308.translation: Lucius Soranus, son of Lucius, and Lucius Arrius, son of Marcus, four-time duovirs and praetors, took care to establish the boundary by decree of the senate.
EDCS-18000308.summary: This inscription records two officials, Lucius Soranus and Lucius Arrius, who were responsible for establishing a boundary marker.

EDCS-18000310.translation: Lucius Vettius, tribune, aedile, quaestor, repeated the gladiatorial games. He also gave two modii of grain to the people of Campania.
EDCS-18000310.summary: This inscription details the career and benefactions of Lucius Vettius, who held several magistracies and provided grain to the populace.

EDCS-18000313.translation: To the departed spirits. For [---]us, son of Caius, of the Falerna tribe, former duumvir, of [---]ae, of [---]nus, of [---]ta, son of [---].
EDCS-18000313.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a man who held the office of duumvir in a Roman colony, with many names and tribal affiliations restored.

EDCS-18000322.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Velleius Cannulanus.

EDCS-18000323.translation: Alexander, clerk of the colony, son of Privatus the treasurer. Alexander, clerk of the colony, son of Privatus the treasurer.
EDCS-18000323.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of Alexander, a clerk and son of a treasurer in a Roman colony.

EDCS-18000324.translation: Caecilia Salutaris, mother, made [this] for Publius Octavius Successus, freedman of Publius, a lictor of the duumvirate in Capua, and for Caecilia Salutaris, daughter of Quintus, for herself and her family, on private land, while alive.
EDCS-18000324.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a mother for her daughter and her daughter's husband, who served as a lictor and freedman in Capua.

EDCS-18000325.translation: To Gaius Campanicus Ursulus, freedman of the colony, from Lupulus, treasurer of the colony of Capua, to his excellent friend.
EDCS-18000325.summary: This is a dedication from a treasurer in Capua to his friend, Gaius Campanicus Ursulus.

EDCS-18000326.translation: To the departed spirits. Soter made [this] for his very unfortunate but deserving son Felicis, who lived 10 years, 11 months, and 25 days.
EDCS-18000326.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a young boy who died after living 10 years, 11 months, and 25 days, set up by his father Soter.

EDCS-18000327.translation: To Macedo, son of Euphrosinus the treasurer, master of the limata family. Salvilla, mother.
EDCS-18000327.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Macedo, son of a treasurer and 'master' of a family group, possibly related to a craft or guild.

EDCS-18000329.translation: To my dearest wife. Lucius Campanius Sosimenes, Augustalis of Capua.
EDCS-18000329.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Campanius Sosimenes, an Augustalis of Capua, to his wife.

EDCS-18000331.translation: Lucius Curtius, Augustalis of Capua, saw to its construction.
EDCS-18000331.summary: This is a dedication indicating that Lucius Curtius, an Augustalis of Capua, commissioned the construction of a monument.

EDCS-18000332.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Salutaris, freedman of Titus, Augustalis, a merchant dealing in footwear, made [this] while alive for himself and for his deserving wife Titiria Felicitas.
EDCS-18000332.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Titus Flavius Salutaris, an Augustalis and shoemaker in Capua, for himself and his wife.

EDCS-18000333.translation: Gaius Julius Antiochus (?), an Augustalis of Capua, made this for himself, and for his wife Julia Nymphe, who deserved excellently of him, and for Gaius Julius Creticus, his son.
EDCS-18000333.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Gaius Julius Antiochus for himself, his wife, and his son, indicating his status as an Augustalis of Capua.

EDCS-18000334.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Marcius Amerimnus, an Augustalis of Capua, made this while alive for himself and for his wife Marcia Celerina, who deserved excellently of him.
EDCS-18000334.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Marcius Amerimnus, an Augustalis of Capua, who dedicated it for himself and his wife during his lifetime.

EDCS-18000335.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Volusia Primitiva made this while alive for Lucius Volusius Threptus, an Augustalis of Capua, her very rare husband with whom she lived for 40 years, and for their daughter Volusia Expectata, and for herself.
EDCS-18000335.summary: A funerary dedication by Volusia Primitiva for her husband, Lucius Volusius Threptus (an Augustalis of Capua), their daughter, and herself, specifying their 40 years of marriage.

EDCS-18000336.text_edition: \n Augustali Capuae patri optimo et Volusiae Hermione filiae piae Lucius Volusius Threptus et Volusia Primitiva et sibi\n
EDCS-18000336.translation: Lucius Volusius Threptus and Volusia Primitiva and (made this) for their excellent father, an Augustalis of Capua, and for their dutiful daughter Volusia Hermione, and for themselves.
EDCS-18000336.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Volusius Threptus and Volusia Primitiva for their father (an Augustalis of Capua), their daughter, and themselves.

EDCS-18000339.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription identifying Marcus Alfius N[---] as a stableman (stabularius).

EDCS-18000341.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-18000341.translation: Publius Ateius Regillus, freedman of Publius, made this for himself and for Publius Ateius Salvius, freedman of Publius, his patron, a gardener; he dedicated the tenth part to Hercules. He lived 102 years, and for Prima, his freedwoman, dearest to him, and for Ateia Iolla, daughter of his patron.
EDCS-18000341.summary: An inscription by Publius Ateius Regillus, a freedman and gardener, dedicating a monument for himself, his patron, and his freedwoman and her family, noting his long lifespan.

EDCS-18000342.translation: Marcus Avidius Aesopus, freedman of Marcus, made this for himself and for Avidia Zosima, freedwoman of Marcus, his wife, an interior decorator; his heirs will not follow this monument or tomb.
EDCS-18000342.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Marcus Avidius Aesopus, a freedman and interior decorator, for himself and his wife, with a clause that his heirs are not bound by it.

EDCS-18000343.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Canuleia Anesis, freedwoman, (dedicated this) for Aulus Canuleius Salutaris, son of Aulus, from Falerna, from Canusium, her patron, who deserved well, and for his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-18000343.summary: A funerary inscription by Canuleia Anesis, a freedwoman, dedicating a monument to her well-deserving patron, Aulus Canuleius Salutaris, and his extended family.

EDCS-18000344.translation: Publius Cervius Diogenes, freedman of Publius, a cloth merchant, made this for himself and his family: for Caesias Bulene, freedwoman of the woman [Caesias?], for Cervia Irena, freedwoman of Publius, my freedwoman, when she is dead I wish her to be buried in the chest which is in the monument; for Lucius Aedius Samon[---], son of Lucius.
EDCS-18000344.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Publius Cervius Diogenes, a freedman and cloth merchant, for himself, his family, his freedwoman Irena, and specifies burial arrangements within the monument for Irena, also mentioning Lucius Aedius Samon[---].

EDCS-18000345.translation: Publius Cervius Diogenes, freedman of Publius, a cloth merchant, made this for himself and his family: for Caesias Bulene, for Cervia Irena, freedwoman of Publius; for Agria Hilara, freedwoman of Marcus, and for Marcus Philemon, freedman of Marcus, and for Marcus Hilarus, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-18000345.summary: An inscription by Publius Cervius Diogenes, a freedman and cloth merchant, dedicating a monument for himself, his family, and several freedpersons belonging to him and others.

EDCS-18000346.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Vera made this for her well-deserving husband, Tiberius Claudius Laconicus, a Greek grammarian, with whom she lived for 26 years.
EDCS-18000346.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, a grammarian, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-18000347.translation: Lucius Cocceius Papa, freedman of Gaius, Lucius, and Marcus, [set this up for] Lucius Valerius Nicephorus, freedman of Lucius, his friend and doctor.
EDCS-18000347.summary: An epitaph honoring a doctor and friend, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-18000349.translation: Epaphas, steward of the associates of Sisapo, his bones lie here, and Provincia, his wife.
EDCS-18000349.summary: A tombstone for Epaphas, a steward, and his wife Provincia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18000350.translation: Gaius Epillius Alexander, freedman of Gaius, a ship-chandler. Staia Salvia, freedwoman of a woman [named] Staia. Gaius Epillius Felix, freedman of Gaius.
EDCS-18000350.summary: An epitaph likely for multiple individuals, including a ship-chandler, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18000351.translation: Eutychus, the incense-seller, his bones lie here.
EDCS-18000351.summary: A tombstone for Eutychus, an incense-seller, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18000352.translation: Eutychus, the steward, and Euagogus, from the flamenship, made [this] for themselves and their families.
EDCS-18000352.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by two individuals, a steward and someone associated with the flaminate, for themselves and their families, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-18000355.translation: On the front, 8 feet; Marcus Hordionius Philargurus, while alive, and Labeo, lantern-maker, and Flavia Philumina, freedwoman of Gaius, his wife, while alive, and their families; on the land, 8 feet.
EDCS-18000355.summary: A funerary monument with dimensions specified, set up by Marcus Hordionius Philargurus and his wife Flavia Philumina, a lantern-maker, for themselves and their families, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-18000356.translation: Gaius Magius Alexander, freedman of Gaius, a shield-maker.
EDCS-18000356.summary: An epitaph for Gaius Magius Alexander, a freedman and shield-maker, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18000358.translation: Minatia Fausta, freedwoman of Gaius, daughter of Gaius Minatius Philodamus, a dyer, made this for herself and her patron. Her bones lie here. Her bones lie here.
EDCS-18000358.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Minatia Fausta, a freedwoman and daughter of a dyer, for herself and her patron, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18000360.translation: Lucius Novius Prepo, freedman of Lucius. Lucius Dionysius, freedman of Lucius Libertianus(?), a perfumer.
EDCS-18000360.summary: An epitaph likely honoring two individuals, possibly related, one of whom was a perfumer, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18000361.translation: The remains of Publius Ocratius Philodamus Bassus, freedman of Decimus, a goldsmith, are interred here.
EDCS-18000361.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedman goldsmith named Philodamus Bassus, recording the location of his remains.

EDCS-18000364.translation: Gaius Satrius Hilarus, freedman of Gaius, an unguentarius (perfumer), and his descendants, will not inherit this monument; the remains of Gaius Satrius Euhodus, freedman of Gaius, are interred here.
EDCS-18000364.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Satrius Hilarus, a perfumer, and his descendants, with the inscription also noting the burial of Gaius Satrius Euhodus.

EDCS-18000365.translation: For Scantia Redempta, a most incomparable woman whose life's example is too great for human mediocrity to fully praise. She was indeed deserving of every kind of praise from a young age, first adorned with divine sanctity and chastity, honored with good morals, devoted to her parents, distinguished by modesty, a mistress of discipline and a leader in learning and medicine, and of singular innocence. She was such a one as to be an example of marriage. She was such a one as to scorn youth, for her husband loved his wife, the sustainer of his life and health. She lived 22 years and 10 months. Flavius Tarentinus and Scantia Redempta, parents, made this for their most sweet daughter and for themselves.
EDCS-18000365.summary: A funerary monument erected by parents for their daughter Scantia Redempta, who died at the age of 22, praising her virtue and exemplary life.

EDCS-18000369.translation: The remains of Quintus Tiburtius Menolaus, freedman of Quintus, a cultrarius (knife-maker), are interred here.
EDCS-18000369.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Tiburtius Menolaus, a freedman knife-maker, indicating the location of his remains.

EDCS-18000370.translation: Marcus Titinius Nicephor, freedman of Marcus Publius, a marble worker.
EDCS-18000370.summary: An epitaph identifying Marcus Titinius Nicephor as a freedman marble worker.

EDCS-18000373.text_edition: \n Lucius Avius Marci libertus Eunicus aerarius magister fani \n
EDCS-18000373.translation: Lucius Avicus Eunicus, freedman of Marcus, aerarius magister fani (master of the temple's treasury/bronze workers).
EDCS-18000373.summary: An inscription for Lucius Avicus Eunicus, a freedman who held a significant financial or craft role in a temple.

EDCS-18000376.translation: The remains of Acuminus, freedwoman of Marcus, and Apollonius, freedman of Lucius, are interred here.
EDCS-18000376.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of two freedpersons, Acuminus and Apollonius.

EDCS-18000377.translation: To the shades below. Ulpia Monime made this for her dearest brother, Aelius Epagathus, and for herself, and for her freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. 10 feet at the front, 11 feet in the field.
EDCS-18000377.summary: A tomb inscription dedicated to Aelius Epagathus by his sister Ulpia Monime, who also reserved space for herself and her household.

EDCS-18000378.translation: Sacred to the shades below. For Publius Aelius Fortunatus, an incomparable son, who lived 14 years, 8 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-18000378.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Fortunatus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-18000380.translation: Of the freedmen and the staff of the Popilian Treasury of Quintus Poblicius, son of Quintus, of the Falernian tribe.
EDCS-18000380.summary: An inscription relating to the freedmen and staff of a public treasury associated with Quintus Poblicius.

EDCS-18000383.translation: Lucius Alfius Cerdo, freedman of Lucius, made (this) from his own (funds). Nasennia Prota, freedwoman of (a woman named) Nasennia. Nasennia, freedwoman of (a woman named) Nasennia. The bones of Pilematius are here, situated in front 4 feet, in depth 4 feet.
EDCS-18000383.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pilematius and several freedmen and freedwomen, likely set up by Lucius Alfius Cerdo.

EDCS-18000385.translation: To Caius Alfius Faustus, freedman of (a woman named) Alfia. To Alfia, daughter of Caius. To Alfius Tertius, freedman of (a woman named) Alfia. Alfius. Alive. Alive. V/[---].
EDCS-18000385.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph, possibly for several members of the Alfii family, with some individuals noted as 'alive', suggesting it might have been prepared in advance.

EDCS-18100411.translation: To Aegippus. To Lucius Mussius Aemilianus Laurentius of Lavinium, distinguished military man, prefect of the transport service of three provinces, Gallia Lugdunensis, Narbonensis, and Aquitanica, for HS 60,000; procurator of Alexandria, Pelusium, Pharos(?), for HS 100,000; procurator of both harbours, for HS 200,000; to the most honest man, the boatmen and ferrymen and members of the five guilds, for his remarkable benevolence and singular integrity towards them. Dedicated on the 15th day before the Kalends of June, under our Lords the Augusti, in their consulship, under the supervision of Numnius Hermogenes and Aurelius Paulinus, quinquennials.
EDCS-18100411.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Mussius Aemilianus, a high-ranking official with financial responsibilities in transportation and harbours, by various professional groups for his integrity and benevolence, dated to the consulship of the Philips.

EDCS-18100842.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Lucius Manlius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Palatina tribe, sacred official, priest of the Roman people four times, duovir of Bovillae, to the sweetest and most indulgent [relative/friend]. Made for him.
EDCS-18100842.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Manlius Severus, honoring his religious and civic roles, likely erected by a close relative or friend.

EDCS-18100915.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-18100915.translation: To Manius Fictorius Synhistus, curator of the aediculae (shrines) of Castor and Pollux, for 16 years; aedile of Castor and Pollux for 6 years, after death, in honor.
EDCS-18100915.summary: This inscription records the service of Manius Fictorius Synhistus as curator and aedile for the shrines of Castor and Pollux, with the honors granted posthumously.

EDCS-18200284.translation: Numerius Baebius Viriatus, freedman of Numerius. Bottia Isia, freedwoman of Caius. Numerius Baebius Philostratus, freedman of Numerius. Lucius Baebius Rufus, son of Numerius. Numerius Baebius Philomusus, freedman of Numerius. Lucius Baebius Laches, freedman of Numerius.
EDCS-18200284.summary: This is a list of names, likely recording members of the Baebius family and their freedmen, possibly for a funerary monument or a legal document.

EDCS-18200285.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, mentioning a freedman named Hilarus and a person named Iusta.

EDCS-18200399.translation: Quintus Sedecianus Silvanus and Accia Dorcas (erected this) for their son Quintus Sedecianus Rufinus, who lived 30 years. To their most unhappy son.
EDCS-18200399.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents for their deceased son, Quintus Sedecianus Rufinus.

EDCS-18300821.text_edition: \n Marco Alleio Quinti filio Menenia Minio IIviro iure dicundo locus sepulturae publice datus ex decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-18300821.translation: To Marcus Alleius, son of Quintus, of the Menenia tribe. A burial plot was publicly granted by decree of the decurions to Minio, who held the office of IIvir iure dicundo.
EDCS-18300821.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial plot to Minio, who held the office of IIvir iure dicundo, by the local council.

EDCS-18700532.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. This place consecrated to Iulia Lucana, who lived 4 years, 7 months, and 8 days.
EDCS-18700532.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Iulia Lucana, indicating the consecration of her burial place.

EDCS-18900005.translation: To the departed spirits. For [3]o Achilleus, freedman of Augustus, a nomenclator from the censuales, who lived 35 years. [3]t Porcia Crescentina made this for her well-deserving husband, with whom she lived 3 years, 3 months, 11 days.
EDCS-18900005.summary: A funerary epitaph for a nomenclator, set up by his wife.

EDCS-18900050.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Lycus, freedman of Augustus, tutor of the boys from Africa(!).
EDCS-18900050.summary: A funerary epitaph for a tutor of boys, identified as a freedman of the emperor.

EDCS-19000540.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Numisius Verissimus, Roman knight, who lived 27 years. Gaius Numisius Nepos, centurion, (made this) for his most dutiful brother, and for Pedania Marcela, his dearest wife, with whom he lived 7 years, (made this) for her deserving of favor.
EDCS-19000540.summary: A funerary epitaph for a Roman knight and his wife, set up by his brother.

EDCS-19301055.translation: Hail... who lived 4 years, 9 months. [3] Ampliata for her daughter.
EDCS-19301055.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for a child, with a partial dedication from her mother.

EDCS-19301057.translation: Quintus Ancharius Licinus, freedman of Quintus, and his co-freedwomen, Baccini. The bones are located here for himself and his fellow freedmen.
EDCS-19301057.summary: An epitaph for a freedman, indicating his burial place and mentioning his co-freedmen and freedwomen.

EDCS-19301058.translation: For Sextus Annius Gluco, freedman of Sextus. Tullia Antigona, freedwoman of Lucius, made this. In the field, 2 feet in front, 2 feet.
EDCS-19301058.summary: A funerary epitaph for a freedman, with precise measurements of the burial plot, set up by his freedwoman.

EDCS-19301059.translation: Lucius Annius Nicephor, freedman of freedmen of Caius, is buried here, and for his good friends. Philargurus, a fellow freedman, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-19301059.summary: An epitaph for a freedman, with a mention of burial for himself and his friends, commissioned by a fellow freedman.

EDCS-19301061.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Anna Proba Plotina, who lived 2 years, 11 months, 15 days.
EDCS-19301061.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young child, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-19301063.translation: Anthus Suettius lived 15 years, modest and chaste. Publius Suettius, his father, made this for his Anthus. The bones are located here.
EDCS-19301063.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young man, set up by his father.

EDCS-19301064.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Antonius Ponticus, who lived 8 years, 10 months, 19 days.
EDCS-19301064.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-19301065.text_edition: \n Antonia Marci liberta Urbana viva fecit sibi et Marco Antonio Davi liberto Philarguro patrono et Caio Raio Cai et |(mulieris) liberto T viro suo\n
EDCS-19301065.translation: Antonia Urbana, freedwoman of Marcus, made this for herself and for Marcus Antonius Philargurus, freedman of Davus, her patron, and for her husband Gaius Raius, freedman of Caius, and for her husband.
EDCS-19301065.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a freedwoman for herself and her patron and husband.

EDCS-19301068.translation: Parents made this for Lucius Arrius Atectus of Surrentum, who lived for 6 years and 21 days.
EDCS-19301068.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by parents for their young son.

EDCS-19301071.translation: Here lies Asteria, a woman of most distinguished memory, most unfortunate, given in marriage as a girl, who lived 27 years, 3 months, and 17 days. Shed tears for me, husband, because fate brought me to you, and nothing after death.
EDCS-19301071.summary: This is a lamenting epitaph for a young woman who died shortly after marriage, set up by her grieving husband.

EDCS-19301077.translation: Lucius Aufustius Zethus, freedman of Lucius, made this while alive for himself and his family: for his wife Statia Nota, for Statia Cupita, freedwoman of the woman [owner?], for Secunda, a freedwoman, and for Felici, a slave.
EDCS-19301077.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a freedman for himself and his household, including his wife, another freedwoman, and a slave.

EDCS-19301080.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Athenio made this for his most beloved father, Marcus Aurelius Athenion.
EDCS-19301080.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by a son for his deceased father.

EDCS-19301081.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Parents [made this] for their most beloved son, Aurelius Auspicus.
EDCS-19301081.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by parents for their deceased son.

EDCS-19301082.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Metia Ianuaria [made this] for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Aurelius Hermippus, with whom she lived for 28 years without any quarrel.
EDCS-19301082.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by a wife for her deceased husband, emphasizing their harmonious marriage.

EDCS-19301083.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Hilara [made this] for her most beloved son, Marcus Aurelius Vilioni, who lived [about] 3 years and 1 month.
EDCS-19301083.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by a mother for her young deceased son.

EDCS-19301084.translation: For Aurelia Aquilirina, her most beloved daughter, who lived 17 years. Her miserable mother, Aelia Aquilia Dionysias, daughter of Titus, made this bedroom.
EDCS-19301084.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a young woman, made by her grieving mother.

EDCS-19301085.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Callistus, freedman of Augustus, and his daughter Herdonia Histricilla made this for their most beloved Aurelia Calliste, who lived 10 years, 1 month, and 18 days.
EDCS-19301085.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a father and daughter for their deceased daughter and sister.

EDCS-19301086.translation: Marcus Babullius Philomusus, freedman of Marcus, to himself and his family: his wife, son, and daughter Babullia Nebris Felix Cale. Their bones are placed here.
EDCS-19301086.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial place for Marcus Babullius Philomusus and his family.

EDCS-19301088.translation: The bones of Baebia are placed here.
EDCS-19301088.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph indicating the burial location of Baebia.

EDCS-19301089.translation: Grief-stricken by the long sorrow of his parents, do not press down this stone, boy Bebryx, for you cover the bones of a tender body. The parent stains the bones with tears and wears out the ashes with weeping. Alas, how pitiable you lie, Bebryx! Not yet twice seven years had life brought you, yet this pyre holds you, beautiful in song. Delight of his master, hope expected by his parents, a short time for the tomb's art. We testify that he learned pious arts. We also testify...
EDCS-19301089.summary: This is a lament on a tombstone for Bebryx, a boy who died young, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire, mourning his death and the grief of his parents.

EDCS-19301090.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Benia Felicissima. Caius Benius [---], Benius Felicissimus, Marcus Ostorius [---], Marcus Ostorius [---], incomparable. She lived 58 years, 2 months, 13 days. For his dearest wife.
EDCS-19301090.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Benia Felicissima, who lived 58 years, 2 months, and 13 days, and was possibly dedicated by her husband Caius Benius and Marcus Ostorius.

EDCS-19301091.translation: The bones of Betilienas, freedwoman of Philo, are placed here.
EDCS-19301091.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating the burial place of Betilienas, freedwoman of Philo.

EDCS-19301093.translation: Titus Blossius Nicetor, freedman of Titus, [and] Quintus Blossius Quintionis, freedman of Caius Marcus, Marcus [---]. Their bones are placed here.
EDCS-19301093.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial place for Titus Blossius Nicetor and Quintus Blossius Quintionis.

EDCS-19301094.translation: The bones of Bonitas Caesellia, daughter of Pudentis, are placed here. S.
EDCS-19301094.summary: This funerary epitaph indicates the burial location of Bonitas Caesellia, daughter of Pudentis.

EDCS-19301095.translation: Marcus Bruitius Diophantus, freedman of Marcus. Oppia Posilla, freedwoman of Publius. Marcus Bruitius Donatus, freedman of Marcus and his wife. Oppia Salvia, freedwoman of his wife. Bruittia Prisca, freedwoman of Marcus.
EDCS-19301095.summary: This funerary epitaph lists several individuals, likely family members and freedmen, buried together, including members of the Bruittius and Oppia families.

EDCS-19301097.translation: The bones of Bruittia Zmyrina, freedwoman of Publius, are placed here.
EDCS-19301097.summary: This funerary epitaph states the burial place of Bruittia Zmyrina, freedwoman of Publius.

EDCS-19301098.translation: Lucius Caecilius, son of Lucius. Lucius Caecilia Salvia made this. Quarta lived 9 years. Their bones are placed here.
EDCS-19301098.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Quarta, who lived 9 years, and was possibly created by Lucius Caecilius and Lucius Caecilia Salvia.

EDCS-19400173.translation: The bones of Caesellia Hymnis, freedwoman of Quintus, are located here; Helena, a freedwoman, made it at her own expense.
EDCS-19400173.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Caesellia Hymnis, commissioned by her freedwoman Helena.

EDCS-19400174.translation: I beg you, traveler, not to harm my property. Caesia Muscis, freedwoman of Lucius, had this made for herself and her kin with her own money. For her patron Lucius Caelsius Teretina, son of Quintus. For her freedman Quartus, her own slave. For her own slave.
EDCS-19400174.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caesia Muscis, commissioned by herself, and also mentions her patron and slaves.

EDCS-19400175.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Calpurnius Rufinus lived 9 years, 2 months, and... days.
EDCS-19400175.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Calpurnius Rufinus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-19400180.translation: Carisia Appia, freedwoman of [---], for herself and her husband Hordionius Cr[---], her husband, lived [---] years. This monument, or sepulcher, will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-19400180.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Carisia Appia and her husband Hordionius Cr[---], with a stipulation about inheritance.

EDCS-19400183.translation: The bones of Aulus Carvilius Teuriscus, freeborn son of Aulus Carvilius, are located here.
EDCS-19400183.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating the burial location of Aulus Carvilius Teuriscus.

EDCS-19400184.translation: For the memory of Carvilia Vitalis, who lived 24 years and 20 days. Servius Calpurnius Paris made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-19400184.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Servius Calpurnius Paris for his wife Carvilia Vitalis, recording her age at death.

EDCS-19400185.translation: For Manius Carulius Saturninus. Carulia Hilara, freedwoman of Manius, made this for herself and her kin. For Manius Carulius, freedman of Manius. Carulia Mystis lived [---]8 years. Manius Tyrannus lived 30 years.
EDCS-19400185.summary: This funerary inscription records Manius Carulius Saturninus, his freedwoman Carulia Hilara, and other family members including freedmen and slaves.

EDCS-19400186.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cassius Narcissus made this for his dearest wife, Cassia Primigenia, for himself and their kin and their descendants.
EDCS-19400186.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Cassius Narcissus for his wife Cassia Primigenia, intended for himself and their family.

EDCS-19400191.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Chaera, daughter. From her parents Nereis and Chaereas.
EDCS-19400191.summary: This is a funerary inscription from parents Nereis and Chaereas for their deceased daughter Chaera.

EDCS-19400198.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Tiberius Claudius Epaphroditus, freedman of Tiberius. From Tiberius Claudius Serenus Tacitus Quietinus, his most excellent father.
EDCS-19400198.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Tiberius Claudius Serenus Tacitus Quietinus for his father Tiberius Claudius Epaphroditus.

EDCS-19400199.translation: To the departed spirits. Sacred. For Tiberius Claudius Optatus, his incomparable freedman.
EDCS-19400199.summary: A funerary dedication to a freedman named Tiberius Claudius Optatus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19400200.translation: To the departed spirits. Sacred. Claudius Marcellus made this for his well-deserving sister, Claudia Marcellina.
EDCS-19400200.summary: A funerary dedication by Claudius Marcellus for his sister, Claudia Marcellina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19400201.translation: To the departed spirits. For Claudia Olympias, Tiberius Claudius Hermes made this for his incomparable wife, who lived 26 years, 7 months, and 18 days, to her who deserved well.
EDCS-19400201.summary: A funerary dedication by Tiberius Claudius Hermes for his wife, Claudia Olympias, who died at the age of 26, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19400202.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Clodius Candidus, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-19400202.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Clodius Candidus, who died at the age of 20, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19400204.translation: To the departed spirits. Sacred. Vibia Polla made this for her deserving husband, Gaius Clodius Florentinus, with whom she lived 17 years, 11 months, and 22 days, to him who deserved well.
EDCS-19400204.summary: A funerary dedication by Vibia Polla for her husband, Gaius Clodius Florentinus, detailing their 17 years of marriage, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19400207.translation: Marcus Cloelius Nicephor, Marcus Cloelius Dasius, Marcus Cloelius Agato made this for the bones of Marcus Cloelius Nicephor, freedman of Marcus (father?), which are placed here.
EDCS-19400207.summary: A funerary monument indicating the burial place of Marcus Cloelius Nicephor and possibly other family members, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19400210.translation: To the departed spirits. Sacred. For Lucius Cominius Felix Ostorianus. Decidia Merop[e] for her deserving wife.
EDCS-19400210.summary: A funerary dedication by Decidia Merop[e] for her deserving husband, Lucius Cominius Felix Ostorianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19400213.translation: The bones of Lucius Cornelius Atticus, freedman of a woman, are placed here.
EDCS-19400213.summary: An inscription marking the burial place of Lucius Cornelius Atticus, a freedman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19400214.translation: Lucius Cornelius Benevolus, freedman of Lucius, for himself and for his patron Lucius Cornelius Falernus, son of Lucius; for his mother Cornelia Helena, freedwoman of Lucius; for his brother Lucius Cornelius Opsequens, freedman of Lucius; for his brother Lucius Cornelius Utilis, freedman of Lucius; for his wife Cytherini, freedwoman of a woman; for his freedman Lucius Cornelius Iucundus, freedman of Lucius; and for Cornelia Primigenia, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-19400214.summary: A multi-person funerary monument set up by Lucius Cornelius Benevolus, a freedman, for himself and various family members and freedmen, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19400216.translation: To the departed spirits. Sacred. Publius Cornelius Iconius, father, and Nebia Donata, parents, for their daughter Adaugenda, who lived 2 years.
EDCS-19400216.summary: A funerary dedication by parents Publius Cornelius Iconius and Nebia Donata for their daughter Adaugenda, who died at the age of 2, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19400217.translation: The bones of Marcus Cornelius Phileros, freedman of Marcus, are placed here. [---] made them.
EDCS-19400217.summary: This inscription records the burial of Marcus Cornelius Phileros, with unclear information about who placed the bones or performed the action.

EDCS-19400218.translation: The bones of Cornelia Faustae are placed here. Marcus Cornelius, freedman of Marcus, her father, made [this].
EDCS-19400218.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Marcus Cornelius for his daughter Cornelia Faustae, indicating the placement of her bones and likely a monument.

EDCS-19400219.translation: Cornelia Talassa made [this] while alive for Marcus Cornelius Phileros and her own [family].
EDCS-19400219.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Cornelia Talassa for herself and Marcus Cornelius Phileros, indicating it was prepared during her lifetime.

EDCS-19400222.translation: The bones of Caius Curtius, son of Marcus, from Falerna, are placed here.
EDCS-19400222.summary: This inscription records the placement of the bones of Caius Curtius, son of Marcus, with a possible indication of his origin from Falerna.

EDCS-19400224.translation: Of Quintus Decirius Hermias, freedman of Quintus. Of Baebia Helena, freedwoman of Lucius. Of Quintus Decirius Amilcas, freedman of Quintus. Of Quintus Decirius Papia, freedman of Quintus.
EDCS-19400224.summary: This inscription lists the names of four individuals, likely family members or freedmen associated with Quintus Decirius, with their respective patrons indicated.

EDCS-19400225.translation: The bones of Quintus Decirius Licinus are placed here.
EDCS-19400225.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the placement of the bones of Quintus Decirius Licinus.

EDCS-19400228.translation: The bones of Deutera are placed here.
EDCS-19400228.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the placement of the bones of Deutera.

EDCS-19400230.translation: The bones of Dorinis are placed here.
EDCS-19400230.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the placement of the bones of Dorinis.

EDCS-19400232.translation: To the departed spirits. Ducenia Agatetyce, who lived VI years and VIII months. Her mother Ducenia Helpis, most unhappy.
EDCS-19400232.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a young girl named Ducenia Agatetyce, recording her age and dedicating her mother, Ducenia Helpis, as the arranger of the tomb.

EDCS-19400233.translation: [---] pater and Eglec/tus, her husband, lived XXII years, II months, and VIIII days.
EDCS-19400233.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of a person (likely female, given the context of a husband) and their spouse, with missing information about the first name.

EDCS-19400236.translation: The bones of Egnatia Hilaera, freedwoman of Gaius Aulus, are placed here. Gaius Egnatius Amphio, freedman of Gaius Aulus, and Zetus, freedman of a woman (possibly Egnatia Hilaera), her sons, have erected this.
EDCS-19400236.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by sons for their freedwoman mother, Egnatia Hilaera.

EDCS-19400237.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Egnatia Laudice, who lived 9 years and 21 days. Egnatius Antimas and Egnatia Laudice, her parents, made this for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-19400237.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl, Egnatia Laudice, erected by her parents.

EDCS-19400238.translation: The bones of Marcus Fufius Gaurus, freedman of Marcus, are placed here.
EDCS-19400238.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Fufius Gaurus.

EDCS-19400244.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Lucius Eppius Dracon. Tiberius Claudius Lascivus, his best friend, and his freedwoman Primilla have made this.
EDCS-19400244.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Eppius Dracon, dedicated by his friend and his friend's freedwoman.

EDCS-19400245.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Epria Marcia lived 7 years and 7 days.
EDCS-19400245.summary: A funerary epitaph for Epria Marcia, recording her short life.

EDCS-19400248.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Titus Etrius Marcellinus, son of Titus, of the Falerna tribe, who lived 17 years, 5 months. Coelia Gnome, a miserable mother, for her most devoted son.
EDCS-19400248.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Etrius Marcellinus, erected by his grieving mother.

EDCS-19400249.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For his sweetest son Eustasius. Epictetus the father made this for his deserving son.
EDCS-19400249.summary: A funerary epitaph for Eustasius, erected by his father.

EDCS-19400251.translation: [For...], his most innocent and obedient wife, with whom I lived 6 years without any complaint, in whom not a single fault could be found, who was the best or most pious [...]. Marcus Fabius Felix for his dearest wife, who lived 21 years, 5 months, 10 days [...]. Octavia Ianuaria, mother of the best daughter, who would have wished to bury me after her death and follow me to the end of life, if fate had not stood in the way [...].
EDCS-19400251.summary: A funerary epitaph for an exemplary wife, likely dating to the 2nd century CE, with extensive praise from her husband and a mention of her mother.

EDCS-19400256.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Gaius Ialpius Cedrus, his best father, well deserving. Titus Flavius Brundisinus, his son.
EDCS-19400256.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Ialpius Cedrus, erected by his son.

EDCS-19400257.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Lucius Flavius Optatus, who lived 7 months. Sextia Polytime made this for her sweetest infant.
EDCS-19400257.summary: A funerary epitaph for an infant, Lucius Flavius Optatus, erected by Sextia Polytime.

EDCS-19400258.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Flavius Priscus, with his wife Flavia Primilla and daughter Flavia Prisca, made this for their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-19400258.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Flavius Priscus for his family and freedpersons.

EDCS-19400261.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Flavia Prisca, his foster-daughter, whom he loved. Lucius Pontius Capitolinus made this.
EDCS-19400261.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Pontius Capitolinus for his beloved foster-daughter, Flavia Prisca.

EDCS-19400262.translation: For Fortunata, a well-deserving mother. Felix and Eros, her sons.
EDCS-19400262.summary: This is a funerary dedication from sons Felix and Eros to their deserving mother, Fortunata.

EDCS-19400265.translation: The remains of Fuficia Erotis, freedwoman of Aulus, lie here.
EDCS-19400265.summary: This funerary inscription records that the remains of Fuficia Erotis, a freedwoman, are interred here.

EDCS-19400266.translation: The remains of Gaius Fulmonius Antiochus, freedman of Gaius, lie here.
EDCS-19400266.summary: This funerary inscription records that the remains of Gaius Fulmonius Antiochus, a freedman, are interred here.

EDCS-19400267.translation: The remains of Gaius Fulmonius Metrophanes, freedman of Gaius, and Deivitis lie here. Freedmen, by the terms of the will, saw to this.
EDCS-19400267.summary: This funerary inscription states that the remains of Gaius Fulmonius Metrophanes and Deivitis are interred here, with arrangements made by freedmen as per the will.

EDCS-19400279.text_edition: \n Marcus Herennius Marci et Acciae libertus Bassus Marco Herennio co patrono et Acciae Marci et |(mulieris) libertae Faustae patronae et Herenniae Sympaschusae uxori libertaeque suae\n
EDCS-19400279.translation: Marcus Herennius Bassus, freedman of Marcus Herennius and of Accia Faustina, to his patron Marcus Herennius, and to his patroness Accia Faustina, and to his wife Herennia Sympaschusa, also a freedwoman.
EDCS-19400279.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Herennius Bassus, a freedman, to his patron, patroness, and wife.

EDCS-19400281.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Gaius Cominius Maximus.

EDCS-19400282.translation: The remains of Heria Secunda, freedwoman of Lucius, lie here. She lived 29 years. Her husband Pampilus made this.
EDCS-19400282.summary: This funerary inscription records that the remains of Heria Secunda, a freedwoman, are interred here, and that her husband Pampilus erected the monument.

EDCS-19400283.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Heria Silvina, who lived 17 years and 7 months. Gaius Herius Eucarpus and Heria Valentina made this for their most devoted daughter.
EDCS-19400283.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Gaius Herius Eucarpus and Heria Valentina for their devoted daughter, Heria Silvina.

EDCS-19400284.translation: The bones of Hilarius, slave of Marcus Clodius, are buried here. He lived 22 years, loving his master and annoying his friends.
EDCS-19400284.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hilarius, a slave, giving his age at death and a brief characterization.

EDCS-19400286.text_edition: \n Lucio Horatio Luci filio Horatia Silenium liberta dat\n
EDCS-19400286.translation: Silenius, a freedwoman, gives this to Lucius Horatius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-19400286.summary: This inscription records a dedication or endowment by a freedwoman to her former master.

EDCS-19400287.translation: To the Divine Manes. Hordeonia Aquilina, daughter, (erected this) for Titus Hordeonius Crescentes.
EDCS-19400287.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a daughter for her father.

EDCS-19400288.translation: The bones of Aulus Hordionius Eronis are buried here. Pactumeia Creste made (this).
EDCS-19400288.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Hordionius Eronis, with Pactumeia Creste as the dedicator.

EDCS-19400289.translation: In the front 12 feet, in the back 12 feet. For Sextus Hordius Eitynchanus.
EDCS-19400289.summary: This is a boundary inscription defining a plot of land measuring 12x12 feet, intended for Sextus Hordius Eitynchanus.

EDCS-19400290.translation: In the front 12 feet, in the back 12 feet. For Sextus Hordionius Epityncano.
EDCS-19400290.summary: This is a boundary inscription defining a plot of land measuring 12x12 feet, intended for Sextus Hordionius Epityncano.

EDCS-19400291.translation: In the front 10 [feet], in the back 12 feet. For Sextus Hordionius Epitynchani.
EDCS-19400291.summary: This is a boundary inscription defining a plot of land measuring 10x12 feet, intended for Sextus Hordionius Epitynchani.

EDCS-19400292.translation: In the front 12 feet, in the back 12 feet. For Sextus Hordionius Epityncani.
EDCS-19400292.summary: This is a boundary inscription defining a plot of land measuring 12x12 feet, intended for Sextus Hordionius Epityncani.

EDCS-19400294.translation: Quintus Hordionius, son of Quintus, freedman. [---]hes. Hordionia Philumina, freedwoman of Quintus. Quintus Hordionius, son of Quintus, freedman, Surus. Quintus Hordionius, freedman of the freedwoman of the Quinti (masters), Suavis, for himself and his patrons. The bones are buried here.
EDCS-19400294.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of multiple individuals, including freedmen and freedwomen, for themselves and their patrons, likely concerning a family tomb.

EDCS-19400297.translation: The bones of Caius Hostius Principis, freedman of Caius, are buried here.
EDCS-19400297.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Hostius Principis, a freedman, indicating the location of his burial.

EDCS-19400298.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Hostia Felix lived 24 years, 8 months, 21 days. Her miserable mother, Hostia Vitalis, [dedicated this].
EDCS-19400298.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hostia Felix, recording her age at death and dedicated by her mother.

EDCS-19400299.translation: The bones of Ostilia Psycara, freedwoman of Lucius, are placed here.
EDCS-19400299.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating the location of the remains of Ostilia Psycara.

EDCS-19400300.translation: The bones of Hostilia Ril[---], freedwoman of Lucius, are placed here. Centius, her fellow freedman, [dedicated this].
EDCS-19400300.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating the location of the remains of Hostilia Ril[---], dedicated by a fellow freedman.

EDCS-19400302.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Januaria, 18 years old, most dutiful daughter, her mother made [this] for her well-deserving [daughter].
EDCS-19400302.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Januaria, recording her age and dedicated by her mother for her dutifulness.

EDCS-19400304.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Ippius Secundinus, who lived 18 years and 3 months, Gaius Ippius Secundinus made [this] from his own funds.
EDCS-19400304.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Ippius Secundinus, recording his age and funded by himself.

EDCS-19400305.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Iteius Justus, my son, placed here.
EDCS-19400305.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iteius Justus, identified as the son of the dedicator.

EDCS-19400306.translation: The bones of Caius Iteius Stablio, freedman of Caius, are placed here.
EDCS-19400306.summary: This is a funerary epitaph stating the location of the remains of Caius Iteius Stablio.

EDCS-19400308.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Julius Hyacinthus, Julia Chrysis made [this] for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-19400308.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Hyacinthus, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-19400309.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Julius Minucius, son of Marcus, of the Palatina tribe, of the Auli[i?] family... [---]
EDCS-19400309.summary: This is a funerary epitaph fragment for Marcus Julius Minucius, recording his name, filiation, and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-19400311.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Julius Philumenus made [this] for his well-deserving wife, Julia Chetidon, and for his son, Caius Julius Ocrates.
EDCS-19400311.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julia Chetidon and Caius Julius Ocrates, dedicated by Caius Julius Philumenus, their husband and father respectively.

EDCS-19400312.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Julius Tertullinus, son of Soema, who lived 40 years and 8 days. Upotiana, his sorrowing mother, placed [this] for her incomparable son.
EDCS-19400312.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a mother to her deceased son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19400314.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Iulia Alce, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400316.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Iulia Vera, who lived 20 years and [about] 7 days. Iulia Festa, her most unhappy mother, made [this].
EDCS-19400316.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a mother to her deceased daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400317.translation: Iunachilia, daughter of Publius, of the Gal... tribe, for herself and her family // in the field 2 feet // [
EDCS-19400317.summary: An epitaph recording land boundaries and ownership, possibly for a family plot, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400319.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Laberia Gamicina, his most chaste and incomparable wife. Marcus Vitronius Paulus [dedicated this].
EDCS-19400319.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband to his deceased wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400321.translation: The bones of Neon, freedman of Lucius Licinius, are laid here.
EDCS-19400321.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial place of a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400322.translation: To Licinia Ariadna, her most excellent mother. Domitius Ruso [dedicated this].
EDCS-19400322.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a son to his deceased mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400323.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Licinia Threptes, his most excellent wife. Publius Aelius Epictetus [dedicated this].
EDCS-19400323.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband to his deceased wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400325.translation: The bones of Livinia Aphrodisia, freedwoman of Gnaeus, and of Felicula are laid here. Gnaeus Livinius, freedman of Gnaeus, and Aria, fellow freedman, made [this].
EDCS-19400325.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial place of two freedwomen, dedicated by their fellow freedmen, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400328.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucceia Tyche.
EDCS-19400328.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucceia Tyche, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400329.translation: Macaria lived 25 years. Her sisters Dionysia and Magia placed this.
EDCS-19400329.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Macaria, set up by her sisters, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400330.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mamia Spes, mother, to her most dutiful son, Marcus Mamius Ianurius, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-19400330.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Mamius Ianurius, set up by his mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400331.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Mammos, my dearest and incomparable wife, well-deserving. Butahides, husband, set this up for her. She lived 25 years.
EDCS-19400331.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mammos, set up by her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400334.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Marcus Marius Hyginianus, who lived 4 years, 7 months. Marcus Marius Callistianus, brother, set this up.
EDCS-19400334.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Marius Hyginianus, set up by his brother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400337.translation: Sacred to the spirits and in memory of Meta Restituta, who lived 36 years, 6 months. Mother, most beloved.
EDCS-19400337.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Meta Restituta, likely set up by her mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400340.translation: 10 feet in the territory of Aulus Minatus Philargyrus, to the left, within 30 feet from there...
EDCS-19400340.summary: This inscription appears to be a boundary marker or land division record, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400341.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Minucius Procillianus. Euhodus, freedman, set this up.
EDCS-19400341.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Minucius Procillianus, set up by his freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400342.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Modestus, servant of Caesar. Iulia Restituta, wife, set this up for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-19400342.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Modestus, servant of Caesar, set up by his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400343.translation: To the sacred departed. Chrysanthus, son of Gaius, lived 22 years, 8 months, 8 days. Crysis, mother, most unhappy, had this monument made for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-19400343.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Chrysanthus, set up by his mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400346.translation: Aciba, freedman, made this for Aulus Munatius Amunta, freedman of Aulus. His bones are here.
EDCS-19400346.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Munatius Amunta, set up by his fellow freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400347.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Munatius Augustalis, son of Marcus, lived for 1 year and 5 months. Marcus Munatius Fuscus made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-19400347.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Marcus Munatius Augustalis, set up by his father.

EDCS-19400348.translation: The bones of Lucius Munnio Alexander, freedman of Lucius, are placed here.
EDCS-19400348.summary: A funerary epitaph indicating the location of the remains of Lucius Munnio Alexander.

EDCS-19400350.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Munnia Restuta made this for her most sweet husband Cipio Campanus, with whom she lived for 31 years. Also for his patron Merenti, and for Cipia Lucilla. They made this.
EDCS-19400350.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a wife to her husband, and also to his patron and another individual named Cipia Lucilla.

EDCS-19400351.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Florentinus made this for his incomparable and well-deserving wife Myrine.
EDCS-19400351.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Florentinus to his wife Myrine.

EDCS-19400352.translation: Quintus Naevius Primus, freedman of a woman, 9 years old. His bones are placed here. He was snatched away.
EDCS-19400352.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Quintus Naevius Primus, noting his age and premature death.

EDCS-19400353.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Naevia Severa, who lived 6 years. Made by Marcia Agatetyche for her daughter.
EDCS-19400353.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Naevia Severa, set up by her mother.

EDCS-19400359.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For his most chaste wife Faustina, daughter of Tiberius. For Nicenus Auspicalis, slave, and for himself.
EDCS-19400359.summary: A funerary epitaph for Faustina, dedicated by her slave Nicenus Auspicalis, who also provided for himself.

EDCS-19400361.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Gaius Novius Proculus, son of Gaius. For Gaius Fisus Saturus, son of Gaius. For Gaius Fisus Saturus, husband. Novia Procula for herself and her family.
EDCS-19400361.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Novia Procula for her husband and two stepsons, for herself and her family.

EDCS-19400362.translation: The bones of Sabda, freedwoman of Gaius Novius. Novia Arbuscula, freedwoman of Gaius, made this. They are placed here.
EDCS-19400362.summary: A funerary monument indicating the placement of the remains of Sabda and possibly Novia Arbuscula.

EDCS-19400363.translation: From the will of Gnaeus Numidius Berullus. For his son Lucius Allius Quartinus, son of Lucius, who lived 7 years, 5 months. And for his concubine Allia Nysa. And for himself, and for his freedman Primogenus, and his freedmen, and for Gaius Valerius Melanta, and for Gaius Rufelleius Chilo. This monument or external tomb shall not follow the heir.
EDCS-19400363.summary: A funerary inscription from a will detailing provisions for a son, concubine, freedmen, and other individuals, with specific instructions regarding the monument.

EDCS-19400366.translation: Numisia Glapira, freedwoman of a woman, made this monument for herself while alive, with an image of Marcus Vibullius Septimus, freedman of Marcus. This monument or tomb does not follow an heir.
EDCS-19400366.summary: A funerary monument erected by a freedwoman for herself, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19400542.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Plotius Hermippus, the purple-dyer.
EDCS-19400542.summary: A funerary dedication to Lucius Plotius Hermippus, a purple-dyer, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600007.translation: The bones of Caius Publilius Philemon, freedman of a woman, are here.
EDCS-19600007.summary: A funerary inscription stating the location of the bones of Caius Publilius Philemon, a freedman, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600008.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. For Publilia Modesta. Pomponia Severa, daughter, and Quintus Pomponius Severus, husband, [erected this] for one deserving well.
EDCS-19600008.summary: A funerary dedication by a daughter and husband for Publilia Modesta, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600009.translation: Pullia Epicaris, freedwoman of Numerius, made [this] for Numerius Pullius Faustus, freedman of Numerius, fellow freedman. The bones are here.
EDCS-19600009.summary: A funerary epitaph created by a freedwoman for her fellow freedman, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600011.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. For Quartio Viipsidius, who lived 90 years, son. [Erected] for a deserving father.
EDCS-19600011.summary: A funerary dedication for Quartio Viipsidius, who lived 90 years, erected by his son, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600015.translation: The bones of Raecia Pamphila, of Puupa Nuna, are here.
EDCS-19600015.summary: A funerary inscription indicating the location of bones, with partially legible names, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600017.translation: The bones of Publius Rammius Erotis are here. Fausta, freedwoman, made [this] for herself and her family.
EDCS-19600017.summary: A funerary epitaph established by a freedwoman for herself and her family, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600021.translation: Gaius Sabidius, uncle of Paetus, and Cusia Hilma, mother, and Paetus, son.
EDCS-19600021.summary: An inscription listing family members: Gaius Sabidius, his nephew Paetus, Paetus's mother Cusia Hilma, and Paetus the son; likely a funerary context from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600023.translation: The bones of Quintus Salevius, son of Marcus, are here.
EDCS-19600023.summary: A funerary inscription stating the location of the bones of Quintus Salevius, son of Marcus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600026.translation: To the departed spirits. To Salonia Augustina, freedwoman of Marcus, for whom Quintus Vibius Clarus lived for 20 years without any complaint.
EDCS-19600026.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Salonia Augustina, dedicated by her husband Quintus Vibius Clarus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600028.translation: The bones of Salvia, freedwoman of Sextus, are placed here.
EDCS-19600028.summary: An epitaph stating that the remains of Salvia, freedwoman of Sextus, are interred here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600030.translation: To the departed spirits. To Saturnina, sweetest daughter, who lived 7 years, 5 months, and 13 days; her mother had this made for her deserving daughter.
EDCS-19600030.summary: A tombstone for a young girl named Saturnina, dedicated by her mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600031.translation: To the departed spirits. To Saufeia Secundilla, deserving. Saufeia Daphne, freedwoman.
EDCS-19600031.summary: A tombstone for Saufeia Secundilla, dedicated by her freedwoman Saufeia Daphne, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600033.translation: To the departed spirits. To Secundus, sweetest; he lived 23 years. Parents made [this] for their son.
EDCS-19600033.summary: A tombstone for Secundus, dedicated by his parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600034.translation: To the departed spirits. For Sedatus. Campania Phronime to her father, who lived 53 years.
EDCS-19600034.summary: A tombstone for Sedatus, dedicated by his daughter Campania Phronime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600036.translation: The bones of Seia Flora, freedwoman of the woman Italia, are placed here. Italia, freedwoman, made [this].
EDCS-19600036.summary: An epitaph stating that the remains of Seia Flora, freedwoman of Italia, are interred here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600037.translation: To the departed spirits. Of Titus Cornelius Sempronius Sabinus, who lived 1 year and 20 days.
EDCS-19600037.summary: A tombstone recording the short life of Titus Cornelius Sempronius Sabinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600041.translation: To the departed spirits. Severus to his deserving mother and [son?].
EDCS-19600041.summary: A tombstone likely dedicated by Severus to his mother and possibly a son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600045.translation: To the departed spirits. For Sextia Aeliana, best friend. Petisana Gemniana and her freedman Sextius Darius, and also for Catenia Ampelis, her daughter, they made [this].
EDCS-19600045.summary: A tombstone dedicated by Petisana Gemniana and Sextius Darius to their friend Sextia Aeliana and her daughter Catenia Ampelis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600047.translation: The bones of Philēmation, freedwoman of a woman, are here.
EDCS-19600047.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that the bones of Philēmation, a freedwoman, are interred here.

EDCS-19600048.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sittia Crescentina, daughter of Publius, to her daughter Sittia Philomena, most excellent.
EDCS-19600048.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Sittia Philomena to her daughter Sittia Crescentina.

EDCS-19600384.summary: This is a tombstone epitaph for Florus, a gardener, stating his age at death.

EDCS-19600519.translation: The bones of Sabinna, freedwoman of Aulus, daughter of Aulus Staberius, are placed here.
EDCS-19600519.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the placement of the bones of Sabinna, a freedwoman.

EDCS-19600520.translation: The bones of Staberia Flora, freedwoman of Publius, are placed here. I ask you, traveler, do not harm me. I ask you, traveler, do not harm me.
EDCS-19600520.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Staberia Flora, with a plea to travelers not to disturb her remains.

EDCS-19600522.translation: The bones of Lucius Stertinius Faustus, son of Marcus of the Falernian tribe, are placed here. Faustus, his freedman, from his own means. He has been honored.
EDCS-19600522.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Stertinius Faustus, with his freedman Faustus as the dedicator.

EDCS-19600523.translation: To the departed spirits. Vettia Maga and her daughter made this for Lucius Stertinius Syle, dearest husband.
EDCS-19600523.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Vettia Maga and her daughter to Lucius Stertinius Syle, identified as a husband.

EDCS-19600525.translation: The bones of Publius Suetius Musicus, freedman of Publius, are placed here.
EDCS-19600525.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating the placement of the bones of Publius Suetius Musicus, a freedman.

EDCS-19600526.translation: The bones of Suetia Trophime, freedwoman of a woman, are placed here. Gnaeus Sextius Gorgia, freedman of Gnaeus, made this.
EDCS-19600526.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the placement of the bones of Suetia Trophime, with the maker identified as Gnaeus Sextius Gorgia.

EDCS-19600527.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred to Sulpicius Honoratus, who lived 23 years, 2 months, and 24 days. Sulpicia Calliste to her son, who deserved well.
EDCS-19600527.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Sulpicius Honoratus, erected by his mother, Sulpicia Calliste, and includes specific details of his age at death.

EDCS-19600528.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Sulpicia Calliste, a most excellent woman. With [the] heirs.
EDCS-19600528.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Sulpicia Calliste, set up by her heirs, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-19600530.translation: Gnaeus Taracius, son of Gnaeus, lived 20 years; his bones are laid here. Alas, alas, Taracei, you were given over to a bitter fate not by the exhaustion of your life, but handed over to death. But since it was fitting for you to flourish in youth, you perished and left your mother in mourning.
EDCS-19600530.summary: This is an epitaph lamenting the premature death of Gnaeus Taracius at the age of 20, possibly from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-19600532.translation: To the memory of Marcus Terentius Eutychus, who lived 28 years. Aurelia Alexandria made this for her incomparable and deserving husband, with whom she lived for 5 years.
EDCS-19600532.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Terentius Eutychus, erected by his wife Aurelia Alexandria, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-19600534.translation: To memory. For Theresia Epiteuxis. Thresia and Publius M. C. ordered (this) to be made.
EDCS-19600534.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Theresia Epiteuxis, commissioned by Thresia and Publius M. C., likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-19600535.translation: Bones of Itochares, freedman of Marcus Tintorius (woman), and of Nicostrata, freedwoman of Marcus Tintorius.
EDCS-19600535.summary: This inscription records the burial place of Itochares and Nicostrata, freedpersons of Marcus Tintorius, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-19600536.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Titia Claudiana. Vultricius Rufinus and Rufonianus (made this) for (their) mother.
EDCS-19600536.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Titia Claudiana, erected by her children Vultricius Rufinus and Rufonianus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-19600537.translation: For Titia Dorcha, freedwoman of Gaius. Quintus Florius Liccaeus, freedman of Quintus. For Faustus, the darling. Quintus Florius Faustus, freedman of Quintus. Quintus Florius Liccaeus, freedman of Quintus, made (this) for himself and his own. Bones are laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. ... under the guidance of Pull[---]
EDCS-19600537.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Quintus Florius Liccaeus and Quintus Florius Faustus, possibly brothers and freedmen, for themselves, their relatives, and Titia Dorcha, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-19600541.translation: Cecilia Secunda for herself and for Gaius Torasius and Publius Confuleius.
EDCS-19600541.summary: This funerary inscription records Cecilia Secunda dedicating a monument for herself and two other individuals, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-19600543.translation: ...ia. Gnaeus Trebius Hermia, freedman of Gnaeus. Trebia Ge, freedwoman of (a woman). Trebia Veneria made (this) for herself and her own, and placed (her) bones here.
EDCS-19600543.summary: This funerary inscription records Trebia Veneria dedicating a burial plot for herself and her family, including freedpersons, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-19600544.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Gaius Trebius Venerianus, who lived 15 years and 10 months. Gaius Enuleius Venerius and Ennia Restuta (made this) for (their) dearest son.
EDCS-19600544.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Trebius Venerianus, who died at the age of 15 years and 10 months, erected by his parents, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-19600545.text_edition: \n Marcus Trebulanus Philomusi libertus Faustus Deciria |(mulieris) liberta Lais fecerunt sibi et Marco Trebulano Philomuso Deciriae |(mulieris) libertae Secundae Fausti Marco Trebulano Primogenio qui vixit Trebulano et fratri\n
EDCS-19600545.translation: Marcus Trebulanus Faustus, freedman of Philomus, and Lais, freedwoman of Deciria, made this for themselves and for Marcus Trebulanus Philomus, for Secunda, freedwoman of Deciria, and for Faustus, and for Marcus Trebulanus Primogenio, who lived, and for Trebulanus and his brother.
EDCS-19600545.summary: This inscription records the construction of a tomb by freedmen for themselves and other family members.

EDCS-19600549.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Turellia Staberia, daughter, to her excellent mother, Turellia Secunda, also called Staberia.
EDCS-19600549.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by a daughter to her mother.

EDCS-19600554.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Valeria, who lived 8 years, 8 months, and 21 days and 10 hours, who deserved well, her mother Valeria Felicissima made this.
EDCS-19600554.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life of a girl named Valeria and was set up by her mother.

EDCS-19600555.translation: To the memory of Valeria Euterpe. Marcus Licinius Olypis, her husband, made this for his deserving wife, with whom he lived for 25 years and 7 months.
EDCS-19600555.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Valeria Euterpe and was made by her husband, mentioning their years of marriage.

EDCS-19600558.translation: Lucius Varius Apella, freedman of Lucius, for himself and his family. To Varia Sabbinis, freedwoman of Lucius, his wife, who lived 28 years. To Varia Helena Sabbinis, freedwoman of Lucius, his mother. To Titus Sextius Erotes Sabbinis, freedman of Titus, his brother. To Lucius Varius Synerotes, freedman of Lucius, his fellow freedman. To Lucius Octavius Cimbros, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-19600558.summary: This funerary inscription honors Lucius Varius Apella, his wife, mother, brother, and fellow freedmen, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-19600559.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Varius Crescens made this for his brother, Caius Varius Proculus, who deserved well.
EDCS-19600559.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument erected by a brother for his deceased sibling.

EDCS-19600563.translation: The bones of Vergilia Conchis, freedwoman of Vergilia, are placed here.
EDCS-19600563.summary: This inscription marks a burial place for Vergilia Conchis, a freedwoman.

EDCS-19600565.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vescinia Eleutheris, for herself and her family, to Titus Vescinus Rufus, son of Titus, her patron, and to Titus Vescinus Rufus, son of Spurius.
EDCS-19600565.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Vescinia Eleutheris to her patron and another individual named Titus Vescinus Rufus.

EDCS-19600566.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cipius Felix made this for his deserving wife, Veseria Marcianenis, with whom he lived for 25 years.
EDCS-19600566.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument erected by Cipius Felix for his wife, Veseria Marcianenis, noting their years of marriage.

EDCS-19600573.translation: The bones of Vibbius Servus, freedman of Lucius, are placed here.
EDCS-19600573.summary: This inscription marks the burial place of Vibbius Servus, a freedman.

EDCS-19600574.translation: Vibius Geminus and Valeria Dignitas for their grandson Valerius Geminus, who lived five years, seven months, and two days.
EDCS-19600574.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of a young boy named Valerius Geminus, dedicated by his relatives.

EDCS-19600575.text_edition: \n Titus Vibius filius Rufus Titia Flavia Quarta posuit\n
EDCS-19600575.translation: Titus Vibius Rufus, son, and Titia Flavia Quarta placed this.
EDCS-19600575.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the placement of a monument by Titus Vibius Rufus and Titia Flavia Quarta.

EDCS-19600577.translation: Publius Vibuleius Primus lived 15 years, 2 months, and 25 days. Lucius Tarquinius Salutaris, his brother, lived 15 years, 1 month, and 6 days. To their parents for their sons, dedicated to the departed spirits.
EDCS-19600577.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of two brothers, Publius Vibuleius Primus and Lucius Tarquinius Salutaris, and their dedication to their parents.

EDCS-19600578.translation: Vibuleia Epigenia made this for Princeps, her freedman, and for Aulus Vibuleius Alexander, her freedman colleague.
EDCS-19600578.summary: This funerary epitaph records Vibuleia Epigenia dedicating a monument to her freedman Princeps and a freedman colleague, Aulus Vibuleius Alexander.

EDCS-19600579.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Marcus Vibuleius Receptus for his sister Vibuleia Euphrosyne, his best sister.
EDCS-19600579.summary: This funerary epitaph records Marcus Vibuleius Receptus dedicating a monument to his sister Vibuleia Euphrosyne.

EDCS-19600585.translation: The bones of Vitellia Muschinis, freedwoman of Lucius, lie here. Lucius Vitellius Agato, freedman of Lucius, her freedman colleague, and Vitellia Quarta, daughter, and Quintius, son, placed this, and Vitellia Tertulla, daughter, made this for herself.
