EDCS-19600585.summary: This funerary epitaph records the placement of remains and the construction of a tomb by family members and a freedman colleague for Vitellia Muschinis and for Vitellia Tertulla.

EDCS-19600587.translation: The bones of Caius Volcacius Amphion, freedman of Caius, lie here. Neria Iliona, his freedwoman wife, made this.
EDCS-19600587.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Caius Volcacius Amphion and the monument dedicated by his wife Neria Iliona.

EDCS-19600589.translation: The bones of Lucius Voluntilius Nicephorus, freedman of Lucius, lie here.
EDCS-19600589.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Lucius Voluntilius Nicephorus.

EDCS-19600592.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Zetus made this for his pious son Campanus, and for himself and his wife Kallaid.
EDCS-19600592.summary: This funerary epitaph records Zetus dedicating a monument to his son Campanus and to himself and his wife Kallaid, noting Campanus lived 33 years, 11 months, and 3 days.

EDCS-19600593.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. For Zosaria, his dearest wife, who lived with me [---]
EDCS-19600593.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication to a wife named Zosaria, likely from her husband, noting her lifespan is incomplete due to damage.

EDCS-19600618.translation: The bones are placed here. [---] for Asicei and [---] Apella and [---] Terentia, concubine, made (this).
EDCS-19600618.summary: This funerary inscription records the placement of bones and the person who provided the tomb, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600622.translation: Antuos lived 70 years. He made (this) for himself and for his descendants.
EDCS-19600622.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Antuos and states that he commissioned the tomb for himself and his descendants, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19600623.translation: The bones of Ollopani are placed here.
EDCS-19600623.summary: This very brief funerary inscription indicates the placement of the remains of Ollopani, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19700209.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Liberalis, prefect of the engineers, of the equestrian order, his most excellent, most pious, sweetest, and most missed comrade. He lived 16 years, 5 months, and 21 days. His most unhappy parents (made this).
EDCS-19700209.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Tiberius Claudius Liberalis, detailing his age at death and his familial relationships, commissioned by his parents, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19700718.translation: I have entrusted the most holy body, Irene, to you, together with the holy ones. Quinta, farewell in peace.
EDCS-19700718.summary: This inscription appears to be a spiritual message or dedication concerning the transfer of a 'most holy body' to Irene, possibly related to a tomb or reliquary, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19700720.translation: Marturius the innocent, who lived 2 years, 10 months, and 2 days, is placed here. His deserving parents made (this). He was laid to rest on the Kalends of October.
EDCS-19700720.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Marturius, including his young age and the date of his burial, commissioned by his parents, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19700722.translation: Here rests in the sleep of peace Noella, who lived more or less 16 years. She was deposited on the 4th day before the Nones of November.
EDCS-19700722.summary: This funerary inscription records the resting place and approximate age of Noella, along with the date of her burial, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19700723.translation: Paccius Trebatius is placed here on the 2nd day before the Kalends of July.
EDCS-19700723.summary: This funerary inscription records the placement of Paccius Trebatius and the date of his burial, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19700741.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Most Bountiful, Hearer, Restorer. Titus Varius Agrippinus pays his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-19700741.summary: This votive inscription records Titus Varius Agrippinus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19700747.translation: To Lucius Alfius Fannius Primus, quaestor, curator of grain, duumvir who held all offices and priesthoods, patron and curator, [---] Aulus Piso, he adorned with gold and silver at great cost the baths, also the Constantinian baths, which had been corrupted by long age, from his resources. Also [---] from the Sabinian order, for his son, for a well-deserving patron, the most excellent duovir, the senate and people of Trebulanus decreed and dedicated a statue.
EDCS-19700747.summary: This lengthy inscription details the public career of Lucius Alfius Fannius Primus, listing his numerous offices and honors, and records the dedication of a statue and the restoration of baths in his honor by the senate and people of Trebulanus, likely dating to the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-19700752.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Terentius Charinus, son of Gaius, of the Palatine tribe, prefect for administering justice in the territory of Mount Diana Tifatina. Gaius Terentius Hypercompus [erected this] for his good son, against his vow.
EDCS-19700752.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated to Gaius Terentius Charinus, who served as a prefect, by his father.

EDCS-19700758.translation: To fortunate Venus. Publius Servilius Aprilis, son of Publius, of the Falernian tribe, twice duovir for five years, quaestor of the treasury, quaestor of public provisions, patron of the municipality of Caiatia, curator of the public calendar of the same municipality, made [this] at his own expense.
EDCS-19700758.summary: This is a public building inscription by Publius Servilius Aprilis, a prominent local official, dedicating a work to Venus at his own expense.

EDCS-19700761.translation: To Drusus, son of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, grandson of the divine Augustus, great-grandson of the divine Julius, consul, pontiff.
EDCS-19700761.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Drusus, son of Tiberius Caesar, a member of the imperial family.

EDCS-19700762.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate V consul III cubulterinis marmoribus exornavit pecunia sua\n
EDCS-19700762.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul for the third time, adorned the cubicularii with marble at his own expense.
EDCS-19700762.summary: This is a public building inscription by Emperor Hadrian, dedicating ornamental marble work to cubicularii at his own expense.

EDCS-19700774.translation: Marcus Gavius Gallus, son of Titus, and Quintus Visellius Gallus, son of Quintus, as duovirs for five years, made the paving around the forum at their own expense.
EDCS-19700774.summary: This public works inscription records the paving of the forum by two duovirs, Marcus Gavius Gallus and Quintus Visellius Gallus, at their own expense.

EDCS-19700780.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Servilius Successus, freedman of Publius, Aug(ustalis), gratis of Caiatia, master of Apollinaris, for himself and Cluvia, his companion in barracks. [These] are buried here. This monument will not follow the heir.
EDCS-19700780.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Publius Servilius Successus, a freedman and local official, and his companion Cluvia, specifying that the monument is not inheritable.

EDCS-19700783.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Raia Sabina, freedwoman of Gaius, mother of Sabinus Secundus, son of Gaius Rai Gemini, a paymaster.
EDCS-19700783.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by Raia Sabina to her son Sabinus Secundus, who was a paymaster.

EDCS-19700785.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. For Aletia Protogenia, daughter of Aletius. Primitivus and Vitalis [erected this] for their most devoted mother.
EDCS-19700785.summary: This is a tomb inscription erected by Primitivus and Vitalis for their mother, Aletia Protogenia.

EDCS-19700786.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. For Antia Soteris, who lived 10 years, 10 months, and 19 days. Ampliatus [erected this] for his sweetest daughter.
EDCS-19700786.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Antia Soteris, who died at a young age, dedicated by her father Ampliatus.

EDCS-19700787.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. For Caecina Marcianus, who lived 10 years, 10 months, and 13 days. Caecina Iucundus [erected this] for his dearest son.
EDCS-19700787.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Caecina Marcianus, who died at a young age, dedicated by his father Caecina Iucundus.

EDCS-19700788.translation: The bones of Caesellia Salvia are here placed; her mother made [this].
EDCS-19700788.summary: A funerary inscription for Caesellia Salvia, made by her mother, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19700789.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Achilleus, the actor, made [this] for his most cherished wife, Castricia Faustina, who deserved well.
EDCS-19700789.summary: A funerary dedication for Castricia Faustina by her husband Achilleus, an actor, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19700790.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Caius Filius Priscus. Maria, his unhappy mother.
EDCS-19700790.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Filius Priscus, dedicated by his mother Maria, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19700796.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Lucius Pacideius Carpio. Lucius Pacideius Carpianus [made this] for his excellent father.
EDCS-19700796.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Pacideius Carpio by his son, Lucius Pacideius Carpianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19700805.translation: Sacred to the Bona Dea. Julius Exuperius dedicated [this].
EDCS-19700805.summary: A dedication to the Bona Dea by Julius Exuperius, the precise date is uncertain but likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19700807.translation: For Drusus, son of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, grandson of the deified Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Julius Caesar, consul, pontifex, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-19700807.summary: An honorary inscription for Drusus, likely commemorating his honors and appointments, dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-19700809.translation: For Marcus Aulius Albinus, son of Marcus, prefect of the first cohort of Breuci, twice quinquennial duovir, quaestor, curator of the public calendar of Cubulternum, patron; and for the Augustales, for the duovir, quinquennial, quaestor, patron of Allifis; the place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-19700809.summary: An honorary inscription honoring Marcus Aulius Albinus as patron of Cubulternum and Allifis, likely dating to the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-19700810.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-19700810.translation: Lucius Fulvius Quintilianus lived 21 years and 2 months. Lucius Fulvius Clemens, scribe of the public administration and Augustalis, master of Cubulteria, and Fadia Vitalis, daughter of Caius, made [this] for their dearest son.
EDCS-19700810.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Fulvius Quintilianus, set up by his father Lucius Fulvius Clemens and mother Fadia Vitalis, from Cubulteria, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-19700815.translation: Publius Medioleius Sextanus, son of Marcus, of the Tromentina tribe, ordered this to be made for himself in his will.
EDCS-19700815.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Publius Medioleius Sextanus in his will, likely from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-19700827.text_edition: \n Praefecti o<per=DII>(is Ministrei Mentis Bonae Samalchio Vitrasi Aeschinus Clodi Zannaeus Clodi Tertius Curti Philippus Hosti Caccabio Vitrasi Communio Ticidi Cerdo Marsi Tertius Marsi Epa Vitrasi Telepus Vitrasi Apollonius Vitrasi \n
EDCS-19700827.translation: The prefects of the Works of the Good Spirit (or 'of the Minister of the Good Spirit'): Samalchius Vitrasi, Aeschinus Clodi, Zannaeus Clodi, Tertius Curti, Philippus Hosti, Caccabius Vitrasi, Communio Ticidi, Cerdo Marsi, Tertius Marsi, Epa Vitrasi, Telepus Vitrasi, Apollonius Vitrasi.
EDCS-19700827.summary: A list of officials, likely associated with a religious or civic college or public works, possibly from a provincial setting in the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-19700829.translation: To Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius Augustus, grandson of the deified Augustus, pontiff, with tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time, augural associate, in accordance with a decree of the Senate.
EDCS-19700829.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, likely erected in accordance with a senatorial decree.

EDCS-19700832.translation: To Lucius Aufellius Rufus, primipilaris of the Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, twice duovir quinquennalis, flaminius of the deified Augustus, patron of the municipality, by the Palatine neighborhood.
EDCS-19700832.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Aufellius Rufus, a distinguished veteran and local official, by the Palatine neighborhood.

EDCS-19700833.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Corinthus, freedman, made this for Gaius Fanius Clemens, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, a veteran of the Praetorian cohort VII.
EDCS-19700833.summary: This is a tombstone erected by a freedman for Gaius Fanius Clemens, a veteran of the Praetorian Guard.

EDCS-19700834.translation: In the Calibus, in the Torquata Curia, Vitrasia was involved in the writing. Tiberius Claudius Felix and Tiberius Claudius Calenus and Quintus Sergius Priscus: since an epistolary decree of Lucius Vitrasius Silvestris was read, Lucius Marcius Vitalio, duovir, spoke to the council regarding what it would be pleasing to do about the matter. They resolved as follows: that the council had long ago understood Lucius Vitrasius Silvestris's zeal and willingness towards our common fatherland, as he had first, when undertaking the duty of providing gladiatorial games at his own expense, fulfilled the task imposed upon him so lavishly that we thought him worthy of being honored with decurional ornaments, and the townspeople with a statue; and now, since he willingly and gladly offers his resources beyond measure, he will provide every year on the Ides of May, his birthday, to us and our children three victoriati coins, to his scribes and their children two victoriati coins, to the Augustales two victoriati coins, and to the townspeople one victoriatus coin. It is resolved by all the inscribed members that public thanks be given to Lucius Vitrasius Silvestris for his love towards us, since he has almost shared his reward with our republic; and that he be permitted to enlarge the inscription on his base as he desires, so that his generosity may be more manifest to all our townspeople. The exemplar of the duovirs' letter is to be set up in a very public place under their edict, so that it may be read clearly from the ground. All have resolved.
EDCS-19700834.summary: This inscription records a decree by the town council honoring Lucius Vitrasius Silvestris for his generous funding of gladiatorial games and other public benefactions, granting him honors and permission to alter his monument's inscription.

EDCS-19700838.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Marcus Ennius Cerealis, sevirus Augustalis. Calibus, who lived 31 years and 5 months. Staia Iusta, his wife, with whom he lived 11 years without any complaint, made this for him as he deserved well.
EDCS-19700838.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Ennius Cerealis, a local religious official, erected by his wife.

EDCS-19700840.translation: Father erected this for Gaius Flavius Pollio Fimbria, grandson of Gaius, great-grandson of Gaius, who was co-opted into the Senate by a decree of the Senate and by vote.
EDCS-19700840.summary: This inscription records the posthumous honoring of Gaius Flavius Pollio Fimbria, who was admitted to the Senate by senatorial decree.

EDCS-19700841.translation: Gaius Furius Numida, son of Gaius, and Marcus Veratius Pollio, son of Gaius, duovirs with judicial power, restored the paved road to the gate, the uppermost part, and on the slopes to the Porta Gemina, and within the Porta Martialis to the Angiporrtus of Matuta, in accordance with a decree of the Senate, and they approved it.
EDCS-19700841.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and infrastructure near city gates by two duovirs, approved by the Senate.

EDCS-19800052.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [To...] Felicis, son of [Caius?] Oclatus.
EDCS-19800052.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone inscription dedicated to a person named Felicis.

EDCS-19800053.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octavius Ianuarius for Octavia Pieris and her son Thallus, who deserved well.
EDCS-19800053.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Octavius Ianuarius for Octavia Pieris and her son Thallus.

EDCS-19800055.translation: The remains of Publius Octavius Philomusus, freedman of Aulus, are placed here. Sacred to the underworld gods and parents, do not violate. In the land fifteen feet, on the road fifteen feet. Philargurus, freedman and partner, erected this.
EDCS-19800055.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Octavius Philomusus, detailing the burial plot and prohibitions, erected by his freedman and partner.

EDCS-19800056.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Octavia Prisca, his deserving wife, Marcus Volumnius Sophron [dedicated this].
EDCS-19800056.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Volumnius Sophron to his wife Octavia Prisca, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-19800059.translation: Ofillia, freedwoman of Epistolio (of the women's establishment), her bones lie here.
EDCS-19800059.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that the remains of Ofillia, a freedwoman, are interred here, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-19800062.translation: The bones of Caius Oranius Eronis, a freedman (of a woman), lie here.
EDCS-19800062.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that the remains of Caius Oranius Eronis, a freedman, are interred here, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-19800068.translation: Quintus Paccius Apollonius, freedman of Quintus, Paccia Apollonia, his fellow freedwoman, made this at her own expense.
EDCS-19800068.summary: A funerary epitaph indicating that Paccia Apollonia dedicated a monument for her fellow freedman and possibly spouse, Quintus Paccius Apollonius, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-19800070.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Pactumeius Falernus, who lived 80 years, and Pactumeia Successa. Lucius Pactumeius Falernus [dedicated this] to his deserving [relatives].
EDCS-19800070.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Pactumeius Falernus for himself and deceased relatives, including Lucius Pactumeius Falernus and Pactumeia Successa, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-19800073.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Papinius Verus and Campania Felicissima made this for themselves while alive, having lived together for 68 years and 10 days.
EDCS-19800073.summary: A funerary epitaph for a couple, Lucius Papinius Verus and Campania Felicissima, who commissioned their monument while alive after 68 years of marriage, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-19800075.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Pardo, dearest and most dutiful son, who lived 8 years and 14 days. His parents made this for their deserving son.
EDCS-19800075.summary: A funerary epitaph by parents for their son Pardo, who died at the age of 8 years and 14 days, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-19800076.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Parthenepaeus. Augusti Zosimus, freedman of the Emperor, [dedicated this to] his most dutiful son.
EDCS-19800076.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman of the Emperor, Zosimus, for his son Parthenepaeus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-19800078.translation: For Marcus Pandius Masculus, freedman of the Marci, and his wife Nice, and their daughter Faustae, their bones lie here.
EDCS-19800078.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Pandius Masculus, his wife Nice, and daughter Faustae, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-19800079.translation: For Lucius Percennius Rufio. Secundio, his freedman, [made this] at his own expense.
EDCS-19800079.summary: A funerary dedication by Secundio, a freedman, for Lucius Percennius Rufio, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-19800080.translation: To the departed spirits. To Publius Pescennius Exspectatus, the wife [dedicated this] to her most excellent husband.
EDCS-19800080.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Publius Pescennius Exspectatus by his wife.

EDCS-19800082.translation: The bones of Pesceniaes Laudica, a freedwoman, are placed here.
EDCS-19800082.summary: This inscription records the burial of Pesceniaes Laudica, a freedwoman.

EDCS-19800084.translation: The bones of Petronia Faust[---], a freedwoman, are placed here.
EDCS-19800084.summary: This inscription records the burial of Petronia Faust[---], a freedwoman.

EDCS-19800088.translation: To the memory of Pinarius Optatianus. His wife Pinaria Felix made this for her deserving husband, who lived 46 years, 5 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-19800088.summary: A funerary monument set up by Pinaria Felix for her husband, Pinarius Optatianus, with his age at death.

EDCS-19800089.translation: Aulus Planius. Plania Philumina made this for herself and her daughters Plani[a ---] and Plani[a ---] Prima. Their bones are placed here.
EDCS-19800089.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Plania Philumina for herself and her daughters, indicating the location of their remains.

EDCS-19800092.translation: Plotia Nica, freedwoman of Lucius, died. Her bones are placed here. Decimus Livinius Archa, freedman of Decimus, a devoted husband, made this monument for himself while alive. He is alive.
EDCS-19800092.summary: This inscription records the death and burial of Plotia Nica and indicates that her husband, Decimus Livinius Archa, erected a monument for himself while alive.

EDCS-19800096.translation: To Marcus Pomponius Salvius, freedman, his bones are placed here.
EDCS-19800096.summary: This inscription marks the burial place of Marcus Pomponius Salvius, a freedman.

EDCS-19800097.summary: This inscription identifies Pomponia Alabandis, a freedwoman of Decimus.

EDCS-19800098.translation: To Pomponia Libera, daughter of Caius, who lived 18 years. Her bones are placed here.
EDCS-19800098.summary: This inscription records the burial of Pomponia Libera, daughter of Caius, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-19800099.translation: To the departed spirits. To Quintus Pontius Rufinus, son of Quintus. His most unhappy mother [dedicated this].
EDCS-19800099.summary: A funerary dedication to Quintus Pontius Rufinus by his grieving mother.

EDCS-19800100.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Popidia Phileta, freedwoman of Servus, and Varacia Crescentilla, freedwoman of Lucius, who lived 26 years, 7 months, and [3] days. For Popidia Felicissima, daughter of Servus, who lived 8 years, 9 months, and 16 days. Servius Felicior to his most excellent mother, his incomparable wife, and his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-19800100.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Servius Felicior for his mother, wife, and daughter, detailing their ages and relationships.

EDCS-19800103.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Nerasio Saturninus, freedman of Lucius, Augustalis of Cumae, who lived 30 years, 3 months, and 20 days. Naevia Fortunata made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-19800103.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Nerasio Saturninus, set up by his wife Naevia Fortunata, giving his age and his civic role.

EDCS-19800106.translation: Publius Plotus, son of Publius, chief magistrate with judicial power, marks ten boundary stones.
EDCS-19800106.summary: This inscription records Publius Plotus, a chief magistrate, marking ten boundary stones, likely in an official capacity.

EDCS-19800108.translation: To Lucius Rubrius Galeo, son of Lucius, chief magistrate with judicial power, quaestor, and senator's legate.
EDCS-19800108.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Rubrius Galeo, a Roman official who held several significant positions.

EDCS-19800109.translation: Lucius Vaccinius, son of Lucius, set this up by decree of the Senate.
EDCS-19800109.summary: This inscription indicates that Lucius Vaccinius erected something by senatorial order.

EDCS-19800119.translation: For Lucius Cornelius Cinna, consul for the second time, purified in the intercalary month.
EDCS-19800119.summary: This inscription refers to Lucius Cornelius Cinna, consul for the second time, and a purification ritual during an intercalary month, likely related to the calendar or a religious event.

EDCS-19800121.translation: For Gaius Abutius Rufus, son of Lucius, from the Falernian region, and Vellius Rufa, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-19800121.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication or record involving Gaius Abutius Rufus and Vellius Rufa, possibly as a couple or family members.

EDCS-19800123.summary: This is a simple funerary or dedicatory inscription for Aria Sacerdos, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-19800125.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Calenia Theophila.
EDCS-19800125.summary: This is a tombstone for Calenia Theophila, indicating a dedication to the departed spirits.

EDCS-19800126.translation: To Castricius Sextilianus, father of the Castricii.
EDCS-19800126.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Castricius Sextilianus, identified as the father of the Castricii family or group.

EDCS-19800127.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Tiberio Claudio Festo Avia Gemella coniugi libens merito fecit\n
EDCS-19800127.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Avia Gemella lovingly and deservedly erected this for her husband, Tiberius Claudius Festus.
EDCS-19800127.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife for her husband, Tiberius Claudius Festus.

EDCS-19800128.translation: [---]ino, son of Lucius Clodius Hilarus, lived 17 years. Niceros (raised this) from his own property out of piety.
EDCS-19800128.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a person named Hilarus, set up by Niceros.

EDCS-19800130.translation: Gaius Julius Cerdo, freedman of Gaius. Iulia Erotis, freedwoman of Gaius. Gaius Julius Epaphroditus, freedman of Gaius.
EDCS-19800130.summary: A tombstone recording three individuals, likely freedmen and freedwomen of Gaius Julius.

EDCS-19800131.translation: To the departed spirits. Iunia Polla erected this for her well-deserving husband, Quintus Lollius Justus.
EDCS-19800131.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife, Iunia Polla, for her husband, Quintus Lollius Justus.

EDCS-19800135.translation: Anteros, the freedman, rebuilt (this) for Marcus Morasius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-19800135.summary: An inscription indicating that a freedman, Anteros, rebuilt a monument for Marcus Morasius.

EDCS-19800136.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sextus Pompeius Priscus, who lived 50 years and 5 months. Sextus Pompeius Priscus (his son) erected this for his most beloved and well-deserving father.
EDCS-19800136.summary: A funerary dedication by a son for his father, Sextus Pompeius Priscus, also named Priscus.

EDCS-19800137.translation: Primogenes the elder and Urbanus, the heirs, set up (this) for Primogeni the younger.
EDCS-19800137.summary: An inscription by heirs, Primogenes the elder and Urbanus, for Primogeni the younger.

EDCS-19800147.translation: Marcus Urbanus Cinnamus (dedicated this) for himself and his family, and for Satria Seilidis, and for Maria Similis, except for his freedman Nymphio.
EDCS-19800147.summary: A self-dedication inscription by Marcus Urbanus Cinnamus for himself, his family, and others, excluding his freedman Nymphio.

EDCS-19800149.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Vultricius Verinus qui vixit annis VIIII mensibus XI diebus XX Marcus Vultricius Iustus filio fecit\n
EDCS-19800149.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Vultricius Verinus, who lived 9 years, 11 months, and 20 days. Marcus Vultricius Iustus erected this for his son.
EDCS-19800149.summary: A funerary dedication by a father, Marcus Vultricius Iustus, for his son, Marcus Vultricius Verinus.

EDCS-19800241.translation: For Felicis, my most beloved, most faithful, godlike son, who lived for two full years, six months. Felix and Nice, his parents, set this up on the Kalends of September, after the consulship of Amantius and Albinus.
EDCS-19800241.summary: A funerary dedication by parents, Felix and Nice, for their young son Felicis, dated by consulship to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-19800244.text_edition: \n filio dulcissimo Marcello qui vixit anno uno diesquae sic XLVI sic Memmius Florentinus et Axia parentes inno eius\n
EDCS-19800244.translation: To Marcello, my sweetest son, who lived for one year and 46 days, his parents Memmius Florentinus and Axia, in sorrow for him.
EDCS-19800244.summary: A funerary dedication by parents to their young son Marcello.

EDCS-19800255.translation: Munatia Reddita, priestess of the XVviri, performed the taurobolium on the twelfth day before the Kalends of December, during the consulship of Glabrio.
EDCS-19800255.summary: A record of a taurobolium performed by a priestess, dated by the consulship.

EDCS-19800256.translation: Lucius Papius Pollio, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, duovir, in memory of his father Lucius Papius Falerna, son of Lucius, established a gladiatorial munus and a banquet for the colonists of Sena and Paebia, and for all the colonists of Sena, a monument at a cost of 12,000 sesterces, according to his will and at the arbitration of Lucius Novercinus Pollio, son of Lucius, of the Pupinia tribe.
EDCS-19800256.summary: A funerary monument established by Lucius Papius Pollio, a local magistrate, in memory of his father, including gladiatorial games and a banquet.

EDCS-19800257.translation: To the Divine Shades. Cocceia Celerina made this for her son Marcus Cocceius Nepos, an example of the rarest filial piety towards her, who enjoyed the hospitality of life for 38 years and 4 months; it was not his lack of virtue that took him from the light, but dark days and a heaped funeral mound that submerged him.
EDCS-19800257.summary: A funerary inscription by a mother for her deceased son, praising his piety and lamenting his death.

EDCS-19800258.translation: Lucius Coceius Tertius and Lucius Coceius Carpus (dedicated this) to Coceius. 15 feet in front, 15 in the field.
EDCS-19800258.summary: A boundary marker or property inscription, possibly related to a tomb or land ownership.

EDCS-19800261.translation: To Valeria Veneria, mother, and Aurelia Cupida, dearest daughter. MONCCPP.
EDCS-19800261.summary: A funerary epitaph for a mother and daughter.

EDCS-19800265.translation: Marcus Cacius Cerna, son of Caius, twice duovir, military tribune, prefect of the engineers, instituted on his birthday the custom of publicly giving a dinner to the people of Sinues(sa). September 10th.
EDCS-19800265.summary: An inscription recording the establishment of a public banquet by a local magistrate on his birthday.

EDCS-19800267.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Victor made this for his well-deserving wife Felicissima.
EDCS-19800267.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband to his wife.

EDCS-19800269.translation: To the Divine Shades. To the memory of Popillius.
EDCS-19800269.summary: A very brief funerary dedication to someone named Popillius.

EDCS-19800270.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Ticho, who lived 12 years, [3] months, and 26 days, his dearest son. His father Meriochus and mother Primilla made this.
EDCS-19800270.summary: A funerary inscription by parents for their young son Ticho.

EDCS-19800650.translation: To Quintus Cominius Abascantus, honored with the insignia of a decurion and perpetual curator of the Augustales, for his service, he erected two statues in the forum of the Genius of the municipality and of the Classis Tutelae, and at their dedication he gave 20 sestertii to each decurion, 12 sestertii to each corporate Augustalis, 8 sestertii to each non-corporate Augustalis, 6 sestertii to each freeborn corporate citizen, and 4 sestertii to each municipal citizen. Furthermore, he gave 110,000 sestertii to the decurions for a celebratory feast on his birthday, the 17th of December, and he also gave 20,000 sestertii to the corporate Augustales. He further granted 10,000 sestertii for the purchase of wine for the same Augustales, so that from the income of these sums, on the aforementioned day each year, a distribution would be made according to his will. He also gave 10,000 sestertii to his most excellent wife Nymphidia Monime, at whose dedication he gave 8 sestertii per person to the corporate Augustales and a banquet. Quintus Cominius Abascantus had established in his will that he had made Nymphidia Monime his heir and had bequeathed to the corporate Augustales of Misenum 10,000 sestertii, on the condition that they would guarantee to his heir that they would not spend this sum on any other purpose, but would manage its income annually as he had stipulated in his will. He desired that Nymphidia Monime should also receive assurances, and that the curators appointed by them, Plaetorius Fortunatus and Aemilius Epagathius, should be willing to provide a guarantee for these matters. It was agreed that the relevant section of the will should be attached to these tablets so that the guarantors would know the basis of their obligations, and then a useful stipulation would be made: 'If the corporate Augustales guarantee to my heir that this my will will be upheld, and that the sum written below will not be spent or transferred to other uses, and that from the income of the money, as written below, it will be spent annually for the cleaning and anointing of the statues of the Genius of the municipality and of the Classis Tutelae, and also for adorning them with 4 sestertii of oil, 16 sestertii of violets, and 16 sestertii of roses, and for my *cenotaphium* each year on the Parentalia, to the ten victors among the wrestlers, 8 sestertii each, to the defeated, 4 sestertii each, for oil 16 sestertii, to the attendants 60 sestertii, for the sand conductor 8 sestertii, for adorning the tomb with violets 16 sestertii, with roses 16 sestertii, and for pouring on my remains 24 pounds of spikenard, and I wish that the magistrates who will be in office on that day in the dining room above the tomb, and the curators of the Augustales who will be in office, spend 100 sestertii for the sacrifice to me on that day, 60 sestertii, and from the remainder 140 sestertii to be spent on the repair of the fortifications of the same *cenotaphium* whenever needed.' Thus I wish the corporate Augustales to receive 10,000 sestertii. By you, Plaetorius Fortunatus, and by you, Aemilius Epagathius, and by the corporate Augustales of Misenum, both present and future, it shall not be done, nor by the corporate Augustales of Misenum, present and future, that this sum of 10,000 sestertii be spent or transferred to any other use than that comprehended in the chapter written above, nor that all these things be done and provided annually on those days and at those times. But if this sum of 10,000 sestertii is transferred or spent for any other use than that comprehended above, and if the things which have been transferred and transcribed from the will of Cominius to these tablets, as stipulated therein, have not been given, done, and provided, then for each of these things, the amount equal to its value, and an equal amount again, shall be paid. And if fraud or deceit is not absent from any of you concerning the matter at hand, then an amount equal to the value of that thing shall be paid. Nymphidia Monime stipulated. Lucius Plaetorius Fortunatus and Quintus Aemilius Epagathius, servants of the corporate Augustales of Misenum, have promised. I, Aemilius Epagathius, have written that I have received from Nymphidia Monime, heir of Cominius Abascantus, 10,000 sestertii bequeathed to my Augustalis masters for the aforementioned purpose, and I have promised in the aforementioned stipulation. I, Plaetorius Fortunatus, have also promised in the aforementioned stipulation. Done at Misenum on the 17th of December, when Marcus Calpurnius Longus and Decimus Velius Fidus were consuls. On the 3rd of the Nones of January, when Servius Scipio Orfitus and Quintus Sossius Priscus were consuls, at Misenum in the Temple of Augustus, which belongs to the corporate Augustales of Misenum, with Atinius Trophimus and Valerius Epaphroditus, curators of their year, reporting on the benefits to be granted to Nymphidia Monime, it was decreed by the corporate Augustales with the consent of all, as written below: 'Since Nymphidia Monime, wife of our late colleague Cominius Abascantus, both in adorning the generous municipality and in showing due gratitude to the most sacred order of decurions and to us and our fellow citizens, was generous with her family's resources, and consistently favored us, with the support not only of herself but also of his memory appealing to us on her behalf, she deserves an honorable title from us, and it is fitting that we worthily reward her good will. It is decreed by the Augustales that Nymphidia Monime be admitted into our body, and that she be given the usual distributions of festival days and the shares that we receive individually.'
EDCS-19800650.summary: This is a legal document detailing the bequests and conditions set forth in the will of Quintus Cominius Abascantus, a benefactor of the town of Misenum, who left substantial sums for public works, religious observances, and gifts to various social classes, with specific instructions for his heir, Nymphidia Monime, and the Augustales guild.

EDCS-19900034.translation: Marcia. Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the divine Augustus, by decree of the Senate, 816, 240 feet.
EDCS-19900034.summary: This inscription records a measurement of 240 feet related to a senatorial decree under Emperor Augustus, likely marking a boundary or construction project.

EDCS-20100050.summary: This is a simple epitaph for Flaciana, indicating she died in peace.

EDCS-20100057.translation: Cre[---] lived 7 years. Sac[---]us made this for his beloved, who deserved well.
EDCS-20100057.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a child named Cre[---] who lived 7 years, dedicated by Sac[---]us.

EDCS-20100064.translation: To the goddess Fortune Genesis, Vibius Felicissimus had it made according to his vow, with the consent of the state.
EDCS-20100064.summary: This dedication records Vibius Felicissimus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Fortune Genesis, with public approval.

EDCS-20100096.translation: To Lucius Pinarius Natta, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, twice duumvir, lived 22 years. For his industry and modesty, the decurions decreed for him a public funeral and a burial place. His mother Bivellia Firma [did this] for her most beloved and pious son, and for herself.
EDCS-20100096.summary: This epitaph honors Lucius Pinarius Natta, who died at 22 and had served as aedile and duumvir, with a public funeral and burial plot decreed by the town council, and was commemorated by his mother.

EDCS-20100100.translation: To the departed spirits. To Aurelia Aristaenete, most kind and rarest of women, who deserved well.
EDCS-20100100.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Aurelia Aristaenete, described as a very kind and rare woman.

EDCS-20100101.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Amullius Euhodus, banker, lived 80 years. Aulus Plautius Restitutus [erected this] for his excellent parent.
EDCS-20100101.summary: This epitaph records the death of Marcus Amullius Euhodus, a banker who lived to 80, and was commemorated by Aulus Plautius Restitutus.

EDCS-20100102.translation: To Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus Germanicus, son of the divine Vespasian, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 14th time, imperator for the 22nd time, consul for the 17th time, censor for life, father of the fatherland. The Augustales, at their own expense.
EDCS-20100102.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Domitian, listing his numerous titles and honors, erected by the Augustales guild at their own expense.

EDCS-20301038.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely part of a larger monument or tomb, simply naming Lucius Vitrasius Antigonus.

EDCS-20301050.summary: This is a dedication to Staia Ampliata, likely a woman of some status, possibly from an epitaph or a dedication to her.

EDCS-20400541.translation: To Quintus Flavius Maesius Egnatius Lollianus, a man of the highest rank, quaestor-candidate, urban praetor, augur of the Roman People and Quirites, count of our Lords the Augusti and Caesars, curator of the Tiber and the sacred sewers of the city, consular curator of public works, consular curator of the water supply and of the Minucian aqueduct, and of Campania. The order and people of Suessa.
EDCS-20400541.summary: This inscription honors Quintus Flavius Maesius Egnatius Lollianus with numerous high offices, dedicated by the local government and people of Suessa, likely dating to the late imperial period.

EDCS-20400549.translation: To Gaius Titius Chresimus, twice Augustalis. The order of decurions granted him the honorary seat of a decurion, and decreed that a stream of water should flow into his house and that he should enjoy public benefits as if he were a decurion, because he, at his own expense, provided a munificent public gladiatorial show for the safety and goodwill of the Emperor Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and by the will of the people, on a private day, in accordance with the dignity of the colony. They also decreed the honor of the decurionate, without payment, for his son Titius Chresimus, because of his father's merits. The order of decurions, the Augustales, and the entire populace. In the year of the consulship of Quintus Sossius Falco and Gaius Julius Erucius Clarus, on the Nones of September, at Suessa, in the library of Matidia, the following acted as scribes: Titus Julius Bassus, Marcus Maesius [---], Marcus Arrius Adiutor, Lucius Mildius [---], Lucius Asinius Marsirianus. Because all who asked [---] and [---]. The decurionate was granted to him [---] and for his [---] a statue was decreed because of the munificence of the private day he provided. Place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20400549.summary: This lengthy inscription from Suessa records the posthumous honors granted to Gaius Titius Chresimus by the local council and populace for his public benefactions, including the conferral of honorary decurionate status on his son, and it also records the date and scribes present at the decree, likely dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-20400550.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-20400550.translation: The order of decurions provided a public funeral for Epidia Procula, daughter of Lucius, and decreed a shield and a statue for her.
EDCS-20400550.summary: This inscription records that the order of decurions provided public funeral rites and honors, including a shield and statue, for Epidia Procula, daughter of Lucius, in the colony of Suessa.

EDCS-20400552.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulia Firma, who kept faith with her husband and was full of loyalty, lived nobly with piety, was snatched from our eyes by an unfavorable fate. Mocimus, freedman of Sabina, sister of Augustus, erected this for his wife, well-deserving.
EDCS-20400552.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Mocimus, a freedman of Sabina (sister of Emperor Augustus), to his wife Iulia Firma, emphasizing her chastity, loyalty, and piety. It dates to the early imperial period.

EDCS-20400554.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Afinius Quartus erected this monument for his most pious and most holy wife, Afinia Trophime, and for himself and his descendants.
EDCS-20400554.summary: A funerary monument erected by Lucius Afinius Quartus for his wife Afinia Trophime, and also intended for himself and his descendants, indicating the construction of a tomb complex. It likely dates to the imperial period.

EDCS-20400556.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Firidia Naice erected this for her well-deserving husband, Lucius Domitius Justus.
EDCS-20400556.summary: A funerary inscription by Firidia Naice for her husband Lucius Domitius Justus, indicating a burial monument and a loving dedication. It dates to the imperial period.

EDCS-20400557.translation: To [---], freedwoman of Lucius Epidius N[---], patron. Lucius Salluvius On[---]
EDCS-20400557.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription that records a dedication from Lucius Salluvius On[---] to his patron, Lucius Epidius [---], who was a freedman of Lucius, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-20400560.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Iulia Capitolina, daughter of Marcus, who lived 15 years, 6 months, and 3 days. Her most pious mother, Modia Longina, erected this for her well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-20400560.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Capitolina, who died at the age of 15 years, 6 months, and 3 days, erected by her devoted mother, Modia Longina. It dates to the imperial period.

EDCS-20400561.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Pompeia Secunda, daughter of Marcus, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-20400562.translation: Lucius Pontius Philodamus, freedman of Lucius; Gaius Popilius Philargyrus, freedman of Gaius; Pontia Ammia, freedwoman of Lucius; Popilia Hedone, freedwoman of Gaius and (his wife).
EDCS-20400562.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing four individuals, likely members of a single family or household, identified by their Roman names and manumission status.

EDCS-20400563.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, that names an individual identified as Pius.

EDCS-20400564.translation: To the Divine Shades, for Gaius Valerius Rufus, for his wife Valeria Chia, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and all their descendants.
EDCS-20400564.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a man, his wife, and their extended household, indicating a family tomb or plot.

EDCS-20400569.translation: [1] Stichus, son of Messius, surveyor(?), (dedicated this) to Juno Populona, as a sacred offering for my father's grandfather.
EDCS-20400569.summary: This is a religious dedication of a sacred object to Juno Populona by Stichus, son of Messius, likely an official surveyor, for his paternal grandfather.

EDCS-20400571.translation: To Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 13th time, consul for the 3rd time, Father of the Country, the Best and Greatest Prince, by decree of the town council of Teanum.
EDCS-20400571.summary: This is a public dedication to Emperor Hadrian by the town council of Teanum, listing his imperial titles and honors, dating to his 13th tribunician power and 3rd consulship.

EDCS-20400573.translation: [To Emperor] Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Pius Felix Invictus Germanicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, Parthicus Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 14th time, consul for the 6th time, designated for the 7th time, the people of Teanum, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-20400573.summary: This is a public dedication to Emperor Gallienus by the people of Teanum, listing his imperial titles and honors, dating to his 14th tribunician power and 6th consulship.

EDCS-20400576.translation: For Publius Tapsenna Proculus, son of Publius, who served in the 10th Praetorian Cohort, for himself and for his wife Pettia Urbana, with whom he lived for 32 years, whose grief he accepted, no other than that of her death, and for his father Publius Tapsenna, son of Lucius, and for his brother Quintus Tapsenna, son of Publius.
EDCS-20400576.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Tapsenna Proculus, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, commemorating his wife, father, and brother, and detailing the duration of his marriage.

EDCS-20400578.translation: To Flavia Coelia Annia Argiva, priestess of Juno Populona, daughter of Lucius Flavius Coelius Priscus, a IIvir and pontifex, a foster child of Teanum, and notary of her father, the place was granted by decree of the town council.
EDCS-20400578.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Flavia Coelia Annia Argiva, a priestess of Juno Populona and daughter of a local magistrate, with the plot granted by the town council.

EDCS-20400579.translation: To Nonia Prisca, priestess of Juno Populona, daughter of Lucius Nonius Diophantus, a IIvir quinqennalis, pontifex, and Roman knight. Her brothers Lucius Nonius Passenius Cleobulus and Philargyrus Felix, and their mother Aurelia Trypherina, granted the place by decree of the town council.
EDCS-20400579.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Nonia Prisca, a priestess of Juno Populona and daughter of a prominent local official, with the plot granted by her brothers and mother by decree of the town council.

EDCS-20400580.translation: To Vitellia Virgilia Felsia, minister of public rites and priestess of Juno Populona, granted the place by decree of the town council. Her mother Virgilia F[---]A [granted the place] by decree of the town council.
EDCS-20400580.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Vitellia Virgilia Felsia, a minister of public rites and priestess of Juno Populona, with the burial plot granted by her mother and the town council.

EDCS-20400581.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Caius Alscidius Lepotius.

EDCS-20400586.translation: Gnaeus Vesiculanus, son of Marcus, to his father Marcus Vesiculanus, son of Vibius, who was a tribune of the plebs, a duovir for dispensing justice, and a prefect for divine matters.
EDCS-20400586.summary: This is an epitaph commemorating Marcus Vesiculanus and listing his public offices, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-20400590.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Atulena Restuta, an incomparable wife, who lived with me for 24 years. Felix [erected this] for [her], who deserved well.
EDCS-20400590.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a husband, Felix, to his wife Atulena Restuta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400591.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Titus Bebenius Licinus Pastorinus, a most sweet infant, who lived 17 years, 10 months, and 14 days. Bebenius Pastorinus and Licinia Felicissima made [this] for their most beloved, incomparable son, who deserved well.
EDCS-20400591.summary: This is a funerary dedication from parents, Bebenius Pastorinus and Licinia Felicissima, to their young son Titus Bebenius Licinus Pastorinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400594.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Sextus Clodius Adiutor. Lucius Fufidius Theramenes [erected this] for his friend.
EDCS-20400594.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Lucius Fufidius Theramenes to his friend Sextus Clodius Adiutor.

EDCS-20400595.translation: To Gaius Clodius, son of Gaius, Cia[---]aud. Cesonilla M[---]o[---] Armanori[---]o[---]. Licinius [---] Pon[---].
EDCS-20400595.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or epitaph with incomplete names and patronymics, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400596.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Lucius Julius Iucundus, freedman of Lucius. Iucundus [erected this] for his deserving father.
EDCS-20400596.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a son, Iucundus, to his father, Lucius Julius Iucundus, a freedman.

EDCS-20400597.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Julia Primilla, freedwoman of Lucius. Iucundus [erected this] for his most holy wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-20400597.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Iucundus to his wife, Julia Primilla, a freedwoman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400600.translation: [---] erected for himself. To Quintus Pontius Paullinus, son of Gaius and Sosis. Pontia Rhodope, his most unhappy mother, made [this] for her best son and for herself. [This] is from the freedman of Quintus Pontius Rufus, who lived 12 years, 1 month, and 7 days. He bought the plot for 200 sesterces. This monument will not pass [to heirs].
EDCS-20400600.summary: This is a funerary monument purchased by a mother, Pontia Rhodope, for her son Quintus Pontius Paullinus and herself, and it records the cost and terms of inheritance, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400607.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Valeria Epictesidi, who lived 25 years and 29 days. Valeria Secundilla, her mother, and Julius Eutyches, her husband, [erected this].
EDCS-20400607.summary: This is a funerary dedication from the mother and husband of Valeria Epictesidi, who died at the age of 25, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400609.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Gaius Vibius Norbanus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, Pomponia Amethusta [dedicates this] to her excellent husband.
EDCS-20400609.summary: A funerary dedication for Gaius Vibius Norbanus, erected by his wife Pomponia Amethusta.

EDCS-20400618.translation: To the Great and Best Mother of the Gods, to whom a bull sacrifice was performed. Sabidia Cornelia, daughter of Titus, willingly and deservedly made this.
EDCS-20400618.summary: A dedication to the Mother of the Gods, likely related to a bull sacrifice, made by Sabidia Cornelia.

EDCS-20400619.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine, consul for the 5th time, imperator for the 6th time, from the villagers of Rufrani whose buildings [are here].
EDCS-20400619.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Caesar, likely Augustus, dedicated by the villagers of Rufrani.

EDCS-20400622.translation: To Marcus Volcius Sabinus, son of Marcus, military tribune, because he brought the Julian aqueduct with his own money. From the villagers of Rupheni.
EDCS-20400622.summary: A dedication to Marcus Volcius Sabinus, a military tribune, by the villagers of Rupheni for funding the Julian aqueduct.

EDCS-20400623.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Sattius Ianuarius, soldier of the 2nd cohort of vigiles, lived 22 years, 6 months. Fullius Ianuarius and Sattia Tymelet [dedicate this] to their sweetest son.
EDCS-20400623.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Sattius Ianuarius, a soldier of the vigiles, who died at age 22, erected by his parents.

EDCS-20400627.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Memmius.
EDCS-20400627.summary: A funerary dedication to the spirits of the underworld for Lucius Memmius.

EDCS-20400629.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Quintus Turranius Aurelius Victorinus, who lived 8 years, 6 months, 6 days. From Aurelius Venustianus, his son.
EDCS-20400629.summary: A funerary dedication for Quintus Turranius Aurelius Victorinus, a child who died at age 8, erected by his father Aurelius Venustianus.

EDCS-20400635.translation: The slave of Sextus Aulienus Nedymus, as magister, gave this as a gift to Camillus Arruntius, when he was consul.
EDCS-20400635.summary: A dedication made by a magister, the slave of Sextus Aulienus Nedymus, to Camillus Arruntius during his consulship.

EDCS-20400648.text_edition: \n Lucio Gabinio Luci filio Teretina Cosmiano tribuno legionis VI a militiis flamini pontifici patrono coloniae Claudi Hermogeniani clarissimi viri proconsulis Africae consocero patri Luci Gabini Luci fili Teretina Aspri pontificis auguris procuratoris Auggustorum nnostrorum et Graphni patronorum coloniarum trium avo Gabini Vindicis Pompeiani patrono et curatori rerum publiacarum Aletrinatium et Interamnatium Lirenatium advokato splendidissimarum coloniarum Venafranorum et Casinatium quod operibus publicis non solum servandis verum et augendis omnem sollicitudinem intenderit formamque aquae ductus perenni incuria conllapsam vir singularis innocentiae afflictis rei publicae viribus restituerit Interamnates Lirenates patrono et curatori rei publicae pecunia sua\n
EDCS-20400648.translation: To Lucius Gabinius Cosmianus, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, tribune of the 6th legion, from military service, flamen, pontifex, patron of the colony of Claudius Hermogenianus, a most distinguished man, proconsul of Africa, father-in-law of Lucius Gabinius Aspra, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, pontifex, augur, procurator of our divine Augusti, and of Graphnus, patrons of the three colonies, advocate for the most splendid colonies of Venafrum and Casinum, and of Interamna Lirenatis, because he dedicated all his concern not only to preserving but also to increasing public works and restored the form of the aqueduct, which had collapsed due to perpetual neglect, with his singular innocence, despite the afflicted resources of the state. The people of Interamna Lirenatis [dedicate this] to their patron and curator of public affairs, with their own money.
EDCS-20400648.summary: A complex honorary inscription honoring Lucius Gabinius Cosmianus for his public works, especially the restoration of an aqueduct, erected by the people of Interamna Lirenatis and mentioning his family and official roles.

EDCS-20400650.translation: Lusia Paullina, daughter of Marcus, wife of Sextus Vetulenius Cerialis, [dedicates this] for herself and for her father, Marcus Vergilius Gallo Lusius, first-rank centurion of the 11th legion, prefect of the cohort of Ubii (infantry and cavalry), decorated with two pure spears and golden crowns by the deified Augustus and Tiberius Caesar Augustus, prefect of the fabrica, military tribune of the 1st cohort, idologus in Egypt, twice duovir, pontifex; and for her brother, Aulus Lusius Gallus, son of Aulus, of the Teretina tribe, military tribune of the 22nd Cyrenaic legion, prefect of cavalry.
EDCS-20400650.summary: A dedication made by Lusia Paullina for herself, her deceased father Marcus Vergilius Gallo Lusius, and her brother Aulus Lusius Gallus, listing their distinguished military and civic careers.

EDCS-20400655.translation: To Rufus, son of Titus, of the Teretina tribe, [tribune] of the soldiers, prefect of the fleet, [aedile? aedile or duovir?] quaestor.
EDCS-20400655.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning various magistracies held by a certain Rufus, likely a public monument from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20400660.translation: Lucius Ovinius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, first officer of the Praetorian cohorts, first spear of the XIIII Gemina legion, tribune of the soldiers of the XI Urban cohort, tribune of the soldiers of the [III?] Praetorian cohort, master of craftsmen, duovir, [erected this monument] to his father Lucius Ovinius Vopiscus, son of Marcus, of the Teretina tribe; to his brother Marcus Ovinius, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe; to his mother Allidia Rufa, daughter of Lucius; and to his wife Pullia Prima.
EDCS-20400660.summary: This epitaph honors Lucius Ovinius Rufus and his family, detailing his distinguished military and civil career, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20400661.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-20400661.translation: To Sextus Pulfennius Salutaris, son of Caius, of the Teretina tribe; Marcus Luccius Valerius Severus, prefect of the IV Gallorum cavalry cohort, tribune of the soldiers of the I Vindelicorum milliary cohort, prefect of the I Pannoniorum ala, curator of the calendar of the colony of Suessa, curator of the temple and treasury of the Vitrasianae of the Caleni, flamen of the deified Trajan, duovir, quaestor, IIIvir, patron of the colony.
EDCS-20400661.summary: This inscription honors Sextus Pulfennius Salutaris and Marcus Luccius Valerius Severus, listing their extensive military, religious, and civic offices, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20400667.translation: To Quintus Cassius, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, quaestor.
EDCS-20400667.summary: This is a short inscription honoring Quintus Cassius, who held the quaestorian office, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20400669.translation: To Caius Cocceius, son of Caius, [of the Teretina tribe?], aedile, quaestor.
EDCS-20400669.summary: This fragmentary inscription records Caius Cocceius, who served as aedile and quaestor, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20400674.translation: Publius Lucanius Quadratus, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, duovir, augur, quaestor twice, dedicated the bathhouse from his own funds. [This monument was erected by] Cassia, daughter of Publius, his wife, and Lucania Procula, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-20400674.summary: This dedication records Publius Lucanius Quadratus's generous donation of a public bathhouse, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20400677.translation: Octavia Clara, daughter of the patron, ordered by her will that this be made to Lucius Octavius, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, her father, and to Lucius Octavius, son of Lucius, son of [Lucius], of the Teretina tribe, her brother, aedile, quaestor for the second time.
EDCS-20400677.summary: This inscription, erected by will, honors Octavia Clara's deceased father and brother, detailing the brother's offices, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20400679.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Tillius Primus made this for Tillia Eutychia, priestess.
EDCS-20400679.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Tillius Primus to his wife Tillia Eutychia, who was a priestess, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20400683.translation: After undertaking a vow for the perpetual safety of the Augustan house, and having presented a gladiatorial munus (show), the people conferred a statue upon Quintus Vibius Rusticus, son of Quintus, son of Caesius, of the Teretina tribe, duovir, quaestor.
EDCS-20400683.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to Quintus Vibius Rusticus by the populace in recognition of his munificence in providing gladiatorial games, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20400694.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Aurelia Victorina, his incomparable wife, who lived 19 years and 2 months. Marcus, administrator of public affairs of the Venafrani, [erected this] for his well-deserving [wife].
EDCS-20400694.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Marcus, an official in Venafrum, to his young wife Aurelia Victorina, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20400695.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Marcus Valerius Victor, scribe of the Venafrani. Pulfennius Severus, his friend, who deserved well, had this made. 19 feet in front.
EDCS-20400695.summary: A funerary dedication by a friend to Marcus Valerius Victor, a scribe, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400696.translation: Lucius Fillius Anteros, a sexvir. Gavia Secunda, his wife.
EDCS-20400696.summary: A funerary dedication to Lucius Fillius Anteros, a sexvir, and his wife, Gavia Secunda.

EDCS-20400697.translation: Gaius Manilius Fortis, Augustalis, patron of the college, and Luculana Species.
EDCS-20400697.summary: An inscription recording the status of Gaius Manilius Fortis as patron of a college and mentioning Luculana Species, likely related to him.

EDCS-20400699.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Aulus Terentius Felix, Augustalis of Venafrum. Aulus Terentius Felix, his father, had this made for his most devoted and deserving son.
EDCS-20400699.summary: A funerary dedication by a father to his deceased son, Aulus Terentius Felix, an Augustalis of Venafrum, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400700.translation: To Quintus Vibius Anteros, freedman of Quintus, an Augustalis. Also to Vibiae Primigenia, Eucumene, and Erotini, freedwomen of Quintus.
EDCS-20400700.summary: A dedication likely honoring Quintus Vibius Anteros, an Augustalis, and three freedwomen, Vibiae Primigenia, Eucumene, and Erotini.

EDCS-20400701.translation: To Quintus Vibius Pius, a sexvir and Augustalis.
EDCS-20400701.summary: A dedication to Quintus Vibius Pius, identified as both a sexvir and an Augustalis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400702.translation: Volcia Sperata, daughter of Marcus, made this for Marcus Volcius Speratus, an Augustalis, and for Volcia Quina.
EDCS-20400702.summary: A dedication by Volcia Sperata to her father, Marcus Volcius Speratus, an Augustalis, and her mother, Volcia Quina.

EDCS-20400705.translation: To the spirits of the departed. "Hurry a little, traveler, for I hasten to go. And when I ask, take these words into your mind: hope to come yourself one day. I, Iustus, was named not by my father but by my mother. Though my father was poor, I lived with wealth of fame. Modulating with the musician's tune, calling alternates, I stirred the gladiators to war. I lived 21 years, 11 months, 29 days. I, Iustus, perished in a bitter death." Parents for an incomparable son.
EDCS-20400705.summary: A lengthy and poetic funerary epitaph for Iustus, who died at the age of 21, likely from the Roman Imperial period, expressing his life and lamenting his death.

EDCS-20400706.translation: Sextus Aulenus Fuscus, freedman of Sextus, a craftsman, for himself and for Aulena Laina, freedwoman of Sextus, and for his own people. 12 feet in front, 17 feet in the field.
EDCS-20400706.summary: A funerary dedication by Sextus Aulenus Fuscus, a craftsman and freedman, for himself, his wife Aulena Laina, and his family.

EDCS-20400707.translation: Narcissus, the steward, for Titus Titucius Florianus and Teia Galla, daughter of Lucius. He lived 25 years. "The promised freedom, which was denied to me as a youth by law, has been granted perpetually by death."
EDCS-20400707.summary: A funerary epitaph for Narcissus, a steward, who died at 25, lamenting that his freedom was granted by death rather than by law.

EDCS-20400711.translation: To Accia Galla, daughter of Marcus, with 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-20400711.summary: This is a funerary boundary marker measuring the plot of land belonging to Accia Galla.

EDCS-20400712.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Acilia Marcellina, who lived 17 years, 8 months, and 15 days. Her siblings Mesurius and Marcellina [dedicated this].
EDCS-20400712.summary: This is a tombstone for Acilia Marcellina, recording her age at death and dedicated by her siblings.

EDCS-20400714.translation: To Lucius Allius Coracinus, freedman of a woman, [deceased]. Allia Hilara, freedwoman of a woman, and Gaius Papius Eros, freedman of Gaius, [dedicated this] while alive, with 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-20400714.summary: This is a funerary boundary marker, possibly for a tomb or plot of land, dedicated by named freedmen and freedwomen.

EDCS-20400715.translation: Gaius Antilius Secundus, freedman of Gaius, for himself, his family, and for Sextus Octavius Felicioni, freedman of a woman, with 12 feet in front.
EDCS-20400715.summary: This is likely a funerary boundary marker for a plot of land, with Gaius Antilius Secundus dedicating it for himself, his family, and another individual.

EDCS-20400716.translation: To Sextus Aulenus Eros, freedman of Sextus, for himself and his family; Aulena Glapira; Sextus Aulenus Proculus, with 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-20400716.summary: This is a funerary boundary marker for a plot of land, with Sextus Aulenus Eros dedicating it for himself and his family, and including other named individuals.

EDCS-20400718.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Babiana Fortunata, who lived 23 years. Her brother Fortunatus [dedicated this] to his excellent sister.
EDCS-20400718.summary: This is a tombstone for Babiana Fortunata, recording her age at death and dedicated by her loving brother.

EDCS-20400721.translation: To Sextus Cassius, son of Sextus, [from the tribe] Teretina, father; to Pacia Polla, daughter of Gaius, mother; to Sextus Cassius, son of Sextus, [and] ...; Marcus Cassius, son of Sextus, ...; [dedicated by the survivor] for himself and [others] while alive.
EDCS-20400721.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription records the dedication of a tomb for a father, mother, and son by a surviving family member.

EDCS-20400722.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Calliope, who lived 21 years. Her sister Venafrania Festa [dedicated this].
EDCS-20400722.summary: This is a tombstone for Calliope, recording her age at death and dedicated by her sister.

EDCS-20400726.translation: To Quintus Cornelius Andrias, freedman of Quintus, with 12 feet in front and 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-20400726.summary: This funerary boundary marker measures the plot of land belonging to Quintus Cornelius Andrias.

EDCS-20400728.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Titus Didius Alexander, deserving of favor. Titus Didius Tryphon, his freedman, [dedicated this].
EDCS-20400728.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Didius Alexander, dedicated by his freedman Tryphon.

EDCS-20400732.translation: Eronis, slave; Luperci, slave; Hilarae, slave; Luperci, slave. Frontage 12 feet; depth 12 feet.
EDCS-20400732.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a funerary plot, possibly for slaves, indicating a frontage of 12 feet and a depth of 12 feet.

EDCS-20400734.translation: For Gaius Ethereius Narcissus, freedman of Gaius; and for Kalibe, freedwoman of Rubria (wife of Gaius). For themselves and their family. Frontage 15 feet; depth 15 feet.
EDCS-20400734.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Ethereius Narcissus and Kalibe, specifying plot dimensions of 15 feet by 15 feet.

EDCS-20400735.translation: For Lucius Fabricius Flaccus, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe; for Arion, freedman of Lucius; for Fabricia Felicula, freedwoman of Lucius; for Lucius Fabricius Cassiotticus, son of Lucius. Depth 25 feet, frontage 20 feet.
EDCS-20400735.summary: A funerary inscription for multiple members of the Fabricii family, detailing plot dimensions of 20 feet frontage and 25 feet depth.

EDCS-20400736.translation: For Lucius Fabricius Peregrinus, freedman of Lucius. Frontage 17 feet; depth 17 feet.
EDCS-20400736.summary: This funerary record specifies the dimensions of a plot for Lucius Fabricius Peregrinus as 17 feet by 17 feet.

EDCS-20400737.translation: For Iurtia Facilis, for herself and for Sabinus, son of Sextus Freius Sabinus. Frontage 12 feet; depth 12 feet.
EDCS-20400737.summary: A funerary dedication for Iurtia Facilis and Sabinus, indicating a plot size of 12 feet by 12 feet.

EDCS-20400740.translation: For Lucius Fundanius Sermonis, son of Quintus, of the Teretina tribe. Depth 12 feet.
EDCS-20400740.summary: This inscription records the depth of a plot belonging to Lucius Fundanius Sermonis as 12 feet.

EDCS-20400743.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot, possibly funerary, as 20 feet frontage and 21 feet depth.

EDCS-20400744.translation: For Staia Quarta, freedwoman of Lucius, wife of Gaius Laelius, son of Publius, of the Teretina tribe. Depth 12 feet; frontage 12 feet.
EDCS-20400744.summary: A funerary inscription for Staia Quarta, wife of Gaius Laelius, detailing plot dimensions of 12 feet by 12 feet.

EDCS-20400745.translation: For Lartia Prima, freedwoman of Gaius. Depth 12 feet.
EDCS-20400745.summary: This inscription records the depth of a funerary plot for Lartia Prima as 12 feet.

EDCS-20400747.translation: C. Q. I. Iucania and her children. Frontage 27 feet.
EDCS-20400747.summary: This inscription, possibly funerary, records the frontage of a plot belonging to Iucania and her children as 27 feet.

EDCS-20400758.translation: To Publius Novius Zethus, his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants, for 22 feet in front and 22 feet in depth.
EDCS-20400758.summary: This is a property boundary inscription, likely for a funerary plot, detailing dimensions granted by Publius Novius Zethus to his freedmen and their families.

EDCS-20400759.translation: To Gaius Numisius, also called Ratiagrus, for himself, his family, and for Numisia Vinda, daughter of Aulus, who deserved well, for 12 feet in the field and 12 feet in front.
EDCS-20400759.summary: This inscription records a property boundary, possibly for a tomb or garden, dedicated by Gaius Numisius (Ratiagrus) to himself, his family, and Numisia Vinda.

EDCS-20400761.translation: To Octavia, freedwoman of Manius, named Provincia, for herself, her family, and for Aufustia Faustilla, freedwoman of Lucius, for 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-20400761.summary: This is a property boundary marker, likely for a burial space, established by Octavia, freedwoman of Manius, for herself, her family, and Aufustia Faustilla.

EDCS-20400762.translation: To Lucius Papius Hilarus, freedman of Lucius, for himself and his heir, and if he wishes, for Papia Irena, his fellow freedwoman, and for no one else, for 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-20400762.summary: This inscription defines property rights, likely for a tomb, granted by Lucius Papius Hilarus, freedman of Lucius, to himself, his heir, and potentially a fellow freedwoman.

EDCS-20400763.translation: To Papia Ursula, freedwoman of a woman, and her family, for 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-20400763.summary: This inscription marks a property boundary, likely for a burial plot, for Papia Ursula and her family.

EDCS-20400764.translation: To [---] Topia, daughter of Publius, of the Teretina tribe, for 20 feet in front and 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-20400764.summary: This inscription appears to demarcate a plot of land, possibly for funerary use, belonging to Topia, daughter of Publius, with specified dimensions.

EDCS-20400766.translation: Gaius Petillius Dexter, son of Gaius; Gaius Petillius Statio, freedman of Gaius; Petillia Quinta, freedwoman of Gaius; made (this) while alive.
EDCS-20400766.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by several individuals, including Gaius Petillius Dexter, his freedman Statio, and Petillia Quinta, who made it during their lifetime.

EDCS-20400767.translation: To the departed spirits. To Lucius Petronius Successus, dearest son, who lived 9 years, 7 months. His mother Ulpia Veneria.
EDCS-20400767.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating Lucius Petronius Successus, a young boy, erected by his grieving mother, Ulpia Veneria.

EDCS-20400768.translation: To Plotia Euticenus, freedwoman of a woman, for herself and her freedmen and freedwomen, for 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-20400768.summary: This inscription establishes property boundaries, likely for a tomb, for Plotia Euticenus and her freed dependents.

EDCS-20400770.translation: To Lucius Pomponius Politus, freedman of Lucius, and to Lollia Quartilla, his fellow soldier/tent-mate, and to Pomponius Inventus, freedman, for himself and his family, for 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-20400770.summary: This inscription delineates property boundaries, likely for a burial plot, for Lucius Pomponius Politus, his comrade Lollia Quartilla, and the freedman Pomponius Inventus.

EDCS-20400773.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Priscus, son of Farnaces and Venafra, and for Festus, his parents made this for their most devoted son who lived 16 years.
EDCS-20400773.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young man named Priscus, set up by his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400776.translation: Gaius Rubrius Quartio, freedman of Maximinus, for himself, his wife, and his kin, with 20 feet in front and 25 feet in depth.
EDCS-20400776.summary: This is a property boundary marker for a burial plot, set up by a freedman named Quartio, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400779.translation: Quintus Sattius Acceptus, freedman of Quintus, made this in his lifetime for himself and his kin, with 12 feet in front and 12 feet in depth.
EDCS-20400779.summary: This is a property boundary marker for a burial plot, set up by a freedman named Acceptus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400781.translation: To Lucius Seius Potens, freedman of a woman; Marcus Celitus Quartio; Gaius Atilius Callitiche, for themselves, their kin, and their freedmen and freedwomen, with 10 feet in depth and 10 feet in front.
EDCS-20400781.summary: This is a property boundary marker for a burial plot, set up by multiple individuals including freedmen, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400783.translation: For Servilia, daughter of Marcus; Lucius Sattius Niger, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe; Gaius Sattius Fronto, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe. Ahia, daughter of Gaius, mother, made this in her lifetime for herself and her kin; this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-20400783.summary: This is a property boundary marker for a burial plot, set up by a mother for herself and her family, with a stipulation that the property will not be inherited, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400784.translation: Servilia Hilara, freedwoman of Gaius, made this in her lifetime for herself and her kin. She lost Rufus, sweet and most excellent, a good man in life for his piety; she grieved and will grieve for him lost, and will not cease to mourn until he is buried.
EDCS-20400784.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period, mourning the loss of a man named Rufus and set up by Servilia Hilara.

EDCS-20400785.translation: Lucius Staius Appollonius, freedman of Lucius, with 12 feet in front and 10 feet in depth.
EDCS-20400785.summary: This is a property boundary marker for a burial plot, set up by a freedman named Appollonius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400789.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Tertius, well-deserving...
EDCS-20400789.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, beginning an epitaph for someone named Tertius.

EDCS-20400791.translation: Gaius Tettius, son of Gaius... for himself; to Soenia, daughter of Titus...; to Gaius Tettius, son of Gaius...; to Tettia, daughter of Gaius...
EDCS-20400791.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording names of individuals, possibly for a funerary monument or property marker.

EDCS-20400792.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly from the Roman Imperial period, containing partial names and numbers, the purpose of which is unclear.

EDCS-20400793.translation: Of Marcus Tilli Amisenus. In the front, 13.5 feet; in the field, 12 feet.
EDCS-20400793.summary: This is a boundary inscription from an agricultural plot, marking land belonging to Marcus Tilli Amisenus, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-20400794.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Quintus Tillius Faustus. His daughter, Tillia Faustina, [erected this] for her deserving father.
