EDCS-20400794.summary: This is a funerary dedication from the Imperial period, by Tillia Faustina to her father, Quintus Tillius Faustus.

EDCS-20400797.translation: For Lucius Valerius Arion, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe. In the front, 6 feet; in the field, 6 feet.
EDCS-20400797.summary: This is a boundary inscription for a plot of land owned by Lucius Valerius Arion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400800.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Varronia Iusta, daughter of Publius. Gaius Marcius Pius [erected this] for his most sweet wife.
EDCS-20400800.summary: This is a funerary dedication from the Imperial period, by Gaius Marcius Pius to his wife, Varronia Iusta.

EDCS-20400801.translation: Venafranius Albanus and Luculana Specie [made this] for themselves and their families while alive. In the front, 14 feet; in the field, 12 feet.
EDCS-20400801.summary: This is a funerary dedication and property boundary marker, likely from the Roman Imperial period, for Venafranius Albanus and Luculana Specie.

EDCS-20400802.translation: Here lie the bones of Quintus Venafranius Hermaiscus. Veveia Danais, his freedwoman, made [this] at her own expense.
EDCS-20400802.summary: This is a funerary inscription from the Roman Imperial period, commemorating Quintus Venafranius Hermaiscus, dedicated by his freedwoman Veveia Danais.

EDCS-20400803.translation: For Sextus Venafranius Primogenus, freedman of the colony, for himself and his family; for Quintus Venafranius Felix, freedman of the colony, for himself and his family. In the front, 12 feet; in the field, 12 feet.
EDCS-20400803.summary: This is a property boundary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording Sextus Venafranius Primogenus and Quintus Venafranius Felix, both freedmen of the colony.

EDCS-20400805.translation: Of Titus Vettuleius Niger, freedman of Titus. In the field, 12 feet; in the front, 12 feet.
EDCS-20400805.summary: This is a boundary inscription for land owned by Titus Vettuleius Niger, a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400811.translation: You who read this monument of death as you pass by, look how unjustly life was given to me, Quintae, only fifteen years old. I lived sweetly for sixteen years. Ascending, I laid down this soul. Now I die in darkness, deserted by light. After life is ended, all come here. Learn to live. Farewell.
EDCS-20400811.summary: This is a poignant epitaph from the Roman Imperial period, lamenting the early death of a fifteen-year-old girl named Quintae, and offering a memento mori to the reader.

EDCS-20400835.translation: To Fortuna. Pacuvia Marcellina willingly placed [this offering].
EDCS-20400835.summary: This is a votive inscription from the Roman Imperial period, dedicated by Pacuvia Marcellina to the goddess Fortuna.

EDCS-20400842.translation: To Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus, son of Emperor Caesar Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, grandson of divine Hadrian, great-grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of divine Nerva, consul, augur, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20400842.summary: This is a public dedication honoring Lucius Aurelius Commodus, likely for his consulship, indicating his imperial lineage and status.

EDCS-20400843.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, son of divine Marcus Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, grandson of divine Pius Antoninus, great-grandson of divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of divine Trajan and divine Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, holding tribunician power for the third time, proconsul, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20400843.summary: This is a public dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), detailing his imperial lineage and titles.

EDCS-20400846.translation: Lucius Arruntius, son of Lucius, consul for the 15th time, master of the rites, saw to the construction of the road and paths and the repair of the sewer at his own expense.
EDCS-20400846.summary: This inscription records Lucius Arruntius, a distinguished individual, funding public works including road maintenance and sewer repair.

EDCS-20400847.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-20400847.translation: To Titus Helvius Basila, son of Titus, aedile, praetor, proconsul, legate of Caesar Augustus, who bequeathed 400,000 sesterces to the people of Atina, so that from its income, grain should be given to their children until they reach adulthood, and afterwards 1,000 sesterces each should be given, his daughter Procula erected this.
EDCS-20400847.summary: This inscription records a legacy of 400,000 sesterces by Titus Helvius Basila to the community of Atina, intended for providing grain and then money to its children, as dedicated by his daughter Procula.

EDCS-20400848.translation: Marcus Tillius, son of Publius, erected this to Titus Helvius Basila, son of Titus, aedile, praetor, proconsul, legate of Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-20400848.summary: This is a memorial erected by Marcus Tillius to Titus Helvius Basila, honoring his public career.

EDCS-20400849.text_edition: \n Marco Rubreno Virio Prisco Pomponiano Magiano Proculo consuli clarissimo viro procons(uli) Africae curatori coloniae Minturnensium curatori coloniae Formianorum praetori candidato quaestori candidato Xviro salio collino civi et patrono ordo et pleps sic Atinas publice Iuniae Arriae Rufinae clarissimae feminae viri consularis filiae eius\n
EDCS-20400849.translation: To Marcus Rubrenus Virius Priscus Pomponianus Magianus Proculus, clarissimon vir, consul, proconsul of Africa, curator of the colony of Minturnae, curator of the colony of Formiae, praetor candidate, quaestor candidate, Xvir of the Salii Collini, citizen and patron; and to the Junia Arria Rufina, clarissimon femina, daughter of a consular, [honored by] the ordo and plebs of Atina publicly.
EDCS-20400849.summary: This is a joint dedication by the town council and people of Atina to Marcus Rubrenus Virius Priscus Pomponianus Magianus Proculus and Junia Arria Rufina, honoring their status and patronage.

EDCS-20400855.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-20400855.translation: To Marcus Tillius Rufus, son of Marcus, of the Teretina tribe, centurion of the 20th Legion Valeria Victrix, from the 300 of the 4th Praetorian Cohort Pia Vindex, first in the camp, honored with public equitation and gifts by the Emperors Severus and Antoninus Augusti, with a hasta pura and a golden crown, centurion of the 12th Urban Cohort and the 1st Vigilum Cohort, evocatus of the divine Emperors Marcus Antoninus and Commodus, patron of the municipality; the liberti [his freedmen] have granted him this place by decree of the decurions. He dedicated it himself on the 10th day before the Kalends of June, when Antoninus was consul for the third time, and he gave gifts to the decurions of 12 nummi each and to the urban plebs of 6 nummi each.
EDCS-20400855.summary: This inscription records the honors and promotions of Marcus Tillius Rufus, a centurion, and his patronage of the municipality, also noting gifts he distributed to the decurions and plebs.

EDCS-20400858.translation: To Quintus Herius Iustus, son of Quintus, of the Teretina tribe, patron of the municipality, flamen of divine Trajan, aedile, duovir twice, quinquennalis, quaestor twice, for his merits, the people of Atina, the decurions, Augustales, arkani, and the six-member college, and the plebs of both sexes, [have granted] this place from the income of a legacy bequeathed to them, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20400858.summary: This inscription records the grant of a public space to Quintus Herius Iustus by the community of Atina, recognizing his merits and public offices, funded by a legacy.

EDCS-20400862.translation: To Gaius Marrius, son of Marcus, grandson of Seppius, of the Teretina tribe, duovir, quaestor for the second time, quinquennalis.
EDCS-20400862.summary: This is a brief dedication honoring Gaius Marrius for his service in various municipal magistracies.

EDCS-20400863.translation: Seia, daughter of Lucius, his mother, erected this to Quintus Minatius Saturninus, son of Quintus, of the Teretina tribe, aedile, duovir, quaestor, quinquennalis, prefect of the artificers.
EDCS-20400863.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Seia, mother of Quintus Minatius Saturninus, honoring his municipal offices.

EDCS-20400865.translation: Gaius Obinius Rufus, son of Gaius, and Sextus Munnius, son of Gaius, as quinquennial duovirs, by decree of the decurions, with public money, took care to pave the street in the cattle market with 53,608 sesterces.
EDCS-20400865.summary: This inscription records the paving of a street in the cattle market by two duovirs using public funds.

EDCS-20400917.translation: To Mnemes, freedwoman of Quintus Meidius.
EDCS-20400917.summary: This is a dedication to Mnemes, a freedwoman, likely on a tomb or altar.

EDCS-20400934.translation: Lucius Accius Terentus built the temple, and erected the marble statue of Jupiter, and willingly fulfilled his vow. Lucius Lollianus Avitus was consul.
EDCS-20400934.summary: This inscription records Lucius Accius Terentus building a temple and dedicating a statue of Jupiter during the consulship of Lucius Lollianus Avitus.

EDCS-20400942.translation: Volusia Nice, freedwoman of Gaius, made this while alive for herself and her husband, for Marcus Obinius, son of Sextus.
EDCS-20400942.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a freedwoman for herself and her husband, possibly related to Marcus Obinius.

EDCS-20400948.translation: Quintus Obinius Crescens, son of Quintus, a freedman, made this while alive for himself and his slave, Pastore.
EDCS-20400948.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Quintus Obinius Crescens for himself and his slave.

EDCS-20400952.text_edition: \n Tittius Quinti filius Teretina Pontanus vivus fecit sibi et Tittiae Spuri filiae ucsori sic Tittio Quinti filio Teretina patri Tittiae Marci filiae matri Lucio Tittio Quinti filio Teretina fratri Cnaeo Tittio Quinti filio Teretina fratri Quinto Tittio Quinti filio Teretina fratri Tittiae Quinti filiae sorori\n
EDCS-20400952.translation: Tittius Pontanus, son of Quintus, of the Teretina tribe, made this while alive for himself, and for Tittia, daughter of Spurius, his wife; for Tittius, son of Quintus, of the Teretina tribe, his father; for Tittia, daughter of Marcus, his mother; for Lucius Tittius, son of Quintus, of the Teretina tribe, his brother; for Gnaeus Tittius, son of Quintus, of the Teretina tribe, his brother; for Tittia, daughter of Quintus, of the Teretina tribe, his sister.
EDCS-20400952.summary: This is a family tomb inscription set up by Tittius Pontanus for himself and his parents, siblings, and wife.

EDCS-20400954.translation: Marcus Papius, son of Marcus, and Lucius Matrius, son of Lucius, as duovirs judging law, by resolution of the scribes, took care to restore the statue of Concord and dedicated it to her; and they took care to build the base, steps, and altar with their own money and approved them. The fourth day before the Ides of October, when Gnaeus Domitius Calvinus and Gaius Asinius Pollio were consuls.
EDCS-20400954.summary: This inscription records the restoration and dedication of a statue of Concord, including its base, steps, and altar, by two duovirs during the consulship of Domitius Calvinus and Asinius Pollio.

EDCS-20400955.translation: Sacred to holy Hercules. Publius Pomponius Noetus fulfilled his vow and received his friends well. Lucius Eggius Marullus and Gnaeus Papirius Aelianus were consuls. The place was granted by decree of the decurions on the Kalends of July.
EDCS-20400955.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Hercules by Publius Pomponius Noetus, with the location granted by the decurions during the consulship of Eggius Marullus and Papirius Aelianus.

EDCS-20400956.translation: Marcus Octavius Calvinus, son of Marcus, and Quintus Laterinus, son of Quintus, as quaestors, by resolution of the scribes, took care to have the temple of Jupiter and the portico with its buildings constructed from public money, starting from the ground.
EDCS-20400956.summary: This inscription records the construction of the temple of Jupiter and its accompanying buildings by two quaestors using public funds.

EDCS-20400957.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-20400957.translation: Iucundus, freedman of Gaius Titedius, as magistrate, gave this altar to the Lares from his own funds. Gaius Antestius Vetus and Decimus Laelius Balbus were consuls.
EDCS-20400957.summary: This inscription records a magistrate, Iucundus, dedicating an altar to the Lares from his own resources during the consulship of Antestius Vetus and Laelius Balbus.

EDCS-20400958.translation: In the consulship of Emperor Caesar (for the 13th time) and Marcus Silvanus, a master of the Lares, for eternal memory, dedicated this from his own funds.
EDCS-20400958.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Lares made by a magister from his own funds during a consulship.

EDCS-20400959.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs Aeternae. Tiberius Claudius Praecilius Ligar(ius) Magonianus, through his father Praecilius Zoticus, brought in the water.
EDCS-20400959.summary: This inscription records the introduction of water by Tiberius Claudius Praecilius Ligar(ius) Magonianus, dedicated to the Nymphs Aeternae.

EDCS-20400965.translation: To Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of a god, consul for the 11th time, imperator for the 7th time, with tribunician power. Publius Rubrius Barba[tus], son of Marcus, of the Maecian tribe.
EDCS-20400965.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Augustus, listing his titles and consulships, set up by Publius Rubrius Barba[tus].

EDCS-20400971.text_edition: \n filius divi Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II consul pater patriae macerias clivi su supra portam Aquinatis a solo faciundas sua pecunia instituit consummavitque\n
EDCS-20400971.translation: Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, son of a god, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, consul, father of the country, built and completed the walls of the slope above the Aquinate gate at his own expense from the ground up.
EDCS-20400971.summary: This inscription records Emperor Alexander Severus financing and completing the construction of walls on a slope near the Aquinate gate.

EDCS-20400978.text_edition: \n Quinto Ummidio Cai filio Teretina Durmio Quadrato consuli XVviro sacris faciundis legato Tiberi Caesaris Augusti provinciae Lusitaniae legato divi Claudi in Illyrico eiusdem et Neronis Caesaris Augusti in Syria proconsuli provinciae Cypri quaestori divi Augusti et Tiberi Caesaris Augusti aedili curuli praetori aerarii Xviro stlitibus iudicandis curatori tabularum publicarum praefecto frumenti dandi ex senatus consulto\n
EDCS-20400978.translation: To Quintus Ummidius Quadratus, son of Caius, of the Teretina tribe, consul for the 15th time, member of the board of fifteen for sacred rites, legate of Tiberius Caesar Augustus in the province of Lusitania, legate of the deified Claudius in Illyricum, and of the same and Nero Caesar Augustus in Syria, proconsul of the province of Cyprus, quaestor of the deified Augustus and Tiberius Caesar Augustus, curule aedile, praetor of the treasury, member of the board of seventeen for judging lawsuits, curator of the public records, prefect for the distribution of grain by decree of the Senate.
EDCS-20400978.summary: This is an extensive cursus honorum detailing the career of Quintus Ummidius Quadratus, listing his numerous public offices and dignities.

EDCS-20400979.translation: Ummidia Quadratilla, daughter of Caius, made the amphitheater and the temple for the people of Casinum with her own funds.
EDCS-20400979.summary: This inscription records Ummidia Quadratilla funding the construction of an amphitheater and a temple for the inhabitants of Casinum.

EDCS-20400982.translation: Gaius Caprius, son of Caius, grandson of Caius, of the Teretina tribe, twice IIvir with judicial power, quinquennalis, pontifex, military tribune. And Cerrinia, daughter of Lucius, his wife.
EDCS-20400982.summary: This inscription records the magistracies and priesthoods held by Gaius Caprius and lists his wife, Cerrinia.

EDCS-20400984.translation: Marcus Obultronius Cultellus, prefect of the artisans, dedicated (this) by order of Emperor Claudius Caesar.
EDCS-20400984.summary: This inscription records the dedication of something by Marcus Obultronius Cultellus, prefect of the artisans, under the orders of Emperor Claudius.

EDCS-20400992.translation: Marcus Lucceius, son of Marcus, grandson of Caius, of the Teretina tribe, IIIIvir with judicial power, dedicated the statue and altar by decree of the Senate.
EDCS-20400992.summary: This inscription records Marcus Lucceius dedicating a statue and altar by senatorial decree.

EDCS-20400993.translation: To Lucius Luccius Hiberus, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, twice IIvir, quinquennalis, patron, priest of the Savadian rites, curator of public affairs for the Interamnates and Lirenates, patron of the same, judge selected from 400, the people of Casinum (set this up) publicly by decree of the decurions, for his merits.
EDCS-20400993.summary: This is a public dedication by the people of Casinum to Lucius Luccius Hiberus, honoring him for his various public offices, patronage, and judicial service.

EDCS-20400994.translation: To Lucius Luccius, son of Lucius, of the Palatine tribe, Ummidius Secundus, decurion, Lucius Luccius, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, Hibera, twice duovir, twice quinquennalis, patron of the colony, has erected this monument as benefactor. The college of artisans, with whom it is permitted to associate by decree of the Senate, has granted this place by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20400994.summary: A funerary monument erected by the college of artisans to Lucius Luccius Secundus, a decurion and patron of the colony, likely in the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-20400997.translation: To Pompeia Phile, daughter of Gnaeus, priestess of the goddesses, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20400997.summary: A dedication to Pompeia Phile, a priestess of the goddesses, set up by decree of the local council, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401000.translation: In the consulship of Nero Claudius Caesar Drusus Germanicus and Lucius Calpurnius Piso, by decree of the senators, Publius Spellius Spellianus Sabinus, quaestor, and Gaius Sattius Calatro, son of Gaius, second quaestor, have seen to it that the road from the Campus Martius to the Forum be paved with stone at public expense.
EDCS-20401000.summary: A public works inscription recording the paving of a road by two quaestors during the consulship of Nero Claudius Caesar Drusus Germanicus and Lucius Calpurnius Piso, likely in the late 1st century BC or early 1st century AD.

EDCS-20401016.translation: Abutia Melpomene, freedwoman of the woman, [and] to Quintus Benius Eron, freedman of Quintus, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-20401016.summary: A boundary marker indicating a plot of land measuring 10 feet, set up by Abutia Melpomene for herself and Quintus Benius Eron, possibly in the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-20401018.translation: Quintus Caecilius Fronto, son of Gaius, for himself and his family, 12 feet in front and 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-20401018.summary: A property marker for Quintus Caecilius Fronto, specifying dimensions of 12 feet (frontage) and 10 feet (depth), likely from the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-20401029.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavia Philumene lived 60 years.
EDCS-20401029.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Philumene, who lived 60 years, dedicated to the underworld deities.

EDCS-20401039.translation: Quintus Heleius, son of Lucius, grandson of Quintus, saw to its construction.
EDCS-20401039.summary: An inscription stating that Quintus Heleius, son of Lucius and grandson of Quintus, supervised the construction of a monument, likely from the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-20401044.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For the boy Hilarion, son of Ummidius(?) Quadratus. [---] lived 16 years. Mam[---], mother, placed this.
EDCS-20401044.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hilarion, a boy aged 16, son of Ummidius Quadratus, placed by his mother.

EDCS-20401056.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Mammia Secundilla, his wife. Pon[---]
EDCS-20401056.summary: A funerary inscription fragment for Mammia Secundilla, the wife of the deceased, dedicated to the underworld deities.

EDCS-20401057.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sabidia Valentina made this for Publius Marcius Festus, her most excellent and dearest husband.
EDCS-20401057.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Sabidia Valentina for her husband, Publius Marcius Festus, describing him as an excellent and dear husband.

EDCS-20401065.translation: Damale Theonas, freedwoman of Paccia, from her own means.
EDCS-20401065.summary: A funerary epitaph for Damale Theonas, set up by herself from her own resources, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401067.summary: A funerary epitaph for Synethe, freedwoman of Marcus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401086.translation: Lucius Stenius Thelgo, freedman of Lucius, for himself. 12 feet in front, 15 feet in the field.
EDCS-20401086.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Stenius Thelgo, a freedman, indicating the dimensions of his plot, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401091.translation: Quintus Venafraius Probus and his partner Tampia Venusta. 10 feet in front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-20401091.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Venafraius Probus and his partner Tampia Venusta, indicating the dimensions of their plot, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401092.text_edition: \n Quinti Veili Publi Quinti liberti Erotis\n
EDCS-20401092.translation: Erotis, freedman of Publius Quintus, freedman of Quintus Veilius.
EDCS-20401092.summary: A funerary epitaph for Erotis, a freedman, indicating his manumission chain, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401093.translation: For Eumenes, freedman of Caius, freedman of Lucius, Caius Ummidius. 12 feet in the field for Ummidia Secunda.
EDCS-20401093.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ummidia Secunda, with Eumenes, freedman of Caius, freedman of Lucius, Caius Ummidius, indicating the dimensions of the plot, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401126.translation: To Gaius Caesar, Imperator, Consul for the third time, Dictator for the third time, Pontifex Maximus, patron of the municipality of the Interamnates Lirenas.
EDCS-20401126.summary: A dedication to Emperor Gaius Caesar (Caligula) in his capacity as patron of the municipality of Interamna Lirenas, dating to his third consulship and dictatorship (39-40 AD).

EDCS-20401127.summary: A funerary inscription dedicating land of unspecified dimensions to Marcus Aurelius Basileus, a vir ducenarius and procurator of the military treasury, by the town council in recognition of his devotion to the community, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401131.translation: The Senate and people of the municipality of Interamna [honored] Lucius Caprius Gallus, son of Lucius, pontiff, duovir with jurisdiction, again quinquennial duovir, [---]
EDCS-20401131.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Caprius Gallus, a prominent local official, likely on a public monument, based on the titles listed.

EDCS-20401132.translation: To Lucius Caprius Gallus, son of Lucius, duovir with jurisdiction, again quinquennial duovir, pontiff, patron of the municipality.
EDCS-20401132.summary: This dedicates a monument to Lucius Caprius Gallus, a local official and patron, listing his titles and honors.

EDCS-20401137.text_edition: \n Quinto Marcio Quinti filio Teretina Regi IIIIviro iure dicundo Vibia Marci filia Tertulla uxor\n
EDCS-20401137.translation: Quintus Marcius Regulus, son of Quintus, of the Teretina tribe, duovir with jurisdiction; his wife, Vibia Tertulla, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-20401137.summary: This text records the dedication by Vibia Tertulla to her husband, Quintus Marcius Regulus, a duovir, likely on a tomb or memorial.

EDCS-20401139.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Orbius Principis, freedman of Marcus, baker, Augustalis seviri, [dedicated this tomb] to Marcus Coelius Hilarus, Ocea and Orbia Nympha, freedwoman of Marcus, and Marcus Orbius Agrypnos, in front 15 feet, in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-20401139.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Orbius Principis and others, recording the dimensions of the burial plot and noting his status as a baker and Augustalis seviri.

EDCS-20401140.translation: Gaius Pacuvius Luter, sevirus of Interamna, again Augustalis, for himself and Antistia Daphne, with whom he lived for 45 years.
EDCS-20401140.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Gaius Pacuvius Luter, an Augustalis sevirus, and Antistia Daphne, noting their long cohabitation.

EDCS-20401141.translation: To Marcus Sentius Crispinus, who held all decurial offices, the council and the entire populace, for his merits and labors, granted him honorary seats and the ornaments of the decurionate without payment, because he restored and embellished the summer baths with his own money and also established the surrounding porticoes, building the pool from the ground up; they decreed that a statue be erected to amplify his memory. The site was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20401141.summary: This inscription records a decree by the local council honoring Marcus Sentius Crispinus for his public works, specifically the restoration of the baths and construction of porticoes, and his free granting of decurionate honors.

EDCS-20401145.translation: Gaius Caninius Philogenes, freedman of Gaius, a dyer, and for Rubria Antiochinis, freedwoman of Lucius, in front 14 feet, in the field 10 feet.
EDCS-20401145.summary: This is a tomb inscription specifying the plot dimensions for Gaius Caninius Philogenes, a dyer, and Rubria Antiochinis.

EDCS-20401146.text_edition: \n Lucio Aefrio Luci filio Teretina Nigro Vibia Marci filia Tertulla filio\n
EDCS-20401146.translation: To Lucius Aefrius Niger, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe; his mother, Vibia Tertulla, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-20401146.summary: This inscription likely records a tomb or memorial dedicated by Vibia Tertulla to her son, Lucius Aefrius Niger.

EDCS-20401147.translation: To the departed spirits. To Cornelia Pentheris, granddaughter of Sextia Cornelia Pantheris, and to Quintus Sextius Paris, [dedicated to] his incomparable mother.
EDCS-20401147.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Sextius Paris to his mother, Cornelia Pentheris, and also to Sextia Cornelia Pantheris.

EDCS-20401149.translation: Novia Polla, daughter of Aquillia, daughter of Gaius, ordered this to be made by will to her mother.
EDCS-20401149.summary: This funerary inscription states that Aquillia Polla commissioned the monument by will for her mother, Novia.

EDCS-20401150.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Nunnia Silvina made this for her well-deserving patroness, Nunnia Severa.
EDCS-20401150.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a freedwoman to her patroness, Nunnia Severa.

EDCS-20401151.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Plotius Liberalis made this for his father, Gaius Plotius Felix, who lived 28 years, 4 months, and 17 days.
EDCS-20401151.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a son to his deceased father, Gaius Plotius Felix, including his lifespan.

EDCS-20401152.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying the remains of Synerotis.

EDCS-20401156.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valeria Quieta made this for her most excellent husband, Marcus Valerius Restitutus, who lived 33 years and 2 months, with whom she lived 15 years, 10 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-20401156.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a wife to her husband, Marcus Valerius Restitutus, detailing their married life and his lifespan.

EDCS-20401157.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Capitolina made this for her well-deserving husband, Lucius Vibullius Fortunatus, who lived 50 years, 6 months, and 7 days.
EDCS-20401157.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a wife to her husband, Lucius Vibullius Fortunatus, including his lifespan.

EDCS-20401158.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [---] who lived [---] years
EDCS-20401158.summary: This is an incomplete funerary dedication to Ulpia Nice[---].

EDCS-20401163.translation: Caecina Paetus, the patron, made this for his freedman, Gaius Caecina Euanthes, his freedman, most excellent and most faithful.
EDCS-20401163.summary: This is a dedication by a patron to his esteemed freedman, Gaius Caecina Euanthes.

EDCS-20401169.translation: Pacuvius Olympicus to his most excellent patroness, Pacuvia, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-20401169.summary: This is a dedication by a client to his patroness, Pacuvia Graphis.

EDCS-20401170.translation: He lives. Quintus Caelius, son of Spurius, lives, a shipbuilder. She lives. His wife, Camidia Aphrodisia, freedwoman of Marcus. Stranger, stop and if it is not bothersome, read through; do not get upset. I advise you to live warmly. Farewell.
EDCS-20401170.summary: This is an epitaph for Quintus Caelius, a shipbuilder, and his wife Camidia Aphrodisia, with a humorous and philosophical message to passersby.

EDCS-20401172.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aemilius Onesimus made this for his freedman and foster-son, Marcus Aemilius Primitivus, well-deserving.
EDCS-20401172.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a man to his freedman and foster-son, Marcus Aemilius Primitivus.

EDCS-20401174.translation: Lucius Manlius Demetrius, son of Lucius, freedman, alive; Manlia Ammia, daughter of Lucius, freedwoman, alive; Lucius Manlius Nicostratus, son of Lucius, freedman; Lucius Manlius Horestes, son of Lucius, freedman; Lucius Manlius Demetrius, son of Lucius, freedman; Lucius Manlius Quartio, son of Lucius, freedman.
EDCS-20401174.summary: This is a funerary monument listing six freedmen and women, all presumably freed by a Lucius Manlius, likely belonging to the same family unit.

EDCS-20401175.text_edition: \n nius Iuvenalis tribunus cohortis I Delmatarum IIvir quinquennalis flamen divi Vespasiani\n
EDCS-20401175.translation: Nius Iuvenalis, tribune of the I Cohort of Dalmatians, quinquennial duovir, flamen of the deified Vespasian.
EDCS-20401175.summary: This inscription lists the career and religious offices held by an individual named Nius Iuvenalis, who served as a military tribune and held local magistracies and priesthoods, likely in the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20401179.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-20401179.translation: Places for burial of the worshippers of Hercules Victor in the Domitian Estate, in front 120 feet, in the field 55 feet, have been donated by Marcus Animisius Priscus and Priscianus.
EDCS-20401179.summary: This is a dedication of land for a burial ground for the cult of Hercules Victor, donated by two individuals, with specific dimensions provided for the plot.

EDCS-20401185.translation: To Quintus Decius Saturninus, son of Quintus, prefect for the quinquennial term of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, pontiff, quaestor, duovir for dispensing justice, and to his children, by public decree of the decurions, because of his generosity and merits towards the state.
EDCS-20401185.summary: This is a public dedication honoring Quintus Decius Saturninus for his service and generosity, likely from the Tiberian period, likely a base for a statue or honorific monument.

EDCS-20401186.translation: To Quintus Decius Saturninus, son of Quintus, grandson of Marcus, minor pontiff, tubicen (trumpet player) of the public rites of the Roman People of Quirites, prefect of the artificers under the consul, three times curator of the Labican and Latin roads, military tribune, prefect of the artificers, duovir for dispensing justice and selecting judges in Asia, quinquennial duovir for dispensing justice in Verona, quaestor twice, duovir for dispensing justice, duovir again, quinquennial prefect, quinquennial prefect of Tiberius Caesar Augustus again, of Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, a third time, of Nero Caesar Germanicus's son, perpetual pontiff and flamen of Rome and the deified Augustus, by the authority and permission of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, and having been co-opted as patron of the colony, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20401186.summary: This is a lengthy funerary epitaph detailing the extensive public career and priesthoods of Quintus Decius Saturninus, who served in various administrative, military, and religious roles across the Roman Empire, likely dating from the Julio-Claudian period.

EDCS-20401189.translation: To Gaius Marcius Censorinus, son of Lucius, consul, augur, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20401189.summary: This is a public dedication honoring Gaius Marcius Censorinus, a consul and augur, likely a base for a statue, commissioned by the local town council.

EDCS-20401191.translation: To Gaius Octavius Appius Suetrio Sabinus, a most distinguished man, pontiff and augur, ordinary consul, imperial legate with proconsular authority over Pannonia Inferior, elected to correct the state of Italy, prefect of the grain supply, judge by delegation of imperial trials, imperial legate with proconsular authority over the province of Raetia, commander of detachments of the Germanic expedition, companion of our Emperor Augustus, legate of the Legio XXII Primigenia, juridical official for Aemilia and Liguria, curator of the new Via Latina, curator of the Republic of the Ocriculani, praetor for liberal causes, tribune and candidate quaestor, to the people of Aquinum [honoring him as] a most rare patron.
EDCS-20401191.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Gaius Octavius Appius Suetrio Sabinus, detailing his extensive military, administrative, and religious career under the early emperors, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE, honoring him as a patron of the people of Aquinum.

EDCS-20401192.translation: Lucius Fufidius Proculus, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe, tribune of the VII Claudian Pia Fidelis Legion, prefect of the artificers under the consul, duovir, aedile, quaestor, made this while alive for his grandson and himself.
EDCS-20401192.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Fufidius Proculus for himself and his grandson, detailing his military service and local magistracies, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20401193.translation: Marcus Barronius Sura, son of Marcus.
EDCS-20401193.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely identifying an individual, possibly on a tomb or dedication, with no further context.

EDCS-20401194.translation: To Marcus Barronius Sura, son of Marcus, of the Oufentina tribe, quinquennial duovir, augur, military tribune, prefect of the artificers, by public decree of the decurions, after death.
EDCS-20401194.summary: This is a posthumous public dedication honoring Marcus Barronius Sura, detailing his magistracies and priesthoods, likely a base for a statue, commissioned by the local town council.

EDCS-20401204.translation: To Gaius Cavarius Apulus, son of Sextus, of the Teretina tribe, a former commissioner of justice, priest, prefect, patron of the municipality, in the name of his mother Cornelia Iusta, daughter of Publius, [he provided] healthy water at his own expense.
EDCS-20401204.summary: This inscription records a public works project, likely the provision of a water supply, funded by Gaius Cavarius Apulus.

EDCS-20401206.translation: To Dentria Polla, daughter of Lucius, priestess of the divine Augusta, erected after her death by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20401206.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by decree to Dentria Polla, a priestess of the deified Augusta.

EDCS-20401207.translation: To Floria Posilla, daughter of Gaius, public priestess; a plot was publicly granted.
EDCS-20401207.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial plot to Floria Posilla, a public priestess.

EDCS-20401209.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-20401209.translation: Lucius Cofius Eros, son of Marcus, a sevir of Victoria, paved the Forum of Aquinum here with his own money, and for his wife Coffia Dionysia, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-20401209.summary: This inscription records Lucius Cofius Eros paving the forum of Aquinum at his own expense, also commemorating his wife.

EDCS-20401212.translation: To Marcus Rubrius Proculus, Augustalis of Casinum, curator of the annona of Casinum; Marcus Rubrius Proculeianus, his most dutiful father, and his wife Sergia Quartilla.
EDCS-20401212.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Rubrius Proculeianus and his wife Sergia Quartilla to Proculus, who held several important civic and religious offices.

EDCS-20401213.translation: Living: Safronia Tertia, freedwoman of a woman. For herself and for Publius Sextilius Primus, haruspex and sevir of Aquinum, and for her family. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in depth.
EDCS-20401213.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a plot of land measuring 12x12 feet, made by Safronia Tertia for herself, her husband Publius Sextilius Primus, and their family.

EDCS-20401233.translation: Of Titus Appellas, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe.
EDCS-20401233.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a larger dedication or tombstone, for Titus Appellas.

EDCS-20401240.translation: Bones of Prima, freedwoman of Baebius, of the Quintus tribe.
EDCS-20401240.summary: This is a simple burial inscription identifying the deceased as Prima.

EDCS-20401280.translation: [---] Salvia for herself and Numerius Si[---] [---] and Marcus Lucius Car[---]
EDCS-20401280.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription indicating Salvia made provisions for herself and at least two other individuals.

EDCS-20401297.translation: Of Gaius Pacuius Polion, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe.
EDCS-20401297.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a larger dedication or tombstone, for Gaius Pacuius Polion.

EDCS-20401298.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Julius Augustanus made this for his incomparable wife Papinia Thallusa, who cultivated poetry, as a mark of well-deserved respect. She lived 35 years.
EDCS-20401298.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband to his deceased wife, commemorating her literary pursuits and providing her lifespan.

EDCS-20401333.translation: While alive, Quintus Vicrius Hilarus, freedman of Quintus, made this for himself and for Secundus. Frontage 12 feet, depth 12 feet.
EDCS-20401333.summary: A funerary dedication made by a freedman for himself and another individual, specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-20401339.translation: Vitulus, son of Lucius Saufeius Lupercus, lived 17 years.
EDCS-20401339.summary: A brief funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Vitulus.

EDCS-20401374.translation: [---]tilius, son of Caius, from the Aemilia tribe, first centurion of the VI Legion, prefect of a cohort, [---] tribune of soldiers, prefect of horsemen, prefect of craftsmen, quinquennial aedile, Augustalis, his funeral was conducted at public expense by decree of the decurions of Aquinum, under the supervision of Anterotus and Cosmus, his freedmen.
EDCS-20401374.summary: An epitaph detailing the extensive public career and honors, including a public funeral, of a Roman official named Curtilius, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20401376.translation: To the departed spirits. For Titus Elvius Fregellanus, my incomparable and dearest husband, a businessman, a decurion of Fabrateria, a Novanian, who had held all offices; he lived 38 years, 9 months, and 12 days. Caecilia Victorina, with whom he lived 16 years, 6 months, and 14 days, made this for him, who deserved well of her.
EDCS-20401376.summary: A funerary epitaph from a wife to her deceased husband, who was a businessman and local official, detailing their married life and his lifespan.

EDCS-20401379.translation: Lucius Pomponius, son of Spurius(?), and Lucius Candilius, son of Marcus, duovirs, from their own funds.
EDCS-20401379.summary: An inscription recording the expenditure by two duovirs, Lucius Pomponius and Lucius Candilius, on a public project.

EDCS-20401380.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vitulius Novianus and Aelia Florida made this for their dearest son, Marcus Vitullius VIOVIAT[---], who died at age 17 years, 6 months, for your labors; your parents have made this monument for you, son, who deserved well.
EDCS-20401380.summary: A funerary epitaph from parents to their deceased son, recording his age at death and expressing their grief and his deserving nature.

EDCS-20401382.translation: To the departed spirits. Annia Amarullis made this for her excellent husband, Marcus Annio Noetus, Augustalis, as a mark of well-deserved respect.
EDCS-20401382.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, who held the position of Augustalis.

EDCS-20401383.translation: To the departed spirits. For Caius Fabraternus Primitivus, Augustalis.
EDCS-20401383.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Fabraternus Primitivus, who held the position of Augustalis.

EDCS-20401392.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gnaeus Annaeus Felix, who lived 23 years, 10 months, 3 days, and for Phile, who lived 16 years, 5 months, 7 days. Gnaeus Annaeus Felix, the father.
EDCS-20401392.summary: A funerary epitaph from a father to his deceased son and another individual, recording their names and ages at death.

EDCS-20401394.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Perge to her incomparable husband, Marcus Annius Florentinus.
EDCS-20401394.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Claudia Perge to her husband Marcus Annius Florentinus.

EDCS-20401395.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Sulpicius Firminus to his deserving wife, Annia Ciloniana, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-20401395.summary: This funerary dedication from Gaius Sulpicius Firminus to his wife Annia Ciloniana was likely set up after her death.

EDCS-20401396.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fronto, father, set up [this memorial] for his most sweet daughter, Apidia Iustina.
EDCS-20401396.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a father, Fronto, for his daughter Apidia Iustina.

EDCS-20401400.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Carpus, husband, made [this memorial] for his incomparable wife, Claudia Flora, who lived thirty-eight years and three months, leaving behind twelve children.
EDCS-20401400.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Claudia Flora at age 38 and is dedicated by her husband Carpus.

EDCS-20401402.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gavia Salutaris made [this memorial] for her most sweet husband, Gavius Tertius.
EDCS-20401402.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Gavia Salutaris to her husband Gavius Tertius.

EDCS-20401405.translation: Gaius Marius Salvius Gallus, freedman of Gaius, for himself and his people, and for Tuccia Philemation, freedwoman of a woman.
EDCS-20401405.summary: This is a property ownership inscription for Gaius Marius Salvius Gallus, his family, and his freedwoman Tuccia Philemation.

EDCS-20401406.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Apidius Fronto to his excellent mother, Mestria Calliste.
EDCS-20401406.summary: This funerary dedication from Gaius Apidius Fronto to his mother Mestria Calliste was likely placed on her tomb.

EDCS-20401407.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Festa, daughter, [set this up] for her most rare mother, Mestria Festa.
EDCS-20401407.summary: This funerary dedication from Festa to her mother Mestria Festa was likely set up on her mother's tomb.

EDCS-20401409.translation: Caius Mevius Tarulus, freedman of Decimus, for himself and his people, in front 14 feet, in the field 16 feet.
EDCS-20401409.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land owned by Caius Mevius Tarulus.

EDCS-20401410.translation: The bones of Naevia Dionysia, freedwoman of Gaius.
EDCS-20401410.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription indicating the burial place of Naevia Dionysia.

EDCS-20401411.translation: To the departed spirits. To Lucius Numitorius Secundus, his most beloved son, who lived nine years and thirty-seven days. Lucius Numitorius Secundus and Carellia Eutychis, his parents.
EDCS-20401411.summary: This is a tombstone set up by parents for their son who died at a young age.

EDCS-20401413.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Paccia Arbuscula, daughter of Sextus. Marcus Herennius Fortunatus, to his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-20401413.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by a husband to his wife.

EDCS-20401416.translation: To the departed spirits. To Pyrallis, his wife, well-deserving. Lucius Plutius Plegon made this.
EDCS-20401416.summary: This is a tombstone set up by a husband for his wife.

EDCS-20401417.translation: ...Rufus, son of Lucius... to himself and Magnae... his daughter... to Illa, his wife... to Ido, his grandson, lived 15 years.
EDCS-20401417.summary: This is a funerary inscription mentioning the deceased, his daughter, wife, and grandson, possibly a family tomb.

EDCS-20401418.translation: Of Marcus Trebellius Primus, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-20401418.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely indicating ownership or dedication by Marcus Trebellius Primus.

EDCS-20401419.translation: Tuccia, freedwoman of Lucius, Marcus, and Gaius.
EDCS-20401419.summary: This inscription records the name and status of Tuccia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-20401420.translation: Marcus Vibius Marcellus, son of Stator, of the Oufentina tribe, to himself and to Stator Vibius Marcellus, son of Stator, of the Oufentina tribe, his father, according to the will.
EDCS-20401420.summary: This funerary inscription records a man dedicating a tomb to himself and his father, as stipulated by a will.

EDCS-20401421.translation: Gaius Vibius Nerullus, son of Gaius, freedman...
EDCS-20401421.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription likely recording the name and filiation of a freedman.

EDCS-20403128.translation: To the departed spirits. To Marcus Aurelius Pastor, soldier of the II Parthica Severiana legion, a Thracian by birth, who lived 46 years, served 24 years. Septimius Vetus, his heir and comrade, made this for the most deserving.
EDCS-20403128.summary: This is a tombstone for a soldier of the II Parthica legion, set up by his heir and comrade.

EDCS-20403130.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-20403130.translation: ...to Flavius Proculeianus, son of Gaius, quinquennalis, curator of the market of Fabrateria Vetus, curator of the new calendar, curator of the Formiani(?), who performed all public duties; to his grandfather, Gaius Mollius Proculeianus, patron of the municipality, in whose name HS 25,000 sesterces were donated to the town council and the people, so that on his birthday, the twelfth day before the Kalends of January, sportulae may be distributed annually; to him for his merits, the devotees and priestess of the goddess Ceres, to their patron, erected a statue, at the dedication of which he feasted everyone present with 50 sesterces and donated to the aforementioned devotees 4,000 sesterces, so that from the interest of this sum, on the tenth day before the Kalends of January, sportulae may be distributed annually on his birthday; because the aforementioned... have not been distributed, that amount will revert to the state, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20403130.summary: This is a decree from the town council of Fabrateria Vetus recording the erection of a statue for Flavius Proculeianus, who endowed public festivals and distributions in his name and that of his grandfather.

EDCS-20403131.translation: To Rufus, dictator and patron, [his] brothers, [and] the most chaste [woman], the aedile of the new Fabrateria, willingly [dedicated this] again; [he] lived twice [for how many?] years.
EDCS-20403131.summary: This inscription, likely a funerary monument, mentions Rufus as a patron and dictator, and commemorates an aedile of the new Fabrateria who lived for an indeterminate number of years, twice over.

EDCS-20403132.translation: To Saenia Balbilla, daughter of Cnaeus, priestess of the divine Faustina, from the decuriae, by a collection of money, on account of her merits.
EDCS-20403132.summary: This dedication honors Saenia Balbilla, a priestess of the divine Faustina, with contributions from the decuriae due to her merits.

EDCS-20403135.translation: To the gods of the underworld. [He/She] made [this] for Saenia Helpis, [his/her] most faithful freedwoman.
EDCS-20403135.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Saenia Helpis, a freedwoman, erected by her patron.

EDCS-20403138.translation: To the gods of the underworld. For Numerius Clodius Proculinus, son of Numerius, of the Palatine tribe, decurion of the colony of Frusinum; fate gave Proculinus [his] thirtieth year; for whom [he] made this [monument] from the life of [his] parents. Numerius Clodius Nymphicus and Vargunteia Procilla, daughter of Marcus, and Numerius Clodius Numerianus, [his] brother, and for themselves and their descendants, made [this].
EDCS-20403138.summary: This funerary inscription honors Numerius Clodius Proculinus, a decurion of Frusinum, who died in his thirtieth year, and was erected by his parents, brother, and for their descendants.

EDCS-20403139.translation: [Lucius] Magneius Niger, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe, twice duovir; [his] son Magneius Rufus, son of Lucius; Magneius Sardus, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe; Quintus Allienus Chresimus, freedman of Quintus; Magneia Iucunda ordered [this] to be made in her will.
EDCS-20403139.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned in the will of Magneia Iucunda and honors Lucius Magneius Niger, who held the office of duovir, along with his son and another Magneius, and Quintus Allienus Chresimus.

EDCS-20403141.translation: To Numerius Clodius Sabinianus, son of Numerius, of the Aniensis tribe, [his] most dutiful son. Numerius Clodius Sabinus and Flavia Hesperis, [his] parents, [dedicated this]. Born from the wish and vows of his parents, when he had brightly completed twenty years and furthermore five months, living blamelessly for thirteen days, the Fates, too swift in death, dared to deprive the parents of their living son and to surround them with heavy grief.
EDCS-20403141.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Numerius Clodius Sabinianus, who died at the age of 20 years, 5 months, and 13 days, lamenting the premature death by his grieving parents.

EDCS-20403142.translation: To Quintus Minucius Alexander, freedman of the woman (Minucia?), [and] Dica, freedwoman of the woman (Minucia?).
EDCS-20403142.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Minucius Alexander and Dica, both freedpersons, likely belonging to the same mistress, Minucia.

EDCS-20403155.translation: Aulus Valgius, son of Caius; Lucius Runtius, son of Caius Sisipus; Marcus Fufidius, son of Marcus; as aediles, by decree of the Senate, undertook [the task] of collecting rainwater and constructing sewers, and they approved them.
EDCS-20403155.summary: This inscription records the work of aediles Aulus Valgius, Lucius Runtius, and Marcus Fufidius, who, by senatorial decree, were responsible for water collection and sewer construction.

EDCS-20403158.translation: Quintus Gavius, son of Caius [?]; Gnaeus Longidius [?]; Gnaeus Tillius [?]; as aediles, by decree of the Senate, [built?] a tower, a wall, and piles for the fullonica [superior?] and inferior.
EDCS-20403158.summary: This inscription details the construction of a tower, wall, and piles for a fullonica (dyeing or laundry workshop) by aediles Quintus Gavius, Gnaeus Longidius, and Gnaeus Tillius, acting under senatorial authority.

EDCS-20403165.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Aemilia Ianuaria and Diphilus, [her] most unhappy parents, made [this] for [their] most dutiful daughter, who lived 8 years, 2 months, and 13 days.
EDCS-20403165.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Aemilia Chrysopolis, who lived 8 years, 2 months, and 13 days, erected by her grieving parents.

EDCS-20403176.translation: To the departed spirits. Optandus made this for his most cherished spouse.
EDCS-20403176.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Optandus to his spouse.

EDCS-20403179.translation: To the departed spirits. [---] of Lucius Pompeius.
EDCS-20403179.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription likely dedicated to Lucius Pompeius.

EDCS-20403185.text_edition: \n Cultores Silvani curante Marco Albio Piero\n
EDCS-20403185.translation: The worshippers of Silvanus, under the care of Marcus Albius Pierus.
EDCS-20403185.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by the worshippers of Silvanus, overseen by Marcus Albius Pierus.

EDCS-20403186.translation: Lucius Sabidius Memor gave this as a gift.
EDCS-20403186.summary: This is a dedication of a gift by Lucius Sabidius Memor.

EDCS-20403189.translation: To Lucius Firmilius, son of Lucius, first centurion, military tribune, prefect of justice, first pontiff, by the Fourth Soran Legion, for honor and valor.
EDCS-20403189.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Firmilius with various military and civic titles, likely set up by the Fourth Soran Legion.

EDCS-20403190.text_edition: \n Marco Baebio Marci filio Romilia Secundo aedili praefecto iure dicundo IIviro quinquennali viocuro ex senatus consulto II decreto decurionum Marcus Baebius Marci filius Romilia Sabinus Marcus Valerius Marci filius Romilia Septiminus Marcus Valerius Marci filius Romilia Secundinus heredes eius ex testamento IS locus datus decreto decurionum cuius dedicatione crustum et mulsum populo divisum est\n
EDCS-20403190.translation: To Marcus Baebius, son of Marcus, of the Romilia tribe, aedile, prefect of justice, quinquennial duovir, vicomagister, by his heirs Marcus Baebius Romilia Sabinus, Marcus Valerius Romilia Septiminus, and Marcus Valerius Romilia Secundinus, from his will. This place was granted by decree of the decurions, at the dedication of which crustum and mulsum were distributed to the people.
EDCS-20403190.summary: This is a funerary or commemorative inscription for Marcus Baebius, detailing his civic offices and the distribution of food and drink to the populace upon its dedication, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-20403205.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Restitutus made this for Tiberius Claudius Primus, an excellent man and most faithful friend, who lived 30 years, 11 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-20403205.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Restitutus to Tiberius Claudius Primus, noting his age and relationship.

EDCS-20403208.translation: To Marcus Florius, son of Publius, of the Cornelia tribe, in width 20 feet.
EDCS-20403208.summary: This inscription records a land measurement, likely for a property boundary or burial plot, for Marcus Florius.

EDCS-20403210.translation: Quintus Gustilius Hilarus, freedman of Quintus, in the field 10 feet [---].
EDCS-20403210.summary: This inscription marks a boundary, possibly for land or a grave, for Quintus Gustilius Hilarus.

EDCS-20403214.translation: To the departed spirits. Sacred to Pudicitia, daughter of Magius Marinianus. Magius Fas[---]us O[---].
EDCS-20403214.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Pudicitia, daughter of Magius Marinianus, by Magius Fas[---]us.

EDCS-20403218.translation: To Q[...], Vai[...]-cula, for herself and Naevia M[...] and Septum[...]. On the front.
EDCS-20403218.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph, likely for a tomb, with a dedication to multiple individuals.

EDCS-20403220.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Oppia Calsiane, mother, made this for her daughter, as it was fitting that the daughter should do this for me. For Vitellia Tallia, daughter of Lucius, who lived 21 years. Septimius Profuturus [made this] for his most excellent wife.
EDCS-20403220.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by a mother for her deceased daughter and by a husband for his wife.

EDCS-20403221.translation: Oppia Dionysia, freedwoman of Caius, ordered this to be made for herself and for Lucius Venelius, son of Titus, of the Romilia tribe.
EDCS-20403221.summary: This funerary epitaph records a double dedication for the patron and freedwoman themselves.

EDCS-20403223.translation: For Plotulena Rufa, freedwoman of Caius. Caius Pontius Surus [paid] from his own funds.
EDCS-20403223.summary: This epitaph records a funerary monument dedicated by Caius Pontius Surus to his freedwoman Plotulena Rufa.

EDCS-20403224.translation: For Marcus Pomponius Mena, son of Marcus.
EDCS-20403224.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Pomponius Mena.

EDCS-20403225.translation: For Publius Roesius Erotus, freedman of Publius.
EDCS-20403225.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Roesius Erotus.

EDCS-20403226.translation: Titus Scomedius Nic[h]ep[h]or, freedman of Titus, made [this] for themselves while alive.
EDCS-20403226.summary: This funerary epitaph records a self-funded monument erected by Titus Scomedius Nic[h]ep[h]or for himself and possibly his family.

EDCS-20403227.translation: Lives. Marcus Spultus Agaton, freedman of Marcus. Lucilia Hilara, freedwoman of a woman.
EDCS-20403227.summary: This epitaph lists two individuals, Marcus Spultus Agaton and Lucilia Hilara, likely deceased residents of the area.

EDCS-20403228.translation: To Marcus Tadius Nigrus, son of Lucius, of the Romilia tribe, by his will, for 3,000 sesterces, under the supervision of Marcus Apstidius, son of Marcus, of the Romilia tribe.
EDCS-20403228.summary: This epitaph records a bequest of 3,000 sesterces by Marcus Tadius Nigrus for a public purpose, overseen by Marcus Apstidius.

EDCS-20403231.translation: For Lucius Valerius Nigrus, son of Quintus, of the Romilia tribe.
EDCS-20403231.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Nigrus.

EDCS-20403232.translation: To Vertuleia Artimisia, freedwoman of Marcus, and to Decia Advena and Vertuleia Chrysarria.
EDCS-20403232.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Vertuleia Artimisia and two other women, possibly family members, with two individuals identified as freedwomen and one potentially bearing a Roman cognomen.

EDCS-20403233.translation: Lucius Vettius Ligus, son of Lucius, of the Romilia tribe.
EDCS-20403233.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Lucius Vettius Ligus, son of Lucius, and noting his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-20403234.translation: To Viaria Secunda, freedwoman of Titus, and her freedwoman.
EDCS-20403234.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Viaria Secunda, a freedwoman, and her own freedwoman.

EDCS-20403235.translation: To Lucius Vibius Hilarus, freedman of Lucius, 20 feet in the field, 24 feet in the front.
EDCS-20403235.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking a plot of land for Lucius Vibius Hilarus, a freedman, with dimensions specified.

EDCS-20403248.translation: [---], of the Romilia tribe, 14 feet in the front.
EDCS-20403248.summary: This inscription, with considerable lacunae, likely marks a funerary plot with a measurement and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-20403254.translation: In the consulship of Gaius Calvisius and Lucius Passienus. Marcus Minius Rufus, son of Marcus, priest six times, and Lucius Vibidius, son of Lucius, priest, made for Jupiter Atratus and the native gods, with a small shrine and a base and a portico, at their own expense.
EDCS-20403254.summary: This is a dedication of a sanctuary, including a small shrine and portico, to Jupiter Atratus and the Indigetes by two priests, Marcus Minius Rufus and Lucius Vibidius, during the consulship of Calvisius and Passienus.

EDCS-20403256.translation: To Felix Victor, a distinguished man, patron, for his merits, by the town council of the Marian Cerei.
EDCS-20403256.summary: This is an honorary inscription from the ordo Cereatium Marianorum to their patron Felix Victor, recognizing his distinguished service and merits.

EDCS-20403257.translation: [---], to Gaius Marius, son of Gaius, seven times consul, praetor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor, augur, military tribune.
EDCS-20403257.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription listing the high offices held by Gaius Marius, son of Gaius, including multiple consulships and other significant magistracies and religious positions.

EDCS-20403262.translation: While alive, to Gaius Mussius Hilarus, freedman of Papia, for himself, and while alive, to Acerronia Salvia, freedwoman of [Acerronius], 12 feet in the front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-20403262.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Mussius Hilarus and Acerronia Salvia, made while they were alive, specifying plot dimensions.

EDCS-20403272.translation: Dedicated on the Kalends of June in the consulship of Lateranus and Rufinus. For this dedication, he gave to the decurions and the six leaders and the Augustales 4 denarii, to the dendrophori 3 denarii, with an addition of bread and wine to the people 1 denarius, under the supervision of Urgulanius Secundus. The place was given by decree of the decurions to Lucius Alfius Valentino, son of Lucius, of the Cornelia tribe, twice duumvir, twice quinquennalis, patron of the municipality of Verulanum, curator of the public affairs of the colony of Casinatium, and patron, by the council of the seviri and Augustales, for his merits.
EDCS-20403272.summary: This is a dedication of a public space, likely a tomb or plot, to Lucius Alfius Valentino, a prominent local official, in recognition of his merits, with records of expenditures and official decisions.

EDCS-20403274.translation: To Quintus Blaesius Rufus, son of Quintus, of the Cornelia tribe. His father, Quintus Blaesius Grupus, freedman of Quintus, and his mother, Vettia Salvia, freedwoman of Marcus, compelled by the untimely death of their son, which was what he was born to do for his parents, made this (monument).
EDCS-20403274.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by parents for their son, Quintus Blaesius Rufus, likely in the early Imperial period.

EDCS-20403340.translation: Marcus Aurelius Diza, soldier of the Second Parthian Severian Legion, VIth century, front rank. He lived 37 years, served 18 years, 7 months, and 15 days. To Brais, his dearest husband.
EDCS-20403340.summary: This is a tombstone for a soldier named Marcus Aurelius Diza of the Second Parthian Severian Legion, who died in service, likely dating to the Severan period.

EDCS-20403342.translation: Aurelius Serrapio, soldier of the Second Parthian Severian Legion, VIIII (century?), front rank. He lived 40 years, 7 months, and 11 days; he served 16 years. Aurelia Rufina, his wife(?) and heir, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-20403342.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius Serrapio, a soldier in the Second Parthian Severian Legion, who died at age 40, with his wife and heir Aurelia Rufina setting up the monument.

EDCS-20403343.translation: [---]. He served as a caligatus (infantryman) for 24 years and as a centurion for 3 years; he is buried here in his prime. Galatia Matrona to her most devoted husband.
EDCS-20403343.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a soldier who served for 27 years, erected by his wife, Galatia Matrona, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20500010.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Carbetania Maxima made this for her most devoted father, Publius Carbetanius Rufus, an equestrian who was a master of laws.
EDCS-20500010.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Publius Carbetanius Rufus, an equestrian and legal expert, by his daughter Carbetania Maxima, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20500033.translation: To Gaius Julius Augusti libertus Heleno, by decree of the town councillors of the municipality of Aletrinum, and by the pledge of the sevirate officials, the townspeople, and the inhabitants, on account of his merits.
EDCS-20500033.summary: This honorary inscription from the municipality of Aletrinum honors Gaius Julius Heleno, a freedman of Augustus, for his services, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-20500035.translation: [---] Thesmus, quaestorian scribe(?), to Venusta, her delight, who lived 13 years [---].
EDCS-20500035.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, possibly for a child or a beloved pet, with the name Thesmus and a mention of Venusta, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20500040.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Felicitas made this for her incomparable husband, who lived with me in marriage for 23 years, 7 months, and 17 days, for the well-deserving.
EDCS-20500040.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed husband, erected by his wife Flavia Felicitas, detailing their 23 years of marriage, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20500044.translation: Manes, I made this hollowed rock. Now I choose a resting place for my wife, Paul(l)a, and in equal rest for Viatorinus, their grandson, who lived 4 years.
EDCS-20500044.summary: This is a funerary epitaph in a rock-cut tomb, set up by Viatorinus for his wife Paul(l)a and their grandson, who died at age 4, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-20500045.translation: Sacred to Fortuna. Gaius Callaeus Securus, by the vow undertaken.
EDCS-20500045.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Fortuna, dedicated by Gaius Callaeus Securus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20500046.translation: Sacred to public health. Quintius Cestianus Proculus, son of Gaius, of the Palatine tribe, a leader among the Ducenii.
EDCS-20500046.summary: This inscription records a dedication to public health by Quintius Cestianus Proculus, likely a private dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20500049.translation: To Faustina Augusta, wife of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Germanicus, with his 27th tribunician power, 3rd consulship, father of the country. Gaius Laberius Quartinus, consul for the 7th time, member of the epulones.
EDCS-20500049.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Empress Faustina and honors Gaius Laberius Quartinus, a distinguished magistrate, likely from the late Antonine period.

EDCS-20500050.translation: From the great-grandson of the deified Nerva, from the grandson of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, chief priest, victor in Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, consul twice, proconsul, with his 11th tribunician power, unconquered emperor. The people of Ferentinum Novani, most devoted to his divine will.
EDCS-20500050.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the people of Ferentinum Novani to Emperor Septimius Severus, highlighting his titles and military victories, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20500051.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the Emperor, of the camp, of the senate, and of the fatherland, pious and fortunate. To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest conqueror of Parthia, Britain, Germania, chief priest, with his 16th tribunician power, emperor twice, consul four times, proconsul, great and unconquered, and most valiant prince. The Senate and the people of Ferentinum.
EDCS-20500051.summary: This inscription honors Empress Julia Domna and Emperor Caracalla, listing their numerous titles and achievements, and is a joint dedication by the Senate and people of Ferentinum, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20500052.translation: To the honor of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, most ample priest of the Unconquered Sun Elagabalus, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul three times, father of the country. The Senate and people of Ferentinum.
EDCS-20500052.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Elagabalus by the Senate and people of Ferentinum, honoring his religious and secular titles, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20500053.translation: To Cornelia Salonina, most holy Augusta, wife of our lord Gallienus Invictus Augustus. The people of Ferentinum Novani, devoted to his divine will and majesty.
EDCS-20500053.summary: This inscription records the devotion of the people of Ferentinum Novani to Empress Cornelia Salonina, wife of Emperor Gallienus, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-20500054.translation: To Titus Pontius Sabinus, son of Titus, of the Palatine tribe, a primipilaris twice, procurator of the province of Narbonensis, quinquennial magistrate with judicial authority, flamen and patron of the municipality. Valeria Procula, daughter of Lucius, his wife, a place granted by decree of the decurions. Titus Pontius Sabinus, son of Titus, of the Palatine tribe, prefect of the 1st cohort of Pannonians and Dalmatians, cavalry, Roman citizens, tribune of the soldiers of the 6th Legion Ferrata, honored with gifts in the Parthian campaign by the deified Trajan with a pure spear, a banner, and a mural crown, centurion of the 22nd Legion Primigenia, chief centurion of the 13th Legion Gemina, primipilaris of the 3rd Legion Augusta, commander of detachments in military campaigns, tribune of the 7th Legion Gemina, 8th Augusta, 22nd Primigenia, tribune of the 3rd cohort of vigiles, 14th urban cohort, 2nd praetorian cohort, primipilaris twice, procurator of the province of Narbonensis, quinquennial magistrate with judicial authority, flamen, patron of the municipality.
EDCS-20500054.summary: This extensive funerary inscription honors Titus Pontius Sabinus, a highly decorated military officer and administrator, detailing his career from centurion to procurator, and is a dedication by his wife Valeria Procula, likely from the early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20500056.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Optatinus, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe, prefect of the 1st equestrian cohort of Aquitanians. Pontia Sabina, daughter of Titus, his mother, a priestess, a place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20500056.summary: This funerary inscription records the honor bestowed upon Quintus Caecilius Optatinus by his mother Pontia Sabina, who was a flaminica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20500057.translation: To Gaius Julius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Publilia tribe, a veteran of the 6th Praetorian cohort, centuria of Aquilius Maximus, former praetor, aedile, quinquennial magistrate with judicial authority in the municipality of Aletrium. Gaius Julius Rufinus, son of Gaius, his brother, for his well-deserving services, and for himself and his descendants.
EDCS-20500057.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Gaius Julius Rufinus to his brother Gaius Julius Rufus, a veteran and local magistrate in Aletrium, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20500059.translation: The council of decurions decreed that it should be restored. Gaius Anicius Pera, worn out by age.
EDCS-20500059.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a base by the local council of decurions for Gaius Anicius Pera, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20500060.translation: To Caecilius Olympicus, my grandfather, and to Catia Primilla, daughter of Lucius, my mother. The same Quirinalis, quinquennalis.
EDCS-20500060.summary: This inscription honors the parents of the dedicator, Quirinalis, who held the office of quinquennalis.

EDCS-20500068.translation: To Gaius Naevius Blaesus, son of Lucius, of the Pob(lilia) tribe, who was IIvir iure dicundo and aedile, by the arbitration of Marcus Acilius, son of Marcus.
EDCS-20500068.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication to Gaius Naevius Blaesus, an official, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-20500069.translation: To Lucius Pacuvius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Pollia tribe, [IIIIvir] with aedilic power, IIIIvir iure dicundo, IIIIvir quinquennalis with censorian power, pontifex, praefectus fabrum. The wine and cakes are given to the townsfolk who request them; if you complain about me being late or lazy, they will be divided for you at the sixth hour. You would complain less if I were [more] tardy or sluggish. [---] of the peregrini. Decurion. [---] [---] [---].
EDCS-20500069.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription or dedication commemorating Lucius Pacuvius Severus, who held multiple high offices in his municipality, and includes a conditional gift of wine and cakes to attendees.

EDCS-20500070.translation: To Lucius Popilius Flaccus, son of Lucius. Set up by the community. [---] [---].
EDCS-20500070.summary: A dedication to Lucius Popilius Flaccus, set up by the community, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-20500071.translation: To Pontia Sabina, daughter of Titus. [---] [---].
EDCS-20500071.summary: A fragmented inscription that appears to be a dedication to Pontia Sabina.

EDCS-20500072.translation: Publius and Marcus Salonius, sons of Tiberius, as aediles, saw to it that the pavement was made at their own expense.
EDCS-20500072.summary: This inscription records that the aediles Publius and Marcus Salonius paid for the paving of a public space.

EDCS-20500073.translation: The council of decurions decreed that the base, consumed by age, be restored to Gaius Tampius Sabinus.
EDCS-20500073.summary: This decree by the decurions records the decision to restore a base dedicated to Gaius Tampius Sabinus, likely due to its age.

EDCS-20500077.translation: To Aulus Quinctilius Priscus, son of Aulus, of the Palatina tribe, who was IIvir with aedilic power, IIIIvir iure dicundo, IIIIvir quinquennalis, adlected by decree of the Senate, pontifex, praefectus fabrum, patron of the municipality. By public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20500077.summary: This is a dedication to Aulus Quinctilius Priscus, a prominent local official and patron, honoring his various public offices and status.

EDCS-20500078.translation: To Aulus Quinctilius Priscus, son of Aulus, of the Palatina tribe, who was IIvir with aedilic power, IIIIvir iure dicundo, IIIIvir quinquennalis, adlected by decree of the Senate, pontifex, praefectus fabrum. In his honor, because of his exceptional generosity which he conferred upon his fellow townsfolk, the Senate decreed that a statue be publicly erected in the forum where he wished. Having accepted the honor, he waived the expense. Here, by decree of the Senate, he repurchased the Ceponian, Roian, and Mamian estates and the Exoscan meadow from the state for 70,000 sesterces and returned them to the public treasury. From their income, he decreed that annually on the 6th day before the Ides of May, his birthday, 4,200 sesterces should perpetually be given: to the attending townsfolk and residents and married women, 1 pound of honey-cakes and a hemina of mulsum; and around the dining rooms, to the decurions, mulsum and a honey-cake and 10 sesterces for sportula. Likewise, to the boys of the curia for their growth and to the VIvir Augustales and to all those with whom it is permitted to dine together, a honey-cake, mulsum, and 8 sesterces. And in my dining room, an additional 1 sestertius per person. And for the adornment of the statue and my images, the state shall perpetually spend 30 sesterces. By the arbitration of the IIvir aedilium, it is favorably decreed that for the plebeian boys without distinction of freedom, a distribution of 30 modii of nuts and the serving of 6 urns of wine for drinks be fittingly provided for their growth.
EDCS-20500078.summary: This extensive inscription details the honors bestowed upon Aulus Quinctilius Priscus, a prominent citizen, including the erection of a statue and his generous endowments for public distributions of food and drink on his birthday, as well as provisions for the maintenance of his statue and images.

EDCS-20500089.translation: Dedicated on the Ides of July, in the consulship of Servianus III and Titus Vibius Varus.
EDCS-20500089.summary: This is a fragmented inscription recording a dedication made on a specific date during the consulship of Servianus and Titus Vibius Varus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-20500091.translation: Gnaeus Acerronius made for himself while alive, and for his [wife?], Acerronia, daughter of Gnaeus, and for Acerronia, daughter of Gnaeus, and for Hostiliena, freedwoman of Publius, and for Hostiliena, freedwoman of Publius.
EDCS-20500091.summary: This funerary monument was made by Gnaeus Acerronius for himself and his family members.

EDCS-20500092.translation: For Acilia Axi[---], who lived [---] years, Aurelius Her[---] made for his well-deserving wife, with whom he lived [---] year(s).
EDCS-20500092.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aurelius Her[---] for his wife, Acilia Axi[---], specifying her lifespan.

EDCS-20500093.translation: To Marcus Atreius Felix, freedman of Marcus, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-20500093.summary: This inscription records a dedication made from a will to Marcus Atreius Felix.

EDCS-20500094.translation: To Quintus Babullius Epaphroditus, freedman of Quintus.
EDCS-20500094.summary: This is a dedication to Quintus Babullius Epaphroditus.

EDCS-20500095.translation: [---] for G(aius?)'s freedwoman Fa[---]P, for herself and for Lasciva, freedwoman of [---], and for Marcus Var(gunteius) Secundus and for Gaius Calleius Aprilis.
EDCS-20500095.summary: This funerary inscription lists multiple individuals for whom a monument was intended, including freedwomen and Roman citizens.

EDCS-20500096.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aemilius Inbentus and Verria Flaccilla made this for their most sweet and well-deserving son, Claudius Caloceros, who lived 7 years, 9 months, and 26 days, and they made it for themselves above.
EDCS-20500096.summary: This is a funerary dedication by parents for their young son, Claudius Caloceros, also reserving space for themselves.

EDCS-20500097.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pompeius Tyticus made this for his well-deserving wife, Claudia Libertas.
EDCS-20500097.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Pompeius Tyticus for his wife, Claudia Libertas.

EDCS-20500100.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. His brothers made this for Felix, their most devoted brother, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-20500100.summary: This is a funerary dedication by brothers for their deceased brother, Felix, noting his age at death.

EDCS-20500103.translation: Hirtia Agatoclia, freedwoman of Aulus, made this monument for herself and for her husband, Gaius Eppius Publius, son of Titus.
EDCS-20500103.summary: This funerary monument was made by Hirtia Agatoclia for herself and her husband, Gaius Eppius Publius.

EDCS-20500105.translation: Lucideia Eleuteris made this for her most devoted brother, Gaius Manlius Chrysampilus.
EDCS-20500105.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Lucideia Eleuteris for her brother, Gaius Manlius Chrysampilus.
