EDCS-20500106.translation: Marcus Livius Clytus, freedman of Augustus, and Marcus Nonius Gallio, son of Lucius, erected this monument in accordance with the will of Oppia, freedwoman of Gnaeus Oppius, for Gnaeus Oppius and for Oppia Hedone, freedwoman of Gnaeus Oppius.
EDCS-20500106.summary: A funerary monument erected by two men, Marcus Livius Clytus and Marcus Nonius Gallio, according to the will of a woman named Oppia, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-20500108.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Cornelius Steptus erected this for his most devoted sister, Servilia Paezusa, and for Gaius Manneius Potitus.
EDCS-20500108.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Cornelius Steptus for his sister Servilia Paezusa and Gaius Manneius Potitus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20500109.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius T. Proculus erected this for his most holy and well-deserving wife Lucilla, who lived...
EDCS-20500109.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius T. Proculus for his wife Lucilla, likely from the Imperial period, mentioning her well-deserved qualities.

EDCS-20500110.translation: To Titia Chila by Erotis and Pusillio of the Quintorum Faleriorum.
EDCS-20500110.summary: A dedication to Titia Chila by Erotis and Pusillio, possibly related to a collegium or group named Quintorum Faleriorum, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20500111.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Virius Agathangelus and Viria Prisca erected this for their son Lucius Virius Amandus, who lived 18 years, 4 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-20500111.summary: A funerary monument erected by Lucius Virius Agathangelus and Viria Prisca for their son Lucius Virius Amandus, who died at the age of 18, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20500112.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Ulpia Athenais erected this for her incomparable husband, Decimus Motilius Soter.
EDCS-20500112.summary: A funerary dedication by Ulpia Athenais for her husband Decimus Motilius Soter, highlighting his incomparable qualities, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20600317.translation: Sacred to their loyalty. Aspania Polla, daughter of Quintus, and Quintus Petronius Irenaeus consecrated this.
EDCS-20600317.summary: A dedication to the abstract concept of 'Fides' (loyalty) by Aspania Polla and Quintus Petronius Irenaeus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20600319.translation: Gaius Luccius Fronto fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-20600319.summary: A simple dedication recording Gaius Luccius Fronto fulfilling a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20600323.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filius divi Commodi frater divi Antonini Pii nepos divi Hadriani pronepos divi Traiani Parthici abnepos divi Nervae adnepos Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XV imperator XII consul III pater patriae et Imperator Caesar Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filius divi Marci Antonini Pii pronepos divi Hadriani abnepos divi Traiani Parthici adnepos Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix pontifex tribunicia potestate X imperator II consul III designatus fortissimus ac super omnes felicissimus princeps viam quae ducit in Villam Magnam silice sua pecunia straverunt\n
EDCS-20600323.translation: Imperator Caesar, son of the divine Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the divine Commodus, grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 15th time, imperator for the 12th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, and Imperator Caesar, son of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, great-grandson of the divine Marcus Aurelius Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, pontifex, with tribunician power for the 10th time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul designate for the 3rd time, the strongest and most fortunate princeps above all, paved the road leading to the Villa Magna with their own money.
EDCS-20600323.summary: An imperial rescript by Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, dating to 212 AD, documenting the paving of a road to the Villa Magna using imperial funds.

EDCS-20600324.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Antonius Trofimus erected this for his excellent patron, Antonius Serenus, the evocatus, who deserved well.
EDCS-20600324.summary: A funerary dedication by Antonius Trofimus for his patron Antonius Serenus, who was an evocatus (a veteran recalled to service), likely from the late Imperial period.

EDCS-20600326.translation: Gaius Camullius, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, from Pisa, a soldier of the 4th Praetorian Cohort, served for 5 years and lived for 25 years.
EDCS-20600326.summary: This is a military tombstone for Gaius Camullius, a soldier who served in the Praetorian Guard.

EDCS-20600331.translation: Euhodus, for Marcus Aurelius Sabinianus, freedman of the Augusti, patron of the citizenry of Anagnina, and also of the college of caplatores, decurion of the lictores, popular denuntiator, and also of the decemviral geruli. The Senate and People of Anagnina, because of his love for his homeland and citizens, having restored the baths, neglected for a long time, with his own money, decreed that a statue be erected from his legacies. On account of its dedication, he gave 5 denarii to the decurions, 2 denarii to the sexviri, 1 denarius to the people, and a sufficient banquet for all.
EDCS-20600331.summary: This is a decree from the Senate and People of Anagnina honoring Marcus Aurelius Sabinianus, who restored public baths, with a statue and games.

EDCS-20600332.translation: For Marcia Aurelia Ceionia Demetrias, a draped woman, for the dedication of the baths, which after a long time they restored to their former appearance at their own expense, the Senate and People of Anagnina decreed that a statue be erected. On account of its dedication, she gave 5 denarii to the decurions, 2 denarii to the sexviri, and 1 denarius to each member of the people, and a sufficient banquet for all.
EDCS-20600332.summary: This is a decree from the Senate and People of Anagnina honoring Marcia Aurelia Demetrias for restoring public baths, with a statue and games.

EDCS-20600333.translation: For Tiberius Claudius Crescentianus, son of Tiberius, of the Publilia tribe, patron of the municipality, quinquennalis, praetor, quaestor, twice priest of the youth of Anagnina. The Senate and People of Anagnina decreed that a statue be erected for their most loving and outstanding patron, because he performed all the duties of his public offices with great zeal and generosity towards his fellow citizens and homeland. A place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20600333.summary: This is a decree from the Senate and People of Anagnina honoring Tiberius Claudius Crescentianus, a municipal patron and official, with a statue for his public service.

EDCS-20600334.translation: For Lucius Cominius Firmus, son of Lucius, grandson of Lucius, of the Palatina tribe, former praetor, quaestor of the treasury and alimentary funds. Oppia Eunoe, freedwoman of Sextus and a woman, is dead. Lucius Cominius Firmus. A model of chastity has perished, grieve for the girl. Oppia is no more, Oppia has been taken away from Firmus. Accept this soul and add to the sacred number. Arria Romano and you, Laodamia of Greek origin, fame preserves this monument for you, worthy of your merits, surviving your death, for yourself, your family, and their descendants.
EDCS-20600334.summary: This monument records the death of Oppia Eunoe and honors Lucius Cominius Firmus, a former magistrate, with verses praising their qualities.

EDCS-20600335.translation: Publius Egulleius Apollonius, Augustalis sevir. Munatia Nobilis, freedwoman of Gaius, beloved and cherished, lived 12 years. No one else will be buried here.
EDCS-20600335.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Egulleius Apollonius and his wife Munatia Nobilis, who died at a young age.

EDCS-20600336.translation: For Marcus Flavius Fimbria, former aedile, quaestor, prefect of the fabrica, augur, and pontifex.
EDCS-20600336.summary: This is a dedication honoring Marcus Flavius Fimbria for his various public and religious offices.

EDCS-20600338.translation: For Flavia Cara Gentia, flaminica. The Senate and People of Anagnina, from the legates of the people, decreed that a statue be erected in honor of Flavius Karus, her father.
EDCS-20600338.summary: This is a decree from the Senate and People of Anagnina honoring Flavia Cara Gentia, daughter of Flavius Karus, with a statue.

EDCS-20600339.translation: For Lucius Pantilius, son of Lucius, former praetor, quaestor, and pontifex. Lucius Pantilius, son of Lucius, father, twice praetor, twice quaestor, Salian priest, pontifex. For Cemellia, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-20600339.summary: This inscription records family members who held prestigious public and religious offices, including father and son Lucius Pantilius, and Cemellia.

EDCS-20600340.translation: For Marcus Rubrius Porculus, son of Lucius, former praetor, aedile, quaestor, pontifex, and Salian priest. The townspeople and inhabitants.
EDCS-20600340.summary: This dedication honors Marcus Rubrius Porculus, a magistrate and priest, by the townspeople and inhabitants of the municipality.

EDCS-20600341.translation: Marcus Titius, son of Gaius, and Marcus Rufelleius, son of Marcus, praetors.
EDCS-20600341.summary: This inscription names two individuals, Marcus Titius and Marcus Rufelleius, both holding the office of praetor.

EDCS-20600342.translation: To Publius Vegellius Primus, son of Publius, of the Publilia tribe, Roman equestrian, twice-in-command, quaestor of supplies, curator of the annonary fund, quaestor of the public treasury, curator of the affairs of the state of Trebula, from the examples of his munificence, a most deserving patron, the college of youths has erected this, for him who had deserved most excellently, on account of his having restored the games of the youths, which had been erased by the wear of time, and on account of this offering of honor, on his birthday, to the same college, on the 4th day before the Kalends of October.
EDCS-20600342.summary: This is a dedication by the college of youths to their patron Publius Vegellius Primus, honoring his restoration of the youth games and his general munificence, dated to September 28th.

EDCS-20600343.translation: Vibia Europa made this for Lucius Vibius Iustus, son of Lucius, of the Publilia tribe, praetor, quaestor, and for Lucius Vibius Severus, freedman of Lucius, who was a sevirus Augustalis.
EDCS-20600343.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Vibia Europa for two individuals, Lucius Vibius Iustus and Lucius Vibius Severus, with the former holding political offices and the latter a religious one.

EDCS-20600346.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aesopos and Daphne made this for [3]nerus, most dutiful, IIvir. He lived 35 years, 6 months.
EDCS-20600346.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated to a man named [3]nerus by Aesopos and Daphne, mentioning his age at death and his role as IIvir.

EDCS-20600347.translation: Publius Sexstilius Tertius Pistor, freedman of Publius, of the Pistor tribe, and Salvia Hilara, freedwoman of Laronia, a pet, lived 10 years. Antis, Laronia's slave, whether free or not. [6] [6] In the front 12 feet, in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-20600347.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Salvia Hilara, a pet, who lived 10 years, possibly commissioned by Antis, a slave of Laronia, and includes funerary plot measurements.

EDCS-20600348.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Titus Aelius Union, who lived 7 years and 39 days.
EDCS-20600348.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Titus Aelius Union, recording his lifespan of 7 years and 39 days.

EDCS-20600349.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aurelius Hermetianus, freedman of Augustus, made this for his well-deserving mother, Aurelia Ugia.
EDCS-20600349.summary: This is a tomb inscription dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Hermetianus to his mother, Aurelia Ugia, indicating a familial relationship and his status as an imperial freedman.

EDCS-20600352.translation: He lived forty-five years, from which 21 sons were born, and Camurius Nicharchus, and Rufina, his daughter, who lived fifty-four years. They made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-20600352.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of a man (45 years) and his daughter Rufina (54 years), noting 21 sons and a Camurius Nicharchus, and refers to the honorees as 'well-deserving'.

EDCS-20600353.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Clementi [3] VIR CAE[3]S, [3] an 18 year old brother.
EDCS-20600353.summary: This tomb inscription is dedicated to Clementi and mentions a brother who was 18 years old.

EDCS-20600354.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Diadumenus and Dionysia for their well-deserving patron, Aulus Flaminius Rusticus.
EDCS-20600354.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Diadumenus and Dionysia to their patron, Aulus Flaminius Rusticus.

EDCS-20600356.translation: To the departed spirits. Julia Serena, her dearest daughter, placed this monument for the deserving Iulia Serena, who lived four years.
EDCS-20600356.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Iulia Serena, set up by her father, Iulius Serenus.

EDCS-20600357.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Ulpius Terpon made this for his most virtuous wife, Maximilla, who deserved well.
EDCS-20600357.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maximilla, erected by her husband, Marcus Ulpius Terpon.

EDCS-20600359.translation: To the departed spirits. Calpurnia Ostoria Pia made this for her most devoted mother, Ostoria, daughter of Spurius, who deserved well.
EDCS-20600359.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ostoria, daughter of Spurius, dedicated by her devoted daughter, Calpurnia Ostoria Pia.

EDCS-20600360.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Philumeno bene merenti Euschemus fratri\n
EDCS-20600360.translation: To the departed spirits. Euschemus for his well-deserving brother, Philumenus.
EDCS-20600360.summary: A funerary epitaph for Philumenus, dedicated by his brother, Euschemus.

EDCS-20600362.translation: Quintus Baebius Victor made this for his wife Volteia Fortunata and for Aulus Apustius Constans, for himself and his descendants.
EDCS-20600362.summary: A funerary monument for Volteia Fortunata and Aulus Apustius Constans, established by Quintus Baebius Victor for his family.

EDCS-20600372.translation: The annually elected magistrates with judicial authority, Publius Hordeonius Gallus, son of Publius, and Marcus Caecilius Rufus, son of Marcus, and Gaius Claudius Priscus, son of Gaius, by decree of the Senate, saw to it that the temple be repaired, the statue be moved, and the base be set up, at public expense.
EDCS-20600372.summary: A public works inscription detailing the repair of a temple, relocation of a statue, and installation of a base, commissioned by four annually elected magistrates.

EDCS-20600373.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Marcus Iulius Martialis dedicated this offering.
EDCS-20600373.summary: A dedication of an offering to the god Silvanus by Marcus Iulius Martialis.

EDCS-20600375.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, son of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus Pius, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Father of the Fatherland, by the Senate and people of Signia.
EDCS-20600375.summary: An inscription dedicating something to the heir Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, son of Septimius Severus, by the people of Signia.

EDCS-20600376.summary: A funerary or dedicatory inscription for Furia Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta, likely incomplete.

EDCS-20600378.translation: Marcus Hordeonius Rufus, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, an augur and annually elected magistrate with judicial authority, gave a feast.
EDCS-20600378.summary: An inscription recording a public feast provided by Marcus Hordeonius Rufus, who held both religious (augur) and civic (IIIIvir i.d.) offices.

EDCS-20600379.translation: To Titus Iulius Eutychētus, patron and rector of the college of dendrophoroi of Signia, quinquennial official, the college of dendrophoroi erected a statue by means of dedicated funds, in recognition of his many frequent benefits and his generosity and gifts which he conferred upon their college; he also provided the dedication ceremony and a banquet for 32,000 sesterces.
EDCS-20600379.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by the college of dendrophoroi of Signia to their patron and rector, Titus Iulius Eutychētus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20600383.translation: Lucius Volumnius Theophilus, freedman of Lucius, sextumvir; Volumnia Arbuscula, freedwoman of Lucius; Lucius Volumnius Zabda, freedman of Lucius, sextumvir; for themselves and for their patron and freedmen.
EDCS-20600383.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Volumnius Theophilus, Volumnia Arbuscula, and Lucius Volumnius Zabda, who were freedmen and sextumviri, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20600391.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Caellia Nobilianetis, daughter of Quintus, my sweetest daughter, who lived 9 years and 4 months.
EDCS-20600391.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caellia Nobilianetis, a young girl who died at age 9, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20600393.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Clytus lived 8 years, 3 months, and 6 days. Marcus Iunius Clytus made this for his sweetest son.
EDCS-20600393.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Marcus Iunius Clytus for his son, Clytus, who died at age 8, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20600394.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Albia [---]ira made this for her most sacred and well-deserving husband, Quintus Coelius Olympos.
EDCS-20600394.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Albia for her husband, Quintus Coelius Olympos, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20600395.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Comicia Athenais made this for her husband, Publius Comicius Philophyrsus, and Publius Comicius Eusebes made this for his father, well-deserving.
EDCS-20600395.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Comicia Athenais for her husband, Publius Comicius Philophyrsus, and by Publius Comicius Eusebes for his father, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20600396.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Verulanus Severianus made this for his incomparable wife, Cosutia Marcellina, who lived 22 years, 3 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-20600396.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Verulanus Severianus for his wife, Cosutia Marcellina, who died at age 22, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20600397.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Crustidius Priscus made this for his wife, Quintinia Callistena, well-deserving, who lived with me for approximately 20 years without any complaint.
EDCS-20600397.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Titus Crustidius Priscus for his wife, Quintinia Callistena, who died after approximately 20 years of marriage, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20600398.translation: For Publius Decimius Boethus, his well-deserving husband.
EDCS-20600398.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Decimius Boethus, dedicated by his husband, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20600399.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Meteius Fortunatus made this for his most sweet mother, Meteia Hilara, well-deserving.
EDCS-20600399.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Quintus Meteius Fortunatus for his mother, Meteia Hilara, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20600400.translation: Quintus Murcius Philius, son of Quintus, freedman, for himself and for Quintus Murcius Hilarus, freedman of Quintus, his fellow freedman, and for Samiaria Aphrodisi, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-20600400.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Quintus Murcius Philius for himself and a fellow freedman, and a freedwoman, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-20600402.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iunia Iulia made this for her husband Gaius Silidius Heliodorus, who lived 55 years, and with whom she lived 30 years, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-20600402.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Silidius Heliodorus, set up by his wife Iunia Iulia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20600403.translation: Ulpia Petronia made this for her well-deserving husband Oppius Tinus, who lived with me 17 years, 4 months, and 5 days, and who lived 48 years, 6 months, and 10 days, without any complaint.
EDCS-20600403.summary: A funerary epitaph for Oppius Tinus, set up by his wife Ulpia Petronia, likely from the Roman Imperial period, mentioning their married life and his age at death.

EDCS-20600404.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Faustina, his daughter, made this for Volcacius Proclus, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-20600404.summary: A funerary epitaph for Volcacius Proclus, set up by his daughter Faustina, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20600405.translation: Volumnia Haline, freedwoman of Quintus, Pupiana: this monument will not follow its heir.
EDCS-20600405.summary: An epitaph indicating that the tomb of Volumnia Haline will not pass to her heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20600409.translation: Sacred to the Good Goddess. Gaius Valerius Martialis, son of Gaius.
EDCS-20600409.summary: A religious dedication to the Bona Dea by Gaius Valerius Martialis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20600411.translation: For [the Emperor Titus Aelius Hadrianus] Antoninus Augustus Pius, great priest, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 3rd time, [his freedman] the freedman [---] procures [---] Usiacae.
EDCS-20600411.summary: A fragment of an inscription honoring Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely set up by a freedman in a procuratorial role, dating to his reign (138-161 CE).

EDCS-20600417.translation: To Lucius Burbuleius Optatus Ligarianus, son of Lucius, of the voting tribe Quirina, consul, companion of the Augustales, legate of the Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius with proconsular power in Syria, in which office he died, legate of the same and of the divine Hadrian with proconsular power in Cappadocia, curator of public works and buildings, prefect of the Saturnalian treasury, proconsul of Sicily, logistes of Syria, legate of the 16th Flavian Firma legion, curator of public affairs of Narbonne, and also of Ancona, and also of Tarricina, curator of the roads Claudia, Cassia, and Cimina, praetor, plebeian aedile, quaestor of Pontus and Bithynia, military tribune with broad stripe of the 9th Spanish legion, decemvir for capital cases, patron of the colony. Rasinia Pietas, nurse of his daughters, placed this at her own expense. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20600417.summary: A lengthy epitaph honoring Lucius Burbuleius Optatus Ligarianus, detailing his extensive career in various administrative, military, and public service roles across the Roman Empire, and noting that his nurse Rasinia Pietas erected his tomb, dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-20600422.translation: The most distinguished town council resolved that a statue should be erected to Publius Baebius Iustus, son of Publius, of the voting tribe Teretina, who had held all public offices, because he always showed equal respect to individuals and to the community as a whole, and because, after his term as duovir, he generously undertook to present three pairs of gladiators with two bears when the games were requested by the people and the show was celebrated with great success, from his generosity, he presented pairs of gladiators with bears. For the dedication of the statue, the decurions gave him three denarii each. Here in Minturnae, for four days, he presented eleven pairs of gladiators, of whom he killed eleven leading gladiators from Campania and also ten savage bears, and the citizens themselves remember that he killed four of the best gladiators in single combat each day for all. Dedicated on the Kalends of August in the consulship of Aemilianus II and Aquilinus.
EDCS-20600422.summary: A decree of the town council of Minturnae honoring Publius Baebius Iustus for his public service and generous funding of gladiatorial games, including a detailed account of his games and their success, dating to 123 CE.

EDCS-20600423.translation: Plotia Iuniana, daughter of Publius, of the voting tribe Aufidia, to her most excellent husband Publius Baebius Iustus, duovir, aedile, quaestor, curator of the calendar of the public affairs of Teanum. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20600423.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Baebius Iustus, husband of Plotia Iuniana, listing his magistracies in Teanum, likely dating shortly after the inscription EDCS-20600422, possibly around 123 CE.

EDCS-20600427.translation: Lucius Gellius Poplicola, son of Lucius, and Gaius Caedicius, son of Gaius, as duovirs, having made weights and measures equal by decree of the Senate, took care to have them set up at their own expense.
EDCS-20600427.summary: This inscription records the work of two duovirs, Lucius Gellius Poplicola and Gaius Caedicius, who standardized weights and measures and paid for the installation of the relevant equipment, likely during the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-20600433.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Arruntius Hedys made this for his daughter Arruntia Proba.
EDCS-20600433.summary: A funerary inscription recording a father's dedication to his deceased daughter, Arruntia Proba, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20600434.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Bito filio dulcissimo et incomparabili qui vixit nnos XV menses VI dies VII horas III Grania Secunda mater fecit\n
EDCS-20600434.translation: To the departed spirits. Grania Secunda made this for her son Marcus Aurelius Bito, most sweet and incomparable, who lived 15 years, 6 months, 7 days, and 3 hours.
EDCS-20600434.summary: A funerary inscription from the Imperial period where a mother, Grania Secunda, dedicates a tomb to her son Marcus Aurelius Bito, detailing his lifespan.

EDCS-20600435.translation: To the departed spirits. His mother made this for her son Lucius Burbuleius Optatus, who lived 26 years and 8 months, as one who deserved well.
EDCS-20600435.summary: A funerary inscription from the Imperial period for Lucius Burbuleius Optatus, noting his age and that his mother dedicated the tomb.

EDCS-20600439.translation: Lucius Cornelius Antiocus, freedman of Merula; Veneria Plusia, freedwoman of Cornelia Lucius; Veneria Prima, freedwoman of Lucius Cornelius Cilix; Lucius Cornelius Calchas, freedman of Lucius... for freedmen and freedwomen to be buried.
EDCS-20600439.summary: This funerary inscription lists several freedmen and freedwomen, possibly members of a family or collegium, and seems to concern burial rights, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20600445.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Eutychidi vixit annos VII menses V diem I Quintus Annius Augustalis et Vettia Donata filiae dulcissimae fecerunt\n
EDCS-20600445.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Annius Augustalis and Vettia Donata made this for their most sweet daughter Eutychis, who lived 7 years, 5 months, and 1 day.
EDCS-20600445.summary: A funerary inscription from the Imperial period where parents, Quintus Annius Augustalis and Vettia Donata, dedicate a tomb to their deceased daughter Eutychis, detailing her age.

EDCS-20800001.translation: Of Marcus Trebius Niger: 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field, for Gaius Mamilius Primigenius, son of Spurius.
EDCS-20800001.summary: This inscription appears to record land dimensions and ownership, likely related to a boundary marker or property deed, possibly from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-20800002.translation: Viscaria, daughter of Gaius, lies here.
EDCS-20800002.summary: A simple funerary epitaph stating the name and parentage of the deceased, Viscaria, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800024.translation: Lucius Cornelius Alexsander, freedman of Lucius, master, quinquennial, interrex for Fontanus, willingly and deservedly erected an altar at his own expense by decree of the college.
EDCS-20800024.summary: This inscription records Lucius Cornelius Alexsander, a freedman and official of a college, dedicating an altar at his own expense, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800027.translation: Decimia Candida, daughter of Gaius, priestess of the Mother of the Gods Attis, gave this as a gift.
EDCS-20800027.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Decimia Candida, a priestess of the Mother of the Gods (Magna Mater), likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800028.translation: In the consulship of Emperor Gordian II Augustus and Pompeianus, Helvia Stephanis, priestess of the Idaean Mother Goddess, performed a taurobolium.
EDCS-20800028.summary: This is a dedication recording the performance of a taurobolium, a ritual sacrifice, by a priestess during the consulship of Gordian II.

EDCS-20800029.translation: Alexander, from a vision of his lady, made this freely.
EDCS-20800029.summary: This is a dedication by Alexander, who made it voluntarily after a vision, likely for a deity or patron.

EDCS-20800030.translation: To Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, in his eleventh year of tribunician power, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, by the people of Formia.
EDCS-20800030.summary: This is a public dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius, listing his imperial titles and dating him to his eleventh tribunician power and fourth consulship.

EDCS-20800032.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, son of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, brother of the deified Commodus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, Arabicus Adiabenicus, pontifex maximus, in his fifth year of tribunician power, imperator for the eighth time, consul for the second time, father of the country, by the colony of Aelia Hadriana Augusta Formiae.
EDCS-20800032.summary: This is a dedication by the colony of Formia to Emperor Septimius Severus, listing his extensive imperial titles and dating him to his fifth tribunician power and second consulship.

EDCS-20800034.translation: To the departed spirits. Nonia Crispinilla, his wife, for her husband Acastus, freedman of Augustus, procurator of the province of Mauretania Tingitana and of the district of Campania, a grieving wife for her grieving husband.
EDCS-20800034.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Acastus, an imperial freedman and procurator, set up by his grieving wife.

EDCS-20800043.translation: Lucius Stertinius Parthenopaeus, freedman of Lucius, to his incomparable friend Lucius Villius Atilianus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, former prefect of the engineers, former prefect of a cohort, military tribune, procurator of Augustus, patron of the colony, who undertook the care of the public gladiatorial games at the request of the council and the people, and having completed what he had promised, remitted the expense of the pair of horses which the people offered from the contribution for a feast, in the year in which the Optimus Imperator Hadrianus Augustus also undertook the honor of the duumvirate. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20800043.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Villius Atilianus, a colonial patron and official, who sponsored gladiatorial games and remitted expenses, with the inscription dating to Hadrian's duumvirate, set up by his freedman friend.

EDCS-20800045.translation: To Titus Flavius Fuscianus, son of Palatina, freedman of Tertiolus Augustus, recently the rationalis and a commentator for the province of Belgica. Flavia Irene, most unhappy mother, made this for her most dutiful son and for herself and her own. He lived 25 years, 7 months, 20 days. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-20800045.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Fuscianus, an imperial official in Belgica, set up by his mother, who also claims the monument for herself and her descendants.

EDCS-20800046.translation: To Laeona, the slave and dispensator, who lived 66 years and served with the utmost diligence in managing the guardianship of the praetor until he died. Amazonicus, freedman of the Augusti, procurator, with his brothers, set this up for their most dutiful father, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-20800046.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Laeona, a slave and dispensator, set up by his sons, Amazonicus (a procurator) and his brothers, honoring their father's diligent service.

EDCS-20800047.translation: To Lucius Varronius Capito, son of Lucius, of the Palatina tribe, scribe of the aedile, accensus velatus, quinquennial duovir, curator of the waters, patron of the colony. The council of the 'regalium', honored by whom he was content, placed this at his own expense. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20800047.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Varronius Capito, a colonial official and patron, set up by the council of the 'regalium' who benefited from his services, with the place granted by the decurions.

EDCS-20800048.translation: To the departed spirits. To Victor, soldier of Marcianus, a slave of the legion XXX Ulpia, deserving, who lived 15 years.
EDCS-20800048.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Victor, a slave serving as a soldier in the XXX Ulpia legion, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-20800049.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Valerius Maximus, son of Lucius, of the Aemilia tribe, a soldier of the 7th Praetorian Cohort. He served for 12 years, lived for 30 years. His fellow soldier Taurus paid for this monument.
EDCS-20800049.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Maximus, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, erected by a fellow soldier.

EDCS-20800050.translation: Lucius Naevius Clemens, son of Aulus, of the Scaptia tribe, from Faesulae, a soldier of the 9th Praetorian Cohort, in the century of Rullus Celeris. He lived for 33 years, served for 10 years.
EDCS-20800050.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Naevius Clemens, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20800051.translation: Gaius Furius Gallus, son of Gaius, of the Aemilia tribe, prefect of light infantry...Hispanian...Gaius Furius, son of Gaius, grandson of Marcus, of the Aemilia tribe...
EDCS-20800051.summary: This is a fragmented inscription mentioning Gaius Furius Gallus, who held a position as prefect of light infantry, possibly of Spanish origin.

EDCS-20800054.translation: For Lucius Arrius Salanus, quinquennial prefect under Tiberius Caesar, designated quinquennial prefect under Nero and Drusus Caesarum, trumpeter of the sacred rites of the Roman people, aedile, augur, interrex, military tribune of the 3rd Augustan Legion, prefect of cavalry, camp prefect, prefect of craftsmen. His wife Oppia erected this.
EDCS-20800054.summary: This is a dedication honoring Lucius Arrius Salanus, who held numerous important civic and military offices in Rome, possibly dating to the 1st century CE.

EDCS-20800055.translation: For Titus Acilius Florentinus, son of Publius, of the Aniensis tribe, who had the public horse, prefect of the 5th Cohort of Breuci. The men of Formiae of all ranks, because he bequeathed 100,000 sesterces in his will, from which a tensae for Minerva was to be made, of one hundred pounds of silver, with all its adornments.
EDCS-20800055.summary: This inscription records a bequest from Titus Acilius Florentinus to the town of Formiae for the construction of a tensae for Minerva, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20800056.translation: Caesia, daughter of Novius, priestess of Ceres. Quintus Caesius...son of Quintus...erected this.
EDCS-20800056.summary: This is a funerary monument for Caesia, a priestess of Ceres, erected by Quintus Caesius.

EDCS-20800057.translation: Marcus Coelius Philero, freedman of Marcus, an attendant for Titus Sextius, imperial official, in Africa, Carthage, aedile, prefect with judicial authority for quinquennial duties in charge of renting property in 83 castles, he built the temple of Tellus at his own expense; twice duovir of Clupia, once of Formis. He adorned the temple of Neptune with colorful stones at his own expense for his very obedient wife Fresidia Flora, freedwoman of Numerius, and for his dear friend Quintus Octavius Antimachus, freedman of a woman.
EDCS-20800057.summary: This is a dedication by Marcus Coelius Philero, who served in various administrative and judicial roles and funded public works, honoring his wife and a friend, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-20800058.translation: Lucius Cemoleius, son of Lucius, and Lucius Statius, son of Lucius, and Quintus Paccius, son of Marcus, as aediles, by decree of the Senate, gave the gates to be made and approved them.
EDCS-20800058.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Cemoleius, Lucius Statius, and Quintus Paccius, acting as aediles and following a senatorial decree, funded and approved the construction of the city gates.

EDCS-20800063.text_edition: \n Marcus Cocceius Marci filius Papiria Nerva consul II sua pecunia\n
EDCS-20800063.translation: Marcus Cocceius Nerva, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, consul for the second time, with his own money.
EDCS-20800063.summary: This inscription likely records a public work funded by Marcus Cocceius Nerva during his second consulship, possibly in the late 1st century CE.

EDCS-20800065.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Tiberius Claudius Thrasyllus, an Augustalis.
EDCS-20800065.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Thrasyllus, identified as an Augustalis, a prominent freedman holding a significant local religious office.

EDCS-20800068.translation: To Quintus Trebellius Martrinus, freedman of Quintus, an Augustalis, for his wife Trebellia Ampliata, and for his freedmen and freedwomen. 17 feet in front, 20 feet from the front.
EDCS-20800068.summary: A funerary dedication specifying dimensions of a plot of land for a tomb, set up by a freedman for his wife and other freed kin, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-20800076.translation: To the departed spirits. For Titus Flavius Servatus, son of Titus, of the Pomptina tribe, from Arretium.
EDCS-20800076.summary: A funerary dedication to the departed spirits for Titus Flavius Servatus, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-20800078.translation: Publius Marcius Charie[---], freedman of Publius, a garland-maker, is buried here.
EDCS-20800078.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Marcius Charie[---], a garland-maker, who is buried here, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800085.translation: To the departed spirits. Albanis Fatali lived 55 years.
EDCS-20800085.summary: A funerary dedication to the departed spirits for Albanis Fatali, recording her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800086.translation: For Quintus Alfius Asinius. 10 feet in front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-20800086.summary: An inscription perhaps related to land division or a boundary marker, mentioning Quintus Alfius Asinius and plot dimensions, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800087.translation: To the departed spirits. To Antonia, daughter of Gaius, of the Aemilia tribe, named LTRVTOS TELMOS FEROS, for his wife. To the departed spirits.
EDCS-20800087.summary: A complex funerary dedication possibly involving a wife with an unusual name and a reference to the departed spirits, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800088.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Apronius Her[---], freedman of Marcus. For Apronia Cypridi. Publius Calvisius Fortunatus, for his relatives, deserving of kindness, and for his freedmen and his descendants.
EDCS-20800088.summary: A funerary monument set up by Publius Calvisius Fortunatus for his relatives and freedmen, dedicated to the departed spirits, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800094.translation: To the departed spirits. Valeria Fortunata made this for her deserving husband, Lucius Caecilius Mpori.
EDCS-20800094.summary: A funerary dedication made by Valeria Fortunata for her deserving husband, Lucius Caecilius Mpori, to the departed spirits, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800097.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Augusti liberto Erasto et Claudiae Nereidi coniugi eius Tiberius Claudius Hyacinthus libertus fecit patrono suo et Tiberio Claudio sepulti liberto suo vixit annis VII sibi et suis libertis libertabus in fronte pedes CV in agro pedes LIIII huic monimento taberna cedet\n
EDCS-20800097.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Erastus, freedman of the emperor Augustus, and to Claudia Nereis, his wife. Tiberius Claudius Hyacinthus, his freedman, made this for his patron and for Tiberius Claudius, his freedman, who lived 7 years. For himself and his own freedmen and freedwomen. 105 feet in front, 54 feet in the field. A shop will yield to this monument.
EDCS-20800097.summary: A comprehensive funerary dedication set up by a freedman for his patron, his own freedman who died young, and for himself and his family, specifying land dimensions and the future status of a shop, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800099.translation: To the departed spirits. For Cominia Felicissima. Publius Publicius Atticus made this for one deserving of kindness.
EDCS-20800099.summary: A funerary dedication made by Publius Publicius Atticus for his deserving wife Cominia Felicissima, to the departed spirits, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800101.translation: Marcus Cornelius Hermaiscus, freedman of Marcus, and Philodamus, freedman of Marcus Paccius, [erected this monument with a frontage of 20 feet and a depth of 22 feet].
EDCS-20800101.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land, likely for funerary purposes, with the names of two individuals, possibly associated with its ownership or development.

EDCS-20800102.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Cnæus Cossutius Erotis.
EDCS-20800102.summary: This is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld for Cnæus Cossutius Erotis, likely a tomb inscription.

EDCS-20800103.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Crepereius Hermetis, his freedman.
EDCS-20800103.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld for Crepereius Hermetis, identified as a freedman.

EDCS-20800104.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Gaius Curtio Zoticus, who lived 15 years, 5 months, and 2 days; Gaius Curtius Matinus [erected this].
EDCS-20800104.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Gaius Curtio Zoticus and was likely erected by Gaius Curtius Matinus.

EDCS-20800106.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Edia Donusa. Tiberius Iulius Iulianus, son of Tiberius, [erected this] for his most dutiful mother.
EDCS-20800106.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld, with Tiberius Iulius Iulianus honoring his mother, Edia Donusa.

EDCS-20800107.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Flavia Zosimes. Quintus Planius Salutaris made this for his excellent wife.
EDCS-20800107.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld, with Quintus Planius Salutaris honoring his wife, Flavia Zosimes.

EDCS-20800108.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Fundania Primilla, freedwoman of Gaius. His daughter made this for her most pious mother.
EDCS-20800108.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld, with a daughter honoring her mother, Fundania Primilla.

EDCS-20800109.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Gaius Iulius Ofellius, and Aulus Octavius Ofellius, and Octavia Zosime, and Octavia Mosc[h]is made this monument for themselves and their relatives, freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants; it shall not follow the heir.
EDCS-20800109.summary: This inscription records the construction of a family tomb by Gaius Iulius Ofellius, Aulus Octavius Ofellius, Octavia Zosime, and Octavia Mosc[h]is, intended for themselves, their freed people, and descendants, with a stipulation that it is not to pass to heirs.

EDCS-20800110.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Gaius Laberius Myrismo. Acilia Felicis[s]ima made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-20800110.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the underworld, with Acilia Felicis[s]ima honoring her husband, Gaius Laberius Myrismo.

EDCS-20800113.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Quintus Lusius Eutyches, her sweetest husband, who lived with me for 44 years. Lucceia Edea, his wife, made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-20800113.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Quintus Lusius Eutyches after 44 years of marriage and was erected by his wife, Lucceia Edea.

EDCS-20800117.translation: To the departed spirits. Mettius Damas made this for his deserving wife, Mettia Successa.
EDCS-20800117.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Mettius Damas for his wife, Mettia Successa.

EDCS-20800120.translation: To the departed spirits of Quintus Minicius Hermaiscus and Minucia Eubulidia.
EDCS-20800120.summary: This is a funerary epitaph mentioning Quintus Minicius Hermaiscus and Minucia Eubulidia, likely a couple.

EDCS-20800122.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Vitruvius Soter made this for Paccia Trophime.
EDCS-20800122.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by Quintus Vitruvius Soter for Paccia Trophime.

EDCS-20800123.translation: To the departed spirits. Epaphroditus, son, made this for Paedaerotis, deserving, who lived 22 years and 31 days.
EDCS-20800123.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Paedaerotis, who lived 22 years and 31 days, set up by her son Epaphroditus.

EDCS-20800126.translation: For Petilia Hilaritas, dearest daughter, who lived 17 years. Her parents made this.
EDCS-20800126.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents for their daughter Petilia Hilaritas, who lived 17 years.

EDCS-20800127.translation: To the departed spirits. Novellus for Petronia Euhodia, daughter of Publius, his foster-child.
EDCS-20800127.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Novellus for his foster-child Petronia Euhodia.

EDCS-20800128.translation: To the departed spirits of Plaria Eupheme. Lucius Plarius Primus.
EDCS-20800128.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Plaria Eupheme, set up by Lucius Plarius Primus.

EDCS-20800131.translation: To the departed spirits. Resillus lived 18 years. Caius Ancharrenus Blastus set this up for his freedman.
EDCS-20800131.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Resillus, who lived 18 years, set up by Caius Ancharrenus Blastus for his freedman.

EDCS-20800134.translation: To the departed spirits. Eros, master and for himself and their descendants, set this up for his most devoted Stephanis, who lived 29 years, 3 months, and 11 days, and for Epictetus, Caesar's husband, and for Euschemone, their son.
EDCS-20800134.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Eros for his wife Stephanis, his stepson Epictetus, and his son Euschemone, as well as for himself and his descendants.

EDCS-20800135.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Antistius Parthenopaeus for his deserving brother, Caius Sulpicius Thelymorphus.
EDCS-20800135.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Publius Antistius Parthenopaeus for his deserving brother, Caius Sulpicius Thelymorphus.

EDCS-20800136.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sulpicia Fortunanta lived 18 years, 8 months. Sulpicia Festa made this for her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-20800136.summary: A tombstone for Sulpicia Fortunanta, erected by her mother Sulpicia Festa, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800138.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Terentius Lotus lived 58 years. Terentia Flora made this for her most devoted father, and also for his mother, Terentia Beryllis, who lived 28 years.
EDCS-20800138.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Terentius Lotus and his mother Terentia Beryllis, erected by his daughter Terentia Flora, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800140.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valeria Victoria, daughter of Sextus, who lived 18 years. Valerius Neptunalis and Harmonia made this for their most devoted daughter.
EDCS-20800140.summary: A tombstone for Valeria Victoria, erected by her parents Valerius Neptunalis and Harmonia, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800141.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Valerius Lucifer, son of Lucius, and Valeria Auctes, his deserving wife. Servius Sulpicius Speratus made this for them and for himself and his own.
EDCS-20800141.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Valerius Lucifer and his wife Valeria Auctes, erected by Servius Sulpicius Speratus, possibly a relative, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800154.translation: To Quintus Also/Asxni. 10 feet in front, 3 feet in the field.
EDCS-20800154.summary: A boundary marker indicating land ownership or division, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-20800172.translation: Lucius Aurelius Apollonius, the pantomime dancer, paid his vow to Mercurius Invictus.
EDCS-20800172.summary: An ex-voto inscription from a pantomime dancer to the god Mercurius Invictus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800181.translation: Quintus Allidius, son of Marcus, grandson of Numerius, from the Volsci tribe, served as aedile, military tribune, prefect of the engineers. Flavia, daughter of Marcus, wife of Allidius, mother of Volsci.
EDCS-20800181.summary: An epitaph listing the career of Quintus Allidius and identifying his wife Flavia, likely from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-20800183.translation: Gaius Licinius Aniensis Fuscus, from Forum Iulii, aged 26 years, with 5 years of service in the 10th urban cohort.
EDCS-20800183.summary: A military inscription for Gaius Licinius Aniensis Fuscus, detailing his service in the Roman army, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800186.translation: Quintus Gavius, son of Quintus, sailor; Marcus Caius, son of Caius; Gaius Braccius, son of Caius; as aediles, they arranged for this to be made by decree of the Senate, and they approved it.
EDCS-20800186.summary: A public works inscription recording the construction and approval of a project by three aediles, likely from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-20800188.translation: Quintus Gavius, son of Quintus, sailor; Marcus Caius, son of Caius; Gaius Braccius, son of Caius; as aediles, they arranged for this to be made by decree of the Senate.
EDCS-20800188.summary: A public works inscription recording the construction of a project by three aediles, likely from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-20800189.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelia Zosime made this for Titus Flavius Geminus, quinquennial augur, worthy, who lived with me for 25 years, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-20800189.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Titus Flavius Geminus, made by his wife Aurelia Zosime, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800196.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Natalis, son of Marcus, of the Aemilia tribe, to whom this statue was erected to demonstrate the goodwill of the town council and the people towards him, for presenting a public games munus, he dedicated it at his own expense.
EDCS-20800196.summary: This is a public dedication of a statue to Marcus Ulpius Natalis, likely an aedile or quaestor, who funded public games, probably in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20800199.translation: To Aemilia Felix, who lived 6 years, 4 months, and 12 days. Manlius Felix and Aemilia Fortunata, her household slaves, made this for their dearest.
EDCS-20800199.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Aemilia Felix, made by her household slaves, likely from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-20800200.translation: To the departed spirits. Seia Tyche made this for Alphius Salbius, with whom she lived for 27 years, one month, and three days, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-20800200.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Alphius Salbius, made by his partner Seia Tyche, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800201.translation: Caius Alfidius Rufio, son of Caius, 12 feet in front, 16 feet in width. Burial place.
EDCS-20800201.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot measuring 12x16 feet, belonging to Caius Alfidius Rufio, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800380.text_edition: \n Marcus Antonius Alexander vivos sic sibi fecit\n
EDCS-20800380.translation: Marcus Antonius Alexander made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-20800380.summary: This is a self-dedicatory funerary inscription for Marcus Antonius Alexander, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20800461.translation: Caius Atta, the retiarius [gladiator], [belonging to the] collegium [of gladiators], to the departed spirits.
EDCS-20800461.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Caius Atta, a retiarius gladiator, and likely his collegium, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20900243.translation: To the departed spirits. Zosime made this for Erucius Spatalus, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-20900243.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Erucius Spatalus, made by his wife Zosime, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-20900247.translation: To the departed spirits. Saturia Picena, mother, [made this] for Minucius Rufus.
EDCS-20900247.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Minucius Rufus, made by his mother Saturia Picena, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-20900252.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Pantuleius Graptiacus, son, and Claudia Magna, daughter of Titus, [made this] for Caius Pantuleius Justus, son of Caius.
EDCS-20900252.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Caius Pantuleius Justus, made by his son Caius Pantuleius Graptiacus and daughter Claudia Magna, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-20900254.translation: Marcus Pettius Diomedes, freedman of Marcus, and Pettia Tertia, freedwoman of Marcus, [dedicated this] on the front, 8 feet.
EDCS-20900254.summary: This is a boundary inscription indicating property lines for Marcus Pettius Diomedes and Pettia Tertia.

EDCS-20900258.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Proculus made this for his dear wife Rutilia Paceaea, who lived 51 years, as a well-deserved gift.
EDCS-20900258.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Julius Proculus for his deceased wife Rutilia Paceaea.

EDCS-20900259.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felicitas made this for her sweetest infant Satus, who lived 7 years, as a well-deserved gift.
EDCS-20900259.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Felicitas for her deceased infant son Satus.

EDCS-20900260.translation: Publius Sestullius Salvius, freedman of Publius, [dedicated this] for the land, 12 feet.
EDCS-20900260.summary: A boundary inscription marking land measurement, possibly related to Publius Sestullius Salvius.

EDCS-20900287.translation: Sacred to Diana. Her parents made [this].
EDCS-20900287.summary: A dedication to the goddess Diana made by the parents of Quintae.

EDCS-20900393.translation: Sacred to Fortuna. Titus Flavius Claudianus, son of Titus, tribune of the V Macedonica legion, giving thanks, erected an altar to the Genius of the family.
EDCS-20900393.summary: A dedication to Fortuna and the Genius of the family by Titus Flavius Claudianus, a military officer.

EDCS-20900394.translation: To our Lady Isis. Flavia Marcellina dedicated this statue of Memphitic Isis, with a silver collar weighing [X] pounds, as a gift. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-20900394.summary: A religious dedication of a statue to Isis by Flavia Marcellina, with the location approved by local officials.

EDCS-20900396.translation: For Rome and for Emperor Caesar, divine son of the deified Augustus. Aulus Aemilius, son of Aulus, took care to have this made at his own expense.
EDCS-20900396.summary: A public works inscription commissioned by Aulus Aemilius for the benefit of Rome and the Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-20900400.translation: For Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, [and for the deified] Augusta. Marcus Iunius Galus Proculus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, equestrian prefect of the deified Augustus's guild of fabric workers, made [this] at his own expense. Pompeia Trebulla, daughter of Quintus, by her will ordered [this] to be restored with 100,000 sesterces.
EDCS-20900400.summary: A complex inscription detailing a combined public work funded by an equestrian officer and a testamentary bequest for Tiberius Caesar and Livia.

EDCS-20900406.translation: [X]arinus, procurator of the treasury, saw to it that this was made during his lifetime with his own money.
EDCS-20900406.summary: An inscription indicating a private individual, a procurator, funding a construction or monument at his own expense.

EDCS-20900407.translation: To Marcus Favonius, son of Marcus, lieutenant. The people of Agrigentum (erected this).
EDCS-20900407.summary: A public dedication by the people of Agrigentum to Marcus Favonius, a lieutenant.

EDCS-20900409.translation: To Tiberius Julius Optatus Pontianus, freedman of Augustus, procurator and prefect of the fleet. Tiberius Julius Optatus, son of Tiberius, of the Fabia tribe, twice a city official, (dedicated this).
EDCS-20900409.summary: A dedication to Tiberius Julius Optatus Pontianus, a freedman of Augustus who held high administrative and military offices, by his son, also named Tiberius Julius Optatus, who was a twice city official.

EDCS-20900411.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Octavio Auli praefecto classis aedili praetori IIviro Caudina Cai filia avonculo sic\n
EDCS-20900411.translation: To Gnaeus Octavius, son of Aulus, prefect of the fleet, aedile, praetor, twice a city official of Caudium. Caius, daughter of Avunculus, (dedicated this).
EDCS-20900411.summary: A dedication to Gnaeus Octavius, who held several important civic and military positions, by Caius, daughter of Avunculus.

EDCS-20900412.translation: To Quintus Roscius Coelius Murena Silius Decianus Vibullus Pius Iulius Euryclus Herculanus Pompeius Falconius, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, consul, member of the XV viri sacris faciundis, proconsul of the province of Asia, governor with proconsular power of the province of Britannia under Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, governor with proconsular power of the province of Moesia Inferior under Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, curator of the Trajanic Road and governor with proconsular power of the province of Judaea, governor of the legion X Fretensis, governor with proconsular power of the province of Lycia and Pamphylia, commander of the legion V Macedonica, decorated with military honors in the Dacian War. (erected by) 3a.
EDCS-20900412.summary: An epitaph for Quintus Roscius Coelius Murena Silius Decianus Vibullus Pius Iulius Euryclus Herculanus Pompeius Falconius, a highly decorated military and political figure who served in various provinces under Trajan and Hadrian.

EDCS-20900413.translation: To Quintus Pompeius Senecio, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, Sosius Priscus, a distinguished youth, Salio Collinus. The people of Tarricina (erected this) by decree of the decurions, as patron.
EDCS-20900413.summary: A dedication by the people of Tarricina to Quintus Pompeius Senecio Sosius Priscus and others, honoring them as patrons.

EDCS-20900416.translation: To Quintus Atinius Murra, son of Marcus, of the Oufentina tribe, military tribune, prefect of the Fabri.
EDCS-20900416.summary: A dedication to Quintus Atinius Murra, who served as a military tribune and prefect of the Fabri.

EDCS-20900417.translation: Antonia Thais, freedwoman of Caius, made (this) for herself. Lucius Paconius, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe; Quintus Durvis, son of Quintus, of the Pupinia tribe, scribe.
EDCS-20900417.summary: A funerary monument set up by Antonia Thais for herself and including Lucius Paconius and Quintus Durvis.

EDCS-20900418.translation: Quintus Aufidius, son of Quintus, and Quintus Magulnius, son of Quintus, saw to the construction by decree of the Senate.
EDCS-20900418.summary: A construction project, authorized by a decree of the Senate, overseen by Quintus Aufidius and Quintus Magulnius.

EDCS-20900419.translation: Caelia Macrina, daughter of Caius, by her will ordered this to be made for 300,000 sesterces, for the adornment and upkeep of which she left 300,000 sesterces. In memory of her son Macrinus, she left 1,000,000 sesterces to the people of Tarricina, so that from the income of this money 100 boys and 100 girls would be given support, 5 denarii per month to each boy colonist, 4 denarii to each girl colonist per month, boys up to 16 years of age, girls up to 14 years of age, so that 100 boys and 100 girls always receive (this) through succession.
EDCS-20900419.summary: A funerary monument and endowment established by Caelia Macrina, who dedicated funds for her own memorial and for the support of 100 boys and 100 girls in Tarricina.

EDCS-20900420.translation: Father and son of Titus Memmius Rufus made (this).
EDCS-20900420.summary: A dedication made by the father and son of Titus Memmius Rufus.

EDCS-20900421.translation: Here lies Marcus Tatius Paetinus, son of Marcus, son of Marcus Tatius Rufus, duovir, and Vedia Tertulla, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-20900421.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Tatius Paetinus and his parents, who held significant civic roles in their community.

EDCS-20900423.translation: To the departed spirits. Freedmen and officials of Tarracina made this for Proculus, who was in public service.
EDCS-20900423.summary: This funerary inscription honors Proculus, a public servant of Tarracina, and was erected by his freedmen and officials.

EDCS-20900427.translation: Lucius Gellius Herma and Sextilia Gellia Turales, two messmates.
EDCS-20900427.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Gellius Herma and Sextilia Gellia Turales as a pair of 'contubernales,' likely fellow soldiers or housemates.

EDCS-20900429.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Castricius Fraterunus made this for his well-deserving wife, Iulia Longina, a freedwoman from Misenum, daughter of Gaius Iulius Firmus, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-20900429.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Quintus Castricius Fraterunus to his deceased wife, Iulia Longina, specifying her origin and age.

EDCS-20900430.translation: Gaius Postumius Pollio, son of Gaius, architect.
EDCS-20900430.summary: This is a brief epitaph identifying Gaius Postumius Pollio and his profession as an architect.

EDCS-20900431.translation: To the departed spirits. Salvius Polis made this for his well-deserving freedman Alexander, a lyre player, from his own resources, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-20900431.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Salvius Polis to his freedman Alexander, a musician, and also includes provisions for himself and his family.

EDCS-20900432.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying Marcus Sempronius Longinus and his likely origin from Leptis.

EDCS-20900434.translation: Aulus Aemilius Vitalis, freedman of Aulus, lived 2 years and 4 days. Aulus Aemilius Staphylus, freedman of Aulus. Aemilia Soteris, freedwoman of Aulus. For Aulus Aemilius Soteres and his family. In front 13 feet, in the field 16 feet.
EDCS-20900434.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life of Aulus Aemilius Vitalis and also designates plots of land for Aulus Aemilius Staphylus, Aemilia Soteris, and Aulus Aemilius Soteres and their families.

EDCS-20900435.text_edition: \n Eventius in diem mortis sine discordia\n
EDCS-20900435.translation: Eventius on the day of death without discord.
EDCS-20900435.summary: This inscription appears to be a testament or statement regarding Eventius's wishes or circumstances at the time of his death.

EDCS-20900436.translation: Annia Auge for herself and for Saturnina, her freedwoman.
EDCS-20900436.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Annia Auge for herself and her freedwoman Saturnina, likely on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-20900438.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Atabia Pia, a mother, made this for her deserving Lucius Atabius Philetus, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-20900438.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Atabius Philetus, erected by his mother, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900439.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Atavius Felix made this for his deserving wife Atavia Lupula and her freedwoman.
EDCS-20900439.summary: This is a tombstone for Atavia Lupula, erected by her husband Lucius Atavius Felix, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-20900441.translation: Gaius Aufidius Stablio(?), freedman of Gaius Aufidius.
EDCS-20900441.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of Gaius Aufidius Stablio(?), likely a tombstone or funerary marker, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21000001.translation: To Pudicitia Metrodora, daughter of Quintus. Her husband and parents made this for their deserving daughter.
EDCS-21000001.summary: This is a tombstone for Pudicitia Metrodora, erected by her husband and parents, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21000002.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quinta conserva, together with her children, [made this] for the deserving Carpophorus.
EDCS-21000002.summary: This is a tombstone for Carpophorus, erected by his partner Quinta and their children, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21000003.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Cessitius Leucipop, freedman, deserving of good treatment. Cessitia Appiana arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-21000003.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Cessitius Leucipop, a freedman, for whom Cessitia Appiana arranged its construction, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21000004.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a personal name, possibly a tombstone, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21000005.translation: Made during his lifetime. For Marcus Cocceius Apollonius, freedman of Augustus, and for his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-21000005.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Marcus Cocceius Apollonius for himself and his household, dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-21000006.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cornelia Olimpias.
EDCS-21000006.summary: This is a tombstone for Cornelia Olimpias, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21000009.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Epaphras [---].
EDCS-21000009.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone inscription for Epaphras, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-21000011.translation: To Anterus, freedman of Lucius Marcius and Lucius.
EDCS-21000011.summary: An inscription recording a freedman named Anterus, who was the freedman of two masters, Lucius Marcius and Lucius.

EDCS-21000015.translation: To the divine shades. Herennius Euporus made this for his well-deserving wife, Herennia Vera.
EDCS-21000015.summary: A funerary dedication by Herennius Euporus to his wife Herennia Vera, dated by the presence of 'Dis Manibus'.

EDCS-21000016.translation: To the divine shades. Marcus Ianiculus Cosnellus lived for 23 years, 7 months, and 21 days. He made this for himself, his family, and his descendants while alive.
EDCS-21000016.summary: A self-funded funerary monument for Marcus Ianiculus Cosnellus and his family, with precise age details.

EDCS-21000017.translation: A monument, ossuary, and place for burning bodies for the Caius Iulius Faustus family and their household.
EDCS-21000017.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument, ossuary, and ustrinum for the family of Caius Iulius Faustus.

EDCS-21000018.summary: A fragment of a dedication to a woman named Iulia [---]ucia.

EDCS-21000019.translation: To the divine shades. Iulia Aphrodisia rests here. She lived 39 years, 4 months, and 8 days. Publius Verrius Echio made this for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-21000019.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Aphrodisia, erected by her husband Publius Verrius Echio, including her lifespan.

EDCS-21000020.translation: To the divine shades. Dionysius, freedman of the emperor, made this for his incomparable wife, Iuventia Prisca.
EDCS-21000020.summary: A funerary dedication by Dionysius, an imperial freedman, to his wife Iuventia Prisca.

EDCS-21000021.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Caius Lucretius Cerialis made this for his well-deserving freedman, Caius Lucretius Heliodorus, [---] father.
EDCS-21000021.summary: A funerary dedication by Caius Lucretius Cerialis to his freedman Caius Lucretius Heliodorus, also mentioning a father.

EDCS-21000022.translation: To the divine shades. Caius Manlius Valens, son of Caius, of the Falernian tribe, lived for 1 year, 3 months. Manlia Paphies, daughter of Caius, lived for 6 years, 1 month, and 20 days.
EDCS-21000022.summary: A funerary inscription for two individuals, Caius Manlius Valens and Manlia Paphies, with their ages and tribal affiliation for the former.

EDCS-21000023.translation: Of Publius Manilius Laetus. In front 15 feet, in depth 18 feet.
EDCS-21000023.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land belonging to Publius Manilius Laetus, likely for a tomb.

EDCS-21000024.translation: To the departed spirits. For Manlia [3]/riae and Gnaeus [3] Deutero N[3]. Gaius Manlius Bani[3] B[" said to be a funerary inscription, possibly for members of the Manlii family.
EDCS-21000024.summary: This text appears to be the beginning of a funerary inscription dedicated to Manlia [3]/riae and Gnaeus [3] Deutero N[3] by Gaius Manlius Bani[3] B[".

EDCS-21000025.translation: To Marcia Polla, daughter of Marcus. Lucius Salvius Vestalis, son of Lucius, [erected this] for his mother.
EDCS-21000025.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Lucius Salvius Vestalis to his mother, Marcia Polla.

EDCS-21000026.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcilna Clementilla. Her father, Clemens, [erected this].
EDCS-21000026.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Clemens to his daughter, Marcilna Clementilla.

EDCS-21000027.translation: Marcus Mevius Bromius, freedman of Marcus, and Mevia Salvia, freedwoman of Bromius, for themselves, their freedmen and freedwomen, and for Marcus Nicephor, freedman of a woman [named] Munatia.
EDCS-21000027.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Mevius Bromius and Mevia Salvia, and also for Marcus Nicephor, a freedman of Munatia.

EDCS-21000028.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Mumius Fadius, by his will, erected [this] for himself, his family, his freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-21000028.summary: This is a funerary inscription established by Lucius Mumius Fadius according to his will, for himself and his descendants.

EDCS-21000029.translation: To Naevia Melpomene, freedwoman of Publius.
EDCS-21000029.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Naevia Melpomene, a freedwoman.

EDCS-21000030.translation: To Nasernia Tertulla, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-21000030.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Nasernia Tertulla.

EDCS-21000031.translation: To the departed spirits. For Obinia Modesta, who lived 60 years. Her sons erected [this] for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-21000031.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Obinia Modesta, who lived 60 years, erected by her deserving sons.

EDCS-21000032.translation: To Octavia Vegetina. Publius Aemilius Primianus and Aemilia Olympias, her children, [erected this] for their most remarkable mother.
EDCS-21000032.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by the children, Publius Aemilius Primianus and Aemilia Olympias, for their mother, Octavia Vegetina.

EDCS-21000033.translation: Marcus Plotius Salvius, freedman of Marcus, alive. Marcus Plotius Anteros, freedman of Marcus, alive. Plotia Dunamis, freedwoman of Marcus. Marcus Plotius Antiochus, freedman of Marcus. Veturia Hilara, freedwoman of Gaius. Marcus Epaphra, freedman of Marcus. Plotia Caerusa, freedwoman of Marcus. Marcus Silvanus, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-21000033.summary: This is a funerary monument established by multiple individuals, including Marcus Plotius Salvius and his family, for themselves and others, likely a columella or similar marker for a burial plot.

EDCS-21000034.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pomponia Ianuaria made this monument for herself, her husband Trophimus, and their descendants; it shall not follow an heir.
EDCS-21000034.summary: A funerary dedication by Pomponia Ianuaria for herself, her husband, and their descendants, indicating that the monument is not inheritable.

EDCS-21000035.translation: While alive, Quintus Refrius, son of Quintus, of the Oufentina tribe. While alive, Quintus Refrius Eros, freedman of Quintus. Egnatia Aucta, freedwoman of a woman. (The text seems to list individuals and their status, possibly for a tomb or as witnesses).
EDCS-21000035.summary: This inscription records several individuals, including a father and son, and their freedmen, likely related to a funerary context or property ownership, dated from the Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-21000036.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Sabina, dearest wife, and Sabina, dearest daughter, aged 17 years, 1 month, 12 days. Manlius [---] and sons, for themselves and their descendants.
EDCS-21000036.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to a wife and daughter, providing ages and names of the dedicator and his sons, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21000037.translation: Lucius Sarronius Bassus, of the Oufentina tribe.
EDCS-21000037.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that names Lucius Sarronius Bassus, a Roman citizen from the Oufentina tribe, likely part of a larger dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-21000038.translation: To Lucius Scutius Philippus, freedman of Lucius. To Scutia Stagonis, freedwoman of Lucius. Lucius Scutius, son of Publius, made [this] while alive.
EDCS-21000038.summary: A dedication by Lucius Scutius, son of Publius, for his freedman Lucius Scutius Philippus and freedwoman Scutia Stagonis, indicating it was made during their lifetimes.

EDCS-21000039.translation: Lucius Scutius Salvius Caccabus, freedman of Lucius. Scutia Prima, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-21000039.summary: This inscription lists two individuals, Lucius Scutius Salvius Caccabus and Scutia Prima, both freedmen of a Lucius, likely forming part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-21000041.translation: Marcus Sestius Sacco, son of Marcus. His wife Atinia Tertulla, daughter of Marcus. For their son Marcus Sestius Sacco. For their son Gaius Sestius Sacco.
EDCS-21000041.summary: A family monument set up by Marcus Sestius Sacco and his wife Atinia Tertulla, commemorating their two sons, Marcus and Gaius Sestius Sacco.

EDCS-21000042.translation: [---], freedman of Marcus Socculus. [---]pelia. [---] Prima. [---], freedman of Marcus Tertullus. [---], freedwoman Secunda.
EDCS-21000042.summary: A fragmentary inscription listing several individuals, likely freedmen and freedwomen, possibly part of a family tomb or record of freed slaves.

EDCS-21000043.translation: Gaius Soranus, son of Publius, of the Oufentina tribe.
EDCS-21000043.summary: A simple inscription identifying Gaius Soranus, son of Publius, and his tribal affiliation (Oufentina), likely part of a larger public or private record.

EDCS-21000044.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sofinia [---] made [this] mother [---] most unfortunate [---] lived [
EDCS-21000044.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to Sofinia, possibly commemorating her mother, and indicating her unfortunate life or death.

EDCS-21000045.translation: Gaius Tarquinius, son of Lucius; Appia (?), daughter of Gaius; Tarquinius Priscus, son of Quintus; Galla, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-21000045.summary: This inscription lists four individuals, likely members of the same family, identified by their praenomen, nomen, filiation, and tribe (Oufentina for Tarquinius Priscus), possibly for a funerary monument or record from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-21000046.translation: Lucius Terentius Rufus, son of Spurius; Lucius Picidius Rufus, son of Spurius, son of Lucius Picidius, freedman of Lucius Dama; Lucius Picidius Zabda, freedman of Lucius, brothers.
EDCS-21000046.summary: This funerary inscription records two brothers, Lucius Picidius Dama and Lucius Picidius Zabda, who were freedmen, and their father Lucius Picidius Rufus, along with another man named Lucius Terentius Rufus, possibly related or a benefactor.

EDCS-21000047.translation: To the departed spirits. Terentia Actitena, who lived 28 years. Antonius Privatus...
EDCS-21000047.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Terentia Actitena, who lived 28 years, set up by Antonius Privatus, likely her husband or relative, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100685.summary: This inscription names Marcus Tullius, son of Marcus, identified by his father's name and tribal affiliation (Oufentina), likely part of a larger monument or record from the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-21100686.translation: To the departed spirits. For Valeria Euplia, for her most dutiful daughter Valeria Helpis, who lived 23 years, 6 months, and 15 days, and for herself and her family.
EDCS-21100686.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Helpis, who died at the age of 23 years, 6 months, and 15 days, set up by her mother Valeria Euplia, for themselves and their family.

EDCS-21100687.translation: To the departed spirits. Valeria Sperata made (this) for her excellent mother Valeria Hygeia and her dutiful sons.
EDCS-21100687.summary: This funerary inscription records that Valeria Sperata dedicated this monument to her mother Valeria Hygeia and her dutiful sons, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-21100688.translation: Gaius Veratius, son of Gaius; Marcus Porcius, son of Gaius.
EDCS-21100688.summary: This inscription lists two individuals, Gaius Veratius and Marcus Porcius, identified by their praenomen, nomen, filiation, and tribal affiliation (Oufentina), possibly for a public record or funerary context.

EDCS-21100689.summary: This inscription simply names Lucius Vibius, likely part of a larger inscription or record from the Roman period.
