EDCS-22901995.translation: Pottery works of Oppius Iustus; dedicated to Paetinus and Apronianus, consuls.
EDCS-22901995.summary: This is a pottery stamp indicating the maker Oppius Iustus and dating to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-22901997.translation: To Primigenius; dedicated by Apronianus and Paetinus, consuls.
EDCS-22901997.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Primigenius, dating to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-22902000.translation: To Publius Decimius Epitynchanus; dedicated by Apronianus and Paetinus, consuls.
EDCS-22902000.summary: This is a dedication to Publius Decimius Epitynchanus, dating to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-22902012.translation: From the estates of Statilius Severus, Titus Camidienus Narcissus; dedicated to Paetinus and Apronianus, consuls.
EDCS-22902012.summary: This pottery stamp indicates ownership from the estates of Statilius Severus and Titus Camidienus Narcissus, dating to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-23000612.text_edition: \n Quintum Postumium Marcum Cerrinium aediles oro vos faciatis Euxinus rogat nec sine Iusto scripsit Hinnulus\n
EDCS-23000612.translation: Euxinus asks that you make Quintus Postumius Marcus Cerrinius aedile; Hinnulus wrote this, not without Iustus.
EDCS-23000612.summary: This is a political appeal or request for support for Quintus Postumius Marcus Cerrinius to become an aedile, written by Hinnulus.

EDCS-23000644.translation: Fundilius Eugamus asks that you make Marcus Lucretius Fronto the duovir, together with his Florus.
EDCS-23000644.summary: This is a request to support Marcus Lucretius Fronto for the position of duovir, made by Fundilius Eugamus.

EDCS-23000651.translation: Ganius asks that you make Gaius Gavius Rufus an aedile.
EDCS-23000651.summary: This is a request for support for Gaius Gavius Rufus to become an aedile, made by Ganius.

EDCS-23000696.translation: Porcellus asks that you make Marcus Lucretius Fronto the duovir with judicial authority.
EDCS-23000696.summary: This is a request for support for Marcus Lucretius Fronto to hold the office of duovir iure dicundo, made by Porcellus.

EDCS-23000716.translation: Holconius Priscus, I ask you to make him aedile.
EDCS-23000716.summary: This is a political appeal or electioneering text, likely from a wall, urging the electorate to support the candidacy of Holconius Priscus for the position of aedile.

EDCS-23000775.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for a young boy named Successus.

EDCS-23000967.summary: This is a personal note, likely from a wax tablet, where Euschenus sends greetings.

EDCS-23001099.translation: We stayed until the sixth day before the Kalends of September at Pompeii.
EDCS-23001099.summary: This inscription records a stay in Pompeii, dated to August 27th, likely by a traveler.

EDCS-23001108.summary: This is likely a funerary epitaph or an ownership mark identifying Gaius Concessus.

EDCS-23001129.translation: Lucius Habonius wounds and insults Caesum Felice(m).
EDCS-23001129.summary: This inscription is a legal accusation or report, detailing an assault and insult by Lucius Habonius against Caesum Felice(m).

EDCS-23001700.summary: This is likely a property marker or funerary inscription identifying ownership by Sextus Murrius Pisanus.

EDCS-23101185.summary: This appears to be a personal note or receipt, with a farewell and a reference to a number, possibly nine items or units, associated with a person named Isidoru.

EDCS-23101210.summary: This inscription seems to be a count or a reference to a group of four, possibly associated with Publius Quintilius, the name being uncertain.

EDCS-23101214.summary: This inscription consists of only names or initials, Quintus and possibly two other initials, and its exact purpose is unclear without further context.

EDCS-23101221.summary: A funerary inscription for Spes Oceanus, possibly a freedman, with a very brief dedication.

EDCS-23101225.translation: Hyacinthus was here; Verginiam, his own.
EDCS-23101225.summary: A funerary epitaph for Verginiam, set up by Hyacinthus, her partner or owner, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23101255.summary: A very brief inscription identifying Quintus Laelius, possibly a name on a tomb or a property marker.

EDCS-23101317.translation: Secundus Crusali, a slave, and Valentina, accepted [this].
EDCS-23101317.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording acceptance by Secundus Crusali, a slave, and Valentina, possibly related to a legal or financial transaction.

EDCS-23101318.summary: A fragment likely referring to inheritance or mixed goods, possibly from a will or inventory.

EDCS-23101334.text_edition: \n Apollinaris medicus Titi Imperatoris hic cacavit bene\n
EDCS-23101334.translation: Apollinaris, physician to Emperor Titus, crapped well here.
EDCS-23101334.summary: A humorous and vulgar inscription from the Roman imperial period recording the defecation of Apollinaris, physician to Emperor Titus.

EDCS-23101390.translation: On the Nones of July, Mus[---] M[---], born on the 8th [day?], on the day before the Nones of Hercules, from Nola, Pompeian, [---] III.
EDCS-23101390.summary: A fragmentary inscription detailing a birth date and possibly a location or affiliation, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23101397.translation: Anseres (geese), 61 in number; ducklings, 126 in number.
EDCS-23101397.summary: An inventory inscription recording the number of geese and ducklings, likely related to agricultural or commercial records.

EDCS-23101432.translation: Epius, the sixth day before the Ides.
EDCS-23101432.summary: A very brief inscription that appears to be a name followed by a date, possibly related to a dedication or funerary context.

EDCS-23101433.translation: Fortunatus loves Amplianda; Ianuarius loves Veneria. We beg, lady Venus, that you keep us in mind, since we Fodo are asking you.
EDCS-23101433.summary: A votive inscription addressed to Venus, expressing love and a plea for remembrance, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23103179.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified emperor Augustus.

EDCS-23200499.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Diana Tifatina.

EDCS-23200977.summary: This inscription records the name of Publius Opetreius Pullus, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23200978.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Ampius Hylas, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23200980.summary: This inscription records the name of Publius Antistius Maximus, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23200983.summary: This inscription records the name of Tiberius Babinius Alexander, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23200988.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Calpurnius Diogenes (?), likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23200989.translation: Of Decimus Caprasius Primus (?), son of Midas
EDCS-23200989.summary: This inscription records the name of Decimus Caprasius Primus (?), son of Midas, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23200990.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Cassius Bassus, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23200991.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Catius Stichus, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23200994.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming the deceased Caius Cornelius Clu[---].

EDCS-23200995.translation: Of Lucius Cornelius Diadumenus, freedman of Communis?
EDCS-23200995.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming the deceased Lucius Cornelius Diadumenus, freedman of Communis or a similar name.

EDCS-23200996.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming the deceased Aulus Cossinius Libanus.

EDCS-23200997.translation: To the spirits of the dead, of Titus, son of Marcus, of the Menenia tribe, Panthera
EDCS-23200997.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming the deceased Titus Panthera, son of Marcus, belonging to the Menenia tribe.

EDCS-23200998.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming the deceased Quintus Dellius Amicus.

EDCS-23201001.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming the deceased Marcus Fabius Lalius.

EDCS-23201005.translation: Of Italicus, freedman of Eppi Rufus
EDCS-23201005.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming the deceased Italicus, freedman of Eppi Rufus.

EDCS-23201007.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming the deceased Caius Julius Nic[---].

EDCS-23201008.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming the deceased Caius Julius Suavis.

EDCS-23201012.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription naming the deceased Lucius Livius Firmus.

EDCS-23201014.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcius Memmius Auctus, likely indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-23201017.translation: Of Musaeus, son of Junius Blaesus.
EDCS-23201017.summary: This inscription records the name of Musaeus, son of Junius Blaesus, possibly as a dedication or ownership marker.

EDCS-23201018.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Ninnius Optatus, likely indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-23201020.summary: This inscription records the name of Aulus Octavius Priscus (?), possibly indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-23201021.summary: This inscription records the name of Quintus Octavius Romulus, likely indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-23201022.translation: Of Onomastus, slave of Marcius Asellinus.
EDCS-23201022.summary: This inscription records the name of Onomastus, a slave of Marcius Asellinus, likely indicating ownership.

EDCS-23201023.translation: Of Publius Paccius Clar(us). Of Publius Paccius Clar(us).
EDCS-23201023.summary: This inscription records the name of Publius Paccius Clar(us), possibly indicating ownership or a dedication, with a repetition.

EDCS-23201026.summary: This inscription records the name of Pompeius Axiocus, likely indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-23201027.summary: This inscription records the name of Titus Pontius Successus, likely indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-23201028.summary: This inscription records the name of Numerius Popidius Priscus, likely indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-23201030.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Proculeius Pylades, likely a freedman or slave.

EDCS-23201032.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Rapinas Optatus, likely a freedman or slave.

EDCS-23201035.summary: This inscription records the name of Quintus Sallustius Inventus, likely a freedman or slave.

EDCS-23201036.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Siculus, son of Gaius, likely a Roman citizen.

EDCS-23201040.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Statilius Phileros, likely a freedman or slave.

EDCS-23201046.summary: This inscription records the name of Publius Varenus Zetus, likely a freedman or slave.

EDCS-23201050.translation: Decimus Volcius Modestus, son of Decimus
EDCS-23201050.summary: This inscription records the name of Decimus Volcius Modestus, son of Decimus, likely a Roman citizen.

EDCS-23201056.summary: This inscription records the name of Quintus Caecilius Tertius, likely a freedman or slave.

EDCS-23201058.summary: This inscription records the name of Tiberius Claudius Euenus, likely a freedman or slave.

EDCS-23201061.summary: This inscription records the name of Tiberius Crassus Firmus, likely a freedman or slave.

EDCS-23201062.translation: Of Gnaeus Domitius, his freedman Suavis.
EDCS-23201062.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedman named Suavis, belonging to Gnaeus Domitius.

EDCS-23201063.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Publius Flavius Largus, possibly on a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-23201064.summary: This inscription names Quintus Maecius Theudas, likely appearing on a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-23201065.summary: This inscription records the name of Caius Messenius Eunomus, possibly on a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-23201066.summary: This inscription bears the name of Marcus Nonius Magnus, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-23201069.summary: This inscription identifies Caius Sallustius Sabinus, likely on a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-23201070.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Aemilius Diogenes, possibly on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23201071.summary: This inscription names Caius Vibius Nymphicus, likely on a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-23201074.summary: This inscription commemorates Lucius Cornelius Adiutor, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23201077.summary: This inscription identifies Caius Ovius Secundus, probably on a funerary monument.

EDCS-23201079.translation: To Publius Acutius Aster, the actor.
EDCS-23201079.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Publius Acutius Aster, an actor.

EDCS-23201084.translation: To Aemilia Rufilla, a most distinguished woman.
EDCS-23201084.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aemilia Rufilla, described as a 'clarissima femina'.

EDCS-23201094.translation: To Alphius Thrasea Priscus, the actor.
EDCS-23201094.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Alphius Thrasea Priscus, who was an actor.

EDCS-23201098.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Amullius Dionysus.

EDCS-23201106.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Antonius Restitutus.

EDCS-23201121.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Aurelius Epaphroditus.

EDCS-23201146.translation: To Aulus Caucius Priscus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-23201146.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aulus Caucius Priscus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-23201148.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Publius Ceius Melicertes.

EDCS-23201151.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Claudius Agrippa.

EDCS-23201181.translation: To Eutyches, steward, slave of the most excellent man Virius Sulpicius Priscus.
EDCS-23201181.summary: This inscription records that Eutyches, a steward, was a slave of Virius Sulpicius Priscus.

EDCS-23201193.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Publius Gabinius Successus.

EDCS-23201292.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Numerius Papius Rufus.

EDCS-23201305.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Quintus Plautius Melanthos.

EDCS-23201346.translation: Belonging to Caius Stlaccius Hermes. (Property) of Stlaccius.
EDCS-23201346.summary: This inscription likely marks a property or tomb belonging to Caius Stlaccius Hermes and Stlaccius.

EDCS-23201350.translation: Palm and laurel, of Symmachus, a man of consular rank.
EDCS-23201350.summary: This inscription likely commemorates Symmachus, a man of consular rank, possibly with symbols of victory or honor.

EDCS-23201493.translation: To the divine shades. Julius Valens, soldier of the II Parthica legion, a stator, and his wife Aelia Severa, and their son Julius Valentinus, a Roman equestrian, made this while alive for themselves, their daughters Julia Valentina and Aelia Valeria, and their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-23201493.summary: This is a funerary monument established by Julius Valens, a soldier and stator of the II Parthica legion, for himself, his wife Aelia Severa, their children, and freedpersons.

EDCS-23300003.translation: Under Emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus, for the fourth time, and Lucius Vitellius, for the third time, consuls, weights having been checked at Articuleiana, by the care of the aediles, 100 pounds of Ligurian [product?].
EDCS-23300003.summary: This inscription records the official checking of weights (100 pounds) at Articuleiana during the consulship of Claudius and Vitellius, under the supervision of the aediles.

EDCS-23300004.translation: Under Tiberius Claudius Caesar, for the fourth time, and Lucius Vitellius, for the third time, consuls, checked at Articuleianum by the care of the aediles.
EDCS-23300004.summary: This inscription records the official checking of goods at Articuleianum during the consulship of Claudius and Vitellius, under the supervision of the aediles.

EDCS-23300005.translation: Under Emperor Vespasian Augustus, for the eighth time, and Titus Emperor, son of Augustus, for the sixth time, consul, checked in the Capitol.
EDCS-23300005.summary: This inscription records the official checking of goods in the Capitol during the consulships of Vespasian and Titus.

EDCS-23300043.summary: This is likely a personal inscription or ownership mark, identifying Zosimus as belonging to or serving the Emperor.

EDCS-23300091.summary: This is a dedication to Tiberius Claudius Severus, likely a tombstone inscription from the Roman period.

EDCS-23300103.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Publius Fannius Opsychus, likely a tombstone inscription from the Roman period.

EDCS-23300128.summary: This is a dedication to Porcia Talassia, with some abbreviations likely indicating her family or status, possibly from the Roman period.

EDCS-23300135.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Titus Vestricius Daphnus, likely a tombstone inscription from the Roman period.

EDCS-23300142.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Antonius Attalus Ulpianus, likely a tombstone inscription from the Roman period.

EDCS-23300144.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Tiberius Claudius Herculanius, likely a tombstone inscription from the Roman period.

EDCS-23300146.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Maecilius Firmus, likely a tombstone inscription from the Roman period.

EDCS-23300148.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Obultronius Apollonius, likely a tombstone inscription from the Roman period, with a possible repetition of the name.

EDCS-23300152.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Sextus Terentius Eros, likely a tombstone inscription from the Roman period.

EDCS-23300154.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Arminius Iulianus, likely a tombstone inscription from the Roman period.

EDCS-23300161.translation: By the authority of Quintus Iunius Rusticus, prefect of the city.
EDCS-23300161.summary: This is a record of an official action taken by the prefect of the city of Rome, Quintus Iunius Rusticus, likely related to a public work or decree.

EDCS-23300165.summary: This is a possessive or genitive inscription, likely indicating ownership or a dedication by Titus Claudius Trofimianus.

EDCS-23300167.summary: This is a possessive or genitive inscription, likely indicating ownership or a dedication by Titus Munatius Primus.

EDCS-23300168.summary: This is a possessive or genitive inscription, likely indicating ownership or a dedication by Sossius Liber.

EDCS-23300209.summary: This is a partially restored possessive or genitive inscription, likely indicating ownership or a dedication by Pacilius Italicus.

EDCS-23300255.translation: Ten pounds, by the authority of Quintus Iunius Rusticus.
EDCS-23300255.summary: This inscription records a quantity of ten pounds, authorized by Quintus Iunius Rusticus, possibly related to a payment or material for a construction or endowment.

EDCS-23300432.translation: Hilarus, freedman of Turpilius, oversaw this on the Ides of Quintilis (August 13) under Gaius Iulius Publius Servilius.
EDCS-23300432.summary: This inscription records the overseer Hilarus, a freedman, and indicates a date and possibly a magistrate or patron named Gaius Iulius Publius Servilius.

EDCS-23400041.translation: To the departed spirits. Gellius Puteolanus made this for his dearest son, Fortunatus, who lived for 2 years, 11 months, and 23 days, from the contribution of the guild.
EDCS-23400041.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a young boy named Fortunatus, erected by his father Gellius Puteolanus, funded by a guild, and dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23400042.translation: To the departed spirits. The guild of dendrophoroi (tree-bearers) made this for the deserving Helvius Edonus.
EDCS-23400042.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Helvius Edonus, erected by his guild, the dendrophoroi, indicating he was a deserving member.

EDCS-23400061.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Naebius Faustus, a soldier of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, in the third century of the trireme, who lived 22 years, 5 months, and 13 days, and served 7 years. His brother Naebius Saturninus, a soldier, made this for his incomparable brother, who deserved well.
EDCS-23400061.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a soldier of the Misenum fleet, set up by his brother.

EDCS-23400062.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Carpini[a], his/her daughter, who lived 10 years, 3 months, and [some days]. [Candidus] made this for [her] who deserved well, [for some years and months].
EDCS-23400062.summary: A fragment of a tombstone for a girl named Carpini[a], with some parts illegible, set up by Candid[us].

EDCS-23400064.translation: Quinta Verania, mother, made this for Caius Julius Fortunatus, who lived 26 years and 8 months.
EDCS-23400064.summary: A tombstone commemorating Caius Julius Fortunatus, who lived 26 years and 8 months, erected by his mother.

EDCS-23400065.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Titus Litucius Trophimus, from Hermaphroditus, his relative.
EDCS-23400065.summary: A tombstone inscription dedicated to Titus Litucius Trophimus by his relative Hermaphroditus.

EDCS-23400067.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Antonio Adbene, whose wife Junia Felicita made this for her most beloved husband, who lived 47 years, 9 months, and 17 days.
EDCS-23400067.summary: A tombstone inscription for Marcus Antonio Adbene, set up by his wife, who provides his lifespan and death date.

EDCS-23400068.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Hippolyte made this for Aurelius Julianus, a man most deserving.
EDCS-23400068.summary: A tombstone inscription for Aurelius Julianus, dedicated by Aurelia Hippolyte.

EDCS-23400074.translation: [...] Messius [---], freedman of Quintus, a teacher, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-23400074.summary: A funerary inscription for Messius Priscus, a teacher and freedman, who died at age 45.

EDCS-23400075.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Julia [---], who lived [X] years, 2 months, [---]. Marcus Ulpius Cres[cens] and Julia Fortunata [made this] for their most beloved daughter.
EDCS-23400075.summary: A tombstone inscription for Julia [---], erected by her parents, Marcus Ulpius Crescens and Julia Fortunata.

EDCS-23400078.translation: Carried off by a harsh law of the sister Fates, I lie in these dark earthly abodes, barely short of five days before completing my fourteenth year by the sun's journey. My name was Longinius Gaius formerly, Proculus was my cognomen, now I am a shadow, not even a shadow, placed beneath the earth, behold, I am covered by the tomb's sepulchre. And no less, born opposite, by my mother's brother, forty-four years and some days, he plunged his lights into eternal night. Here, like my brother, he is buried with me on the Stabian shore, he slips into the waters of Acheron. Now, may you, fortunate traveler, if you know anything after death, do not find it burdensome to say: 'May the ashes of Antonius and Proculus rest gently.' Caius Longinius Priscus, father, trierarch of the Misenum praetorian fleet, and Licinia Procilla, mother, for their most beloved son.
EDCS-23400078.summary: A lengthy epitaph for two brothers, Gaius Longinius Proculus (who died shortly before his 14th birthday) and another son of the parents, erected by their father, a trierarch of the Misenum fleet, and their mother.

EDCS-23400079.translation: For Julius Longinus, a principal in the colony of Misenum, from the decurions, who lived 54 years and 55 days. His wife Julia Maria made this for him, well-deserving.
EDCS-23400079.summary: A tombstone inscription for Julius Longinus, a prominent citizen of Misenum, erected by his wife.

EDCS-23400081.translation: To Bettia Felicitas, most innocent woman, her husband Batinius Iulius [dedicated this] to his dearest wife.
EDCS-23400081.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband to his wife, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23400082.translation: To Cornelia Ferocia, who lived 59 years, 11 months, and 16 days, Cornelius Carpophorianus [dedicated this] to his most sweet mother.
EDCS-23400082.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelia Ferocia, detailing her lifespan and erected by her son, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23400083.translation: Licinia made this monument or tomb for her deserving husband, Gaius Valerius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Falerna tribe, while she was alive; it will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-23400083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, specifying that the burial plot is not inheritable, likely from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-23400122.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph or ownership marker for Aulus Alfius Fortunatus.

EDCS-23400161.translation: Marcus Nigidius [erected this] at his own expense.
EDCS-23400161.summary: This is an inscription indicating that Marcus Nigidius funded the construction or dedication of a monument.

EDCS-23400168.summary: This inscription likely refers to Secundus, a retiarius (gladiator type), possibly marking his tomb or a dedication.

EDCS-23400208.translation: Sextus Pompeius, freedman of Sextus, willingly fulfilled his vow to Neptune.
EDCS-23400208.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to the god Neptune by Sextus Pompeius, a freedman.

EDCS-23400209.translation: Veratius Caprius and his wife Viciria Myrinene, having made this memorial for themselves while alive, [also made it] for their family.
EDCS-23400209.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the construction of a memorial by a couple for themselves and their descendants.

EDCS-23400211.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Mother and father made [this] for their most miserable son, Publius Afinius Firminus, who lived 3 years, 6 months, and 28 days.
EDCS-23400211.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, Publius Afinius Firminus, erected by his grieving parents.

EDCS-23400212.translation: Gaius Suellius [---] for himself and for Valeria [---], his concubine, and [---] the daughter of Valeria Quarta [---].
EDCS-23400212.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription likely establishing burial rights for Gaius Suellius, his concubine, and a daughter, possibly from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-23400247.translation: Suavis watched a performance for Servius Lentulus Quintius Iunius on the Kalends of July.
EDCS-23400247.summary: This inscription records a private notice concerning a performance attended by Servius Lentulus Quintius Iunius on July 1st, possibly during the reign of Augustus or Tiberius.

EDCS-23400270.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aulus Agathopus, who lived 22 years, 2 months, and 22 days. His parents (dedicated this) to their most devoted son.
EDCS-23400270.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Agathopus, who died at the age of 22 years, 2 months, and 22 days, dedicated by his parents, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400271.translation: Appuleia Felix, daughter of Sextus, lived 5 years.
EDCS-23400271.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Appuleia Felix, daughter of Sextus, who died at the age of 5, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400273.translation: Castricia Demetria made this for her student Euporatus, who lived 21 years, (dedicated) to him as one who deserved well.
EDCS-23400273.summary: This funerary epitaph records Castricia Demetria dedicating a monument to her student Euporatus, who died at the age of 21, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400275.translation: Pupidius Chrestenus, who lived 42 years. Gaius Cornelius Puteolanus made this for his most sweet wife.
EDCS-23400275.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pupidius Chrestenus, who died at the age of 42, dedicated by his wife's husband, Gaius Cornelius Puteolanus, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400276.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Stlacius Florentius, student, lived 3 years, 5 months, and 11 days. His parents (dedicated this) to their most sweet son.
EDCS-23400276.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Stlacius Florentius, a student, who died at the age of 3 years, 5 months, and 11 days, dedicated by his parents, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400282.translation: In memory of Gaius Rafidius Sabinus. Leo, the steward of the Emperor, and Rafidia Campana (dedicated this) to their most sweet son.
EDCS-23400282.summary: This funerary epitaph records Leo, an imperial steward, and Rafidia Campana dedicating a monument to their son, Gaius Rafidius Sabinus, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400283.translation: [...] for new arrivals without injury and inconvenience. They were in the Senate seventy [...] under consuls Publius Pomponius Magianus [and...].
EDCS-23400283.summary: This fragment likely concerns regulations for new arrivals and their treatment within the Senate, with a mention of consuls Publius Pomponius Magianus and possibly another, dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400284.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Epimachus, son of Epimachus. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23400284.summary: This funerary epitaph records a burial place granted by decree of the local council for Epimachus, son of Epimachus, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400288.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelius Artemidorus, who lived [X] years, 7 months, and 17 days. His parents (dedicated this) to their most sweet son.
EDCS-23400288.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Artemidorus, who died at an unspecified age but with 7 months and 17 days, dedicated by his parents, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400290.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Eutucetus, his father, made this for his most beloved son, who lived 23 years, 10 months, and 7 days; for I hoped you would do well for me, deserving one.
EDCS-23400290.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father for his deceased son, lamenting his death and expressing hope for his own well-being.

EDCS-23400291.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Firminus Quinquennalis, his deserving fellow slave and brother.
EDCS-23400291.summary: A funerary epitaph honoring Firminus Quinquennalis, who is described as both a fellow slave and a brother.

EDCS-23400293.translation: For Grania Clara, freedwoman of Aulus, and her freedwoman. She lived 23 years. She never caused me any stomach trouble, except that she is dead.
EDCS-23400293.summary: A funerary epitaph for Grania Clara, expressing grief through a unique statement of her lack of causing trouble, implying she was a good companion.

EDCS-23400294.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucceia Prisca, her foster-daughter, and her mother Hygia. Lucceia Isias made this for the deserving one.
EDCS-23400294.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Lucceia Isias for her foster-daughter Lucceia Prisca and Prisca's mother, Hygia.

EDCS-23400296.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Mamidia [---], the deserving one. Eutyces her husband and Vitalio her son made this, and for Amullia Olimpias [---].
EDCS-23400296.summary: A funerary monument set up by a husband and son for Mamidia, and also for Amullia Olimpias.

EDCS-23400297.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Ossucula, deserving one. She lived 17 years and 18 days.
EDCS-23400297.summary: A simple funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Ossucula.

EDCS-23400302.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aulus Vesonius Hermes and Aulus Vesonius Fructuosus, for themselves and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-23400302.summary: A funerary dedication by Aulus Vesonius Hermes and Aulus Vesonius Fructuosus for themselves, their freedmen, and their descendants.

EDCS-23400303.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Vibius Anicetus, who lived 57 years. By Megiste, his freedwoman.
EDCS-23400303.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Vibius Anicetus, set up by his freedwoman Megiste.

EDCS-23400304.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Ulpia Fortunata, who lived 10 years. Gaius Puteolanus Fortunatus made this for his most dutiful daughter, the deserving one.
EDCS-23400304.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by Gaius Putelolanus Fortunatus for his daughter Ulpia Fortunata, emphasizing her youth and dutifulness.

EDCS-23400305.translation: [To the spirits of the departed.] For Diomedes [---]eplicia [---].
EDCS-23400305.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription, likely intended for someone named Diomedes.

EDCS-23400309.translation: To the divine shades. Caius Domitius Lucanus, a veteran of Egypt, lived for 50 years. Quintus Mettius Apollinaris and Caius Maximius Apollinaris, his heirs, made this for him as deserving.
EDCS-23400309.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Domitius Lucanus, a veteran, set up by his heirs, dated likely to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400310.translation: To the divine shades. For Aulus Iulius Valentis, also called Diza son of Serrius, a scribe from the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, who served for 25 years and lived for 40 years. Aulus Iulius Iulianus and Aulus Iulius Staleianus, his sons, made this for their deserving father and for his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-23400310.summary: A funerary inscription for Aulus Iulius Valentis, a scribe in the Praetorian fleet, erected by his sons, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400311.translation: To the divine shades. Artorius Valens, an Alexandrian by birth, a trierarch, lived for 26 years and served for 6 years. Iulia Agrippina made this for him as deserving.
EDCS-23400311.summary: A funerary inscription for Artorius Valens, a trierarch of Alexandrian origin, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE, set up by Iulia Agrippina.

EDCS-23400312.translation: To the divine shades. For Claudia Zosime, who lived for 22 years and 6 months, an incomparable and unsuitable wife. Fabius Antonius, optio, made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-23400312.summary: A funerary inscription for Claudia Zosime, the wife of Fabius Antonius, an optio, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400314.translation: To the divine shades. For Iulius Aquila Silvanus, an Italian, a trierarch, who served for 35 years and lived for 10 years. Scentia Valeria, his wife, made this for him as deserving.
EDCS-23400314.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Aquila Silvanus, an Italian trierarch, set up by his wife, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400315.translation: To the divine shades. For Marcus Iulius Marinus, a veteran of our Emperor, who lived for 73 years, 3 months, and 7 days. His freedmen made this for their deserving patron.
EDCS-23400315.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Marinus, a veteran, erected by his freedmen, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400316.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. For Iulius Marinus, a veteran of our Emperor, who lived for 60 years, 7 months, and 8 days. Iulia Felicitas made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-23400316.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Marinus, a veteran, set up by his wife, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400321.translation: For Caius Iulius Magnus, son of Caius, of the Falernian tribe, a ship commander and chief of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, who was elected to the order of decurions and to the office of duumvir by decree of the decurions and by popular request. The townspeople, respecting him, gave 20,000 sesterces at his dedication. Dedicated on the day before the Ides of August in the consulship of Saturninus and Gallus, under the supervision of Quintus Servilius [---] and Tiberius Claudius Philetus, duumvirs.
EDCS-23400321.summary: An honorary inscription for Caius Iulius Magnus, a prominent local official in Misenum, detailing his career and public benefactions, dated to the consulship of Saturninus and Gallus (likely 193 CE).

EDCS-23400322.translation: To the divine shades, sacred. For Lucius Annaeus [---], who was elected to the order [---], who lived for [---] years. Lucius Annaeus Cam[---], his son, made this for his deserving [son ---].
EDCS-23400322.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Annaeus [---], likely set up by his son, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400328.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. For Visellia Paulina, daughter of Lucius. Publius Septimenus Celer, son of Publius, made this for his mother, under the supervision of Publius Septimenus Onesimus.
EDCS-23400328.summary: A funerary inscription for Visellia Paulina, erected by her son Publius Septimenus Celer with the assistance of Publius Septimenus Onesimus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400331.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Ursulus, steward of Diana, and his companions Threptus, Trohimus, Alcides, Eutychus, Cosmus, Faustus, by divine vision.
EDCS-23400331.summary: A dedication to Silvanus, made by Ursulus and his companions, likely from a rural estate or villa.

EDCS-23400333.translation: Tiberius Cervonius Maximus, son of Andronicus, of the Quirina tribe, soldier of the 7th Praetorian Cohort, century of Tutus, served for 8 years, lived for 29 years, and is buried in this very stone; he ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-23400333.summary: A military tombstone for Tiberius Cervonius Maximus, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23400335.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ursus, son of Crescent, Augustalis, to his most excellent father Caius Curedius Crescent, freedman of Caius.
EDCS-23400335.summary: A funerary dedication from Ursus to his father, Caius Curedius Crescent, who held the honorary title of Augustalis.

EDCS-23400337.translation: [To] Audasia(?), wife of Apate, with whom he lived for 43 years.
EDCS-23400337.summary: A funerary dedication to a woman named Audasia, noting her marital lifespan.

EDCS-23400339.translation: To the departed spirits. To Manius Cassius Babullianus, dearest son, who lived 2 years, 5 months, and 4 days. Cassia Clymene and his father Babullianus, most unfortunate parents.
EDCS-23400339.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy, Manius Cassius Babullianus, set up by his grieving parents.

EDCS-23400343.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nicenus, living, perhaps erected this. Titus Pomponius Salutaris.
EDCS-23400343.summary: A funerary dedication, possibly erected by Titus Pomponius Salutaris for Nicenus.

EDCS-23400355.translation: To Lucius Veturius Homunculus, son of Lucius, of the tribe (not specified), tribune of soldiers twice in the 15th Apollinaris Legion and the 7th Macedonian Legion, prefect of cavalry, pontiff, by decree of the decurions, at public expense.
EDCS-23400355.summary: A public monument honoring Lucius Veturius Homunculus for his military service and civic roles, likely dating to the Roman Empire.

EDCS-23400357.translation: [... descendant] of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and of the deified Nerva, the Senate and People of Anagnina.
EDCS-23400357.summary: An inscription relating familial descent from emperors, likely indicating high status, for the people of Anagnina.

EDCS-23400358.translation: To the departed spirits. To Ancharia [---], wife, well-deserving.
EDCS-23400358.summary: A funerary dedication to Ancharia, a wife, by her husband.

EDCS-23400361.translation: The most sorrowful and most innocent Faustina, always attentive to divine law, her mother, with her whole heart, made for the most fortunate, ever-dwelling virgin daughter, in peace.
EDCS-23400361.summary: A funerary epitaph for a deceased virgin daughter, commissioned by her mother, emphasizing her virtue and devotion.

EDCS-23400386.translation: To Aulus Aemilius, son of Aulus, of the Setinian tribe, as patron, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-23400386.summary: This is a dedication to a patron by decree of the local council, likely honoring a distinguished citizen.

EDCS-23400388.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Aufidius, a freedman, and Flavia Eutychia, his wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-23400388.summary: A tombstone inscription for a freedman and his wife, dedicated to the Divine Manes, indicating a burial monument.

EDCS-23400389.translation: [...] to his wife [3]phila, to Icarus [3]nnidus, to Felix [filled in]
EDCS-23400389.summary: Fragmentary tombstone inscription listing several names, suggesting it honored multiple deceased individuals or was part of a larger funerary monument.

EDCS-23400390.translation: To the Divine Manes. A most dutiful mother made this impious deed for her dearest son, Julius Marcus, who lived 23 years, and for herself and her spouse.
EDCS-23400390.summary: A tombstone inscription where a mother commemorates her deceased son and also dedicates it for herself and her husband, expressing grief over the son's death.

EDCS-23400391.translation: Lucius Junius Cerdo. Iunia Anna, freedwoman of Lucius, [bought this land]. 20 feet in the field, 12 feet in the front.
EDCS-23400391.summary: An inscription possibly related to land ownership or burial plots, specifying dimensions and naming individuals, including a freedwoman.

EDCS-23400392.summary: A fragmented inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, identifying a freedwoman named Manilia.

EDCS-23400393.translation: To the Divine Manes. Otacilius Successus for his son Otacilius Priscianus.
EDCS-23400393.summary: A tombstone inscription where Otacilius Successus dedicates a monument to his son, Otacilius Priscianus.

EDCS-23400394.translation: Quintus Pollus Kandaules, freedman of the woman, for himself and for Antonia Apollonia, freedwoman of the woman. Lucius [---] freedman, for Quintus Pollus, son of Quintus, [---]. Lucius [---]
EDCS-23400394.summary: A complex funerary inscription that names several individuals, including freedmen and their patrons, possibly commemorating a family burial.

EDCS-23400395.translation: Marcus Tatius Pusio, freedman of Marcus. Marcus Tatius Menemachus, freedman of Marcus. Marcus Tatius Hilarus, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-23400395.summary: An inscription recording three individuals named Marcus Tatius, all freedmen of a Marcus Tatius, likely indicating a family or household connection.

EDCS-23400398.translation: [...] 9 years old. [---]alia, daughter. [---]rimo A[---] did this for [him/her] well deserving.
EDCS-23400398.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription that mentions a child's age and identifies a daughter, with a concluding phrase indicating it's a well-deserved memorial.

EDCS-23400405.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Valeria Frontina, of the Gnigissae tribe, from the community of Coropissus, of the village Asseridus, daughter of our lord Valerius Fronto Aquilienus, who lived 29 years, 3 months, and 21 days. Valerius Montanus, ship’s captain, from the same community and village, made this for his most beloved wife, well-deserving.
EDCS-23400405.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Frontina, set up by her husband, a ship's captain, indicating her tribal origin and the village of her family.

EDCS-23400406.translation: Aulus Maianius Hy[mni]s, freedman Philero[s], cloth merchant. Maiania Grata, freedwoman of Aulus, freedwoman. Aulus Maianius Pamphilus, son of Lucius, 12 feet.
EDCS-23400406.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary monument for Phileros and possibly Maiania Grata, with an indication of a measurement possibly related to a tomb or plot size.

EDCS-23400407.translation: Lucius Pomponius Bithus, perfumer, for himself and for his patroness Pomponia Prima, for his son Lucius Pomponius Ofentinus, for his wife Pomponia Glycera, for his son Lucius Pomponius Aditus, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe, and for his son Scirtia Primigenia, son of Spurius.
EDCS-23400407.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely set up by Lucius Pomponius Bithus, a perfumer, for himself and his family members, including his patroness, wife, and children.

EDCS-23400409.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Egnatius Similis, who lived 8 years, 10 months, and 3 days. Quintus Egnatius Similis, his father, made this for his most devoted son.
EDCS-23400409.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Egnatius Similis, set up by his father, Quintus Egnatius Similis.

EDCS-23400410.translation: Caius Iulius Critolaus, son of Apollonius.
EDCS-23400410.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication or identification for Caius Iulius Critolaus, son of Apollonius.

EDCS-23400411.translation: Lucius Laelius [?]. Laelia, freedwoman of Lucius [?]. Lucius Laelius Apies, freedman of Lucius [?]. Laelia, freedwoman of Lucius [?].
EDCS-23400411.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, likely freedmen and freedwomen of a certain Lucius Laelius, possibly indicating family relationships or a shared funerary monument.

EDCS-23400412.translation: Caius Nautius Trupho, freedman of Caius. Furia Helena, freedwoman of Lucius. Lucius Demetrius, freedman of a woman. Furia Albana, freedwoman of a woman. Lucius Homuncio, freedman of a woman. Furia Acantis, freedwoman of a woman.
EDCS-23400412.summary: This inscription lists several freedmen and freedwomen and their patrons, likely marking a family tomb or indicating ownership of property.

EDCS-23400415.translation: Publius Plavius Anteros, freedman of Publius. Fundia Possila, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-23400415.summary: This inscription records the names of Publius Plavius Anteros, a freedman, and Fundia Possila, daughter of Marcus, possibly related or associated.

EDCS-23400416.translation: Caius Propertius Sabinus, son of Caius. Marcia Philtate, freedwoman of a woman. Caius Marcius [?], son of Marcius [?]. Marcia Restituta, freedwoman of a woman. Marcia Thais, freedwoman of a woman.
EDCS-23400416.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, including Caius Propertius Sabinus and several freedwomen of unknown male patrons, possibly related to a family tomb.

EDCS-23400417.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Septimia Silvana, daughter [?].
EDCS-23400417.summary: This is a funerary epitaph fragment for Septimia Silvana, indicating her filial relationship.

EDCS-23400418.translation: To the departed spirits. For [3]ia Verecunda, [3]ennius Faustus [made this for his wife and dearest daughter] [3]rus and for Feridia Ve[3]da, his most dutiful daughter, [who lived] 3 years, 4 months, and [?] days.
EDCS-23400418.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a daughter and perhaps her mother, identified by the epithet 'dutiful daughter', with a fragment of the father's name given, dating possibly to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400419.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying Vergilia, daughter of Gaius, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23400421.translation: Caius Truttidius Prepon made this for Vibia Laudice, daughter of Gaius, his wife, and for Gaius Vibius Helion, his father-in-law.
EDCS-23400421.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Caius Truttidius Prepon for his wife Vibia Laudice and his father-in-law Gaius Vibius Helion, suggesting a family connection and dating likely to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400422.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Vibius Helion.
EDCS-23400422.summary: A funerary dedication to Gaius Vibius Helion, invoking the departed spirits, probably from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400423.translation: To the departed spirits. For Vibia Laudice, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-23400423.summary: A funerary dedication to Vibia Laudice, daughter of Gaius, invoking the departed spirits, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400430.translation: [3]lienus. [3] Rufus for himself and for [3]odia Erotis, his concubine, and for Prima, [his] slave, and [3]id() his slave, and for his own family.
EDCS-23400430.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication made by Rufus for himself, his concubine, and his slaves, possibly dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-23400431.translation: Caius Julius Olympus. Caius Julius Olympus.
EDCS-23400431.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary marker or dedication by Caius Julius Olympus, possibly for himself or a relative, with the name repeated for emphasis.

EDCS-23400434.translation: For Claudia Polla, his blessed wife, who lived with me without fault for 14 years, 7 months, and 7 days. Sotericus, [servant] of our Emperor, [made this] for her deserving [of it].
EDCS-23400434.summary: This epitaph commemorates Claudia Polla, wife of Sotericus, a servant of the emperor, detailing their 14-year marriage and dating possibly to the imperial period.

EDCS-23400435.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Iulia Cleopatra made the monument, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23400439.translation: Caius Clodius Maximus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, procurator of the emperor in charge of grain, quaestor, aedile, duovir, dedicates the statue of Spes as a gift.
EDCS-23400439.summary: This inscription records the career and dedication of a statue of Spes by Caius Clodius Maximus, an imperial official, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-23400441.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Attilius Pudens made this for his father, Lucius Attilius Senecae, and his mother, Grattia Romana, well deserving.
EDCS-23400441.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Attilius Pudens for his parents, Lucius Attilius Senecae and Grattia Romana, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400442.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Caecilius Phoenix made this for his patron, Quintus Caecilius Hermes, well deserving.
EDCS-23400442.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Quintus Caecilius Phoenix for his patron, Quintus Caecilius Hermes, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400444.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Clodia Saturnina made this for her well-deserving mother, Iulia Dia.
EDCS-23400444.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Clodia Saturnina for her mother, Iulia Dia, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400451.translation: To our Lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, son of the deified Constantius Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-23400451.summary: This is an inscription honoring Emperor Constantine I, likely erected during his reign (306-337 CE).

EDCS-23400528.translation: Petronia Damalis [dedicated this to] Publius Curtius Pannycus.
EDCS-23400528.summary: This is a dedication by Petronia Damalis to Publius Curtius Pannycus, the nature of which is unspecified but may be funerary or votive, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400529.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Claudia Arginai(?) lived for 4 years and 30 days.
EDCS-23400529.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the short life of Claudia Arginai(?), likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400533.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Lucius Verus, who reigned from 161 to 169 CE.

EDCS-23400542.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Pomponia Prisca, his most beloved wife, and for Titus Pomponius Securus, freedman and heir. Gaius Fabraternus Primitivus [made this].
EDCS-23400542.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Fabraternus Primitivus for his wife Pomponia Prisca and his freedman and heir Titus Pomponius Securus, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400543.summary: This is an incomplete dedication to Tiberius Claudius Carpophorus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23400544.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For the son, Julius Preponti(?), who lived 4 years, [X] months, and 22 days. Iulius Iulianus and Claudia Au[---]ina [made this for their] most sweet [son], well deserving.
EDCS-23400544.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, Julius Preponti(?), recorded by his parents Iulius Iulianus and Claudia Au[---]ina, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23800033.summary: This is an honorary dedication to Gaius Sittius Magnus, likely from the 1st century BCE.

EDCS-23800059.text_edition: \n Holconium Priscum IIvirum fullones universi rogant\n
EDCS-23800059.translation: The fullers ask Holconius Priscus, duovir.
EDCS-23800059.summary: This inscription records a request by the guild of fullers to Holconius Priscus, a duovir, likely dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23800074.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a request to Cuspia Pansa, who held the office of aedile, possibly from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23800076.translation: Iunianus asks Gaius Cuspis Pansa, aedile.
EDCS-23800076.summary: This inscription records a request made by Iunianus to Gaius Cuspis Pansa, who served as aedile, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23800085.summary: This inscription records a request by Tyrsus to Gaius Cornelius, who held the office of aedile, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23800088.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Veranius, who held the office of quinquennalis, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23800096.translation: To Gaius Cuspis, aedile; if glory is to be given to one living virtuously, glory ought to be given to this young man.
EDCS-23800096.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Cuspis as an aedile, praising his virtuous conduct and suggesting he deserves glory, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23800098.text_edition: \n Marcum Epidium Sabinum duumvirum iure dicundo oro faciatis sanctus ordo facit Suedio Clementi sancto iudici feliciter\n
EDCS-23800098.translation: We beg you to make Marcus Epidius Sabinus, duovir for dispensing justice. The honorable order makes it so for the good and honorable judge, Suedius Clemens, may he prosper.
EDCS-23800098.summary: This inscription is a plea to the honorable order to appoint Marcus Epidius Sabinus as duovir, praising the judge Suedius Clemens, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23800121.translation: I beg you, sirs, to make Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus duovir.
EDCS-23800121.summary: This inscription records a plea to appoint Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus to the office of duovir, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23800126.translation: Primilla asks Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus, duovir.
EDCS-23800126.summary: This inscription records a request by Primilla to Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus, who held the office of duovir, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-23800156.translation: I ask you, you (plural) to make Lucius Ceius Secundus, twice a magistrate, worthy of public office. Papilio.
EDCS-23800156.summary: This is a plea from Papilio asking the public to elect Lucius Ceius Secundus as twice a magistrate, deeming him worthy of public office.

EDCS-23800190.translation: Secundus, son of Lucius, as aedile.
EDCS-23800190.summary: This inscription records Secundus, son of Lucius, holding the office of aedile.

EDCS-23800197.summary: This inscription records Cuspius Pansa holding the office of aedile.

EDCS-23800203.translation: Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus, twice a magistrate.
EDCS-23800203.summary: This inscription records Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus as twice a magistrate.

EDCS-23800204.translation: Ascla asks (you to make) Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus, twice a magistrate.
EDCS-23800204.summary: This inscription is a request from Ascla for the public to elect Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus as twice a magistrate.

EDCS-23800217.translation: Lucius Ceius Secundus, twice a magistrate.
EDCS-23800217.summary: This inscription records Lucius Ceius Secundus holding the office of twice a magistrate.

EDCS-23800259.translation: I ask you, you (plural) to make Sittius Magnus worthy of public office as twice a magistrate.
EDCS-23800259.summary: This is a plea asking the public to elect Sittius Magnus as twice a magistrate, deeming him worthy of public office.

EDCS-23800267.text_edition: \n Lucium Albucium Celsum aedilem Tigillinus Celer Africanus\n
EDCS-23800267.translation: Lucius Albucius Celsus, aedile. Tigillinus Celer Africanus (dedicates).
EDCS-23800267.summary: This inscription records Lucius Albucius Celsus as aedile and was dedicated by Tigillinus Celer Africanus.

EDCS-23800337.translation: I ask you, you (plural) to make Lucius Ceius Secundus, twice a magistrate, worthy of public office.
EDCS-23800337.summary: This is a plea asking the public to elect Lucius Ceius Secundus as twice a magistrate, deeming him worthy of public office.

EDCS-23800342.translation: I ask you, you (plural) to make Lucius Popidius Secundus aedile.
EDCS-23800342.summary: This is a plea asking the public to elect Lucius Popidius Secundus as aedile.

EDCS-23800348.translation: To Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus, duovir.
EDCS-23800348.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus, who held the office of duovir.

EDCS-23800360.translation: Lollius Synhodus, his client, asks that you make Lucius Popidius Secundus aedile; Papilio wrote this.
EDCS-23800360.summary: This is a request by a client, Lollius Synhodus, for the election of Lucius Popidius Secundus as aedile, with the scribe identified as Papilio.

EDCS-23800363.translation: I ask that you make Sittius Magnus duovir.
EDCS-23800363.summary: This is a plea for the election of Sittius Magnus as duovir.

EDCS-23800382.translation: I ask that you make Lucius Popidius Secundus aedile, worthy of public office.
EDCS-23800382.summary: This is a request for the election of Lucius Popidius Secundus as aedile, emphasizing his worthiness for public service.

EDCS-23800384.summary: This inscription honors Gavia, who served as duovir.

EDCS-23800386.summary: This is a dedication to Numerius Popidius Rufus, who served as aedile.

EDCS-23800407.translation: To Gaius Sittius Calventius Magnus, duovir in the exercise of jurisdiction; Papilio wrote this.
EDCS-23800407.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Sittius Calventius Magnus, duovir, with the scribe identified as Papilio.

EDCS-23800410.translation: I ask that you make Gaius Gavius Rufus duovir in the exercise of jurisdiction.
EDCS-23800410.summary: This is a plea for the election of Gaius Gavius Rufus as duovir, emphasizing his role in administering justice.

EDCS-23800413.summary: This is an incomplete dedication to Numerius Popidius Rufus, who likely held a public office.

EDCS-23800422.translation: To Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus, duovir in the exercise of jurisdiction, I ask that you make him.
EDCS-23800422.summary: This is a request for the election of Gaius Calventius Sittius Magnus as duovir, highlighting his judicial authority.

EDCS-23800426.summary: This is a dedication to Popidius Rufus, likely a public official, possibly from an honorific monument.

EDCS-23800433.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Ceius, whose full name and purpose of the monument are lost.

EDCS-23800434.translation: I ask you to make Pansa aedile.
EDCS-23800434.summary: This is a plea or request for a person named Pansa to be elected as an aedile (a local magistrate).

EDCS-23800440.translation: Saturninus requests that Popidius Secundus be made aedile.
EDCS-23800440.summary: This is a request from Saturninus for Popidius Secundus to be elected as an aedile.

EDCS-23800445.translation: To Popidius, duumvir with judicial authority.
EDCS-23800445.summary: This is a dedication to Popidius, who held the high office of duumvir iure dicundo (chief magistrate with judicial power).

EDCS-23800450.summary: This is a dedication to Popidius Rufus, with the purpose of the monument incomplete.

EDCS-23800451.translation: Epidius Hymenaeus requests that Lucius Popidius Secundus be made aedile, worthy of public office.
EDCS-23800451.summary: This is a request from Epidius Hymenaeus for Lucius Popidius Secundus to be elected aedile, noting his suitability for public service.

EDCS-23800649.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Caltilius Diadumenus lived 35 years. Lucius Caltilius Euhodus, senior, made this for his excellent freedman. // Lucius Caltilius Diadumenus is buried here.
EDCS-23800649.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lucius Caltilius Diadumenus at age 35 and was erected by his former owner and patron, Lucius Caltilius Euhodus.

EDCS-23800650.translation: [---] / Marcus Umbilius Maximinus / Sextus Sextilius Julianus / Gaius Veturius Testius Amandus, Roman eques / [---] Callistianus / [---], Roman eques [---] / Lucius Me[---] / Aulus Calpurnius [---] / who[---] / Lucius Sorm[---] / Titus Heli[---] / Lucius Messen[ius ---] / Marcus Sulpi[cius ---] / quinquennales [---] / Marcus [---]
EDCS-23800650.summary: This inscription lists the names of several prominent men, including Roman equites and quinquennales (officials elected every five years), possibly related to a civic or religious college.

EDCS-23800659.summary: This inscription likely records a date (the Nones of June) and a personal name, Agape, possibly from a calendar or a personal marker.

EDCS-23800660.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly for a person named Antonius, indicating a burial spot.

EDCS-23800674.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a son of Ocella.

EDCS-23800677.translation: He served 17 years and lived 37 years. Lucius Calpurnius Longinus, soldier of the 3rd Praetorian Cohort, of the century of Quintus Iulius Musici, made (this) for his most dutiful soldier. He is buried here.
EDCS-23800677.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Calpurnius Longinus, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, recording his service and lifespan, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23800679.translation: Marcus Caeselius Savius, freedman of Marcus, in the field 20 feet, in the front [number illegible] feet.
EDCS-23800679.summary: This inscription likely marks a boundary, possibly of a plot of land owned by Marcus Caeselius Savius.

EDCS-23800681.translation: Quintus Flaminius Epapra and Flaminia Habra.
EDCS-23800681.summary: This inscription appears to be a simple listing of names, possibly on a tomb or monument shared by Quintus Flaminius Epapra and Flaminia Habra.

EDCS-23800682.translation: Caius Fufius Theotimus, freedman of Caius, and Paconia Cresta, freedwoman of Marcus, and Fufia Hilara, freedwoman of woman Fufia, and Fufia Aucta, freedwoman of Caius. Hilara [illegible] built this ustrina (place for burning bodies) for half its part; this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-23800682.summary: This is an inscription for a shared tomb and associated ustrina, built by Hilara for herself and other family members, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23800683.translation: Marcus Fundilius Melito, freedman of Marcus. Fundilia Aucta, freedwoman of Marcus (plural). Marcus Fundilius Albanus and Latinia Posilla, freedwoman of Marcus.
EDCS-23800683.summary: This inscription lists members of the Fundilius family, possibly on a tomb or monument, including freedmen and their patrons.

EDCS-23800684.translation: To the Divine Shades. Furia Ostiensis. Aulus Furius Hermes and Furia Ianuaria, most unhappy parents, (made this) for their most dutiful daughter who lived 2 years, 10 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-23800684.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Furia Ostiensis, dedicated by her parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23800686.translation: To the Divine Shades. [illegible]piae Liberali lived 46 years. Callicius made (this) for his deserving wife.
EDCS-23800686.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Callicius to his wife, Liberalis, who lived 46 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23800688.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius Manlius [illegible]enaeus lived 15 years, 6 months, 8 days. The parents made (this).
EDCS-23800688.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Caius Manlius [illegible]enaeus, dedicated by his parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23800689.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Modius [---] Aelia [---] lived [---] years [---].
EDCS-23800689.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Modius and Aelia, likely a family memorial, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800690.translation: To Munatius [---]. Iulia Lucilla [mother] [erected this] to her incomparable son. Lived 5 years [---] days [---].
EDCS-23800690.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulia Lucilla to her son, Munatius, highlighting his brief life, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800692.translation: Gaius Plotius, son of Gaius, freedman [dedicated this] to Gaius Plotius, son of Gaius, freedman [---] [---] Gaius? [---].
EDCS-23800692.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary or commemorative monument for two freedmen named Gaius Plotius, possibly father and son, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800693.translation: To the departed spirits. Calpurnius Fro nimus made [this] for his deserving wife Popilia Sabina, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-23800693.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Calpurnius Fro nimus for his wife Popilia Sabina, who died at age 25, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800694.translation: [---] son of Lucius, freedman Isargurus [dedicated this] to [---]ae, freedwoman Felicula [---] Veneria and [---]allis, wife of Marcus Run[tius?] [---] Moschis, wife of Runtius [---].
EDCS-23800694.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to record dedications or be a funerary monument for several individuals, including freedmen and a freedwoman, with connections to the family of Marcus Runtius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800696.translation: Sperata lived 81 years. [She] is buried here.
EDCS-23800696.summary: A simple tombstone recording the name and age of Sperata, who was buried here, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800697.translation: [---] Marcia, freedwoman of Marcus, Tutilla [erected this] [Lucius] Marcius, freedman of Lucius and his wife, Phoenix for himself and his family [---] Marcia, freedwoman of [---] Marcia, freedwoman of Lucius, Prima [---] [Lucius?] Marcius, freedman of [---] Quartio [---] Marciae, freedwoman of Lucius, Timae.
EDCS-23800697.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Phoenix for his family, including freedwomen named Marcia, Prima, and Timae, and possibly other freedmen, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800698.translation: Quintus Ofillius Memor, a mirror maker, lived 40 years. He is buried here. His colleagues erected this.
EDCS-23800698.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Ofillius Memor, a specularia (mirror maker), erected by his colleagues, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800700.translation: To Marcia Curia Euodia, the Hebrew. Gaius Vetulenus Melissus made [this] for his deserving wife and relative, who lived together 32 years, and brought with him [---] 48 years, and also for Gaius Vetulenus Euhodo, son, who lived 22 years.
EDCS-23800700.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcia Curia Euodia and her son Gaius Vetulenus Euhodo, erected by Gaius Vetulenus Melissus, likely her husband, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800705.translation: [---] who lived 40 years [---] 8 months. Paulinus made [this] for his deserving, incomparable colleague.
EDCS-23800705.summary: A funerary dedication by Paulinus for his colleague, who lived 40 years and 8 months, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800708.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Lucius Gavidius [---] gave and dedicated this gift willingly and deservedly to the worshippers of the god Mithras.
EDCS-23800708.summary: A dedication to Mithras by Lucius Gavidius, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23800710.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, Titus Suedius Clemens, tribune, after hearing the cases and taking the measurements, restored to the public property of the Pompeians that was possessed by private individuals.
EDCS-23800710.summary: A public works inscription from Pompeii, detailing the restitution of public land by Titus Suedius Clemens under Vespasian (69-79 CE).

EDCS-23800716.translation: Aprio, son of [---], who lived fifty years, ten months, and 20 days, made [this] for his deserving mother.
EDCS-23800716.summary: A funerary dedication by Aprio for his mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23800722.summary: A simple name inscription, likely a tombstone or dedication, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23800732.summary: A name inscription, possibly a tombstone or the name of a builder/dedicant, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23800733.summary: A funerary inscription for a child named Veneria, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23800737.summary: A funerary inscription for a child named Hermes, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23800755.translation: By decree of the Senate, the Augustales were created, those who contributed to the worship of the divine house. Patrons elected: Lucius Flavius Argentarius, Pollio Maximus, Marcus Caecilius Calventius Quadratus, Marcus Caecilius Calventius Quadratus the son, Marcus Caecilius Calventius Quadratus the younger, Lucius Lollius Hedylalus, Titus Vettulenus Nepos father of the Augustales, Publius Carsidius Priscus, Marcia Polybiane priestess of the Augustales, Flavia Festa mother of the Augustales. Corporati (members of the guild): Lollius Terpsichorus, Pomponius Agon, Herennius Leonides, Felixs col(onorum) ar(carius), Claudius Felixs, Iulius Felicissimus, Liternius Felixs, Puteolanus Puteolanorum servant tabularius, Lucius Aemilius Capito, Iulius Felixs. Plebs (common members): Fufius Eutyches, Aemilius Primitivus, Osculenus Sosthenes, Arellius Iulianus, Arellius Felixs, Clodius Urbicus, Caecilius Castor, Flavius Abascantus, Lollius Archelaus, Antonius Eutyches, Aufidius Demetrius, Gellius Felicio, [[6]], Iulius Ianuarius, Lollius Ianuarius, Veratius Venustus, Antonius Mercurialis, Aelius Ianuarius, Sextus Trabius Agathemerus, Marcus Verrius Felicissimus. Marcus Caecilius Calventius Quadratus, in honor of himself and his sons, made and dedicated at his own expense the pavements of the house and dining room.
EDCS-23800755.summary: An extensive inscription from Ostia detailing the creation of Augustales (a priesthood associated with the imperial cult), elected patrons, guild members, and common members, with a significant donation by Marcus Caecilius Calventius Quadratus for the paving of a building.

EDCS-23801587.translation: Caius Vettidius Philemo, freedman of Caius; Vinicia Nice, freedwoman of Caius, pious and fruitful; Caius Vettidius Anteros, freedman of Caius; Lucius Servius Auctus, freedman of Lucius. In the front 21 feet, in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-23801587.summary: A property boundary inscription, likely for a burial plot, from the Roman Imperial period, listing freedmen and their patrons.

EDCS-23801626.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Telesphor made [this] for Vitalis, his deserving tent-mate, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-23801626.summary: A funerary dedication from a soldier or member of a contubernium (tent group) for his deceased comrade, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23900001.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-23900001.translation: With tribunician power 20 times, emperor, consul, father of his country. To the divine spirits of Iulia Maxima, Gargilius Urbanus erected this for his well-deserving daughter. He made it.
EDCS-23900001.summary: This inscription seems to be a fragmented dedication to an emperor and a funerary monument for Iulia Maxima, erected by her father, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-23900004.translation: A place assigned to the guild of bakers by Papirius Dionysius, then Prefect of the Annona, with the permission of the decurions.
EDCS-23900004.summary: This inscription records the allocation of a plot of land to the bakers' guild by a prefect of the grain supply, indicating an official grant of space.

EDCS-23900022.translation: To the divine spirits of Quintus Aelius Comitialis. His heirs made this according to his will.
EDCS-23900022.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that the heirs of Quintus Aelius Comitialis erected his tombstone as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-23900026.translation: To the divine spirits of Titus Abius Ammiadus, his well-deserving freedwoman, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-23900026.summary: A funerary inscription for a freedwoman named Ammiadus, erected by Titus Abius, her former master, and recording her age at death.

EDCS-23900031.translation: To the divine spirits of Quintus Baebius Mena. His heirs, Quintus Baebius Maximus, his wife Baebia Adiecta, and his daughter Baebia Menais, made this for him.
EDCS-23900031.summary: This funerary inscription records the deceased Quintus Baebius Mena, for whom his heirs (including his wife and daughter) erected the monument.

EDCS-23900033.translation: To the divine spirits of Iuliana, who lived 27 years, 1 month, and 14 days. Marcus Publicius Speratus made this for his most beloved wife and for Cincia Helpidius, his most sacred mother-in-law, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-23900033.summary: This funerary inscription honors a wife named Iuliana and her mother-in-law, erected by Marcus Publicius Speratus, providing ages at death and indicating a close family relationship.

EDCS-23900038.translation: To the divine spirits of Cornelius Zo[...]
EDCS-23900038.summary: A very fragmentary funerary inscription that begins with a dedication to the divine spirits and the name Cornelius Zo[...].

EDCS-23900040.translation: To the divine spirits of Durdenia [...], who lived [...]
EDCS-23900040.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription beginning with a dedication to the divine spirits and indicating the deceased's name and lifespan.

EDCS-23900044.translation: To the divine spirits of Aulus Egrilius Hilarianus, son of Aulus, of the Palatina tribe.
EDCS-23900044.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Aulus Egrilius Hilarianus, including his filiation and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-23900048.translation: To the divine spirits. Nicostratus made this for his well-deserving wife Eutychia.
EDCS-23900048.summary: A funerary inscription where Nicostratus erected a monument for his wife Eutychia, praising her good qualities.

EDCS-23900051.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Aulus Fabius Trofimus, his dearest freedman.
EDCS-23900051.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aulus Fabius Trofimus to his freedman.

EDCS-23900052.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Aulus Fabius Fabianus, son of Aulus Fabius, Roman knight, decurion of Ostia, who lived 22 years, 11 months, and 20 days. Aulus Fabius Trofimus, his father, made this.
EDCS-23900052.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a father for his son, a Roman knight and decurion, specifying his age at death.

EDCS-23900053.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Aulus Fabius Trophimus, his father, of outstanding devotion. Aulus Fabius Primigenianus, his son.
EDCS-23900053.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a son for his devoted father.

EDCS-23900054.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Fonteius Fonteianus lived 1 year, 10 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-23900054.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a child, Caius Fonteius Fonteianus, specifying his age at death.

EDCS-23900055.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Cale made this for her well-deserving husband, Caius Galerius Thessalus.
EDCS-23900055.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a wife for her husband, Caius Galerius Thessalus.

EDCS-23900057.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gavia Casta, his wife, and his parents, Julius Euhelpistus. To the well-deserving. She lived 17 years and 59 days.
EDCS-23900057.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Julius Euhelpistus for his wife Gavia Casta and his parents, specifying his wife's age at death.

EDCS-23900062.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Manlius Blandus made this for his well-deserving wife, Iulia Asclepidoteni.
EDCS-23900062.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Quintus Manlius Blandus for his wife, Iulia Asclepidoteni.

EDCS-24100206.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Optimo [---]
EDCS-24100206.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication to someone named Optimo.

EDCS-24100207.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aulus Ostiensis Asclepiades made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-24100207.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Aulus Ostiensis Asclepiades for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-24100222.translation: Aulus Terentius Stephanus, freedman of the Auli Terentii, Aulus Terentius Stephanus, freedman of Aulus Terentius, freedman(?), Terentia Nardis, freedwoman of Aulus, in front 25 feet, in the field 20 feet [---]
EDCS-24100222.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land acquired by freedmen and freedwomen of the Aulus Terentius family, possibly for funerary purposes.

EDCS-24100225.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Ulpia Helpidia, my dearest wife.
EDCS-24100225.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Ulpia Helpidia, set up by her husband.

EDCS-24100431.translation: To Lucius Arrius Hermetus, a vase-maker. Aulus Egrilius Plarianus (dedicates this).
EDCS-24100431.summary: This funerary dedication is for Lucius Arrius Hermetus, a vase-maker, set up by Aulus Egrilius Plarianus.

EDCS-24300015.translation: The clients, with the followers of Isis, ask that you make Cuspium Pansam aedile.
EDCS-24300015.summary: This is a request to elect Cuspium Pansam as aedile, made by his clients and members of the Isiac cult.

EDCS-24300031.translation: Gaius Sempronius Rhyseros fulfilled his vow to Queen Isis.
EDCS-24300031.summary: This inscription records Gaius Sempronius Rhyseros fulfilling a vow made to the goddess Isis Regina.

EDCS-24300032.translation: [Our friend] Pollio built (this) for [Serapis] and Isis.
EDCS-24300032.summary: This is a dedication of a building or monument to the gods Serapis and Isis, erected by Pollio.

EDCS-24300033.translation: Titus Statilius Optatio decorated the statue with small columns and an aegis.
EDCS-24300033.summary: This inscription records Titus Statilius Optatio decorating a statue with architectural elements and an aegis.

EDCS-24300368.text_edition: \n Caium Lollium Fuscum IIvirum viis aedibus sacris publicis procurandis Asellinas sic rogant sic nec sine Zmyrina\n
EDCS-24300368.translation: The Asellinae ask that you make Gaius Lollius Fuscus, twice aedile, responsible for public roads and sacred buildings; and they do not do so without Zmyrina.
EDCS-24300368.summary: This is an electoral request for Gaius Lollius Fuscus to be appointed to oversee public works, made by the Asellinae.

EDCS-24300376.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Marcus Lucretius Frontonus.

EDCS-24300381.translation: I ask you, sirs, to make Fuscus aedile.
EDCS-24300381.summary: This is an electoral request asking for Fuscus to be made aedile.

EDCS-24300426.text_edition: \n Caium Cuspium Pansam aedilem oro faciatis Purpurio cum Paridianis\n
EDCS-24300426.translation: I ask you, sirs, to make Gaius Cuspium Pansam aedile, Purpurio with the Paridiani.
