EDCS-32301023.translation: From the estates of Agathyrsus, freedman of Augustus, by Quintus Pomponius Ianuaris, in the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-32301023.summary: This inscription records a delivery from the estates of Agathyrsus, an imperial freedman, by Quintus Pomponius Ianuaris, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32301026.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the figlinae of Annius Verus, by Publius P. B. of Salarese.
EDCS-32301026.summary: This inscription records a delivery from the figlinae of Annius Verus, attributed to Publius P. B. of Salarese, dated to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-32301027.translation: In the consulship of Hiberus and Sisenna, by Peducaeus Lupus, from the figlinae of our Emperor Rhodinianus.
EDCS-32301027.summary: This inscription records a delivery from the figlinae of Emperor Rhodinianus, by Peducaeus Lupus, dated to the consulship of Hiberus and Sisenna.

EDCS-32301037.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, by Pomponius Vitalis, from the estates of Annius Verus Quintianus.
EDCS-32301037.summary: This inscription records a delivery from the estates of Annius Verus Quintianus, by Pomponius Vitalis, dated to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-32301039.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, by Publius P. B. of Salarese, from the figlinae of Annius Verus.
EDCS-32301039.summary: This inscription records a delivery from the figlinae of Annius Verus, attributed to Publius P. B. of Salarese, dated to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-32301041.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the figlinae of Cornelius Mallus of Salarese, by Publius P. B.
EDCS-32301041.summary: This inscription records a delivery from the figlinae of Cornelius Mallus of Salarese, by Publius P. B., dated to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-32301281.translation: From the estates of Annius Verus Quintianus, under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.
EDCS-32301281.summary: This inscription records that goods came from the estates of Annius Verus Quintianus, dated to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-32700079.translation: Belonging to Aulus Sempronius Paternus Hermianus.
EDCS-32700079.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely indicating ownership by Aulus Sempronius Paternus Hermianus.

EDCS-32800145.translation: From the potteries of Domitia Domitiana Sulpiciana, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-32800145.summary: This inscription indicates that the pottery originated from the works of Domitia Domitiana Sulpiciana, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800153.translation: From the potteries of Domitia Domitiana Sulpiciana, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-32800153.summary: This inscription indicates that the pottery originated from the works of Domitia Domitiana Sulpiciana, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800154.translation: From the potteries of Domitia Domitiana Sulpiciana, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-32800154.summary: This inscription indicates that the pottery originated from the works of Domitia Domitiana Sulpiciana, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800155.translation: From the estates of Domitia Domitiana, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-32800155.summary: This inscription records that goods came from the estates of Domitia Domitiana, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800189.translation: From the potteries of Domitia Domitiana Sulpiciana, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-32800189.summary: This inscription indicates that the pottery originated from the works of Domitia Domitiana Sulpiciana, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800191.translation: From the potteries of Claudius Liberalis Sulpicianus, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-32800191.summary: This inscription indicates that the pottery originated from the works of Claudius Liberalis Sulpicianus, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800198.translation: From the estates of Domitia Domitiana, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-32800198.summary: This inscription records that goods came from the estates of Domitia Domitiana, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800208.translation: From the potteries of Domitia Domitiana Sulpiciana, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-32800208.summary: This inscription indicates that the pottery originated from the works of Domitia Domitiana Sulpiciana, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800228.translation: From the estates of Lucilla Veri, product of Major's pottery workshop, under Commodus and Lateranus as consuls.
EDCS-32800228.summary: This is a mark from a pottery workshop indicating the origin of the product, dating to the consulship of Commodus and Lateranus.

EDCS-32800245.translation: From the potteries of Domitia Domitiana, Sulpician factory, under Paetinus and Apronianus as consuls.
EDCS-32800245.summary: This is a mark from a pottery workshop indicating its owner and the consuls of the year, Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800249.translation: Under Paetinus and Apronianus as consuls, from the estates of Domitia Domitiana.
EDCS-32800249.summary: This inscription indicates the origin of goods from the estates of Domitia Domitiana, dated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800261.translation: From the potteries of Domitia Domitiana, Sulpician factory, under Paetinus and Apronianus as consuls.
EDCS-32800261.summary: This is a stamp from a pottery indicating its owner and the year of production, marked by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800265.translation: From the potteries of Tempesina, Aburnius Caedicianus, son of Publius Servilius Firmus, under Paetinus and Apronianus as consuls.
EDCS-32800265.summary: This pottery stamp identifies the workshop, owner, and the year, indicated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-32800266.translation: From the potteries of Tempesina, Quintus Aburnius Caedicianus Vismathus Fortunatus, under Apronianus and Paetinus as consuls.
EDCS-32800266.summary: This is a mark from a pottery workshop, identifying its owner and the year of production, marked by the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-32805485.translation: Acratus with [sums of money]; [---]rus the tiler with [sums of money]; [---]ros the builder with [sums of money]; Metrodas with [sums of money], under Aulus Vitellius and Lucius Vipstanus Poplicola as consuls; Tiberius Claudius Amarantus; Tiberius Claudius Epaphroditus; [---]ctus the steward; [[---]] under Quintus Veranius and Gaius Pompeius Gallus as consuls; [---] the gardener; [---] the sacristan; [---] Amarantus; [---] the prefect of Linius; under Gaius Antistius Vetus and Marcus Suillius Nerullinus as consuls; [---]us the steward; [---]nus the steward; Cosmus; Cautus; under Tiberius Claudius Augustus V and Servius Cornelius Orphitus as consuls; // ; Tiberius Claudius Daphnus; [[---]]; Euphemus the doorkeeper; Claudia Faustina; Aetoria Phlogis; Claudia Hellas with sums of money; under Gaius Lucius Telesinus and Gaius Suetonius Paullinus as consuls; Pannychus; Sita from the infirmary; Aulus Caecilius Atisci[---]; Claudia Corinthia; [[---]]; under Lucius Julius Rufus as consul; Claudia Tyche; Tiberius Claudius, son of Quintus Pontus, the prefect; Antonius Faustus; Albanus the steward; under Publius Galerius Trachalus as consul; Antonia Musa; Claudia Zosime; Iulia Secunda with sums of money; under Sulpicius Galba II and Titus Vinius Rufinus(?) as consuls; [---]allus; [---]s; [---]nus.
EDCS-32805485.summary: This fragmentary inscription lists numerous individuals, possibly recipients of payments or beneficiaries, along with their occupations and names, dated across multiple consular years from the 1st to 2nd centuries CE.

EDCS-32900010.translation: For Valeria Nice, from the estates of Plotina Augusta, product of the pottery workshop, under Paetinus and Apronianus as consuls.
EDCS-32900010.summary: This inscription marks a pottery product, dedicating it to Valeria Nice and originating from the estates of Plotina Augusta, dated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-33000001.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Tiberius Claudius Celadus, deserving of favor, from Mammia Sympherusa.
EDCS-33000001.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Tiberius Claudius Celadus, set up by Mammia Sympherusa.

EDCS-33000004.translation: To Alcimus, who lived 50 years, 2 months, and 15 days. From Nice, his wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-33000004.summary: This is a tombstone inscription commemorating Alcimus, who lived to be 50 years, 2 months, and 15 days, and was honored by his wife Nice.

EDCS-33000005.translation: To the shades of the departed. Gaius Iulius Priscus to his well-deserving wife Antonia Chysdis, who lived 35 years, 7 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-33000005.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antonia Chysdis, erected by her husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000008.translation: To the shades of the departed. Salvia Sympherusa to her dearest husband Publius Aelius Perigenes.
EDCS-33000008.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Perigenes, dedicated by his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000009.translation: Sacred to the shades of the departed. Cnaeus Aius Felix lived 19 years. Charis to her well-deserving son.
EDCS-33000009.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cnaeus Aius Felix, erected by his mother Charis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000012.translation: ... to [his] most innocent... who lived 7 years, 8 months, and 3 days. Gaius Claudius Q. and Afrodisia made [this] for their sweetest son.
EDCS-33000012.summary: A funerary epitaph for an unnamed child, erected by his parents Gaius Claudius Q. and Afrodisia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000013.translation: To the shades of the departed. For Lucius Faenius Alcinus.
EDCS-33000013.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Faenius Alcinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000016.translation: To the shades of the departed. ... Caecilius ... tianus ... [dedicated this] to their most beloved son Caecilius Genialis, who lived 24 years, [and?] 10 months, a well-deserving person, [he?] made [this].
EDCS-33000016.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilius Genialis, likely erected by his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000017.translation: To the shades of the departed. Aviania Maximilla to her well-deserving husband Publius Rufinus Successus, who lived more or less 60 years. [This was made] by Clodia Felicissima. Plot granted.
EDCS-33000017.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Rufinus Successus, erected by his wife Aviania Maximilla, also noting the granting of the burial plot by Clodia Felicissima, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000018.translation: To the shades of the departed. Nigidia Secunda to her well-deserving husband Sextus Satius Ephemu(s).
EDCS-33000018.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Satius Ephemus, erected by his wife Nigidia Secunda, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000020.translation: Sacred to the shades of the departed. Aspasius lived 24 years, 5 months, and 21 days. Heron, Dynamis, and Abdas made [this] for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-33000020.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aspasius, erected by his parents Heron, Dynamis, and Abdas, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000021.translation: To the shades of the departed. Exorata lived 6 years...
EDCS-33000021.summary: A funerary epitaph for Exorata, likely for a child, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33000025.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Sabina, daughter of Gorgias, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-33000025.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sabina, daughter of Gorgias, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-33000028.text_edition: \n AVGEN Aulus Avianius Cilo vixit annos VII dies LVI Aulus Avianius Vestalis pater\n
EDCS-33000028.translation: Aulus Avianius Cilo lived 7 years and 56 days. His father, Aulus Avianius Vestalis.
EDCS-33000028.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Avianius Cilo, who died at a young age, set up by his father.

EDCS-33000031.translation: Secunda, freedwoman of Augustus, a weaver, lived 24 years.
EDCS-33000031.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Secunda, a freedwoman of Augustus and a weaver, who died at the age of 24.

EDCS-33000032.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Amullius Epaphroditus.
EDCS-33000032.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Amullius Epaphroditus.

EDCS-33000286.translation: From the estates of our Emperor Tiberius Claudius Irenaeus, when Paetinus and Apronianus were consuls.
EDCS-33000286.summary: This inscription records the management of imperial estates under the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus, likely during the reign of Tiberius Claudius.

EDCS-33000292.translation: From the estates of our Emperor Tiberius Claudius Irenaeus, when Paetinus and Apronianus were consuls.
EDCS-33000292.summary: This inscription records the management of imperial estates under the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus, likely during the reign of Tiberius Claudius.

EDCS-33000293.translation: From the estates of our Emperor Tiberius Claudius Irenaeus, when Paetinus and Apronianus were consuls.
EDCS-33000293.summary: This inscription records the management of imperial estates under the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus, likely during the reign of Tiberius Claudius.

EDCS-33200001.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Quintus Sittius Flaccus, primipilaris tribune of the 10th Praetorian Cohort. Anicia Caecilia, daughter of Marcus, made this for her excellent husband.
EDCS-33200001.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Anicia Caecilia for her husband, Quintus Sittius Flaccus, a high-ranking officer in the Praetorian Guard.

EDCS-33400631.summary: This inscription likely refers to property or a dedication associated with Aurelius Caesar.

EDCS-33400723.translation: Publius Helvius, son of Publius, a quaestor, augur, [at his own expense] // for Augustus.
EDCS-33400723.summary: This is a dedication by Publius Helvius, a local magistrate and priest, likely for the emperor Augustus, funded by himself.

EDCS-33400724.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aemilius Genesius made this hypogeum, 14 feet long and 14 feet wide, for his well-deserving wife Plautia Euplia, and for himself and his well-deserving freedwoman Aemilia Athenais. Whoever opens this hypogeum after the three burials above it, or brings in and places a dead person or the bones of the dead, will have to pay a penalty of 10,000 sesterces to the Republic of Puteoli.
EDCS-33400724.summary: A funerary inscription from Puteoli, dated to the imperial period, detailing the construction of a hypogeum and specifying penalties for unauthorized burials.

EDCS-33400726.translation: For her well-being and that of her family, Luccia Primitiva promised this vow to Fortuna Taurianensis on the twelfth day before the Kalends of August.
EDCS-33400726.summary: A votive inscription from Puteoli, likely of the imperial period, recording a promise made to the goddess Fortuna.

EDCS-33400732.translation: For Decimus Lucretius Valens, son of Decimus, of the Menenia tribe, who was honored by Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus with public equestrian rank, admitted into the order of decurions at age eight, and later served as aedile and judge. Here, with his father, he presented 35 pairs of gladiators along with their substitutes and a legitimate hunt. The order of decurions decreed that for his funeral he should receive 3,000 sesterces, a burial plot, public praise, and an equestrian statue in the forum to be paid for from public funds. The Augustales, pagans, and their attendants, the Fortunales(?), the scabillarii, and the Foreses also decreed that pedestrial statues and shields be provided. He lived for [age].
EDCS-33400732.summary: A funerary inscription for Decimus Lucretius Valens from Puteoli, likely dating to the mid-1st century CE, detailing his career honors, lavish games, and the public honors decreed for his funeral by various civic bodies.

EDCS-33500407.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Antonius Communis lived for 24 years.
EDCS-33500407.summary: A simple tombstone inscription from an unknown location, likely of the imperial period, recording the name and age of the deceased.

EDCS-33500408.translation: To the Divine Manes. Callistus, freedman, made this for Claudia Genice.
EDCS-33500408.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown location, likely of the imperial period, recording the maker and the deceased.

EDCS-34000010.translation: To the Great Genius of the Colony of Puteoli. Marcus Aurelius Hilario, freedman of Augustus, devoted to the divinity of this place, gave and dedicated this gift.
EDCS-34000010.summary: A dedication to the Genius of the Colony of Puteoli by an imperial freedman, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-34000011.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iustitia, of Sardinian origin(?), optio of the liburnian galley I, lived for 32 years and served for 17 years. Her incomparable, most beloved husband Manilia Veneria made this for her, a well-deserving person.
EDCS-34000011.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown location, likely of the imperial period, for a woman who served as an optio on a galley and was married to a soldier.

EDCS-34000012.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Gaius Domitius Aquilinus, a soldier ('manipularis') on the trireme Tauro, who lived 28 years and served 7 years. His heirs, Pontius Memor, a centurion, and Iulius Marinus, from the same unit, took care of this; Valerius Romanus remade it for him, a well-deserving person.
EDCS-34000012.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown location, likely of the imperial period, for a Roman sailor who served on a trireme, with his heirs and another individual responsible for the monument.

EDCS-34000013.translation: To the Divine Manes. For [name and rank] of the quadrireme Minerva, who served for 9 years. His heir Salvius Salutaris, optio of the trireme Venere, made this for him, a well-deserving person.
EDCS-34000013.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown location, likely of the imperial period, for a Roman sailor who served on a quadrireme, with his heir and a superior officer making the dedication.

EDCS-34000014.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Gaius Iulius Saturninus, a soldier ('manipularis') on the trireme Salvia, who served 31 years. Maximius Bettius, Aelius Valerianus, and Didia Amazon made this monument.
EDCS-34000014.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown location, likely of the imperial period, for a Roman sailor with a long service record, dedicated by three individuals.

EDCS-34000015.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Titus Terentius Maximus, soldier from the trireme Iove, a Bessian by nationality, who lived 40 years and served 20 years. Caius Julius Philo of the trireme Mercurio, Quintus Domitius Optatus of the quadrireme Minerva, his heirs, with Sulpicius Priscus, optio of the trireme Iove, overseeing, have erected this.
EDCS-34000015.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Terentius Maximus, a soldier, with details about his service and the individuals who commissioned the monument.

EDCS-34000016.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Caius Longinius Maximus, from the liburna ship Clementia, a Pamphylian by nationality, who lived 45 years and served 25 years. Lucius Valerius Valens, a scribe and duplicarius, his heir, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-34000016.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Longinius Maximus, a sailor, detailing his life span and military service, erected by his heir.

EDCS-34000017.translation: Marcus Antonius Crescens, son of Marcus, lived 13 years.
EDCS-34000017.summary: This is a short funerary inscription for Marcus Antonius Crescens, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-34000018.translation: Caius Attius Princeps, son of Caius, of the Palatina tribe, lived 26 years.
EDCS-34000018.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Attius Princeps, a young man, stating his name, father, tribal affiliation, and age at death.

EDCS-34000019.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Aurelia Marciana, an incomparable woman, who lived 25 years and 7 months. Marcus Aurelius Ptolemaeus wrote this for his deserving wife and for their other children and freedmen.
EDCS-34000019.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Marciana, commissioned by her husband, highlighting her exceptional qualities and also including other family members.

EDCS-34000020.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Rebir(a) Primitiva, well-deserving. Julius Maximus lived 60 years.
EDCS-34000020.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rebir(a) Primitiva, stating her name and age at death, commissioned by Julius Maximus.

EDCS-34000021.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Publius Bennis Augendus and Bennis Exoratus and Bennias Charis and their descendants.
EDCS-34000021.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, commemorating multiple members of the Benni family and their descendants.

EDCS-34000023.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Caesias Procula, who lived 39 years and 20 days. Hibius Neptunalis, her son, and Caesia Marciana, her sister, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-34000023.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caesias Procula, detailing her age at death and commissioned by her son and sister.

EDCS-34000024.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Cascellius Ingenuus, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-34000025.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Cascellius Stephanus, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-34000027.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Cincia Tyche, his well-deserving wife, Gaius Ducenius Athenodorus made this.
EDCS-34000027.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his wife, Cincia Tyche.

EDCS-34000028.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Claudia Horaea, her patroness, well-deserving, freedwoman, they made this at their own expense.
EDCS-34000028.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by freedmen for their patroness, Claudia Horaea.

EDCS-34000029.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Claudius Victor lived 22 years.
EDCS-34000029.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Claudius Victor, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-34000030.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cornelia Hermionene, Cornelius Hermogenes and Cornelius Aquilinus made this for their well-deserving mother.
EDCS-34000030.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by sons for their mother, Cornelia Hermionene.

EDCS-34000033.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Feridia Augurina, who lived 9 years and 9 months, her parents made this for their sweetest daughter, well-deserving.
EDCS-34000033.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by parents for their daughter, Feridia Augurina, recording her age at death.

EDCS-34000034.translation: Of the slaves of Fulvius Menophilus: in front 12 feet, in the field 16 feet.
EDCS-34000034.summary: This inscription likely records land boundaries or dimensions associated with the slaves of Fulvius Menophilus.

EDCS-34000035.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gaudentius, who lived 12 years and 12 months, Publicius Ianuarius made this for his pupil.
EDCS-34000035.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by Publicius Ianuarius for his pupil, Gaudentius, recording his age at death.

EDCS-34000036.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Genucius Aeros lived 2 months and 7 days.
EDCS-34000036.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and extremely short lifespan of Lucius Genucius Aeros.

EDCS-34000037.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Ignatius Fuscus lived 1 year and 11 months.
EDCS-34000037.summary: This funerary epitaph records the name and lifespan of Lucius Ignatius Fuscus.

EDCS-34000039.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Julia Priscilla, who lived 3 years and 31 days, her mother made this for her sweetest daughter.
EDCS-34000039.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by a mother for her daughter, Julia Priscilla, recording her age at death.

EDCS-34000040.translation: To the departed spirits. Helvius Martialis, a grieving father, made this for his cherished adopted daughter Iuliaes Trheptenis, who lived for 6 years, 11 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-34000040.summary: A funerary epitaph for an adopted daughter, set up by her grieving father.

EDCS-34000041.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Naevius Asper made this for his most beloved son, Publius Iuventius Celsus, who lived for 16 years.
EDCS-34000041.summary: A funerary epitaph for a son, set up by his father.

EDCS-34000046.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Oppius Eutychetes Magonianus, his most beloved son, who lived for 10 years and 3 days.
EDCS-34000046.summary: A funerary epitaph for a son who died at a young age.

EDCS-34000047.translation: To the departed spirits. Popilia Zenis made this for her well-deserving patroness Popila, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-34000047.summary: A funerary epitaph for a patroness, set up by her freedwoman.

EDCS-34000048.translation: Marcus Salonius Celsus for himself and for Egnatia Agele and for his son Marcus Salonius Celsus, who lived for 13 years, and for his family.
EDCS-34000048.summary: A funerary epitaph for a man, his wife, his son, and his family.

EDCS-34000049.translation: To the departed spirits. [---] for his son, Gaius Seius Aga[---], who was buried here.
EDCS-34000049.summary: A funerary epitaph, partially restored, for a son.

EDCS-34000050.translation: To the departed spirits. Sela Olympia for her wife [---], with whom she lived for 10 years [---] without any complaint; she lived for 39 years, 3 months [---], and 18 days. She made this.
EDCS-34000050.summary: A funerary epitaph for a wife, set up by her surviving partner, mentioning their years together and the deceased's lifespan.

EDCS-34000051.translation: To the departed spirits. Septymia Elpis for herself, for Publius Trofymus her husband, and for Sextus Prosdocimus, Caia Septymia, Sextus Maximus, and Rodine, her freedwomen, and for Publius Trofymus and her freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-34000051.summary: A funerary epitaph for a woman, establishing provisions for herself, her husband, freedpersons, and descendants.

EDCS-34000052.translation: Hail! Titinia Danae, freedwoman of Seleucus, lived for 18 years.
EDCS-34000052.summary: A funerary epitaph for a freedwoman who died at a young age.

EDCS-34000053.translation: To the departed spirits. Valeria Nice made this for her well-deserving adopted daughter Valerius Arsenoe, who lived for 8 years, 8 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-34000053.summary: A funerary epitaph for an adopted daughter, set up by her adoptive mother.

EDCS-34000054.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Demetrius made this for his wife, Vibiae Protenis.
EDCS-34000054.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Demetrius to his wife, Vibiae Protenis.

EDCS-34000056.translation: So that you may know, guest, my name was Briseis. I had not yet completed seventeen times seven months, but my fate was unjust; cruel death snatched me away from my parents and my Albano before I could live more years.
EDCS-34000056.summary: This epitaph records the premature death of a young girl named Briseis, who died before reaching 119 months of age.

EDCS-34000057.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Clytius.
EDCS-34000057.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph for someone named Clytius.

EDCS-34000058.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Rufina made this for her most dutiful and sweetest son, Demetrius Eutychus. He lived here for 16 years and 5 days.
EDCS-34000058.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Demetrius Eutychus, who lived for 16 years and 5 days, and was commemorated by his mother Rufina.

EDCS-34000060.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fortunatus Eutyches lived for 5 years and 5 months. His parents made this.
EDCS-34000060.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Fortunatus Eutyches, who lived for 5 years and 5 months, and was commemorated by his parents.

EDCS-34000061.translation: Paris, the mulio, beloved of O[...], is buried here.
EDCS-34000061.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Paris, a mulio (mule driver), who was the beloved of someone.

EDCS-34000062.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---]lo Rufus. Rufus, his patron, [---] for the deserving. He lived 20 years [---] days.
EDCS-34000062.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates a man named Rufus, who lived 20 years and was honored by his patron, also named Rufus.

EDCS-34000073.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, mentioning Lucius Balabius Eutactus.

EDCS-34000074.translation: Of Egnatius Certus and his son, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-34000074.summary: This inscription likely records the names and association of Egnatius Certus and his son, possibly as part of a larger monument or dedication.

EDCS-34000075.summary: This inscription is a dedication or commemoration of Publius Manlius Modestus.

EDCS-34000076.translation: Of Publius Octavius Luttatus Quintilianus, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-34000076.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Publius Octavius Luttatus Quintilianus, likely honoring him as a distinguished individual.

EDCS-34000367.translation: To Gaius Apollonius Priscus, a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, a native of Alexandria, who lived 40 years. Lucius Fabullius Serenus, a duplicarius of the same fleet, his heir, made this.
EDCS-34000367.summary: This funerary inscription honors Gaius Apollonius Priscus, a soldier in the Misenum fleet, with his heir Lucius Fabullius Serenus as the dedicator, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-34000368.translation: Marius Xenophon made this for Marius Priscus, patron, well deserving. [---] of the Praetorian fleet of Misenum [---] who lived 3 years, 10 months [---] made for him well deserving.
EDCS-34000368.summary: This funerary inscription records that Marius Xenophon erected a monument for his patron Marius Priscus, a member of the Misenum fleet, who died young.

EDCS-34100470.translation: To Claudia Laudica, freedwoman of the emperor, who lived 55 years.
EDCS-34100470.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Laudica, a freedwoman of an emperor, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-34100471.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Trebia Veneria lived 7 years, 6 months, 3 days. Her father made this.
EDCS-34100471.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Trebia Veneria, who died at the age of 7 years, 6 months, and 3 days, erected by her father.

EDCS-34100475.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Allius Felix, who lived 21 years, 6 months, 16 days. Lucius Allius Felix and Gavia Successa for their dearest son.
EDCS-34100475.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Allius Felix at the age of 21 and is dedicated by his parents, Lucius Allius Felix and Gavia Successa.

EDCS-34100476.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Calvia Successa, who lived 53 years, 4 months, 13 days. Lucius Allius Felix made this for his incomparable, well-deserving wife.
EDCS-34100476.summary: This funerary inscription honors Calvia Successa, who died at the age of 53, and was erected by her husband Lucius Allius Felix.

EDCS-34100479.translation: The colonists who contributed to the statue: Tiberius Iulius Pistus, Lucius Salvius Mercurialis, Publius Manneius Priscus, Tiberius Claudius Vitalis, Lucius Vipstanius Zeno, Gnaeus Mat(i)cus Valens, Caius Pomponius Amandus, Lucius Domitius Silvanus, Caius Butronius Agrippa, Publius Vocconius Adauctus, Marcus Brittius Mercurialis, Tiberius Claudius Clemens, Caius Verrius Aemilianus, Lucius Manneius Capitolinus the father, Caius Naevius Callistus, Quintus Decimius Iustianus, Lucius Larcius Cornelianus, Publius Mussius Felix, Publius Mussius Proculus, Caius Carrinas Felixs, Caius Carrinas Suavis, Marcus Luscidius Tertius, Caius Iulius Proculus, Tiberius Iulius Faustinus, Caius Pomponius Augustalis, Caius Pomponius Zosimus, Marcus Tatius Largus, Quintus Servilius Marianus, Lucius Manneius Philantus, Lucius Manneius Capitolinus the son, Lucius Manneius Nereus, Lucius Manneius Manneianus. Lucius Pa[---]us [---], Lucius Patulcius Celsus, Caius Cornelius Grat[u]s, Caius Manlius Amoenius, Quintus Aecilius Phoebus, Caius Aecilius Hermes, Caius Avilius Sarinianus, Caius Avilius Natalis, Lucius Statius S[---]s, Sextus Iulius Ingenu[u]s, Lucius Pompeius Maximianu[s], Lucius Pompeius Priscus, Publius Sarronius Pius, Caius Avilius [---]us, Publius Sempron[i]us Restitu[t]us, Caius Iulius [---]us, Publius Aemilius R[---]nus, Caius Iulius Philippus, Titus Flavius Faustus, Caius Arrius Saturninus, Lucius Aristius A[---]mus, Publius Acilius Acil[i]anus, Tiberius Claudius Narcissus, Marcus Vettius Satu[r]ninus, Lucius Furius Secundinus, Caius Octavius [---]monus, Marcus [---]ianus, Gnaeus Maticius Valens junior, Caius Iulius [---]l[---]tas, Caius Mevius [---]i[---]om[---], Quintus Terentius Saeclaris, Caius Scatienus Macrinus, Sextus Apuleius N[a]talis, Tiberius Claudius Attalus, Sextus Iulius Rufus, Tiberius Claudius Primianus. Quintus Annius Hymnus, Publius [---]s Priscus, Publius Cincius Philetus, Servius Sulpicius Lar[---]us, Servius Sulpicius Iulianus, Servius Sulpicius Saturninus, Tiberius Claudius Atimetus, Caius Manlius Geminus, Quintus Decimius Capito, Caius Avilius Fortunatus, Aulus Ofilius Marcianus, Caius Carrinas Felicissimus, Caius Carrinas Fortunatus, Lucius Iulius Earinus, Aulus Sittius Euhodus, Caius Fundanius Celsus, Caius Fundanius Tyrannus, Aulus Sittius Diodorus, Tiberius Claudius Axoranus.
EDCS-34100479.summary: This inscription lists the names of numerous colonists who contributed to the erection of a statue, indicating a collective civic project likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-34100583.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or ownership marker for Quintus Pomponius Maternus.

EDCS-34100584.summary: This inscription likely indicates ownership or is a dedication to Pontia Hepyra.

EDCS-34100585.summary: This is a dedication to Ulpia Marciana, likely a member of the imperial family.

EDCS-34200204.translation: To Marcus Veius Marcellus, while alive, a place for a monument by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-34200204.summary: This inscription records a plot of land granted for a monument to Marcus Veius Marcellus by local councillors.

EDCS-34300747.translation: To Alfia Servilla, freedwoman of Numerius.
EDCS-34300747.summary: This is a dedication to Alfia Servilla, a freedwoman.

EDCS-34300751.translation: To Festa, daughter of Apuleius, she lived 17 years.
EDCS-34300751.summary: This tombstone records the life and death of Festa, daughter of Apuleius, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-34300752.summary: This tombstone records the death of Ianuarius at the age of 2.

EDCS-34300753.translation: To Publius Mancius Diogenes, freedman of Publius, according to the will and by the arbitration of Mancia Dorinis, freedwoman of Publius.
EDCS-34300753.summary: This inscription records the bequest of property to Publius Mancius Diogenes, a freedman, by Mancia Dorinis.

EDCS-34300754.summary: This tombstone records the death of Saturnina at the age of 20.

EDCS-34300756.translation: To Titia Optata, freedwoman of the woman.
EDCS-34300756.summary: This is a dedication to Titia Optata, a freedwoman.

EDCS-34300757.translation: To Marcus Tullius, son of Marcus, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-34300757.summary: This inscription records a grant or honour bestowed upon Marcus Tullius by local councillors.

EDCS-34300758.translation: To Tutia Licentia, freedwoman of the woman.
EDCS-34300758.summary: This is a dedication to Tutia Licentia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-34300759.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Conva Veiaes, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-34300760.translation: To the departed spirits. Pomponia Tyche lived 19 years, 9 months, and 23 days. Marcus Volcius Nucerinus made this for her, well-deserving, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-34300760.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponia Tyche, detailing her age at death and stating that Marcus Volcius Nucerinus dedicated the monument to her and his family.

EDCS-34300765.translation: To the departed spirits. C. Aeclanius Fortunatus, councilor of the Aeclani, twice chief magistrate, and a distinguished holder of public office, lived 35 years, 11 months, and 1 day. Aeclanius Iovanus, his freedman, dedicated this.
EDCS-34300765.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates C. Aeclanius Fortunatus, detailing his life and public offices, and notes it was dedicated by his freedman.

EDCS-34300766.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Ancharius Primus, his son Primus and his wife Successa made this for him, well-deserving. He lived 36 years.
EDCS-34300766.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Quintus Ancharius Primus, stating it was erected by his son and wife and noting his age at death.

EDCS-34300769.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Insteius Maximus lived 20 years and 9 months. Munatius Hilarianus made this for his deserving brother.
EDCS-34300769.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by Munatius Hilarianus to his brother, Gaius Insteius Maximus, and states his age at death.

EDCS-34300770.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Marius Venustus, son of Lucius of the Palatine tribe, who lived 18 years, 4 months, and 17 days, and for his mother Atilia Felicula, and for his deserving father Lucius Marius.
EDCS-34300770.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Lucius Marius Venustus, his mother Atilia Felicula, and his father Lucius Marius, detailing the ages of Lucius Marius Venustus at death.

EDCS-34300771.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Orbius Primitivus, father, well-deserving. His son arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-34300771.summary: This funerary epitaph was arranged by a son for his well-deserving father, Lucius Orbius Primitivus.

EDCS-34300772.translation: To the departed spirits. For Publius Plotus Sabinus, father, most devoted, well-deserving. Iunia Priscilla made this.
EDCS-34300772.summary: This funerary epitaph was made by Iunia Priscilla for her devoted and well-deserving father, Publius Plotus Sabinus.

EDCS-34400144.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Insteius Phosphorus lived 44 years. Insteius Hesper made this for his dearest brother, and Insteia Pelagia for her most devoted wife, well-deserving.
EDCS-34400144.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Quintus Insteius Phosphorus and was erected by his brother Insteius Hesper and his wife Insteia Pelagia.

EDCS-34400145.summary: This inscription likely marks the tomb or property of Quintus Vilius Flaccus.

EDCS-34400146.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Iulia Saturnina, who lived about 18 years, 9 months, and 4 days. Iulius Atenororus, soldier, made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-34400146.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Saturnina, set up by her husband, a soldier named Iulius Atenororus.

EDCS-34400147.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Peticia Benaria, who lived 35 years, for his most holy, well-deserving wife. Lucius Plaetorius Primitibus.
EDCS-34400147.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Peticia Benaria, dedicated by her husband, Lucius Plaetorius Primitibus.

EDCS-34400148.translation: To [---]llius O[---]. Iulia Romana made this for her well-deserving husband while alive, with her children. She lived 11 years.
EDCS-34400148.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for a husband, set up by his wife Iulia Romana and their children, and also records the wife's short lifespan.

EDCS-34400149.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Corinthias. Marcus Ulpius Daphnus made this for his well-deserving tent-mate.
EDCS-34400149.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Corinthias, dedicated by his tent-mate Marcus Ulpius Daphnus.

EDCS-34400151.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aurellius Tertullus Hemeteri.
EDCS-34400151.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurellius Tertullus Hemeteri, with no dedicator named.

EDCS-34400152.translation: For Annia Procula. Annius Zosimus made this for his well-deserving wife, who lived 20 years, 2 months, and 12 days.
EDCS-34400152.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annia Procula, set up by her husband Annius Zosimus.

EDCS-34400153.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Apisia Prima, Apisius Philinus made this for his most sweet and loving wife, and for Apisia Vitalis, for her most dutiful mother. She lived 37 years, 11 months, and 2 days.
EDCS-34400153.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Apisia Prima and her mother Apisia Vitalis, set up by Apisius Philinus.

EDCS-34400154.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cattius Paramynthius, who lived 32 years and 3 months. Iulia Caesia made this for her well-deserving wife.
EDCS-34400154.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cattius Paramynthius, set up by his wife Iulia Caesia.

EDCS-34400155.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sacred to Charis.
EDCS-34400155.summary: This is a funerary dedication, possibly a tombstone, to Charis.

EDCS-34400157.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Curio Eristus, dearest and most dutiful son, who lived 9 years and 8 months. His father and mother [made this].
EDCS-34400157.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Curio Eristus, a young boy, dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-34400158.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Geminius Hagnus, freedman, made this for his well-deserving Geminia Helpidi.
EDCS-34400158.summary: A freedman sets up a tombstone for Geminia Helpidi, likely his wife or relative, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34400159.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary monument, mentioning Caius Hostius Mercator, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34400160.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Hostius Helius made this for his most dutiful son, Caius Hostius Protogenes, who lived 19 years.
EDCS-34400160.summary: A father sets up a tombstone for his son, Caius Hostius Protogenes, who died at age 19, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34400161.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Her father gave this to Ingenuus, a most sweet infant, who lived 5 years and 2 months.
EDCS-34400161.summary: A father dedicates a tombstone to his infant son, Ingenuus, who died at a very young age, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34400162.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. The impious father made this for his most sweet son, Quintus Lucceius Concordianus, who lived two years, 11 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-34400162.summary: A father sets up a tombstone for his son, Quintus Lucceius Concordianus, who died in early childhood, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34400163.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcia Phileta, wife of Anthus.
EDCS-34400163.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Marcia Phileta, wife of Anthus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34400164.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Manius Mummius Priscus for himself, and for his mother, and for Mummius [---], and for Vibpsan[---]
EDCS-34400164.summary: A funerary inscription by Manius Mummius Priscus for himself and other family members, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34400165.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---] Primitia, for her well-deserving [---] of Nigra, [---] of Publius Herennius, who lived 63 years.
EDCS-34400165.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription, possibly for Primitia, daughter of Nigra and possibly related to Publius Herennius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34400166.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Decimus Rupilius Anencletus, her deserving husband, who lived 65 years, 2 months, and 15 days. His wife Hilara made this.
EDCS-34400166.summary: A wife, Hilara, sets up a tombstone for her husband, Decimus Rupilius Anencletus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34400167.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Soter, freedman of Augustus, a physician.
EDCS-34400167.summary: A tombstone for Soter, a physician and freedman of Emperor Augustus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34400168.translation: To the departed spirits. Sulpicia Thalussa to Sulpicia Soteris, well-deserving.
EDCS-34400168.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Sulpicia Thalussa to Sulpicia Soteris, likely a freedwoman or family member, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34500007.translation: In the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, Zosimus, son of Annius Verus.
EDCS-34500007.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording the name of Zosimus, son of Annius Verus, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-34500051.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, Alcidis.
EDCS-34500051.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording the name of Alcidis during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-34500052.translation: A dolium from the pottery works of Anterus, freedman of our Caesar, in the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-34500052.summary: This inscription records a dolium (large earthenware jar) from the pottery works of Anterus, a freedman of the emperor, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-34500054.translation: A dolium from the pottery works of Anterus, freedman of our Caesar, in the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-34500054.summary: This inscription records a dolium (large earthenware jar) from the pottery works of Anterus, a freedman of the emperor, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-34600003.translation: Spes Arellia Musa, freedwoman of Lucius, pious and holy, lived 81 years with her fellow freedman Epaphroditus.
EDCS-34600003.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Spes Arellia Musa, recording her age and relationship with her fellow freedman Epaphroditus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34600005.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Valerius Beryllus to his most dutiful son, who lived 17 years, 11 months, and 11 days. My dearest son, this tomb was built by the piety of a very poor father, worthy of a greater monument.
EDCS-34600005.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Marcus Valerius Beryllus to his young son, detailing his age and expressing the father's grief and limited means, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34600006.translation: To the departed spirits. Valerius Speratus and Valerius Primus made this for their incomparable mother, Valeria Artemisia, who lived 50 years.
EDCS-34600006.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Valerius Speratus and Valerius Primus to their mother, Valeria Artemisia, noting her age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34600007.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Aemilius Alexander, veteran of the III Triremes squadron, lived 40 years. Alexandria made this for her well-deserving father.
EDCS-34600007.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from Alexandria to her father, Lucius Aemilius Alexander, a naval veteran, noting his age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34600008.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Marcus Valerius Alexandrus, a common sailor of the trireme Concordia, by nation (?).
EDCS-34600008.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Alexandrus, a sailor on the trireme Concordia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34600009.translation: To Gaius Ovius Sollemnis, son of Spurius, who lived 13 years, 1 month, and 10 days. Tiberius Claudius Severus, prefect with judicial authority, and his father Tiberius Claudius Honoratus, duovir and patron, and his mother Ovia Tyche, dedicate this monument or tomb, which will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-34600009.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by parents for their deceased son, Gaius Ovius Sollemnis, and records the parents' civic and familial titles, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34600010.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Gaius Julius Hermas, freedman, a seller of incense. Agathangelus, freedman, and his wife Licinia Nais.
EDCS-34600010.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Hermas, a freedman and incense seller, dedicated by his freedman colleague and his wife, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34600012.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Titus Flavius Castrensis. Doryphorus, his father, for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-34600012.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Castrensis, dedicated by his father Doryphorus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34600019.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 16th time, consul for the 3rd time. By decree of the decurions of the colony of Liternum.
EDCS-34600019.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, listing his titles and honors, likely erected by the colony of Liternum and dating to his reign (161-180 AD).

EDCS-34700004.text_edition: \n cum publica im ur prole loca riam Geminam m endi aedificandique undique publice posuit F IIIIvir iure dicundo recitand\n
EDCS-34700004.translation: --- with public from the public offspring --- Gemina --- to build and --- from all sides publicly erected F (?). --- IIIIvir with judicial authority ---
EDCS-34700004.summary: This inscription appears to record the public erection of a building or structure by a 'Gemina' and mentions a IIIIvir with judicial authority, with much of the text lost or illegible, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34700005.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a dedication to the god Volturnus by Publius Varius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34700006.translation: Sacred to the shades of the underworld. --- for Quartilla --- Junius Acilius, son, to his mother, and Junius Acilius, to his excellent and deserving wife.
EDCS-34700006.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Junius Acilius to his mother Quartilla and his wife, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34700009.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Marcus Agrippa, who was consul in 43, 37, and 28 BC, likely dating to the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-34700015.translation: Quintus Fufius Licinus, son of Quintus, made [this monument] for himself while alive, and for Quintia Zosima, freedwoman of Gaius. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-34700015.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Fufius Licinus and Quintia Zosima, detailing that it was built by Licinus for himself and Zosima while alive, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34700024.summary: This inscription names Eucarpi, a freedman of the emperor Augustus, possibly as the owner or dedicatee of a monument.

EDCS-34700041.translation: To the Divine Shades. Flavia Euagria made this for her most dutiful husband, Titus Flavius Caralitanus, for his piety.
EDCS-34700041.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Flavia Euagria to her husband, Titus Flavius Caralitanus, honoring his piety, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-34700044.translation: [---] the freedman Phaenippus [---] had undertaken to have constructed the [opus pilarum(?) and the substructures] at his own expense.
EDCS-34700044.summary: This fragmentary inscription records that the freedman Phaenippus paid for the construction of pilasters and substructures, suggesting a public works project.

EDCS-34700049.translation: Belonging to the Quintilii. Quintus Athenaeus, freedman of Augustus, made this.
EDCS-34700049.summary: This inscription indicates that Quintus Athenaeus, a freedman of Augustus, created this object, possibly a tomb or a private monument for the Quintilii family.

EDCS-34700056.translation: Antonina made this for her husband Amphion, most sweet, well-deserving.
EDCS-34700056.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Antonina to her beloved and deserving husband, Amphion, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-34700058.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria lived 45 years. Tiberius Claudius Anoptes made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-34700058.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Valeria at age 45 and is a dedication from her husband, Tiberius Claudius Anoptes.

EDCS-34700060.translation: To the Divine Shades. Herennius Saturnus made this for his well-deserving wife, Firmina.
EDCS-34700060.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Herennius Saturnus to his wife Firmina, honoring her deserving nature.

EDCS-34700061.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Ulpius Zoticus, freedman, made this for the well-deserving Marcus Ulpius Eutyches.
EDCS-34700061.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from the freedman Marcus Ulpius Zoticus to Marcus Ulpius Eutyches, likely his patron.

EDCS-34700065.translation: Iulia Aphrodisia Veneria, freedwoman of the deified Augustus.
EDCS-34700065.summary: This inscription identifies Iulia Aphrodisia Veneria as a freedwoman of the deified Emperor Augustus, possibly marking a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-34700066.translation: Expectatus, the darling of Gaius Fannius Anterus and Fannia Scynda, lived for two years and 45 days.
EDCS-34700066.summary: This inscription records the short life of Expectatus, a beloved child of Gaius Fannius Anterus and Fannia Scynda, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-34700158.translation: To the departed spirits. To Valeria Gratilla, who lived 27 years, 3 months, and 9 days. Ancharius [---]attes made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-34700158.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Gratilla, set up by her husband Ancharius.

EDCS-34700250.text_edition: \n Marco Fabio Marci filio Marci nepoti Falerna Onesimiano\n
EDCS-34700250.translation: To Marcus Fabio Onesimianus, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, of the Falerna tribe.
EDCS-34700250.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription for Marcus Fabio Onesimianus, likely from the early imperial period, noting his name and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-34700251.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Iulius Mero, son of Marcus, of the Palatina tribe, from the Thelesinorum, pontifex [---]. Iulia [---] daughter Iulia [---] to be made [---]
EDCS-34700251.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius Mero, a pontifex, with incomplete dedicatory information from his daughters.

EDCS-34700252.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph or memorial for Marcus Satrius Scipio.

EDCS-34700254.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelius Restutus made this for his well-deserving wife Aurelia Hilaritas, with whom he lived 19 years. She lived 31 years.
EDCS-34700254.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Hilaritas, set up by her husband Aurelius Restutus, noting their marital duration and her lifespan.

EDCS-34700256.translation: [---] to Calpurnius(?) Fortunatus, who lived [---]X years and 25 days. Severus [---] dearest [---]
EDCS-34700256.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Calpurnius(?) Fortunatus, with a partial dedication from Severus.

EDCS-34700261.translation: To the departed spirits. For my husband Marcus Statio Hylas, with whom I lived 40 years, and for my sweetest daughter Sabina, and for themselves, and their freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-34700261.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Statio Hylas, set up by his wife, also commemorating their daughter, freedmen, and descendants.

EDCS-34700265.translation: To the departed spirits. For Passer, from Martialis, his patron.
EDCS-34700265.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Passer, set up by his patron Martialis.

EDCS-34700266.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Marcus Plinius Silenus, who lived 27 years and 11 days. Grassidia Secunda [made this] for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-34700266.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Plinius Silenus, set up by his wife Grassidia Secunda, noting his age at death.

EDCS-34700270.translation: The bones of Marcus Ti[l]ius Blastes are placed here. Tillia Phsuche made this from her own funds.
EDCS-34700270.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating the burial place of Marcus Ti[l]ius Blastes, funded by Tillia Phsuche.

EDCS-34801112.translation: Here lies Ulpia, a most innocent woman, who lived
EDCS-34801112.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Ulpia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34801113.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Neria, daughter of Gaius, who lived for years [ ] months 3.
EDCS-34801113.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Neria, son of Gaius, indicating her lifespan and dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34801121.translation: The bones of Aristonicus rest well here.
EDCS-34801121.summary: This funerary inscription marks the resting place of Aristonicus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34801122.summary: This is a dedication, possibly funerary, from Aucta to her nurse, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34801126.translation: The bones of Lucius Memmius Hilarius, freedman of Lucius, are placed here.
EDCS-34801126.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Memmius Hilarius, a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34801127.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Genice, freedwoman of Lucius Minutius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34801128.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, mentioning Murellia and Philemation, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34801130.translation: Opsia dedicated an altar to Fides
EDCS-34801130.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Opsia to the goddess Fides, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34801134.translation: Publius Planius Diomedes, freedman of Publius, and Lychnis, freedwoman of [a woman], and Auctus, freedman.
EDCS-34801134.summary: This inscription records the social connections and freed status of Publius Planius Diomedes, Lychnis, and Auctus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34801135.translation: Rufa, freedwoman of Publius Vorena, Ge
EDCS-34801135.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely funerary, mentioning Rufa, a freedwoman of Publius Vorena, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34801141.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Zosima Thalamium mater misera\n
EDCS-34801141.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Zosima to Thalamium, her miserable mother.
EDCS-34801141.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Zosima to her mother Thalamium.

EDCS-34801144.translation: The remains of Philippus, freedman of Aulus Postumius Albinus, are placed here.
EDCS-34801144.summary: This is an epitaph recording the burial place of Philippus, freedman of Aulus Postumius Albinus.

EDCS-34801356.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Ascellius Posdocimus, who lived [---] years.
EDCS-34801356.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Ascellius Posdocimus, with the age at death missing.

EDCS-34900259.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Valerio Hymno Valeria Marci filia Sabina parenti pientissimo\n
EDCS-34900259.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valeria Sabina, daughter of Marcus Valerius Hymnus, for her most dutiful parent.
EDCS-34900259.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Valeria Sabina for her parent, Marcus Valerius Hymnus.

EDCS-34900737.translation: Viciria Aphrodisia, freedwoman of Marcus, made [this] for herself and her family.
EDCS-34900737.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Viciria Aphrodisia for herself and her relatives.

EDCS-34900738.translation: Gaius Naevoleius Chrestus, freedman of Gaius, son of Marcus, from Allifae, Augustalis and quaestor of the Augustales, made [this] for himself and for Caeparia Archeni, wife of Marcus Ahian Primogenus, while alive.
EDCS-34900738.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Naevoleius Chrestus, a prominent freedman and local official, for himself and his wife.

EDCS-34900739.translation: The Mother of the Gods, priestess of the collegium dendrophororum.
EDCS-34900739.summary: This inscription likely records the title of a priestess associated with the cult of the Mother of the Gods and a collegium of tree-bearers.

EDCS-34900760.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a dedication to a freedman named Summus and a woman named Antonina.

EDCS-34900761.translation: [---] to Rufa, his sister / [---] to Irene, his wife / [---] to Saturnina / [---]s / [---]
EDCS-34900761.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a funerary dedication listing several deceased women: Rufa (sister), Irene (wife), and Saturnina.

EDCS-34900762.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or epitaph, with only the beginning of a name and an abbreviation visible.

EDCS-34900767.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Eutichetis, wife of Cornelius, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-34900769.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Numisia Dia, his wife, who lived 35 years, 4 months, and to Plotia Tertulla, his daughter, who lived...
EDCS-34900769.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating Numisia Dia and her daughter Plotia Tertulla, providing their ages at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900770.summary: This fragment appears to be a dedication or funerary text for Gaius Laetilius Leo, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900773.translation: To Gnaeus Placentius, son of Quintus, from the Stellatina tribe. To Gnaeus Placentius Cordus, son of Gnaeus. To Heria Tertia, freedwoman of Gaius. To Heria Venusta, freedwoman of Gaius. To Dignitas, freedwoman of the woman.
EDCS-34900773.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, likely family members or clients, and includes tribal affiliation and manumission status, suggesting a Roman Republican or early Imperial context.

EDCS-34900775.summary: This inscription fragment likely refers to a member of the imperial family, possibly Titus Caesar, and could be a dedication or funerary text from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34900776.translation: The bones of Augerina are laid here. She lived 27 years. Her mother for her daughter.
EDCS-34900776.summary: An epitaph for Augerina, stating her age at death and mentioning her mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35000003.translation: Gaius Marcius Ambitio, son of Gaius, built the temple of Augustus at his own expense.
EDCS-35000003.summary: This is a dedication for the construction of a temple to Augustus by Gaius Marcius Ambitio, funded by himself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35000056.translation: ...of Gaius Marcius Athictus... Marcia Callistes... and for himself and his family.
EDCS-35000056.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a funerary inscription or dedication for Gaius Marcius Athictus and Marcia Callistes, possibly including a self-dedication for their family, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35000058.translation: To the memory of Quintus Urbanus Secundus, who lived 5 years, 11 months, 25 days. Hostilius Fructus and Urbania Secunda, parents, made [this] for their sweetest son, well-deserving.
EDCS-35000058.summary: A moving funerary epitaph for a young child, Quintus Urbanus Secundus, set up by his parents, Hostilius Fructus and Urbania Secunda, during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35001056.translation: Lucius Pacideius Epitynchanus for himself and his family, 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the ground.
EDCS-35001056.summary: This inscription records a land dedication or burial plot reservation by Lucius Pacideius Epitynchanus for himself and his family, specifying the dimensions of the plot, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35001057.translation: To Lucius Lollius, son of Spurius, and to his mother Lollius. Stranger, whoever you are who reads this monument, stop and read to the end. I was once Lucius Lollius, much beloved by my friends; I had lived twenty years, but now miserably, torn from my mother's embrace, I was born [and] taken away... [lost text] ... a wicked [lost text] to the gods, piety thus... My life will be spoken of in fame; other things are of no avail to those who weep. They erected this.
EDCS-35001057.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown date, recounting the life and premature death of Lucius Lollius, who expresses his love for his mother and friends.

EDCS-35001068.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown date, recording the name of Tertia Aemilia Vitalis.

EDCS-35001069.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown date, recording the name of Athemerius.

EDCS-35100431.translation: To the shades below. To Helpidius, sweetest wife. Titus M. Restitutus, son of Titus, with whom she lived 20 years and 2 months.
EDCS-35100431.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Helpidius, dedicated by her husband Titus M. Restitutus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35100437.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Emperor, son of the divine Constantius the Pious. By the lords emperors Valentinianus and Valentius, the Pious, Fortunate Augusti, fathers of the country. 126.
EDCS-35100437.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Emperor Constantine and later emperors Valentinianus and Valentius, likely from the 4th century CE, possibly related to a public work.

EDCS-35100440.translation: To the shades below. To Pompeia Sappho. Hermias, steward of the emperors, made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-35100440.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompeia Sappho, dedicated by her husband Hermias, a steward of the emperors, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35100441.text_edition: \n Praedia Dianae Tifatinae Imperator Caesar Vespasianus Augustus consul VIII fines agrorum dicatorum Dianae Tifatinae a Cornelio Sulla ex forma divi Augusti restituit\n
EDCS-35100441.translation: Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, in his eighth consulship, restored the boundaries of the lands dedicated to Diana Tifatina by Cornelius Sulla, according to the plan of the divine Augustus.
EDCS-35100441.summary: This is a public inscription from 79 CE, recording Emperor Vespasian's restoration of land boundaries dedicated to the goddess Diana Tifatina.

EDCS-35200276.translation: To the shades below. Abascantus, slave of our emperor, steward, made this for his well-deserving wife Fortunata. She lived 30 years. Whoever reads my inscription and says 'May the earth be light on you' wishes you well.
EDCS-35200276.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fortunata, dedicated by her husband Abascantus, a steward for the emperor, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-35400777.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the estates of Claudia Marcellina.
EDCS-35400777.summary: This inscription, dating to 334 CE, records a transaction or distribution related to the estates of Claudia Marcellina.

EDCS-35400813.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the estates of Claudia Marcellina.
EDCS-35400813.summary: This inscription, dating to 334 CE, records a transaction or distribution related to the estates of Claudia Marcellina.

EDCS-35400817.translation: From the pottery works of Cusinius Messalinus, in the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus.
EDCS-35400817.summary: This is a mark from the pottery works of Cusinius Messalinus, dated to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus.

EDCS-35400829.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus, Cosc(onius) Herm(etis?).
EDCS-35400829.summary: This is a name, possibly Cosc(onius) Herm(etis?), dated to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus.

EDCS-35500565.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. A plot of land granted to Publius Aelius Trypho, [belonging to] Marcus Antonius Pius.
EDCS-35500565.summary: This inscription records a plot of land granted to Publius Aelius Trypho, associated with Marcus Antonius Pius.

EDCS-35500566.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Antoninus, son of Marcus, lived 8 years, 7 months, 17 days. Marcus Antonius Zosimus made [this] for his most dutiful son, a plot donated by the best patrons.
EDCS-35500566.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Antoninus, who died at a young age, with the monument erected by his father, Marcus Antonius Zosimus.

EDCS-35500570.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Epictesis, freedwoman of Gorgias, made [this] for herself and for her patron Gorgias.
EDCS-35500570.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Epictesis, a freedwoman, for herself and her patron Gorgias.

EDCS-35500571.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Flavius Apro. Venuleia Felicitas made [this] for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-35500571.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Venuleia Felicitas for her husband, Quintus Flavius Apro.

EDCS-35500572.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Of Caius Considius Successus, who was also called Rufinus.
EDCS-35500572.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Spirits of the Departed for Caius Considius Successus, also known as Rufinus.

EDCS-35500575.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Veneria Aphrodisia made [this] for her most dutiful daughter, who lived 1 year, 4 months, 18 days.
EDCS-35500575.summary: This funerary inscription was made by a mother, Veneria Aphrodisia, for her daughter who died in infancy.

EDCS-35500580.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Sabinia Attica. Marcus Cocceius Onesimus made [this] for his well-deserving wife, with whom he lived 6 years, 5 months, who lived 19 years.
EDCS-35500580.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Marcus Cocceius Onesimus for his wife Sabinia Attica, detailing their married life and her age at death.

EDCS-35500647.translation: To Lucius Aurelius Pylades, freedman of Augustus, a mime performer, first of his time, crowned with the 'hieronicus' four times, patron of the parasiti, priest of Apollo, honored in synod. In Puteoli, by decree of the decurions, with decurional and duumviral honors, as augur, for his love of country and exceptional generosity in giving a gladiatorial show and a passive hunt, by the indulgence of the most sacred emperor Commodus Pius Felix Augustus. Centuria Antia.
EDCS-35500647.summary: This is a detailed honorary inscription for Lucius Aurelius Pylades, a distinguished mime performer and public benefactor, likely from the reign of Commodus.

EDCS-35500656.translation: To Titus Cinnius Albanus and Abullia Tallusa and their family, in front 12 feet, in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-35500656.summary: This is a boundary inscription from an unknown location, specifying land dimensions for Titus Cinnus Albanus and Abullia Tallusa.

EDCS-35700039.translation: To the Divine Manes. By the will, by which Flavia Tibeius gave Claudius Priscinus, half of the part to those entering on the right side, to Lucius Pomponius Donatus, likewise to Lucius Pomponius Donatus the Younger, freedman, and their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-35700039.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely related to a will, granting property to Lucius Pomponius Donatus and his descendants, possibly from Flavia Tibeius.

EDCS-35700040.translation: To the Divine Manes. Friends made (this) for Nugasus, who deserved well, and his wife and son.
EDCS-35700040.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by friends for Nugasus, his wife, and son.

EDCS-35700046.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Claudia Berutis, grandmother and sweetest nurse, Tiberius Claudius Maximus and Claudia Matrona, her grandchildren, made (this) for her who deserved well.
EDCS-35700046.summary: This is a funerary inscription by grandchildren, Tiberius Claudius Maximus and Claudia Matrona, for their beloved grandmother and nurse, Claudia Berutis.

EDCS-35700047.translation: To Nonia Theodora, she lived 2 years, 1 month, 28 days. Gaius Nonius Ianuarius and Nonia Olympias, parents, made (this) for their daughter.
EDCS-35700047.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Nonia Theodora, made by her parents.

EDCS-35700051.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcella made (this) for her husband Claudius Edanus, who deserved well.
EDCS-35700051.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Marcella for her deserving husband, Claudius Edanus.

EDCS-35700055.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Manlia Septimina, Egria Faustina (made this) for her most devoted mother, and Aulus Egilius Theodorus for his incomparable husband.
EDCS-35700055.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Egria Faustina to her mother Manlia Septimina and by Aulus Egilius Theodorus to his husband.

EDCS-36100086.translation: Pottery works of Dionysius for Domitia, daughter of Publius, Lucilla Paetina and Apronianus, consuls.
EDCS-36100086.summary: This is a potter's mark indicating production by Dionysius for Domitia Lucilla, dating to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-36100093.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus. (Property) of Fortunatus.
EDCS-36100093.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription referencing the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication by Fortunatus.

EDCS-36100156.translation: Pottery works of Dionysius for Domitia, daughter of Publius, Lucilla Paetina and Apronianus, consuls.
EDCS-36100156.summary: This is a potter's mark indicating production by Dionysius for Domitia Lucilla, dating to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-36100157.translation: From the estates of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, during the consulship of Aulus Pontius Clodianus Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-36100157.summary: This inscription likely records goods or property originating from the estates of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, dated by the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-36100158.translation: From the estates of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, during the consulship of Aulus Pontius Clodianus Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-36100158.summary: This inscription likely records goods or property originating from the estates of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, dated by the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-36100160.translation: Felix Caricus made this tiled work from the estates of Lucilla, during the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus.
EDCS-36100160.summary: This inscription records the production of tiled work by Felix Caricus, sourced from the estates of Lucilla, and is dated by the consuls Pontianus and Atilianus.

EDCS-36100168.translation: Abascantus, of Gnaeus Domitius Trophimus, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-36100168.summary: This inscription likely refers to a person named Abascantus, associated with Gnaeus Domitius Trophimus, and is dated by the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-36100177.translation: Tiled work from the estates of Doryphorus, of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-36100177.summary: This inscription records tiled work produced by Doryphorus for the estates of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, dated by the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-36100182.translation: Felix Caricus made this tiled work from the estates of Lucilla, during the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus.
EDCS-36100182.summary: This inscription records the production of tiled work by Felix Caricus, sourced from the estates of Lucilla, and is dated by the consuls Pontianus and Atilianus.

EDCS-36100206.translation: Tiled work from the estates of Dionysius, of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-36100206.summary: This inscription records tiled work produced by Dionysius for the estates of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, dated by the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-36100207.translation: Tiled work from the estates of Doryphorus, of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-36100207.summary: This inscription records tiled work produced by Doryphorus for the estates of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, dated by the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-36100234.translation: Tiled work from the estates of Doryphorus, of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-36100234.summary: This inscription records tiled work produced by Doryphorus for the estates of Domitia Plotilla Lucilla, dated by the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-36100243.translation: During the consulship of Commodus and Pompeianus, from the estates of Domitia Lucilla, workshop of Pompeius Felix (?).
EDCS-36100243.summary: This inscription records production from the workshop of Pompeius Felix (?), operating from the estates of Domitia Lucilla, during the consulship of Commodus and Pompeianus.

EDCS-36100266.translation: Doliare production of Dionysius for Domitia Lucilla, daughter of Publius, under the consulship of Paetus and Apronianus.
EDCS-36100266.summary: This inscription records an item related to the doliare production of Dionysius, dedicated to Domitia Lucilla during the consulship of Paetus and Apronianus.

EDCS-36100313.translation: Under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus, [belonging to] Eutychus.
EDCS-36100313.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or dedication by Eutychus, with a date indicated by the consuls.

EDCS-36100329.translation: Doliare production of Dionysius for Domitia Lucilla, daughter of Publius, under the consulship of Paetus and Apronianus.
EDCS-36100329.summary: This inscription records an item related to the doliare production of Dionysius, dedicated to Domitia Lucilla during the consulship of Paetus and Apronianus.

EDCS-36400245.translation: Aulus Veturius Rufus, son of Aulus, lives [here], [with a boundary of] 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-36400245.summary: This inscription marks the living status and land boundaries of Aulus Veturius Rufus.

EDCS-36700006.translation: Panthera, son of Titus Dentatus, of the Menenia tribe.
EDCS-36700006.summary: This is a personal inscription identifying Panthera by name, filiation, and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-37100050.translation: Terentia, daughter of Aulus Cluvius, built the crypt and chalcidicum on her own land with her own money, by senatorial decree and decree of the curia.
