EDCS-37100050.summary: This monumental inscription records Terentia's construction of a crypt and chalcidicum, funded privately but authorized by senatorial and local decrees.

EDCS-37100053.translation: Terentia, daughter of Aulus Cluvius, [dedicates this to] the Bona Dea.
EDCS-37100053.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Bona Dea by Terentia, daughter of Aulus Cluvius.

EDCS-37100055.translation: To the departed spirits. Plarius Gratus and his son Quintus Plarius Privatus, [dedicate this] to their father Plarius Privatus.
EDCS-37100055.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Plarius Privatus by his son and grandson.

EDCS-37100056.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Resus gave [this] to Quintus Herius Victoriano, [who lived] 35 years and some months.
EDCS-37100056.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication to Quintus Herius Victoriano, noting his age at death.

EDCS-37100064.translation: Titus Trebius Terus, a soldier from the praetorian fleet of Misenum, lived 22 years, served 4 years. His heirs, Lucius Furnius Fuscus and Decimus Pasenius Atimetus, [set this up] for a well-deserving person.
EDCS-37100064.summary: This is a military funerary inscription for Titus Trebius Terus, detailing his service and age, and erected by his heirs.

EDCS-37100069.translation: Monument for Sextia, daughter of Lucius, of the Kani family, publicly erected by decree of the decurions of the colonists, by order, because she was generous towards the colony.
EDCS-37100069.summary: A public monument erected for a woman named Sextia in recognition of her generosity to the colony, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-37700257.translation: This inscription is too fragmented to provide a meaningful translation. It appears to be a very extensive list of officials, professions, and dates, possibly related to civic administration or a calendar of events. Names like Tiberius Caesar, Camillus Arruntius, and Gnaeus Domitius appear, along with roles such as 'topiarius' (gardener), 'librarius' (scribe), 'dispensator' (steward), and 'atriensis' (attendant). The text includes references to consulships and monetary amounts (HS).
EDCS-37700257.summary: A highly fragmented inscription that seems to be a detailed administrative record or a calendar of events, listing various officials, professions, and significant dates from the Roman imperial period, with many lacunae.

EDCS-37800002.translation: Magrius Sagitta, son of the Eagle, Marcus Venidius Vitulus, quaestors; Marcus Silanus, Decimus Valerius Asiaticus (twice), elected on the Kalends of March, in place of Valerius Vetus; Antistius, elected on the Kalends of July; Decimus Laelius Balbus, elected on the Kalends of October; Quintus Coelius Gallus, Aulus Badius Sextus, as the four officials for the market; Marcus Plinius Gallus, Marcus Oppius Valens, as aediles; Lucius Vipstanus Poplicola, Messalla Vipstanus [---] served their magistracies from the Kalends of [---] to the [---] Augustan.
EDCS-37800002.summary: This inscription lists various elected officials, including quaestors, market officials (quattuorviri), and aediles, along with the dates they assumed office, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-37800004.translation: Consuls [---]nianus; Lucius Ragonius Quintianus, consuls; Marcus Magrius Bassus, [---]; Marcus Umbrius Primus, consuls; Titus Flavius Coellianus, [---]; Ceionius Proculus, consuls; [---] Helvius Clemens, [---]; Flavius Decimus, consuls; [---]ninius Maximus, [---]aeus Axius, Marcus Ulpius Civilis, as duovirs; [---]ninus, Publius Rapellius Marcellus, as aediles; [---]us, Publius Sextilius Iulianus, as quaestors. [---] on the Kalends of [---]; [---] L[---]; Gaius A[---]; Gaius Pu[---]. On the Kalends of January [---]; On the Kalends of [---]; Ie[---]; On the Kalends of March [---]; On the Kalends of April [---]; On the Kalends of May [---]. Publius Pu[---]
EDCS-37800004.summary: A fragmentary list of Roman magistrates and officials, including consuls, duovirs, aediles, and quaestors, with some dates mentioned, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-37801281.translation: Hold me because I flee and bring me back to the broad road to my master Flavius.
EDCS-37801281.summary: A 'fugitivi' (runaway slave) inscription, likely from the imperial period, intended to be affixed to a runaway slave to identify their owner.

EDCS-37801286.translation: Hold me and bring me back to Apronianus the palace attendant, to the golden napkin, on the Aventine, because I fled.
EDCS-37801286.summary: A 'fugitivi' (runaway slave) inscription, likely from the imperial period, intended to be affixed to a runaway slave to identify their owner, Apronianus.

EDCS-37801939.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, leges, tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-37801939.translation: To my most sweet [daughter? wife?], Mena[---], wife of Victor[---] [---] eight four [---] is deposited here in the burial house, on the ninth day before the Kalends of [March?], when Flavius Stilicho, a man of the highest distinction, was consul for the second time. [---] on the day before the dies comitialis. [---] on the Nones, dies fastus. [---] eight, dies fastus. [---] seven, dies comitialis. [---] six, dies comitialis. [---] nineteen, Vitiosus, by senatorial decree concerning the birthday of Antonius, dies fastus. [---] eighteen, Carmetalia, dies nefas, piaculum. [---] seventeen, dies nefas, piaculum, holidays by senatorial decree, because on that day the temple of Concord was dedicated in the forum. [---] sixteen, dies nefas, piaculum, holidays by senatorial decree, because on that day Augusta married the divine Augustus. [---] fifteen, dies comitialis. [---] fourteen, dies comitialis. [---] thirteen, dies comitialis. [---] twelve, dies comitialis. [---] eleven, dies comitialis. [---] ten, dies comitialis. [---] nine, dies comitialis. [---] eight, dies comitialis. [---] seven, dies comitialis, to Castor and Pollux in the forum. [---] six, dies comitialis. [---] four, dies fastus. [---] three, dies nefas, piaculum, holidays by senatorial decree, because on that day the altar of Augustus was dedicated in the Campus Martius. [---] the day before, dies comitialis. [---] on the Kalends of February, dies nefas. [---] four, dies nefas. [---] three, dies nefas. [---] on the day before, dies nefas. [---] on the Ides, dies nefas, piaculum. [---] sixteen, dies nefastus. [---] fifteen, Lupercalia, dies nefas, piaculum. [---] fourteen, endotercesis. [---] thirteen, Quirinalia, dies nefas, piaculum. [---] twelve, dies comitialis. [---] eleven, dies comitialis. [---] ten, dies comitialis. [---] nine, Feralia, dies fastus, piaculum, funeral rites for Gaius Caesar. [---] eight, dies comitialis. [---] seven, Terminalia, dies nefas, piaculum. [---] six, Regifugium, dies nefastus. [---] five, dies comitialis. [---] four, endotercesis. [---] three, Equirria, dies nefas, piaculum. [---] the day before, dies comitialis. [---] on the Kalends of March, dies nefas, piaculum. [---] six, dies fastus. [---] five, dies comitialis. [---] three, endotercesis. [---] the day before, Equirria, dies nefas, piaculum. [---] on the Ides, dies nefas, piaculum, holidays for Jupiter. [---] seventeen, dies fastus. [---] sixteen, Liberalia, Agonalia, dies nefas, piaculum. [---] fifteen, dies comitialis. [---] fourteen, Quinquatrus, dies nefas, piaculum. [---] thirteen, dies comitialis. [---] twelve, dies comitialis. [---] eleven, dies nefastus. [---] ten, Tubilustrium, dies nefas, piaculum. [---] nine, quando rex comitiavit, dies fastus. [---] eight, dies comitialis. [---] seven, dies comitialis. [---] six, dies nefas, piaculum, holidays because on that day Gaius Caesar received Alexandria. [---] the day before, dies comitialis. [---]
EDCS-37801939.summary: This inscription is a highly fragmented funerary epitaph and also appears to incorporate fragments of a Roman calendar, listing dates, religious observances, and possibly significant historical events, with mentions of consuls and imperial figures. The funerary portion is addressed to a beloved deceased, while the calendar fragments detail dies fasti/nefasti and festivals.

EDCS-37900003.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Gaius Signius Celer, son of Gaius, of the Palatina tribe. And P[---]
EDCS-37900003.summary: A funerary dedication (dis manibus) for a man named Gaius Signius Celer, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-37900021.text_edition: \n M EO MESES cohortis IIII vigilum centuria Terti MVACIV O\n
EDCS-37900021.translation: M EO MESES of the cohort of 4 vigiles, century of Tertius MVACIV O
EDCS-37900021.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning a military unit, specifically a century of the fourth cohort of vigiles, with some illegible text.

EDCS-37900081.translation: Of Tiberius Claudius Serenus, a man of the highest distinction.
EDCS-37900081.summary: An inscription honoring Tiberius Claudius Serenus, a man of senatorial rank (clarissimus vir), likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-38000153.translation: To Atria Moschara, a woman of the highest distinction.
EDCS-38000153.summary: This inscription honors Atria Moschara, identified as a woman of high social standing, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-38000266.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely for Lucius Octavius Laenas, potentially on a tombstone or other personal monument.

EDCS-38000267.summary: This is an ownership or funerary inscription for Servius Octavius Laenas Pontianus, likely indicating his name on a monument or property.

EDCS-38001184.translation: Primi Aulus Aristus Menander, barrel-maker, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38001184.summary: This inscription identifies Primi Aulus Aristus Menander, a barrel-maker, and dates the record to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38001199.summary: This is a maker's mark or signature, stating that Cogitatus was the craftsman responsible for the object.

EDCS-38001200.summary: This is a maker's mark or signature, stating that Julius Eutychianus was the craftsman responsible for the object.

EDCS-38001201.translation: Made by Faustus, slave, under the supervision of Daphnus, freedman of the Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus, as procurator.
EDCS-38001201.summary: This inscription records that Faustus, an imperial slave, created the object under the supervision of Daphnus, an imperial freedman acting as procurator, likely during the reign of Domitian.

EDCS-38001203.translation: Quintus Athenaeus, freedman of Augustus, made it.
EDCS-38001203.summary: This inscription records that Quintus Athenaeus, a freedman of Emperor Augustus, was the maker of the object.

EDCS-38001204.translation: Quintus Athenaeus, freedman of Augustus, made it.
EDCS-38001204.summary: This inscription records that Quintus Athenaeus, a freedman of Emperor Augustus, was the maker of the object.

EDCS-38001206.summary: This is a maker's mark or signature, stating that Titus Vinicius Mamilianus was the craftsman responsible for the object.

EDCS-38001207.summary: This is a dedication by Auruncleius Chresimus, likely for a monument he commissioned.

EDCS-38001210.translation: To Matidia, daughter of Augustus, aged 82. Salonius Epictetus made it for Matidia, daughter of Augustus.
EDCS-38001210.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Matidia, daughter of Augustus, and notes that Salonius Epictetus erected it.

EDCS-38001211.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription referring to Emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus, possibly part of a larger dedication or official marker.

EDCS-38100019.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Flavius Maximus and Caninius Paculus made this for Flavia Alumna, who deserved well, who lived for 4 years and 7 months.
EDCS-38100019.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages and names of the dedicants, Flavius Maximus and Caninius Paculus, for Flavia Alumna.

EDCS-38100031.translation: Primiti[ba?] slept in the Lord. She lived for [3]0 years. Her sons made [this] for their sweet-souled mother. [3]PI[3] [3]P[--]
EDCS-38100031.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records the lifespan of Primiti[ba?] and was made by her sons.

EDCS-38100035.summary: This is a simple dedication, likely funerary, to Arria Priscilla.

EDCS-38100038.translation: [--]. Aemilius Filonicus, son of Lucius, lived for 31 years. Claudius Savinianus, his heir, made this for him as deserving well.
EDCS-38100038.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Aemilius Filonicus and notes that his heir, Claudius Savinianus, commissioned the monument.

EDCS-38100040.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Vibia Tyche made this for her dearest, well-deserving husband, Lucius Vibius Zosimus.
EDCS-38100040.summary: This funerary inscription records Vibia Tyche's dedication to her husband, Lucius Vibius Zosimus.

EDCS-38100051.translation: [--] Faventinus, freedman of Augustus, procurator [--]. Urbanus made it [--].
EDCS-38100051.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions Faventinus, a freedman and procurator of Augustus, and indicates that Urbanus made the monument.

EDCS-38100052.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Bovillanus Marcianus, who lived for [3]2 years, 7 months, and 14 days. II Bovillani [made this for him?].
EDCS-38100052.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Bovillanus Marcianus and indicates that II Bovillani commissioned the monument.

EDCS-38100053.translation: To the departed spirits. Successus lived 1 year, 1 month, 12 days. His parents, Successus and Tyche, made this.
EDCS-38100053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Successus, set up by his parents.

EDCS-38100105.translation: Pompeius Aspri, son of Marcus, of the Collina tribe, lived 4 years, 6 months, 11 days. For the children, Marcus Pompeius Atimetus and Cincia Saturnina.
EDCS-38100105.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Pompeius Aspri, commemorating him and possibly other children of Marcus Pompeius Atimetus and Cincia Saturnina.

EDCS-38100109.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a title or dedication by Gaius Geminius Rufus, who held the office of quaestor.

EDCS-38100114.translation: Annius Paccius Callisthenes [for himself] and for Annius Paccius Gennaeus, for Paccia Naide, freedwoman of Sex-tius, and for Annius Aristaenetus, freedman of An-nius.
EDCS-38100114.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Annius Paccius Callisthenes for himself and several other individuals, likely family members or freedmen.

EDCS-38100115.translation: For Gaius Julius Severus, son of Gaius, of the Palatina tribe, who lived 28 years, and for Gaius Julius Crescentes Clarano, freedman of Augustus, his father, [this monument stands] 50 feet in the front and 65 feet in the rear.
EDCS-38100115.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Severus and his father Gaius Julius Crescentes Clarano, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-38100167.translation: To the most beloved wife Paulina, her husband made this for her, a deserving wife in peace, who lived 33 years, [and was] 18 years in marriage, [laid to rest].
EDCS-38100167.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deceased wife, Paulina, noting her age at death and the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-38100176.translation: For Decimus Iunius Rufus, son of Decimus [---], and Decimus Iunius Dio [---], [monument with] 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the rear.
EDCS-38100176.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a burial plot dedicated to members of the Iunius family, likely father and son.

EDCS-38100196.translation: Cornelia Sponsilla, daughter of Spurius. Lucius Pontius Victor, freedman of Lucius. 14 feet in front and 15 feet in the rear.
EDCS-38100196.summary: This inscription records the owners or recipients of a burial plot, Cornelia Sponsilla and Lucius Pontius Victor, along with its dimensions.

EDCS-38100197.translation: Marcia Quarta. 21 feet in front and 17 feet in the rear.
EDCS-38100197.summary: This inscription indicates the owner, Marcia Quarta, and the dimensions of a burial plot.

EDCS-38100198.translation: For Gaius Petronius Labeo, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, and for his father and patron, Gaius Petronius Quartio, freedman of Labeo. Sabinus and Sabina made this.
EDCS-38100198.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by Sabinus and Sabina for their father and patron, Gaius Petronius Quartio, and also for Gaius Petronius Labeo.

EDCS-38100204.translation: To the departed spirits. [Text illegible]arus [Text illegible] January.
EDCS-38100204.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual whose name is mostly lost, mentioning the month of January, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-38100206.translation: To Terminalis, well-deserving, who lived [possibly] one year.
EDCS-38100206.summary: A funerary epitaph for a person named Terminalis, likely a child given the mention of a short lifespan, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-38100208.translation: To the departed spirits. Grania Urbana. Publius Granius Urbanus.
EDCS-38100208.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Publius Granius Urbanus for Grania Urbana, likely his wife or daughter, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-38301835.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus, for Callistus.
EDCS-38301835.summary: An inscription honoring Callistus, dated to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-38301867.translation: From the potteries of Quintus Asinius Marcellus. Caius Nunidius Fortunatus made the tile work, in the consulship of Quintus Articuleius Paetus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38301867.summary: This is a product inscription from a tile factory, indicating Caius Nunidius Fortunatus produced tile work from the kilns of Quintus Asinius Marcellus, dated to the consulship of Paetus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38301868.translation: From the potteries of Quintus Asinius Marcellus, tile work. By Caius Nunidius Fortunatus, in the consulship of Paetus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38301868.summary: This is a product inscription from a tile factory, crediting Caius Nunidius Fortunatus for tile work from the kilns of Quintus Asinius Marcellus, dated to the consulship of Paetus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38301903.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus, for Asiaticus.
EDCS-38301903.summary: An inscription honoring Asiaticus, dated to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-38400058.text_edition: \n Titus Octavius Sermone Marcus Fidustius V mutus Sermone Fidustium vius Irena Plotiaes sic deficere ex Anesu quaestu caput OLAVS d scribo oc<u=I>los exiit Membra omnia lincua sic illa ex Acres ILA MATIE bonus qua \n
EDCS-38400058.translation: Titus Octavius Sermone. Marcus Fidustius V... from the quest of Anesus, his head... the eyes came out. All his limbs, the tongue, like that... the good [name]...
EDCS-38400058.summary: A fragmented and difficult inscription that appears to be a description of a physical affliction or deformity, possibly intended as a funerary text or a medical record, with Roman names and geographical references.

EDCS-38400059.text_edition: \n Malcio Nicones oculos manus dicitos sic brachias uncuis sic capillos caput pedes femus venter sic natis umbilicus sic pectus mamillas collus sic os buccas dentes labias mentus sic oculos frontem supercilia scapulas umerum nervias ossu merilas venter mentula crus questu lucru valetudines defico sic in has tabellas Rufa Pulica manus dentes oculos b<r=S>achia venter sic mamillas pectus ossua sic merilas venter sic crus os pedes frontes uncuis sic dicitos sic venter sic umbilicus sic cunnus sic quas ilae Rufas sic Publica sic defico sic in has tabellas \n
EDCS-38400059.translation: Malcius Nicones. Eyes, hands, arms, nails, hair, head, feet, thigh, belly, navel, chest, nipples, neck, mouth, cheeks, teeth, lips, chin, eyes, forehead, eyebrows, shoulders, upper arm, sinews, bones, [belly], penis, thigh, quest, sickness, infirmities... Rufa Pulica, hands, teeth, eyes, arms, belly, nipples, chest, bones, [belly], thigh, mouth, feet, foreheads, nails, belly, navel, vulva, and what... Rufa, like that. I describe, in these tablets...
EDCS-38400059.summary: A highly detailed and explicit anatomical description, possibly a medical text or a curse tablet, listing body parts and afflictions, attributed in part to Rufa Pulica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-38701833.translation: From the potteries of Avidus Quietus.
EDCS-38701833.summary: A maker's mark or producer's inscription from the pottery workshops of Avidus Quietus, indicating the origin of ceramic products.

EDCS-38701844.translation: From the estates of Agathyrsus, freedman of Augustus, and of Quintus Pomponius Ianuarius, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38701844.summary: This inscription records goods from the estates of Agathyrsus and Quintus Pomponius Ianuarius, dated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38701845.translation: From the estates of Agathyrsus, freedman of Augustus, and Quintus Pomponius Ianuarius, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38701845.summary: This inscription records goods from the estates of Agathyrsus and Quintus Pomponius Ianuarius, dated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38701870.translation: Doliare ware from the estates of Domitia Lucilla Aprilis, under the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus.
EDCS-38701870.summary: This inscription records doliare ware from the estates of Domitia Lucilla Aprilis, dated by the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus.

EDCS-38701875.translation: Under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus. Belonging to Felix.
EDCS-38701875.summary: This inscription records ownership by Felix, dated by the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-38701876.translation: Under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus. Felix [indicates something, possibly ownership or a type of product].
EDCS-38701876.summary: This inscription records a mention of Felix, dated by the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-38701880.translation: From the potteries of Flavia Pelagia, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38701880.summary: This inscription records goods from the potteries of Flavia Pelagia, dated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38701881.translation: From the potteries of Flavia Procula, doliare ware, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38701881.summary: This inscription records doliare ware from the potteries of Flavia Procula, dated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38701900.translation: From the estates of Julia Albana, workshop of Primigenius, under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.
EDCS-38701900.summary: This inscription records goods from the estates of Julia Albana, from the workshop of Primigenius, dated by the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-38701903.translation: Under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus. From the estates of Junius Sulpicianus.
EDCS-38701903.summary: This inscription records goods from the estates of Junius Sulpicianus, dated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38701904.translation: Under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus. From the estates of Junius [Sulpicianus?], [property] of Publius Clodius Herc[...].
EDCS-38701904.summary: This inscription records goods from the estates of Junius Sulpicianus (?), property of Publius Clodius Herc(ule?), dated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38701919.translation: From the potteries of Neratia Quartilla, under the consulship of Paetin(us) and Apronianus.
EDCS-38701919.summary: This inscription records goods from the potteries of Neratia Quartilla, dated by the consulship of Paetin(us) and Apronianus.

EDCS-38701927.translation: From the potteries of Papirius, under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetin(us).
EDCS-38701927.summary: This inscription records goods from the potteries of Papirius, dated by the consulship of Apronianus and Paetin(us).

EDCS-38701935.translation: From the potteries of Pullaenus Pollio, under the consulship of Paetin(us) and Apronianus.
EDCS-38701935.summary: This inscription records goods from the potteries of Pullaenus Pollio, dated by the consulship of Paetin(us) and Apronianus.

EDCS-38701995.translation: From the potteries of Fortunatus of Quintanenses, under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetin(us).
EDCS-38701995.summary: This inscription records goods from Fortunatus of Quintanenses, dated by the consulship of Apronianus and Paetin(us).

EDCS-38701996.translation: From the potteries of Camillus of Quintanenses, under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetin(us).
EDCS-38701996.summary: This inscription records goods from Camillus of Quintanenses, dated by the consulship of Apronianus and Paetin(us).

EDCS-38701997.translation: From the potteries of Annius Cer[---] Quintanensis, under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetin(us).
EDCS-38701997.summary: This inscription records goods from Annius Cer[---] Quintanensis, dated by the consulship of Apronianus and Paetin(us).

EDCS-38702013.translation: From the potteries of Statius Severus Macedo and Nunnidius Restitutus, and Leon[---], under the consulship of Paetin(us) and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702013.summary: This inscription records goods from the potteries of Statius Severus Macedo and Nunnidius Restitutus, and Leon[---], dated by the consulship of Paetin(us) and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702030.translation: On the Ides of March, from the Myrinian potteries, under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetin(us).
EDCS-38702030.summary: This inscription records goods from the Myrinian potteries, dated to the Ides of March and the consulship of Apronianus and Paetin(us).

EDCS-38702045.text_edition: \n Ex praediis Arriae Fadillae Caepionianis Rufelli Felicis Paetino et Aproniano consulibus\n
EDCS-38702045.translation: From the estates of Arria Fadilla, of the Caepionian family, of Rufus Felix, under the consulship of Paetin(us) and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702045.summary: This inscription records goods from the estates of Arria Fadilla and Rufus Felix, dated by the consulship of Paetin(us) and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702046.translation: From the estates of Arria Fadilla, of the Caepionian family, of Proclus, under the consulship of Paetin(us) and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702046.summary: This inscription records goods from the estates of Arria Fadilla and Proclus, dated by the consulship of Paetin(us) and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702050.translation: From the estates of Gaius Curiatius Cosanus Caepionianus, under the consulship of Euripus Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702050.summary: This inscription records a property owner, identifying land belonging to Gaius Curiatius Cosanus Caepionianus, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702087.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a property mark or ownership statement, identifying Gaius Curiatius Atictus.

EDCS-38702097.translation: From the potteries of Quintus Asinius Marcellus, the work of Gaius Nuncius Fortunatus, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702097.summary: This inscription marks a product from the potteries of Quintus Asinius Marcellus, identifying the maker Gaius Nuncius Fortunatus and dating it to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702104.translation: Under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, to Apollonius.
EDCS-38702104.summary: This inscription is a dedication or acknowledgment to Apollonius, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702106.translation: From the estates of Marcus Fabius Lychimnus, from the property of Annius Verus, under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.
EDCS-38702106.summary: This inscription identifies property belonging to Marcus Fabius Lychimnus, derived from the estates of Annius Verus, and is dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702107.translation: Under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, Zosimus, slave of Marcus Annius Verus.
EDCS-38702107.summary: This inscription identifies Zosimus, a slave of Marcus Annius Verus, and is dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702126.translation: Under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, belonging to Flavius Posidonius.
EDCS-38702126.summary: This inscription indicates ownership by Flavius Posidonius and is dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702132.translation: From the estates of Caesar, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702132.summary: This inscription refers to property from the estates of Caesar, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702143.translation: From the potteries of Tempesinum, a tile belonging to Servilius Firmus, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702143.summary: This inscription marks a tile from the potteries of Tempesinum, belonging to Servilius Firmus, and is dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702144.translation: From the estates of Aburnius Caecidianus Firmus, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702144.summary: This inscription identifies property belonging to Aburnius Caecidianus Firmus and is dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702145.translation: From the kilns of Sextus Aburnius Caecidianus, tile of Servilius Firmus, in the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702145.summary: This inscription records a tile from the kilns of Sextus Aburnius Caecidianus, manufactured during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702146.translation: From the kilns of Sextus Aburnius Caecidianus, tile of Servilius Firmus, in the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702146.summary: This inscription records a tile from the kilns of Sextus Aburnius Caecidianus, manufactured during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702159.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the kilns of Cornelia Manliola, Salarese, from the property of Publius P. B.
EDCS-38702159.summary: This inscription marks a product from the kilns of Cornelia Manliola, originating from the property of Publius P. B., during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-38702163.translation: In the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, from the estates of Julius Eutactus, Salarese.
EDCS-38702163.summary: This inscription indicates goods from the estates of Julius Eutactus, originating from Salarae, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702164.translation: In the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, from the estates of Julius Eutactus, Salarese.
EDCS-38702164.summary: This inscription indicates goods from the estates of Julius Eutactus, originating from Salarae, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-38702165.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the property of Lucius Iurius Crescentis.
EDCS-38702165.summary: This inscription records goods from the property of Lucius Iurius Crescentis, during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-38702172.translation: From the estates of Quintus Servilius Pudentis, Naeviana, in the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.
EDCS-38702172.summary: This inscription marks products from the estates of Quintus Servilius Pudentis, originating from Naeviana, during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-38702176.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the property of Aulus Gabinius Successus, Salarese, from the kilns of Iulianus H.
EDCS-38702176.summary: This inscription records goods from the property of Aulus Gabinius Successus, originating from Salarae and the kilns of Iulianus H., during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-38702177.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the property of Aulus Gabinius Successus, Salarese, from the kilns of Iulianus S.
EDCS-38702177.summary: This inscription records goods from the property of Aulus Gabinius Successus, originating from Salarae and the kilns of Iulianus S., during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-38702179.translation: In the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the estates of Turranius Primus, Salarese.
EDCS-38702179.summary: This inscription indicates goods from the estates of Turranius Primus, originating from Salarae, during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-38702180.translation: Under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, T. T. Ac. T. Gal.
EDCS-38702180.summary: This is a fragment from a dedication or an official record, naming the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus and the official T. T. Ac. T. Gal., likely from the period they served.

EDCS-38702181.translation: T. T. Ac. T. Gal., under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702181.summary: This inscription lists the names T. T. Ac. T. Gal. along with the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus, indicating a date during their consulship.

EDCS-38702182.translation: Under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, C. P. G. T. T.
EDCS-38702182.summary: This inscription records the names C. P. G. T. T. alongside the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus, dating it to their term in office.

EDCS-38702183.translation: Under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, C. P. G. T. T.
EDCS-38702183.summary: This inscription mentions C. P. G. T. T. and the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus, establishing a chronological marker for the inscription.

EDCS-38702184.translation: C. P. G. T. T., under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702184.summary: This inscription names C. P. G. T. T. and indicates the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus, placing it within their period of office.

EDCS-38702185.translation: C. P. G. T. T., under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702185.summary: This inscription records the names C. P. G. T. T. and the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus, dating it to their consular year.

EDCS-38702187.translation: Under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the estates of Marcus Ulpius Ulpianus Salar(ensis).
EDCS-38702187.summary: This inscription records a transaction or property transfer originating from the estates of Marcus Ulpius Ulpianus, dated by the consuls Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-38702190.translation: From the workshops of Sergia Paulina, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-38702190.summary: This inscription indicates that the item or work originates from the workshops of Sergia Paulina and is dated by the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-39100130.translation: To Isis, Apollon Horos, Harpokrates. By Marcus Opsius Fannianus, strategos, prefect of the grain supply by decree of the Roman senate, agoranomos, treasurer of Pontus Bithynia, chiliarch of the fifth Macedonian legion, having governed ten men, holding office at Rome.
EDCS-39100130.summary: This is a dedication by Marcus Opsius Fannianus, who held various high-ranking military and administrative positions in the Roman Empire, including strategos and chiliarch, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-39100146.translation: By decree of the council [the city of the Neapolitans], Publius Aelius Antigenedes, a Nicomedean and Neapolitan, who served as archiereus of the sacred synod of the Dionysiac artists, the first and only one in all time to win the following contests, which he alone competed in without oil, Rome, the New City, and the all-around contest, and also Puteoli, first distinguished by the lord Emperor Antoninus Pius, and similarly as follows. It also includes the selections in Nicomedia, his fatherland, at the same contest of Asclepieia, playing the kithara and aulos, he ceased at the age of 35, having played for the people of Rome for twenty years.
EDCS-39100146.summary: This honorary inscription honors Publius Aelius Antigenedes for his athletic and musical achievements, as well as his public service, likely dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius (138-161 CE).

EDCS-39100153.translation: To Lucius Crepereius Proclus, former consul and proconsul, their own benefactor, the elected officials of Artemis, in return for a favor.
EDCS-39100153.summary: A dedication by local officials to a former governor, Lucius Crepereius Proclus.

EDCS-39100155.translation: Titus Flavius Artemidorus, son of Artemidorus, from Kyrene, Artemidorus, an Adanean, also called Antiochenus from Daphne, having won the first-ever great Kapetolian games, the men's pancratium, Olympiad 2, Pythian 2, Nemean 2, Actian games in Neapolis for men's pancratium, Isthmian games for boys' pancratium, the common games of Asia in Smyrna for boys' pancratium, and in the following penteteris, men's wrestling and pancratium, and again men's pancratium, first among men for the common games of Asia in Pergamon, men's pancratium in Ephesus, Balbilla for boys' [pancratium], and again in the following penteteris men's pancratium in Alexandria, the sacred penteteric games, the common games of Syria, Cilicia, Phoenicia in Antioch, 2 games in Ephesus, Tralles, 3 in Sardis, 5 in Laodicea, [he has] the shield from Argos and 17 other penteteric games, under the supervisor Artemidorus, son of Athenodorus, an Adanean, with Athenodorus [supervising].
EDCS-39100155.summary: An extensive victory list inscribed by Artemidorus, a celebrated athlete from Adana, detailing his numerous victories in various games and disciplines across the Roman East, likely set up around the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-39100206.translation: Memorial for Nike, aged 1 year and 30 days.
EDCS-39100206.summary: A funerary marker for Nike, indicating her age at death.

EDCS-39100227.translation: Here lies Charitosa, who lived 14 years and 33 days. She rested 10 days before the Kalends of May.
EDCS-39100227.summary: A tombstone for Charitosa, giving her age at death and date of repose.

EDCS-39100247.translation: To the Chthonic Gods. Antistius Julianus, MAY HE BE MADE GREAT.
EDCS-39100247.summary: An invocation to the underworld deities by Antistius Julianus, possibly a personal vow or aspiration.

EDCS-39100249.translation: Aurelius Philo/kyrios from Pergamon to his dearest freedman Aurelius Antiochus from Pergamon, in memory.
EDCS-39100249.summary: A memorial inscription set up by Aurelius Philokyrios for his freedman Aurelius Antiochus.

EDCS-39100250.translation: Be of good cheer, Baia Glauce, good woman.
EDCS-39100250.summary: A short, comforting epitaph for Baia Glauce.

EDCS-39100252.translation: Epinepea, daughter of Sakerdos, ship-owner from Korykion, aged 22 years.
EDCS-39100252.summary: An epitaph for Epinepea, a ship-owner from Korykion, stating her age.

EDCS-39100258.translation: To the Chthonic Gods. Quintus Calpurnius Rufus from Ephesus [for] Quintus Calpurnius Bryon, his freedman.
EDCS-39100258.summary: A dedication to the underworld gods by Quintus Calpurnius Rufus for his freedman Quintus Calpurnius Bryon.

EDCS-39100370.translation: [Marcus Aurelius Heron] dedicated this for the good of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, Fortunate, Pious, Augustus, and Julia [Mamaea] Augusta, mother of Augustus, to Zeus Helios the Great Sarapis and the associated gods. Marcus Aurelius Heron, neokoros of Sarapis in Portus, dedicated this for the good of the community, under Larginius Vitalion, chief attendant and chamberlain, and Aurelius Ephebus and Salonius Theodotus, hierophants and chamberlains, and the sacred college.
EDCS-39100370.summary: A dedication by Marcus Aurelius Heron, a temple official for the god Sarapis, to various deities, including the emperor and empress, likely during the reign of Severus Alexander (222-235 CE).

EDCS-39100371.translation: To Jupiter Helios the Great Sarapis and the associate gods, Marcus Aurelius Sarapion, wrestler, the paradoxical, together with his father Marcus Aurelius Demetrius, also called Harpocrates, councilor of the most glorious city of the Alexandrians, having prayed and having been successful, we have dedicated in good fortune for Chrysantha.
EDCS-39100371.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Helios Sarapis and other gods by Marcus Aurelius Sarapion and his father Marcus Aurelius Demetrius, councilor of Alexandria, for the well-being of Chrysantha.

EDCS-39100372.translation: For the safety and return and eternal endurance of our Lords Emperors Severus and Antoninus, and Julia Augusta, and all their household, and for the safe voyage of the entire fleet, Gaius Valerius Serenus, temple attendant of the great Sarapis, the supervisor of the entire Alexandrian fleet, under the prefecture of Claudius Julianus, for prosperity.
EDCS-39100372.summary: A dedication to the goddess Adrastia and her adornments by Gaius Valerius Serenus, temple attendant of Sarapis and supervisor of the Alexandrian fleet, for the well-being of the emperors and the fleet.

EDCS-39100376.translation: Serenus, the eldest temple attendant, I have dedicated.
EDCS-39100376.summary: A dedication by Serenus, the eldest temple attendant, to an unspecified deity.

EDCS-39100380.translation: Priests and priestess of the great god Dionysus of the holy and pious cohort of the Traiani, Lucius Suallius Leonidas and Julia Rufina, with Secundus as assistant.
EDCS-39100380.summary: A dedication related to priests and priestesses of the god Dionysus from the Traiani cohort.

EDCS-39100381.translation: Good fortune! The sacred, inviolable, autonomous, faithful, pious, illustrious, and great city of the Gazaeans, by decree of the ancestral god, has dedicated its benefactor, Emperor Caesar Marcus Antoninus Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, the most god-beloved, ruler of the world, through Tiberius Claudius Papiirius, supervisor of the sanctuary.
EDCS-39100381.summary: A dedication by the city of Gaza to Emperor Gordian III, recognizing him as a benefactor and praising him as ruler of the world, likely from the period of his reign (238-244 CE).

EDCS-39100409.translation: To Antinoos, enthroned with the gods in Egypt.
EDCS-39100409.summary: A dedication to the deified emperor Hadrian's companion, Antinoos, associating him with the gods of Egypt.

EDCS-39100476.translation: Marnas. Tiberius Claudius Papiirius, having served as supervisor, I have dedicated from his own funds.
EDCS-39100476.summary: A personal dedication by Tiberius Claudius Papiirius, identifying him as a supervisor and stating the monument was funded privately.

EDCS-39300214.translation: During the consulship of Statius Marcus Br[---] and Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-39300214.summary: A dating clause for an unspecified event or document, referencing the consuls Statius Marcus Br[---], Paetinus, and Apronianus, likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-39700524.translation: This stele, because of memory, I, Archias, have carved for you, the good woman. She was called Psycholbos.
EDCS-39700524.summary: A funerary stele set up by Archias for his wife, mentioning her name and a characteristic 'good woman,' and possibly a nickname or epithet 'Psycholbos'.

EDCS-40100029.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Sanctus Iohannis.

EDCS-40100042.summary: This is a simple inscription possibly identifying a person named Speratus, likely from a funerary context.

EDCS-40100083.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a dedication to a young man named Sex[--], with the rest of the text lost.

EDCS-40100088.translation: To the divine spirits. Lucius Cornelius Sabinus made this for Sulpicia Modesta, an incomparable woman, well-deserving.
EDCS-40100088.summary: This funerary epitaph records the erection of a monument by Lucius Cornelius Sabinus for his wife Sulpicia Modesta, praising her qualities.

EDCS-40100097.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-40100097.translation: Sepulchre(?) of Maurus, servant of God.
EDCS-40100097.summary: This inscription appears to mark the burial place of Maurus, described as a servant of God, likely from a Christian context.

EDCS-40100098.translation: Maximus, bishop, who is also a confessor of Christ.
EDCS-40100098.summary: This inscription identifies Maximus as a bishop and a confessor of Christ, likely a post-mortem title or honor.

EDCS-40100099.summary: This inscription simply records the name and title of Vincentius as a bishop.

EDCS-40100109.translation: The sons for their well-deserving mother Aurelia Euticiana. She died on the seventh day before the Ides of June.
EDCS-40100109.summary: This epitaph records the death of Aurelia Euticiana on a specific date, commemorated by her sons.

EDCS-40100116.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-40100116.summary: This inscription marks a place belonging to Donatus, whose occupation is indicated by the term 'isiciarius', possibly a maker of some sort.

EDCS-40100118.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely identifying a person named Felix, possibly the son or associate of someone named Magnus.

EDCS-40100119.translation: The name of this distinguished young man was Felix, who lived for twelve duplicate years and eighteen days. He departed life at the eighth hour. Here he left his beauty and grief to his parents.
EDCS-40100119.summary: This epitaph commemorates Felix, a young man who died at the age of 24 (12 x 2), on a specific day and time, leaving his parents in sorrow.

EDCS-40200108.summary: This is a Greek funerary epitaph for Annía, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-40200111.summary: This is a Greek funerary epitaph, possibly naming Gabianos Primus and Lampadis.

EDCS-40200112.summary: This is a Greek funerary epitaph, potentially naming Gabeianós, Sabine, and Epipolis.

EDCS-40200139.summary: This is a Greek funerary epitaph listing three female names: Avgoureína, Pantagápē, and Eucárpē.

EDCS-40200159.summary: This is a Latin funerary epitaph for Sanctus Protasius.

EDCS-40300005.translation: During the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus, from the estates of the heirs of Junius Sulpicianus, Pilagyrus(?).
EDCS-40300005.summary: This inscription records the source of a product, Pilagyrus(?), from the estates of Junius Sulpicianus' heirs, during the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus.

EDCS-40300029.translation: During the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus, from the estates of the heirs of Junius Sulpicianus, Pilagyrus(?).
EDCS-40300029.summary: This inscription records the source of a product, Pilagyrus(?), from the estates of Junius Sulpicianus' heirs, during the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus.

EDCS-40300054.translation: During the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus, from the estates of the heirs of Junius Sulpicianus, Pilagyrus(?).
EDCS-40300054.summary: This inscription records the source of a product, Pilagyrus(?), from the estates of Junius Sulpicianus' heirs, during the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus.

EDCS-40300075.translation: From the estates of Julia Albana, workshop of the Communis, during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.
EDCS-40300075.summary: This inscription records the origin of goods from Julia Albana's estates, produced by the workshop of Communis, during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-40300077.translation: Pottery work from the estates of Gaius Julius Stephanus, workshop of Asiaticus II and Aquilinus as consuls.
EDCS-40300077.summary: This inscription records pottery production from Gaius Julius Stephanus' estates, during the consulship of Asiaticus II and Aquilinus.

EDCS-40300078.translation: From the estates of Iulia Albana, by the workshop of Hermias, during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus.
EDCS-40300078.summary: This inscription likely records goods or provisions originating from the estates of Iulia Albana, processed by Hermias' workshop, and dated by the consuls Apronianus and Paetus.

EDCS-40900151.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Curtidius Primus [erected this] for his brother Quintus Curtidius Chresimus.
EDCS-40900151.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Curtidius Primus to his brother Quintus Curtidius Chresimus.

EDCS-40900157.translation: Lucius Cornelius, son of Spurius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, twice duovir, quaestor, praetor, and duovir with judicial authority, erected this for himself and his brother Tertius while alive.
EDCS-40900157.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Cornelius, who held various municipal offices, for himself and his brother Tertius.

EDCS-40900158.translation: Varia Tertulla, daughter of Caius, mother of her sons. // Burial of Marcellinus, a laudable man, chief physician, on the 8th day before the Kalends of May, during the consulship of Theodorus, vir clarissimus. // Burial of Claudius Successus, defender, on the 7th day before the Ides of April, during the consulship of Flavius Probus, vir clarissimus.
EDCS-40900158.summary: This inscription records the burials of Marcellinus, a chief physician, and Claudius Successus, a defender, with specific dates and consuls noted.

EDCS-41100037.translation: To Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, our best and most indulgent patron. Fortunatus, his freedman, ordered this to be made with 300,000 sesterces by his will.
EDCS-41100037.summary: This is a dedication by Fortunatus, a freedman, to Emperor Antoninus Pius, specifying a bequest of 300,000 sesterces for its construction.

EDCS-41100041.translation: To Domitia Galatia, daughter of Lucius Domitius Tiberianus, aedile, quinquennial duovir. [When the most splendid council] [offered her the honor of the priesthood] [of Minerva]...
EDCS-41100041.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to record an honor bestowed upon Domitia Galatia, who held municipal offices, likely related to the priesthood of Minerva.

EDCS-41700005.translation: Of Pothymenus, during the consulship of Paetus and Apronianus.
EDCS-41700005.summary: This inscription, likely a dedication or record of ownership, is dated by the consuls Paetus and Apronianus and refers to an individual named Pothymenus.

EDCS-41700090.translation: Of Primigenius, during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus.
EDCS-41700090.summary: This brief inscription, likely a dedication or label, is attributed to Primigenius and dated by the consuls Apronianus and Paetus.

EDCS-41700125.translation: Of Onesimus, during the consulship of Apronianus and Paetus.
EDCS-41700125.summary: This short inscription, possibly a dedication or ownership mark, refers to Onesimus and is dated by the consuls Apronianus and Paetus.

EDCS-41700131.translation: Worked stone from the estates of Plaetor Nepos, by Aristi Thallus, during the consulship of Paetinus and Aepronianus.
EDCS-41700131.summary: This inscription records a work of pottery ('opus doliare') from the estates of Plaetor Nepos, produced by Aristi Thallus, and dated by the consuls Paetinus and Aepronianus.

EDCS-41700154.translation: Titus Flavius Eros, priest, gave this offering.
EDCS-41700154.summary: This is a dedication by Titus Flavius Eros, a priest, of a gift, likely a religious offering.

EDCS-41700240.text_edition: \n Lucius Campanius Luci filius Flaccus tribunus militum legionis XV IIvir pontifex quaestor II aedilis praefectus fabrum et sibi et Verae uxori \n
EDCS-41700240.translation: Lucius Campanius Flaccus, son of Lucius, military tribune of the fifteenth legion, member of the board of pontiffs, quaestor, aedile, prefect of the mechanics, for himself and his wife Vera.
EDCS-41700240.summary: This inscription lists the career and honors of Lucius Campanius Flaccus, a military officer and local magistrate, possibly on a tomb.

EDCS-41700244.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Tettius Eutychius, Tettia Aspasia, and Tettia Repentina for their parents, Tettius Alexander, decurion of Capua.
EDCS-41700244.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by children for their father, Tettius Alexander, a decurion of Capua.

EDCS-41800253.translation: Doliary ware of Oppius Justus, when Paetinus and Apronianus were consuls.
EDCS-41800253.summary: This inscription records the production of doliary ware (likely pottery) by Oppius Justus during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-41800271.translation: Doliary ware of Oppius Justus, when Paetinus and Apronianus were consuls.
EDCS-41800271.summary: This inscription records the production of doliary ware (likely pottery) by Oppius Justus during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-42200001.translation: Pompaios Athenodoros to his own patron.
EDCS-42200001.summary: This is a dedication by Pompaios Athenodoros to his patron.

EDCS-42200002.translation: Synoria, wife of Aemilius Scientes. Purchased for 30 jugera.
EDCS-42200002.summary: This inscription appears to record a property transaction, possibly the sale of land measuring 30 jugera to Synoria, wife of Aemilius Scientes.

EDCS-42200008.translation: Bibius, son of Epilytos. Bibius, son of Epilytos.
EDCS-42200008.summary: This inscription, potentially a funerary marker, repeats the name Bibius, son of Epilytos, twice.

EDCS-42200011.translation: Farewell, Dica, daughter of Trebius, worthy one. Farewell, Heracleides, son of Dionysius, worthy one. Farewell, Chaireas, son of Maraios, worthy one.
EDCS-42200011.summary: This is a funerary epitaph with multiple farewells to named individuals, possibly within a family tomb.

EDCS-42200013.translation: Domitia Calliste, priestess of Athena, a Sicel, who became publicly honored by the Senate.
EDCS-42200013.summary: This inscription honors Domitia Calliste, a priestess of Athena, of Sicilian origin, who achieved public recognition from the Senate.

EDCS-42200014.translation: Heracleidas, son of Astos. Iseias, daughter of Astos. Greetings. Astos, son of Heracleidas.
EDCS-42200014.summary: This is a funerary epigram for Iseias and Astos, likely from the Roman period in Greece, mentioning multiple generations of family members.

EDCS-42200015.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph naming Epilytos and his father Bibios, likely from the Roman period in Greece.

EDCS-42200016.translation: Epilytos, son of Epilytos, priest of the Divine Caesar. Bibios, son of Epilytos. Monis, son of Epilytos.
EDCS-42200016.summary: This funerary inscription names Epilytos, priest of the Divine Caesar, along with his father and brother, also named Epilytos and Bibios respectively, suggesting a family of some standing in Roman Greece.

EDCS-42200017.summary: This is a funerary epitaph naming Epilytos and his father Trebius, likely from the Roman period in Greece.

EDCS-42200034.translation: Pakea, daughter of Epilytos. Epilytos, son of Epilytos. Tarchios, son of Epilytos. Aristole, daughter of Epilytos. Epilytos, son of Epilytos. Trebius, son of Epilytos.
EDCS-42200034.summary: This funerary inscription lists several family members, all related to Epilytos, indicating a prominent family in Roman Greece with multiple individuals named Epilytos.

EDCS-42300055.translation: In the consulship of Apro(nianus) and Pae(tinus); of Herculanus (estate).
EDCS-42300055.summary: This is a legal or administrative notation indicating a date by the consuls Apronianus and Paetinus and referencing the estate of Herculanus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-42300060.translation: In the consulship of Apro(nianus) and Pae(tinus); of M. Vinicius Herculanus.
EDCS-42300060.summary: This is a legal or administrative notation indicating a date by the consuls Apronianus and Paetinus and referencing the property of M. Vinicius Herculanus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-42300061.translation: From the estates of Ulpia Accipta, in the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-42300061.summary: This is an administrative record indicating property belonging to Ulpia Accipta, dated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-42300068.translation: Doliare, belonging to L. Rustius Lygdamus, in the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-42300068.summary: This inscription records a property or object belonging to L. Rustius Lygdamus, dated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-42300092.translation: From the estates of Ulpia Accipta, in the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-42300092.summary: This is an administrative record indicating property belonging to Ulpia Accipta, dated by the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-42300105.translation: In the consulship of Apro(nianus) and Paetus. Of Marcus Vinicius Herculanus.
EDCS-42300105.summary: This inscription records a dedication or ownership by Marcus Vinicius Herculanus, dated to the consulship of Apro(nianus) and Paetus.

EDCS-42400111.translation: Registration of Equitia Psamathe. HS 374 nummi, which money came into the stipulation of Lucius Caecilius Iucundus from the auction of Equitia Psamathe, with the commission paid less. Equitia Psamathe declared she had from Lucius Caecilius Iucundus. Done at Pompeii on the day before the Nones of [a month in the consulship of Nero, [a third consul], and a second consul]. Of Quintus Appuleius Severus, Quintus Arrius Proculus, Publius Sittius Zosimus, Aulus Messius Phronimus, Marcus Cestillius Philodemus, Marcus Epidius Secundus, Numerius Popidius Amaranthus, Gnaeus Helvius Apollonius.
EDCS-42400111.summary: This inscription details a financial transaction involving the registration of Equitia Psamathe's debt and payment, likely related to an auction, and lists several witnesses or involved parties, with a dating fragment suggesting the reign of Nero.

EDCS-42500118.translation: To the Chthonic Gods. Annia Daphne and Artemidorus of Caros, for my own monument, in memory. She lived 1 year, 4 months, 23 days.
EDCS-42500118.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the Chthonic Gods by Annia Daphne and Artemidorus of Caros for their own monument, recording the lifespan of one of them.

EDCS-42500130.translation: To the Chthonic Gods. Lucius Aelius Flavius Diodorus from Prusa, who lived on Olympus, 22 years, 3 months, 4 days.
EDCS-42500130.summary: This epitaph dedicated to the Chthonic Gods records the life span of Lucius Aelius Diodorus from Prusa, who is described as having lived on Olympus.

EDCS-43400244.translation: Of Statius Marcius Bassus Caepionianus. In the consulship of Paetinus and Apro(nianus).
EDCS-43400244.summary: This inscription records the name of Statius Marcius Bassus Caepionianus, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apro(nianus).

EDCS-43400310.translation: Of Cervonius, son of Titus, of the Oufentina tribe.
EDCS-43400310.summary: This fragment records the name and tribal affiliation of Cervonius, son of Titus.

EDCS-43400312.translation: To Alfia, daughter of Caius, Maxuma.
EDCS-43400312.summary: This inscription is an epitaph for Alfia Maxuma, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-44100245.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ammia, mother, and her brother Deuter, his brother Italicus, made [this] for their most unhappy brother, who lived 27 years and 21 days.
EDCS-44100245.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the spirits of the departed for a brother named Italicus, erected by his mother Ammia and his brother Deuter, recording his age at death.

EDCS-44100246.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ammias and Mercator, slave of our Caesar, made [this] for their well-deserving brother, who lived 40 years and 3 months, and for Italicus, his son, who lived 8 years, 2 months, and 25 days.
EDCS-44100246.summary: This funerary epitaph honors two individuals, Mercator (a slave of Caesar) and his son Italicus, recording their ages at death and erected by Ammias and Mercator.

EDCS-44100247.translation: To the spirits of the departed. He made [this] for his dearest wife, Iulia Fortunata Aciva, slave and deputy overseer of our Caesar, with whom he lived 17 years and 5 months.
EDCS-44100247.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulia Fortunata Aciva, a slave and deputy overseer of Caesar, and states the duration of her marriage.

EDCS-44100248.translation: To the divine shades. Marcus Ulpius Aceratus made (this) for Tertulla Flavia Optata, his cherished freedwoman and partner.
EDCS-44100248.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a freedman named Marcus Ulpius Aceratus to his beloved partner Tertulla Flavia Optata.

EDCS-44100249.translation: Flavia Marcella made (this) for her well-deserving husband Simon, who lived 75 years, and for her well-deserving mother Iaria, who lived 30 years, and for her well-deserving sister Arsinoaeni, who lived 9 years.
EDCS-44100249.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Flavia Marcella for her husband, mother, and sister, providing their ages at death.

EDCS-44100251.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Aemilia Cale, mother, for her most dutiful daughter Autronia Nicario, daughter of Lucius, and for herself.
EDCS-44100251.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a mother, Aemilia Cale, for her devoted daughter Autronia Nicario, and also for herself.

EDCS-44100262.translation: To Popilia Salsula, the governess. Popilius Virro, the most distinguished man.
EDCS-44100262.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Popilius Virro, a distinguished man, to his governess Popilia Salsula.

EDCS-44100268.translation: In the consulship of Gaius Velleius Paterculus and Marcus Manilius Vopiscus, on the ninth day before the Kalends of August. Lucius Venidius Ennychus testified that a daughter was born to him from his wife Livia Acte. Acted at Herculaneum. [---] Mari [---] M. Fabi F[---] C. Messieni [---] L. Venidi [Ennychi] M. Badi Bla[sti] [M. Valeri Dia[coni(?)] [---] Calpurni [---] [L. (?)] Aeli Chresimi(?)]
EDCS-44100268.summary: This inscription records a birth announcement and likely served as a legal document or marker related to the birth of a daughter to Lucius Venidius Ennychus and Livia Acte, during the consulship of Velleius Paterculus and Manilius Vopiscus.

EDCS-44100270.translation: By order of Jupiter Best and Greatest Heliopolitanus. For Gaius Stennius Crispus, priest and caretaker. (Erected by) the priests and zygophori.
EDCS-44100270.summary: This inscription indicates an order from Jupiter Heliopolitanus for the priests and zygophori to honor Gaius Stennius Crispus, who served as a priest and caretaker.

EDCS-44100271.translation: Lucius Domitius Pudens, patron of the Tyrian district, built the shop and the cook's kitchen at his own expense from the ground up, in honor of his patronage, for the people of the Tyrian district.
EDCS-44100271.summary: Lucius Domitius Pudens, patron of the Tyrian district, funded and built a shop and kitchen for the inhabitants of his district.

EDCS-44500184.translation: The stonework of Quintus Oppius Justus, from the Licinian estates of Domitia Lucilla, in the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-44500184.summary: This inscription records the construction of stonework by Quintus Oppius Justus, likely using materials or land from Domitia Lucilla's estates, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-45300145.translation: To Lucius Celius, son of Lucius, of the Menenia tribe, twice a magistrate with judicial power, military tribune appointed by the people, from his will.
EDCS-45300145.summary: This inscription is a posthumous dedication to Lucius Celius, honoring his public offices and military service, as established in his will.

EDCS-45300146.translation: Veia Barchilla, daughter of Numerius, for herself and for her husband Numerius Agrestinus Equitius Pulcher.
EDCS-45300146.summary: This funerary inscription records Veia Barchilla's dedication for herself and her husband, Numerius Agrestinus Equitius Pulcher.

EDCS-45300148.translation: To Gaius Munatius Faustus, Augustalis and pagan, by decree of the decurions, for himself and his wife Naevoleia Tyche.
EDCS-45300148.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Gaius Munatius Faustus, an Augustalis and pagan official, for himself and his wife, as decreed by local authorities.

EDCS-45300149.translation: For Melissaea, daughter of Numerius, to Marcus Servilius, father, and Marcus Servilius, son.
EDCS-45300149.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Servilius (father and son) for Melissaea, daughter of Numerius.

EDCS-45300151.translation: To Lucius Barbidius Communis, freedman of Lucius, magistrate of the district of August Felix Suburbani, for himself and his wife Pithia Rufilla, freedwoman of Publius, and for his freedmen Vitalis and Ianuarius.
EDCS-45300151.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication by Lucius Barbidius Communis, a magistrate of the August Felix Suburbani district, for himself, his wife, and his freedmen.

EDCS-45300152.translation: To Gaius Cuspius Cyrus, freedman of Gaius, magistrate of the district of August Felix Suburbani, and to his wife Vesvia Iucunda. To Gaius Cuspius Salvius, freedman of Gaius, magistrate of the district of August Felix Suburbani.
EDCS-45300152.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gaius Cuspius Cyrus and his wife Vesvia Iucunda, both associated with the August Felix Suburbani district, and also to Gaius Cuspius Salvius, also a magistrate of the same district.

EDCS-45300154.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that likely records someone born for the good of the state, possibly a civic honorific.

EDCS-45500048.translation: He lived 23 years and 8 months. Severus and Vibia Aciliana made this for their incomparable son.
EDCS-45500048.summary: This inscription is a tombstone commemorating a son who died at the age of 23 years and 8 months, erected by his parents Severus and Vibia Aciliana.

EDCS-45500050.translation: The bones of Lucius Aulus Cerinthus, freedman of Lucius, are located here.
EDCS-45500050.summary: This inscription marks the burial place of Lucius Aulus Cerinthus, a freedman, indicating the location of his remains.

EDCS-45700036.text_edition: \n iae Quinti filiae Salviae annos vixit L menses II\n
EDCS-45700036.translation: Salvia, daughter of Quintus, lived 50 years and 2 months.
EDCS-45700036.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Salvia, daughter of Quintus, who lived for 50 years and 2 months, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-45700041.translation: To the departed spirits. To Hostia Triphonina, to whom Julius Festus dedicated this for his wife, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-45700041.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Julius Festus to his wife, Hostia Triphonina, acknowledging her merit and devotion.

EDCS-45700042.translation: To Barbia Prima, daughter of Publius, who lived 17 years and 30 days. Barbius Venerianus, her brother, [erected this] for his most dutiful sister.
EDCS-45700042.summary: This inscription is a tombstone erected by Barbius Venerianus for his sister Barbia Prima, who died at the age of 17 years and 30 days.

EDCS-45700043.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aelia Protogenia lived for 14 years, 6 months, and 3 days. Her parents [made this].
EDCS-45700043.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelia Protogenia, likely set up by her parents.

EDCS-46400966.text_edition: \n Lucius Egnatius Luci filius Teretina Mamaecianus haruspex porticum sedilia de sua pecunia faciunda coeravit idemque probavit\n
EDCS-46400966.translation: Lucius Egnatius Mamaecianus, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, a haruspex, arranged for the construction of the portico and seating at his own expense and also approved it.
EDCS-46400966.summary: An inscription recording the construction of a portico and seating by Lucius Egnatius Mamaecianus, a haruspex, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-46500010.translation: Caius Vettius Anicetus, freedman of Caius, quinquennalis, of the fabrum navalium, made [this] for himself and for Otilia Ampliata, his most holy wife, and for Caius Vettius Anicetianus, son of Caius Vettius, and for Caius Vettius Otacilianus, son of Caius Vettius, and for Vettia Olympia, daughter of Caius Vettius, and for Caius Vettius Olympicus, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. In front [XX] feet, in the territory [XX] feet. They live.
EDCS-46500010.summary: A funerary monument set up by Caius Vettius Anicetus for his family, including his wife, sons, daughter, and descendants, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-46500011.translation: Caius Arunculeius Alexander made [this] for Caius Fabius Agrippinus.
EDCS-46500011.summary: A dedication made by Caius Arunculeius Alexander for Caius Fabius Agrippinus, possibly a funerary monument.

EDCS-46500013.translation: [...] soldier of the [3rd] cohort, in the [century] of Egnatius, lived for 21 years, is buried here. Clodia Iuvenalis, his mother, made this for her most devoted son.
EDCS-46500013.summary: A funerary epitaph for a soldier, likely from the Roman army, erected by his mother, Clodia Iuvenalis.

EDCS-46500015.translation: Aur(elius) Auluzanus, who lived for 50 years, served for 25 years. Aurelia Fabia made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-46500015.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Auluzanus, a soldier, erected by his wife Aurelia Fabia.

EDCS-46500018.translation: To Fortuna, the child of Jupiter, Primigenia. Lucius Ruficanus Nothus [made this...]. [He] added [this] at his vow, a place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-46500018.summary: A dedication to the goddess Fortuna Primigenia, likely made in fulfillment of a vow and approved by local authorities.

EDCS-46500019.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Celadus, a fellow freedman of Elainus, freedman of Augustus, manager of marble works.
EDCS-46500019.summary: A funerary inscription for Elainus, freedman of Augustus, set up by his fellow freedman and colleague, Celadus.

EDCS-46500023.summary: A dedication or ownership marker possibly related to property or a building, made by Publius Fabius Eutyches.

EDCS-46500024.summary: A dedication or ownership marker possibly related to property or a building, made by Publius Fabius Eutyches.

EDCS-46500025.summary: This is a dedication made by Publius Fabius Eutyches.

EDCS-46500026.summary: This is a dedication made by Publius Fabius Eutyches.

EDCS-46500027.summary: This inscription records the name Sextus Vettulenus Gemellus.

EDCS-46500034.translation: For Rupilia Amaryllis, freedwoman of Titus, 16 feet in front.
EDCS-46500034.summary: This inscription marks a property boundary of 16 feet belonging to Rupilia Amaryllis, a freedwoman.

EDCS-46500040.translation: Fufia Viticula, freedwoman of Caius, made (this) for herself and for Verecundus, freedman of Caius (her husband), and for her husband Lucius Favonius Apella, and for her freedman freedman Lucius Fufius Ianuarius, and for her freedwoman freedwoman Fufia Viticula. Sestia Telete, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-46500040.summary: This is a funerary or commemorative dedication by Fufia Viticula for herself and several freedmen and her husband, along with a separate dedication for Sestia Telete.

EDCS-46500043.text_edition: \n Quinto Lucilio Quinti filio Publilia Valenti procuratori Augusti XX hereditatium trans Padum et <per> Aemiliam usque Aquileiam curatori rei publicae Privernatium patrono coloniae tribuno cohortis VI equestris civium Romanorum tribuno legionis XXX Ulpiae victricis praefecto cohortis primae Flaviae IIviro flamini coloniae Veronensium Privernates publice decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-46500043.translation: To Quintus Lucilius, son of Quintus, Publia Valens, procurator of the Augusti for inheritances beyond the Po and curator for the region of Aemilia up to Aquileia, curator of the public affairs of Privernum, patron of the colony, tribune of the VIth equestrian cohort of Roman citizens, tribune of the XXXth Ulpia Victrix legion, prefect of the First Flavian cohort, duovir, and flamen of the colony of Verona, by the people of Privernum, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-46500043.summary: This is a public dedication honoring Quintus Lucilius for his numerous administrative and military positions by the citizens of Privernum.

EDCS-46500045.translation: To [---]dius Gallus, son of Caius, of the Oufentina tribe, and [---]ia Rufa, daughter of Lucius, his mother.
EDCS-46500045.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to a father and son, with the mother identified.

EDCS-46500046.translation: To Caius Julius, son of Caius, of the Oufentina tribe. Dedicated on the third day before the Kalends of August, when Falcon and Clar[us] were consuls.
EDCS-46500046.summary: This is a dedication of a monument to Caius Julius, dated by the consuls Falcon and Clar[us].

EDCS-46500050.summary: This is a dedication made by Annaeus, a plumber.

EDCS-46600989.translation: Sacred to Pinaria Irene and the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-46600989.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Pinaria Irene.

EDCS-46600991.translation: To the departed spirits. Julius Augurius made this for Marcus Tannonius Gerinianus, an incomparable student, who lived 18 years, 8 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-46600991.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Tannonius Gerinianus, a student, and was set up by Julius Augurius.

EDCS-46600992.translation: To the departed spirits. Minucius Minuciannus made this for Minucia Genialis, his ward, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-46600992.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Minucia Genialis and was set up by her guardian, Minucius Minuciannus.

EDCS-46600993.translation: [---]. [---]paus lived 25 years. Saturnina made this for her deserving wife.
EDCS-46600993.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a person named Paus, who lived 25 years, and was made by his wife Saturnina.

EDCS-46600996.translation: To the departed spirits. Marius, the father, made this for Victoria, his devoted daughter.
EDCS-46600996.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Victoria and was set up by her father, Marius.

EDCS-46600997.translation: To the departed spirits. Gerellana Victoria made this for Ofilia Rufina, her most devoted mother.
EDCS-46600997.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Ofilia Rufina and was set up by her daughter, Gerellana Victoria.

EDCS-46600999.translation: Umbreia Utilis, freedwoman of Lucius, for herself and for her guardian Marcus Heius Dionysius and for Gaius Marc[---] Rutilionus and Callistus [---] [---].
EDCS-46600999.summary: This funerary inscription records Umbreia Utilis setting up a tomb for herself and others, including her guardian Marcus Heius Dionysius.

EDCS-46601000.translation: To the departed spirits. For Laelia Secundilla, who lived 3 years. [---]lia Secun[---] her most devoted mother [made this?].
EDCS-46601000.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Laelia Secundilla, who lived 3 years, and was likely set up by her mother.

EDCS-46601001.translation: To the departed spirits. Calpurnius Rusticus lived 30 years, 2 months, and 10 days. Calpurnia Elpis arranged for this to be made for her deserving husband.
EDCS-46601001.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Calpurnius Rusticus and was arranged by his wife, Calpurnia Elpis.

EDCS-46601002.translation: To the departed spirits. M. Annius Eutychetes lived 20 years. Annia Restituta, his mother, for her sweetest son.
EDCS-46601002.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of M. Annius Eutychetes, who lived 20 years, and was set up by his mother, Annia Restituta.

EDCS-46601003.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Julius Trifolinus, son of Gaius, of the Falernian tribe, who lived 50 years and 21 days. Spes, his freedwoman, for her deserving patron.
EDCS-46601003.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Julius Trifolinus and was set up by his freedwoman, Spes.

EDCS-46601004.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. S<u=Y>ettia Iotape [erected this] for her parents Minucia Euterpe and Minucius Prosdoca.
EDCS-46601004.summary: A funerary dedication by S<u=Y>ettia Iotape to her parents, Minucia Euterpe and Minucius Prosdoca.

EDCS-46601005.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Minucia Euhodia.
EDCS-46601005.summary: A funerary dedication to Minucia Euhodia.

EDCS-46601009.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Irene, who lived 70 years.
EDCS-46601009.summary: A funerary dedication for Irene, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-46601010.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Popilius Hermolaus, on land granted to him free of charge by the agents of the estate of Naevius Fastus, Tryphon and Auxeticus and Adrastus, with Naevius Chryseros as procurator, has dedicated this for the body of his daughter Popilia Clarilla, aged 9 years, 11 months, and 12 days, and he has also been given the right to place his own body [here] whenever he dies, by the same people, out of kindness, so that she may be joined with her daughter.
EDCS-46601010.summary: A funerary dedication by Popilius Hermolaus for his daughter Popilia Clarilla, and a grant for his own burial beside her.

EDCS-46601011.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Minucia Trophimes. Minucia Euhodia [erected this] for her excellent mother.
EDCS-46601011.summary: A funerary dedication by Minucia Euhodia for her mother, Minucia Trophimes.

EDCS-46601012.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Philetus made this for his colleague Naevius Colonus.
EDCS-46601012.summary: A funerary dedication by Philetus for his colleague Naevius Colonus.

EDCS-46601013.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Minucius Trophimus [erected this] for his excellent father, Marcus Minucius Felix, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-46601013.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Minucius Trophimus for his father, Marcus Minucius Felix.

EDCS-46601015.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Heracla, freedwoman of Glanus.
EDCS-46601015.summary: A funerary dedication for Heracla, freedwoman of Glanus.

EDCS-46601016.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Aemilia Sp[---].

EDCS-46601018.translation: Modesta, who lived 2 years, 2 months, and 4 days. For Augenis.
EDCS-46601018.summary: A funerary dedication for Modesta, who died at a very young age, possibly for Augenis.

EDCS-46601019.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Publius Cornelius Crescentianus, son of Publius, of the Palatina tribe, who lived 7 years, 5 months, and 14 days. Cornelius Amandus, his father, for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-46601019.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Publius Cornelius Crescentianus, set up by his father.

EDCS-46601021.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Cusinius M[---]linus for his son Cusinius Augurio, who lived 15 years, 8 months, and 66 days, well-deserving.
EDCS-46601021.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Cusinius Augurio, erected by his father.

EDCS-46601022.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Constantia, his wife, made this for her husband, Extricatus, a gardener, servant of our Empress Faustina, most excellent and dutiful, who lived 40 years, 8 months, and 27 days.
EDCS-46601022.summary: A funerary epitaph for Extricatus, a gardener and freedman of Empress Faustina, set up by his wife.

EDCS-46601023.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Extricatus, who lived 11 years, 9 months, and 9 days. His mother made this for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-46601023.summary: A funerary epitaph for a boy named Extricatus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-46700045.translation: 461.
