For Apollo, freedman of Iustus, for purchased cooked linen, all accounted for at Nola, under the consulship of Lucius Oppius Curtius Rufus and Caius Terentius Vespasianus. [---] Marcus As[e]llus. [---] Apolloni Iusti. [---]emptum linum coc[tum(?)] omne actum Nola(e) L(ucio) Oppio Curtio R[ufo] co(n)s(ulibus) C(ai) Ter[ent]i Vespasiani M(arci) Livi [---]em[---] [M(arci) Etesi(?)] Co[---]i [---]i N[i]ca[---] M(arci) Claudi Hy[---]i C(ai) Metieni Nymphici D(ecimi) Iuni Sabini.
EDCS-46700045.summary: A fragmentary inscription from Nola listing names and possibly related to accounts, dating to the consulship of Lucius Oppius Curtius Rufus and Caius Terentius Vespasianus.

EDCS-47400398.text_edition: \n Cohors III vigilum i qui descenderunt in vexillatione Ostis Idibus Augustis in Idibus Decembribus Pudente et Pollione consulibus centuria Claudi Quintus Livius Quinti filius Salutaris frumentum publicum accipit die VIIII tabula XLVIII kapite centesimo Marcus Ulpius Celestinus frumentum publicum accipit die VII tabula XLI kapite centesimo Lucius Octavius Sabinus frumentum publicum accipit die VII tabula LVIIII kapite centesimo Publius Cassienus Hilarus frumentum publicum accipit die VII tabula LVIIII kapite centesimo Lucius Nonius Luci libertus Proclus frumentum publicum accipit die I tabula LVIIII kapite centesimo Lucius Laesina Luci filius Marcellinus frumentum publicum accipit die tabula kapite centesimo Quintus Aemilius Theseus frumentum publicum accipit die tabula kapite centesimo Lucius Plutius Agathopus frumentum publicum accipit die VII tabula LXXXII kapite centesimo Lucius Lepidius Restutus frumentum publicum accipit die tabula kapite centesimo Lucius Plutius Iulianus frumentum publicum accipit die tabula kapite centesimo Caius Vennius Leo frumentum publicum accipit die tabula kapite centesimo Curam egit Vennius Leo bonis bene\n
EDCS-47400398.translation: Cohort III of the Vigiles, who descended in a detachment to Ostia from the Ides of August to the Ides of December, under the consulship of Pudens and Pollio. From the century of Quintus Livius Salutaris, son of Quintus, tributary recipient of public grain on the 9th day, tablet 48, at the 100th heading. Marcus Ulpius Celestinus, tributary recipient of public grain on the 7th day, tablet 41, at the 100th heading. Lucius Octavius Sabinus, tributary recipient of public grain on the 7th day, tablet 59, at the 100th heading. Publius Cassienus Hilarus, tributary recipient of public grain on the 7th day, tablet 59, at the 100th heading. Lucius Nonius Proclus, freedman of Lucius, tributary recipient of public grain on the 1st day, tablet 59, at the 100th heading. Lucius Laesina Marcellinus, son of Lucius, tributary recipient of public grain on the [---] day, tablet [---], at the 100th heading. Quintus Aemilius Theseus, tributary recipient of public grain on the [---] day, tablet [---], at the 100th heading. Lucius Plutius Agathopus, tributary recipient of public grain on the 7th day, tablet 82, at the 100th heading. Lucius Lepidius Restutus, tributary recipient of public grain on the [---] day, tablet [---], at the 100th heading. Lucius Plutius Iulianus, tributary recipient of public grain on the [---] day, tablet [---], at the 100th heading. Caius Vennius Leo, tributary recipient of public grain on the [---] day, tablet [---], at the 100th heading. Vennius Leo managed with care for the good, for the good.
EDCS-47400398.summary: A record of grain distribution to members of Cohort III of the Vigiles stationed at Ostia during a specific period in the consulship of Pudens and Pollio.

EDCS-47500671.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Chariton, son of Hedylo, most sweet son, who lived 9 years, 9 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-47500671.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Chariton, son of Hedylo.

EDCS-48000008.translation: For Quintus Vibius Rufus, son of Quintus, of the Teretina tribe. His grandmother Nio[---]. Aedile, Quaestor twice. In the front [---], in the field [---].
EDCS-48000008.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Vibius Rufus, mentioning his grandmother and his public offices, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-48000016.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Lucius Caedius Ad[---]
EDCS-48000016.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph beginning with a dedication to the Spirits of the Departed.

EDCS-48000019.translation: Lais, freedwoman of Metilia, made this while alive for herself and for Caius Doius Sura, and for Lucius Metilius Vitalis, freedman of Metilia, her son.
EDCS-48000019.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Lais, a freedwoman, for herself and other family members.

EDCS-48000020.translation: To Munnia Prima, freedwoman, daughter of a woman. In the front 12 feet, in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-48000020.summary: A funerary boundary marker for Munnia Prima, indicating the dimensions of her plot, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-48000024.translation: For Caius Umbrius Silon, freedman, son of Caius. For himself and his family. In the front 24 feet, in the field 15 feet.
EDCS-48000024.summary: A funerary boundary marker for Caius Umbrius Silon, specifying plot dimensions, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-48000037.translation: For Caius Quirinrius, son of Aulus, of the Teretina tribe. In the front 20 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-48000037.summary: A funerary boundary marker for Caius Quirinrius, detailing the dimensions of his plot, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-48000038.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Fundilia Secundina.
EDCS-48000038.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Fundilia Secundina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-48700134.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Marcus Vassidius Paullinus, son of Caius. He lived for 5 years, 9 months. This burial plot was publicly granted to him by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-48700134.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Marcus Vassidius Paullinus, whose plot was granted by the town council, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-48700930.translation: Tiberius Claudius Fortunatus for himself and for Flavia Fortunata, his deserving wife, and for their freedmen and freedwomen, and for the descendants of Fortunata, all of them except for Claudia Heuresis, in such a way that that place with the building, the best and greatest, from their family name, these things which are acted upon do not follow the heir.
EDCS-48700930.summary: A funerary dedication by Tiberius Claudius Fortunatus for his family, establishing specific inheritance conditions for a burial plot and building, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49400008.translation: Of them... [area] for the purpose of repairing or inspecting... eight feet of land for the conveyance of his water.
EDCS-49400008.summary: A fragmentary inscription concerning land allocated for water management, likely related to an aqueduct or fountain, from the Roman period.

EDCS-49500015.translation: Fructosus, patron of the guild of [stuppatores?], made the temple and cave of Mithras from the ground up with his own money.
EDCS-49500015.summary: A dedication by Fructosus, a guild patron, for the construction of a Mithraeum, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-49700172.translation: For Marcus Cutius Severus, son of Tiberius, and Caius Fabius Passienus Saturninus, son of Caius, as quinquennial aediles. Refurbished from the offerings collected by Quintus Asconius Modestus, son of [---], prefect, and Quintus Pomponius [---]alis, prefect. Marcus Cutius Severus, son of Tiberius; Lucius Tuccius Bassus, son of Lucius; Marcus Modiarius Clemens, son of Marcus; Quintus Fabius Modestus, son of Quintus; Caius Oppius Asiaticus, son of Caius; Caius Plaetorius Ianuarius, son of Caius. Caius Fabius Passienus, son of Caius; Aulus Ancharius Taurus, son of Aulus; Caninius [---]. Lucius Volcacius [---]; Lucius Acestius [---]; Marcus Tusculanius Amianthus, son of Marcus. [---] Severus, son of [---]; Melissus [---]. Flavius Pacatus(?), son of Lucius. Decimus Cornelius Firmus, son of Decimus; Decimus Fonteius Liberalis, son of Decimus; [---] Maius Fuscus, son of Marcus; Marcus Popillius Laenas, son of Marcus; [---] Cusinius Bithynicus, son of Lucius; Lucius Volunseius Flamininus, son of Lucius. Also the Augustales, caretakers of the temple of Castor and Pollux.
EDCS-49700172.summary: An inscription detailing the refurbishment of the temple of Castor and Pollux by aediles and listing numerous contributors and officials, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-50500211.translation: Numerius Audius, a wool-worker, and Numerius Audius.
EDCS-50500211.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning two individuals, likely a father and son, identified by their names and profession as wool-workers.

EDCS-50500213.translation: In this tomb the body of Antonia Vitellia, who lived 13 years, 4 months, and 1 day, was placed, under the care of Marcus Aufustius Paternion and Flavia Vitellia, her parents, as a result of a donation.
EDCS-50500213.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of a young girl, Antonia Vitellia, with her parents identified as the dedicants and benefactors.

EDCS-50500219.translation: To Gaius Cordevius Malchion, by his son [---]ria, freedwoman of Lucius, Apollonia.
EDCS-50500219.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Cordevius Malchion, set up by his freedwoman daughter Apollonia.

EDCS-50500220.translation: To the departed spirits. Cottia Ianuaria. Marcus Postumius Saturninus, her brother.
EDCS-50500220.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cottia Ianuaria, dedicated by her brother Marcus Postumius Saturninus.

EDCS-50500222.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Lucius Dentrius Clemens, son of Lucius. Lucius Dentrius Proculus, to his father, who deserved well.
EDCS-50500222.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Dentrius Clemens, dedicated by his father Lucius Dentrius Proculus with the phrase 'to one who deserved well'.

EDCS-50500227.translation: To Titus Vetillius Maternus [---]lius, who lived 7 years, who [---]
EDCS-50500227.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Titus Vetillius Maternus, indicating his age at death and likely his parentage.

EDCS-50500231.translation: Servius Ofillius Felix, freedman of Servius, for himself and for Pontia Pierina, freedwoman of Lucius, his wife, and for Lucius Pontius, freedman of Gaius, Hil[---]. (Set up) on the front 12 feet, in the field 11 feet.
EDCS-50500231.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the tomb owner, his wife, and another individual, with dimensions for the front and field.

EDCS-51000105.translation: Diocles dedicated and presented this gift to the Unconquered God Mithras on account of the honor of Gaius Lucretius Menander, his father.
EDCS-51000105.summary: This is a dedication of a gift to the god Mithras, made by Diocles in honor of his father, Gaius Lucretius Menander.

EDCS-51200050.translation: [---] Surredius(?), son of [---], Oufentina tribe. Surredia Erate, freedwoman of Marcus, on the front 13 feet, in the field 16 feet.
EDCS-51200050.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Surredia Erate, likely related to a man named Surredius, with measurements for the plot.

EDCS-51200074.translation: Orfia, freedwoman of the woman, Phryne, is buried here.
EDCS-51200074.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating that Orfia Phryne, a freedwoman, is buried in this location.

EDCS-51200076.translation: To Aulus Fuficius, freedman of Aulus, and Fufia, freedwoman of a woman, from his testament, 1,000,000 sesterces.
EDCS-51200076.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording a bequest of 1,000,000 sesterces from a will, likely for the burial of the deceased.

EDCS-51200085.translation: I, Tiberius Claudius Pierus, of quaestorian rank, have received from Lucius Cominius Primus, as a loan, 10,000 sesterces, owed from the ledger of Gnaeus Sentius, condemned by senatorial decree. Written under the consulship of Marcus Vettius Bolanus and Marcus Arruntius Aquila, on the Nones of October. I, Tiberius Claudius Pierus, of quaestorian rank, have written that I received from Lucius Cominius Primus, through his slave Festus, 10,000 sesterces, owed from the ledger of Gnaeus Sentius, condemned by senatorial decree. Done. By Tiberius Claudius Pierus, of quaestorian rank; Caius Pomponius Adventus, of quaestorian rank; Tiberius Claudius Syntrophus; Caius Hortensius Felix; Titus Vibius Primus; Lucius Cratinus; Tiberius Claudius Pierus, of quaestorian rank.
EDCS-51200085.summary: This document is a loan receipt, detailing the repayment of 10,000 sesterces by Tiberius Claudius Pierus to Lucius Cominius Primus, with several witnesses listed. It dates to the consulship of Marcus Vettius Bolanus and Marcus Arruntius Aquila.

EDCS-51200087.translation: To Gnaeus Pompeius Euphrosynus, who was honored with the bisellium and a statue, and furthermore with decurional ornaments, by the order of the decurions, whose decree is written below. On the Ides of January, in the curia of the Basilica Augusta Anniana, all were present for the writing. Lucius Pontius Ingenuus and Titus Clodius Felix, the IIviri, spoke concerning the awarding of decurional ornaments to Pompeius Euphrosynus, an honorable man. As to what they thought should be done regarding this matter, they decreed thus: Since Pompeius Euphrosynus, in consideration of his munificence, has deserved well of our republic, it is pleasing to this order to award decurional ornaments to him, in such a way that he may receive all the customary privileges.
EDCS-51200087.summary: This inscription records a decree by the order of decurions to award honorary decurional ornaments and privileges to Gnaeus Pompeius Euphrosynus for his munificence and service to the state. The decree was made in the Basilica Augusta Anniana.

EDCS-51200088.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Egnatia Successa to her well-deserving husband, Publius Manlius Laurinus, son of Spurius.
EDCS-51200088.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her deceased husband, Publius Manlius Laurinus.

EDCS-51200089.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Nemonia Euphrosyne to her excellent husband, Marcus Nemonius Augurinus, son of Marcus, who had discharged all the offices in his native land.
EDCS-51200089.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Nemonia Euphrosyne to her husband, Marcus Nemonius Augurinus, honoring his fulfillment of public offices.

EDCS-51200090.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Marcus Nemonius Stephanus, who lived for 2 years, 2 months, and 5 days. His parents to their sweetest son.
EDCS-51200090.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, Marcus Nemonius Stephanus, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-51200091.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Nemonius Puteolanus made this for his well-deserving wife, Aelia Prisca, who lived 64 years, 9 months, and 22 days.
EDCS-51200091.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Nemonius Puteolanus for his wife, Aelia Prisca.

EDCS-51200092.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sempronius Herma, his father, to Aulus Sempronius Paternus, decurion of Puteoli, who had discharged all offices and duties.
EDCS-51200092.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Sempronius Herma to his father, Aulus Sempronius Paternus, a decurion of Puteoli who had held many public offices.

EDCS-51200093.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Publius Aelius Julianus and Iulia Cleopatra, his parents, for their most sweet son, Publius Aelius Iulianus, who lived 3 years, 9 months, and 16 days, well-deserving.
EDCS-51200093.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, Publius Aelius Iulianus, dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-51600814.translation: Marcus Manneius Memno, freedman of Marcus, and Furia Fausta, freedwoman of a woman, in front 20 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-51600814.summary: This inscription appears to mark a burial plot of a specific size (20x20 feet) for Marcus Manneius Memno and Furia Fausta.

EDCS-51700956.translation: To Augustus, may it be successful!
EDCS-51700956.summary: This is a very brief inscription wishing success to Emperor Augustus, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-51701002.translation: Seizing the opportunity, I did not pass up writing to you so that you would know I am well. Antenor, having escaped from the midst of the Achaeans, was able to reach the Illyrian seas.
EDCS-51701002.summary: This is a personal letter, likely a letter sent by Antenor to inform someone of his well-being and his journey to the Illyrian coast, probably from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-51800216.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: L. Caltilius Stephani and Caltilia Moschis.
EDCS-51800216.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the 'Dis Manes' (spirits of the underworld) for L. Caltilius Stephani and Caltilia Moschis, likely a family tomb from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-52000045.text_edition: \n Aponius |(mulieris) libertus Dio medicus pater vivit ossa heic sic sita sunt Luci Aponi Luci liberti Fausti amantissumi sic Aponia Ammia mater vivit\n
EDCS-52000045.translation: Dio, physician, freedman of Aponius (a woman), his father, is alive; his bones lie here as follows: to the most beloved Lucius Aponius Faustus, freedman of Lucius; his mother Aponia Ammia is alive.
EDCS-52000045.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph recording the burial arrangements and the parentage of Lucius Aponius Faustus, likely from a Roman imperial period tomb.

EDCS-52600138.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Caius Varius Euthycetus, who lived 3 years and 3 months. Papia Tyrannis, to her deserving wife.
EDCS-52600138.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Varius Euthycetus, recording his age at death and dedicated by his wife, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-52600140.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the name and age at death of Vesennia Faustilla, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-52600154.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Zosimenes, who deserved well.
EDCS-52600154.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the 'Dis Manes' for Zosimenes, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-52600158.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Crittius Erotis, who lived 30 years. Crittia Olympias, his most unhappy mother.
EDCS-52600158.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Crittius Erotis, dedicated by his grieving mother, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-52600159.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Munatius Pinnianus, dearest husband, who lived approximately 50 years. Cocceia Fortuna ta, his wife, made this, with her sons, to him who deserved well.
EDCS-52600159.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Munatius Pinnianus, dedicated by his wife and sons, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-52600160.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Egnatia Euthenia, who lived 22 years, 7 months, and 11 days. Aulus Egnatius Alypus, to his deserving wife.
EDCS-52600160.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Egnatia Euthenia, recording her age at death and dedicated by her husband, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-52600161.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Eutrapelus [set this up] for his devoted mother, Plutia Pia.
EDCS-52600161.summary: A funerary dedication by Eutrapelus for his mother, Plutia Pia.

EDCS-52600162.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gnaeus Haius Diadumenus [set this up] for his most excellent freedman, Cn. Haius Carpus.
EDCS-52600162.summary: A funerary dedication by Gnaeus Haius Diadumenus for his freedman, Cn. Haius Carpus.

EDCS-52600163.summary: An inscription possibly related to a workshop or its owner, Primitivus.

EDCS-52600165.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Arescusa, mother [set this up] for her sweetest daughter Iulianena, who deserved well. She lived 9 years, 7 months, 14 days.
EDCS-52600165.summary: A funerary dedication by Aelia Arescusa for her daughter Iulianena, who died at a young age.

EDCS-52600166.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Of Iulius Cornelius Maurus, a Roman knight.
EDCS-52600166.summary: A funerary dedication for Iulius Cornelius Maurus, a Roman knight.

EDCS-52600167.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aquilia Secunda set this up for her well-deserving parents, Iulius Iulianus and Aquilia Maxima.
EDCS-52600167.summary: A funerary dedication by Aquilia Secunda for her parents, Iulius Iulianus and Aquilia Maxima.

EDCS-52600168.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Vitalis, dutiful, lived 12 years, 3 months.
EDCS-52600168.summary: A funerary dedication for Vitalis, who died at the age of 12 years and 3 months.

EDCS-52600180.translation: Lucius Upellius, son of Mamius, of the Menenia tribe.
EDCS-52600180.summary: An inscription recording the name and tribal affiliation of Lucius Upellius.

EDCS-52600300.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Camurena Hieratica, slave of our Emperor Caesar, made and consecrated [this].
EDCS-52600300.summary: A funerary dedication by Camurena Hieratica, a slave of the emperor, possibly for herself or a relative.

EDCS-52600303.translation: For Tranquillitas. Melior, son of Voturia, of the Voturia tribe, a quinquennial perpetual priest of the deified Vespasian, placed this at his own expense for the honor.
EDCS-52600303.summary: A dedication by Melior, a priest of the deified Vespasian, for the honor of Tranquillitas.

EDCS-52800508.translation: To Ducenia Thallusa for herself and for her husband Tiberius Claudius Epagathus, and for her daughter Claudia Honorata, and for her freedmen and freedwomen, and for all their descendants, and for her very good friend Titus Terentius Epaphra.
EDCS-52800508.summary: This epitaph records a dedication made by Ducenia Thallusa for herself, her family, and her freedmen, and for a friend.

EDCS-52800518.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Marcus Antonius Chrysomallus, from his patron, his freedman, the best.
EDCS-52800518.summary: This epitaph is a dedication to Marcus Antonius Chrysomallus by his freedman who is also his patron.

EDCS-53000372.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Critonia Venusta, the best mother, and to Critonia Quarthilla, the most gentle grandmother, from Lucius Naevius Natalis for himself and his descendants.
EDCS-53000372.summary: This epitaph is a dedication by Lucius Naevius Natalis to his mother and grandmother, and for his descendants.

EDCS-53400826.summary: This is a simple ownership inscription, likely from a funerary monument or property marker, belonging to Lucius Munatius Adiectus.

EDCS-53400917.translation: Of Quintus Meidius, son of Quintus, freedman Philocenis, on the front [space for] 10 [feet?].
EDCS-53400917.summary: This is a boundary inscription indicating the width of a property belonging to the freedman Philocenis, son of Quintus Meidius.

EDCS-53400918.translation: [Space for illegible text]. Minatus, freedman of Quintus, in the field [space for illegible text] 13 feet.
EDCS-53400918.summary: This is a fragment of a boundary inscription in a field, likely measuring the property of Minatus, a freedman of Quintus.

EDCS-53600251.translation: From the estates of Agathyrsus, freedman of Augustus, of Quintus Pompilius Paetinus and Apronianus, consuls.
EDCS-53600251.summary: This inscription records the origin of goods from the estates of Agathyrsus, freedman of Augustus, and specifies the consuls Paetinus and Apronianus, dating it to their consulship.

EDCS-53600252.translation: Apronianus and Paetinus, consuls, from the workshops of Cornelius Mall(---), son of Publius, of Salarese, B(---).
EDCS-53600252.summary: This inscription indicates products from the workshops of Cornelius Mall(---), specifying the consuls Apronianus and Paetinus, dating it to their consulship.

EDCS-53600301.translation: Apronianus and Paetinus, consuls. Of Pomponius Vitalis, from the estates of Annius Verus, the Quintian holdings.
EDCS-53600301.summary: This inscription refers to property belonging to Pomponius Vitalis, sourced from the estates of Annius Verus, and dates to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-53600302.translation: Apronianus and Paetinus, consuls. Of Pomponius Vitalis, from the estates of Annius Verus, the Quintian holdings.
EDCS-53600302.summary: This inscription refers to property belonging to Pomponius Vitalis, sourced from the estates of Annius Verus, and dates to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

Caetronius Primitibus sic Helbius sic Secundus Perellius Saturninus Iulius Sallustius Mallius Rusticus Mallius Cassianus Ostulenius Lucanus Iulius Fortunatus Iulius Lucrio Dasumius Dexter Cosidius Clementinus Caecilius Saturus Plarius Pribatus sic Sallustius Calpurninus Tettius Lupercianus Domitius Certus Antonius Maro\n

EDCS-54601010.text_edition: \n Lucio Ceionio Commodo Sexto Vettuleno Civica Pompeiano consulibus ante diem V Idus Iunias Lanuvii in templo Antinoi in quo Lucius Caesennius Rufus patronus municipi conventum haberi iusserat per Lucium Pompeium um quinquennalem cultorum Dianae et Antinoi pollicitus est se conlaturum eis ex liberalitate sua HS XV (quindecim) milium nummum usum die natalis Dianae Idibus Augustis HS CCCCquadringenos nummos et die natalis Antinoi V Kalendas Decembres HS CCCCquadringenos nummos et praecepit legem ab ipsis constitutam sub tetrastylo Antinoi parte interiori perscribi in verba infra scripta Marco Antonio Hibero Publio Mummio Sisenna consulibus Kalendis Ianuariis collegium salutare Dianae et Antinoi constitutum Lucio Caesennio Luci filio Quirina Rufo dictatore III idemque patrono kaput ex senatus consulto populi Romani quibus permissum est convenire collegiumque habere liceat qui stipem menstruam conferre volent ad facienda sacra in it sic collegium coeant neque sub specie eius collegi nisi semel in mense coeant stipem conferendi causa unde defuncti sepeliantur quod faustum felix salutareque sit Imperatori Caesari Traiano Hadriano Augusto totiusque domus Augusti nobis nostris collegioque nostro et bene adque sic industrie contraxerimus ut exitus defunctorum honeste prosequamur itaque bene conferendo universi consentire debemus ut longo tempore inveterescere possimus tu qui novos in hoc collegio intrare voles prius legem perlege et sic intra ne postmodum queraris aut heredi tuo controversiam relinquas lexs sic collegi placuit universis ut quisquis in hoc collegium intrare voluerit dabit kapitulari nomine HS C nummos et vini boni amphoram item in menses singulos asses V item placuit ut quisquis mensibus continui sex non pariaverit et ei humanitas acciderit eius ratio funeris non habebitur etiamsi testamentum factum habuerit item placuit quisquis ex hoc corpore nostro pariatus decesserit eum sequentur ex arca HS CCC nummi ex qua summa decedent exequiari nomine HS L nummi qui ad rogus sic dividentur exequiae autem pedibus fungentur item placuit quisquis a municipio ultra milliarium XX decesserit et nuntiatum fuerit eo exire debebunt electi ex corpore nostro homines tres qui funeris eius curam agant et rationem populo reddere debebunt sine dolo malo et si quit sic in eis fraudis causa inventum fuerit eis multa esto quadruplum quibus funeraticium eius dabitur hoc amplius viatici nomine ultro citro singulis HS XX nummi quodsi longius a municipio supra milliarium XX decesserit et nuntiari non potuerit tum is qui eum funeraverit testator rem tabulis signatis sigillis civium Romanorum VII et probata causa funeraticium eius satis dato amplius neminem petiturum deductis commodis et exequiario e lege collegi dari ei debebit a nostro collegio dolus malus abesto neque patrono neque patronae neque domino neque dominae neque creditori ex hoc collegio ulla petitio esto nisi si quis testamento heres nominatus erit si quis intestatus decesserit is arbitrio quinquennalis et populi funerabitur item placuit quisquis ex hoc collegio servus defunctus fuerit et corpus eius a domino dominave iniquitatae sepulturae datum non fuerit neque tabellas fecerit ei funus imaginarium fiet item placuit quisquis ex quacumque causa mortem sibi adsciverit eius ratio funeris non habebitur item placuit ut quisquis servus ex hoc collegio liber factus fuerit is dare debebit vini boni amphoram item placuit quisquis magister suo anno erit ex ordine albi ad cenam faciendam et non observaverit neque fecerit is arcae inferet HS XXX nummos insequens eius dare debebit et is eius loco restituere debebit ordo cenarum VIII Idus Martias natali Caesenni patris V Kalendas Decembres natali Antinoi Idibus Augustis natali Dianae et collegi XIII Kalendas Septembres natali Caesenni Silvani fratrais sic pridie Nonas natali Corneliae Proculae matris XIX Kalendas Ianuarias natali Caesenni Rufi patroni municipi magistri cenarum ex ordine albi facti quoquo ordine homines quaterni ponere debebunt vini boni amphoras singulas et panes assium II qui numerus collegi fuerit et sardas numero quattuor strationem caldam cum ministerio item placuit ut quisquis quinquennalis in hoc collegio factus fuerit is a sigillis eius temporis quo quinquennalis erit immunis esse debebit et ei ex omnibus divisionibus partes duplas dari item scribae et viatori a sigillis vacantibus partes ex omni divisione sesquiplas dari placuit item placuit ut quisquis quinquennalitatem gesserit integre ei ob honorem partes sesquiplas ex omni re dari ut et reliqui recte faciendo idem sperent item placuit ut quis quid queri aut referre volet in conventu referat ut quieti et hilares diebus sollemnibus epulemur item placuit si quisquis seditionis causa de loco in alium locum transierit ei multa esto HS IIII nummum si quis autem in obprobrium sic alter alterius dixerit aut tumultuatus fuerit ei multa esto HS XII nummum si quis quinquennali inter epulas obprobrium sic aut quid contumeliose dixerit ei multa esto HS XX nummum item placuit ut quinquennalis sui cuiusque temporis diebus sollemnibus ture et vino supplicet et ceteris officiis albatus fungatur et diebus natalibus Dianae et Antinoi oleum collegio in balinio publico ponat antequam epulentur\n

EDCS-55300322.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying a man by his name and patronymic.

EDCS-55300323.translation: Lucius Decius Rufus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-55300323.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying a man by his full name and patronymic.

EDCS-55300325.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying a woman by her name and her former master.

EDCS-55400006.translation: Publius Claudius Abascantus, son of Publius Claudius Abascantianus, to his most dear son. I, Publius Claudius Abascantus, quinquennial magistrate of the dendrophorus guild of Ostia, have permitted the erection of a statue in the Field of the Mother of the Gods, the infantile one, under the consulship of Plautianus and Geta, on the ninth day before the Kalends of April.
EDCS-55400006.summary: This inscription records the permission granted by a magistrate for the erection of a statue in Ostia, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE based on the consuls.

EDCS-55702360.translation: On the front, 14 feet; in the field, 16 feet.
EDCS-55702360.summary: This is a boundary inscription, likely part of a larger funerary monument, indicating the dimensions of a plot of land.

EDCS-55702366.translation: Camerinus Antistius Vetus, praetor, at the request of Marcus Caecilius Primus, granted possession, by edict, to Cn. Nassius Zenon, of the goods which had belonged to Herennia Tertia, upon her death, according to the terms of her will. Done on the 12th day before the Ides of October, under the consulship of Quintus Curtius Rufus and Spurius Oppius. [List of witnesses follows]. Camerinus Antistius Vetus, praetor, at the request of Marcus Caecilius Primus, granted possession, by edict, to Cn. Nassius Zenon, of the goods which had belonged to Herennia Tertia, upon her death, according to the terms of her will. Done on the 12th day before the Ides of October, under the consulship of Quintus Curtius Rufus and Spurius Oppius.
EDCS-55702366.summary: This inscription records a legal transaction where a praetor transfers ownership of an estate according to a will, dated to 55 CE.

EDCS-55702368.translation: Of Gnaeus Atellius Miserinus, son of Gnaeus.
EDCS-55702368.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, identifying a man by his name and patronymic.

EDCS-55800015.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-55800015.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (likely Caracalla) from the local council, highlighting his imperial lineage and dating it to his reign (198-217 CE).

EDCS-56300198.translation: [...]ro / [...]ia Avita [made this] for her most excellent husband. On the front, 150 feet; in the field, 150 feet.
EDCS-56300198.summary: This is a funerary epitaph indicating a wife's dedication to her husband and specifying the dimensions of their burial plot.

EDCS-56500220.translation: To Gnaeus Octavius, son of Gnaeus, military tribune, quaestor, and designated praetor. Caudina, daughter of Caius, [made this for her] son.
EDCS-56500220.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a mother to her son, who had a distinguished career in the Roman military and magistracy.

EDCS-56500221.translation: Caudina, daughter of Caius, of Crispus.
EDCS-56500221.summary: This is a tombstone for Caudina, daughter of Caius, belonging to Crispus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56500233.translation: Of the Caians (or of Caius), of the Alfii, Dionysis and Italicus. In front 52 feet, in the field 26 feet.
EDCS-56500233.summary: This inscription appears to record land boundaries or dimensions belonging to Dionysis and Italicus of the Alfii family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56500255.translation: Marcus Barronius Sura, son of Marcus.
EDCS-56500255.summary: This is a simple personal name inscription, likely for a tombstone or ownership marker, of Marcus Barronius Sura, son of Marcus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57000110.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. Aecia Cupita made this for her mother Capronia Veneria and her husband Marcus Vescenius Cinnamus, with whom she lived for 35 years, 6 months, and 5 days, a deserving woman.
EDCS-57000110.summary: This funerary inscription honors Capronia Veneria and Marcus Vescenius Cinnamus, erected by their daughter and wife, Aecia Cupita, and records the length of the marriage, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57100004.translation: Iustus, of the Emperor, lived 50 years.
EDCS-57100004.summary: This is a short funerary inscription for Iustus, who lived 50 years and was possibly an imperial freedman or servant, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57100008.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, conqueror of Armenia, the Medes, the Parthians, in his eighth tribunician power, third consulship, father of the fatherland. Gnaeus Sergius Praenestinus, son of Gnaeus, of the Palatina tribe, [restored text indicating his actions] expanded this.
EDCS-57100008.summary: This inscription dedicates or records work done by Gnaeus Sergius Praenestinus in honor of Emperor Marcus Aurelius (as Verus), detailing his titles and dignities, dating to his co-rule with Marcus Aurelius, likely around 161-169 AD.

EDCS-57100016.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. For Gaius Julius Italicus, soldier from the praetorian fleet of Misenum, on the trireme Hercules. Iulia Helpidus for her deserving husband.
EDCS-57100016.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Italicus, a soldier from the Misenum fleet, erected by his wife Iulia Helpidus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57100021.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. For his most rare wife, Rustia Epicharis. Gaius Rustius Secundus.
EDCS-57100021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rustia Epicharis, erected by her husband Gaius Rustius Secundus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57100046.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. For Vitellia Callista.
EDCS-57100046.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vitellia Callista, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57100047.translation: Licinia Laeis, freedwoman of Gaius. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-57100047.summary: This inscription likely marks a plot of land or a tomb, indicating dimensions and belonging to Licinia Laeis, a freedwoman of Gaius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57100053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased Successa, who died at 19 years of age.

EDCS-57100150.summary: This is a very brief epitaph, likely part of a larger inscription, identifying Heia as the daughter of Papius.

EDCS-57200065.translation: Gaius Julius Apella, freedman of Gaius and Lucius Sertorius; Iulia Aphrodisia, freedwoman of Gaius; Iulia Eleutheris, freedwoman of Gaius and his wife; Gaius Julius Latinus, freedman of Gaius and his wife; Iulia Sabbatis, freedwoman of his wife.
EDCS-57200065.summary: This is a funerary epitaph listing multiple individuals, likely family members or freedmen associated with a freedman of Gaius and Lucius Sertorius, indicating their relationships.

EDCS-57200067.translation: Of Decimus Nonius Agatho, son of freedman Decimus. In the front 15 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-57200067.summary: This inscription likely marks a burial plot or property boundary, identifying the owner as Decimus Nonius Agatho and specifying its dimensions.

EDCS-57200068.translation: Lucius Appuleius Eros. Villia Faustina. In the front 20 feet, in the field 25 feet.
EDCS-57200068.summary: This inscription, possibly marking a tomb or property, names Lucius Appuleius Eros and Villia Faustina and provides dimensions for the plot.

EDCS-57200069.translation: Titus Mallius Philoxenus, freedman of Titus. In the front 15 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-57200069.summary: This inscription records the name Titus Mallius Philoxenus, a freedman, and specifies the dimensions of a plot of land, likely for burial or property.

EDCS-57200072.translation: Titus Manlius Alexsa, freedman of Titus. Labicia, daughter of Marcus. In the front 20 feet, in the field 25 feet.
EDCS-57200072.summary: This inscription, likely marking a grave or property, names Titus Manlius Alexsa and Labicia, daughter of Marcus, and provides dimensions for the plot.

EDCS-57200076.translation: Of Gaius Celius Salvius Armeni, freedman of Gaius Celius.
EDCS-57200076.summary: This inscription likely dedicates a monument or marks a tomb, naming Gaius Celius Salvius Armeni as a freedman of Gaius Celius.

EDCS-57200077.translation: Maria Chreste, freedwoman of Gaius. Gaius Marius Brutus, son. In the front 16 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-57200077.summary: This funerary inscription names Maria Chreste and Gaius Marius Brutus, specifying their familial relationships and the dimensions of a plot.

EDCS-57300070.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Motho, slave of our Caesar. To Hilarius and Filumena, most dutiful and dearest sons, with their own, he made this. Hilarius lived 2 years, 4 months, and 19 days. Filumena, 14 years, 5 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-57300070.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to two sons, Hilarius and Filumena, by their parent Motho, a slave of Caesar, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-57400162.translation: Gaius Volusius Salvius, son of Gaius, freedman of Gaius Volusius. Heracleo, freedman of Gaius Volusius. Heraclea, freedwoman of Gaius Volusius.
EDCS-57400162.summary: This inscription lists the names of freedmen and freedwomen, likely belonging to the Volusia gens.

EDCS-57400163.translation: Sextus Aelius Seno, freedman of Sextus, and patron. Sempronia Agathemeris, freedwoman of Decimus, and patrona. Sextus Sempronius Nanus, freedman of Sextus, freedman of his wife. Fausta, freedwoman of Sempronius, freedman of his wife. // Sabinus, freedman of Sextus, freedman of his wife. Sextus Sempronius Eutucus, freedman of Sextus, freedman of his wife. Sempronia Elpis, freedwoman of Sextus, freedwoman of his wife. Sempronia Hilara, freedwoman of Sextus, freedwoman of his wife. Sextus Sempronius Epaphroditus, freedman of Sextus, freedman of his wife. Heirs do not succeed to this monument except those whose names are inscribed.
EDCS-57400163.summary: This is a funerary inscription detailing family members and stating that only those named are to inherit the monument.

EDCS-57400164.translation: Publius Cornelius Euhemerus, freedman of Publius. Aridia Eutaxia, freedwoman of Aridius. In front 15 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-57400164.summary: This is a boundary inscription for a funerary plot measuring 15 feet in front and 20 feet in depth.

EDCS-57500021.translation: Decimus Nonius Philomusus, freedman of Maurus, made this for himself and his family. In front 15 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-57500021.summary: This funerary inscription records the dimensions of a plot (15x20 feet) and names the dedicator.

EDCS-57500022.translation: The mother, Viva Atania Polla, daughter of Decimus, mourns her dearest son, Gaius Atanius Bassus, son of Gaius, pious, good, modest. She made this monument or sepulchre from her own money for herself. Heirs shall not succeed to this. In front 30 feet, in the field 25 feet.
EDCS-57500022.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a mother for her beloved son, also reserving space for herself, and stating that only named heirs can inherit.

EDCS-57500023.translation: Sergia Helena, freedwoman of Aulus, made this for herself and for Susa, his freedman, and for Acilianus and Felix, her own slaves, and for Sergia Pamphila, freedwoman of Aulus, from her own money. This monument does not follow an heir. In front 15 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-57500023.summary: This funerary inscription by Sergia Helena names herself, two freedmen, and two slaves for whom the monument is intended, specifying its dimensions and hereditary restrictions.

EDCS-57500024.translation: Lucius Volumnius Achibae, freedman of Lucius. Cornelia Chreste, freedwoman of Arrius.
EDCS-57500024.summary: This inscription simply lists the names of two individuals, Lucius Volumnius Achibae and Cornelia Chreste, likely related to a funerary monument.

EDCS-57500025.translation: Gaius Geminius Stabilio, freedman of Gaius. // Gaius Geminius Stabilio, freedman of Gaius. Bruttia Aletheia, freedwoman of Lucius. Bruttia Prima, freedwoman of Gaius and of his wife. Gaius Geminius Primus, freedman of Gaius. // Gaius Geminius Stabilio, freedman of Gaius. Bruttia Aletheia, freedwoman of Lucius. Bruttia Prima, freedwoman of Gaius and of his wife. Gaius Geminius Primus, freedman of Gaius.
EDCS-57500025.summary: This inscription appears to be a repeated listing of names, likely for a family tomb, possibly indicating multiple burials or a duplication of inscription.

EDCS-57500026.translation: Lucius Poblicius Diodorus, freedman of Lucius, for himself and for Lucius Poblicius Demetrius, his patron, his freedman. In front 15 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-57500026.summary: This funerary inscription specifies the dimensions of the plot (15x20 feet) and dedicates the monument to the patron and his freedman.

EDCS-57500027.translation: Publius Manlius Achiba, freedman of Publius. Lucilia Corinthis, freedwoman of Aulus. Publius Manlius Mambugae, freedman of Publius.
EDCS-57500027.summary: This inscription lists the names of three individuals, likely freedmen and their patrons, possibly related to a funerary monument.

EDCS-57500028.translation: Quintus Vitellius Amphio, freedman of Quintus (son of Aulus?), Iunia Suavis, freedwoman of Gnaeus, and Vitellia Secunda, freedwoman of Quintus (son of Aulus?), for themselves and their families.
EDCS-57500028.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Vitellius Amphio and his family, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-57500031.translation: Lucius Volusius Nicephor, freedman of Lucius; Lucius Volusius Antiochus, freedman of Lucius; Volusia Philematium, freedwoman of Lucius; Scantia Salvia, mother of the woman, freedwoman of Marcus; Caelia Andromacha, daughter, freedwoman of the woman. In life: Lucius Volusius Quartio, freedman of Lucius. (Ages: 20 and 15).
EDCS-57500031.summary: This is a complex funerary epitaph listing several individuals, likely members of the Volusia family and their freedmen, with ages possibly indicated for two individuals, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-57500032.summary: This is a very short funerary inscription, likely a tomb marker, for Lucius Volusius Nicephor, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-57500033.summary: This is a very short funerary inscription, likely a tomb marker, for Lucius Volusius Nicephor, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-57600009.translation: To the departed spirits. For Cassia Felicisima, who lived 38 years and 2 months. Sex(tus) Pompeius Genialis made (this) for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-57600009.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Sex(tus) Pompeius Genialis to his wife Cassia Felicisima, providing her age at death, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-57600010.translation: To the departed spirits. For Titus Flavius Demosthenes, who lived 23 years and 8 months. (From) his incomparable sister to her brother.
EDCS-57600010.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from a sister to her brother, Titus Flavius Demosthenes, providing his age at death, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-57600038.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Abeius Sereno, who lived with me 20 years.
EDCS-57600038.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Abeius Sereno, stating he lived with the dedicator for 20 years, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-57700031.translation: To the departed spirits. For Caius He[...]
EDCS-57700031.summary: This is a very fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for a 'Caius He...', dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-57700032.translation: Caius Lucceius Heraclida, freedman of Caius. In front 16 feet, in the field 19 feet.
EDCS-57700032.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land belonging to Caius Lucceius Heraclida, a freedman, likely for funerary purposes, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-58400066.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Augusto patri patriae sublevatori orbis restitutori operum publicorum indulgentissimo optimoque principi Campani ob insignem erga eos benignitatem decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-58400066.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Augustus, father of the fatherland, supporter of the world, restorer of public works, most indulgent and best of princes. The Campanians, by decree of the decurions, (erected this) for his exceptional kindness towards them.
EDCS-58400066.summary: This is a public inscription praising Emperor Hadrian for his benefactions to the region of Campania, likely dating to his reign (117-138 CE).

EDCS-58400071.translation: Iulius Trophimus vowed this and, having been reminded, dedicated it to the blessed goddess Fortuna, the first offspring of the goddesses. If you deem me worthy of my vow and my dream, O blessed one, welcome me! Dedicated on the 9th of April, when Claros and Cethegus were consuls.
EDCS-58400071.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Fortuna by Iulius Trophimus, likely on a shrine or altar, with a specific dedication date and consuls provided.

EDCS-58700004.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription, possibly a tombstone or epitaph, greeting Victoria.

EDCS-58800031.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Sergius Paullus [erected this] for his friend Titus Severinius Victor, procurator of the province of Cilicia, also of the twentieth prefecture of inheritance in Campania, and of Bithynia, honored with three military commands.
EDCS-58800031.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Lucius Sergius Paullus to his friend Titus Severinius Victor, a high-ranking imperial official with extensive administrative duties, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-59100065.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Flavia Felicitas made this for her well-deserving husband Marcus Aquilius Victus, and Aquilius Victorinus for his deserving father.
EDCS-59100065.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aelia Flavia Felicitas for her husband Marcus Aquilius Victus and by Aquilius Victorinus for his father, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-59100094.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Timetus, the deserving slave, Sepullius erected [this].
EDCS-59100094.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Sepullius placed a memorial for his deserving slave, Timetus.

EDCS-59200006.translation: To Juno Plautiae Hymnianae, granddaughter, who lived 1 year, 3 months, and 19 days. Plautia Germanilla, grandmother, and Cornelius Aelianus, foster father, most sorrowful.
EDCS-59200006.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a young girl named Plautiae Hymnianae, erected by her grandmother and foster father, indicating her young age at death.

EDCS-59300045.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aurelius Taurus made this for his well-deserving wife, Claudia Prisca.
EDCS-59300045.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by Publius Aurelius Taurus for his wife Claudia Prisca, describing her as well-deserving.

EDCS-59300047.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Phronimus [erected this] for his most devoted brother, Quintus Satrius Alexander.
EDCS-59300047.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Phronimus to his brother Quintus Satrius Alexander, whom he describes as most devoted.

EDCS-59400007.translation: Publius Avvius Hedus, son of Publius, praetor, interrex, [erected this] at his own expense.
EDCS-59400007.summary: This inscription records Publius Avvius Hedus, son of Publius, who held the high offices of praetor and interrex and funded the monument himself.

EDCS-59400100.inscription_type: tituli fabricationis, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-59400100.text_edition: \n Lucius Hortensius Heraclidanus classis praetoria Misenensis\n
EDCS-59400100.translation: Lucius Hortensius Heraclidanus of the praetorian fleet of Misenum.
EDCS-59400100.summary: This is a funerary or commemorative inscription identifying Lucius Hortensius Heraclidanus as a member of the elite praetorian fleet stationed at Misenum.

EDCS-59400101.translation: To the most unconquered and most indulgent Emperor Probus Augustus, chief priest, consul for the eighth time, for the fourth time.
EDCS-59400101.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Probus, likely from the late 3rd century CE, honoring his imperial titles.

EDCS-59400115.translation: Acilius Celadus perhaps dedicated this to Juno Sospita, the Great Mother, and Isis, who gave oracles.
EDCS-59400115.summary: This is a dedication to the goddesses Juno Sospita and Isis, made by Acilius Celadus, probably in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-59700003.translation: Marcus [Can]tan[eus...] Proc[ul]us and [his wife?] Valer[ianae] and [his daughter?] Victori[a] his son, and Marcus Cantaneus Vestalis, his freedman freedman, and their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. In the front 35 feet, in the field 32 and a half feet. From the gate 11 miles.
EDCS-59700003.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, detailing property dimensions and family members, including freedmen.

EDCS-59700004.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Carullius Decembris, who lived 8 years, 8 months, and 18 days. Lucius Carullius Romulus and Iubentia Melitine made this for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-59700004.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Carullius Decembris, who died at a young age, set up by his parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-59700005.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelia Galatia, mother, made this for her son Aulus Cornelius Verus Amarantianus, who lived 12 years, 2 months, and 27 days.
EDCS-59700005.summary: This is a tombstone for Aulus Cornelius Verus Amarantianus, who died young, set up by his mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-59700006.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Sextius Aprilis, his freedman, made this for himself and for Caius Sextius Besso, his son, and for Sextius Primus, freedman, and for Sextia Corinthias, freedwoman, and for [his wife?] and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. In the front [X] feet, in the field 19 feet.
EDCS-59700006.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, for the original owner, his son, freedmen, and their descendants, with property dimensions.

EDCS-59700008.translation: To the departed spirits. Veratia Fortunata made this for Marcus Valerius Trophimus.
EDCS-59700008.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Valerius Trophimus, made by Veratia Fortunata, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-59700013.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, mentioning the name Arruntius.

EDCS-59700024.translation: To the departed spirits. Mutia Tychē made this for her husband Aulus Mucius Successus and for herself.
EDCS-59700024.summary: This is a tombstone for Aulus Mucius Successus, made by his wife Mutia Tychē who also prepared it for herself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-59700029.translation: To the departed spirits. Acilia Beronice made this for her granddaughter Seia Sabina, who lived 12 years and 6 months, for her well-deserving granddaughter.
EDCS-59700029.summary: This is a tombstone for Seia Sabina, who died young, set up by her grandmother Acilia Beronice, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-59700031.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Gaius Septimius Eutyches, from his own funds, and Uttedius Felix, from his own funds, granted to themselves the rights of this monument, which had been granted to them by Marcus Cornelius Felicissimus while he was alive; Eutyches also granted (rights to) a portion to his most happy wife, Valeria.):, a most excellent woman, and to their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-59700031.summary: This funerary inscription records the granting of rights to a monument by Gaius Septimius Eutyches and Uttedius Felix, likely made in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-59700032.translation: To the gods of the underworld. For Claudia Nices, Claudius Myron made (this) for his incomparable wife Cleopatra.
EDCS-59700032.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudius Myron to his wife Claudia Nices (also referred to as Cleopatra), likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-59700035.translation: To the gods of the underworld. For Rhodinus, his freedman and teacher. Gaius Julius Silvanus (dedicated this).
EDCS-59700035.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Gaius Julius Silvanus to his freedman and former teacher, Rhodinus, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-59700065.translation: To the gods of the underworld. For Marcus Suellius Hermetis, whom his freedwoman Suellia Psyche made (this) for her well-deserving husband and for herself.
EDCS-59700065.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Suellia Psyche for her husband Marcus Suellius Hermetis and herself, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-59700066.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aulo Caedicio Auli filio Saeculari praetori sacris Volkani faciundis qui vixit nnis V mensibus XI diebus X\n
EDCS-59700066.translation: To the gods of the underworld. For Aulus Caedicius Saecularis, son of Aulus, who served as praetor and was in charge of the sacred rites of Vulcan, who lived 5 years, 11 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-59700066.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Aulus Caedicius Saecularis, a young boy who held a priestly office, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-59700067.translation: Dedicated to the gods of the underworld. For Utia Tyche, dearest daughter of Utia Auxesina, who lived 4 years, 3 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-59700067.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Utia Auxesina to her daughter Utia Tyche, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-59700071.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Aelio Augusti liberto Secundo Caesonia Egloge marito\n
EDCS-59700071.translation: To the gods of the underworld. For Titus Aelius Secundus, freedman of Augustus. Caesonia Egloge (dedicated this) for her husband.
EDCS-59700071.summary: This funerary inscription was dedicated by Caesonia Egloge to her husband, Titus Aelius Secundus, a freedman of the emperor, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-59700097.translation: To the gods of the underworld. For Marsenia Vitalis, who lived 21 years, 5 months, and 18 days, an incomparable woman.
EDCS-59700097.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of Marsenia Vitalis, a young woman, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-59700106.translation: To the gods of the underworld. For Chryseros, freedman of Faustus [Augustus' freedman].
EDCS-59700106.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription mentions Chryseros, a freedman of Faustus, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-59700108.translation: To the gods of the underworld. For Aurelia Prisca, daughter of Lucius. Lucius Aurelius Priscus (her father) made (this) for his most dutiful daughter, who lived 19 years, 3 months, and 22 days.
EDCS-59700108.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Lucius Aurelius Priscus for his daughter Aurelia Prisca, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-59700109.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caaelia Damocratina made this for her most beloved husband, Lucius Claudius Pudentianus, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-59700109.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Claudius Pudentianus, set up by his wife, Caaelia Damocratina.

EDCS-59700110.translation: Dionysias made this for herself and for her sweet son, Nonius Felix, who lived 31 years, 9 months, and some days. Lucius Septimius Constantins rests here.
EDCS-59700110.summary: A funerary epitaph for Dionysias, her son Nonius Felix, and Lucius Septimius Constantins, likely a family tomb.

EDCS-59700128.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Gaius Ostiensis Eu[---] and for Gaius Ostiensis Te[---].
EDCS-59700128.summary: A funerary epitaph for at least two individuals named Gaius Ostiensis, possibly father and son or brothers.

EDCS-59700133.translation: [---]ori, her grandson, who lived 3 years, 8 months, and 6 days, made this.
EDCS-59700133.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child, possibly set up by a grandparent, recording their lifespan.

EDCS-59700137.translation: Petronia Heliodora, freedwoman of Lucius, made this for her patron Lucius Petronius Felix, freedman of Lucius, and for her fellow freedman Lucius Petronius Stephano. The front is 16 feet, the land is 30 feet.
EDCS-59700137.summary: A funerary epitaph for freedmen and a patron, also including property dimensions, likely marking a burial plot.

EDCS-59700138.translation: [---]ric, [---]ntino, who lived 13 years, 2 months, and 2 days.
EDCS-59700138.summary: A funerary epitaph for an individual named 'ntino' (likely a nickname or part of a name), recording their short lifespan.

EDCS-59700145.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Iulius [---], who lived [---]. Aelia Am[---] to one who deserved well.
EDCS-59700145.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Iulius, set up by Aelia Am[---], indicating a deserving recipient.

EDCS-59700148.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Betulenius Primitiva to her deserving husband Mercurius, (she) paid. To the spirits of the underworld. Atia Felix to her most beloved wife Fructa Secunda, Isidorus to his most beloved wife Fructa Secunda, who lived 41 years and closed [her life].
EDCS-59700148.summary: A double funerary epitaph with two separate dedications to deceased spouses, one by Betulenius Primitiva to Mercurius, and another by Isidorus to Fructa Secunda.

EDCS-59700149.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Aelia Tertullina, her most beloved mother. Aurelius Tertullinus and Gaiana, children, to one who deserved well.
EDCS-59700149.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelia Tertullina, dedicated by her children Aurelius Tertullinus and Gaiana.

EDCS-60000010.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Philetus lived 3 years, 6 months, 8 days.
EDCS-60000010.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Philetus, recording their short lifespan.

EDCS-60000011.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Flavonius Agathopus.

EDCS-60000100.translation: Gaius Arrius Primus, a priest of Augustus.
EDCS-60000100.summary: This funerary inscription names Gaius Arrius Primus, who held the priesthood of Augustus.

EDCS-60000101.translation: Gaius Arrius Avius lived for 15 years.
EDCS-60000101.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Gaius Arrius Avius, who died at age 15.

EDCS-60000102.translation: To Gaius Arrius Tertius, who lived for 22 years.
EDCS-60000102.summary: This funerary inscription records that Gaius Arrius Tertius lived for 22 years.

EDCS-60000114.translation: To the departed spirits. For Publius Manlius Valens, who lived for one year, eight months, and twelve days. His parents, Longinus and Zotica, and his brother Valentino, who lived for five months, made this.
EDCS-60000114.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Publius Manlius Valens by his parents and brother, detailing their lifespans.

EDCS-60000118.translation: To the departed spirits. Aureli[---] Vivio[---] PO[---]
EDCS-60000118.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription.

EDCS-60000122.translation: To the departed spirits. Serenus of Alexandria lived for 6 years and 7 months.
EDCS-60000122.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life of Serenus of Alexandria, who died at age 6 years and 7 months.

EDCS-60000124.translation: Chrysis, a homeborn slave, lived for 13 years.
EDCS-60000124.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Chrysis, a homeborn slave, who died at age 13.

EDCS-60200103.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a dedication or ownership mark.

EDCS-60200119.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus GeI vixit annos II Ge\n
EDCS-60200119.translation: To the departed spirits. [Name] lived for 2 years.
EDCS-60200119.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual who died at the age of 2.

EDCS-60200138.summary: A funerary inscription with a lacuna, recording the life of an individual.

EDCS-60200179.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Prunice.

EDCS-60300078.summary: A dedication to Publicia Salvia, likely funerary.

EDCS-60400001.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Pontia Felicitas for her most dutiful son Pontius Felix, or his elder son [?]. He lived for 11 years, 11 months, and 27 days. For freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. If anyone wishes to sell or alienate [this property], it shall then belong to the Augustales of Puteoli.
EDCS-60400001.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Pontius Felix, with a clause regarding the disposition of property, likely from Puteoli, dated by the mention of Augustales.

EDCS-60500010.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Iusta, daughter of Caius, lived for 35 years, 3 months, and 15 days. Caius Iulius Plotianus for his sweetest sister.
EDCS-60500010.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Iusta, set up by her brother, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-60500011.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelius Iustus, a husband well-deserving, with whom [she] lived for 35 years. To the Spirits of the Departed. Otacilia Isias for her well-deserving husband Marcus Caecilius Frugifer.
EDCS-60500011.summary: A double funerary epitaph for two spouses, Aurelius Iustus and Marcus Caecilius Frugifer, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-60500035.translation: To Lucius Varronius Capito, son of Lucius, of the Palatina tribe, scribe of the aediles, a veiled attendant, quinquennial duumvir, patron of the colony. Gavia Eugenia, daughter of Lucius, [erected this] for her husband. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-60500035.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Varronius Capito, a prominent local official, likely from a Roman colony, listing his career and mentioning the grant of burial land.

EDCS-60600019.translation: Tiberius Claudius Iustus Lollianus gave this from his own funds.
EDCS-60600019.summary: A dedication by Tiberius Claudius Iustus Lollianus, recording a personal contribution.

EDCS-60600027.translation: To Ducenius Aquilinus, our sweet son, who lived for 8 years, 10 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-60600027.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Ducenius Aquilinus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-60600047.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Marcus Gessius Maximus, who lived for 35 years, 6 months, and 24 days. Gessia Marcia, mother, made this for her most dutiful son. The site was granted by Aurelia Hilara.
EDCS-60600047.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Gessius Maximus, set up by his mother, with the burial plot granted by Aurelia Hilara, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-60700077.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Varius Euanthes made this for his most exceptional wife, Varia Saturnina, with whom he lived for 32 years.
EDCS-60700077.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deceased wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60700078.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Helpis made this for her well-deserving husband, Aulus Gabinius Helion, who lived 65 years, 9 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-60700078.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her deceased husband, indicating his lifespan and likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60700085.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Sextus Offanius Satyrus made this monument for himself, his wife Marcia Phaedra, his son Sextus Offanius Satyrus, his children, his brother Sextus Offanius Trophimus, his slaves, his freedwomen, and their descendants; this external monument shall not pass to an heir, nor shall it be alienated, nor shall the bones or ashes or bodies of the dead be brought into it by my will or that of my aforementioned relatives, except for those mentioned above, nor shall it be permitted to be transferred to another name.
EDCS-60700085.summary: This is a complex funerary monument inscription detailing the intended burial rights for Sextus Offanius Satyrus, his family, and freedpersons, with strict conditions on inheritance and use, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60800025.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication to Marcia Britionia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60800027.translation: Marcus Asellinius Callimachus, Augustalis, lived 60 years.
EDCS-60800027.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Asellinius Callimachus, an Augustalis, indicating his age and likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60800028.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Ianuarius, a freedman of Col(onus?), lived 28 years.
EDCS-60800028.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ianuarius, a freedman, indicating his age and likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60800030.translation: Geminia Verecunda lived 70 years, 11 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-60800030.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Geminia Verecunda, indicating her lifespan and likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60800031.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Marcella, who lived 10 years and 15 days, Mammula made this for her deserving beloved.
EDCS-60800031.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Mammula for Marcella, likely her daughter, indicating her young age and dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60800034.summary: This is a funerary dedication identifying Viria Quarta as a freedwoman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-60800087.translation: To Father Dis. Titus Flavius Adiectus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-60800087.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Dis by Titus Flavius Adiectus.

EDCS-61000033.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely identifying the owner or subject related to Titus Flavius Phileconteus.

EDCS-61400198.translation: Quintus Vibius Patulus, son of Quintus; Vibia Tertia, daughter of Quintus; to their own [family?].
EDCS-61400198.summary: This is a funerary inscription or dedication possibly for Quintus Vibius Patulus and Vibia Tertia.

EDCS-61500350.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the lifespan of Terpnos.

EDCS-61500352.translation: Marcus Epidius Monimus lived for 30 years.
EDCS-61500352.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the lifespan of Marcus Epidius Monimus.

EDCS-61500353.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Epidius Amphinomus.

EDCS-61500355.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the lifespan of Livia Calliope.

EDCS-61500357.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the lifespan of Cornelia Helpis.

EDCS-61500358.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the lifespan of Liberalis.

EDCS-61500359.translation: Of Acisclus, son of Gaius Naevius, son of Marcus, of the Menenia tribe.
EDCS-61500359.summary: This is a funerary inscription or identification marker for Acisclus, son of Gaius Naevius.

EDCS-61500360.translation: To Marco Popidio Coclea, my son.
EDCS-61500360.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marco Popidio Coclea, son of the dedicator.

EDCS-61500361.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Geganius Hymenaeus, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-61500362.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age at death of Felix.

EDCS-61500363.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Valeria Pothine, likely funerary.

EDCS-61500365.translation: Mythus Epidi Flacci, freedman, lived 75 years.
EDCS-61500365.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, status as a freedman, and age at death of Mythus.

EDCS-61500366.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Epidius Dioscorus, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-61500367.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age at death of Ianuarius.

EDCS-61500369.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age at death of Vivia Crocine.

EDCS-61500370.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marco Epidius Thychnus, likely funerary.

EDCS-61500371.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Aemilius Milo, likely funerary.

EDCS-61500373.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Epidia Euhodia, who died at 18 years of age.

EDCS-61500374.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating the monument to Epidia Veneria.

EDCS-61500376.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Primigenia, who died at 18 years of age.

EDCS-61500377.translation: Of Lucius Livineius Dex, [likely a tomb inscription].
EDCS-61500377.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name of Lucius Livineius Dex.

EDCS-61500378.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Felicio, who died at 28 years of age.

EDCS-61500379.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Gonus, who died at 30 years of age.

EDCS-61500380.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Glypte, who died at 4 years of age.

EDCS-61600198.translation: Capidia Tertia, freedwoman of Caius, [in an area of] 12 feet.
EDCS-61600198.summary: This inscription likely records a land measurement or boundary related to Capidia Tertia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-61600204.translation: [3] Octavius, son of Caius; [3]avius; [3c] Flavius; [3] Octavius, son of Caius [---]
EDCS-61600204.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a family tomb or a record of multiple individuals.

EDCS-61600205.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Thesaeus, my incomparable husband, who lived about 14 years, 2 months, and 5 days. [His] wife [---] [---]
EDCS-61600205.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a wife to her husband, Thesaeus, detailing his lifespan.

EDCS-61600206.translation: To Tressia, freedwoman of Erotinus. Lucius Blaius Suavis [dedicated this].
EDCS-61600206.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Blaius Suavis to his freedwoman Tressia.

EDCS-61600214.translation: Vibius Ingenuus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-61600214.summary: This inscription records Vibius Ingenuus fulfilling a vow, likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-61700244.translation: For Marcus Curtio Felix, freedman of Marcus, and Curtia Cale, freedwoman of Marcus. In the front, 12 feet; in the field, 10 feet.
EDCS-61700244.summary: This is a funerary boundary inscription defining land for Marcus Curtio Felix and Curtia Cale.

EDCS-61700245.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Curtius Eucharistus made this for his fellow freedman Gaius Curtius Thalamus, who deserved well.
EDCS-61700245.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Curtius Eucharistus to his fellow freedman Gaius Curtius Thalamus.

EDCS-61700246.translation: To the Divine Shades. [For] Tullius Primigenius, who deserved well.
EDCS-61700246.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication to Tullius Primigenius.

EDCS-61700648.translation: To Marcus Cisvitius Menander, freedman of Marcus, [his] fellow freedman.
EDCS-61700648.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication, for Marcus Cisvitius Menander, a freedman and fellow freedman.

EDCS-61700649.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, mentioning Rufria and Aemilia.

EDCS-61700650.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Marcus Valerius Dionysius, aged 18 years.
EDCS-61700650.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Marcus Valerius Dionysius, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-61700651.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Licinius [---]. Gaius Licinius [---]us F() [son/heirs] made [this]. [---]us E[---]us his freedman, [his] heirs made [this].
EDCS-61700651.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication for Gaius Licinius, with a complex dedication by freedmen and heirs.

EDCS-61700700.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Fabia Victoria, who lived 14 years and 3 months. Flavia Severa, mother, made this.
EDCS-61700700.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Flavia Severa to her daughter Fabia Victoria, who died at age 14.

EDCS-61700701.translation: To Irenenus, Julia Celebrina, his mother.
EDCS-61700701.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a mother to her deceased son, likely an epitaph.

EDCS-61700702.translation: Petronia Longina made this for her son, Marcus Petronius Valentinus.
EDCS-61700702.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a mother to her deceased son, likely an epitaph.

EDCS-61700705.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Augurinus, who lived 21 years. Numisia Casuntia made this for her well-deserving husband. To the spirits of the underworld. For Claudius Successimus.
EDCS-61700705.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Augurinus, set up by his wife, and possibly also mentioning Claudius Successimus.

EDCS-61700724.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Arrenia Dynamis. Quintus Arrenius Victor, her freedman, made this for his well-deserving [former owner].
EDCS-61700724.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by a freedman for his former patroness.

EDCS-61700725.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Arrenia Mellita made this for her well-deserving husband, Quintus Arrenius Victor.
EDCS-61700725.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a wife to her husband.

EDCS-61800302.translation: Marcus Satrius, son of Marcus, [killed] enemy Marcus Luteinus Simedus, son of Marcus. [May there be] envy and ruin to you, so that there may not be unity to you in these bones, as sharp [things] in these bones. Satrius in his bones, so [---] from snarling dogs. [---] let it be to [---]. If you have led [---] all [---].
EDCS-61800302.summary: This is a highly unusual curse inscription, possibly a malediction intended to disrupt the burial of an enemy and protect the deceased's remains.

EDCS-61800444.translation: For Lucius Mursonius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Teretina tribe, [and] Mursonius, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, as IIviri (magistrates) exercising judicial power.
EDCS-61800444.summary: This inscription likely records the dedication of a public building or monument by two magistrates, possibly brothers, in a colony.

EDCS-61800450.translation: To [---]inius, consul, patron of the decurions, by decree of the town council, by public authority of the colonists.
EDCS-61800450.summary: This is a public dedication honoring a patron, likely a former magistrate, by the colonists of a town.

EDCS-61900023.translation: Derecia Methe, daughter of Spurius, lived 21 years.
EDCS-61900023.summary: This is a simple epitaph recording the name, parentage, and age at death of a woman.

EDCS-61900024.translation: Of Lucius Sepunius Sandilianus, 20 feet in front, 20 feet into the land, granted by public authority by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-61900024.summary: This inscription records the public allocation of land, specifying dimensions and authorizing body.

EDCS-61900025.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedwoman named Afreia Prima.

EDCS-61900026.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Numerius Alleius Auctus.

EDCS-61900027.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aninia Didime.

EDCS-61900031.translation: Aulus Campius Antiochus, freedman of Titus.
EDCS-61900031.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Campius Antiochus, a freedman.

EDCS-61900040.translation: Helle, a little girl, lived for 4 years.
EDCS-61900040.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the short life of a young girl named Helle.

EDCS-61900041.translation: Titus Muttius Proculus lived for 25 years.
EDCS-61900041.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life of Titus Muttius Proculus.

EDCS-61900042.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Muttius Martialis.

EDCS-61900043.translation: Nicostratus for himself and for Popidia Ecdoche, his concubine, and for his family.
EDCS-61900043.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Nicostratus for himself, his concubine Popidia Ecdoche, and their family.

EDCS-61900046.translation: Eumachia, daughter of Lucius, for herself and her family.
EDCS-61900046.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Eumachia, daughter of Lucius, for herself and her family.

EDCS-61900047.translation: Lucius Muttius Aprilis lived for 20 years.
EDCS-61900047.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the life of Lucius Muttius Aprilis.

EDCS-61900048.translation: To Cnaeus Alleius Eros, freedman of Cnaeus, who was created Augustalis by grace, for whom the Augustales and villagers decreed honors of 2000 sesterces at his funeral; he lived 22 years.
