EDCS-61900048.summary: This is a funerary honorific inscription for Cnaeus Alleius Eros, a freedman and Augustalis, detailing the considerable sum decreed for his funeral by local officials and citizens.

EDCS-61900049.translation: Cnaeus Alleius Logus, who deserved well of all the collegia.
EDCS-61900049.summary: This is a dedication or epitaph honoring Cnaeus Alleius Logus for his meritorious service to various collegia (colleges or associations).

EDCS-61900050.translation: Pomponia Decharcis, mother of Allei Mai and Allei Nobilis.
EDCS-61900050.summary: This is an epitaph identifying Pomponia Decharcis as the mother of two individuals named Allei, indicating family relationships within a local context.

EDCS-61900051.summary: This is a simple epitaph recording the lifespan of a child named Spiron.

EDCS-61900052.summary: This is a very brief inscription likely from a tombstone, identifying the deceased as a father and indicating the relationship to a son.

EDCS-61900054.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Gaius Munatius Faustus.

EDCS-61900055.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Lucius Naevoleius Eutrapelus.

EDCS-61900056.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely an epitaph or a dedicatory label, for a person named Munatia Euche.

EDCS-61900057.summary: This is a simple epitaph recording the lifespan of a child named Helpis.

EDCS-61900058.summary: This is a simple epitaph recording the very short lifespan of an infant named Primigenia.

EDCS-61900059.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Arsinoe, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-61900060.translation: Psyche lived for 3 years and 6 months.
EDCS-61900060.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Psyche, who died at the age of three years and six months.

EDCS-61900061.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Atimetus, who died at the age of twenty-six.

EDCS-61900063.summary: This is a dedication or honorific inscription for Aulus Veius Atticus, who held the position of Augustalis.

EDCS-61900064.summary: This is a dedication or honorific inscription for Aulus Veius Atticus, who held the position of Augustalis.

EDCS-61900065.translation: Aulus Veius Nymph(i)us lived for 28 years.
EDCS-61900065.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Aulus Veius Nymph(i)us, who died at the age of twenty-eight.

EDCS-61900066.translation: To Marcus Herennius Epidianus, son of Aulus, twice a magistrate with judicial power, the council granted the place of a monument out of honor by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-61900066.summary: This inscription records a decree by the town council to grant a plot for a monument to Marcus Herennius Epidianus, a local magistrate, out of respect.

EDCS-61900110.summary: This is a funerary epitaph simply naming Egnatuleia Felicitas.

EDCS-61900112.translation: To Arellia Tertulla, daughter of Numerius, the decurions granted her a burial place after death and decreed funeral rites at public expense.
EDCS-61900112.summary: This inscription records the town council's decision to grant a burial plot and public funeral rites to Arellia Tertulla, daughter of Numerius.

EDCS-61900114.translation: Lucius Betutius Niger, son of Quintus, of the Oufentina tribe, soldier of the 2nd Praetorian Cohort, lived for 20 years, served for 2 years.
EDCS-61900114.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Betutius Niger, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, recording his age at death and length of service.

EDCS-61900117.summary: This is a funerary inscription of Vibia Sabina, likely for her own tomb.

EDCS-61900118.translation: Lucius Manilius Saturninus, son of Quartus, of the Romilia tribe, from Ateste, served as an *speculator* for 5 years, lived for 24 years. His brother erected this.
EDCS-61900118.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Manilius Saturninus, a soldier from Ateste, who served as a *speculator* and died at 24; his brother erected it.

EDCS-61900119.translation: Sextus Caesernius Montanus, son of Spurius, of the Velina tribe, a *speculator* from Aquileia, served for 11 years, lies here.
EDCS-61900119.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Caesernius Montanus, a soldier (*speculator*) from Aquileia, who served for 11 years.

EDCS-61900121.translation: Lucius Ceius Serapio, freedman of Lucius, an *argentarius* (money changer/banker), and Helvia, daughter of Marcus, his wife, dedicated this.
EDCS-61900121.summary: This is a dedication by Lucius Ceius Serapio, a banker, and his wife Helvia.

EDCS-61900125.summary: This is a dedication to Flavius Philoxenus.

EDCS-61900126.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Agathea Flavia.

EDCS-61900128.translation: Caius Minatius Iucundus, *magister pagani* (local official) of the *pagus* (district) Augusti Felicis Suburbani. In width 10 feet, in depth 16 feet.
EDCS-61900128.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land allocated to Caius Minatius Iucundus, a local official, for a public purpose.

EDCS-61900129.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antistia Auxesis, a freedwoman.

EDCS-61900132.translation: Aulus Clodius Iustus, son of Aulus, for himself, and for Aulus Aegialus, freedman of a woman, his father, and for Tironia Repentina, freedwoman of a woman, his mother.
EDCS-61900132.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aulus Clodius Iustus for himself, his freedman father Aegialus, and his freedwoman mother Repentina.

EDCS-61900134.translation: Clodia Nigella, freedwoman of a woman, a public pig-keeper, [allocated] from a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-61900134.summary: This inscription records the allocation of a public plot to Clodia Nigella, a pig-keeper, as determined by a decree of the town council.

EDCS-61900136.translation: Aulus Clodius Pompeianus, son of Aulus.
EDCS-61900136.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Clodius Pompeianus, son of Aulus.

EDCS-61900137.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucidus, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-62000176.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Acris, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-62000177.summary: This is likely a funerary epitaph or a name inscription for Aulus Dentatius Fortunatus.

EDCS-62000178.summary: This is likely a funerary epitaph or a name inscription for Aulus Dentatius Felix.

EDCS-62000179.translation: To Aulus Dentatius Celsus, freedman of Aulus.
EDCS-62000179.summary: This is a dedication or funerary epitaph for Aulus Dentatius Celsus, a freedman.

EDCS-62000180.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompeia Aucta, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-62000181.summary: This is a dedication or funerary epitaph for Lucius Barbidius Vitalis.

EDCS-62000182.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a boy named Vitalis, who died at the age of 3.

EDCS-62101249.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Quintus Primulus Nabalius, who lived 6 years, 4 months, and 28 days.
EDCS-62101249.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits for Quintus Primulus Nabalius, recording his age at death.

EDCS-62101295.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Cartilius Lucrio, by this deed of monument, concerning land that belongs to him which he inserted into this title by permission of Publius Nonius Valerianus; also to his well-deserving son Gaius Cartilius Heraclianus, son of Gaius; and to his wife Agria Semne; and to their son Gaius Plarius Ianuarius in the front part [of the plot], a quarter share; and to freedmen and freedwomen, and to Gaius Cartilius Calliae and Marcus Marcius Euangelus in the left part, along with their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-62101295.summary: This funerary inscription records the allocation of burial plots within a family monument for Gaius Cartilius Lucrio, his son, wife, and extended family, with specific divisions of space and bequests.

EDCS-62101300.translation: Gaius Antistius Vetus, son of Gaius, grandson of Gaius; Gaius Valerius Flaccus Tanurianus, son of Lucius; Publius Vergilius Pontianus, son of Marcus; Publius Catienus Sabinus, son of Publius; Tiberius Vergilius Rufus, son of Tiberius; as curators of the riverbanks and channel of the Tiber, by decree of the Senate, have established the boundaries [for a length of] 3 feet, without prejudice to public or private rights.
EDCS-62101300.summary: This inscription records the work of six officials who, acting as curators of the Tiber under senatorial decree, established boundary markers for the river channel, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-62500005.translation: To Numerius Trebonius, son of Numerius, of the Voturia tribe, a citizen, elected aedile by decree of the decurions to perform the sacred rites of Vulcan; he lived 13 years and 6 months. Numerius Trebonius Glenus, his freedman, his father, made [this]; and for Annia Veneria, freedwoman of Lucius, his holy wife; and for Lucius Annio Hilarus, freedman of Lucius, [their] patron; and for Annia Veneria; and for Falerius Optatus.
EDCS-62500005.summary: This tombstone commemorates Numerius Trebonius, a young boy who served as aedile for sacred rites, and was erected by his freedman father and also honors his wife and others.

EDCS-62501341.translation: Pottery (made) by Doryphorus, from the estate of Domitia Lucilla, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-62501341.summary: This is a potter's stamp indicating the origin of the pottery from the estate of Domitia Lucilla, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus (likely 167 CE).

EDCS-62600957.translation: Pottery (made) from the estates of Flavius Aper, from the workshop of Calpetanus Crescens, during the consulship of Condianus and Maximus.
EDCS-62600957.summary: This is a potter's stamp indicating the origin of the pottery from the estates of Flavius Aper and the workshop of Calpetanus Crescens, dated to the consulship of Condianus and Maximus (likely 209 CE).

EDCS-62600958.translation: Pottery (made) from the estates of Flavius Aper, from the workshop of Calpetanus Crescens, during the consulship of Condianus and Maximus.
EDCS-62600958.summary: This is a potter's stamp indicating the origin of the pottery from the estates of Flavius Aper and the workshop of Calpetanus Crescens, dated to the consulship of Condianus and Maximus (likely 209 CE).

EDCS-62601076.translation: Pottery (made) from the estates of Domitia Lucilla Aprilis, during the consulship of Pontianus and Attilanus.
EDCS-62601076.summary: This is a potter's stamp indicating the origin of the pottery from the estates of Domitia Lucilla Aprilis, dated to the consulship of Pontianus and Attilanus (likely 189 CE).

EDCS-62601084.translation: From the potteries of Pullaenus Pollio, during the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-62601084.summary: This is a potter's stamp indicating the origin of the pottery from the potteries of Pullaenus Pollio, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus (likely 167 CE).

EDCS-62601100.translation: During the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the estates of Claudia Marcellina.
EDCS-62601100.summary: This is a stamp indicating the origin of goods from the estates of Claudia Marcellina, dated to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus (likely 167 CE).

EDCS-62601136.translation: During the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus, from the estates of Domitia Domitiani.
EDCS-62601136.summary: This is a stamp indicating the origin of goods from the estates of Domitia Domitiani, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus (likely 167 CE).

EDCS-62601148.translation: Under the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus, from the estates of Claudia Marcellina.
EDCS-62601148.summary: This inscription records a supply originating from the estates of Claudia Marcellina, dated to the consulship of Apronianus and Paetinus.

EDCS-62601152.translation: Doliare work from the estates of Domitia Lucilla and Dionysius Lucilla, under the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.
EDCS-62601152.summary: This inscription documents doliare work from the estates of Domitia Lucilla and Dionysius Lucilla, dated to the consulship of Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-62601169.translation: To the divine shades. Secunda lived 7 years and 9 days.
EDCS-62601169.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Secunda, who died at the age of 7 years and 9 days.

EDCS-62900220.translation: For Quintus Veleius, son of Quintus, of the Oufentina tribe, and his heirs, 10 feet in front and 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-62900220.summary: This inscription likely records a land grant or boundary marker for Quintus Veleius and his heirs, specifying dimensions for a plot of land.

EDCS-62900280.translation: Lucius Haedinius Charidemus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-62900280.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Lucius Haedinius Charidemus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-62900284.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or ownership marker for Lucius Valerius Marcianus.

EDCS-62900285.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or ownership marker for Caius Bettius Amandus.

EDCS-62900287.translation: To the divine shades. Claudia [name] lived [age] and Aulus Sev[erus] lived [age].
EDCS-62900287.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Claudia and Aulus Severus.

EDCS-63500019.translation: To the divine shades. The freedwoman Metia Accentia made this for her deserving patroness.
EDCS-63500019.summary: This funerary inscription records that Metia Accentia, a freedwoman, erected a monument for her deserving patroness.

EDCS-63500020.translation: To the divine shades. Alexandria Scantiae lived 19 years.
EDCS-63500020.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Alexandria Scantiae, who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-63500021.translation: Agatho the charioteer lived 30 years. Marculla (did this) while alive.
EDCS-63500021.summary: This is a tombstone for a charioteer named Agatho, erected by Marculla.

EDCS-63500022.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Cornelius Cornelianus, son of Marcus, of the Aniensis tribe, from Cremona, a soldier of the 3rd Praetorian Cohort, century of Priscus. He served for 11 years and lived 30 years. Gavia Ingenua made this for her well-deserving husband, under the care of Orvius Rufinus, his fellow soldier.
EDCS-63500022.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Cornelius Cornelianus, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, erected by his wife Gavia Ingenua.

EDCS-63500051.translation: To the departed spirits. (His) son erected this for Calpurnius Eufratas, well-deserving, who lived 13 years, 9 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-63500051.summary: This is a tombstone for a boy named Calpurnius Eufratas, erected by his son, who died at age 13.

EDCS-63500072.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Maria Dicnilla made this for her father Aurelius Nicator, because of his dutifulness.
EDCS-63500072.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Maria Dicnilla for her father Aurelius Nicator.

EDCS-63600391.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Lucius Vibius Phainus for his well-deserving wife Cornelia Clitia Helices.
EDCS-63600391.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Lucius Vibius Phainus for his wife Cornelia Clitia Helices.

EDCS-63600393.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. Marcus Ulpius Felix made this for his most sweet wife Numia Thaida, with whom he lived 20 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-63600393.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Marcus Ulpius Felix for his wife Numia Thaida, whom he lived with for 20 years.

EDCS-63600413.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Clodius Cerialis.

EDCS-63600425.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Epagathus (made this) for Crescentilla, his native girl, who lived 2 years and 11 months.
EDCS-63600425.summary: This is a tombstone for Crescentilla, erected by Titus Flavius Epagathus.

EDCS-63600426.translation: Of Publius Licinius, freedman of Publius. Of Publius Rabirius. Of Publius Aenarius. The bones lie here.
EDCS-63600426.summary: This inscription lists the owners of a burial plot, likely indicating the disposition of remains.

EDCS-63600427.translation: Lucius Amatius (made this) for his [---] Augena (mother? wife?). In the front 4 feet, in the field 3 and a half feet.
EDCS-63600427.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a burial plot and seems to be related to an Amatius family, possibly dedicating the plot.

EDCS-63600428.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Antonio Felice, his incomparable wife, who lived with me for 4 years and 25 days, who lived 25 years and 20 days.
EDCS-63600428.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a wife named Antonio Felice, set up by her husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63600431.translation: The bones of Pactumeia Eleuternis, freedwoman of Quintus and Caius, and of Philadespotus, freedman of Lucius Avi, are buried here. Quintus Vehillius Tertius for his sister and Caius Hordionius Dasius for himself and his family.
EDCS-63600431.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial location of Pactumeia Eleuternis and establishes a family burial plot for Quintus Vehillius Tertius and Caius Hordionius Dasius and their relatives, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63600432.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aurelius lived [---]. For [my] sweetest infant.
EDCS-63600432.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for a child named Marcus Aurelius, set up by an unknown person, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63600439.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For [---]enia, his most exceptional wife, Domitius Octavianus with his sons Domitius Octavianus Mamidius and Maxsumilla.
EDCS-63600439.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maxsimilla, wife of Domitius Octavianus, and their sons, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63600440.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Numerius Enateius Martius, his wife Catatvitas.
EDCS-63600440.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Catatvitas, wife of Numerius Enateius Martius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63600441.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Flavius Chrys[---]. Flaveia Protogenia [---].
EDCS-63600441.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph, likely for Gaius Flavius Chrys[---] and Flaveia Protogenia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63600447.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Iunia Cherusia, set up by her own expense.
EDCS-63600447.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iunia Cherusia, set up by herself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63600450.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. To Publius Murarius Taurus, his deserving wife Claudia Felicitas.
EDCS-63600450.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Murarius Taurus, set up by his wife Claudia Felicitas, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63600458.translation: The bones of Pescennia Prima, freedwoman of Caius, are buried here, according to her will.
EDCS-63600458.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Pescennia Prima, a freedwoman, according to her will, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63600462.translation: The bones of Staia Philemationis, freedwoman of Marcus, are buried here.
EDCS-63600462.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Staia Philemationis, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63900019.translation: To the departed spirits. For Decidia Chaptusa.
EDCS-63900019.summary: A funerary dedication to Decidia Chaptusa.

EDCS-63900065.translation: The remains of Annia, daughter of Quintus, are buried here in a plot of five feet.
EDCS-63900065.summary: A funerary inscription indicating the burial location and size of the plot for Annia.

EDCS-63900066.translation: Chrysippus, freedman of Lucius Fusius, for himself and his people, lives. ... Rufi, freedman of Publius.
EDCS-63900066.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription mentioning Chrysippus and Rufi.

EDCS-63900067.translation: To the departed spirits. For Albia, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-64000005.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Cornelius Euaristus, freedman of a woman, an Augustalis. Lucius Cornelius Euaristus, Lucius Cornelius Varus, and Cornelia Margaris, his children, and Julia Laeta, his wife, had this made according to his will. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-64000005.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Cornelius Euaristus, commissioned by his family and granted land by the town council.

EDCS-64000007.translation: Acilius Glabrio, very distinguished man. Maecia Praetextata, very distinguished woman.
EDCS-64000007.summary: An inscription honoring two distinguished individuals, Acilius Glabrio and Maecia Praetextata.

EDCS-64000008.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly related to ownership or dedication by Quintus Egnatius Taurus.

EDCS-64000012.translation: Caius Gavius Garra, son of Caius, held the praetorship. He was taken away at the age of 21. His funeral and burial place were provided publicly by a decree of the senate and an order of the municipality.
EDCS-64000012.summary: A public funerary inscription for Caius Gavius Garra, who died young after holding the praetorship, with public honors granted for his funeral and burial.

EDCS-64000042.translation: To Herennia Afrodisia, his incomparable wife, who lived 27 years, 5 months, and 25 days. Marcus Tillius Ianuarius made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-64000042.summary: A funerary epitaph for Herennia Afrodisia, erected by her husband Marcus Tillius Ianuarius.

EDCS-64200006.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Cantinius Maximus, son of Lucius, of the Menenia tribe. From Nuceria Constantia. Tribune of the 9th praetorian cohort and the 14th urban cohort and the 4th vigiles, first centurion twice, procurator of the imperial estates. His heir had this made.
EDCS-64200006.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Cantinius Maximus, detailing his military and administrative career, commissioned by his heir.

EDCS-64400024.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Livia Veneria, by Livia Aphrodite, her most devoted patroness.
EDCS-64400024.summary: This funerary dedication is to Livia Veneria, set up by her patroness Livia Aphrodite.

EDCS-64400025.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Calvius Alexander, by Marcus [---].
EDCS-64400025.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication to Gaius Calvius Alexander, with the dedicant being Marcus.

EDCS-64400026.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Antistius Domitus, who lived 13 years and 11 months. Quintus Antistius Domitus and Lutatia Eudia, his parents, made this for their dearest son.
EDCS-64400026.summary: A funerary dedication by parents Quintus Antistius Domitus and Lutatia Eudia for their son Quintus Antistius Domitus, who died at the age of 13 years and 11 months.

EDCS-64400045.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Aurelia Faustina, by Pontianus, servant of our Emperor, for his most holy and well-deserving wife, he made this.
EDCS-64400045.summary: A funerary dedication by Pontianus, a servant of the emperor, for his wife Aurelia Faustina.

EDCS-64400047.translation: [3] Peduceus, pious, who lived 21 years. Peducaea [3] Musa(?), who lived [3] years. Lucius Pe[duceus ---].
EDCS-64400047.summary: A fragmented epitaph recording the lifespans of at least two individuals, possibly family members named Peduceus and Musa.

EDCS-64400057.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Statilia Victrix, most innocent soul, in her 26th year [---]. Titus Statilius Achil[leus](?), for his most dear [wife](?), with whom he lived 20 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-64400057.summary: A funerary dedication by Titus Statilius Achil[leus](?) to his wife Statilia Victrix, mentioning her age and their years of marriage.

EDCS-64400106.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Veneria, who lived approximately 45 years. Victor, for his incomparable wife, with whom he lived without quarrel 13 years, 1 month, and 15 days, well-deserving, he made this.
EDCS-64400106.summary: A funerary dedication by Victor for his wife Veneria, noting her approximate age at death and their 13 years of marriage.

EDCS-64400288.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Flavius Zoticus, Augustalis, who lived 67 years, 5 months, and 1 day. Iunia Ianuaria, for her husband, and Flavia Eutyce and Flavia Vitalis, for their father, well-deserving, they made this.
EDCS-64400288.summary: A funerary dedication by Iunia Ianuaria and her children Flavia Eutyce and Flavia Vitalis for their husband and father, Flavius Zoticus, an Augustalis.

EDCS-64400290.summary: A very fragmented inscription that likely records the name of Lucius Vettius.

EDCS-64400374.translation: To Paula, dearest daughter, who lived 25 years and [3] months. To Florentina, sweetest daughter, who lived [3] years. To Valentin, most beloved son, who lived [---].
EDCS-64400374.summary: A fragmented funerary dedication to deceased children Paula, Florentina, and Valentin.

EDCS-64400402.translation: The bones of Fiducia, freedwoman of Rusticus, are placed here.
EDCS-64400402.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedwoman, indicating her burial place.

EDCS-64400431.summary: This inscription records the maker of a piece of work, likely a building or art object.

EDCS-64400433.translation: To the departed spirits. The parents made this for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-64400433.summary: This is a funerary dedication from parents to their deceased daughter.

EDCS-64400434.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. The sons [made this] for Aulus Vesonius Felix, who lived 42 years, 11 months.
EDCS-64400434.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Aulus Vesonius Felix and was set up by his sons.

EDCS-64400461.translation: To [3]ius, son of Gaius, of the Voting Tribe Elia, who lived 18 years, 8 months, 15 days. His parents, Secundus [son of 3]ius and Heipis, [made this] for their most dutiful son, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and all their descendants.
EDCS-64400461.summary: This funerary epitaph is a dedication by parents for their deceased son, extending provisions for their freedmen and descendants.

EDCS-64500181.translation: To Jupiter Flazus. Publius Avius Pier fulfilled his vow for his grandmother, Secundilla, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-64500181.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Flazus by Publius Avius Pier on behalf of his grandmother.

EDCS-64500182.translation: To Nero Caesar, son of Germanicus Caesar, grandson of Emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Augustus, brother of Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, to the Augustan flamen, comrade of the Titian Arval brotherhood, quaestor, publicly [honored].
EDCS-64500182.summary: This inscription honors Nero Caesar, detailing his imperial lineage and priestly/official roles, likely from the Julio-Claudian period.

EDCS-64500183.translation: For Honoratus, son of Lucius Aurelius Flaccus, of the voting tribe Galeria, son of Sempronius Hispanus, a primus pilus of the Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, tribune of the 1st cohort of vigiles, tribune of the 14th urban cohort, tribune of the 8th praetorian cohort, primus pilus of the Legio XIII Gemina, honored with military gifts by the Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus Germanicus. Xanthus and Sympheron, freedmen of the procurator of the province of Narbonensis, and procurator of the province of Belgica, made this with their own money. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-64500183.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the freedmen of a procurator for Honoratus, a distinguished military officer and official, likely in the late 1st century AD.

EDCS-64500222.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for a child named Sura, set up by her parents.

EDCS-64500223.translation: Canoniatus [---] from the district of C(---) XX [---] father
EDCS-64500223.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a funerary dedication, mentioning a person named Canoniatus and their father.

EDCS-64500234.translation: To the spirits of the departed. ... Scis... aged 10 years. Sever... mother?
EDCS-64500234.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from a tombstone, records the death of a young individual named Sever, possibly identified by a fragmented name and indicated to be 10 years old, with mention of a mother.

EDCS-64500235.translation: To Aurelia Sura, who lived 20 years, 5 months, and 10 days. Aelius Vitalis, soldier of the II Parthica Pia legion, made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-64500235.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Aurelia Sura, who lived to be 20 years old, and was memorialized by her husband, Aelius Vitalis, a soldier of the II Parthica Pia legion.

EDCS-64500236.translation: To Aurelia Sura, who lived 35 years. Aurelius Apolodorus, a veteran, made this for his dearest, well-deserving wife.
EDCS-64500236.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aurelia Sura, who lived to be 35 years old, and was memorialized by her husband, the veteran Aurelius Apolodorus.

EDCS-64500238.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for the eternal memory of Domitia Valentina, who lived 31 years and 2 months. Valerius Valentinus, soldier of the II Parthica Severiana legion, for his most beloved, well-deserving wife. [Erected by] Aurelius Mucapor, her optio and procurator.
EDCS-64500238.summary: This funerary inscription honors Domitia Valentina, who lived 31 years and 2 months, and was erected by her husband, Valerius Valentinus, a soldier of the II Parthica Severiana legion, and possibly also by Aurelius Mucapor, who held the roles of optio and procurator.

EDCS-64500239.translation: ...orio. ...lived... years. Licinius, father, placed this memorial. Aelia Marc... mother, father and mother made [it] for the well-deserving.
EDCS-64500239.summary: This funerary inscription records the placement of a memorial by Licinius for an unnamed individual, with his wife Aelia Marc... also contributing, for their well-deserving child.

EDCS-64500241.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Dissa(?), soldier of the II Parthica(?) legion, lived 34 years, served 13 years. Aurelius Aulussanus, his heir, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-64500241.summary: This funerary inscription honors Aurelius Dissa(?), a soldier of the II Parthica(?) legion, who lived 34 years and served for 13 years, with the monument erected by his heir, Aurelius Aulussanus.

EDCS-64500242.translation: ...mo. Aurelius Muscianus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-64500242.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Muscianus fulfilling a vow, likely a votive offering or dedication.

EDCS-64500243.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For ...vio Herculiano, soldier of the II Parthica Severiana legion, who lived 50 years and 5 days, served 26 years in the VIIIth century, of the first pilus. His heir, Aelius Marcellus(?), made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-64500243.summary: This funerary inscription honors ...vio Herculiano, a soldier of the II Parthica Severiana legion who lived 50 years and served 26 years, with the monument erected by his heir, Aelius Marcellus(?).

EDCS-64500247.translation: Marcus Aurelius Ma[--]us, soldier of the II Parthica Severiana legion, Xth century, of the posterior pilus, who lived 31 years, 3 months, and 3 days, served 10 years. Aurelia Dioclia, his wife and heir, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-64500247.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Marcus Aurelius Ma[--]us, a soldier of the II Parthica Severiana legion, who lived 31 years and served 10 years, and was memorialized by his wife and heir, Aurelia Dioclia.

EDCS-64500248.translation: ... of the II Parthica legion... IIIrd century, of the posterior hastati, who served 18 years, 4 months, and 20 days, who lived 35 years and 10 days. Aurelia Marcell[a], his wife and [son? A]ur[elius?]
EDCS-64500248.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription records the life and service of an unnamed soldier of the II Parthica legion, who lived 35 years and served 18 years, and was memorialized by his wife, Aurelia Marcell[a], and possibly a son.

EDCS-64500251.translation: To Sergius Heracleon, who lived for 1 year, 11 months, and 20 days. Sergius Honoratus(?) and his relatives made [this] for [him] as one deserving well.
EDCS-64500251.summary: A funerary dedication for a child named Sergius Heracleon, set up by his father and relatives.

EDCS-64500253.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Hesper, who lived for 1 year and 5 days.
EDCS-64500253.summary: A funerary dedication for a child named Hesper, specifying her short lifespan.

EDCS-64500259.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Aurelius Longinianus, son of Lucius, of the voting tribe Quirina.
EDCS-64500259.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Aurelius Longinianus, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-64500429.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, supreme pontiff, holder of tribunician power for the 13th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of his country, the most excellent and greatest prince. The people of Teanum [by decree] of the decurions [erected this].
EDCS-64500429.summary: A dedication by the city of Teanum to Emperor Hadrian, dating to his 13th year of tribunician power (around 129 AD).

EDCS-64500452.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Saenia Valentia made [this] for her well-deserving patron, Senius Vitalio.
EDCS-64500452.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedwoman, Saenia Valentia, for her patron Senius Vitalio.

EDCS-64500550.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavia Fortunata, who lived for 30 years and 6 months.
EDCS-64500550.summary: A funerary dedication for Flavia Fortunata, stating her age at death.

EDCS-64500551.translation: To Felicitas, who lived for 5 years, 7 months, 21 days, and 3 hours. Valeria Spes made [this] for her well-deserving foster-child.
EDCS-64500551.summary: A funerary dedication by Valeria Spes for her foster-child Felicitas, detailing a very precise lifespan.

EDCS-64500552.translation: To Marcus Julius Apuleius, also known as Euporius, who lived for 26 years and 5 months. His mother, Aelia Augurina, made [this] for her most beloved, well-deserving son.
EDCS-64500552.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother for her son, Marcus Julius Apuleius Euporius.

EDCS-64500553.translation: To Felicitas, her most beloved foster-child, who lived for 3 years, 7 months, 22 days, and 9 hours. Processus and Daphne made [this] for [her] as one deserving well.
EDCS-64500553.summary: A funerary dedication by Processus and Daphne for their foster-child Felicitas.

EDCS-64500554.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Petronianus, who lived for 1 year and 3 months. Petronianus, [his] father, made [this] for his most beloved son in honor.
EDCS-64500554.summary: A funerary dedication by a father for his infant son, Gaius Julius Petronianus.

EDCS-64500555.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavia Secundilla, who lived 48 years, 2 months, and 5 days. Bruttius Felicio made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-64500555.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Secundilla, set up by her husband Bruttius Felicio, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64500571.translation: To the holy Restuta Victoria, wife. Taurus, freedman of Augustus, made this tomb for his deserving, rare, and chaste wife, who lived 30 years; and for her mother Fulvia Felicula, and for her stepfather Sextus Poppaeus Thalamus, for their deservingness.
EDCS-64500571.summary: A funerary epitaph for Restuta Victoria, her mother Fulvia Felicula, and her stepfather Sextus Poppaeus Thalamus, set up by Taurus, a freedman of Augustus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64500574.text_edition: \n <M=LT>(arco) Aurelio Valerio Maximiano Inbicto sic Augusto ordo Labicanorum QQuintanensium devotus numini maiestatique eius ST SCP cos statixerunt ex potestat procuratoris proba DIOLVABAO impensa locus datus decreto decurionum Kalendis Iunio(is) Dextro II consulibus Thrasea Prisco conssulibus NESTHIRA ATNCHAER IIIIviri iure dicundo \n
EDCS-64500574.translation: Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus, Invictus, thus Augustus. The council of the Quintanensian Labicani, devoted to his divine majesty, erected this monument. The site was granted by decree of the decurions in the potestas of the procurator, at the expense of Diolvabao. On the Kalends of June, with Dextrus and Thrasea Priscus as consuls. Nesthira and Atnchaer, the fourth-rank magistrates with judicial power.
EDCS-64500574.summary: A dedication of a public monument or building by the local council of Labicani to the emperor Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus, dating to his reign (236-238 AD).

EDCS-64600121.summary: A brief epitaph recording the lifespan of Titus Flavius Apollinaris, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600122.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Mettia Tertia, may you fare well. Marcus Mettius Sabinus made this for his most rare freedwoman, his dearest wife, deserving of honor.
EDCS-64600122.summary: A funerary epitaph for Mettia Tertia, set up by her husband and former master Marcus Mettius Sabinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600144.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Nepos, who lived 3 years, 7 months, and 23 days. Varzo made this for his deserving son.
EDCS-64600144.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young child named Nepos, set up by his father Varzo, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600145.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Varzo, soldier of the II Parthica legion, made this for Irenis, his sweetest daughter, who lived 3 years and 11 months, deserving of honor. [---]
EDCS-64600145.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Irenis, set up by her father Aurelius Varzo, a soldier, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600146.translation: ] who lived 2 years, 1 month, and 3 days. Aurelius Carus for his deserving son.
EDCS-64600146.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young child, set up by his father Aurelius Carus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600147.translation: Aurelius Xeno and Aemilia Secundilla, father and mother, made this for their sweetest son Aurelius Marcellinus, who lived 1 year, 8 months, and 22 days, deserving of honor.
EDCS-64600147.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young child named Aurelius Marcellinus, set up by his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600148.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Marcellina, who lived 6 years, 2 months, and 15 days. Aurelius Estula and Aurelia [---] parents for their sweetest daughter, deserving of honor.
EDCS-64600148.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Aurelia Marcellina, set up by her parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64600149.translation: To the shades of the underworld. To the legion (II Parthica?) Pia Felix, Faithful, Eternal? in the 4th century, the later standard-bearer. He lived 30 years, 7 months, 11 days. He served 5 years. Acilius Verinus, his kinsman and first heir, made this for his deserving relative.
EDCS-64600149.summary: This funerary inscription records the life span and service of a soldier in the Legio II Parthica, erected by his heir.

EDCS-64600156.translation: To Aurelius Eutus, a soldier-born slave. He lived 1 year, 11 months, 11 days. Aurelius Valens, a soldier, made this for his deserving soldier-born.
EDCS-64600156.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, likely the son of a soldier, erected by a soldier.

EDCS-64600161.translation: To [ Aurelia ? ]stes who lived [3] years. [3] and his mother? Aelia Apicula [---].
EDCS-64600161.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription names Aelia Apicula and possibly her child, with an incomplete lifespan.

EDCS-64600200.translation: To the shades of the underworld. To Claudia Ampliata, who deserved well. Musicus, the steward, and Favor, slaves of Caesar from Corinth, made this.
EDCS-64600200.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument for Claudia Ampliata by her steward and household slaves.

EDCS-64600278.translation: To the shades of the underworld. To Marcus Amullius Postumus, son of Marcus, of the Palatine tribe. Amullia Quieta, daughter of Marcus, for her most dutiful brother.
EDCS-64600278.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Amullius Postumus and was erected by his sister.

EDCS-64600279.translation: To the shades of the underworld. To Tiberius Claudius Menodotus, dearest son. He lived 7 years, 5 months, 3 hours. Tiberius Claudius Eutychianus, his father.
EDCS-64600279.summary: This funerary inscription records the early death of Tiberius Claudius Menodotus, erected by his father.

EDCS-64600281.translation: To the shades of the underworld. To Lucius Annius Artemon. Caecilia Onesime, his wife, made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-64600281.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Annius Artemon, erected by his wife.

EDCS-64600282.translation: To the shades of the underworld. To Quintus Aemilius Hilarus. He lived 50 years. Aemilia Chrysis, his daughter, made this for her most dutiful father.
EDCS-64600282.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Quintus Aemilius Hilarus, erected by his daughter.

EDCS-64600283.translation: To the shades of the underworld. To Gavinius Felix. Flavia Afe made this.
EDCS-64600283.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gavinius Felix, erected by Flavia Afe, likely his wife.

EDCS-64600284.translation: To the shades of the underworld. To Marcus Minucius Synecdemus, father. Marcus Minucius Valerianus and Pactumeia Victoria for their deserving father.
EDCS-64600284.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Minucius Synecdemus, erected by his son and daughter-in-law.

EDCS-64600285.translation: To the shades of the departed. Messia Ampelis lived 58 years and 2 months.
EDCS-64600285.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Messia Ampelis.

EDCS-64800405.translation: Marcus Ennius Euaristus made (this) for Matidia, daughter of Augustus.
EDCS-64800405.summary: A dedication by Marcus Ennius Euaristus to Matidia, daughter of Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-64800407.summary: An ownership or dedication inscription belonging to Marcus Vipsanius Primigenus.

EDCS-64800414.translation: To the shades of the departed. For Veronia Amanda, follower of Isis, who lived 38 years, 4 months, and 21 days. Quintus Vibius Herma (made this) for his most excellent wife, with whom he lived 7 years and 3 months.
EDCS-64800414.summary: A tombstone for Veronia Amanda, a follower of Isis, set up by her husband Quintus Vibius Herma.

EDCS-64800416.translation: Of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, (for the town of) Albanum, under the supervision of Daphnis, freedman of Augustus, the procurator.
EDCS-64800416.summary: An inscription related to the construction or administration of Albanum under the procuratorship of Daphnis during the reign of Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-64800480.translation: To the shades of the departed. Of Caius Volussius Isochrysius.
EDCS-64800480.summary: A tombstone for Caius Volussius Isochrysius.

EDCS-64800486.translation: Sextus Numisius Urbanus, freedman of Sextus, lives (here/on this land). In the front, 16 feet; in the field, 30 feet.
EDCS-64800486.summary: A funerary inscription marking land ownership boundaries for Sextus Numisius Urbanus.

EDCS-64800501.summary: An ownership or dedication inscription belonging to Marcus Claudius Thettalis.

EDCS-64800553.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Caesonia Tertulla.

EDCS-64800556.translation: To the shades of the departed. For Pullaenia Secunda, daughter of C[---]
EDCS-64800556.summary: A partial tombstone inscription for Pullaenia Secunda.

EDCS-64800557.translation: Vibia, freedwoman of Vibia, [an inscription for] herself and [her family].
EDCS-64800557.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Vibia, a freedwoman, for herself and her relatives.

EDCS-64800558.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-64800558.translation: To Titus Flavius, freedman of the emperor, [---]roti, of the grain fisc, [---]ri from Africa, a great baker in the city, [---] to the nummularius in the temple of Hercules Olivarius of captured goods, [---] to the father of Titus Flavius Irenaeus, twice-duovir and patron of the colony, grandfather of the Flavii [---]ani and Severiani, Roman knights and patrons of the colony, to whom the most holy order gave the honor of a seat and the ornaments of the decurionate, to the patron of the regal individuals for his merits. A place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-64800558.summary: This is a dedication to a prominent figure, Titus Flavius Irenaeus, likely a magistrate and patron of the colony, likely dating to the Flavian period, honoring his father and grandfather.

EDCS-64900248.translation: Gaius Volumnius, son of Gaius, of the Collina tribe; Volumnia Lais, freedwoman of Gaius, wife; Gaius Volumnius, son of Gaius, of the Collina tribe, Paetus; Sextus, son of Gaius, of the Collina tribe, Veiento; Gaius Volumnius Salvius, freedman of Gaius; Occia Acte, wife of Salvius.
EDCS-64900248.summary: This inscription lists members of the Volumnius family and their freedmen, likely for funerary purposes, and includes their tribal affiliations.

EDCS-64900351.translation: To the divine spirits. To Grania Vitalina, who lived 47 years, an incomparable wife.
EDCS-64900351.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Grania Vitalina, who is described as an incomparable wife.

EDCS-64900392.translation: Expenses 3000 sesterces. For the clerk Syntyche, [---]r[---], [---]a[---], [---]elli, for Celeris, for Erotis, for Marcus Burrius Euphemus, for Lucius Mammius Felix(?), for Marcus [---], for Sextus Atellius Se[---].
EDCS-64900392.summary: This is a record of expenses, likely for a funerary arrangement or a contract, listing various individuals and an amount of money.

EDCS-64900394.translation: In the consulship of Titus Clodius Eprius Marcellus and Gaius Junius Marullus, on the day before the Nones of December. Publius Comicius Severus wrote that I received from Lucius Cominius Primus [---].
EDCS-64900394.summary: This document records a financial transaction, likely a payment received, from the time of the consuls Titus Clodius Eprius Marcellus and Gaius Junius Marullus.

EDCS-64900395.translation: Of Quintus Granius Verus, [of Marcus Clodius Magnus] Munatianus, [---]a[---]mi, [of Publius] Flavius Largus, of Marcus Aquilius [---], of Aulus Tetteius Mystis, of Gaius Petronius Stephanus, the father, of Publius Comicius Severus, of Gaius Petronius Stephanus, of Sextus Caecilius Carpus, of Gaius Opsius Staphylus, [of Quintus] Junius Apsyrtus, [of Lucius Marius] Chryserotis, of Marcus Nonius Abascantus.
EDCS-64900395.summary: This inscription lists individuals, likely creditors or witnesses, associated with financial transactions or legal matters, possibly related to the EDCS-64900394 document.

EDCS-64900396.translation: Tabellae donationis. For the clerk Themidis(?), son of Marcus Julius Primigenius, vendor(?), on the Ides of February, in the reign of Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus, Lucius Antistius Vetus and [---] being consuls. [---] of Quintus Lollius, [---] of Gaius Petronius F[---], [---] of Marcus Clodius S[---], [---] of Quintus Trebius H[---], [---] of Marcus Badius Blastus, [---] of Aulus Tetteius Stephanus(?), [---] of Marcus Burrius Euphemus(?).
EDCS-64900396.summary: This document records a donation, possibly for a religious or public purpose, during the consulship of Lucius Antistius Vetus and under Emperor Nero.

EDCS-64900397.translation: From [---] of the payment made for the fund of Comicianus Alficianus. [---] of Publius Comicius Severus, slave. Of Marcus Rufellius Robia. Of Quintus Marius Hispanus. Of Tiberius Crassus Firmus. Of Quintus Junius Theophilus. Of Gaius Alficius Vitalis. [---] of Publius Comicius Severus, slave.
EDCS-64900397.summary: This inscription details a payment made for a specific property, the fund of Comicianus Alficianus, and lists individuals involved, possibly as witnesses or parties to the transaction.

EDCS-64900398.translation: To Marcus Crassus Frugi and Gaius Laecanius Bassus, consuls, on the day before the Ides of June. Publius Comicius Severus wrote that I received from Lucius Cominius Primus from the price of the fund of Linisianus, which I sold to him, two hundred and ninety-five thousand sesterces, of which sum I received before this day two hundred [---] thousand three [---] fifty, and today [---] thirty [---].
EDCS-64900398.summary: This document records a significant real estate transaction by Publius Comicius Severus, detailing a partial payment for the sale of the fund of Linisianus.

EDCS-64900608.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Caelius Moschion, former priest of Augustus and also quinquennial official of the colony of Ostia, made this for himself while alive, and for his wife Sergia Adladi, and for his sons, and also for his freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants, by the concession of Ulpius Zoticus and Grptus Faustus.
EDCS-64900608.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Marcus Caelius Moschion for himself and his family, likely in Ostia, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-64900864.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or ownership mark, referring to Lucius Acilius Strabo.

EDCS-64900865.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or ownership mark, referring to Lucius Acilius Strabo.

EDCS-64900866.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or ownership mark, referring to Lucius Acilius Strabo.

EDCS-64900867.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or ownership mark, referring to Lucius Acilius Strabo.

EDCS-64900877.translation: To the departed spirits. Evangellus lived for one year and 3 months.
EDCS-64900877.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Evangellus, recording his short lifespan.

EDCS-64900931.translation: [...] his son / [...] lived 19 years / [...]via, freedwoman of Gaius Communis.
EDCS-64900931.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young man, mentioning his father and a freedwoman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-64900966.translation: [...]RM / [...] Victori / [...]ia Telusia / [...] wife(?) / [...] on the Kalends of October(?).
EDCS-64900966.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription that seems to record names and a date, possibly related to a funerary or dedicatory context.

EDCS-64900991.translation: To Matidia Augusta, daughter of the Emperor. Quintus Classicus Primus made this.
EDCS-64900991.summary: This is a dedication to Matidia, daughter of Emperor Trajan, likely erected by Quintus Classicus Primus.

EDCS-64900994.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Ovius Eventius Felix, who lived for two years and 8 months. Marcus Ovius Felix, father, made this for his dearest, incomparable son, at his own expense.
EDCS-64900994.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a father for his young son, Marcus Ovius Eventius Felix, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-64901129.translation: To Marcus Titius Iustus, son of Marcus, of the Palatine tribe. He lived 50 years. Made by a deserving person.
EDCS-64901129.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Titius Iustus, who lived 50 years, with the maker noted as 'bene merenti'.

EDCS-64901130.translation: [Son?] of Decimus, freedman. [---]us, freedman of his patron. To Dionysius, freedman.
EDCS-64901130.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription possibly recording relationships between freedmen and their patron.

EDCS-64901131.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Titus Aelius Pythagoras, freedman of Augustus, procurator of inheritances in the region of Campania, curator of pleasure estates. He lived 75 years. Titus Aelius Pythagoras, for his most dutiful son, and for himself, and for his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, made this in his lifetime. In front, 26 feet; on the side, [3] feet.
EDCS-64901131.summary: A tombstone for Titus Aelius Pythagoras, a freedman of Augustus and an imperial official, who lived 75 years, set up by himself for his family and descendants.

EDCS-64901145.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Harmonia, a well-deserving slave, made this for Claudia Optatina. She lived 18 years, 11 months, 5 days.
EDCS-64901145.summary: A tombstone for Claudia Optatina, a slave who lived 18 years, 11 months, and 5 days, with the monument erected by Harmonia.

EDCS-65200004.translation: Publius Sextilius Epiphanes, freedman of Publius. Flavia Tertia made this from her own funds. In the front, 3.5 feet; in the depth, 3 feet.
EDCS-65200004.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Sextilius Epiphanes, indicating dimensions of the plot and that Flavia Tertia paid for it.

EDCS-65200005.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Geminia Fortunata. She lived 26 years.
EDCS-65200005.summary: A tombstone for Geminia Fortunata, who lived 26 years.

EDCS-65200006.translation: Publius Granius Cordus, son of Publius, son of Publius. The bones are here.
EDCS-65200006.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Granius Cordus, stating that his bones are buried there.

EDCS-65200008.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Rufaedia Vena, freedwoman of Publius.
EDCS-65200008.summary: A fragmentary tombstone for Rufaedia Vena, a freedwoman.

EDCS-65200017.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Rodo[p]es. She lived 16 years, 2 months. Hermes, father.
EDCS-65200017.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Rodo[p]es, who lived 16 years and 2 months, erected by her father, Hermes.

EDCS-65200018.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Publius Saenius Verus, his master, who lived 60 years. Aelius Charito made this for a well-deserving recipient. To the spirits of the departed. For Publius Saenius Verus, his master, who lived 60 years. Aelius Charito made this for a well-deserving recipient.
EDCS-65200018.summary: A tombstone for Publius Saenius Verus, a master who lived 60 years, erected by his freedman Aelius Charito, with the text repeated.

EDCS-65200020.translation: To the departed spirits. Primilla to her husband Marcus Hortensius Eutyches, and his son to his father, who deserved well of them.
EDCS-65200020.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Marcus Hortensius Eutyches by his wife and son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65200021.translation: To the departed spirits. Brinna Menander, Parthenope, and Drosis made this for their most devoted daughter, Brinnia Helias, daughter of Brinnia, daughter of Caius, who lived [for]... years.
EDCS-65200021.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by parents for their beloved daughter Brinnia Helias, indicating she died at a young age during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65200022.summary: A simple tombstone for Clodia Gnome, recording her name and age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65200023.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelia Maxima made this for her husband Aurelius Diligens, who deserved well.
EDCS-65200023.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by a wife for her husband Aurelius Diligens, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65200024.translation: To the departed spirits. His mother made this for Marcus Octavius Crescentianus, who lived 25 years, 10 months, and 18 days.
EDCS-65200024.summary: A funerary epitaph from a mother for her son Marcus Octavius Crescentianus, providing precise age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65200025.translation: To the departed spirits. For Tiberius Julius Verecundus, who lived 16 years.
EDCS-65200025.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Julius Verecundus, a young man who died at 16, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65200026.translation: To the departed spirits. Julius Primitibus made this for his deserving wife Antonia Sabina, who lived 23 years, and also for Julius Parthenopeus, his freedman.
EDCS-65200026.summary: A funerary epitaph by a husband for his wife Antonia Sabina and their freedman Julius Parthenopeus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65200027.translation: To the departed spirits. Velia Rufina, her daughter Cornelia Agathe, and her freedman Cornelius Epigonus made this for their excellent husband and master, Cornelius Agathon, who lived 50 years.
EDCS-65200027.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by a wife, daughter, and freedman for Cornelius Agathon, a man who lived to 50, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65200067.translation: The bones of Hostia Lycninis, freedwoman of Marcus, are placed here. Marcus Hostius Cerdo, freedman of Marcus, to his wife...
EDCS-65200067.summary: A funerary inscription indicating the burial of Hostia Lycninis, with a dedication from her husband Marcus Hostius Cerdo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65200068.translation: Caius Marcius Heuticus, freedman of Caius. His bones are placed here. Marcia, his fellow freedwoman, made this at her own expense.
EDCS-65200068.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Marcius Heuticus, erected by his fellow freedwoman Marcia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65200069.translation: The bones of Caius Lurius Clarus are located here.
EDCS-65200069.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating the location of the remains of Caius Lurius Clarus.

EDCS-65200073.translation: The bones of Primi Albi are located here.
EDCS-65200073.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating the location of the remains of Primi Albi.

EDCS-65200078.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Calpurnius Exoriens made this for his most excellent wife, Calpurnia Hilara.
EDCS-65200078.summary: A funerary dedication by Calpurnius Exoriens to his wife Calpurnia Hilara, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-65200125.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Trebonia Flaccilla, daughter of Lucius, made this for her well-deserving husband, Quintus Gargennius Bassus.
EDCS-65200125.summary: A funerary dedication by Trebonia Flaccilla to her husband Quintus Gargennius Bassus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-65200141.translation: Melanthus made this for his wife Psechas, with whom he lived for... years.
EDCS-65200141.summary: A funerary dedication by Melanthus to his wife Psechas, with a partially preserved record of their married life.

EDCS-65200142.translation: This monument or tomb belongs to Apella, freedman of the deified Augustus and Augustalis, for himself and for Phoebe, a fellow freedwoman, and for his son Aratus; it will not pass to his heir.
EDCS-65200142.summary: A funerary inscription from the imperial period establishing a tomb for Apella, his fellow freedwoman Phoebe, and son Aratus, with a non-hereditary clause.

EDCS-65200152.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Julius Symphorus, a most excellent and studious youth, who lived 19 years and 17 days. His most unhappy parents, Symphor and Phyllis, [dedicated this] to their most devoted son.
EDCS-65200152.summary: A funerary dedication by parents Symphor and Phyllis to their son Lucius Julius Symphorus, who died at age 19, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-65300001.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Iulia Delicata. The patron...
EDCS-65300001.summary: A funerary dedication to Iulia Delicata, with a mention of a patron, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-65300002.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Cominius Eutyches, a most sweet son, well-deserving, [from] parents, who lived 15 years.
EDCS-65300002.summary: A funerary dedication by parents to their son Gaius Cominius Eutyches, who died at age 15, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-65300003.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Roma, to her most chaste wife, made this.
EDCS-65300003.summary: A funerary dedication by Flavia Roma to her wife Flavia Roma, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-65300004.translation: To the divine shades, Serius Peregrinus, Sentia Hesperis, his wife, and his children, deserving, made (this).
EDCS-65300004.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a husband and children for his deserving wife, Sentia Hesperis.

EDCS-65300091.translation: For Cocceia Galla, a most distinguished lady.
EDCS-65300091.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Cocceia Galla, a woman of high social standing.

EDCS-65300092.translation: To Aiaces Maximaes, a most distinguished lady.
EDCS-65300092.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aiaces Maximaes, a woman of high social standing.

EDCS-65300093.translation: To Aiaces Maximaes, a most distinguished lady.
EDCS-65300093.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aiaces Maximaes, a woman of high social standing.

EDCS-65400047.translation: The bones of Aulus Staberi Philargurus, freedman of Aulus, are located here.
EDCS-65400047.summary: This funerary inscription marks the burial place of Aulus Staberi Philargurus, a freedman.

EDCS-65900049.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating Cornelia Salonina Augusta.

EDCS-65900053.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to record a land measurement, possibly related to a boundary or burial plot.

EDCS-65900071.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely referring to Gnaeus Domitius Auctus.

EDCS-66200016.translation: To Gaius Julius Erucius Geminus, lictor of three decuries.
EDCS-66200016.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Julius Erucius Geminus, a lictor holding a significant position.

EDCS-66700001.translation: ... Primus El[---], to whom by decree of the Senate [---] Bassus Marcus [---] from his place [---]
EDCS-66700001.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to record a grant or allocation of land made by senatorial decree to someone named Primus, possibly related to Marcus Bassus.

EDCS-66700002.translation: Marcus Larcius, son of Marcus, of the Mass... tribe, ordered this to be made and restored at public expense, according to a decree of the registered citizens.
EDCS-66700002.summary: This inscription is a public works record from an unknown location, likely indicating the restoration or construction of a building or structure.

EDCS-66700003.translation: Gaius Aeclanius Quartio, son of Gaius, freedman of Gaius, made this for his father Philo, freedman of Gaius, and for his mother Martha, freedwoman of Marcus.
EDCS-66700003.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a son for his parents, likely dating to the late Republic or early Imperial period, given the nomenclature.

EDCS-66700004.translation: Afinia Helvi, daughter of Lucius. Helvia Secunda, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-66700004.summary: This inscription appears to be a tombstone or memorial for two women, Afinia Helvi and Helvia Secunda, indicating their parentage and tribal affiliations.

EDCS-66700005.translation: Gaius Titurius Felix, freedman of Gaius, made this for Decitiae Fortunatae, freedwoman of Lucius, and for himself, and for Gaius Titurius Sabinus, son of Gaius, of the Teretina tribe.
EDCS-66700005.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Titurius Felix for a freedwoman and himself, and also for Gaius Titurius Sabinus, possibly a relative or patron.

EDCS-66700006.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fannis Heliodora lived 14 years, 5 months, and 10 days. Fannia Quinta made this for her cherished foster-daughter.
EDCS-66700006.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Fannis Heliodora, erected by Fannia Quinta, likely her foster mother, and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-66700007.translation: [---]us, son of Marcus, of the Tromentina tribe, while alive; for [---]o, son of Marcus, his father; [---]ratia Nais.
EDCS-66700007.summary: This inscription appears to be a tombstone with incomplete information about the deceased and a surviving son, possibly honoring his father as well.

EDCS-66700008.translation: For [---]oni, freedman of Lucius, and his wife Paccia, daughter of Gaius. [---] and Paccia made this for themselves.
EDCS-66700008.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a couple, possibly naming the husband as Philo, for themselves and possibly other family members.

EDCS-66700009.translation: Larcia, daughter of Marcus, [---] for Marcus Larcius, son of Publius, of the Teretina tribe, [---] priest, duumvir [---]. For Rammia [---]. From the will of Publius Larcius, son of Publius, of the Teretina tribe [---].
EDCS-66700009.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication, likely ordered by a will, for Marcus Larcius, who held priestly and magistratial offices, and possibly other family members.

EDCS-66700010.translation: For himself and for [---] Philodamus, and for Faustus his freedwoman, [---]esimus.
EDCS-66700010.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating dedication for multiple individuals, including a freedman named Philodamus and a freedwoman named Faustus, and possibly the dedicator.

EDCS-66700011.translation: Marcus Coionius, son of Gaius, made this.
EDCS-66700011.summary: This is a simple dedication or funerary monument made by Marcus Coionius, son of Gaius, possibly for himself or a family member.

EDCS-66700013.summary: This inscription names two individuals, Tussen(a)es and Fest(a)es.

EDCS-66700014.translation: For the health of the Emperor Gordian Augustus, in his second consulship, and Pompeianus, when they were consuls.
EDCS-66700014.summary: This is a dedication made for the well-being of Emperor Gordian III and the consul Pompeianus, dated by the consulship.

EDCS-66700016.translation: To the departed spirits. He made this for Hypatus, son of Marcus, deserving well.
EDCS-66700016.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a man named Hypatus, set up by an unspecified person.

EDCS-66700017.translation: To the departed spirits. Man[---]. Quintus? Ho[---]. Quintus [---].
EDCS-66700017.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription, possibly mentioning the names Man--- and Quintus.

EDCS-66700020.summary: This inscription names two individuals, Tussen(a)es and Fest(a)es.

EDCS-66700021.translation: Pompeia Catulla to Aglavus, her freedman and nurse, who deserved well.
EDCS-66700021.summary: This is a dedication by Pompeia Catulla to her freedman and nurse, Aglavus.

EDCS-67100001.translation: To Publius Turcius Severus, son of Publius, of the Stellatina tribe, twice duovir, curator of the public affairs of Interamna and Lirinatum, patron of the colony of Casinate.
EDCS-67100001.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Publius Turcius Severus, detailing his public offices in several towns.

EDCS-67400184.translation: Bruttius, son of Publius. To the Good Goddess and Copia. He added a kitchen, and a basin [---].
EDCS-67400184.summary: This is a dedication to the Bona Dea and Copia, describing additions made to a building, possibly a kitchen and basin.

EDCS-67400186.translation: Marcus Sulpicius Eutyches, who was also called Passaras, VIvir Augustalis and quinquennial curator of the same, made with his own money the pavement of the area of the temple of the Mother of the Gods and a small wall on the right side for those entering, and he also gave curtains for the temple of the Mother of the Gods and at the entrance to the sanctuary as a gift. Furthermore, he restored and dedicated the castellum, corrupted by age, at his own expense, on the twelfth day before the Kalends of October.
EDCS-67400186.summary: This is a detailed dedication by Marcus Sulpicius Eutyches (Passaras) listing several building projects and restorations he funded for the temple of the Mother of the Gods and its precinct.

EDCS-67400187.translation: Freely vows. To the Great Goddess, Mother of Idaea, Julia Gemella.
EDCS-67400187.summary: This is a votive offering by Julia Gemella to the Great Mother Goddess, Magna Mater Idaea.

EDCS-67400490.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Marcia Apricula, an incomparable woman. Marcus Verrius Calliparthenus, her husband, [dedicated this].
EDCS-67400490.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcia Apricula, dedicated by her husband Marcus Verrius Calliparthenus.

EDCS-67400491.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Luccia Disciana, who lived 35 years, 10 months, and 19 days. Gaius Julius Flavianus, a retired soldier, made this for his incomparable and deserving wife.
EDCS-67400491.summary: A funerary epitaph for Luccia Disciana, detailing her lifespan and dedicated by her husband Gaius Julius Flavianus.

EDCS-68200040.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Tiberius Claudius Mansuetus, veteran of Legio IIII Flavia Felix, from Pergamum in Asia, lived 61 years. Tiberius Claudius Magnus, his freedman and heir, made this for his deserving patron.
EDCS-68200040.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Mansuetus, a veteran, dedicated by his freedman and heir.

EDCS-68900026.summary: A very brief inscription, likely funerary, indicating that Felicitas resides here, possibly in a tomb.

EDCS-69000012.translation: To Chrestilla. Cornelia Chereste, freedwoman of Titus, [dedicated this] to her mother.
EDCS-69000012.summary: An epitaph dedicated by Cornelia Chereste to her mother, Chrestilla.

EDCS-69000013.translation: For the living. For Caius Paquius Astragalus, freedman of Caius, and for Caius Dion, freedman of Caius, and for Psycharion, freedwoman of Dentria. [---]
EDCS-69000013.summary: An inscription dedicating a monument or plot of land to several living individuals, including freedmen and freedwomen.

EDCS-69000064.translation: Allia Nygmis, freedwoman of Marcus, lived 7 years.
EDCS-69000064.summary: A funerary epitaph for Allia Nygmis, recording her age at death and her relationship as a freedwoman.

EDCS-69000065.translation: Aulus Seius Dasius, freedman of Aulus. 20 feet in the field, 24 feet in the front.
EDCS-69000065.summary: An inscription likely related to property boundaries, indicating measurements for a plot of land owned by Aulus Seius Dasius.

EDCS-69000068.translation: Entrusted to Hortensius Tatinus, son of Tatinus, so that his memory and health might fail from exhaustion.
EDCS-69000068.summary: A peculiar inscription that seems to invoke a curse or express ill will towards Hortensius Tatinus, possibly related to a debt or dispute.

EDCS-69000083.translation: For the living. For Lucius Plautius Scurra, son of Marcus (tribe Oufentina), and Rusticelia Rufa, freedwoman of a woman, and Plautia Tertia, daughter of Lucius (tribe Oufentina). I loved you so much, my father, that after four months I followed you and left mourning for my grandfather; what a son ought to do for a son, the father did for the son. This monument will not follow an heir.
EDCS-69000083.summary: A complex funerary inscription indicating that a father (Lucius Plautius Scurra) preceded his daughter (Plautia Tertia) in death, and the daughter's tomb was built by her father who then died and followed her, and the tomb is not to be inherited.

EDCS-69000084.translation: Lucius Nonius (suffect consul), Quintus Creticus Aulus Licinius (suffect consul), Lucius Luicilius (suffect consul), Marcus Furius Camillus, Sextus Nonius, Lucius Apronius, Aulus Vibius (suffect consuls), Quintus Sulpicius, Gaius Poppaeus, Marcus Papius, Quintus Poppaeus (suffect consuls), Gaius Claudius, Lucius Cornelius, Gaius Julius (dictator without master of horse), Publius Servilius, Marcus Lepidus, Lucius Cassius (suffect consuls), Germanicus Caesar, Gaius Fonteius (suffect consul), Visellius Munatius Plancus, Gaius Silis (suffect consul), Caecina (suffect consul), Sextus Appuleius, Sextus Pompeius, Drusus Caesar, Gaius Norbanus (suffect consul), Marcus Silanus (suffect consul), Sisennus Statilius, Scribonius, Gaius Julius (dictator), Marcus Antonius (master of horse?), Quintus Fufius, Publius Vatinus, Gaius Julius, Marcus Aemilius, Gaius Julius (dictator), Marcus Aemilius (master of horse?).
EDCS-69000084.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary list of Roman consuls and other high-ranking officials, likely from a fasti consulares or a similar official record, possibly dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-69200010.translation: Publius Lucretius Phallaeus, son of Publius, freedman, Augustalis priest, lives. Caecilia Thais, freedwoman of Gaius, lives. Nysa, freedwoman of Gaius, lives.
EDCS-69200010.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Lucretius Phallaeus, an Augustalis priest, and two freedwomen, Caecilia Thais and Nysa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200011.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to a woman named Calligona, likely a tombstone or a dedication, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200013.translation: B. C. of Asellius Rufus Aemilianus.
EDCS-69200013.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragmentary dedication or ownership mark, possibly relating to an individual named Asellius Rufus Aemilianus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200014.translation: Of Furius Placidus, a man of consular rank.
EDCS-69200014.summary: This is an ownership mark or dedication for Furius Placidus, a person of high status, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200039.translation: Gaius Poppaeus lived here with Hymetus, of the 'ηʹ' faction. He loves and will be loved. His age was 'ναʹ' (55). Its number is 'ϡραʹ' (840).
EDCS-69200039.summary: This inscription is a personal statement by Gaius Poppaeus about his dwelling, his age, and possibly a property value or duration, likely from the Roman Imperial period, incorporating Greek letters and numerals.

EDCS-69200050.summary: This is a simple tombstone inscription recording the name and lifespan of Curtia Phyle, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200051.translation: Lucius Mettius Martialis, son of Marcus, of the Pollia tribe, from Carreae, served as a scout for 10 years and lived 30 years.
EDCS-69200051.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Mettius Martialis, a soldier who served as a scout, detailing his military service and lifespan, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200060.summary: This is a fragmentary ownership mark or dedication, possibly for property or a tomb, belonging to Gaius Oli Ampliatus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69200199.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a name, possibly a tombstone or ownership mark, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-69500137.translation: To the shades of the departed. Gaius Farr[u]fanus Fav[o]rabilis, who lived 10 years [and] 6 months, and his most unhappy mother Farrufana Primitiva set this up for her most unhappy son.
EDCS-69500137.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Gaius Farr[u]fanus Fav[o]rabilis, set up by his mother.

EDCS-69500138.translation: To the shades of the departed. For Longeia Veneria, from Longeia Severina, her most sweet daughter.
EDCS-69500138.summary: A funerary epitaph for Longeia Veneria, set up by her daughter.

EDCS-69500145.summary: A fragmentary inscription with only partial names preserved.

EDCS-69800176.translation: To the shades of the departed. [Axia], freedwoman of [Axi]us, [belonging to] Gymnasia Leontis, mother [Axia], freedwoman of Axius, [belonging to] Leontis. Marcus Vibius Dio[---]. Marcus Vibius Dio, freedman of Quinctus Lucius Marcus. Menophilus, freedwoman of Gallus Vibus Paciaecorum, wife of Menophilus. Heracleo, son of Gallus and Vibia. And Arco, son of Gallus and Vibia. And Menophilus, son of Gallus and Vibia. Anulla, daughter of Caepor(?). Secunda, wife of Caeporis, is the wife of Secundae Caeporis. Father is Heracleo Lucius Vibius Cripo and wife is Secunda. [S]anucia(?), his wife. Quintus Vibius Tuscus, freedman of Paeciaecorum Attin, his wife is Publ[i--]. And his brother Marcione. Luipor, freedman of Paciae[corum], his wife is Flora, and his son is Luipor. Quintus Fabius, son of [---] Annia. Fabia Luxinia, daughter of Annia. Fabius [---], father of Annia. Scaevola, father of Annia. [---], who is Annia's brother(?). Quintus Axius, father of the boy, was their master. Lucius Fulvius, son of Numerius. Severa(?), wife of Numerius.
EDCS-69800176.summary: A complex funerary inscription listing family relationships and possibly marking a family burial plot, with multiple individuals named and their familial connections or status as freedpersons indicated.

EDCS-70000083.translation: Publius [---] Aulus Egrilius Rufus, son of Aulus [---]
EDCS-70000083.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning Publius and Aulus Egrilius Rufus, likely a dedication or funerary text.

EDCS-70100089.translation: ] to [his] spouse? Lived 5 months [and] 3 days. Ampliatus laid [this] with peace, deposited. Nones? [---]
EDCS-70100089.summary: A funerary epitaph for someone named Ampliatus, indicating a short lifespan and mentioning a spouse, with a possible date reference to the Nones.

EDCS-70100096.translation: Insteia Eutaxis, freedwoman of Caius. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field. By the authority of Titus Passennus Sura, son of Titus, of the Teretina tribe.
EDCS-70100096.summary: A boundary marker inscription defining a plot of land, specifying dimensions and indicating it was set up under the supervision of Titus Passennus Sura.

EDCS-70100103.translation: By the authority of the Emperor, our lord, Maxentius, the unconquered and perpetual Augustus.
EDCS-70100103.summary: A formal inscription indicating an order or decree issued under the authority of Emperor Maxentius.

EDCS-70100107.translation: To the shades of the departed. To Maia Adelphe, Servilius Barnaeus set this up for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-70100107.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maia Adelphe, set up by her husband Servilius Barnaeus.

EDCS-70400048.summary: A fragmentary inscription beginning with the names of Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, likely part of a larger official dedication or monument.

EDCS-70700045.translation: [Cor]nelius Nicephorus, freedman of Lucius, and freedman of the suburban country district, for Cornelia Hilara, Cornelia Thala, his fellow freedwoman, and for all his own.
EDCS-70700045.summary: This funerary inscription records a freedman, Nicephorus, setting up a tomb for his fellow freedwomen, Cornelia Hilara and Cornelia Thala, and himself.

EDCS-70700175.translation: Gaius Caesius Catulus, son of Gaius, of the Aemilia tribe, a horseman, and Caesia Creusis, his freedwoman.
EDCS-70700175.summary: This inscription identifies Gaius Caesius Catulus, an equestrian, and his freedwoman Caesia Creusis.

EDCS-70800021.translation: Marcus Nigidius Vaccula, with his own money.
EDCS-70800021.summary: This inscription indicates that Marcus Nigidius Vaccula funded a project or monument with his personal funds.

EDCS-70800022.translation: Marcus Nigidius Vaccula, with his own money.
EDCS-70800022.summary: This inscription indicates that Marcus Nigidius Vaccula funded a project or monument with his personal funds.

EDCS-70800023.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication by Marcus Epidius Flaccus.

EDCS-70800024.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication by Lucius Ceius Secundus.

EDCS-70800025.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication by Marcus Stlaborius Auctus.
