EDCS-70800026.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication by Gaius Arrius Crescens.

EDCS-70800027.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or a dedication by Decimus Octavius Quartio.

EDCS-70800028.translation: Of Lucius Helvius Severus. // Of Lucius Helvius Severus.
EDCS-70800028.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial for Lucius Helvius Severus, possibly repeated for emphasis or clarity.

EDCS-70800029.summary: This is a dedication or ownership mark by Marcus Fabius Secundus.

EDCS-70800049.translation: To Everae... daughter, most beloved, who lived 15 years, 6 months, and 15 days. Everata...
EDCS-70800049.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a beloved daughter named Everae, recording her age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70800051.translation: To Hercules Victor, Hermogenes the steward of the Mamurran estate fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-70800051.summary: This is a votive inscription from a steward named Hermogenes to the god Hercules Victor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70800055.translation: [---] Longus, freedman; [---] and [---]a, freedwoman of [---] (his wife/partner),
EDCS-70800055.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly funerary, mentioning freedmen and freedwomen, with names partially lost.

EDCS-70800080.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely funerary, identifying a freedwoman named Quarta.

EDCS-70800081.translation: To the divine shades. Successus made [this] for Vibia Corge, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-70800081.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Successus to his deserving wife, Vibia Corge, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70800085.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, mentioning Marcus Atilius and Atilia, with lacunae.

EDCS-70800091.translation: His patrons made [this] for Marcus Onenius Secundus, who lived 23 years, 3 months, and 14 days, the deserving freedman.
EDCS-70800091.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedman, Marcus Onenius Secundus, erected by his patrons, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70800172.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Claudia Isidora, daughter of Marcus, likely part of a funerary or ownership record.

EDCS-70800199.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Coceia Secunda, recording her age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70800200.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Tiberius Claudius Fructus, his parents Claudius Philammon and Fructa, to their most dutiful and most reverent son.
EDCS-70800200.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated by parents to their beloved son, Tiberius Claudius Fructus.

EDCS-70800201.translation: To Aulus Novius Philostratus, freedman of Lucius, perfumer, by will and by the authority of Surus, freedman.
EDCS-70800201.summary: This inscription records the funerary dedication to Aulus Novius Philostratus, a perfumer, established by will.

EDCS-70800202.translation: During the consulship of Marcus Vinicius and Lucius Cassius, on the day before the Kalends of March. Marcus Laelius Optatus, freedman of Marcus, son of Publius and Lucius. Marcus Verrius Hyla, freedman of Marcus, son of a woman. Lucius Camelius Cladus, freedman of Lucius, son of a woman. Aulus Mevius Polyclitus, freedman of Aulus and Castricia. Caius Marcius Felix, freedman of Caius. Publius Hetereius Euphemus, freedman of Publius. Aulus Mevius Epaphra, freedman of Aulus and Castricia, from Castricium.
EDCS-70800202.summary: This is a complex inscription from the consulship of Marcus Vinicius and Lucius Cassius, listing multiple individuals, likely associated with a collegium or a specific event, possibly related to public works or a funerary association.

EDCS-70800203.translation: During the consulship of Germanicus Caesar and Caius Fonteius Capito. To the duovir [Cluvi]us Celere, son of Manius. Marcus Marius Pothus, freedman of Marcus. Gnaeus Saenius Faustus, freedman of Gnaeus, son of women. Caius Nautius Felix, freedman of Caius.
EDCS-70800203.summary: This inscription, dating to the consulship of Germanicus Caesar and Caius Fonteius Capito, names officials and freedmen, likely related to local administration or a public dedication.

EDCS-70800205.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Fullonia Secundina, who lived 24 years and 31 days. Fullonia Maxima, her mother.
EDCS-70800205.summary: This is a tombstone inscription by a mother, Fullonia Maxima, to her daughter Fullonia Secundina, recording her age at death.

EDCS-70800206.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Claudia Semne, most dutiful daughter of Claudia Magna, who lived 12 years, 3 months, and 22 days.
EDCS-70800206.summary: This tombstone inscription commemorates Claudia Semne, recording her age at death and identifying her mother, Claudia Magna.

EDCS-70800208.translation: To Caius Tadius [---]. Caius Tadius [---]. Cornelia [---].
EDCS-70800208.summary: This fragmented inscription mentions at least two individuals named Caius Tadius and Cornelia, likely part of a dedication or a family record.

EDCS-70800209.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Annia Eutychia. Priscianus [---].
EDCS-70800209.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Annia Eutychia, with the name Priscianus also present, possibly indicating the dedicator or another individual.

EDCS-70800210.translation: [---] 5 months. Caecilia Veneria, mother, made this for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-70800210.summary: This fragmented inscription records a dedication made by Caecilia Veneria, a mother, for her son, and includes a duration of 5 months.

EDCS-70800211.translation: During the consulship of Lucius Munatius and Caius Silius [---]. Publius Stlaccius [---], freedman of Publius. Publius Gessius [---], freedman of Publius. Lucius Tetteius [---], freedman of Lucius. [---]
EDCS-70800211.summary: This inscription from the consulship of Lucius Munatius and Caius Silius lists several freedmen and is significantly fragmented, likely part of a larger dedication or record.

EDCS-70800248.translation: Maria Chreste, freedwoman of Caius. Caius Marius Brutus, son, freedman of Caius. Frontage 16 feet, field 20 feet.
EDCS-70800248.summary: This funerary epitaph records a plot of land measuring 16 feet in front and 20 feet in the field, dedicated by Caius Marius Brutus to his freedwoman Maria Chreste.

EDCS-70800249.summary: This is a dedication on a tombstone for Caltilia Felicula, the grandmother, likely by a descendant.

EDCS-70800307.summary: This inscription lists three individuals, two of whom are identified as Augustales, possibly referring to members of a collegium or high-ranking citizens.

EDCS-70800309.translation: Fufuleia Philotaera, freedwoman of Marcus. The heirs do not succeed.
EDCS-70800309.summary: This funerary inscription states that the heirs of Fufuleia Philotaera, freedwoman of Marcus, are not entitled to inherit, possibly indicating a specific arrangement or debt.

EDCS-70800311.translation: Philo[---], freedwoman of [---]a. For herself and her husband Antigonus Alexander, freedman of Cotiaeus, Augustalis, and for her daughter Carvilia Clara, and for Aprilis, slave. This monument will not pass to the heirs.
EDCS-70800311.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for multiple individuals, including freed slaves and a slave, indicating that the monument is not to be inherited by heirs.

EDCS-70800371.translation: To Pollio Iulius Clementianus, a most perfect man, who for himself and for the fatherland, and for the citizens, conferred many pleasures and supported countless necessities with his labors and honors; and to the weary order, he conferred no small benefits. The entire number of the curia has placed this statue in recognition of his above-mentioned merits and labors.
EDCS-70800371.summary: This dedication honors Pollio Iulius Clementianus with a statue, acknowledging his generous contributions to the fatherland, citizens, and the curia (city council or guild).

EDCS-70800376.translation: For the health of Cornelius Aemilianus and his wife Tityaena, we, the college of the devoted, have restored this.
EDCS-70800376.summary: This inscription records the restoration of something by a collegium (association or guild) for the well-being of Cornelius Aemilianus and his wife.

EDCS-70800377.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcianus, who lived 25 years, 6 months, and 10 days. Mother and father made this for their most beloved, well-deserving son.
EDCS-70800377.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcianus, who died at the age of 25, made by his grieving parents.

EDCS-70800378.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Publicius Signinus, who lived 70 years and 6 months, his sons made this for their deserving father.
EDCS-70800378.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Publicius Signinus, who lived to 70 years and 6 months, erected by his sons.

EDCS-70800382.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aurelius Theodorus, most beloved son. Aurelia Bassa, mother, made this.
EDCS-70800382.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Theodorus, made by his mother, Aurelia Bassa.

EDCS-70800383.summary: This is a dedication to Tiberius Claudius Servandus, with the rest of the inscription missing.

EDCS-70900030.translation: Property of the freedmen of Publius Tadius (a woman), Tarus and Antoninus, freedmen of Publius Tadius. In front 13 feet, in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-70900030.summary: This is a boundary marker for a plot of land, indicating its dimensions and ownership by freedmen.

EDCS-70900031.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Eglecte made this for her daughter Iulia Hygeia and her husband Fortunatus, deserving of remembrance.
EDCS-70900031.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Iulia Eglecte for her daughter and husband.

EDCS-70900170.summary: This appears to be the beginning of a dedication or epitaph for Titus Titius, possibly made by himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-70900171.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Catilia Fortunata made this for her very dear husband Lupus, an actor, deserving of remembrance.
EDCS-70900171.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Catilia Fortunata to her husband Lupus, who was an actor.

EDCS-70900179.summary: This inscription is heavily damaged and appears to be part of a funerary epitaph, mentioning individuals whose relationships and names are largely lost.

EDCS-70900180.translation: ]s, of the slaves of Postumus Fabius / [---] / [---] fellow slaves made (this).
EDCS-70900180.summary: This inscription, though incomplete, records a dedication or memorial made by slaves belonging to Postumus Fabius.

EDCS-70900197.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Lucius Avianus, likely to a deity.

EDCS-70900227.summary: This is a simple inscription likely naming an individual, Petacia Vitalis, possibly on a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-70900228.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Marcus Petacius Communis.

EDCS-70900229.translation: To Marcus Petacius, son of Marcus, of the Menenia tribe.
EDCS-70900229.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Petacius, son of Marcus.

EDCS-70900230.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Petacia Montana, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-70900231.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Petacia Rufilla.

EDCS-70900232.summary: This is likely a funerary epitaph for Marcus Petacius Dasius.

EDCS-70900233.translation: Marcus Petacius Severus, son of Marcus, of the Menenia tribe, lived 17 years.
EDCS-70900233.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Petacius Severus, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-70900235.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amandus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-70900236.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Curvia Atalante, who died at the age of 28.

EDCS-70900239.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felicula, who died at the age of 8.

EDCS-70900240.translation: Fortunatus, a slave of Pisullia, lived 4 years.
EDCS-70900240.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fortunatus, a slave, who died at the age of 4.

EDCS-70900241.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fortunata, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-70900242.summary: This inscription records the names of two individuals, Lasaea and Venusta.

EDCS-70900244.summary: This inscription records the name of Poppaea Corinna.

EDCS-70900245.translation: Quinta lived for 1 year and [3] months
EDCS-70900245.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Quinta.

EDCS-70900247.translation: Quintus Caecilius Iunionius, son of Quintus, lived 27 years
EDCS-70900247.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, parentage, and age of Quintus Caecilius Iunionius.

EDCS-70900248.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age of Fortunatus.

EDCS-70900249.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age of Ianuarius.

EDCS-70900250.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Gaius Quintius Restitutus.

EDCS-70900251.summary: This inscription records the names of Lucius Gavius Iucundus.

EDCS-70900252.translation: To Cornelia Primigenia, who lived 45 years
EDCS-70900252.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age of Cornelia Primigenia.

EDCS-70900253.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age of Ursilla.

EDCS-70900254.translation: To Dellia, freedwoman of Quintus, named Chia.
EDCS-70900254.summary: This is a dedication to Dellia Chia, a freedwoman of Quintus.

EDCS-70900255.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Venustus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-70900256.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ories, recording his age at death.

EDCS-70900278.summary: This records a bequest by Marcus Lucretius Rufus.

EDCS-70900279.summary: This records a bequest by Marcus Lucretius Rufus.

EDCS-70900280.summary: This records a bequest by Marcus Lucretius Rufus.

EDCS-70900285.translation: To Popidia Venusta, from Popidius Cotidianus.
EDCS-70900285.summary: This is a dedication to Popidia Venusta by Popidius Cotidianus.

EDCS-70900323.translation: To Lucius Melissaeus Plocamus, and to Fausta, freedwoman of the woman Thorasius, and to Prisca, freedwoman of the woman Thorasius, for themselves and their families.
EDCS-70900323.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Lucius Melissaeus Plocamus and two freedwomen, Fausta and Prisca, for themselves and their families.

EDCS-70900324.summary: This inscription appears to be a name, possibly identifying a person named Topyrus.

EDCS-70900325.translation: To Marcus Tullius, son of Marcus, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-70900325.summary: This records a dedication to Marcus Tullius, son of Marcus, made by order of the town council (decurions).

EDCS-70900380.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sergia Maior, identified by her gentilician and cognomen, and her father's name.

EDCS-70900458.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Agatho, identified as a freedman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-70900459.translation: To Volusia Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, aged 32.
EDCS-70900459.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Volusia Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, indicating her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-70900460.translation: To Volusia Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, aged 21.
EDCS-70900460.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Volusia Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, indicating her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-70900465.translation: To Volusia Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, aged 20.
EDCS-70900465.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Volusia Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, indicating her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-70900466.translation: To Volusia Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, aged 18.
EDCS-70900466.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Volusia Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, indicating her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-70900467.summary: This inscription likely records ownership or dedication by Gaius Julius Silanus, possibly related to a monument or property, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70900519.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Egnatius, indicating he is buried at this location, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70900520.translation: Lucius Licinius Marius, freedman of Lucius, perfumer. Licinia Musa, freedwoman of Lucius. Licinia Nice, mother.
EDCS-70900520.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Licinius Marius, his freedwoman Licinia Musa, and her mother Licinia Nice, with Marius identified as a perfumer, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70900575.translation: Of our Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus.
EDCS-70900575.summary: This inscription is part of a dedication or dedication to the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-70900681.translation: To the departed spirits. To Septimia Severa, her dearest and most innocent foster-daughter, who lived 18 years. Silvanus, freedman of the Emperor, made this for her deserving of merit.
EDCS-70900681.summary: A funerary epitaph for Septimia Severa, set up by her foster-father, Silvanus, a freedman of the emperor.

EDCS-70900683.translation: To the departed spirits, to the memory of Servilia Ampiata. Quintus Servilius Aprilis, her husband, for his dearest wife and himself.
EDCS-70900683.summary: A funerary epitaph for Servilia Ampiata, set up by her husband, Quintus Servilius Aprilis.

EDCS-70900684.translation: To the departed spirits. For Macrinus, freedman of the Emperor, procurator of the Vicesima hereditatium (inheritance tax) for the Tarricina region. Aelia Helpis made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-70900684.summary: A funerary epitaph for Macrinus, an imperial freedman and tax procurator, set up by his wife Aelia Helpis.

EDCS-70900685.translation: A decree of the Senate, which was made on the authority of Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, dictator. [---] in the Senate were [---] aediles [---] Iunii / Aulus Co[---]anus and Acerius(?) Sabinus [---]ius Augurinus(?) and Tiberius Latinius Rufinus [---]annius Lucius Annius Vegetus [---] Licinus(?) Gaius Iuventius Celer [---]cus II [---] Lucilius(?) Longus [---] Quintus Avonius
EDCS-70900685.summary: A fragmented record of a senatorial decree, likely concerning public officials or administrative matters, with several names of senators and officials.

EDCS-70900686.translation: Of Quintus Luscius Frugi, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-70900686.summary: A fragmented inscription likely identifying a freedman named Quintus Luscius Frugi.

EDCS-70900777.translation: The Laurentes Lavinates [---] of Publius Lucretius Clementis
EDCS-70900777.summary: A partial inscription mentioning the people of Laurentum and Lavinium and possibly related to Publius Lucretius Clementis.

EDCS-70900778.summary: A dedication to Emperor Verus Augustus.

EDCS-70900890.translation: To the departed spirits. For Mummia Hilariatas. Quintus Fabius Pollius Laertius made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-70900890.summary: A funerary epitaph for Mummia Hilariatas, set up by her husband Quintus Fabius Pollius Laertius.

EDCS-70900891.translation: To Nicander, his excellent freedman. Tiberius Claudius Pollio, his patron.
EDCS-70900891.summary: A dedication from a patron, Tiberius Claudius Pollio, to his freedman Nicander.

EDCS-70900898.translation: [---] for himself and his sister, and for his sons [Marcus] Vinicius Onesimus, freedman of Marcus. He made [this].
EDCS-70900898.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription mentioning a person setting up a monument for themselves, their sister, and their sons, including a freedman named Onesimus.

EDCS-70901046.translation: To the departed spirits. His well-deserving wife made [this] for Optatus Silvanus, her well-deserving husband, who lived 70 years.
EDCS-70901046.summary: A funerary epitaph for Optatus Silvanus, set up by his wife.

EDCS-70901054.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Vibius Valens, a sesquiplicarius (petty officer) of the trireme Capricorno, his heir, made [this] for Tiberius Claudius Phoebus, a maniple-commander of the trireme Virtute, an Asian by nation, who lived 30 years and served 14 years, his well-deserving deceased.
EDCS-70901054.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Phoebus, a naval officer, set up by his heir, Lucius Vibius Valens.

EDCS-70901056.translation: Lucius Domitius Primigenius for himself, and for his wife Domitia Thya, and for their freedmen and freedwomen, and for their descendants.
EDCS-70901056.summary: A dedication inscription for a family tomb or monument, listing the owner and beneficiaries.

EDCS-70901078.summary: A fragmentary inscription that likely records the name and possibly a title or affiliation of an individual named Proculus, possibly related to the emperor.

EDCS-70901079.translation: [To the departed spirits] for Marcus Popilius Faustus. Vinicia Secunda made [this].
EDCS-70901079.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Popilius Faustus, set up by Vinicia Secunda.

EDCS-71000044.translation: [... to] Apollo Sanctus, a temple [...]. Julius (?) Athyron and Julia Aty[...]
EDCS-71000044.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording the dedication of a temple to Apollo Sanctus by individuals named Julius (?) Athyron and Julia Aty[...].

EDCS-71000243.summary: A fragmentary inscription simply stating the name of Lucius Aburius Successus, possibly as an owner or dedicator.

EDCS-71000244.translation: Publius Maccius Velasianus and Publius Maccius Mamianus Fubzanus, heirs, replaced [this] at their own expense for their father Lucius Maccius Papi, son of Papius, and for their mother Spellia Ovi, daughter of Ovius, and for their wife Epidia Auli, daughter of Aulus.
EDCS-71000244.summary: An inscription recording the restoration of a monument by heirs for their deceased parents and wife.

EDCS-71000245.summary: A very short inscription, likely an ownership mark or a dedication by Marcus Celius Africanus.

EDCS-71000248.translation: For Marcus Artorius Philero, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-71000248.summary: An inscription dedicating something to Marcus Artorius Philero, a freedman of Marcus.

EDCS-71000249.summary: This is a dedication to Naevia Quartilla.

EDCS-71000252.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Epidius Antychus.

EDCS-71000253.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of Vibia Pelagia.

EDCS-71000256.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Ulpius Primillus erected this for his well-deserving wife, Munia Maxima, who lived for 60 years and was his wife for 45 years, and for him.
EDCS-71000256.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Marcus Ulpius Primillus for his wife Munia Maxima, detailing their marriage and her lifespan.

EDCS-71000267.summary: This fragmentary inscription names a man and his father, likely part of a larger dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-71000309.translation: Tiberius Novius, son of Tiberius, Ru[---].
EDCS-71000309.summary: This fragmentary inscription names Tiberius Novius, son of Tiberius, and is likely part of a personal monument.

EDCS-71000310.translation: Lucia Pullia Marga, freedwoman of Gaius, wife of Lucius Pullius Gallus, son of Gaius, of the Teretina tribe.
EDCS-71000310.summary: This inscription identifies Lucia Pullia Marga as the wife of Lucius Pullius Gallus, providing her status and his filiation and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-71000509.translation: Marcus Marius, son of Gaius, Papus, the man. Publius Ummidius [---], father [---].
EDCS-71000509.summary: This inscription names Marcus Marius Papus and likely records a familial relationship with Publius Ummidius.

EDCS-71000514.summary: This is a simple epitaph indicating the burial place of Suellius.

EDCS-71000527.translation: To Puppienius Rufus, son of [---], of the Romilia tribe, his sister's husband.
EDCS-71000527.summary: This inscription is a dedication from a man to his brother-in-law, Puppienius Rufus.

EDCS-71000528.translation: To the divine shades of Minucia Ianuaria, his most excellent wife, her husband erected [this monument].
EDCS-71000528.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to a woman named Minucia Ianuaria by her husband.

EDCS-71000529.translation: Captia Hetaera, freedwoman of Marcus; Auge Posilla, freedwoman of the woman Captia.
EDCS-71000529.summary: This is a funerary epitaph listing two women, possibly related or freedwomen of the same patron.

EDCS-71000534.summary: This inscription appears to be a simple tombstone or memorial for Lucius Nunnius Rufus.

EDCS-71000589.translation: Iulia Thallusa, his mother, placed [this monument] with her own money for Publius Acilius Chresimus; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-71000589.summary: This is a funerary monument for Publius Acilius Chresimus, set up by his mother, Iulia Thallusa, with land granted by local authorities.

EDCS-71000632.translation: [...] for himself and his family; he died [on the same day as] Lucius Dasius, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-71000632.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary dedication for a person and their family, noting a simultaneous death with Lucius Dasius.

EDCS-71000633.translation: Caius Veturius [?]; Marcus Racilius [?]; Marcus Racilius [?]; Marcus Racilius [?].
EDCS-71000633.summary: This inscription lists several names, possibly related to a family or a group, but the context is incomplete.

EDCS-71000638.summary: This is a partial inscription, likely the beginning of a name or dedication.

EDCS-71000685.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods, Lucius Iulius Martialis, priest, [dedicated this]; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-71000685.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Mother of the Gods by a priest named Lucius Iulius Martialis, with the land granted by local authorities.

EDCS-71000692.translation: Quintus Granius Histia, freedman of Quintus; Arellia Prima, freedwoman of Caius; [another?] Arellia, freedwoman of Caius; Granius [...]
EDCS-71000692.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, likely freedmen and their patrons or family members, with some illegible portions.

EDCS-71000694.translation: To the divine shades of Hastas, freedwoman of the woman Caius, who was Augustalis.
EDCS-71000694.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hastas, identified as a freedwoman and possibly holding a public office or status as Augustalis.

EDCS-71000702.translation: To the quaestor of the alimenta, who lived 3 years, 3 months, and 17 days, which he himself owed to his parents, Titus Mecenas Priscus, father, and Cinacina, mother, made this monument.
EDCS-71000702.summary: This is a funerary monument for a quaestor of the alimenta, set up by his parents.

EDCS-71000706.translation: Silo. He looked on the Ides of April for Lucius...
EDCS-71000706.summary: A very fragmentary inscription that may record a transaction or observation, possibly related to a legal or administrative matter.

EDCS-71000727.translation: Decimus Veturius Athenio, freedman of Decimus, an Augustalis, for himself(?) and...
EDCS-71000727.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription identifying a freedman named Athenio who held the title of Augustalis.

EDCS-71000741.summary: A severely damaged inscription that likely records the maker of an object or monument, but the object and full context are lost.

EDCS-71000742.translation: To the departed spirits. Hereas, freedwoman of the Augusti, for her dearest daughter Chelidon, made this.
EDCS-71000742.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Chelidon by her mother Hereas, a freedwoman of the emperors.

EDCS-71000743.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Cocceius Niger, a common soldier from the trireme, for Satyr[a], of Egyptian nationality, [---]
EDCS-71000743.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for a soldier named Gaius Cocceius Niger, possibly dedicated to a woman named Satyra.

EDCS-71000744.translation: Cocceius Niger, from the trireme, made this for his well-deserving tentmate Satyra Chelidon.
EDCS-71000744.summary: A funerary monument set up by a soldier named Cocceius Niger for his deserved tentmate, Satyra Chelidon.

EDCS-71000745.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Antonius Valerianus lived 10 years, 4 months, and 25 days. Gaius Antonius Bassus, his father, for his most beloved son.
EDCS-71000745.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Antonius Valerianus, who died at a young age, set up by his father.

EDCS-71000746.translation: To the departed spirits. For Fabia Procula. Zosimus.
EDCS-71000746.summary: A concise funerary epitaph for Fabia Procula, with the dedicant named Zosimus.

EDCS-71200515.translation: To Calvia Santilla, a married woman. Gaius Seppius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, pontifex, praetor(?), quinquennial official, and Gaius Seppius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, set this up for his mother. She lived 27 years and 6 months.
EDCS-71200515.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Calvia Santilla by her husband and son, detailing their civic and religious offices and her age at death.

EDCS-71200516.translation: By will from Regilla, freedwoman, [---] under the supervision of Caius Apronius, son of Marcus, [---] son of Lucius, [---] from the Galeria tribe, according to the will [---] 3000 sesterces [---]
EDCS-71200516.summary: This inscription, likely part of a tombstone, details a legacy of 3000 sesterces left in a will by Regilla, with supervision by Caius Apronius.

EDCS-71200518.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; this was made for Marcus Ulpius Caro, an undeserving husband.
EDCS-71200518.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife to her deserving husband, Marcus Ulpius Caro.

EDCS-71200519.translation: To Lucius Stertinius Hyacinthus, freedman, a most rare example and one who deserved very well of me.
EDCS-71200519.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Stertinius Hyacinthus, a freedman, as an exceptional individual deserving of praise.

EDCS-71200520.translation: [---] Onesimus [---], freedman of Publius [---] [---] // Lives. Of Marcus Papius Celsus, son of Marcus, [---]
EDCS-71200520.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to record the survival of Marcus Papius Celsus, son of Marcus, and possibly mentions his freedman Onesimus.

EDCS-71200521.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; Marcus Clodius Crescentianus made this for his most devoted wife, Marcia Felicissima.
EDCS-71200521.summary: A funerary monument erected by Marcus Clodius Crescentianus for his beloved wife, Marcia Felicissima.

EDCS-71200523.translation: Marcus Octavius, son of Marcus, of the Publilia tribe, soldier, scout from Verona, served for 7 years, lived for 27 years.
EDCS-71200523.summary: This epitaph records the military service of Marcus Octavius, a scout from Verona who died at the age of 27 after serving for 7 years.

EDCS-71200525.translation: Clodia, daughter of Caius, freedwoman, while alive, made this for her sister Gemella, daughter of Caius, freedwoman, while alive.
EDCS-71200525.summary: This epitaph records that Clodia, a freedwoman, made a tomb for herself and her sister Gemella, also a freedwoman.

EDCS-71200526.summary: A simple dedication stating that Alexander is the maker of the object or monument.

EDCS-71200527.translation: Of Quintus Hirrius Phoenicis // Of Quintus Hirrius Phoenicis.
EDCS-71200527.summary: This inscription appears to be a formal declaration of ownership or a dedication by Quintus Hirrius Phoenicis.

EDCS-71200528.translation: Gaius Clodius Rufus, son of Gaius, [was the recipient] // To Gaius Clodius Rufus.
EDCS-71200528.summary: This inscription is likely a dedication to Gaius Clodius Rufus, possibly a tombstone or a dedication of a monument.

EDCS-71200532.translation: Antonia Smyrna, Decimus Antonius Antiochus, Antonia Iucunda.
EDCS-71200532.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing three individuals: Antonia Smyrna, Decimus Antonius Antiochus, and Antonia Iucunda.

EDCS-71200535.translation: Lucius, son of Publius [---]. Lives for Marcus Papius Celsus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-71200535.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription that records a deceased person named Lucius and mentions Marcus Papius Celsus.

EDCS-71200537.translation: To the departed spirits. Successus, a freedman of Caesar.
EDCS-71200537.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Successus, a freedman of Caesar.

EDCS-71200540.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely a funerary dedication, mentioning Marcus Fulvius Eutrapelus.

EDCS-71200542.translation: To Quintus Marcius Stichus, freedman of the deified Titus, to his well-deserving wife Marcia.
EDCS-71200542.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates a monument from Quintus Marcius Stichus to his wife Marcia.

EDCS-71200543.translation: To the departed spirits. For Secundus Fabentius Alexandri, slave, who lived 27 years, 2 months, 22 days. Claudia Amaryllis to her most dutiful son.
EDCS-71200543.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Secundus Fabentius Alexandri, a slave who lived to 27 years, set up by his mother Claudia Amaryllis.

EDCS-71200544.translation: To the good departed spirits. Sotericus made (this) for the well-deserving Apra.
EDCS-71200544.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Sotericus for Apra.

EDCS-71200546.translation: Vibiana Euphrosyne, freedwoman of Gnaeus. Gnaeus Vibianus Menesteus, freedman of Gnaeus. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-71200546.summary: This inscription records the burial plot dimensions for Vibiana Euphrosyne and Gnaeus Vibianus Menesteus, both freedpersons of Gnaeus.

EDCS-71200547.translation: Flavia Iulitta made (this) for her pupil, who lived 18 years.
EDCS-71200547.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Flavia Iulitta for her pupil.

EDCS-71200552.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lucius Hortensius Asclepiades. Hortensia Sperata to her most pious father.
EDCS-71200552.summary: This funerary inscription is from Hortensia Sperata to her father, Lucius Hortensius Asclepiades.

EDCS-71200557.translation: To Euno, slave of Hermet, as a public contractor for Athens.
EDCS-71200557.summary: A dedication to Euno, a slave and contractor, likely a funerary or honorary inscription, dating from the Roman period.

EDCS-71200558.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning the name Publius Caesoneius.

EDCS-71200568.translation: Aulus Sulpicius Primigenius made this for himself and for his wife, Cominia Ianuaris.
EDCS-71200568.summary: A tombstone set up by Aulus Sulpicius Primigenius for himself and his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71200570.translation: Flavia Felicissima made this for her incomparable husband Faventinus, with whom she lived well for 20 years and 7 months, a deserving man.
EDCS-71200570.summary: A tombstone for Faventinus, set up by his wife Flavia Felicissima, recording their 20 years and 7 months of marriage.

EDCS-71200584.translation: Julius Fascanus for his wife Vaetrimaela Concordia, a deserving good woman, who lived for 10 years and [---].
EDCS-71200584.summary: A tombstone for Vaetrimaela Concordia, set up by her husband Julius Fascanus, recording her lifespan and virtue.

EDCS-71200588.translation: Sacred to Neptune. Lucius Virtius Epaphroditus and Lucius Cornelius Hesper, lessees of the salt pans of Rome, dedicated this, under the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus.
EDCS-71200588.summary: A dedication to Neptune by the lessees of Roman salt pans, dating to the consulship of Pontianus and Atilianus.

EDCS-71200602.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Varius Calocerus donated part of his monument to Marcus Ulpius Martialis and his daughter Pulia Secunda, and to their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-71200602.summary: A funerary inscription by Lucius Varius Calocerus, donating part of his monument to his family and their descendants.

EDCS-71200603.translation: Marcus Pompeius Antiochus and Didia Successa set up this statue of Aesculapius by order, under the priest Gaius Cornelius Alypus.
EDCS-71200603.summary: A dedication of a statue of Aesculapius by Marcus Pompeius Antiochus and Didia Successa during the priesthood of Gaius Cornelius Alypus.

EDCS-71200692.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-71200692.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurellius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus Magnus Pius, grandson of the deified Severus Pius, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the third time, consul, father of the country. I have appended the names of the soldiers who served in the four urban cohorts Severianae, numbered X, XXI, XII, XIIII, who performed their military service bravely and piously, granting them the right of intermarriage, at least with single women and their first wives, so that even if they married women of peregrine status, they would have children as if born from two Roman citizens. This was done on the 7th day before the Ides of January, under the consulship of Appius Claudius Iulianus (II) and Gaius Bruttius Crispinus. Cohort XI Urbana Severiana. For Lucius Camelios Severus, son of Lucius, of the Palatina tribe. From Puteoli. This was copied and verified from a bronze tablet which is fixed at Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near the temple of Minerva. Valens, Myron, Hermes, Protas.
EDCS-71200692.summary: An imperial rescript issued by Emperor Severus Alexander in 224 AD, granting certain rights and privileges to soldiers of the urban cohorts, and inscribed on a bronze tablet for public record.

EDCS-71400234.translation: Nuptial (record) with Claudia, daughter of Tiberius Lupercilla, for 200,000 sesterces.
EDCS-71400234.summary: A fragment likely recording a marriage settlement or dowry, mentioning Claudia and a monetary value.

EDCS-71400236.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Hercolanius.

EDCS-71400417.translation: Gaius Geminius Gemellus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, lived 49 years.
EDCS-71400417.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Geminius Gemellus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-71400448.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 16th time, consul for the 3rd time, [---
EDCS-71400448.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, recording his titles and powers.

EDCS-71400542.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pelagius made this tomb for his well-deserving foster father and master, Pinnius Aurelius Benerius, who lived for more or less 50 years.
EDCS-71400542.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Pelagius to his foster father and master, Pinnius Aurelius Benerius, who lived approximately 50 years.

EDCS-71400547.translation: Lucius Vibius Philargurus, freedman of Lucius, Vibia Ammia, freedwoman of Lucius, wife, Vibia Primigenia, freedwoman of Lucius. Lucius Vibius Philargurus the younger placed this for his freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-71400547.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Vibius Philargurus the younger for his freedmen and freedwomen, and also mentions his wife and daughter.

EDCS-71400554.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 14th time, imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of his country. Made at his own expense.
EDCS-71400554.summary: This is an inscription recording the titles and building activities of Emperor Trajan, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-71400556.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelius Sporus for his dearest son Aelius Sporinus, who lived 1 year and 6 months.
EDCS-71400556.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child, Aelius Sporinus, set up by his father, Aelius Sporus.

EDCS-71400557.translation: Aulus Aemilius Primus, freedman of Aulus, made this for himself and for his wife Tatia Antiochis, and for his daughter Tatia Prima, and for Lucius Kaninius Primus, and for Gaius Marcius Auctus.
EDCS-71400557.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Aemilius Primus, recording himself, his wife, daughter, and two other individuals.

EDCS-71400560.translation: To the departed spirits. Blaesia Zosime and Blaesia Eutychis, and Epidia Felicia, wife, made this for Marcus Blaesius Zosimus, who lived 52 years, 3 months, and 12 days, the well-deserving.
EDCS-71400560.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by the wife and daughters for Marcus Blaesius Zosimus, detailing his lifespan.

EDCS-71400561.translation: [---] Favonius(?), son of Marcus, duovir quinquennalis, sacred to Mercury(?).
EDCS-71400561.summary: This is a dedication to Mercury(?), possibly by a magistrate named Favonius, dating from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-71400565.translation: To the departed spirits. To [3]ae Primae, son of [3] Suavis, for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-71400565.summary: A funerary dedication to a mother named Prima, with her son as the dedicator.

EDCS-72000112.translation: In the consulship of Gaius Velleius Paterculus and Marcus Manilius Vopiscus, on the fourth day before the Nones of September. Quintus Iunius Theophilus writes that he has promised to allow Aulus Tetteius Severus to receive all the fruits of his share from the Blandian estate, to use them, to transport them, and to take them out, and that the wine and the stable are to be available and safe until the first Ides of August, and that he may take out, transport, lead away, and auction all his property and livestock; Aulus Tetteius Severus requested that all these things be done and guaranteed; Quintus Iunius Theophilus promised. Done at Herculaneum.
EDCS-72000112.summary: A legal document recording a property lease agreement concerning agricultural produce and wine from the Blandian estate, dated to 77 CE.

EDCS-72000113.translation: Marcus Nonius Fuscus swore by Jupiter, the divine powers, and the Genius of Emperor Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus that the girl Nais is his slave and his property, and that he possesses her, and that she is not bound to him in common, publicly, or privately. Later, Lucius Cominius Primus accepted the girl Nais, who is the subject of this document, for safekeeping by mancipatio for 1 sestertius from Marcus Nonius Fuscus for 600 sesterces, with Numerius Blaesius Saturninus acting as libripens. Done at Herculaneum on the thirteenth day before the Kalends of February, in the consulship of Publius Marius and Lucius Afinius Gallus.
EDCS-72000113.summary: A legal document recording a transfer of ownership of a female slave named Nais, likely related to a debt or a trust arrangement, dated to 67 CE.

EDCS-72200182.translation: Lucius Adriaticus Hermi, Aulus Egrilius Bassus, Sextus Vindius Euplus, Caius Cornelius Chrys, Lucius Faenius Prov, Gnaeus Cornelius Eut, [as] quinquennales, [were elected] by decree of the decurions. Tiberius Claudius Severus Proculus, Caius Aufidius Victorinus, Aufidius Victorinus, [and] Gavius Iorius, [as] quinquennales, [were elected] by decree of the decurions. Lucius Papio Crescens, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Sextus Iulius Asclepiades, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Marcus Vibius Onesimus, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Lucius Calpurnius Fortunatus, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Marcus Pomponius Hilarus, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions, [and] priest. Marcus Iulius Proculus, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Caius Fulvius Tyrannus, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Lucius Satrius Hermes, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Caius Fulvius Pontianus, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Quintus Caelius Hospitalis, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Titus Marcius Chrysostomus, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Caius Calpurnius, son of Caius, Celer, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Marcus Cotusius Antiochus, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Lucius Antius Ianuarius, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Caius Veturius Mercurius, [as] quinquennal, by decree of the decurions. Lucius Pompon, Quintus Caecilius, Marcus Mollicius T, Titus Flavius O, Marcus Ulpius H, Publius Claudius H, Publius Aelius Ve, Sextus Setorius Eu, Lucius Cornelius Sa, Marcus Annaeus A, Publius Aurelius Da, Caius Laecanius Iuc, Caius Laecanius Alex, Marcus Iunius Diog, Marcus Iunius Diog, Lucius Casperius Car, Tiberius Cladius Cornelius Pr, Sextus Carminius Phili, Quintus Aeronius Antio, Sextus Avienius Nic, Lucius Sextus Callitych, Sextus Pontius Agathamer, [as] quinquennales, by decree of the decurions. In the consulship of Titus Flavius Nymph[--] and O[--]nne. [Many names follow, likely quinquennales]. ...[elected] by decree of the decurions. Marcus Acilius Faustinus, Aulus Triarius Rufinus, [consuls]. Caius Iulius Euthichen(o), [elected]. Quintus Turranius Dydimus. Publius C. Publius C. Quintus Quintus Pl. Caius Cur. Marcus Ann. L. Caius Fulvius. Marcus Aemilius. Lucius Fabius. Lucius Pvius. In the reign of Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus and Marcus Oclatinus Adventus, consuls. Lucius Gettius. Titus Flavius. Marcus Clo. Publius Petronius Omy, [as] quinquennales. Publius Claudius Callid. Marcus Terentius Her. Aulus Egrilius Victori. Quintus Vettius Aphrodisias. Publius Aelius Zosimio. In the second consulship of Quintus Aiacius Modestus and Marcus Maecius Probus. Quintus Vettius Aphrodisio, Aulus Livius Stratone, Caius Prastina Alexandro, Aulus Egrilius Victorico, [as] quinquennales. Marcus Sextus. Lucius Osti. Marcus Valerius. Marcus Aemi. EDCS-72200182.summary: A long and complex inscription listing numerous quinquennales, officials elected by the decurions in a Roman municipality, likely Herculaneum, with multiple consular dating formulae and references to emperors from the late 2nd and early 3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-72200194.translation: To Gnaeus Saenius(?) Glaucus, freedman of Gnaeus, the excellent perfumer, and to Glaucus(?), patron of [3]o. Gnaeus Saenius(?) Bassus, freedman of Gnaeus, for himself and his family.
EDCS-72200194.summary: A funerary dedication to a perfumer named Glaucus, with the inscription set up by his freedman and patron.

EDCS-72300006.translation: To Betitia Pullentina, a woman of wonderful chastity and most innocent soul, who lived 46 years, 4 months, and 16 days. Claudius Bassus made this for his incomparable and well-deserving wife. Dedicated on the fourth day before the Nones of September.
EDCS-72300006.summary: A funerary epitaph for Betitia Pullentina, recording her virtues and lifespan, set up by her husband Claudius Bassus, dated to the early imperial period.

EDCS-72300038.summary: A brief inscription identifying Gaius Monius Diocles, possibly on a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-72300042.summary: A dedication to Venidia, daughter of Quintus, possibly on a tomb.

EDCS-72300138.translation: To the departed spirits. To Aulus Egrilius Mali Puilghri(!), son of Aulus, of the Palatine tribe, aedile, elected to perform the sacred rites of Vulcan, and second praetor. Aulus Egrilius Agathopus, sevirus Augustalis, made this for his patron(?) and himself.
EDCS-72300138.summary: A funerary monument for Aulus Egrilius Mali Puilghri, who held the offices of aedile and second praetor, dedicated by Aulus Egrilius Agathopus, a sevirus Augustalis, likely in Ostia or nearby.

EDCS-72300169.translation: [Dedication] to Vibius, son of [3], for his well-deserving wife, Cannelius Proc[--]clarius [---].
EDCS-72300169.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription dedicating a monument to a wife, with her husband Vibius and possibly Cannelius Proc[--]clarius involved.

EDCS-72300198.translation: Marcus Calidius Ingenuus dedicated this, having paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-72300198.summary: This is a dedication from Marcus Calidius Ingenuus, likely a freedman, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-72300206.translation: Gaius Luccius, son of Gaius, freedman.
EDCS-72300206.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription identifying Gaius Luccius, a freedman.

EDCS-72300210.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fronto, a household slave, lived 20 years, 7 months.
EDCS-72300210.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fronto, a slave who died at the age of 20 years and 7 months.

EDCS-72300211.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Maria Zosime lived 23 years.
EDCS-72300211.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maria Zosime, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-72300212.translation: To [the spirits of the departed of] the freedman Mena, son of Caius Vronius.
EDCS-72300212.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Mena, a freedman.

EDCS-72300213.translation: Marcia Alexandria made this for her father Marcius Onesimus, who deserved well, and for her mother.
EDCS-72300213.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Marcia Alexandria for her deserving father, Marcius Onesimus, and her mother.

EDCS-72300218.translation: To the spirits of the departed of Spedia Tych[e]. Gaius Julius...
EDCS-72300218.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Spedia Tych[e], possibly set up by Gaius Julius.

EDCS-72300220.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or tombstone, mentioning Tiberius Claudius Hostirius.

EDCS-72300222.translation: ... freedman of Zenon Numeni[us]... Blastus, freedman of Aulus Nonius son of Aulus...
EDCS-72300222.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning two freedmen, one belonging to Zenon Numeni[us] and the other identified as Blastus.

EDCS-72300225.translation: To Gaius Sempronius Gracchus, son of Titus, of the pollia tribe, and to his mother Gavena, daughter of Titus; and to Quintus Sempronius Gracchus, son of Gaius, of the Falerna tribe, from the will, and to Sempronia Chrysarion, freedwoman of Quintus, by the arbitration of the heirs.
EDCS-72300225.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription detailing the beneficiaries of a will, including parents and a freedwoman, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-72300228.translation: Lucius Urinae(us) Varenus, son of Lucius, [made?] ancestral and paternal [property?]. Lucius Urinae(us) Firmus, son of Lucius, son of Lucius, provided for himself and his own. Lucius Urinae(us) Fronto, son of Lucius, son of Lucius, attended.
EDCS-72300228.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary or property-related marker, possibly detailing familial land ownership and provision for descendants, with at least two generations of Lucius Urinae(us) mentioned. Dating is uncertain without further context.

EDCS-72300245.translation: Valerius Sabinianus [?]. Valerius Eurota [?]. Valerius Aureliol(us) Valerius [?]. Valerius Pistus the centurion. Valerius [?]. Valerius Castor, assistant (cornicularius) [?]. Valerius Mucianus, an actor. Valerius Dionusis(!). Valerius Augustus, the centurion. Valerius DER[---]TES[---]. Valerius Eugenes, the centurion. Valerius Sarmati[o?] [?]. Valerius Sabinianus IERRA Valerius [?] SA[---]. Valerius Victorinus [?]. Valerius Cadmus [?]. Valerius Longus VALC[---]. Valerius Na[---]. Valerius Isidor(us), the centurion. Valerius DVRA, the centurion. Simus [?]. Valerius Mestrianus [?]A Valerius BRIAC[---]MA. Valerius Bassus, an armorer. Valerius Ermogene(s), the centurion. Valerius Victor, an official (ordinatus?). Valerius Eptacentus, the centurion. These names, the conservator (?) have paid off TVS m[---].
EDCS-72300245.summary: This inscription is a fragmented list of names, many of them associated with the Valerius gentile, including military ranks like centurion and roles such as actor and assistant, likely serving as a tombstone or record of individuals associated with a particular group or place. The date is indeterminate due to fragmentation.

EDCS-72300258.summary: This is a brief marker indicating the extent of an inscription or area, attributed to Dionysius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72300259.translation: To the Divine Shades. Cassia Rufilla made this for her well-deserving husband, Sextus Braetius Fidelis.
EDCS-72300259.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her husband, implying a Roman Imperial period date and expressing marital devotion.

EDCS-72300260.translation: To Lucius Allidius Prothymus, freedman of Numida. Chreste, freedwoman, from her own funds, [made?] this monument; it will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-72300260.summary: This epitaph records a monument erected by a freedwoman (Chreste) for a freedman (Lucius Allidius Prothymus), indicating the costs were borne by the dedicator and that the property was not to be inherited, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72300261.translation: [Numerius] Ummidius Molliculus, son of Lucius, [from the Aemilia tribe], aedile, interrex. Lucius Ummidius Molliculus, son of Numerius, [from the Aemilia tribe]. Lucius Ummidius Molliculus, son of Lucius, [from the Aemilia tribe].
EDCS-72300261.summary: This inscription lists members of the Ummidius family, with at least one individual holding the office of aedile and interrex, suggesting a Republican or early Imperial date for this public or familial record.

EDCS-72300266.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph or a dedication naming Gaius Utius and his father, Caius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72300267.translation: Zethus, a house-born slave, Pontianus, son of his freedwoman Apias, lived for 16 years, rests here.
EDCS-72300267.summary: This epitaph records the life and death of Zethus Pontianus, a young slave (verna) who lived for 16 years and is buried here, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72300273.translation: Rusticelia Euhemeria, freedwoman of Aulus, made [this] for herself and for her fellow freedman Aulus Rusticelius Heron.
EDCS-72300273.summary: This epitaph records a monument created by a freedwoman for herself and her fellow freedman, indicating a Roman Imperial period date and a close relationship between the two.

EDCS-72600050.text_edition: \n Vadimonium factum Caio Mario Crescenti in XII Kalendas a Marco Herennio MMduo milia denariorum in foro Augusti ante Dianam Luciferam ad compitum de hora quinta ipsi dari stipulatus est Marcus Herennius Capito spopondit Caius Marius\n
EDCS-72600050.translation: A summons was made to Caius Marius Crescens for the twelfth day before the Kalends of [Month], for two thousand denarii, in the Forum of Augustus, before Diana Lucifera, at the crossroads, from the fifth hour. Marcus Herennius Capito stipulated that it be given to him; Caius Marius promised.
EDCS-72600050.summary: This inscription records a legal summons (vadimonium) issued by Marcus Herennius Capito to Caius Marius Crescens for a debt of 2000 denarii, to be paid at a specific location and time, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72600286.summary: This is a funerary inscription naming Gaius Terentius Maximus, likely a tombstone or monument to him.

EDCS-72600300.summary: This is a simple wish for good health or longevity addressed to a person named Crescens.

EDCS-72600384.translation: From the workshops of Quintus Asinius Marcellus, the tiled work was done by Gaius Nunnidius Fortunatus for Quintus Articuleius Paetinus and Apronianus, when they were consuls.
EDCS-72600384.summary: This inscription records the completion of tiled work by Gaius Nunnidius Fortunatus for Quintus Asinius Marcellus, under the consulship of Quintus Articuleius Paetinus and Apronianus.

EDCS-72800012.translation: Cornificia, freedwoman of Lucius, gave this gift willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-72800012.summary: This is a dedication of a gift, possibly an offering or a part of a building, by Cornificia.

EDCS-72800014.translation: Gaius Plotus Albanus [---] Gaius Mevius Festus [---] in this work
EDCS-72800014.summary: This is a fragmented inscription likely related to a construction project, mentioning Gaius Plotus Albanus and Gaius Mevius Festus.

EDCS-72800015.translation: Marcia, freedwoman of the mulier, 12 feet in front, 6 feet in the field.
EDCS-72800015.summary: This inscription possibly marks a property boundary or burial plot belonging to Marcia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-72800022.summary: This is a dedication of a sacred object or place to the deity Hercules Cefranus.

EDCS-73000085.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, possibly mentioning Aemilianus as the dedicator for his wife.

EDCS-73000137.translation: To Arria Mercurina, his freedwoman and wife, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-73000137.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Arria Mercurina, identified as both a freedwoman and wife, and noting her lifespan.

EDCS-73000140.translation: To the Shades Below. Athenais, to Felix, with his son Secundus, for himself and...
EDCS-73000140.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the underworld deities, naming Athenais, Felix, and Secundus, and indicating it's for themselves and others.

EDCS-73000316.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment mentioning possibly Gaius Curtius Alexand[---], with a significant portion missing.

EDCS-73000353.translation: Ele[---], freedman, son of Quintus, Frat[---], Gem[---]
EDCS-73000353.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription that seems to name a freedman, Ele[---], son of Quintus, with other names also incomplete.

EDCS-73000363.translation: To the departed spirits. To Erotes, the freedman, deservedly honored by Publius Nonius Aelianus.
EDCS-73000363.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Publius Nonius Aelianus to his deserving freedman, Erotes.

EDCS-73000472.translation: Marcus Fulvius Florus lived for 14 years.
EDCS-73000472.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Marcus Fulvius Florus, who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-73000479.translation: Furia Atticilla made this for her best-loved and deserving friends, Furia Corinthias and Furia Secunda.
EDCS-73000479.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Furia Atticilla for two friends, Furia Corinthias and Furia Secunda.

EDCS-73000480.translation: To the departed spirits. Furia Euhemer[---], who lived for 10 years and 3[---] days, was made [this by] Furia Cynegis, her pupil.
EDCS-73000480.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Furia Cynegis to her pupil, Furia Euhemer[---], who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-73100032.translation: To the departed spirits. [---] Hermetianetis, who lived 16 years, 3 [months?] and 4 days. [Erected by] Felix, her husband, to the deservedly honored.
EDCS-73100032.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Hermetianetis at the age of 16 years, and was dedicated by her husband, Felix.

EDCS-73100114.translation: To the departed spirits. Livia Iusta lived for 11 years [---].
EDCS-73100114.summary: This funerary inscription records that Livia Iusta lived for 11 years, with a lacuna for the remaining lifespan.

EDCS-73100155.translation: Marcus Manlius Felix made this for himself, for his daughter Manlia Deutera, for his son Marcus Manlius Successus, and for his daughter Iunia Spicula. Frontage 30 feet, land 25 feet.
EDCS-73100155.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription by Marcus Manlius Felix for his family, specifying plot dimensions.

EDCS-73100191.translation: Aulus Mucius Antiochus, freedman of Aulus; Mucia Tateis, freedwoman of Aulus; Aulus Mucius Antiochus, freedman. Frontage 20 feet, land 25 feet.
EDCS-73100191.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial plot owned by Aulus Mucius Antiochus and Mucia Tateis, both freedpersons of Aulus Mucius.

EDCS-73100192.translation: Aulus Mucius Antiochus, freedman of Aulus; Mucia Tateis, freedwoman of Aulus. Aulus Mucius Antiochus, freedman. Frontage 20 feet, depth 25 feet.
EDCS-73100192.summary: This inscription is a property boundary marker set by Aulus Mucius Antiochus and Mucia Tateis, likely freedpersons of a master named Aulus Mucius.

EDCS-73100193.translation: Aulus Mucius Antiochu(s), freedman of Aulus; Mucia, freedwoman of Aulus. Aulus Mucius Antiochus, freedman. Frontage 20 feet, depth 25 feet.
EDCS-73100193.summary: This inscription is a property boundary marker set by Aulus Mucius Antiochus and Mucia, likely freedpersons, with the name Tateis deliberately erased.

EDCS-73100194.translation: May they live! Aulus Mucius Antioc(hus), freedman of Aulus, patron; Mucia Tateis, freedwoman of Aulus, freedwoman. Aulus Mucius Antiochus, freedman.

EDCS-73100234.translation: Publius Paconius Nicephor, freedman of Publius, son of Spurius, of the Palatine tribe, made this for himself and his family.

EDCS-73100245.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to mention the name Pelagi[---] and likely concerns an individual or individuals.

EDCS-73100313.translation: [---] Quartinus [---] and Afrodisi[---] for Flavia Tycha[---], compatriot[---]

EDCS-73100342.translation: Marcus Saenius Aristo made this for himself and for his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. Frontage 20 feet, depth 25 feet.
EDCS-73100342.summary: This is a property boundary marker, likely part of a larger funerary complex, established by Marcus Saenius Aristo for himself and his freed dependents.

EDCS-73100448.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valeri(a) Fusc[---], daughter of Lucius, who lived 8 years, 10 months [---]
EDCS-73100448.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria, daughter of Lucius, who died at a young age, with the inscription being completed by Valerius Fusc[---].

EDCS-73100472.summary: This inscription, from a columbarium, indicates the presence of two columbaria and possibly three jars, suggesting a funerary context.

EDCS-73100496.translation: [Gaius Volturcius Olympus made this for himself and Volturcia Chosmia, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. Length 20 feet [---] width [---] // Publius Durdenius Acutus, freedman of Publius and his wife, made this for himself and for Durdenia Prima, freedwoman of Publius, his wife, and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. Length 20 feet, width 16 feet.

EDCS-73100601.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary text, mentioning a name.

EDCS-73100852.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Cominia [---]sae made this. Cominius? Asclepidotes, her most dutiful, made [this].
EDCS-73100852.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Cominius Asclepidotes for Cominia [---]sae, likely his wife or a close relative.

EDCS-73100874.translation: Libralis made this for his well-deserving daughter [---]mela.

EDCS-73101032.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, mentioning a son and the body.

EDCS-73101045.translation: For Florentina. / [---] to Fabio Florus / [---] to the son of Q. Fabio Tertullus / [---] and their descendants. / [---] deserving, this pleases the survivors / [---] I lie thus, Florentina, forever. / [---] pious and holy brothers, / [---] and sweet, farewell. / [---] in the Elysian fields. / [---] on the front 3 feet, in the field 20 feet.

EDCS-73400412.translation: Titus Manlius, Titus' freedman, Alexsa (miswritten for Aexa?), daughter of Marcus Labicia. 20 feet on the front, 25 feet in the field.
EDCS-73400412.summary: This is a boundary inscription for a plot of land, indicating ownership by Titus Manlius and Labicia, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-74200630.summary: This inscription likely marks a tomb or property belonging to Quintus Poppaeus Erotis.

EDCS-74200631.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Stlaccius Epitynchanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200632.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Arellius Successus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200633.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Caesius Valentis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200634.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Numerius Herennius Nardus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200635.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Siminius Stephanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200638.summary: This is likely a funerary epitaph for Lucius Melissaeus Castor Augustalis, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74700673.translation: [...]toni, son of Sextus, [...]di, made for himself while alive.
EDCS-74700673.summary: This is a funerary epitaph that records the deceased as [...]tonius, son of Sextus, who made the monument for himself during his lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74700695.translation: Of Lucius Mammius Flaccus(?); Quintus Marcius Hispanus; Tiberius Crassius Firmus; Gaius Clodius Celer(?); Gaius Messenius [---]; Lucius [---]; Marcus Volusius [---]; Marcus Volusius [---]; Publius Vitellius Epagathus; Lucius Cominius Primus

EDCS-74700739.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, Titus Suedius Clemens, tribune, after hearing the cases and making measurements, restored to the public of Pompeii properties possessed by private individuals.

EDCS-74800033.summary: A partial inscription with a name, likely a funerary or dedicatory text.

EDCS-74800035.summary: A simple inscription bearing a personal name, potentially funerary or dedicatory.

EDCS-74800038.translation: Let the mistress of the world, Rome, remember. Let him who wrote, Adamas, remember.
EDCS-74800038.summary: A short inscription in Greek where Adamas asks for remembrance from Rome, possibly a personal dedication or curse.

EDCS-74800066.summary: A simple Latin epitaph stating that Romulus rests here.

EDCS-74800076.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-74800076.translation: To Gnaeus Pompeius Euphrosynus, honored after the decree of a bisellium and a statue was made for him, and also with decurional ornaments by the order of decurions, whose decree is written below. On the Ides of January, in the curia of the Anniana basilica, all were present for the writing. Lucius Pontius Ingenuus and Titus Clodius Felix, IIviri, spoke concerning the decree of decurional ornaments for the worthy Pompeius Euphrosynus, asking what they wished to be done in this matter. Thus they resolved: Since Pompeius Euphrosynus has deserved well of our republic by his generosity, it is pleasing to this order that decurional ornaments be decreed to him, so that he may receive all privileges. In return for the benefits of the most splendid order, although he had previously distributed 50 sesterces to each decurion, and also to their children and to those who were not decurions, and 30 sesterces to each wife, and 20 sesterces to each scribe, and 8 sesterces to each freeborn colonist, and 8 sesterces to each Augustalis, and 4 sesterces to each freedman colonist; again he gave in the second distribution 50 sesterces to each decurion, and 4 sesterces to each freeborn colonist, and Augustalis, and freedman colonist. Under the consulship of Publius Iuventius Celsus and Quintus Julius Balbus. On the 11th day before the Ides of March. Gnaeus Pompeius Euphrosynus, after the construction and assignment to the republic of the temple work and the two distributions made (as written above), placed an image of Serapis weighing 6 pounds of gold in the temple of Serapis and consecrated it. And in order to better satisfy his affections, which he has abundantly shown towards the colony, and also in fulfillment of his promise to give after his death while alive, he provided a triple distribution of milk in the temple of Serapis, as written below: 150 sesterces to each decurion, and 12 sesterces to each freeborn colonist, Augustalis, and freedman colonist. These are the five distributions which he gave either before this day or on this day.
EDCS-74800076.summary: A complex inscription detailing honors bestowed upon Gnaeus Pompeius Euphrosynus by the decurions of a colony, including a bisellium, statue, and decurional ornaments, and his subsequent generous distributions of money and provisions, along with the dedication of a statue of Serapis.

EDCS-74900082.summary: A short inscription indicating that Marcus Aemilius Festibus was the maker of this object, likely a tombstone or a votive offering.

EDCS-74900165.summary: A fragmentary inscription that likely refers to an emperor, possibly Aurelius.

EDCS-74900280.translation: Under the reign of the Emperor Commodus Augustus.
EDCS-74900280.summary: This inscription refers to the reign of Emperor Commodus.

EDCS-74900294.summary: This is a dedication made by Marcus Ennius Glycon.

EDCS-75000100.translation: Alexander lived for 3 years. Primigenia lived for 6 years and 3 months.

EDCS-75000105.translation: Rufus, of the Oufentina tribe, for himself and for his wife Dionysia, and for his stepson Niger, of the Oufentina tribe, by will.
EDCS-75000105.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Rufus for himself and his family, established by his will.

EDCS-75200026.summary: This is a simple dedication or name inscription for Lucius Bruttius Eros.

EDCS-75200359.translation: In the consulship of Satr... and Domitius Messalla, of Gnaeus Terentius Rufus, son of Gnaeus, of the Oufentina tribe.
EDCS-75200359.summary: This inscription likely records a person's name and tribal affiliation within the context of a specific consular dating, possibly from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-75200360.translation: To Munatia Primigenia, from Tiberius Julius Agathangelus.
EDCS-75200360.summary: This is a dedication from Tiberius Julius Agathangelus to Munatia Primigenia.

EDCS-75200361.translation: To the Divine Manes. I was born from nothing, to nothing I will return, and you who read this, hope that you will be thus. To my best husband.

EDCS-75200366.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Ulpius Serenus, freedman of the Emperor, for Tertia and [---]phu, well-deserving.
EDCS-75200366.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Ulpius Serenus, a freedman of the emperor, for two individuals named Tertia and [---]phu.

EDCS-75400236.summary: This inscription is a simple genitive attribution, likely marking ownership or a dedication by Lucius Fabricius Masculus.

EDCS-75400240.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Ilo, prefect of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, procurator of the Great Circus, procurator of the Dacian Circus, procurator of the Twentieth Treasury, prefect of the transport service, prefect of the Alexandrian fleet (?), prefect of the praetorian cavalry wing, tribune of the soldiers of the Seventh Claudian Legion Pia Fidelis, prefect of the Second Cohort of Gauls, prefect of the First Cohort of Bosphorans.
EDCS-75400240.summary: This is a detailed career inscription detailing the extensive military and administrative career of Tiberius Claudius Ilo, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-75500103.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; of Marcus Amullius Epagathus, freedman of Primigenius.
EDCS-75500103.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the deceased Marcus Amullius Epagathus, freedman of Primigenius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-75600039.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; to Aulus Ofillius Aquilinus Vitalis, son of Quintus, of the Fabian tribe, nomenclator, to Put[eol]is(?). He lived 3 years, 3 months, 3 days. Maria Restituta, most unhappy, [to her] most devoted [son(?)]

EDCS-75600058.translation: Gaius Vibius Figulus, son of Numerius, duovir, centurion primipilus.
EDCS-75600058.summary: This inscription records the civic and military career of Gaius Vibius Figulus, likely from a Roman colony, dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-75800043.translation: To Coruncania Chreusis, freedwoman of Marcus, wife of Gaius Fabius Eronus, freedman of Gaius.
EDCS-75800043.summary: This inscription is a dedication, likely funerary, to Coruncania Chreusis, wife of the freedman Gaius Fabius Eronus.

EDCS-75800044.translation: To the parents Caecilius Chila and Eron Anthos; Lucius Caecilius Secundus, freedman (of the imperial family?), Augustalis, made (this) also for himself.
EDCS-75800044.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Caecilius Secundus setting up a monument for his parents, Caecilius Chila and Eron Anthos, and also for himself, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-75800046.translation: [Lucius] Antiochus, freedman of Lucius [Egnatius]; Naevia Epistolium, freedwoman of Numerius; Lucius Egnatius Syneros, freedman of Lucius, lives; for himself and for his patron.

EDCS-75800048.translation: Titus Salvius, son of Titus, of the Teretina tribe.
EDCS-75800048.summary: This inscription is a simple gentilician and tribal notation, likely for an individual of Roman citizenship, possibly a military or administrative record.

EDCS-75800050.translation: To the spirits of the underworld; of Gaius Iulius Licinianus, son of Gaius, of the Teretina tribe; [lacuna]; 5 months, 23 days.
EDCS-75800050.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Gaius Iulius Licinianus, including his age at death, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-75800051.translation: Under the arbitration of Philargyrus, freedman.
EDCS-75800051.summary: This inscription likely records an arbitration award, possibly related to property or a legal dispute, overseen by a freedman named Philargyrus.

EDCS-75800063.translation: [Name], son of Marcus, of the Teretina tribe; [Name], son of Gaius, of the Teretina tribe; [Name].
EDCS-75800063.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment listing individuals with their patronymic and tribal affiliation, likely part of a longer record such as a census list or property ownership document.

EDCS-75800070.translation: To [Name], wife; to Marcus Epidius Bassus, son of Marcus, of the Teretina tribe; to his daughter Epidia Polla, his granddaughter, who lived 14 years; to Marcus Epidius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Teretina tribe; to his slave Secunda, who lived [X] years; for himself and his family.
EDCS-75800070.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating multiple family members, including a wife, son, granddaughter, and a slave, with their ages at death and intended for the deceased and their descendants.

EDCS-75800084.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Orfania Prima, his wife, made this for Gaius Pontius Aprilis, who lived 54 years, a deserving husband.
EDCS-75800084.summary: This is a tombstone erected by a wife, Orfania Prima, for her deceased husband, Gaius Pontius Aprilis, noting his age at death and her devotion.

EDCS-75800139.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Poppaeus Euhemer made this for himself, his freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-75800139.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Gaius Poppaeus Euhemer for himself and his household, including freed slaves and their future generations.

EDCS-76000147.translation: Publicia Procula, daughter of Spurius, midwife and also ‘obstetrix’ (a female healer or midwife), made this for herself, her family, her freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-76000147.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Publicia Procula, identified as a midwife, for herself and her household, including her family and freed slaves.

EDCS-76000183.translation: Suavis Nymphica Successa, daughter of Publius; Lydia, daughter of Tiberius, send greetings.
EDCS-76000183.summary: This is a greeting or a dedication from two women, Suavis Nymphica Successa and Lydia, possibly to each other or as a general salutation.

EDCS-76000184.summary: This very brief inscription likely marks a dwelling or a grave, identifying a 'handsome man' who resided or is buried there.

EDCS-76200237.translation: Lucius Ninnius Rufus, son of Lucius, gave his vow freely and deservedly to Jupiter.
EDCS-76200237.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Lucius Ninnius Rufus to the god Jupiter, acknowledging a vow fulfilled.

EDCS-76200246.translation: To the departed spirits. For Sextus Patulcius, son of Sextus, from Claudia Pio, centurion of the Legio VII Gemina Felix, chosen into the order of decurions, twice chosen into the order of decurions, as praetor in Cumae.
