EDCS-76300081.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name on a tomb, for Lucius Caecilius Aphrodisius.

EDCS-76300082.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name on a tomb, for Tiberius Claudius Amphion.

EDCS-76300103.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name on a tomb, for Caius Sulpicius Rufus.

EDCS-76300104.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name on a tomb, for Quintus Nolanius Primus.

EDCS-76300105.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a name on a tomb, for Quintus Mestrius Maximus.

EDCS-76400072.translation: Polla and Tertulla, daughters of Gnaeus Luccius, with their own money.
EDCS-76400072.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the contribution by Polla and Tertulla, daughters of Gnaeus Luccius, made with their own funds.

EDCS-76400073.translation: Polla and Tertulla, daughters of Gnaeus Luccius, with their own money.
EDCS-76400073.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the contribution by Polla and Tertulla, daughters of Gnaeus Luccius, made with their own funds.

EDCS-76400074.translation: Polla and Tertulla, daughters of Gnaeus Luccius, with their own money.
EDCS-76400074.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the contribution by Polla and Tertulla, daughters of Gnaeus Luccius, made with their own funds.

EDCS-76400096.translation: To the Chthonic Gods. Claudius Erotion and Claudia Hipponoe made [this] for Claudius Gorgos, their brother and kinsman, [who lived] 30 years.
EDCS-76400096.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Chthonic Gods by Claudius Erotion and Claudia Hipponoe for their brother Claudius Gorgos, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-76400150.translation: Marcus Petacius Dasius, freedman of Marcus, [for] Marcus Petacius Severus, son of Marcus, [and] for Petacia Vitalis, freedwoman of Marcus, freedwoman.
EDCS-76400150.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Petacius Dasius for Marcus Petacius Severus and Petacia Vitalis, documenting familial relationships within the Petacius family.

EDCS-76500009.translation: Maxima Lepidia made this ossuary for herself and her family.
EDCS-76500009.summary: This is an epitaph recording the construction of an ossuary by Maxima Lepidia for her family.

EDCS-76500010.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Mulvius Protus made this for the well-deserving Publius Octanius Ianuarius.
EDCS-76500010.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from a tomb, records Gaius Mulvius Protus dedicating a monument to Publius Octanius Ianuarius.

EDCS-76500011.translation: To her well-deserving son, Italus, who lived 24 years.
EDCS-76500011.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription commemorating a son named Italus.

EDCS-76500012.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Leopardus.
EDCS-76500012.summary: A very brief funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated to Leopardus.

EDCS-76500013.translation: Ambrosia, freedwoman of Claudius Germanicus, made this for Iason, her darling.
EDCS-76500013.summary: This epitaph records Ambrosia, a freedwoman, dedicating a monument to her beloved Iason.

EDCS-76500014.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Felix made this for his most pious brother Italicus, who lived 26 years and 6 months.
EDCS-76500014.summary: This funerary inscription records Titus Flavius Felix dedicating a monument to his brother Italicus.

EDCS-76500015.summary: A very brief inscription, likely a tombstone, bearing the name Publius Olius Buccio.

EDCS-76500016.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cypaerus [dedicated this] to Ianuarius [and] to Claudius [his] friend.
EDCS-76500016.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription records Cypaerus dedicating a monument to Ianuarius and Claudius.

EDCS-76500017.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lappia Felicitas, [dedicated by] Aulus Lappius Ephebus, her son.
EDCS-76500017.summary: This funerary inscription records Aulus Lappius Ephebus dedicating a monument to his mother, Lappia Felicitas.

EDCS-76500018.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Kalotyche, her most pious son. Anapausis, his mother, made this.
EDCS-76500018.summary: This epitaph records Anapausis, the mother, dedicating a monument to her pious son, Kalotyche.

EDCS-76500019.translation: To Felici and Agathopus, Erastus the vicar.
EDCS-76500019.summary: This is a dedication by Erastus, a vicar, to Felici and Agathopus, likely deities or freedmen, and is dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-76500020.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Felicion, a kind father, from his daughter Iovina.
EDCS-76500020.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felicion, set up by his daughter Iovina, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-76500055.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Porcia Maximmilla, a most sweet infant, who lived 3 years, 4 months, and 16 days.
EDCS-76500055.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Porcia Maximmilla, an infant, detailing her lifespan and dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-76600005.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or ownership mark, for Caius Poppaeus Idrius, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-76600120.translation: In the consulship of Aulus Licinius Nerva Silius and Marcus Vestinus Atticus, on the day before the Kalends of February. Lucius Cominius Primus wrote that I owe Aulus Lucius (?) Apronianus 3000 sesterces. Done on the Kalends of December. I received the sum from Lucius Patulcius Sabinus for the price of wine, which he sold to him. Done at Herculaneum. Of Lucius Cominius Primus, of Quintus Junius Paratus, of Marcus Nonius Primus, of Lucius Cominius Primus.
EDCS-76600120.summary: This is a legal and financial document from Herculaneum, recording a debt and its repayment, dated to the consulship of Licinius Nerva Silius and Vestinus Atticus (likely AD 59 or AD 86), with a list of witnesses.

EDCS-76600175.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or ownership mark, for Aulus Vettius Conviva, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-76600176.translation: Lucius Aulius [---]. Aulia, freedwoman of Lucius [---]. Lucius Aulius [---].
EDCS-76600176.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record names, possibly related to a family or a group, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-76600269.translation: Lucius Caelsius Bassus, son of Marcus [---].
EDCS-76600269.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that records the name and filiation of Lucius Caelsius Bassus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-76600273.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vibius Capitolinus lived 8 years and 21 months. Vibius Philoponus, the father, and Doris, the mother, made this for their most devoted son.
EDCS-76600273.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibius Capitolinus, erected by his parents Vibius Philoponus and Doris, detailing his age and dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-76600278.translation: To Juno, Sextus Leucomius Acontius dedicated two silver peacocks with marble bases, his vow fulfilled.
EDCS-76600278.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Juno, made by Sextus Leucomius Acontius, recording the offering of two silver peacocks with marble bases, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-76600285.translation: To Quintus Veilius Frontonis, son of Quintus, and to Publius Veilius Maxsimus, son of Quintus, sons of Quintus Veilius Erotis, freedman of Publius Quintus, and of Veilia Hilara, freedwoman of Publius Quintus; [space for 3 characters] 10 feet.
EDCS-76600285.summary: This is a boundary inscription marking a property, likely set up by the freedmen Erotis and Hilara for their sons Frontonis and Maxsimus, possibly indicating land division or inheritance.

EDCS-76600288.translation: Of Gnaeus Ampuleius Tertus, son of Gnaeus, [from the] Teretina tribe: 12 feet in front, 16 feet in the field.
EDCS-76600288.summary: This inscription likely denotes the dimensions of a property belonging to Gnaeus Ampuleius Tertus, possibly for land registration or a sale.

EDCS-76600289.translation: Of Gnaeus Ampuleius Tertus, son of Gnaeus, [from the] Teretina tribe: 12 feet in front, 16 feet in the field.
EDCS-76600289.summary: This inscription likely denotes the dimensions of a property belonging to Gnaeus Ampuleius Tertus, possibly for land registration or a sale.

EDCS-76600316.translation: [He] built this temple to [you], Sancta(?), from the ground up; priests(?) Publius Acutius Primus and Septimius Eu[---] [---]
EDCS-76600316.summary: This is a dedication inscription for a temple, potentially to a goddess named Sancta, built by priests including Publius Acutius Primus and Septimius Eu[---].

EDCS-76600317.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that a priest named Sabinus placed the monument, though the identity of the deceased is lost.

EDCS-76700002.translation: To the departed spirits of Alia Soterias.
EDCS-76700002.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Alia Soterias.

EDCS-76700007.translation: To Felicitas, who lived 2 years, 12 months, 1 day. Eutychianus and Caliste, parents, made [this] for their most sweet daughter. Farewell, Dulcis.
EDCS-76700007.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Felicitas, set up by her parents Eutychianus and Caliste, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76700010.translation: To the departed spirits. Attia Tryfosa made [this] for her deserving husband, Gaius Larcius Felix.
EDCS-76700010.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Attia Tryfosa honors her well-deserving husband, Gaius Larcius Felix.

EDCS-76700014.translation: To Marcus Mollic[---], [who lived] 9 months. His father [---]lus made [this].
EDCS-76700014.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Marcus Mollic[---], set up by his father, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76700016.translation: To the departed spirits. Sextus Publicius Cerdon, son of Sextus Publicius, who lived 7 years and 20 days. Sextus Publicius [---] father [made this].
EDCS-76700016.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Publicius Cerdon, who died at the age of 7, erected by his father Sextus Publicius.

EDCS-76700020.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Valeria Bassilia made this for her deserving husband, Gaius Julius Martialis, with whom she lived for 30 years.
EDCS-76700020.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76700032.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying a freedwoman, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76700067.translation: Burial place of Marcus Laelius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Falernian tribe.
EDCS-76700067.summary: This inscription marks the burial plot of Marcus Laelius Maximus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76700087.translation: Quarta, freedwoman of Clodia, wife of Quintus; Phileros, freedman of Lucius; Arria, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-76700087.summary: This is a funerary epitaph listing several individuals, likely freedpeople and their patrons or family members, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76800060.summary: This inscription likely designates a public space or building belonging to the citizens of Pompeii, possibly dating from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-76800061.translation: Of Marcus Spurius, son of Marcus.
EDCS-76800061.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying Marcus Spurius, son of Marcus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76800063.translation: Publius Tintirius Adiutor, freedman of Publius, and for his daughter Tintiria Festa, who lived 7 years, and for himself and his wife Pontia Hedymalenus and his own.
EDCS-76800063.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial arrangements and family members of Publius Tintirius Adiutor, including his young daughter and wife, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76900002.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription with illegible portions, making its purpose and dating uncertain.

EDCS-76900097.translation: of Ampliatus, one year old, and 3 months.
EDCS-76900097.summary: This inscription records the age of a deceased child, Ampliatus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76900109.translation: To Hedia, [his/her] wife, and [his/her] own people.
EDCS-76900109.summary: This inscription is a dedication from an unknown individual to his wife Hedia and family, likely from an epitaph.

EDCS-76900110.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Iunia Paula made this for Lucius Junius [---]aso, [her] father, who deserved well.
EDCS-76900110.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Iunia Paula for her well-deserving father, Lucius Junius [---]aso.

EDCS-77200001.translation: Held at Herculaneum on the eighth day before the Ides of June, when Marcus Valerius Corvinus and Gaius Fonteius Agrippa were consuls.
EDCS-77200001.summary: This inscription records a legal or administrative act dated by the consuls Marcus Valerius Corvinus and Gaius Fonteius Agrippa in Herculaneum.

EDCS-77700097.summary: This is a simple identification of a woman, Fufedia, and her father Minatus, likely part of a funerary context.

EDCS-77700098.translation: To Quintus Valerius Hermetus, freedman of Quintus, [and to ---] Fortunatianus.
EDCS-77700098.summary: This incomplete inscription appears to be a dedication or funerary text honoring Quintus Valerius Hermetus and possibly another individual named Fortunatianus.

EDCS-77700128.translation: To Avidius Secunda, wife of Prosius Bassa, daughter of Statilius Maximus, consular augur, [and daughter] of Titus Avidus Quietus, consular augur.
EDCS-77700128.summary: This is a dedication on a tomb to Avidius Secunda, the wife of Prosius Bassa and daughter of two consular augurs, Statilius Maximus and Titus Avidus Quietus.

EDCS-78800018.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius? D[...]
EDCS-78800018.summary: This is an incomplete funerary dedication to a person whose name begins with 'D'.

EDCS-78800049.translation: Thallus, son of Asellinus, son of Pro[---].
EDCS-78800049.summary: This inscription likely identifies an individual named Thallus, son of Asellinus, and provides a partial patronymic or gentilicium.

EDCS-78800050.summary: This is a dedication, possibly funerary, to an individual named Gaius Sittius Magnus.

EDCS-78800061.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Florius Florentinus, Roman knight, son of Lucius of the Palatina tribe. Lucius Florius Euprepes, his freedman, [set this up] for his deserving patron.
EDCS-78800061.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Lucius Florius Euprepes, a freedman, to his patron Lucius Florius Florentinus, a Roman knight.

EDCS-78800081.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Porcia Cartine made this for her well-deserving son, Lucius Porcius Saturninus, who lived for 15 years, 1 month, and 7 days.
EDCS-78800081.summary: A funerary dedication made by a mother for her deceased son.

EDCS-78800082.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Floria Secundilla made this for her well-deserving Aulus Tari Hermes, who lived for 55 years.
EDCS-78800082.summary: A funerary dedication made by Floria Secundilla for her deceased partner or relative, Aulus Tari Hermes.

EDCS-78800083.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lollia Samerais, an extremely unhappy mother, made this for Lollia Genialis.
EDCS-78800083.summary: A funerary dedication made by a mother for her deceased daughter, Lollia Genialis.

EDCS-78800084.translation: Lucius Poblicius Saturninus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Juno Regina.
EDCS-78800084.summary: A votive dedication by Lucius Poblicius Saturninus to the goddess Juno Regina.

EDCS-78800085.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---] Plotia [---]
EDCS-78800085.summary: An incomplete funerary dedication for Plotia.

EDCS-78800086.translation: Peticia Quinta, daughter of Spurius, [dedicated this] for herself, and for Quintus Peticus Hespero, her dearest freedman and slave-born, who deserved well of her, and for Tiberius Claudius Phaedrus, her most faithful friend, and for my freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, all.
EDCS-78800086.summary: A funerary dedication by Peticia Quinta for herself, her freedman, a friend, and her other slaves and their descendants.

EDCS-78800087.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Annius Apollonius [dedicated this] for his well-deserving Pomponia Iusta.
EDCS-78800087.summary: A funerary dedication by Annius Apollonius for Pomponia Iusta.

EDCS-78800088.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Tarcius Vitalio, her brother, and Gnaeus Varius Crescens made this for their incomparable and well-deserving Porcia Helpidi, who lived for 38 years, 3 months, and 4 days.
EDCS-78800088.summary: A funerary dedication made by a brother and a family friend for Porcia Helpidi.

EDCS-78800089.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vergilia Euplia [made this] for her most excellent mother, Lucceia Hora.
EDCS-78800089.summary: A funerary dedication made by Vergilia Euplia for her deceased mother, Lucceia Hora.

EDCS-78800090.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Satria Epicharis made this for her well-deserving Antoninus, who lived for 12 years.
EDCS-78800090.summary: A funerary dedication made by Satria Epicharis for her slave-born boy, Antoninus.

EDCS-78800096.translation: To the departed spirits. Victorina made this for Marcus Valerius Myrismo, her deserving freedman.
EDCS-78800096.summary: A funerary dedication by a patron to a deserving freedman.

EDCS-78800097.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Turranius Barsemas made this for his well-deserving wife, Iunia Cithegilla, who lived 30+ years, 6 months, and 15+ days.
EDCS-78800097.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his well-deserving wife, including her age at death.

EDCS-78800098.translation: To the departed spirits. Demetrius, her father, made this for Antistia Demetria, who lived 2 years, 4 months, and 17 days, his well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-78800098.summary: A funerary dedication by a father for his well-deserving daughter, including her age at death.

EDCS-78800099.translation: To the departed spirits. His sons made this for Gaius Fisio Blasto, their father, who lived 83 years. Fisius Bitalio and Fisius Blastus.
EDCS-78800099.summary: A funerary dedication by sons for their father, including his age at death.

EDCS-78800100.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Maximus [made this] for Fulvia Epimelia, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-78800100.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his well-deserving wife.

EDCS-78800101.summary: A tombstone recording the age of a deceased individual.

EDCS-78800102.translation: To the departed spirits. Her parents placed this for Ianuaria, who lived 11 months and 7 days.
EDCS-78800102.summary: A funerary dedication by parents for their child, including her age at death.

EDCS-78800103.translation: Marcus Verrius Protoctetus [son of Marcus] for himself and for Marcus Verrius Aegialus, his most excellent patron, and for Verria Constantia, and for their freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-78800103.summary: A funerary dedication for the dedicator, his patron, his wife, and their freedmen and descendants.

EDCS-78800107.translation: To the departed spirits. Valerius Saturnius, a soldier of the praetorian fleet at Misenum, made this for his well-deserving Iulia Serena.
EDCS-78800107.summary: A funerary dedication by a soldier for his wife.

EDCS-78800108.translation: To the departed spirits. [Someone] for [Valens] Iulius Valens, who lived [about] 30+ years, 1 month, and [several] days.
EDCS-78800108.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication recording the name and partial age of the deceased.

EDCS-78800110.translation: To the departed spirits. Parents made this for their well-deserving son, Titus Hordionius Synetus, who lived for 3 years, 3 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-78800110.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young child, recording his age and dedication by his parents.

EDCS-78800111.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julia Crescens, likely a dedication by a family member.

EDCS-78800113.translation: To the departed spirits. Frunita, who lived for 20 years, 4 months, and 19 days. Her father, Frunitus, for his well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-78800113.summary: A funerary epitaph for Frunita, detailing her age and dedicated by her father.

EDCS-78800114.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Paccius Artema made this for his wife, Marcia Pontia, who lived for 85 years and 5 months, well-deserving.
EDCS-78800114.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcia Pontia, dedicated by her husband and recording her age.

EDCS-78800115.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Valerius Narcissus, Faustion, his excellent fellow freedman.
EDCS-78800115.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Marcus Valerius Narcissus by his fellow freedman Faustion.

EDCS-78800116.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Attius He[---], who lived [---]
EDCS-78800116.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph mentioning Caius Attius He[---] and his lifespan.

EDCS-78800118.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Aurelius Zoccati, who lived for 37 years, 9 months, and 28 days. Aurelia Massa for her incomparable husband.
EDCS-78800118.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Zoccati, recording his age and dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-78800119.summary: An incomplete inscription possibly mentioning Flavius Tyrannus.

EDCS-78800121.translation: Gaius Datius Clarinu made this for himself while alive, and for his family, freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-78800121.summary: A self-dedicated tomb inscription, intended for the owner, family, and freed dependents.

EDCS-78800146.summary: An incomplete inscription possibly referring to Matidia Augusta.

EDCS-79000001.summary: This is a vulgar graffito possibly naming an individual and insulting them.

EDCS-79000028.translation: To Maxentius, the most brave and pious lord and our prince, to Maximianus, the most noble Caesar, Manilius Rusticianus, vir perfectissimus, prefect of the grain supply, curator of the colony of Ostia, most devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-79000028.summary: This is a dedication to the emperors Maxentius and Maximianus, likely erected by Manilius Rusticianus, prefect of the grain supply and curator of Ostia.

EDCS-79000051.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication to Mars, initiated by Publius Mucius.

EDCS-79000057.translation: To Luccilius Longus, son of Publius, of the Aniensis tribe, augur.
EDCS-79000057.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication for Luccilius Longus, an augur.

EDCS-79000058.summary: This inscription, possibly a formal record, names Marcus Baebius as dictator, with a correction.

EDCS-79100162.translation: To Aulus Cavius Squilla Gallicanus, to Sextus Carminius Vetus.
EDCS-79100162.summary: This appears to be a dedication or listing of names, possibly for two individuals.

EDCS-79100169.translation: To the departed spirits of Claudia Chaerusa, daughter of Tiberius.
EDCS-79100169.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Claudia Chaerusa.

EDCS-79100171.translation: To Julia Severa, an incomparable mother. Quintus Lucius Phaeder.
EDCS-79100171.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Julia Severa, set up by Quintus Lucius Phaeder.

EDCS-79100172.translation: Aulus Pantuleius Pertica, son of Aulus, is alive. Racilia Philetaera, daughter of Marcus, wife, has died. Aulus Pantuleius Atilius, son of Aulus, is alive. In the front 30 feet, in the field 40 feet.
EDCS-79100172.summary: This inscription records the living status of two individuals and the death of one, likely concerning property boundaries.

EDCS-79200073.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly dedicating something to Decimus Lucretianus, with Octavia mentioned.

EDCS-79200074.translation: To the departed spirits. Sagaris, the actor. Cornelius Dexter [erected this].
EDCS-79200074.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sagaris, an actor, by Cornelius Dexter.

EDCS-79200092.summary: This inscription likely records the names of two individuals, Lucius Tettius and Samia.

EDCS-79200106.translation: [3] Anni(?), son of Lucius, freedman of [3].
EDCS-79200106.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary dedication, mentioning individuals with Roman names and freedman status.

EDCS-79200111.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the name of Gaius Claudius Andronicus.

EDCS-79700008.summary: This inscription simply records the name Gaius Valerius Mus.

EDCS-79700011.translation: Lucius Novius Sariander, freedman of Lucius, was given to death on the 6th day before the Ides of September, during the consulship of Quintus Marcius Rufus and Lucius Nonius Asprenas.
EDCS-79700011.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Lucius Novius Sariander, a freedman, with a precise date in the consulship of Marcius Rufus and Nonius Asprenas.

EDCS-79700032.translation: [Erected] as Augustalis in Puteoli, by decree of the decurions, he made [this] with his own money.
EDCS-79700032.summary: This inscription records a public building or monument funded by an Augustalis in Puteoli, enacted by decree of the local council.

EDCS-79700033.translation: [To?] Eninius Carito, soldier from the praetorian fleet at Misenum, in the centuria of Cassius Celer. He lived 33 years. Perennia Charitas [to her?] deserving husband. [Frontage] 12 feet, [area] 12 feet.
EDCS-79700033.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Eninius Carito, a soldier of the Misenum fleet, erected by his wife Perennia Charitas, detailing his age, unit, and the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-79700034.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Frontinius Severus, of Corsican origin, lived 40 years, served 23 years. Lucius Severius Frontinus, his son, [erected this] to his deserving father.
EDCS-79700034.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Frontinius Severus, a Corsican who served in the army, erected by his son.

EDCS-79700035.translation: To the departed spirits. Of Gnaeus Domitius Onesimus. Domitia Arsinoe [erected this] to her deserving wife.
EDCS-79700035.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gnaeus Domitius Onesimus by his wife Domitia Arsinoe.

EDCS-79700036.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gnaeus Domitius Zenobius, son of Gnaeus Domitius Euhelpistus, [erected this] for his father, well deserving.
EDCS-79700036.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a son to his deserving father.

EDCS-79700059.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription that possibly records the death of a person named Anteros.

EDCS-79700061.summary: This is a dedication to Aulus Trebius, who held the office of aedile.

EDCS-79700062.summary: This is a short, informal inscription, possibly a funerary or a playful message, including farewells and instructions to drink.

EDCS-79700065.translation: Successus asks for Ruf us to be made IIvir.
EDCS-79700065.summary: This is a request inscription, likely related to an election or appointment, where Successus supports Ruf us for the office of IIvir.

EDCS-79700066.summary: This is a dedication to Modestus, who held the office of aedile.

EDCS-79700070.translation: To Tudicia, freedwoman of Marcus Crotinus, and to her descendants, Gaius Sacratorius Cinna [erected this] for his wife and his own family.
EDCS-79700070.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a husband to his wife and their descendants.

EDCS-79700071.translation: Hetaerus, son of Rarius Pompeius, lived 15 years.
EDCS-79700071.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the life and age of a young person named Hetaerus.

EDCS-79700072.translation: Of Gaius Grattilius Speratus, of Marcus Clodius Magnus, son of Marcus, of Munatius, of Gaius Julius Nymphodotus, of Gaius Grattilius Chryseros, [servants] of Gaius Grattilius Speratus.
EDCS-79700072.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription listing several individuals, possibly members of a household or a collegium, associated with Gaius Grattilius Speratus.

EDCS-79700105.translation: Tegeticula asks for Rufus to be made IIvir.
EDCS-79700105.summary: This is a request inscription, likely related to an election or appointment, where Tegeticula supports Rufus for the office of IIvir.

EDCS-79700106.translation: Equitius asks that you make Lucius Ceius a duovir and Helvius Sabinus an aedile.
EDCS-79700106.summary: This is a votive request urging citizens to elect Lucius Ceius as duovir and Helvius Sabinus as aedile.

EDCS-79700109.translation: Petronia eagerly asks that you make Lucius Ceius Secundus a duovir with judicial authority.
EDCS-79700109.summary: This is a votive request urging citizens to elect Lucius Ceius Secundus as duovir with judicial authority.

EDCS-79700111.translation: Hyp[sae]us asks that you make Rufus a duovir, a good man worthy of public office.
EDCS-79700111.summary: This is a votive request urging citizens to elect Rufus as a duovir, praising his character.

EDCS-79800014.summary: This is a simple name inscription, possibly identifying a person or owner.

EDCS-79800080.summary: This is a short funerary or farewell inscription to a person named Sabine.

EDCS-79800108.summary: This inscription contains a possible personal name, Eros, and an abbreviation of uncertain meaning.

EDCS-79800112.summary: This inscription is an insult, labeling Valerius as homosexual.

EDCS-79800116.summary: This is a simple name inscription, identifying Gaius Julius Felix.

EDCS-79800122.summary: This inscription contains the Latin name Iucundus, followed by its Greek transliteration.

EDCS-79800129.translation: Mustelica asserts that she will fornicate.
EDCS-79800129.summary: This inscription is a vulgar and assertive statement by Mustelica regarding sexual activity.

EDCS-79800133.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a graffito, naming a slave named Fraudans.

EDCS-79800135.translation: Felicio, son of Gallianus, to his slaves, many greetings, perpetual and abundant.
EDCS-79800135.summary: This is a greeting from Felicio, son of Gallianus, to his slaves, likely from the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-79800137.summary: This inscription identifies a person named Trucca, possibly a slave or a young woman, noted as 'verna' (native-born).

EDCS-79800151.translation: Mecius, get out of the way! Omen.
EDCS-79800151.summary: This appears to be a rude or defiant graffito, possibly a warning to move aside, possibly with an omen or curse.

EDCS-79800158.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription that begins with a woman's name, Sebidia, and possibly refers to a soldier ('milites').

EDCS-79800160.summary: This inscription simply names two individuals, Auctus and Actius, likely a dedication or a record of association.

EDCS-79800170.summary: This inscription records two names, Modestus and Euodus, with Modestus acting as a witness, possibly for a legal transaction or agreement.

EDCS-79800218.summary: This inscription provides the names of two individuals, Pamphilus and his father Phidotius, likely a dedication or a familial record.

EDCS-79800257.translation: Bound by life, bound by hand, bound by soul, bound by hair, bound by inner parts, I will place this boundary stone; may Maximus' flesh, his kidneys, his liver, his old age, and his sharp sight perish, I pray, may Maximus eat flesh, kidneys, and liver.
EDCS-79800257.summary: This is a magical curse inscription, likely a boundary marker, invoking a curse upon someone named Maximus.

EDCS-79800260.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that names two individuals, Ampliatus and Auctus, likely part of a dedication or record.

EDCS-79800263.summary: A short epitaph or personal note wishing Rusticus well.

EDCS-79800264.summary: An epitaph or personal note describing Mnesteres as careful.

EDCS-79800281.summary: A personal inscription expressing love for Beneta by Atimetus.

EDCS-79800332.summary: A brief epitaph or personal message wishing Sepiricus well.

EDCS-79800339.summary: A list of three female names, likely dancers or muses, possibly an epitaph or dedication.

EDCS-79800344.summary: A personal statement or insult directed at someone named Columbus.

EDCS-79800355.translation: I love Volumnius most of all.
EDCS-79800355.summary: A personal inscription expressing strong affection for Volumnius.

EDCS-79800361.summary: A cryptic inscription possibly recording a quantity or count associated with Vesubius.

EDCS-79800375.summary: A dedication or inscription for Proculus, mentioning him as unique or singular.

EDCS-79800384.summary: An inscription that likely records an achievement or action performed by Repentinus.

EDCS-80000234.translation: I ask you, men, to make Lucius Ceius Secundus duovir and Sabinus aedile, a good man.
EDCS-80000234.summary: This is a political appeal urging voters to elect Lucius Ceius Secundus as duovir and Sabinus as aedile.

EDCS-80000235.translation: Popidius Felicio, his client, asks you to make Lucius Ceius Secundus duovir.
EDCS-80000235.summary: This is a political appeal from a client, Popidius Felicio, urging voters to elect Lucius Ceius Secundus as duovir.

EDCS-80000237.translation: Astylus asks you to make Ceius duovir.
EDCS-80000237.summary: This is a political appeal from Astylus urging voters to elect Ceius as duovir.

EDCS-80000238.summary: This is a dedication by Astylus, likely for Helvium Sabinum, possibly a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-80000241.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a personal note or epitaph, containing a greeting and farewell.

EDCS-80000243.translation: Lucius Caecilius Capella, duovir for dispensing justice.
EDCS-80000243.summary: This inscription records the title of Lucius Caecilius Capella as duovir iure dicundo.

EDCS-80000249.translation: Orphaeus makes Lucius Ceius Secundus aedile.
EDCS-80000249.summary: This is a political appeal from Orphaeus urging voters to elect Lucius Ceius Secundus as aedile.

EDCS-80000250.translation: I ask you, men, to make Lucius Ceius Secundus duovir. Secundanus asks.
EDCS-80000250.summary: This is a political appeal from Secundanus urging voters to elect Lucius Ceius Secundus as duovir.

EDCS-80000251.translation: I ask you, men, to make Lucius Ceius Secundus duovir.
EDCS-80000251.summary: This is a political appeal urging voters to elect Lucius Ceius Secundus as duovir.

EDCS-80000254.translation: ... Lucius Petilius Flaccus asks you to make [him] duovir for dispensing justice. Marcus O[...]
EDCS-80000254.summary: This is a fragmentary political appeal from Lucius Petilius Flaccus seeking election as duovir iure dicundo.

EDCS-80000255.summary: This is an incomplete funerary or dedicatory inscription mentioning Vettius and Gaius Frontonius, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-80000256.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary or dedicatory, honoring Lucius Caecilius.

EDCS-80000257.translation: To Suettius and Epidius, the duovirs.
EDCS-80000257.summary: This inscription records the dedication to Suettius and Epidius, who held the office of duovir, likely from the republican or early imperial period.

EDCS-80000258.translation: Vote for Gnaeus Helvius Sabinus as aedile, a good man worthy of the state.
EDCS-80000258.summary: This is a political recommendation urging voters to elect Gnaeus Helvius Sabinus as aedile, likely from the late republican or early imperial period.

EDCS-80100001.translation: To the departed spirits. Catilia Attia made this for her most beloved and well-deserving husband, Titus Clodius Gillianus.
EDCS-80100001.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Titus Clodius Gillianus by his wife Catilia Attia, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-80100002.translation: Lives. Marcus Clodius, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe. In the front, 12 feet; in the rear, 14 feet.
EDCS-80100002.summary: This is a boundary inscription, possibly for a tomb or land plot, specifying dimensions and mentioning Marcus Clodius of the Oufentina tribe.

EDCS-80200055.translation: Spatali, servant of Servius Cornelii Zosimus.
EDCS-80200055.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Spatali, who was the servant of Servius Cornelii Zosimus, likely of a humble social status.

EDCS-80200059.summary: This is a brief inscription, likely funerary or dedicatory, honoring Lucius Sepunius Symphonius.

EDCS-80200060.summary: This is a very short inscription fragment, likely indicating ownership or dedication by Publius Crusus Faustus.

EDCS-80200074.translation: Of the Flavian Colony Augusta Puteolana, workshop of Caius N(---) S(---).
EDCS-80200074.summary: This inscription identifies the workshop of Caius N(---) S(---) within the Colonia Flavia Augusta Puteolana, likely an official marker from the imperial period.

EDCS-80200075.translation: Of the Flavian Colonia Augusta of Puteoli // From the workshop of Caius, son of N., S.
EDCS-80200075.summary: This inscription likely refers to a workshop or a craftsman associated with the colonia of Puteoli.

EDCS-80200085.summary: This is a personal name, possibly a tomb inscription or a dedication by Gaius Julius Victor.

EDCS-80200108.summary: This inscription records the name of an individual, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication by Er[o]tus.

EDCS-80200109.translation: From Marcus Fabius Eupl[i] / From Marcus Lucretius Epaphra / From Lucius Numisius Me[---]a[---]i / From Lucius Marcius Chryseros / From Marcus Fabius Eu[p]li // [From Marcus Fabius Eupli] 5,000 sesterces for the man
EDCS-80200109.summary: This text appears to be a list of names followed by a monetary amount, possibly related to a funerary monument or a communal expense.

EDCS-80200110.translation: From Marcus Nonius Magnus / [From Publius?] Marcius [---] / From Marcus Caecilius Potitus / From Numerius Blaesius Saturninus / From Marcus Ofillius Longinus / From Marcus Cl[o]dius Magnus Munatianus / From Aulus [Castric]ius(?) [---]r[---]i / From Quintus Junius Secundus / From Gaius Lucius Clemens / From Marcus Calator[ius]
EDCS-80200110.summary: This inscription is a list of names, likely representing individuals contributing to a monument or project, possibly from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-80200141.translation: Cuspius / Pansam, aedile // I ask you to make him
EDCS-80200141.summary: This inscription is a plea or recommendation for Cuspius Pansam, who was an aedile.

EDCS-80200142.summary: This is a personal name with manumission indication, likely part of a funerary or ownership record.

EDCS-80200165.summary: This is a personal name, possibly a tomb inscription or a dedication by Marcus Ampliatus.

EDCS-80200170.summary: This inscription records two personal names, possibly indicating a relationship or ownership, with 'Felix' appearing twice.

EDCS-80200173.summary: This inscription identifies 'Florus' with the descriptor 'insularis', likely referring to someone associated with an apartment building.

EDCS-80200174.summary: This is a brief farewell message, likely an epitaph or a casual inscription.

EDCS-80200179.translation: To Modia, may you be fortunate.
EDCS-80200179.summary: This is a short dedication or well-wishing inscription, possibly for a person named Modia.

EDCS-80200182.summary: This is a personal, possibly humorous, inscription about a bodily ailment or condition.

EDCS-80200183.summary: This is a simple naming inscription, identifying an individual and their parentage.

EDCS-80200187.summary: This is a dedication or memorial inscription for Marcus Suetus.

EDCS-80200188.summary: This is a brief farewell message, likely an epitaph or a casual inscription.

EDCS-80200191.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Avianiae Aegiale, most sweet and most dutiful mother, from her son Atilius Artema. To Masuria, a most excellent wife. Hail and farewell.
EDCS-80200191.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a son to his mother and a wife, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-80200192.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Avianius Felicioni, dearest pupil, was made (this monument) by his parents.
EDCS-80200192.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by parents to their beloved pupil, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-80400048.translation: To Aelius, the priest. Ascalonia, may you be fortunate. Made by the painter Claudius Primus, working for Vaturi(us) Vitalis.
EDCS-80400048.summary: This inscription records a dedication to a priest and a woman named Ascalonia, with the maker identified as a painter, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-80400056.summary: This is a short inscription identifying an individual named Mopsus with the title 'leader', possibly a local official or head of a group.

EDCS-80400063.summary: This is a dedicatory or congratulatory inscription likely to a woman named Cestilia.

EDCS-80400065.summary: This is a personal greeting from Cyrillus to Liccaeus.

EDCS-80400091.summary: This inscription likely describes two enslaved people named Anomalus and Vere.

EDCS-80400092.summary: This inscription identifies a person named Furuncule as a cook.

EDCS-80400094.translation: [---] to the duumvir Decimus P[---], son of Primus, [---] requests and that he may do [---].
EDCS-80400094.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a request or petition directed to a duumvir in the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-80700095.translation: In the second consulship of Emperor Agrippa, on the Ides of October, Lucius St[---] Nicephorus says this arrived with [his wife?] Stactene.
EDCS-80700095.summary: This fragment records an event, possibly a shipment or arrival, that occurred during the consulship of Agrippa, identified by its date and a witness.

EDCS-80700118.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment for a very young child.

EDCS-80700130.translation: To [---] Aninius, son of Lucius, [---] free[---]
EDCS-80700130.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to be a dedication or memorial, mentioning a man named Aninius.

EDCS-80700150.translation: Caius Julius Pholi's freedman, Diadumenus, to himself and to his freedmen and freedwomen of either sex whom he remembered in his will.
EDCS-80700150.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedman, Diadumenus, who is dedicating a tomb for himself and his own freedpeople.

EDCS-80700151.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. His parents made this for Caius Julius Phoebus, who lived 11 years and 6 months, well-deserving.
EDCS-80700151.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Caius Julius Phoebus, set up by his parents.

EDCS-80700152.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His parents made this for the well-deserving Iulius Valens, who lived 11 years.
EDCS-80700152.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Iulius Valens, set up by his parents.

EDCS-80700153.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Faltonius Trebelicus, who lived 75 years.
EDCS-80700153.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Faltonius Trebelicus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-80700155.translation: Expenses 900 sesterces. Nonia Successa, with Numerius Vibius Clemens as guarantor, on the 4th day before the Nones of November. [List of witnesses/debtors including] P. Fannius Pudentis, P. Flavius Largus, M. Burrius Clemens, Sex. Caecilius Carpus, M. Nonius Cypaerus, Q. Junius Apsyrtus, Cn. Pollus Ianuaris, L. Mammius Felicionis(?), C. Lollius Crescens, N. Vibius Clemens.
EDCS-80700155.summary: A financial record, possibly a loan or debt settlement, with a list of individuals involved, dated to November 1st in the consulship of P. Fannius Pudentis and P. Flavius Largus (or possibly other consuls, depending on the full dating context).

EDCS-81100049.translation: Gnaeus Lucceius, son of Gnaeus, a poet, first in popular plays, second in Atellan farces, and first in 'exodi'. Domitia [dedicates this] to the poet.
EDCS-81100049.summary: An epitaph or dedication for the poet Gnaeus Lucceius, highlighting his achievements in various theatrical genres.

EDCS-81200051.translation: From the pottery works of Publilia, belonging to Zosimus and Iulia Dynamis.
EDCS-81200051.summary: An inscription indicating ownership or origin from the pottery works of Publilia, associated with Zosimus and Iulia Dynamis.

EDCS-81200052.translation: From the estates of Faustina Augusta, pottery work by Victor.
EDCS-81200052.summary: Indicates pottery produced from the estates of Faustina Augusta, made by Victor.

EDCS-81200053.translation: Of Lucius Lurius Priscus (or similar name).
EDCS-81200053.summary: A simple ownership or dedication marking, likely for a property or object.

EDCS-81200062.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Fortuna. Lucius Aemilius Burrus, ship captain. Dedicated on the 6th day before the Ides of April, in the consulship of Rusticus II and Aquilinus.
EDCS-81200062.summary: A dedication to Emperor Augustus and the goddess Fortuna by Lucius Aemilius Burrus, a ship captain, dated to 8 April, in the year of the consulship of Rusticus II and Aquilinus (likely AD 165).

EDCS-81200066.translation: Eufrosyne, who lived in peace for more or less 3 years, 7 months, and 7 days, with her partner, in peace.
EDCS-81200066.summary: A funerary epitaph for Eufrosyne, who died at a young age, mentioning her peace and her partner.

EDCS-81200067.summary: A dedication or epitaph fragment for Pescennia Charitium.

EDCS-81200082.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Numerius Cadio Gallus, likely on a monument or object.

EDCS-81200083.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Isargyros [---]dotos, with the beginning and part of the name lost.

EDCS-81200084.summary: This inscription is a dedication to [---]llius, son of Marcus, with significant portions of the name and description lost.

EDCS-81400212.translation: Marcus Pettilius Alexander arranged for this to be made for his mother, himself, and his family.
EDCS-81400212.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Pettilius Alexander for his family, indicating the construction of a tomb or monument.

EDCS-81500041.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Larcius Gaenialis arranged for this to be made for his wife Martilla Eusaevia, and for his children (sons and daughters), and for her freedmen and freedwomen, and for her children and their descendants.
EDCS-81500041.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Larcius Gaenialis for his wife, children, and her freedmen, specifying beneficiaries of a tomb.

EDCS-81500043.summary: This inscription is likely a funerary dedication or ownership marker for Sextus Pomponius Valens.

EDCS-81600011.translation: Quinctia, daughter of Caius, gave this gift to Fortuna Primigenia.
EDCS-81600011.summary: This is a votive dedication by Quinctia to the goddess Fortuna Primigenia.

EDCS-81800025.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. These three sarcophagi within the tomb were granted to Gaius Terentius Amaranthus and were given as a surrounding area by Vaesonius Faustinianus, Laeta Primitiva, and Secunda.
EDCS-81800025.summary: This inscription records the allocation of three sarcophagi within a tomb to Gaius Terentius Amaranthus, with Vaesonius Faustinianus, Laeta Primitiva, and Secunda involved in the grant.

EDCS-81800026.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Crescentia placed this tombstone for her brother Aurelius Crescens, who lived for about 40 years, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-81800026.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by Aurelia Crescentia for her deceased brother Aurelius Crescens, providing his approximate age and praising him.

EDCS-81800027.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Olympia, daughter, made this for her most beloved mother Tettienia Capriola, a well-deserving woman.
EDCS-81800027.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by Olympia for her mother Tettienia Capriola, praising her and identifying the dedicator.

EDCS-81800053.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lovers of this man, Aurelius Alexander, a deserving friend.
EDCS-81800053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by friends for Aurelius Alexander, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82000090.translation: To Gaius Caesar, son of Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, prince of youth, pontiff, consul, emperor, who lived 14 years and was designated consul; the colonists of Axurnates [dedicate this] to their patron.
EDCS-82000090.summary: This is a dedication by the colonists of Axurnates to Gaius Caesar (son of Augustus), who died at age 14, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-82000091.translation: To Lucius Caesar, son of Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, prince of youth, augur, whom the people chose as consul, having lived 14 years; the colonists of Axurnates [dedicate this] to their patron.
EDCS-82000091.summary: This is a dedication by the colonists of Axurnates to Lucius Caesar (son of Augustus), who died at age 14, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-82000096.translation: [... from the] fleet of Misenum? served 11 years, lived 37 years. A Dalmatian by birth, Gaius Petronius Palus, his heir, made this memorial for him, a well-deserving person. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-82000096.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a sailor from the Misenum fleet, possibly of Dalmatian origin, who served 11 years and died at 37, set up by his heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82300108.translation: Cn(aeus) Lucceius [dedicates] this sacred offering to Apollo.
EDCS-82300108.summary: This is a religious dedication to the god Apollo by Cn(aeus) Lucceius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82400012.summary: This is a simple ownership mark or a very brief funerary dedication for Marcus Epidius Erastus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82800041.translation: In the consulship of Pupilius Agrippa and Tubero Fabius.
EDCS-82800041.summary: This inscription records a date by naming the consuls Pupilius Agrippa and Tubero Fabius, likely from the Late Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-82900002.translation: Lucius Senatius Firmus, son of Lucius, of the Menenia tribe, aedile, for himself and his parents and his freedwoman Daphne.
EDCS-82900002.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Lucius Senatius Firmus, an aedile, for himself, his parents, and his freedwoman Daphne, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82900003.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a funerary epitaph or ownership mark, for Lucius Senatius Firmus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82900004.summary: This is a funerary epitaph or record of manumission for Daphne, a freedwoman of Lucius Senatius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82900005.summary: This is a personal name, likely an epitaph or a dedication by Lucius S<e=I>natius Ianuarius.
